# 2.4 Uplink

## 2.4.1 The Algorithm Specification Document

### 2.4.1.0-1 The Algorithm Specification Document The 5Gmax algorithm specification versions - Overview (ID: 11101303)

**2.4.1.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11101306`)

The Algorithm Specification Document
The 5GMAX algorithm specification versions referred to in this chapter:

* For 5G21A release, 5GMAX version is 10.35
* Starting from 5G21B onwards and up to 22R4 release
* There is no invalidation for 5GMAX version 10.35 for the existing requirements, unless it’s done with one feature (eg. Algorithm updates feature).
* New 5GMAX version is added only for specific algorithm associated to one feature
* Starting from 23R1 release
* For each subfeature with 5GMAX algorithm update(s), the respective 5GMAX spec. version shall be added in the appropriate release version object
* Release-based versioning is partially considered:
* Creation of a new object for each system release with 5GMAX algorithm update
* No propagation of 5GMAX versions from release N-1 to release N.
* In case of delay for 5GMAX algorithm specification delivery (i.e. more than two weeks after CP2 approval), store the draft including the algorithm updates under CFAM folder and refer to it as temporary link until the final 5GMAX version delivery

---

### 2.4.1.0-2 The Algorithm Specification Document The 5Gmax algorithm specification document (ID: 11101310)

**2.4.1.0-2.0-15**  (ID: `13361299`)

The Algorithm Specification Document
The 5Gmax algorithm specification document 5GMax-5G NR Algorithm Specification LaTeX.url **is available on Sharepoint folder:**

<https://nokia.sharepoint.com/sites/5gmax/Shared%20Documents/Forms/AllItems.aspx?FolderCTID=0x0120005835DA4A693F4F459973F25EDCB28D07&viewid=7893e168%2D7e35%2D404c%2Dbcff%2D15511812f2cd&id=%2Fsites%2F5gmax%2FShared%20Documents%2FGeneralDocuments%2F5G%20Algorithm%20Specifications>

The specific version of document is available via "Version history".

Refer to [11101303](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcoPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) for 5GMAX version references overview.

[CB010737-B] Version of the document is 13.140 for the introduction of 'PmMinBased' method for PUCCH Format3 DTX detection on Loki and Thor L1 HW. [End CB010737-B]

[CB008465-AA] Version of the document is 13.140 for UL CoMP 8Rx (4Rx+4Rx) support on Thor L1 HW. [End CB008465-AA]

**Table Supported physical channels and HW platform by the 5Gmax algorithm specification document.**

|  |  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Channels | HW platform |  |  |  |  |
| Loner | Loki | Thor | Thor (Radio 7-2e) | GPU |  |
| PRACH | X | X | X | X | X |
| PUSCH | X | X | X | X | X |
| PUCCH | X | X | X | X | X |
| SRS | X | X | X | X | X |
| RIM-RS |  | X | X | X | - |
| RIM-RI |  | X | X | X | - |
| First Feature |  |  | CB007487-B CB007487-C CB007487-D | CB007595-B CB007595-C CB012564-A CB012564-B | CB016066-C |

---

### 2.4.1.0-3 The Algorithm Specification Document The algorithm specification document for Massive Mimo and Beamforming SRS beam managment (ID: 11101361)

**2.4.1.0-3.0-11**  (ID: `13454738`)

The Algorithm Specification Document
For TDD FR1 DU (for 7-2a) and Radio (for 7-2e), the algorithms related to Massive Mimo and Beamforming are described in Massive MIMO Algorithm Specification on the following link:

<https://nokia.sharepoint.com/:w:/r/sites/5gmax/_layouts/15/Doc.aspx?sourcedoc=%7BC7D041A7-9D30-43D5-9233-3FE4F70347B7%7D&file=Massive%20MIMO%20Algorithm%20Specification.docx&action=default&mobileredirect=true>

Version of the document is 3.77 about the PRACH Probing algorithm for FDD BF in eCPRI 7-2a.

Version of the document is 3.228 about the PUSCH and a-SRS split panel beamforming compensation factor for FDD BF in eCPRI 7-2a.

Version of the document is (TBC) YYY for PUSCH and SRS beamforming with MU-MIMO for TDD eCPRI 7-2a with DU for GPU L1 HW.

**[CB016062-BD] Version of the document (TBC) is  
- ZZZ section z.z.z.z for MMSE based short-term beam management (see ([11107356](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koeeA_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) for GPU L1 HW**

* NNN, Comb4 (KTC=4) group partition for Long-term (see ([11106868](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kocBwvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))
* KKK for Short-term beam (see ([11107356](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koeeA_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))  for GPU L1 HW
* EEE, simplified SINR estimation for SRS in section e.e.e.e for GPU L1 HW  **[End CB016062-BD]**

---

## 2.4.2 PUSCH Reception

### 2.4.2.0-1 PUSCH Reception Document Owner Rosalia Parello (Nokia) (ID: 11101397)

### 2.4.2.1 Resource allocation

#### 2.4.2.1.1 Start symbol for PUSCH

##### 2.4.2.1.1.0-1 Start symbol for PUSCH Supported start symbol for PUSCH FR1 TDD (ID: 11101406)

**2.4.2.1.1.0-1.0-6**  (ID: `13472374`)

Start symbol for PUSCH
Start symbol is given by 3GPP parameter (S) **in 38.214 6.1.2.1. Refer to [11517856](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_tUk9nvv1Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ).**

For the supported mapping type per cell refer to [11349366](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubd87vvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)

Start symbol indicates the index of the first OFDM symbol of PUSCH (regardless of the mapping type) in a slot used by a UE.

Note: Configuration from 5G-L2-PS is as per [11115802](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpyFlPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

DU and Radio shall support below configurations for different L1 HWs:

|  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **Start symbols (S)** | **Loner** | **Loki** | **Thor** | **Thor (Radio 7-2e)** | GPU | Odin |
| 0 | supported | supported | supported | supported | supported | Supported |
| 2 | supported | - | - | - | - | - |
| **First Feature** |  |  | CB007487-B CB007487-C | CB007595-B | CB016066-C | CB014420-HA |

**NOTE: From E2E perspective, Start symbol equals to 2 is not supported**

---

##### 2.4.2.1.1.0-2 Start symbol for PUSCH Supported start symbol for PUSCH FR1 FDD (ID: 11101427)

**2.4.2.1.1.0-2.0-8**  (ID: `13472384`)

Start symbol for PUSCH
Start symbol is given by 3GPP parameter (S) **in 38.214 6.1.2.1. Refer to 5G\_UP\_IF\_2195\_replaced([11517856](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_tUk9nvv1Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)).**

For the supported mapping type per cell refer to 5G\_UP\_392\_replaced([11349366](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubd87vvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

Start symbol indicates the index of the first OFDM symbol of PUSCH (regardless of the mapping type) in a slot used by a UE. Configuration from 5G-L2-PS is as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2424\_replaced([11115802](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpyFlPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

|  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Start symbols (S) | Loner | Loki | Thor | Thor (FHE) | GPU | Odin |
| 0 | supported | supported | supported | supported | supported | Supported |
| 2 | supported | supported | supported | supported | supported |  |
| 3 | - | supported | supported |  | - |  |
| First Feature |  |  | CB007487-D | CB013976-C | CB016406-A | CB014420-HF |

---

##### 2.4.2.1.1.0-3 Start symbol for PUSCH Supported start symbol for PUSCH FR2 TDD (ID: 11101444)

**2.4.2.1.1.0-3.0-2**  (ID: `11101451`)

Start symbol for PUSCH
Start symbol is given by 3GPP parameter (S) in 38.214 6.1.2.1. Refer to 5G\_UP\_IF\_2195\_replaced([11517856](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_tUk9nvv1Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

For the supported mapping type per cell refer to 5G\_UP\_392\_replaced([11349366](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubd87vvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

Start symbol indicates the index of the first OFDM symbol of PUSCH (regardless of the mapping type) in a slot used by a UE. Configuration from 5G-L2-PS is as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2424\_replaced([11115802](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpyFlPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Start symbols (S) | Loner | Loki | Thor | vDU |
| 0 | supported | supported | supported | - |
| 2 | - | - | - | - |
| First Feature |  |  | CB010576-A |  |

#### 2.4.2.1.2 Number of symbols for PUSCH

##### 2.4.2.1.2.0-1 Number of symbols for PUSCH Supported number of symbols for PUSCH FR1 TDD (ID: 11101457)

**2.4.2.1.2.0-1.0-9**  (ID: `13472397`)

Number of symbols for PUSCH
Length is given by 3GPP parameters (L) in 38.214 6.1.2.1.

DU and Radio shall support Length (L) values as given below for FR1 TDD:

|  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **Number of symbols L** | **Loner** | **Loki** | **Thor** | **Thor (Radio 7-2e)** | **L1 GPU** | Odin |
| 9 | supported | - | - | - | - | - |
| 10 | supported | supported | supported | supported | supported | - |
| 11 | supported | supported | supported | supported | supported | - |
| 12 | supported | supported | supported | supported | supported | - |
| 13 | supported | supported | supported | supported | supported | supported |
| 14 | supported | supported | supported | supported | supported | supported |
| **First feature** |  |  | CB007487-B CB007487-C | CB007595-B | CB016066-C | CB014420-HA |

When multiple UEs are scheduled in the same cell and slot, refer to [11101517](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knYR2_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) and [11101818](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knb8OvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) for L1 limitations

---

##### 2.4.2.1.2.0-2 Number of symbols for PUSCH Supported number of symbols for PUSCH for FR1 FDD (ID: 11101484)

**2.4.2.1.2.0-2.0-8**  (ID: `13472405`)

Number of symbols for PUSCH
Length is given by 3GPP parameters (L) **in 38.214 6.1.2.1.**

Supported Length (L) is given below for FR1 FDD:

|  |  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Number of symbols L | Loner | Loki | Thor | GPU | Odin |
| 8 | - | supported | supported | - | - |
| 9 | - | supported | supported | - | - |
| 10 | supported | supported | supported | supported | - |
| 11 | supported | supported | supported | supported | - |
| 12 | supported | supported | supported | supported | - |
| 13 | supported | supported | supported | supported | Supported |
| 14 | supported | supported | supported | supported | Supported |
| First Feature |  |  | CB007487-D | CB016406-A | CB014420-HF |

The DU shall support mixed type A and type B configuration for different UEs in a cell within the same slot, the supported symbol length combination with PUSCH mapping type A or B, please refer to [11517856](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_tUk9nvv1Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ).

When multiple UEs are scheduled in the same cell and slot, refer to [11101517](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knYR2_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) and [11101818](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knb8OvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) for L1 limitations

---

##### 2.4.2.1.2.0-3 Number of symbols for PUSCH Supported number of symbols for PUSCH for FR2 TDD (ID: 11101507)

**2.4.2.1.2.0-3.0-2**  (ID: `11101514`)

Number of symbols for PUSCH
Length is given by 3GPP parameter (L) in 38.214 6.1.2.1.

Supported Length (L) is given below for FR2:

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Number of symbols L | Loner | Loki | Thor | vDU |
| 10 | supported | supported | [CB010576-D] supported             [End CB010576-D] | - |
| 11 | supported | supported | [CB010576-A] supported [End CB010576-A] | - |
| 12 | supported | supported | [CB010576-A] supported [End CB010576-A] | - |
| 13 | supported | supported | [CB010576-D] supported [End CB010576-D] | - |
| 14 | conformance tests only | conformance tests only | [CB010576-D] conformance tests only [End CB010576-D] | - |
| First Feature |  |  | CB010576-A |  |

##### 2.4.2.1.2.0-4 Number of symbols for PUSCH L1 limitation when multiple UEs per cell per slot (ID: 11101517)

**2.4.2.1.2.0-4.0-6**  (ID: `13472415`)

Number of symbols for PUSCH
When multiple UEs are scheduled in the same cell and slot (refer to [11397111](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_uf04dvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) for PUSCH capacity per L1 HW and technology):

* the DU, for Loner, Loki, Thor, [CB014420-HA] Odin [End CB014420-HA] and GPU**L1 HWs, and Radio, for Thor 7-2e L1 HW,**shall support different values L for all the multiplexed (FDMed or SDMed **when applicable) UEs**\*\*\*\*

Refer to [11101457](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knXq0fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ), [11101484](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knYR0vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) and [11101507](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knYR2PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) for the supported values L per L1 HW and technology

---

#### 2.4.2.1.3 Start PRB for PUSCH

##### 2.4.2.1.3.0-1 Start PRB for PUSCH Starting PRB for PUSCH (ID: 11101539)

**2.4.2.1.3.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `11101545`)

Start PRB for PUSCH
The PRBs that are allocated by the long PUCCH cannot be allocated for the PUSCH. This results in a restricted set of possible starting PRBs for the PUSCH allocation for FR1.

In PRACH slot, the PRBs which are occupied by the preamble (msg1) cannot be allocated for the PUSCH. This results in a restricted set of possible starting PRBs for the PUSCH allocation.

**[CB007595-B]**

Note:

This requirement shall be also supported by Radio (L1) for eCPRI 7-2e.

**[End CB007595-B]**

---

##### 2.4.2.1.3.0-2 Start PRB for PUSCH Supported starting PRB for PUSCH in FR1 TDD (ID: 11101549)

**2.4.2.1.3.0-2.0-5**  (ID: `13332667`)

Start PRB for PUSCH
DU and Radio shall support starting PRB for PUSCH in FR1 TDD as follows:

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **L1 HW** | **Starting PRB** | **First Feature** |
| Loner | [0..UL\_bandwidth-min\_PRB\_size] |  |
| Loki | [0..UL\_bandwidth-min\_PRB\_size] |  |
| Thor | [0..UL\_bandwidth-min\_PRB\_size] |  |
| Thor (Radio 7-2e) | [0..UL\_bandwidth-min\_PRB\_size] | CB007595-B |
| L1 GPU | [0..UL\_bandwidth-min\_PRB\_size] | CB016066-C |

(Values in the table are implicitly cumulative within FR and BWP)

* UL\_bandwidth is maximum number of PRBs in the BW, as specified in [11094261](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmaoifvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)
* min\_PRB\_size is minimal number of PRBs, as specified in [11101624](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knZf_fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)

Note: 5G-L1-UL shall ensure that startPrb + numPrb <= max(numPrb)

---

##### 2.4.2.1.3.0-3 Start PRB for PUSCH Supported starting PRB for PUSCH in FR1 FDD (ID: 11101564)

**2.4.2.1.3.0-3.0-4**  (ID: `13427737`)

Start PRB for PUSCH
Starting PRB for PUSCH in FR1 FDD is as follows :

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **L1 HW** | **Starting PRB** | **First Feature** |
| Loner | [0..UL\_bandwidth-min\_PRB\_size] |  |
| Loki | [0..UL\_bandwidth-min\_PRB\_size] |  |
| Thor | [0..UL\_bandwidth-min\_PRB\_size] | CB007487-D |
| GPU | [0..UL\_bandwidth-min\_PRB\_size] | CB016406-A |

(Values in the table are implicitly cumulative within FR and BWP)

* UL\_bandwidth is maximum number of PRBs in the BW, as specified in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_22\_replaced([11094308](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmbPlPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
* min\_PRB\_size is minimal number of PRBs, as specified in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_772\_replaced([11101647](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knaHBPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

Note: 5G-L1-UL shall ensure that startPrb + numPrb <= max(numPrb)

---

##### 2.4.2.1.3.0-4 Start PRB for PUSCH Supported starting PRB for PUSCH in FR2 TDD (ID: 11101577)

**2.4.2.1.3.0-4.0-2**  (ID: `11101585`)

Start PRB for PUSCH

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  |  | First Feature |
| Loner | [0..64] |  |
| Loki | [0…64]    For conformance tests: 0 |  |
| Thor | [CB010576-A] 0 For conformance tests: 0 [End CB010576-A]     [CB010576-D] [0…64]  [End CB010576-D] | CB010576-A |
| vDU | - |  |

Note: 5G-L1-UL shall ensure that startPrb + numPrb <= max(numPrb)

---

#### 2.4.2.1.4 Number of PRB for PUSCH and UL NR Waveforms

##### 2.4.2.1.4.0-1 Number of PRB for PUSCH and UL NR Waveforms UL NR waveforms (ID: 11101591)

**2.4.2.1.4.0-1.0-3**  (ID: `11101601`)

Number of PRB for PUSCH and UL NR Waveforms
3GPP defines two possible waveforms for PUSCH (cf. 38.211 section 6.3.1.4) either Transform Precoding disabled (CP-OFDM waveform) or Transform Precoding enabled (DFT-s-OFDM waveform). 5G\_UP\_ALG\_16646\_replaced([11101589](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knZf9PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) shows the waveforms support with the correponding number of PRBs.

[Before CB009514-F]

Note:

For eCPRI 7-2e, Transform Precoding (DFT-s-OFDM waveform) is not valid in Radio(L1).

[End CB009514-F]

---

12813920:
Number of PRB for PUSCH and UL NR Waveforms
When transform precoding is disabled, i.e. for CP-OFDM, the total number of PRBs (nPRB as per 38.214 6.1.4) supported within BWP used by UE in given slot is as per below.

In case of RAT0, it is defined as total number of PRBs occupied by all RBGs (as per 38.214 6.1.2.2).

In case of RAT1, it is defined as LRBs (as per 38.214 6.1.2.2).

When transform precoding is enabled via MRBTS/NRBTS/NRCELL/actUlDftSOfdm **[CB008148-A]   (refer to [11360790](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucw9aPvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) ) for PUSCH granted by PDCCH DCI\_0\_1 and via MRBTS/NRBTS/NRCELL/actUlTransPreUsageEnh for PUSCH MSG3 and/or PUSCH granted by PDCCH DCI\_0\_0**[End CB008148-A] **[Before CB008148-A] (except to**[CB008148-A]  also for **[End CB008148-A] PUSCH scheduled by RAR grant: [11513340](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_tT19zPv1Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ) ) number of PUSCH PRBs is limited to selected value according 3GPP formula (see [11496001](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_tSCm__v1Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)):*****\****\*

[image-0][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical expression: $2^{a\_2} \cdot 3^{a\_3} \cdot 5^{a\_5}$. The expression consists of three terms multiplied together. Each term is a base number raised to a power. The bases are 2, 3, and 5, and their respective exponents are denoted by $a\_2$, $a\_3$, and $a\_5$. The multiplication is indicated by the dot symbol ($\cdot$).[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_1663.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

where: (α2, α3, α5 is a set of non-negative integer)

##### 2.4.2.1.4.0-3 Number of PRB for PUSCH and UL NR Waveforms Supported number of PRBs for PUSCH for FR1 TDD (ID: 11101624)

**2.4.2.1.4.0-3.0-9**  (ID: `13472416`)

Number of PRB for PUSCH and UL NR Waveforms
DU and Radio shall support the below values for the number of PUSCH PRBs:

|  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Number of PRBs | Waveform | Loner | Loki | Thor | Thor (Radio 7-2e) | GPU | Odin | First Feature |
| [1..UL\_bandwidth] | CP-OFDM | Supported (Except 1 PRB) | supported | supported | supported | supported | [CB014420] supported [End CB014420] |  |
| [1..UL\_bandwidth] | DFT-s-OFDM | supported (Except 1 PRB) | supported (\*) | supported (\*) | supported (\*) | - |  | 5GC000545-C |

Refer to [11094261](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmaoifvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) for UL\_bandwidth=maximum number of PRBs )depending on the BW size.

This range is valid for all the supported DMRS configurations in [11101751](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knbVJ_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)

Note: CP-OFDM refers to Transform Precoding disabled.

DFT-s-OFDM refers to Transform Precoding enabled.   
(\*) Supported for PDCCH DCI\_0\_1, and/or PUSCH MSG3 and/or PUSCH granted by PDCCH DCI\_0\_0 as function of System setup configuration as per NIDD MRBTS/NRBTS/NRCELL/actUlDftSOfdm and MRBTS/NRBTS/NRCELL/actUlTransPreUsageEnh. Refer to [11101605](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knZf-fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

---

##### 2.4.2.1.4.0-4 Number of PRB for PUSCH and UL NR Waveforms Supported number of PRBs for PUSCH for FR1 FDD (ID: 11101647)

**2.4.2.1.4.0-4.0-7**  (ID: `13472431`)

Number of PRB for PUSCH and UL NR Waveforms

|  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Number of PRBs | Waveform | Loner | Loki | Thor | GPU | Odin | First Feature |
| [x..UL\_bandwidth] | CP-OFDM | supported, x=2 | supported, x=1 | supported, x=1 | supported, x=1 | [CB014420-HF] supported, x=1 [End CB014420-HF |  |
| [x..UL\_bandwidth] | DFT-s-OFDM | - | supported, x=1 (\*) | Supported, x=1 (\*) | - | - | 5GC000545-B |
| [x..100] | DFT-s-OFDM | supported, x=2 | - | - | - | - | CB007781-A |

Refer to [11094320](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmbPmPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) for UL\_bandwidth=maximum number of PRBs depending on the BW size.  
This range is valid for all the supported DMRS configurations in [11101782](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knb8MPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).  
Note: CP-OFDM refers to Transform Precoding disabled. DFT-s-OFDM refers to Transform Precoding enabled.

(\*) Supported for PDCCH DCI\_0\_1, and/or PUSCH MSG3 and/or PUSCH granted by PDCCH DCI\_0\_0 as function of System setup configuration as per NIDD MRBTS/NRBTS/NRCELL/actUlDftSOfdm and MRBTS/NRBTS/NRCELL/actUlTransPreUsageEnh  
refer to [11101605](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knZf-fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)

---

##### 2.4.2.1.4.0-5 Number of PRB for PUSCH and UL NR Waveforms Supported number of PRBs for PUSCH for FR2 TDD (ID: 11101663)

**2.4.2.1.4.0-5.0-2**  (ID: `11101670`)

Number of PRB for PUSCH and UL NR Waveforms

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Number of PRBs | Waveform | Loner | Loki | Thor |
| {3, 48, 66} | CP-OFDM | supported | supported | supported |
| {2, 4, 8, 14, 16, 32, 64\*} | CP-OFDM | supported | supported | [CB010576-D] supported [End CB010576-D] |
| First Feature |  |  |  | CB010576-A |

This range is valid for all the supported DMRS configurations in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_805\_replaced([11101805](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knb8NvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

Note: CP-OFDM refers to Transform precoding disabled.

\*Note: 64PRBs is not supported from E2E, see 5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_2029\_replaced([11277080](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpLfx_vcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

---

### 2.4.2.2 DM-RS configuration

#### 2.4.2.2.0-1 DM-RS configuration DMRS Sequence Generation (ID: 11101677)

**2.4.2.2.0-1.0-4**  (ID: `11101690`)

DM-RS configuration
If transform precoding is not enabled, as defined in section 6.4.1.1.1.1 of 38.211, the Demodulation Reference Signal for "CP-OFDM" PUSCH waveform (5G\_UP\_ALG\_16648\_replaced([11101591](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knZf9fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is based on "Pseudo-random" sequence generation defined in section 5.2.1 of 38.211.

If transform precoding is enabled, as defined in section 6.4.1.1.1.2 of 38.211, the Demodulation Reference Signal for "DFT-s-OFDM" PUSCH waveform (5G\_UP\_ALG\_16648\_replaced([11101591](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knZf9fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is based on:

* "Low-PAPR sequence generation Type 1" defined in section 5.2.2 of 38.211
* or "Low-PAPR Sequence generation Type2" (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_792\_replaced([11101692](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knauFPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) defined is section 5.2.3 of 38.211

See 5G\_UP\_ALG\_21100\_replaced([11101728](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knbVIPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for DMRS Sequence Generation supported values per board.

Note: For eCPRI 7-2e:

[Before CB009936-F]

* Low-PAPR Sequence generation Type2[End CB009936-F]

---

#### 2.4.2.2.0-2 DM-RS configuration DMRS configuration - Parameters reference (ID: 11101692)

**2.4.2.2.0-2.0-5**  (ID: `12806983`)

DM-RS configuration
DU and Radio (for 7-2e) shall support the DM-RS configuration for a PUSCH reception which is identified thanks to the:

*DMRS-UplinkConfig structure defined in 38.331:*

* *dmrs-Type: The DM-RS configuration type, see also section 6.4.1.1.3 of 38.211.*

Supported value is "1" (value not present in the RRC message).

* **maxLength: The** max number of OFDM symbols used for the front-loaded DM-RS (single-symbol**or double-symbol DMRS), see section 6.4.1.1.3 in 38.211 with reference to "time index" in Table 6.4.1.1.3-5 in 38.211.**\*\*\*\*
* *dmrs-AdditionalPosition: The maximum number of additional DM-RS on top of the front-loaded DM-RS, see 38.211 the index in Table 6.4.1.1.3-3 for single-symbol DM-RS (*maxLength=1) or in Table 6.4.1.1.3-4 for double-symbol DM-RS (*maxLength=2).*\*\*
* *PUSCH-TimeDomainResourceAllocation structure defined in 38.331*
* *mappingType: The PUSCH mapping type, see section 6.4.1.1.3 in 38.211*
* *dmrs-UplinkTransformPrecoding (higher-layer parameter in 38.211) OR* dmrs-UplinkTransformPrecoding-r16 **(Information Element in 38.331)** the Reference-Signal sequence is generated with "Low-PAPR Sequence generation Type1" defined by clause §5.2.2 of 38.211, or with "Low-PAPR Sequence generation Type2" defined by clause §5.2.3 of 38.211, as specified in 38.211 §6.4.1.1.1.2**, when transform precoding is enabled.****\***\*

See [11101728](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knbVIPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) for supported Types in L1 HW Variant.

**[CB008148-A] RACH-ConfigCommon structure defined in 38.331:**

* msg3-transformPrecoder (higher-layer parameter in 38.214 §6.1.3) that enables the transform precoder for Msg3 transmission with "Low-PAPR Sequence generation Type1" for the DM-RS sequence generation (refer to [11101677](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knaHDfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). **[End CB008148-A]**

*ServingCellConfigCommon structure defined in 38.331:*

* *dmrs-TypeA-Position: The position of the front-loaded DM-RS for the PUSCH mapping type A, see also section 6.4.1.1.3 of 38.211*
* The number of DMRS CDM group(s) without data, see the index Tables 7.3.1.1.2-6 to 7.3.1.1.2-23 in 38.212. Where the number of DMRS CDM group(s) without data of values 1, 2, and 3 refers to CDM groups {0}, {0,1}, and {0, 1,2} respectively

*ConfiguredGrantConfig structure defined in 38.331.*

* *dmrs-SeqInitialization: See the definition of nSCID as specified in 38.211 §6.4.1.1.1.1, when transform precoding is disabled,*
* *dmrs-SeqInitialization: See the definition of nSCID as specified in 38.211 §6.4.1.1.1.2**, when transform precoding is enabled,*\*\*

Please see [11101709](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knauGfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) for *dmrs-SeqInitialization supported values per board.*

The number of OFDM symbol allocated for the PUSCH transmission as defined in [11101400](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knXDuvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

* For the DMRS configurations per L1 HW, refer to:
* [11101751](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knbVJ_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) for TDD FR1, [11101782](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knb8MPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) for FDD and [11101805](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knb8NvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) for TDD FR2

---

#### 2.4.2.2.0-3 DM-RS configuration DMRS Sequence for paired UEs in MU-MIMO (ID: 13313026)

**2.4.2.2.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `13313030`)

DM-RS configuration
If Transform Precoding is enabled (refer to [11101677](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knaHDfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) with "Low-PAPR sequence generation Type 1" DMRS Sequence Generation (refer to [11101677](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knaHDfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)),

DU and Radio shall expect PUSCH programmed for all UEs paired in MU-MIMO mode ([11355469](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucROKvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)), with the same DMRS PUSCH waveform within same CDM group (refer to rationale in [13310564](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_LGklsM33EfCVOOJMfwsFiw)) . For DMRS configuration definition and supported details refer to:

* DMRS Sequence Generation supported values per board, in [11101728](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knbVIPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)
* DMRS Configuration supported values per board in FR1 TDD, in [11101751](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knbVJ_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)
* DMRS Related Information and DMRS Ports allocation for UL MU-MIMO for different sub-cell type ([11094165](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmaBcPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) respectively with PUSCH Receiver order:   
  - 4RX in [11104156](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kn5POvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)   
  - 8RX in [11104222](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kn6dUfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)
* 16RX in [11104360](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kn7refvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

#### 2.4.2.2.0-5 DM-RS configuration Supported DMRS Sequence Generation (ID: 11101728)

**2.4.2.2.0-5.0-9**  (ID: `13473236`)

DM-RS configuration
DU and Radio shall support DMRS Sequence generation [11101677](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knaHDfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) for different L1 HW variants as follow :

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| L1 HW | Pseudo-Random | Low-PAPR Type 1 | Low-PAPR Type 2 | First Feature |
| Loner | supported for FR1 FDD/TDD and FR2 | supported for FR1 FDD/TDD | - |  |
| Loki | supported for FR1 FDD/TDD and FR2 | supported for FR1 FDD/TDD (\*) | supported for FR1 TDD |  |
| Thor | supported for FR1 FDD/TDD | supported for FR1 FDD/TDD | supported for FR1 TDD |  |
| Thor (Radio 7-2e) | supported for FR1 TDD | supported for FR1 TDD (\*) | supported for FR1 TDD |  |
| GPU | supported for FR1 TDD/FDD | - | - | CB016066-C |
| Odin | Supported for FR1 TDD [CB014420-HF] /FDD [End CB014420-HF] | - | - | CB014420-HA |

(\*) Note: DU or Radio shall support also for PUSCH MSG3, for details refer to [11101692](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knauFPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

#### 2.4.2.2.0-6 DM-RS configuration Supported DMRS configuration for PUSCH in FR1 TDD (ID: 11101751)

**2.4.2.2.0-6.0-9**  (ID: `13470337`)

DM-RS configuration
Supported DMRS configuration in [11101692](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knauFPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) for TDD FR1 is given in the below table.

Please refer to corresponding section in RRM concepts for more details : [11349350](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubd86vvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

DU and Radio shall support the DMRS configurations given in the below table for different L1 HWs:

|  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **L1 HW** | **DMRS configuration Type** | **DMRS Length** | **Mapping Type** | **Additional Position** | **Type A position** | **CDM Group** | **First Feature** |
|  | *ulDmrsConfigType* {1, 2} | *ulDmrsLen* {1, 2} | *ulDmrsMappingType* {Type A, Type B} | *ulDmrsAddPos* {0, 1, 2, 3} | *ulDmrsTypeAPos* {2, 3} | *numOfDmrsCdmGroup* *WithoutData {1, 2, 3}* |  |
| Loner | 1 | 1 | {Type A, Type B} | {0,1,2} 2 only for PUSCH scheduled by RAR or DCI0\_0 | {2, 3} | {1, 2} |  |
| Loki | 1 | 1 | {Type A, Type B} | {0,1,2} | {2, 3} | {1, 2} |  |
| Thor | 1 | 1 | {Type A, Type B} | {0,1,2} | {2 **,3}** | {1, 2} | CB007487-B CB007487-C |
| Thor (Radio 7-2e) | 1 | 1 **(note 1),  2 (note 2)** | {Type A , Type B}   (note 1) | { **0,** 1, 2} (note 1)\*\*\*\* | {2 , 3 **} (note 1)** | { 1 (note 2),  2 (note 1) } | CB007595-B |
| L1 GPU | 1 | 1 | {Type A , Type B ) | {0, 1, 2} 2 only for PUSCH scheduled by RAR or DCI 0\_0 | {2 , 3 **}** | **{ 1,**2 }\*\*\*\* | CB016066-C |

(note 1) (note 2)For eCPRI 7.2e, Radio (for Thor 7.2e L1 HW) shall support referred DMRS configurations inputs and provide related outputs for subsequent PUSCH processing steps (refer to PUSCH overview processing scheme [11101887](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kncjTPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) to DU (for either Thor or GPU L1HW).

For eCPRI 7.2e, the DU (for Thor and GPU L1 HW), and the Radio (for Thor 7.2e L1 HW) shall support *ulDmrsMappingType Type B, or TypeA, and*ulDmrsAddPos = 1.\*\*

[CB010737-D] For eCPRI-7-2e, the DU and the Radio on Thor L1 HW shall also support *ulDmrsAddPos = 0 with DMRS mapping type A. [End CB010737-D]*

For **DMRS configuration with DMRS "Length"** = 2**, refer to [11349390](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubej9fvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).**\*\*\*\*

#### 2.4.2.2.0-7 DM-RS configuration Supported DMRS configuration for PUSCH in FR1 FDD (ID: 11101782)

**2.4.2.2.0-7.0-7**  (ID: `13478650`)

DM-RS configuration
Supported DMRS configuration in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_792\_replaced([11101692](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knauFPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for FDD FR1 is given in the below table.

Please refer to corresponding section in RRM concepts for more details : 5G\_UP\_388\_replaced([11349350](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubd86vvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

|  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **L1 HW** | **DMRS configuration Type** | **DMRS Length** | **Mapping Type** | **Additional Position** | **Type A position** | **CDM Group** | **First Feature** |
|  | *ulDmrsConfigType* {1, 2} | *ulDmrsLen* {1, 2} | *ulDmrsMappingType* {Type A, Type B} | *ulDmrsAddPos* {0, 1, 2, 3} | *ulDmrsTypeAPos* {2, 3} | *numOfDmrsCdmGroupWithoutData {1, 2, 3}* |  |
| Loner | 1 | 1 | {Type A, Type B} | {0,1,2} 2 only for PUSCH scheduled by RAR or DCI0\_0 | {2,3} | {1, 2} |  |
| Loki | 1 | 1 | {Type A, Type B} | {0, 1, 2} | {2,3} | {1, 2} |  |
| Thor | 1 | 1 | **{Type A, Type B}** | {0, 1, 2} | **{2,3}** | { 1, 2} | CB007487-D |
| GPU | 1 | 1 | {**[CB016062-AD]  Type A,** [End CB016062-AD]  Type B}\*\*\*\* | {0 **[CB016062-AD] 1** [End CB016062-AD]  2 only for PUSCH scheduled by RAR or DCI 0\_0\*\*\*\* | **[CB016062-AD]** {2,3}[End CB016062-AD]\*\*\*\* | **[CB016062-AD]** { 1,[End CB016062-AD] 2}\*\*\*\* | CB016406-A |

The DU shall support mixed DMRS type A and DMRS type B configuration for different UEs in a cell within the same slot.

#### 2.4.2.2.0-8 DM-RS configuration Supported DMRS configuration for PUSCH in FR2 TDD (ID: 11101805)

**2.4.2.2.0-8.0-3**  (ID: `11101814`)

DM-RS configuration
Supported DMRS configuration in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_792\_replaced([11101692](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knauFPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for TDD FR2 is given in the below table.

Please refer to corresponding section in RRM concepts for more details : 5G\_UP\_388\_replaced([11349350](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubd86vvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

|  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  | **DMRS configuration Type** | **DMRS Length** | **Mapping Type** | **Additional Position** | **Type A position** | **CDM Group** | **First Feature** |
|  | *ulDmrsConfigType* {1, 2} | *ulDmrsLen* {1, 2} | *ulDmrsMappingType* {Type A, Type B} | *ulDmrsAddPos* {0, 1, 2, 3} | *ulDmrsTypeAPos* {2, 3} | *numOfDmrsCdmGroupWithoutData* *{1, 2, 3}* |  |
| Loner | 1 | 1 | {Type A, Type B} Type A only for conformance test | {0,1,2} 2 only for PUSCH scheduled by RAR or DCI0\_0 | {2} 2 used for conformance tests only | {2} |  |
| Loki | 1 | 1 | { **[CB007783-A]** Type A **[End CB007783-A]** , Type B } | {0,1,2} 2 only for PUSCH scheduled by RAR or DCI0\_0 | **[CB007783-A]** {2,3} **[End CB007783-A]** | {      **[CB007783-B] 1** **[End CB007783-B] , 2}** | CB007783-A CB007783-B |
| Thor | 1 | 1 | {Type B} | {0,2} 2 only for PUSCH scheduled by RAR or DCI0\_0 {1} | **-** | {2} | CB010576-D |
| vDU | - | - | - | - | - | - |  |

For each scheduled UE on PUSCH, DMRS positions per slot, [image-0][image\_desc]The image is a blurred abstract representation of colors and shapes. It appears to be a gradient of colors, transitioning from a light beige on the left to darker grays and blues in the center, and then to white on the right. There are no discernible objects, text, or equations in the image.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_1725.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0] (see 38.211 section 6.4.1.1.3) depend on:

* MappingType and 1st DMRS time domain position, see [11101692](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knauFPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)
* Additional DMRS time domain position, see [11101692](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knauFPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)
* Duration ld, see [11498873](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_tSQpaPv1Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ), [11101404](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knXDu_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) and ([11101455](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knXq0PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)
* maxLength (or ulDmrsLen), see [11101692](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knauFPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)

When multiple UEs are scheduled in the same cell and slot (refer to 5G\_UP\_3196\_replaced([11397111](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_uf04dvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for PUSCH capacity per L1 HW and technology):

* For all the frequency domain multiplexed (FDMed) UEs, the DU, for Loner, Loki and Thor and GPU L1 HWs, and Radio, for Thor 7-2e L1 HW, shall support different DMRS positions as function of the below parameters:
* MappingType and 1st DMRS time domain position, refer to [11349366](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubd87vvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)
* Additional DMRS time domain position, refer to [11349402](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubej-fvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) [CB010737-B] and [13411747](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ob2h4xGIEfG3gf_qmG8Vww?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) [End CB010737-B]
* Duration ld, refer to [11498873](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_tSQpaPv1Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ) and [11517856](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_tUk9nvv1Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)
* For all the multiplexed UEs in the spatial domain when applicable (SDMed or MU-MIMO paired UEs, see [11355469](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucROKvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), the DU, for Loki, Thor and GPU**L1 HWs, and Radio, for Thor 7-2e L1 HW, have the following limitation:**
* The DMRS positions for all the paired UEs in the same PRB shall be the same (i.e. same maxLength, [CB010737-D] mapping type, [End CB010737-D] 1st DMRS time domain position and additional DMRS time domain position)

#### 2.4.2.3.0-1 PTRS configuration UL PTRS configuration in FR1 TDD (ID: 11101852)

**2.4.2.3.0-1.0-3**  (ID: `13332674`)

PTRS configuration
DU shall support UL PT-RS enabled or disabled as follows:

|  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  | Waveform | PTRS {disabled/enabled} | LPT-RS (38.211 §6.4.1.2.2.1) | KPT-RS  (38.211 §6.4.1.2.2.1) | number of antenna ports for PTRS (38.214 §6.2.3) | *UL-PTRS-RE-offset (38.211 §6.4.1.2.2.1)* | First Feature |
| Loner | CP-OFDM | {disabled,enabled} | {1} | {2,4} | {1} | {0} |  |
| Loki | CP-OFDM | {disabled,enabled} | {1} | {2,4} | {1} | {0} |  |
| Thor | CP-OFDM | {disabled,enabled} | {1} | {2,4} | {1} | {0} | CB008325-5G-AA |
| GPU | CP-OFDM | {disabled} | - | - | - | - | CB016066-C |
| Loner | DFT-s-OFDM | {disabled,enabled} | {1} | N/A | {1} | {0} | CB009188-A |
| Loki | DFT-s-OFDM | {disabled,enabled} | {1} | N/A | {1} | {0} | 5GC000545-B 5GC000545-C |
| Thor | DFT-s-OFDM | {disabled,enabled} | {1} | N/A | {1} | {0} | CB008325-5G-AA |
| GPU | DFT-s-OFDM | {disabled} | - | - | - | - | CB016066-C |

|  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  | Waveform | PTRS {disabled/enabled} | LPT-RS (38.211 §6.4.1.2.2.1) | KPT-RS  (38.211 §6.4.1.2.2.1) | number of antenna ports for PTRS (38.214 §6.2.3) | UL-PTRS-RE-offset (38.211 §6.4.1.2.2.1) | First Feature |

| Loki | CP-OFDM | {disabled,enabled} | {1} | {2,4} | {1} | {0} |  |
| vDU\* | [Before CNI-103796-M] CP-OFDM [End CNI-103796-M] | [Before CNI-103796-M] {disabled} [End CNI-103796-M] | - | - | - | - |  |
| Thor | CP-OFDM | {disabled,enabled} | {1} | {2,4} | {1} | {0} | CB008325-5G-BA |
| Loki | DFT-s-OFDM | {disabled,enabled} | {1} | N/A | {1} | {0} | 5GC000545-B 5GC000545-C |
| Loner | DFT-s-OFDM | {disabled,enabled} | {1} | N/A | {1} | {0} | CB007781-A |
| Thor | DFT-s-OFDM | {disabled,enabled} | {1} | N/A | {1} | {0} | CB008325-5G-BA |

In the whole overview subsection, the source of the figures are available on the link below:
 
This section provides functional block description for PUSCH signal processing divided in 3 parts :
(1) Front End Processing: first actions when signal is received.
\* in DU for CPRI and eCPRI 7.2a. In CPRI FE Processing includes CP removal and FFT operations. Refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_15158\_replaced([11101922](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kndKUvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
(2) Reference Signal Processing: mainly DMRS processing to achieve Channel Estimation.
\* in DU for CPRI and eCPRI 7.2a. Refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_15161\_replaced([11102011](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kndxY_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
\* in Radio for ecPRI 7.2e .
(3) Data Processing: actions to decode data once Channel Estimation is available.
\* in DU, for CPRI and eCPRI 7.2a.
\* for eCPRI 7.2e Data Processing is split in 2 parts:
\* (3a) in Radio: Data Equalization Processing,
\* (3b) in DU: Data Post-Equalization Processing
Refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_15163\_replaced([11102109](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kneYdPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), or to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_15164\_replaced([11102180](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kne_hvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for Transform Precoding.
 
CPRI
[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a flowchart illustrating a process within a "DU" (Data Unit). The flowchart consists of three main processing blocks arranged horizontally and labeled "Front End Processing (1)", "DMRS Processing (2)", and "DATA Processing (3)". These blocks are enclosed within a dashed rectangle representing the DU.
Arrows indicate the flow of data:
- An arrow goes from "Front End Processing (1)" to "DMRS Processing (2)".
- An arrow goes from "DMRS Processing (2)" to "DATA Processing (3)".
There are also external inputs and outputs:
- An arrow originates from "DMRS Processing (2)" and points to a block labeled "To L2 Long Term Average".
- An arrow originates from "DATA Processing (3)" and points to the same block "To L2 Long Term Average".
- An arrow originates from a block labeled "From L2 Long Term Average" and points to "DMRS Processing (2)".
- An arrow originates from the same block "From L2 Long Term Average" and points to "DATA Processing (3)".
The blocks "To L2 Long Term Average" and "From L2 Long Term Average" are shaded gray and are positioned to the right of the main processing blocks.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_1741.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]
 
eCPRI 7.2a
[image-1][image\_desc]The image is a flowchart illustrating a digital unit (DU) processing pipeline. The pipeline begins with a "CP Removal & FFT" block, which feeds into a "Front End Processing (1)" block. This block, along with the "CP Removal & FFT" block, is enclosed within a dashed rectangle labeled "DU".
From "Front End Processing (1)", the signal proceeds to a "DMRS Processing (2)" block. This block also receives input from a "From L2 Long Term Average" block. The output of "DMRS Processing (2)" then goes to a "DATA Processing (3)" block.
The "DATA Processing (3)" block receives input from "DMRS Processing (2)" and also from the "From L2 Long Term Average" block. The output of "DATA Processing (3)" is directed to a "To L2 Long Term Average" block. There is also a feedback loop from "DATA Processing (3)" back to "DMRS Processing (2)".[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_1741.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]
 
 
eCPRI 7.2e
[image-2][image\_desc]The image is a block diagram illustrating a signal processing flow within a radio system and a digital unit (DU). The diagram is divided into two main sections: "Radio" and "DU".
Within the "Radio" section, there are four processing blocks arranged sequentially:
1. **CP Removal & FFT**: This is the first block, receiving input from the left.
2. **Front End Processing (1)**: This block receives input from "CP Removal & FFT".
3. **DMRS Processing (2)**: This block receives input from "Front End Processing (1)". It also has a feedback loop from "DATA Equalization Processing (3a)".
4. **DATA Equalization Processing (3a)**: This block receives input from "DMRS Processing (2)". It outputs signals labeled "XIRC" and "βIRC" to the "DU" section. It also provides feedback to "DMRS Processing (2)".
The "DU" section contains one main processing block:
1. **DATA Post-Equalization Processing (3b)**: This block receives the "XIRC" and "βIRC" signals from the "DATA Equalization Processing (3a)" in the "Radio" section. It also receives input from a block labeled "From L2 Long Term Average".
There are two output blocks from the "DU" section:
1. **To L2 Long Term Average**: This block receives output from the "DATA Post-Equalization Processing (3b)".
2. **From L2 Long Term Average**: This block provides input to the "DATA Post-Equalization Processing (3b)".
The diagram uses arrows to indicate the flow of signals between the blocks. Dashed lines delineate the "Radio" and "DU" sections.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_1741.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2]
\* For Xirc IQ unnormalized data and normalization factor βIRC inputs to processing in DU refer to [Before CNI-112184-CC] 5G\_UP\_8063\_replaced([11380879](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_uepMv_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_7641\_replaced([11380423](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_uemwcvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) [CNI-112184-CC]  SRAN\_SiSo\_FH.2909\_replaced([10950352](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_3XbeDPuyEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and SRAN\_SiSo\_FH.2973\_replaced([10950177](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_3XWlkfuyEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) [End CNI-112184-CC]

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a flowchart illustrating a signal processing pipeline. The flowchart begins with a "CP removal" block, followed by an "FFT" block. A vertical line splits the flow, labeled "L1 High" above and "L1 Low" below. After the FFT block, the flow continues to an "RB demapping" block, then to a "TO compensation" block.
Following the "TO compensation" block, there is a larger rectangular box encompassing two sub-blocks. The upper sub-block is labeled "Frequency offset compensation (data+dmrs)". An arrow points from a gray rectangular box at the top, labeled "Long term CFO from L2", into this "Frequency offset compensation" block.
Inside the larger box, there are two smaller blocks connected by an arrow. The first is labeled "Intra symbol FOC + intersymbol FOC (+ upconversion CPRI only)". The second is labeled "ICI removal". The output of the "ICI removal" block is the final output of the flowchart.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_1744.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

Please refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8606\_replaced([11101890](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kncjTfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for the source link of the figure(s).

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a flowchart illustrating a signal processing chain. The flowchart begins with a block labeled "CP removal," followed by "FFT," and then "TO compensation." After "TO compensation," there is a block for "RB demapping."
To the right of "RB demapping," there is a larger rectangular box encompassing two sub-blocks. The top sub-block is labeled "Frequency offset compensation (data+dmrs)." Inside this box, there is another smaller rectangular box labeled "Intra symbol FOC + intersymbol FOC."
Above the "Frequency offset compensation" box, there is a gray rectangular box with the text "Long term CFO from L2." An arrow points from this gray box downwards to the "Frequency offset compensation" box.
Arrows indicate the flow of data between the blocks, moving from left to right.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_1746.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

ECPRI

[image-1][image\_desc]The image is a flowchart illustrating a signal processing pipeline. The flowchart is divided into two main vertical sections labeled "L1 High" and "L1 Low".
On the left side, under "L1 High", there are three rectangular boxes connected by arrows, representing sequential processing steps:
1. "CP removal"
2. "FFT"
3. "RB demapping"
A vertical line separates these initial steps from the right side of the flowchart.
On the right side, there is a larger rectangular box labeled "Frequency offset compensation (data+dmrs)". Inside this box, there is another nested rectangular box containing the text "Intra symbol FOC + intersymbol FOC".
Above the "Frequency offset compensation" box, there is a gray rectangular box with the text "Long term CFO from L2", with an arrow pointing down to the "Frequency offset compensation" box.
An arrow emerges from the "Frequency offset compensation" box and points to the right, indicating the output of this stage.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_1746.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]

[CB008360-A] For MU receiver :

[image-2][image\_desc]The image is a flowchart illustrating a signal processing pipeline. The flowchart begins with a "CP removal" block, followed by an "FFT" block. A vertical line separates these initial blocks from subsequent stages, with labels "L1 High" and "L1 Low" positioned above and below this line, respectively. After the "FFT" block, there is an "RB demapping" block. This block then feeds into a larger, nested rectangular block. The outer rectangle is labeled "Frequency offset compensation (data+dmrs)". Inside this, a smaller rectangle contains the text "(Intra symbol FOC + intersymbol FOC)" with the word "DISABLED" prominently displayed below it in red, wavy font. Finally, an arrow leads from the "Frequency offset compensation" block to a "TO compensation" block, which is the last stage in the depicted process.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_1746.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2]

[End CB008360-A]

Thor L1 HW front end processing:

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a flowchart illustrating a signal processing chain, likely within a telecommunications system. The flowchart depicts several processing blocks connected by arrows, indicating the flow of data.
Here's a breakdown of the elements:
**Left Side:**
\* **L1 High** and **L1 Low**: These labels suggest two different processing layers or paths.
\* **CP removal**: A block indicating the removal of a cyclic prefix.
\* **FFT**: A Fast Fourier Transform block.
\* **RB demapping**: A block for resource block demapping.
**Center:**
\* **Frequency offset compensation (dmrs)**: A large encompassing box that groups several frequency offset compensation (FOC) steps.
\* **data Inter-symbol & Intra-symbol FOC**: This block is labeled as "not performed in Front End".
\* **dmrs Inter-symbol FOC**: This block is labeled as "DISABLED in Product".
\* **dmrs Intra-symbol FOC**: This block is part of the frequency offset compensation.
\* **Long term CFO from L2**: A block above the "Frequency offset compensation (dmrs)" box, indicating a source of long-term carrier frequency offset (CFO) information from Layer 2. An arrow points from this block down into the frequency offset compensation section.
**Right Side:**
\* **TO compensation**: A block indicating time offset compensation. An arrow points from the "dmrs Intra-symbol FOC" block to this block.
The flowchart visually represents a process where a signal undergoes cyclic prefix removal, FFT, and RB demapping. Subsequently, frequency offset compensation is applied, with specific components for data and DMRS (Demodulation Reference Signal). The diagram also highlights that certain compensation steps are either not performed in the front end or are disabled in the product. Finally, time offset compensation is performed.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_1749.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

[CB009028-5G-A] For MU receiver :[image-1][image\_desc]The image is a flowchart illustrating a signal processing pipeline, likely within a telecommunications system. The flowchart depicts a sequence of operations performed on a signal, with distinct stages labeled.
On the left side, there are two vertical lines labeled "L1 High" and "L1 Low," suggesting different processing layers or signal paths.
The main processing flow starts with a block labeled "CP removal," followed by "FFT" (Fast Fourier Transform), and then "RB demapping." These operations are typically part of a receiver chain in a digital communication system.
Following "RB demapping," the signal enters a larger box titled "Frequency offset compensation (dmrs)." This box contains three sub-blocks, each representing a specific type of frequency offset compensation:
1. **data Inter-symbol & Intra-symbol FOC**: This block is marked with "not performed in Front End."
2. **dmrs Inter-symbol FOC**: This block is marked with "DISABLED in Product."
3. **dmrs Intra-symbol FOC**: This block is marked with "not performed in Front End."
Above the "Frequency offset compensation (dmrs)" box, there is a separate block labeled "Long term CFO from L2," with an arrow pointing down into the compensation section, indicating that long-term Carrier Frequency Offset (CFO) information is received from Layer 2 (L2) and likely used in the compensation process.
Finally, after the frequency offset compensation stage, the signal proceeds to a block labeled "TO compensation," which likely refers to Time Offset compensation, and then continues to the right, implying further processing.
The flowchart uses rectangular boxes to represent processing steps and arrows to indicate the flow of data or signals between these steps. The text within the boxes describes the operations and their status (e.g., performed, disabled, not performed).[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_1749.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]

[End CB009028-5G-A]

Note: Same for CPRI and eCPRI 7-2

Please refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8606\_replaced([11101890](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kncjTfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for the source link of the figure(s).

12687013:
Front End Processing
For eCPRI 7.2e and Thor L1 HW the Radio shall support the following processing in front end for:

* **[Before CB008235-X] NRBTS\NRCELL.numberOfRxBeamforming=4Rx (*) \*\*[End CB008235-X]***\*
* NRBTS\NRCELL.numberOfRxBeamforming = 16RX for either 8Rx or 16Rx Single User (SU) receiver order

**[Before CB008235-X] (*) Note: From E2E perspective with eCPRI 7-2e, NRBTS\NRCELL\numberOfRxBeamforming =4Rx is not supported. It is prohibited by PDL rule. \*\*[End CB008235-X]***\*

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a block diagram illustrating a signal processing chain, likely for a wireless communication system. The diagram shows a sequence of processing steps from left to right, with some branching and external inputs.
Here's a breakdown of the components:
1. **CP removal**: This block likely removes the Cyclic Prefix from the incoming signal.
2. **FFT**: This block performs a Fast Fourier Transform, converting the signal from the time domain to the frequency domain.
3. **Radio**: A vertical line labeled "Radio" separates the initial processing stages from subsequent ones, suggesting a transition point or interface.
4. **RB demapping**: This block likely de-maps the signal into Resource Blocks.
5. **L1 Low**: A label indicating a lower layer of the physical layer (Layer 1).
6. **Frequency offset compensation (dmrs)**: This is a larger encompassing block that contains three sub-blocks for frequency offset compensation, specifically related to Demodulation Reference Signals (DMRS).
\* **data Inter-symbol & Intra-symbol FOC**: This sub-block handles frequency offset compensation for data symbols, both inter-symbol and intra-symbol. It is noted as "not performed in Front End".
\* **dmrs Inter-symbol FOC**: This sub-block handles inter-symbol frequency offset compensation using DMRS. It is marked as "DISABLED in Product".
\* **dmrs Intra-symbol FOC**: This sub-block handles intra-symbol frequency offset compensation using DMRS.
7. **Long term CFO from L2**: An external input to the "Frequency offset compensation (dmrs)" block, indicating that long-term Carrier Frequency Offset (CFO) information is received from Layer 2.
8. **TO compensation**: This block performs Timing Offset compensation.
The diagram shows a flow where the signal undergoes CP removal, FFT, and RB demapping. Then, it enters a frequency offset compensation stage, which utilizes DMRS for compensation and also receives long-term CFO from a higher layer. Finally, the signal proceeds to Timing Offset compensation. The distinction between data and DMRS compensation, and the disabling of one of the DMRS compensation components in the product, are key details highlighted in the diagram.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_1754.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

For MU receiver

[image-1][image\_desc]The image is a flowchart illustrating a signal processing chain. The process begins with "CP removal," followed by "FFT," and then "RB demapping." A vertical line labeled "Radio" intersects the flow after "FFT," and another line labeled "L1 Low" extends downwards from the intersection.
The "RB demapping" output feeds into a larger block labeled "Frequency offset compensation (dmrs)." This block contains three sub-blocks:
1. "data Inter-symbol & Intra-symbol FOC" with the note "not performed in Front End" below it.
2. "dmrs Inter-symbol FOC" with the note "DISABLED in Product" below it.
3. "dmrs Intra-symbol FOC" with the note "not performed in Front End" below it.
An arrow points from a gray box labeled "Long term CFO from L2" to the "Frequency offset compensation (dmrs)" block, specifically above the "dmrs Inter-symbol FOC" sub-block.
Finally, the output of the "Frequency offset compensation (dmrs)" block leads to a block labeled "TO compensation."[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_1754.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]

Please refer to [11101890](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kncjTfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) for the source link of the figure(s).

Loner L1 HW Reference Signals processing

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a flowchart illustrating a signal processing procedure. The flowchart consists of several rectangular boxes representing different processing steps and arrows indicating the flow of data between them.
Here's a breakdown of the elements in the flowchart:
\* **Input Blocks:**
\* "DMRS REs from RE DEMAPPING"
\* "PTRS REs from RE DEMAPPING"
\* **Processing Blocks:**
\* "Pilot generation"
\* "Raw Channel Estimation"
\* "Layer separation"
\* "FO estimation (DMRS, PTRS)"
\* "Signal power estimation"
\* "Noise Power estimation"
\* "MMSE smoother selection"
\* "MMSE smoothing"
\* "Rank, PMI measurement"
\* "FD interpolation"
\* "Delay spread estimation"
\* "IFFT"
\* "TO estimation"
\* "DTX"
\* **Output/Intermediate Blocks:**
\* "To L2 Long Term Average" (appears twice, once connected to "FO estimation" and once to "TO estimation")
The flowchart starts with "DMRS REs from RE DEMAPPING" and "PTRS REs from RE DEMAPPING" as inputs. "Pilot generation" feeds into "Raw Channel Estimation". "DMRS REs from RE DEMAPPING" also feeds into "Raw Channel Estimation". The output of "Raw Channel Estimation" goes to "Layer separation".
"Layer separation" then leads to several parallel processing paths:
\* One path goes to "FO estimation (DMRS, PTRS)", which then connects to "To L2 Long Term Average".
\* Another path goes to "Signal power estimation", which then connects to "MMSE smoother selection".
\* A third path goes to "MMSE smoothing", which then connects to "FD interpolation".
"FO estimation (DMRS, PTRS)" also receives input from "PTRS REs from RE DEMAPPING".
"Signal power estimation" and "Noise Power estimation" both feed into "MMSE smoother selection".
"Noise Power estimation" also connects to "To L2 Long Term Average".
"MMSE smoother selection" leads to "Rank, PMI measurement".
"MMSE smoothing" receives input from "Layer separation" and "Delay spread estimation".
The "Raw Channel Estimation" output also feeds into "IFFT". The output of "IFFT" then splits into three paths:
\* "Delay spread estimation"
\* "TO estimation"
\* "DTX"
"Delay spread estimation" feeds into "MMSE smoothing".
"TO estimation" feeds into "To L2 Long Term Average".
Finally, "FD interpolation" appears to be an output or an intermediate step leading to further processing. The "To L2 Long Term Average" blocks suggest that certain estimated values are sent for long-term averaging at Layer 2.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_1760.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

Please refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8606\_replaced([11101890](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kncjTfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for the source link of the figure(s).

Reference Signals Processing
The DU shall support following Reference Signals **processing**:

* For CPRI and Loki L1 HW:

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a flowchart illustrating a signal processing chain. The flowchart begins with "DMRS, PTRS REs from RE DEMAPPING" and "Pilot Generation" feeding into "Raw Channel Estimation". The output of "Raw Channel Estimation" is split into two paths: one goes to "Layer separation" and the other to "iFFT". "Layer separation" leads to "Delay Spread estimation" and "DTX". "iFFT" also feeds into "DTX". "DTX" has an output labeled "TO DATA Processing".
Parallel to the "Raw Channel Estimation" path, there's a path starting with "Noise Power estimation" and "Signal Power estimation". Both of these receive input from "Raw Channel Estimation". "Noise Power estimation" and "Signal Power estimation" both feed into "MMSE Smoother Selection". "MMSE Smoother Selection" then feeds into "MMSE Smoothing".
"Delay Spread estimation" and "DTX" also feed into "MMSE Smoothing". The output of "MMSE Smoothing" is labeled "Hsmoothed, dmrs" and goes to "FD interpolation".
There's another input labeled "PTRS REs from DEMAPPING" which, along with "Hraw, ptrs", feeds into "FO Estimation (DMRS, PTRS)". The output of "TO phase derotation" also feeds into "FO Estimation". "TO phase derotation" receives input from "Rank, PMI measurement". "Rank, PMI measurement" receives input from "MMSE Smoother Selection".
The output of "FD interpolation" is labeled "TO DATA Processing". The output of "FO Estimation" is also labeled "TO DATA Processing".
Below the "DTX" block, there is a "Time Offset estimation" block which feeds into "DTX".
Finally, there's a "Noise + Interference Covariance Matrix calculation" block which receives input from "Time Offset estimation" and "MMSE Smoothing". This block also has an output labeled "TO DATA Processing".
There is a final block labeled "To L2 Long Term Average" which receives input from "FO Estimation" and "Noise + Interference Covariance Matrix calculation".[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_1764.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

* For eCPRI and Loki L1 HW:

[image-1][image\_desc]The image is a flowchart illustrating a signal processing chain. The flowchart consists of several rectangular boxes representing different processing steps, connected by arrows indicating the flow of data.
Here's a breakdown of the elements:
**Input/Starting Points:**
\* **DMRS, PTRS REs from RE DEMAPPING:** This is an input block, likely representing Demodulation Reference Signals (DMRS) and Physical Downlink Shared Channel Transmission Reference Signals (PTRS) extracted from Resource Elements (REs) after a demapping process.
\* **Pilot Generation:** This block suggests the generation of pilot signals, which are often used for channel estimation.
**Processing Blocks:**
\* **Raw Channel Estimation:** This block performs initial estimation of the channel.
\* **TO Compensation:** This likely refers to Time Offset Compensation.
\* **Layer separation:** This block separates different signal layers.
\* **iFFT:** This represents the Inverse Fast Fourier Transform, a common operation in communication systems.
\* **Noise Power estimation:** Estimates the noise power in the signal.
\* **Signal Power estimation:** Estimates the signal power.
\* **Delay Spread estimation:** Estimates the delay spread of the channel.
\* **DTX:** This could stand for Discontinuous Transmission or a similar concept related to signal activity.
\* **Time Offset estimation:** Estimates the time offset.
\* **MMSE Smoother Selection:** Selects an appropriate MMSE (Minimum Mean Square Error) smoother.
\* **MMSE Smoothing:** Applies MMSE smoothing to the signal.
\* **TO phase derotation:** Compensates for phase rotation caused by time offsets.
\* **Rank, PMI measurement:** Measures the rank and Precoding Matrix Indicator (PMI), which are important for MIMO (Multiple-Input Multiple-Output) systems. This block is highlighted with a shaded background.
\* **FD interpolation:** Likely refers to Frequency Domain interpolation.
\* **FO Estimation (DMRS, PTRS):** Estimates the Frequency Offset (FO) using DMRS and PTRS signals. This block also has a shaded background.
\* **PTRS REs from DEMAPPING:** Another input block, similar to the first one.
\* **Noise + Interference Covariance Matrix calculation:** Calculates the covariance matrix of noise and interference.
**Output/Destination Points:**
\* **To DATA Processing:** Several arrows point to this label, indicating that processed data is sent for further processing.
\* **To L2 Long Term Average:** This block, also highlighted with a shaded background, suggests that some processed information is sent for a Layer 2 (L2) long-term averaging function.
**Connections and Data Flow:**
The flowchart shows a complex interdependency between these blocks. For example:
\* Raw Channel Estimation is influenced by Pilot Generation.
\* TO Compensation is followed by iFFT.
\* Noise Power estimation and Signal Power estimation feed into MMSE Smoother Selection.
\* MMSE Smoothing receives input from MMSE Smoother Selection and potentially other sources.
\* TO phase derotation is influenced by TO Compensation and feeds into Rank, PMI measurement and FO Estimation.
\* Hsmoothed,dmrs is an output of MMSE Smoothing and an input to FD interpolation.
\* The output of FD interpolation is fed into FO Estimation.
\* The output of FO Estimation is sent to DATA Processing.
\* The output of Noise + Interference Covariance Matrix calculation is sent to To L2 Long Term Average.
The overall diagram depicts a sophisticated channel estimation and processing pipeline, likely within a wireless communication system.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_1764.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]

* For Thor **HW L1 HW**:

[image-2][image\_desc]The image is a flowchart illustrating a signal processing chain. The flowchart begins with "Pilot generation" which feeds into "Raw Channel Estimation". The output of "Raw Channel Estimation" is split into two paths: one goes to "Layer separation", and the other goes to "Noise Power estimation" and "Signal Power estimation".
The "Layer separation" output is further processed by "MMSE Smoother Selection" and "MMSE Smoothing". The "Noise Power estimation" and "Signal Power estimation" also feed into "MMSE Smoother Selection".
Another branch starts with "Delay Spread estimation" and "Time Offset estimation". The "Time Offset estimation" feeds into "DTX" and "TO phase derotation". The "DTX" output is labeled "To DATA Processing". The "Delay Spread estimation" output is fed into "MMSE Smoothing".
The "MMSE Smoothing" output is split into two paths: one goes to "FD interpolation" and the other is labeled "Hsmoothed,dmrs" which feeds into "TO phase derotation". The "FD interpolation" output is labeled "To DATA Processing".
The "TO phase derotation" output feeds into "Rank, PMI measurement" and "FO Estimation (DMRS, PTRS)". The "Rank, PMI measurement" is a shaded box. The "FO Estimation (DMRS, PTRS)" receives input from a dashed box labeled "PTRS REs from DEMAPPING" and also from "Hraw, ptrs". The output of "FO Estimation (DMRS, PTRS)" is labeled "To DATA Processing".
The "Time Offset estimation" also feeds into "Noise + Interference Covariance Matrix calculation". The output of this calculation is labeled "To DATA Processing".
Finally, there is a path from "MMSE Smoothing" and "Noise + Interference Covariance Matrix calculation" that leads to a shaded box labeled "To L2 Long Term Average".[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_1764.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2]

**[CB013204-A] The DU shall support following Reference Signals** processing[Before CB013204-A-UPD001] for Single User (SU) MIMO Receiver **[End** CB013204-A-UPD001] taking as input also the Noise Covariance Matrix processing:  
- when NRBTS\NRCELL\actOptPuschPrbAlloc = true , **- and** **only when PUSCH receiver programmed on 1 or 2 PRBs grant**- [CB013204-A-UPD002] and ***\*only when PUSCH receiver order is greater than 2RX* \*[End CB013204-A-UPD002]:*****\*******\****\*\*

* for CPRI and Loki L1 HW

[image-3][image\_desc]The image is a flowchart illustrating a signal processing chain. The flowchart consists of several rectangular boxes representing processing steps and arrows indicating the flow of data between these steps.
Here's a breakdown of the components:
**Input/Initial Stages:**
\* **DMRS, PTRS REs from RE DEMAPPING:** This is an input block, likely representing Demodulation Reference Signals (DMRS) and Physical Downlink Shared Channel Transmission Reference Signals (PTRS) extracted from Resource Elements (REs) after demapping.
\* **Raw Channel Estimation:** This block takes input from the "DMRS, PTRS REs from RE DEMAPPING" block and likely performs initial channel estimation.
\* **Pilot Generation:** This block generates pilot signals, which are used for channel estimation. It feeds into the "Raw Channel Estimation" block.
**Intermediate Processing Stages:**
\* **Layer separation:** This block takes output from "Raw Channel Estimation" and separates different data layers.
\* **iFFT:** This block performs an Inverse Fast Fourier Transform, a common operation in communication systems. It receives input from "Layer separation".
\* **Noise Power estimation:** This block estimates the noise power in the signal. It receives input from the "DMRS, PTRS REs from RE DEMAPPING" block.
\* **Signal Power estimation:** This block estimates the signal power. It receives input from the "Noise Power estimation" block.
\* **Delay Spread estimation:** This block estimates the delay spread of the channel. It receives input from "Raw Channel Estimation".
\* **DTX (Discontinuous Transmission):** This block likely handles discontinuous transmission detection. It receives input from "Raw Channel Estimation" and "Time Offset estimation".
\* **Time Offset estimation:** This block estimates the time offset in the signal. It receives input from "Raw Channel Estimation".
**Core Processing and Refinement:**
\* **MMSE Smoother Selection:** This block selects an appropriate MMSE (Minimum Mean Square Error) smoother. It receives input from "Noise Power estimation" and "Signal Power estimation".
\* **MMSE Smoothing:** This block performs MMSE smoothing on the channel estimate. It receives input from "MMSE Smoother Selection" and "Layer separation".
\* **TO phase derotation:** This block performs phase derotation based on time offset. It receives input from "MMSE Smoothing".
\* **Rank, PMI measurement:** This is a shaded block, indicating a key measurement or calculation. It receives input from "TO phase derotation".
\* **FD interpolation:** This block performs frequency domain interpolation. It receives input from "TO phase derotation" and is labeled with "Hsmoothed, dmrs" as input.
\* **FO Estimation (DMRS, PTRS):** This block estimates frequency offset using DMRS and PTRS. It receives input labeled "Hraw, ptrs" from a dashed box labeled "PTRS REs from DEMAPPING".
**Output and Averaging:**
\* **To DATA Processing:** Multiple blocks have outputs labeled "To DATA Processing", indicating that processed data is sent for further processing. These include "DTX", "MMSE Smoothing", and "FD interpolation".
\* **Noise + Interference Covariance Matrix calculation:** This block calculates the noise and interference covariance matrix. It receives input from "DTX" and "Time Offset estimation".
\* **To L2 Long Term Average:** This is a shaded block, indicating a final stage of averaging or aggregation. It receives input from "Noise + Interference Covariance Matrix calculation".
The flowchart depicts a complex channel estimation and processing pipeline, likely for a wireless communication system, involving various estimation, smoothing, and correction techniques.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_1764.004.png[/image\_path][/image-3]

* for eCPRI and Loki L1 HW:

[image-4][image\_desc]The image is a flowchart illustrating a signal processing chain. The flowchart begins with an input labeled "DMRS, PTRS REs from RE DEMAPPING" feeding into "Raw Channel Estimation". A "Pilot Generation" block also feeds into "Raw Channel Estimation".
From "Raw Channel Estimation", the signal proceeds to "TO Compensation". This block then feeds into "Layer separation".
In parallel, "Noise Power estimation" and "Signal Power estimation" blocks are shown. Both of these, along with the output of "Raw Channel Estimation", feed into "MMSE Smoother Selection". The output of "MMSE Smoother Selection" then goes to "MMSE Smoothing".
The "TO Compensation" block also has an output that goes to an "iFFT" block. The output of "iFFT" is not explicitly shown as going anywhere, but it is implied to be part of the processing chain.
Another branch of the flowchart includes "Delay Spread estimation" and "DTX" (likely Discontinuous Transmission). "Delay Spread estimation" receives input from "Raw Channel Estimation". "DTX" receives input from "Delay Spread estimation" and "Time Offset estimation". "Time Offset estimation" receives input from the main signal path after "Raw Channel Estimation". The output of "DTX" is labeled "To DATA Processing".
The output of "MMSE Smoothing" is labeled "Hsmoothed,dmrs" and feeds into "FD interpolation". The output of "FD interpolation" is also labeled "To DATA Processing".
A separate input labeled "PTRS REs from DEMAPPING" with an associated input "Hraw, ptrs" feeds into "FO Estimation (DMRS, PTRS)". The output of "TO phase derotation" also feeds into "FO Estimation (DMRS, PTRS)". "TO phase derotation" receives input from "MMSE Smoother Selection".
The "Rank, PMI measurement" block receives input from "TO phase derotation" and is shaded in gray.
The output of "FO Estimation (DMRS, PTRS)" is split, with one part going to "To DATA Processing" and another part going to "To L2 Long Term Average".
The "Noise + Interference Covariance Matrix calculation" block receives input from "Time Offset estimation" and "DTX". Its output is labeled "To DATA Processing".
Finally, the "To L2 Long Term Average" block receives input from "FO Estimation (DMRS, PTRS)" and is also shaded in gray.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_1764.005.png[/image\_path][/image-4]

* for Thor L1 HW:

[image-5][image\_desc]The image is a flowchart illustrating a signal processing chain. The flowchart begins with "Pilot generation" and "Raw Channel Estimation". The output of "Raw Channel Estimation" is fed into "Layer separation".
Several parallel processing paths originate from or are influenced by the initial stages. "Noise Power estimation" and "Signal Power estimation" are two such paths, both receiving input from "Raw Channel Estimation". The outputs of these two estimations feed into "MMSE Smoother Selection".
Another branch involves "Delay Spread estimation" and "Time Offset estimation". The output of "Time Offset estimation" feeds into "DTX" and "TO phase derotation". "DTX" also receives input from "Delay Spread estimation" and has an output labeled "To DATA Processing".
The "MMSE Smoother Selection" output feeds into "MMSE Smoothing". The output of "MMSE Smoothing" is labeled "Hsmoothed,dmrs" and is fed into "FD interpolation". The output of "FD interpolation" is also labeled "To DATA Processing".
The "TO phase derotation" output is fed into "Rank, PMI measurement" and "FO Estimation (DMRS, PTRS)". The "Rank, PMI measurement" block has a shaded background. The "FO Estimation (DMRS, PTRS)" block receives an additional input from a dashed box labeled "PTRS REs from DEMAPPING" and is labeled with "Hraw, ptrs". The output of "FO Estimation" is also labeled "To DATA Processing".
The "Time Offset estimation" output also contributes to "Noise + Interference Covariance Matrix calculation". The "Noise + Interference Covariance Matrix calculation" block receives input from "DTX" and has an output labeled "To DATA Processing".
Finally, there is a block labeled "To L2 Long Term Average" which receives input from "Noise + Interference Covariance Matrix calculation" and "FO Estimation (DMRS, PTRS)". This block also has a shaded background.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_1764.006.png[/image\_path][/image-5]

**[End CB013204-A]**

Please refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8606\_replaced([11101890](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kncjTfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for the source link of the figure(s).
