Reference Signals Processing
For eCPRI 7.2e and Thor L1 HW, the Radio shall support the following Reference Signals **processing with:**

* **[Before CB008235-X] NRBTS\NRCELL\numberOfRxBeamforming =4Rx (*) \*\*[End CB008235-X]***\*
* NRBTS\NRCELL\numberOfRxBeamforming = 16RX: either for 8Rx, or for 16Rx Single User (SU) MIMO receiver order
* NRBTS\NRCELL\numberOfRxBeamforming=16RX and NRBTS\NRCELL\NRULMUMIMO\ulMaxMuMimoLayer **= 8** for Multi User (MU) MIMO receiver on own user's beams. For not own user’s beams refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_27757\_replaced([11102087](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kndxbvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).\*\*\*\*

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a flowchart illustrating a signal processing chain. The flowchart begins with "Pilot generation" and "Raw Channel Estimation". The output of "Raw Channel Estimation" is fed into "Layer separation".
Several parallel processing paths originate from or are influenced by the initial stages:
\* **Noise and Signal Power Estimation:** "Noise Power estimation" and "Signal Power estimation" blocks are present. Their outputs feed into "MMSE Smoother Selection".
\* **MMSE Smoothing:** The output of "Layer separation" and "Signal Power estimation" are inputs to "MMSE Smoother Selection". The output of "MMSE Smoother Selection" is then processed by "MMSE Smoothing".
\* **Delay Spread and Time Offset:** "Delay Spread estimation" and "Time Offset estimation" blocks are also shown.
\* **DTX and iFFT:** "DTX" receives input from "Delay Spread estimation" and outputs to "To DATA Processing". "iFFT" receives input from "Raw Channel Estimation".
\* **Frequency Domain Processing:** "TO phase derotation" receives input from "MMSE Smoothing" and "Rank, PMI measurement". "FD interpolation" receives input from "MMSE Smoothing" and outputs to "TO phase derotation". "FO Estimation" receives input from "TO phase derotation" and outputs to "To DATA Processing".
\* **Noise + Interference Covariance Matrix Calculation:** This block receives input from "MMSE Smoothing" and "Time Offset estimation", and outputs to "To DATA Processing".
\* **To L2 Long Term Average:** This block receives input from "FO Estimation" and "Noise + Interference Covariance Matrix calculation".
The flowchart also indicates data flow to "To DATA Processing" from "DTX", "FD interpolation", and "Noise + Interference Covariance Matrix calculation". A "Rank, PMI measurement" block is also present, receiving input from "TO phase derotation".
The blocks are arranged in a hierarchical and interconnected manner, suggesting a complex signal processing algorithm. Some blocks are shaded grey, possibly indicating specific functionalities or stages.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_1768.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

Note: Radio shall always perform additional **Time Domain "Layer Separation" for 'double-symbol' (refer to [11101692](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knauFPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) front-loaded DM-RS.**

**[Before CB008235-X]  (*) Note: \*\*From E2E perspective with eCPRI 7-2e, NRBTS\NRCELL\numberOfRxBeamforming =4Rx is not supported. It is prohibited by PDL rule.****[End CB008235-X]*\*\*\*

For eCPRI 7.2e and Thor L1 HW, the Radio shall support the following Reference Signals **processing**with DTX step processing taking as input also the Noise Covariance Matrix processing when:

* NRBTS\NRCELL.numberOfRxBeamforming = 16RX: either for 8Rx, or for 16Rx receiver order,
* and with NRBTS\NRCELL\actOptPuschPrbAlloc = true,
* and **only when PUSCH programmed on 1 or 2 PRBs.**

[image-1][image\_desc]The image is a flowchart illustrating a signal processing chain. The flowchart begins with "Pilot generation" and "Raw Channel Estimation". The output of "Raw Channel Estimation" is fed into "Layer separation".
Several parallel processing paths originate from different stages. "Noise Power estimation" and "Signal Power estimation" feed into "MMSE Smoother Selection". "Raw Channel Estimation" also feeds into "iFFT".
The "Layer separation" output is split and fed into "MMSE Smoothing" and "Delay Spread estimation". The "iFFT" output is also fed into "MMSE Smoothing".
The "MMSE Smoother Selection" output goes to "TO phase derotation". The "MMSE Smoothing" output is labeled "Hsmoothed,dmrs" and goes to "FD interpolation".
"Delay Spread estimation" and "DTX" are inputs to "Time Offset estimation". "DTX" also has an output labeled "To DATA Processing". "Time Offset estimation" also has an output going to "Noise + Interference Covariance Matrix calculation".
The output of "FD interpolation" goes to "TO phase derotation" and also has an output labeled "To DATA Processing". The output of "TO phase derotation" goes to "FO Estimation" and "Rank, PMI measurement". "FO Estimation" has an output that goes to "To L2 Long Term Average".
The "Noise + Interference Covariance Matrix calculation" has an output labeled "To DATA Processing" and another output that goes to "To L2 Long Term Average".
The flowchart depicts a complex system with multiple interconnected modules for channel estimation, smoothing, interpolation, and estimation of various parameters like time offset, frequency offset, and noise/interference.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_1768.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]

Please refer to [11101890](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kncjTfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) for the source link of the figure(s).

Note: For eCPRI 7-2e, PTRS based FOE is not in scope in Radio(L1).

Reference Signals Processing
11102092:
Reference Signals Processing
11102092:
Reference Signals Processing
11102092:
Reference Signals Processing
11102092:
Reference Signals Processing
For eCPRI 7.2e and Thor L1 HW, the Radio shall support the following Reference Signals processing with:

* [CB008235-B] NRBTS\NRCELL.numberOfRxBeamforming=16RX and NRBTS\NRCELL\NRULMUMIMO\ulMaxMuMimoLayer = 8 for Multi User (MU) MIMO receiver for not own user's beams. For own user’s beams refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26192\_replaced([11102062](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kndxavvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). [End CB008235-B]

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a flowchart illustrating a signal processing chain. The flowchart begins with "Pilot generation" and "Raw Channel Estimation". The output of "Raw Channel Estimation" is fed into "Layer separation".
Parallel to "Raw Channel Estimation", there are three blocks: "Noise Power estimation", "Signal Power estimation", and "FD-PDP estimation". The outputs of these three blocks are fed into "MMSE Smoother Selection".
The output of "Layer separation" is fed into "MMSE Smoothing". The output of "MMSE Smoother Selection" also feeds into "MMSE Smoothing".
The output of "MMSE Smoothing" is labeled "Hsmoothed,dmrs" and is fed into "FD interpolation". The output of "FD interpolation" is fed into "TO phase derotation".
The output of "TO phase derotation" is fed into "FO Estimation" and also leads to "To DATA Processing".
Additionally, there is a block labeled "Rank, PMI measurement" which receives input from "MMSE Smoother Selection".
The output of "MMSE Smoothing" also feeds into "Noise + Interference Covariance Matrix calculation", which then leads to "To DATA Processing".
The overall flowchart depicts a process that involves channel estimation, noise and signal power estimation, smoothing, interpolation, phase derotation, and frequency offset estimation, ultimately leading to data processing.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_1770.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

Please refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8606\_replaced([11101890](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kncjTfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for the source link of the figure(s).

Note: For eCPRI 7-2e, PTRS based FOE is not in scope in Radio(L1).

Note: Radio shall always perform additional Time Domain "Layer Separation" for 'double-symbol' (refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_792\_replaced([11101692](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knauFPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) front-loaded DM-RS.

[Before CNI-103796-M] vDU L1 HW Reference Signals processing

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a flowchart illustrating a signal processing procedure. The flowchart consists of several rectangular boxes representing different processing steps and arrows indicating the flow of data between them.
Here's a breakdown of the elements in the flowchart:
\* **Long term CFO from L2**: This is an input block, likely representing frequency offset information from a higher layer (L2).
\* **Frequency offset compensation (dmrs)**: This block takes input from "Long term CFO from L2" and "DMRS REs from RE DEMAPPING". It performs frequency offset compensation using Demodulation Reference Signals (DMRS).
\* **DMRS REs from RE DEMAPPING**: This is another input block, providing DMRS Resource Elements (REs) from a re-demapping process.
\* **TO shifted Pilot generation**: This block generates time-offset shifted pilots.
\* **Raw Channel Estimation**: This block receives input from "TO shifted Pilot generation" and "Frequency offset compensation (dmrs)". It performs initial channel estimation.
\* **Layer separation**: This block takes the output of "Raw Channel Estimation" and separates different layers of the signal.
\* **FO estimation (DMRS)**: This block estimates frequency offset using DMRS. It also has an output going to "To L2 Long Term Average".
\* **To L2 Long Term Average**: This is an output block, likely for long-term averaging of frequency offset information.
\* **Modulation**: This is an input block, likely representing the modulation scheme used.
\* **MMSE smoother selection**: This block takes input from "Modulation" and "Layer separation". It selects an appropriate MMSE (Minimum Mean Square Error) smoother.
\* **MMSE smoothing**: This block takes the output of "MMSE smoother selection" and performs MMSE smoothing on the signal.
\* **Noise Power estimation**: This block estimates the noise power. It receives input from "MMSE smoothing".
\* **Signal power estimation**: This block estimates the signal power. It receives input from "MMSE smoothing".
\* **FD interpolation (revert TO compensation)**: This block takes the output of "MMSE smoothing" and performs frequency-domain interpolation, reverting the time offset compensation.
\* **TO estimation**: This block estimates the time offset. It receives input from "Raw Channel Estimation".
The arrows show the following data flow:
\* "Long term CFO from L2" -> "Frequency offset compensation (dmrs)"
\* "DMRS REs from RE DEMAPPING" -> "Frequency offset compensation (dmrs)"
\* "TO shifted Pilot generation" -> "Raw Channel Estimation"
\* "Frequency offset compensation (dmrs)" -> "Raw Channel Estimation"
\* "Raw Channel Estimation" -> "Layer separation"
\* "Raw Channel Estimation" -> "TO estimation"
\* "Layer separation" -> "FO estimation (DMRS)"
\* "Layer separation" -> "MMSE smoother selection"
\* "FO estimation (DMRS)" -> "To L2 Long Term Average"
\* "Modulation" -> "MMSE smoother selection"
\* "MMSE smoother selection" -> "MMSE smoothing"
\* "MMSE smoothing" -> "FD interpolation (revert TO compensation)"
\* "MMSE smoothing" -> "Noise Power estimation"
\* "MMSE smoothing" -> "Signal power estimation"[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_1773.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

Please refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8606\_replaced([11101890](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kncjTfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for the source link of the figure(s). [CNI-103796-M] vDU L1 HW is no more supported [End CNI-103796-M]

GPU L1 HW Reference Signals processing

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a flowchart illustrating a signal processing chain. The flowchart begins with a block labeled "Long term CFO from L2" which feeds into a block labeled "TOC & FOC (DMRS)". Another input to "TOC & FOC (DMRS)" comes from a block labeled "DMRS REs from RE DEMAPPING".
From "TOC & FOC (DMRS)", an arrow leads to "Raw Channel Estimation". Simultaneously, an arrow from "TOC & FOC (DMRS)" also leads to a block labeled "TOE & FOE (DMRS)".
The output of "Raw Channel Estimation" feeds into "Layer separation". The output of "Layer separation" then feeds into "MMSE Smoothing".
The "TOE & FOE (DMRS)" block has an output arrow that goes to a block labeled "To L2 Long Term Average". It also has an arrow that feeds into "MMSE smoother selection".
The output of "MMSE smoother selection" feeds into "MMSE Smoothing".
The "MMSE Smoothing" block has three output arrows. One arrow leads to "FD interpolation (revert TO compensation)". Another arrow leads to "Noise Power estimation". The third arrow leads to "Signal Power estimation".
The overall flowchart depicts a process that involves channel estimation, layer separation, MMSE smoothing, and various estimations like frequency offset compensation, noise power, and signal power, with some outputs being sent to higher layers (L2).[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/img-02252232.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

Please refer to [11101890](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kncjTfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) for the source link of the figure(s).

L1 Loner HW data processing

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a flowchart illustrating a data processing pipeline for a "Loner" system. The flowchart begins with a decision point "Is msg 3". If the answer is "yes", the process goes to "PNC (based on DMRS)". If the answer is "no", it proceeds to a diamond shape labeled "5G\_UP\_ALG\_925".
From the "Is msg 3" "yes" path, there's a connection to "PNC (based on DMRS)". This block also receives input from a path labeled "(c)" originating from the "5G\_UP\_ALG\_925" diamond.
The "5G\_UP\_ALG\_925" diamond has two outgoing paths: "(c)" and "(a)". Path "(c)" leads to "PNC (based on DMRS)". Path "(a)" leads to "EQUALIZATION".
There are also blocks labeled "PTRS" and "PNE" which feed into a "PNC" block. This "PNC" block receives input from path "(b)" originating from the "5G\_UP\_ALG\_925" diamond. The output of this "PNC" block also feeds into "EQUALIZATION".
The "EQUALIZATION" block receives input from path "(a)" and the "PNC" block (fed by PTRS, PNE, and path (b)). It also receives input from a block labeled "DATA REs (from RE DEMAPPING + FOC)". The output of "EQUALIZATION" is an arrow pointing to the right, indicating the final output of this stage.
Below the "Is msg 3" decision, there's a box labeled "Channel estimates from DATA symbols". This box contains two sub-boxes: "FD interpolation (from DMRS + FOC)" and "Time Domain interpolation". An arrow connects "FD interpolation" to "Time Domain interpolation". An arrow also points from "Time Domain interpolation" to the "Is msg 3" decision block, suggesting it provides input for that decision.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_1776.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

Please refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8606\_replaced([11101890](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kncjTfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for the source link of the figure(s).

L1 Loki HW data processing

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a flowchart illustrating a data processing pipeline labeled "Data Processing Loki". The flowchart uses rectangular boxes for processes and diamond shapes for decision points. Arrows indicate the flow of data or control.
Here's a breakdown of the elements:
\* **Title:** "Data Processing Loki" is displayed at the top left.
\* **Decision Point 1:** A diamond shape labeled "Is msg 3" has two outgoing paths: "yes" and "no".
\* The "yes" path leads to a process box labeled "PNC (based on DMRS)".
\* The "no" path leads to another diamond shape.
\* **Process Boxes connected to Decision Point 1:**
\* "PNC (based on DMRS)" receives input from the "yes" branch of "Is msg 3".
\* **Decision Point 2:** A diamond shape labeled "5G\_UP\_ALG\_927, 5G\_UP\_ALG\_929" receives input from the "no" branch of "Is msg 3" and is also connected via a line labeled "(c)" to a process box. It has three outgoing paths labeled "(a)", "(b)", and "(c)".
\* The path labeled "(c)" leads to a process box.
\* The path labeled "(b)" leads to a process box.
\* The path labeled "(a)" leads to a process box.
\* **Process Boxes connected to Decision Point 2:**
\* A process box labeled "PTRS" has an arrow pointing to a process box labeled "PNE".
\* The "PNE" box has an arrow pointing to a process box labeled "PNC (based on PTRS)".
\* The "PNC (based on PTRS)" box receives input from the "(b)" path of Decision Point 2.
\* The "PNC (based on DMRS)" box also has an arrow pointing to the "EQUALIZATION" box.
\* The "PNC (based on PTRS)" box also has an arrow pointing to the "EQUALIZATION" box.
\* **Process Box:** "EQUALIZATION" receives input from both "PNC (based on DMRS)" and "PNC (based on PTRS)", as well as from a box labeled "DATA REs (from RE DEMAPPING + FOC)". It also has an outgoing arrow indicating further processing.
\* **Process Box:** "DATA REs (from RE DEMAPPING + FOC)" is connected to the "EQUALIZATION" box.
\* **Process Box:** "PNC (based on LT FOE)" receives input from the "(d)" path of Decision Point 2 and has an input from a gray box labeled "LT FOE".
\* **Input Block:** A larger rectangular block labeled "Channel estimates from DATA symbols" contains two smaller boxes:
\* "FD interpolation (from DMRS + FOC)"
\* "Time Domain interpolation"
An arrow connects "FD interpolation (from DMRS + FOC)" to "Time Domain interpolation". An arrow also points from "Time Domain interpolation" to the "EQUALIZATION" box.
\* **Labels:** Various labels like "(a)", "(b)", "(c)", "(d)", "yes", "no" are used to denote connections and decision outcomes.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_1778.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

NOTE: PNC based on LT FOE (d) is performed at the Equalization step

[CB008360-A]

The figure above applies for MU receiver with the following update:

* only inter-FOC is performed

For both SU receiver and MU receiver,

* only DMRS based PNC is performed (as PTRS shall be disabled in NIDD when UL MU MIMO is enabled).

[End CB008360-A]

Please refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8606\_replaced([11101890](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kncjTfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for the source link of the figure(s).

L1 Thor HW HW data processing :

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a flowchart illustrating a process within a 5G communication system. The flowchart begins with a decision point labeled "Is msg 3". If the answer is "yes", the flow goes upwards to a process labeled "PNC (based on DMRS)". If the answer is "no", the flow moves to the right to a diamond shape containing "5G\_UP\_ALG\_927, 5G\_UP\_ALG\_929".
From the "Is msg 3" decision, there is also a path leading to "Channel estimates from DATA symbols", which is a rectangular box containing two sub-processes: "FD interpolation (from DMRS + FOC)" and "Time Domain Interpolation".
The diamond shape "5G\_UP\_ALG\_927, 5G\_UP\_ALG\_929" has three outgoing paths labeled (a), (b), and (c).
Path (c) leads to "PNC (based on DMRS)".
Path (b) leads to "PNC (based on PTRS)". Before reaching this, there are two intermediate boxes labeled "PTRS" and "PNE" which feed into "PNC (based on PTRS)".
Path (a) leads to "EQUALIZATION".
There is also a path labeled (d) originating from the "5G\_UP\_ALG\_927, 5G\_UP\_ALG\_929" diamond, which goes to "PNC (based on LT FOE)". This process is fed by a box labeled "LT FOE".
The "EQUALIZATION" process receives input from "PNC (based on PTRS)" and "DATA REs (from RE DEMAPPING + FOC)". The output of "EQUALIZATION" goes to "DATA Intra-FOC & POST-Equalization Processing".
The "PNC (based on DMRS)" process also has an output that feeds into "EQUALIZATION".
In summary, the flowchart depicts a system that uses different algorithms and data sources (DMRS, PTRS, LT FOE, DATA symbols) to perform channel estimation, PNC (Physical Network Control), and equalization, ultimately leading to data processing.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_1781.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

In the figure above, related to FOC :

* only inter-FOC is performed at equalization
* intra-FOC is performed after equalization
* PNC based on LT FOE (d) is performed at the Equalization step

[CB009028-5G-A]

The figure above applies for MU receiver with the following update:

for both SU receiver and MU receiver,

only DMRS based PNC is performed (as PTRS shall be disabled in NIDD when UL MU MIMO is enabled).

[End CB009028-5G-A]

Please refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8606\_replaced([11101890](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kncjTfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for the source link of the figure(s).

###### 2.4.2.4.1.3.0-4 Data Processing Data Processing for Thor Radio 7-2e L1 HW (ID: 11102144)

**2.4.2.4.1.3.0-4.0-6**  (ID: `13485785`)

Data Processing
13485785:
Data Processing
13485785:
Data Processing
Radio for Thor 7-2e L1**HW shall:**

* perform DATA processing as below depicted when NRBTS\NRCELL.numberOfRxBeamforming = 16RX for either 8Rx or 16Rx Single User (SU) receiver order
* **[CB016354-A] support referred operations and provide related processing outputs to DU for either Thor L1HW (refer to [11102129](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kneYefvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) or GPU L1HW (refer to [13335729](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_KeMZ4Nd1EfCkssdSu47EKw?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) for corresponding DATA Post-Equalization processing steps.**[End CB016354-A]\*\*\*\*

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a flowchart illustrating a signal processing procedure within a communication system, likely a 5G system, as indicated by the "5G\_UP\_ALG\_927, 5G\_UP\_ALG\_929" block. The flowchart is divided into two main sections, "DU" (likely Distributed Unit) and "L1 Radio".
The process begins with a decision block "Is msg 3". If the answer is "yes", the signal proceeds to a block labeled "PNC (based on DMRS)". If the answer is "no", it goes to a diamond shape containing "5G\_UP\_ALG\_927, 5G\_UP\_ALG\_929".
There is a separate path for "Channel estimates from DATA symbols" which involves "FD interpolation (from DMRS + FOC)" and "Time Domain interpolation".
From the "5G\_UP\_ALG\_927, 5G\_UP\_ALG\_929" block, there are two outgoing paths:
1. Path (c) leads to the "PNC (based on DMRS)" block.
2. Path (a) leads to an "EQUALIZATION" block.
There is also a block labeled "PNC (based on LT FOE)" which receives input from "LT FOE" and also from path (d) originating from the "5G\_UP\_ALG\_927, 5G\_UP\_ALG\_929" block. The output of "PNC (based on LT FOE)" also feeds into the "EQUALIZATION" block.
The "EQUALIZATION" block receives input from "PNC (based on DMRS)" and "PNC (based on LT FOE)". It also receives input from a block labeled "DATA REs (from RE DEMAPPING + inter FOC)". The output of the "EQUALIZATION" block is labeled "XIRC" and "BIRC".
Finally, "XIRC" and "BIRC" are fed into a block labeled "DATA Intra-FOC & POST-Equalization Processing", which is located in the "L1 Radio" section.
The flowchart uses standard flowchart symbols: rectangles for processes, diamonds for decisions, and arrows to indicate the flow of data or control. The text within the blocks describes specific signal processing steps and algorithms.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_1786.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

In the figure above, related to FOC :

* only inter-FOC is performed at equalization in Radio
* intra-FOC is performed in DU
* PNC based on LT FOE (d) is performed at the Equalization step

For Xirc IQ unnormalized data and normalization factor βIRC inputs to processing in DU refer to SRAN\_SiSo\_FH.2909\_replaced([10950352](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_3XbeDPuyEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and SRAN\_SiSo\_FH.2973\_replaced([10950177](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_3XWlkfuyEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Please refer to [11101890](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kncjTfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) for the source link of the figure(s).

[Before CNI-103796-M] L1 vDU HW data processing

FOC is moved before equalization.

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a flowchart illustrating a signal processing chain. The flowchart consists of several rectangular boxes representing processes and arrows indicating the flow of data.
Here's a breakdown of the elements:
1. **"Long term CFO from L2"**: This is a process or input located at the top of the flowchart, indicated by a gray rectangular box. An arrow points downwards from it.
2. **"DATA REs from RE DEMAPPING"**: This is an input or process on the left side of the flowchart, represented by a rectangular box. An arrow points from this box to the "Frequency offset compensation (data)" box.
3. **"Frequency offset compensation (data)"**: This process is located in the upper middle section of the flowchart. It receives input from "Long term CFO from L2" and "DATA REs from RE DEMAPPING". An arrow points from this box to the "EQUALIZATION" box.
4. **"EQUALIZATION"**: This process is located in the upper right section of the flowchart. It receives input from "Frequency offset compensation (data)". An arrow points from this box to the right, and another arrow points downwards to the "DTX" box.
5. **"Channel estimates from DATA symbols"**: This is a larger rectangular box encompassing two sub-processes, located in the lower middle section of the flowchart.
\* **"FD interpolation (from DMRS + FOC)"**: This sub-process is on the left within the "Channel estimates from DATA symbols" box. An arrow points from this sub-process to the "Time Domain interpolation" sub-process.
\* **"Time Domain interpolation"**: This sub-process is on the right within the "Channel estimates from DATA symbols" box. An arrow points from this sub-process to the "EQUALIZATION" box.
6. **"DTX"**: This is an output or process located at the bottom right of the flowchart. It receives input from the "EQUALIZATION" box.
7. **"Post Combined SNR"**: This is a label associated with the output from the "EQUALIZATION" box, indicated by an arrow pointing downwards towards "DTX" and then a label "Post Combined SNR" to the right of this arrow.
The flowchart depicts a process where data symbols are demapped, undergo frequency offset compensation, and then equalization. Channel estimates are derived from data symbols through frequency domain and time domain interpolation, and these estimates are used to aid the equalization process. The output of the equalization is further processed or measured for "Post Combined SNR" and potentially leads to "DTX".[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_1789.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

Please refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8606\_replaced([11101890](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kncjTfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for the source link of the figure(s).  [CNI-103796-M] vDU L1 HW is no more supported [End CNI-103796-M]

For Data processing GPU L1 HW shall perform FOC before Equalization step.

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a flowchart illustrating a signal processing chain. The flowchart consists of several rectangular boxes representing processes and arrows indicating the flow of data.
Here's a breakdown of the elements:
1. **"Long term CFO from L2"**: A grey rectangular box at the top, indicating a source of long-term Carrier Frequency Offset (CFO) information from Layer 2. An arrow points downwards from this box.
2. **"DATA REs from RE DEMAPPING"**: A rectangular box on the left, representing data Resource Elements (REs) obtained from Resource Element (RE) demapping. An arrow points to the right from this box.
3. **"Frequency Offset Compensation (DATA)"**: A rectangular box in the center-left, receiving input from "DATA REs from RE DEMAPPING" and "Long term CFO from L2". This process compensates for frequency offset in the data. An arrow points to the right from this box.
4. **"Channel Estimates from DATA symbols"**: A larger rectangular box at the bottom, encompassing two sub-processes. An arrow points upwards from this box to the "EQUALIZATION" process.
\* **"FD interpolation (from DMRS) + FOC"**: A rectangular box within "Channel Estimates from DATA symbols", on the left. It indicates Frequency Domain (FD) interpolation using Demodulation Reference Signals (DMRS) and Frequency Offset Compensation (FOC). An arrow points to the right from this box.
\* **"Time Domain Interpolation (Virtual DMRS based)"**: A rectangular box within "Channel Estimates from DATA symbols", on the right. It indicates time domain interpolation based on virtual DMRS.
5. **"EQUALIZATION"**: A rectangular box in the center-right, receiving input from "Frequency Offset Compensation (DATA)" and "Channel Estimates from DATA symbols". This process performs equalization. Two arrows point away from this box:
\* One arrow points to the right, labeled **"DTX"**, indicating the final output or a data transmission signal.
\* Another arrow points downwards and to the right, labeled **"Post-Combined SNR"**, indicating the post-combined Signal-to-Noise Ratio.
The flowchart depicts a process where data REs are demapped, frequency offset is compensated using long-term CFO, and channel estimates are generated through FD and time domain interpolation. These compensated data and channel estimates are then used for equalization, producing the final output and its associated SNR.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/img-02252230.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

Please refer to [11101890](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kncjTfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) for the source link of the figure.

---

Data Processing for Transform Precoding
L1 Loner data processing

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a flowchart illustrating a data processing procedure labeled "Data Processing (Transform Precoding) Loner".
The flowchart begins with a decision point "Is msg 3".
If the answer is "yes", the flow goes to "PNC (based on DMRS)".
If the answer is "no", the flow goes to a diamond-shaped decision block labeled "5G\_UP\_ALG\_925".
From "PNC (based on DMRS)", there is an arrow pointing to "Demodulator".
The "5G\_UP\_ALG\_925" block has three outgoing paths labeled (a), (b), and (c).
Path (c) leads back to "PNC (based on DMRS)".
Path (a) leads to "Demodulator".
Path (b) leads to a block labeled "Retrieve PTRS".
From "Retrieve PTRS", there is an arrow to a block labeled "PNE".
From "PNE", there is an arrow to a block labeled "PNC (based on PTRS )".
There is an arrow from "PNC (based on PTRS )" to "Demodulator".
Below the "5G\_UP\_ALG\_925" block, there is a larger rectangular box containing information about "Channel estimates from DATA symbols". This box has two sub-boxes:
1. "FD interpolation (from DMRS + FOC)"
2. "Time Domain interpolation"
There is an arrow from "FD interpolation (from DMRS + FOC)" to "Time Domain interpolation".
To the left of the flowchart, there are two blocks:
1. "EQUALIZATION"
2. "IDFT"
There is an arrow from "EQUALIZATION" to "IDFT".
There is an arrow from "IDFT" to the "Is msg 3" decision block.
Below "EQUALIZATION", there is a block labeled "DATA REs (from RE DEMAPPING + FOC)".
There is an arrow from "DATA REs (from RE DEMAPPING + FOC)" to "EQUALIZATION".
There is an arrow from "Channel estimates from DATA symbols" (specifically from the "FD interpolation (from DMRS + FOC)" sub-box) to "DATA REs (from RE DEMAPPING + FOC)".[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_1792.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

Please refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8606\_replaced([11101890](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kncjTfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for the source link of the figure(s).

Data Processing for Transform Precoding
Loki L1 HW data processing

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a flowchart illustrating a data processing pipeline for transform precoding, labeled "Loki". The flowchart begins with a decision point "Is msg 3", which branches into "yes" and "no" paths.
The "yes" path leads to an "IDFT" block, which then feeds into an "EQUALIZATION" block. The output of "EQUALIZATION" is connected to "DATA RES (from RE DEMAPPING + FOC)".
The "no" path from "Is msg 3" leads to a diamond-shaped decision block containing "5G\_UP\_ALG\_927" and "5G\_UP\_ALG\_929". This block has three outgoing paths labeled "(a)", "(b)", and "(c)".
Path "(c)" from the decision block leads to a "PNC (based on DMRS)" block, which in turn connects to a "Demodulator" block.
Path "(a)" from the decision block also leads to the "Demodulator" block.
Path "(b)" from the decision block leads to a "Retrieve PTRS" block, followed by a "PNE" block, and then a "PNC (based on PTRS )" block. The output of "PNC (based on PTRS )" also connects to the "Demodulator" block.
Below the main flowchart, there is a section labeled "Channel estimates from DATA symbols". This section contains two blocks: "FD interpolation (from DMRS + FOC)" and "Time Domain interpolation". An arrow indicates that "FD interpolation (from DMRS + FOC)" feeds into "Time Domain interpolation". An arrow also points from "Time Domain interpolation" to the "EQUALIZATION" block. Additionally, an arrow points from "DATA RES (from RE DEMAPPING + FOC)" to the "FD interpolation (from DMRS + FOC)" block.
The overall flowchart depicts a process that involves message checking, algorithm selection, signal processing steps like IDFT and equalization, demodulation, and channel estimation through interpolation.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_1794.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

Please refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8606\_replaced([11101890](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kncjTfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for the source link of the figure(s).

Thor L1 HW data processing

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a flowchart illustrating a data processing pipeline for transform precoding, labeled "Thor". The flowchart begins with a decision point "Is msg 3". If the answer is "yes", it proceeds to a process labeled "DATA REs (from RE DEMAPPING + FOC)", which then leads to "EQUALIZATION" and "IDFT". If the answer to "Is msg 3" is "no", the flow goes to a diamond shape containing "5G\_UP\_ALG\_927" and "5G\_UP\_ALG\_929".
From the "Is msg 3" "yes" branch, the "IDFT" output is not explicitly shown to connect to any subsequent block, but it is implied to be part of the data processing.
From the "5G\_UP\_ALG\_927 / 5G\_UP\_ALG\_929" diamond, there are three outgoing paths labeled "(a)", "(c)", and a direct path to "Retrieve PTRS".
Path "(c)" leads to "PNC (based on DMRS)", which then connects to the "Demodulator".
Path "(a)" leads to the "Demodulator".
The path to "Retrieve PTRS" leads to a block labeled "(b)" which then goes to "PNE" and then to "PNC (based on PTRS)".
There is also a block labeled "Channel estimates from DATA symbols" which contains two sub-blocks: "FD interpolation (from DMRS + FOC)" and "Time Domain interpolation". The output of "FD interpolation" is not explicitly shown, but "Time Domain interpolation" has an arrow pointing towards the "EQUALIZATION" block, suggesting it provides input for equalization.
Additionally, there is a path labeled "(d)" originating from the "Retrieve PTRS" block, leading to "FOE (based on PTRS)". The output of "FOE (based on PTRS)" is not explicitly shown.
The "Demodulator" block is the final output shown in the main processing path.
The text in the image is:

(from
RE DEMAPPING + FOC)

Channel estimates from DATA symbols
FD interpolation
(from DMRS +
FOC)

PTRS
(b)
PNE
PNC (based on
PTRS)
(d)
FOE (based on
PTRS)[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_1796.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

Please refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8606\_replaced([11101890](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kncjTfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for the source link of the figure(s).

Thor L1 HW data processing

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a flowchart illustrating a process within a "DU" (likely Digital Unit) and "L1 Radio" context. The flowchart depicts decision points, processing steps, and data flow.
Here's a breakdown of the elements:
**Decision Points:**
\* **"Is msg 3"**: A diamond shape indicating a conditional check.
\* **"yes"**: Leads to a process block labeled "PNC (based on DMRS)".
\* **"no"**: Leads to a diamond shape labeled "5G\_UP\_ALG\_927, 5G\_UP\_ALG\_929".
\* **"5G\_UP\_ALG\_927, 5G\_UP\_ALG\_929"**: Another diamond shape, likely representing a selection or condition based on specific algorithms.
\* **"(c)"**: An arrow pointing from the "yes" branch of "Is msg 3" to this diamond.
\* **"(a)"**: An arrow pointing from this diamond downwards.
**Processing Blocks:**
\* **"PNC (based on DMRS)"**: A rectangular block, likely representing a "Pre-coding Network Controller" or similar function, utilizing "DMRS" (Demodulation Reference Signals).
\* An arrow labeled "(c)" feeds into this block from the "5G\_UP\_ALG\_927, 5G\_UP\_ALG\_929" diamond.
\* An arrow points from this block to the "EQUALIZATION" block.
\* **"Channel estimates from DATA symbols"**: A larger rectangular box containing two sub-processes:
\* **"FD interpolation (from DMRS + FOC)"**: A rectangular block.
\* **"Time Domain interpolation"**: A rectangular block, with an arrow from "FD interpolation" feeding into it.
\* **"EQUALIZATION"**: A rectangular block, a key processing step.
\* An arrow from "PNC (based on DMRS)" feeds into it.
\* An arrow labeled "(a)" from the "5G\_UP\_ALG\_927, 5G\_UP\_ALG\_929" diamond also feeds into it.
\* A rectangular block labeled "DATA REs (from RE DEMAPPING + inter FOC)" feeds into it.
\* Outputs from this block are labeled "XIRC" and "BIRC".
\* **"DATA REs (from RE DEMAPPING + inter FOC)"**: A rectangular block, providing input data to "EQUALIZATION".
\* **"PNC (based on LT FOE)"**: A rectangular block, likely another type of PNC, utilizing "LT FOE" (likely Long Term Frequency Offset Estimation).
\* An arrow labeled "(d)" feeds into this block from the "5G\_UP\_ALG\_927, 5G\_UP\_ALG\_929" diamond.
\* An arrow from "LT FOE" feeds into this block.
\* **"LT FOE"**: A shaded rectangular block, representing the input to the "PNC (based on LT FOE)" block.
\* **"DATA POST-Equalization Processing"**: A rectangular block, representing the final processing stage after equalization.
\* Receives inputs "XIRC" and "BIRC" from the "EQUALIZATION" block.
**Data Flow and Labels:**
\* Arrows indicate the direction of data flow.
\* Labels like "(c)", "(a)", and "(d)" are used to connect different parts of the flowchart, possibly referring to specific signal paths or message types.
\* "DU" and "L1 Radio" are labels indicating functional partitions or layers.
The flowchart appears to describe a process for channel estimation and equalization, potentially in a wireless communication system like 5G, where different algorithms and reference signals are used based on certain conditions.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_1798.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

Please refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8606\_replaced([11101890](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kncjTfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for the source link of the figure(s).

[Before CNI-103796-M] L1 vDU HW data processing

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a flowchart illustrating a signal processing chain. The flowchart begins with a block labeled "DATA REs (from RE DEMAPPING + FOC)". An arrow leads from this block to a block labeled "EQUALIZATION". Another arrow goes from "EQUALIZATION" to a block labeled "IDFT".
A diamond-shaped decision block labeled "Is msg 3" is positioned to the right of the "IDFT" block. An arrow from "EQUALIZATION" points to the left input of this decision block. The decision block has two outgoing paths: "yes" and "no".
The "yes" path from "Is msg 3" leads to a block labeled "PNC (based on DMRS)". An arrow then goes from "PNC (based on DMRS)" to a block labeled "Demodulator".
The "no" path from "Is msg 3" bypasses the "PNC (based on DMRS)" block and directly leads to the "Demodulator" block.
Below the "EQUALIZATION" and "IDFT" blocks, there is a larger rectangular box containing the text "Channel estimates from DATA symbols". Inside this box, there are two connected blocks. The first block is labeled "FD interpolation (from DMRS + FOC)", and an arrow leads from it to the second block, labeled "Time Domain interpolation". An arrow from the "Time Domain interpolation" block points upwards to the "EQUALIZATION" block, indicating feedback.
Finally, an arrow exits the "Demodulator" block to the right, representing the output of the process.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_1801.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

Please refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8606\_replaced([11101890](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kncjTfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for the source link of the figure(s).  [CNI-103796-M] vDU L1 HW is no more supported [End CNI-103796-M]

Raw Channel estimation for DMRS Sequence Type 2
DU (for Loki and Thor L1 HW) [CB009936-F] and Radio (for Thor 7-2e) [End CB009936-F] shall support Release16 "Low-PAPR DMRS Type2" (5G\_UP\_10530\_replaced([11360966](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucyLjfvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) Pilot Sequence generation with DMRS sequences modulated using [image-0][image\_desc]The image contains the mathematical expression "π/2" rendered in a blurry, stylized font. The colors appear to be a gradient of blue, orange, and yellow, with a black outline.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_1806.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]BPSK and DFT-s-OFDM with Raw Channel Estimation Algorithm enhancement, needed to mitigate negative impacts of DMRS Type2 sequence generation on System Performance. For L1 Algorithm impact on Measurements (as referred by 5GMax algorithm specifications in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_695\_replaced([11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), §2.3.1.17 chapter) and System Performance metrics refer to 5G\_UP\_13354\_replaced([11361085](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucyymvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

NOTE: DU [CB009936-F] or Radio [End CB009936-F] shall support "Low-PAPR DMRS Type2" (5G\_UP\_10530\_replaced([11360966](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucyLjfvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) Pilot Sequence generation under assumption that the (FDSS) Frequency Domain Spectral Shaping (cf. 5G\_UP\_10567\_replaced([11361037](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucyylvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) technique is needed in System, implemented in UE Transmitter side and transparent to gNB receiver that is agnostic about FDSS usage in UE Transmitter.

11102258:
Raw Channel Estimation with eCPRI7.2e
For Thor 7-2e HW, for L1 implementation optimization, Radio shall support the introduction of Raw Channel Estimation (refer to $2.3.1.1 of 5GMax algorithm specification, 5G\_UP\_ALG\_695\_replaced([11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) with:

* Raw CEs computation based only on the first DMRS port in each CDM group;
* Received signal ('y') overwritten by Raw Channel Estimates. Which means that Radio shall use raw CEs instead of received signal for:
* Interference and Noise Power (5G\_UP\_ALG\_1652\_replaced([11108250](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koo2FvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) computation, as defined in $2.3.5.1 and $2.3.1.6 section in 5GMax algorithm specification (5G\_UP\_ALG\_695\_replaced([11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))
* covariance matrix of “disturbance” [image-0][image\_desc]The image contains the text "Rdd" in a blurry, stylized font. The letters are in a gradient of colors, with shades of orange, yellow, and purple visible. The background is white, and the text is framed by a black border.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_1809.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0] (5G\_UP\_ALG\_10631\_replaced([11102901](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knpXlPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) computation, as defined in $2.3.6.2 section in 5GMax algorithm specification (5G\_UP\_ALG\_695\_replaced([11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

Rational: Using Raw CEs is expected to be mathematically equivalent to using the received signal and it simplifies algorithm implementation.

---

##### 2.4.2.4.2.3 Time Domain Interpolation

###### 2.4.2.4.2.3.0-1 Time Domain Interpolation Overview (ID: 11102265)

**2.4.2.4.2.3.0-1.0-5**  (ID: `13335883`)

Time Domain Interpolation
The channel estimation algorithm is described in section 2.3.1 of the 5GMax algorithm specification (see [11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

DU Radio (for 7-2e) shall perform Channel estimation and use the Channel estimation output to compute the channel coefficients for data symbol demodulation (cf. [11102113](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kneYdfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ), [11102122](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kneYd_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ), [11102167](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kne_g_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ), [11102129](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kneYefvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) .

When programmed to receive PUSCH configured with multiple DMRSs symbols, DU and Radio shall perform Time Domain interpolation to determine (DMRS) Channel estimates onto each DATA symbol, supported by L1 HW as below:

|  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  | Loner |  | Loki | Thor | Thor (Radio 7-2e) | GPU |
| Time Domain Interpolation method | Mapping Type B | Mapping type A | Linear interpolation | Linear interpolation | Linear interpolation | Closest |
| Closest, Closest with simple interpolation | Closest, Mixed interpolation |  |  |  |  |  |
| Requirement for updates | [11102282](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kngNofvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) | [11102289](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kngNo_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) | [11102295](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kngNpfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) | [11102295](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kngNpfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) | [11102295](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kngNpfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) | [13335888](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2qHYQthtEfCmxLW1M7HMrQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) |
| **First Feature** |  |  |  | CB007487-B CB007487-C CB007487-D | CB007487-B | CB016066-C |

Note: Except for Loner L1 HW, when FOE is not valid (see [11102603](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knkfF_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) and Compensation not yet applied, DU (for Loki, Thor, [CB016066-C] and GPU [End CB016066-C] L1 HWs) and Radio (for Thor 7-2e) shall perform Closest DMRS method.

---

###### 2.4.2.4.2.3.0-2 Time Domain Interpolation Time Domain Interpolation for Loner L1 HW for PUSCH mapping type B (ID: 11102282)

**2.4.2.4.2.3.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11102286`)

Time Domain Interpolation
For Loner L1 HW, when 2 DMRS symbols (i.e. 1FL DMRS and 1 additional DMRS) is present and when the distance between, at least, one OFDM data symbol and the two DMRS symbols is > 4 (for instance 14 symbols), ClosestDMRS with Simple Interpolation algorithm (section 2.3.1.8.3 of the 5GMAX specification) is used for channel estimation time domain interpolation.

For all other cases and symbols, the ClosestDMRS algorithm (section 2.3.1.8.2 of the 5GMAX specification) is used.

---

###### 2.4.2.4.2.3.0-3 Time Domain Interpolation Time Domain Interpolation for Loner L1 HW for PUSCH mapping type A (ID: 11102289)

**2.4.2.4.2.3.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11102292`)

Time Domain Interpolation
For Loner L1 HW, when at least two DMRS symbols (i.e. 1 FL DMRS and 1 or 2 additional DMRS) is present, mixed interpolation algorithm (section 2.3.1.8.4 of the 5GMAX specification) is used for channel estimation time domain interpolation, except for the case foeValid equals to FALSE (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3333\_replaced([11151157](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksXfhvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) where ClosestDMRS algorithm (section 2.3.1.8.2 of the 5GMAX specification) is used for channel estimation time domain interpolation. Refer 5G\_RAN\_ARC\_7575\_replaced([11616055](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FamyWfwKEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for the benefit of mixed interpolation algorithm.

---

###### 2.4.2.4.2.3.0-4 Time Domain Interpolation Time Domain Interpolation for Loki and Thor L1 HW (ID: 11102295)

**2.4.2.4.2.3.0-4.0-3**  (ID: `11102305`)

Time Domain Interpolation
For Loki and Thor L1 [CB007595-B] and Thor (Radio 7-2e) [End CB007595-B] HW, in case of multiple DMRS symbols, linear interpolation algorithm shall be used.

If FOE is not valid and FOC has not been applied Closest DMRS is used.

---

###### 2.4.2.4.2.3.0-5 Time Domain Interpolation Time Domain Interpolation for vDU L1 HW (ID: 11102309)

**2.4.2.4.2.3.0-5.0-2**  (ID: `11102314`)

Time Domain Interpolation
[Before CNI-103796-M] In case of multiple DMRS symbols, closest DMRS shall be used. [End CNI-103796-M]

---

###### 2.4.2.4.2.3.0-6 Time Domain Interpolation Time Domain Interpolation for GPU L1 HW (ID: 13335888)

**2.4.2.4.2.3.0-6.0-1**  (ID: `13335889`)

Time Domain Interpolation
~~[TBC]~~ DU for GPU L1 HW shall perform Closest DMRS method for Time Domain Interpolation Algorithm when programmed to receive PUSCH configured with:  
- multiple DMRSs symbols  
- modified with Virtual DMRS Method when 2 DMRS. Refer to 5GMax algorithm specification (see [11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---
