###### 2.4.2.4.5.2.0-7 Phase noise compensation per L1 HW Phase noise compensation for vDU L1 HW in FR1 (ID: 11102832)

**2.4.2.4.5.2.0-7.0-2**  (ID: `11102836`)

Phase noise compensation per L1 HW
[Before CNI-103796-M] For vDU L1 HW:

UL PTRS is not supported, so PNE and PNC is not performed on PUSCH.

Note: As stated in 5GMAX algo spec. chapter 2.3.3.5, the FOC on data is done after the channel equalization where the block FOC contain both intra and inter symbol FOC while the PNC is not there.

[End CNI-103796-M]

---

###### 2.4.2.4.5.2.0-8 Phase noise compensation per L1 HW Phase noise compensation for GPU L1 HW in FR1**** (ID: 13335960)

**2.4.2.4.5.2.0-8.0-1**  (ID: `13335961`)

Phase noise compensation per L1 HW
For GPU L1 HW, UL PTRS is not supported: PNE and PNC is not performed on PUSCH.

Note: As stated in 5GMAX algo specification ([11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), DU for GPU L1HW shall perform FOC on data after the channel equalization with both intra and inter symbol FOC, and no PNC.

---

#### 2.4.2.4.6 Equalization

##### 2.4.2.4.6.1 Number of layers supported during equalization

###### 2.4.2.4.6.1.0-1 Number of layers supported during equalization Supported operation per L1 HW for SU receiver (ID: 11102843)

**2.4.2.4.6.1.0-1.0-5**  (ID: `13335997`)

Number of layers supported during equalization
DU (for Loner, Loki, Thor, **[CB016066-C] and GPU** [End CB016066-C] L1HWs) and Radio (for Thor 7-2e L1 HW) shall support in PUSCH Data processing (see [11102109](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kneYdPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) the equalization operations as follows:\*\*\*\*

* 2 layers MIMO equalization for 2 layers spatial multiplexing,
* SIMO equalization for 1-layer spatial multiplexing
* SIMO equalization for singleTx.

DU and Radio shall perform the equalization operation with inputs:

* the number of layers  
  - and the precoding information (W is the precoding matrix, cf. [11103323](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knuQHvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ), as defined by 3gpp, W=1 for single Tx).

DU and Radio shall apply in the equalization the normalization factor averaged per symbol.

For eCPRI 7-2e, DU shall perform the post-Equalization operations based on PUSCH DATA received from Radio. For PUSCH DATA, see [11380879](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_uepMv_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

See Equalization algorithm details section “SIMO MIMO Equalizer” of 5GMax algorithm specification (cf. [11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

###### 2.4.2.4.6.1.0-2 Number of layers supported during equalization Supported operation per L1 HW for MU receiver (ID: 11102856)

**2.4.2.4.6.1.0-2.0-6**  (ID: `13378487`)

Number of layers supported during equalization
DU for Loki, Thor L1 HW:

shall support equalization types on PUSCH as below:

2 layers MIMO equalization for 2 UEs, 1 layer spatial multiplexing for each UE for C4 subcells.

DU for Thor **[CB016061-B2] and GPU** [End CB016061-B2] L1 HW:\*\*\*\*

Additionally supported equalization types on PUSCH as below:

4 layers MIMO equalization for 2 UEs or more, up to 2 layer spatial multiplexing for a given UE for C8 subcells.

Thor L1 HW in Radio 7-2e

Additionally supported equalization types on PUSCH as below:

4 layers MIMO equalization for up to 4 **UEs per PRB for C16'4 subcells (cf. [11094165](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmaBcPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).**

The normalization factor in equalization is averaged per symbol.

The equalization operation is described in section “2.3.6 SIMO MIMO Equalizer” of 5GMax algorithm specification (see [11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

The information needed to choose the equalization type are the number of layers and the precoding information (W is the precoding matrix as defined by 3GPP)

---

###### 2.4.2.4.6.1.0-3 Number of layers supported during equalization Specific case for PUSCH scheduled by RAR UL grant during random access (ID: 11102872)

**2.4.2.4.6.1.0-3.0-5**  (ID: `13336001`)

Number of layers supported during equalization
DU (for Loner, Loki, Thor [CB016066-C] and GPU [End CB016066-C] L1 HWs) and Radio (for Thor (Radio 7-2e) L1 HW) shall support SIMO equalization ([11102843](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knoJgvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and decoding operation when programmed to receive PUSCH DATA scheduled with single antenna transmission by RAR UL grant during the random-access procedure.

For this scheme, refer to description in section 2.3.6 of the 5Gmax algorithm specification (see [11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

PUSCH IRC receiver
For Loki and [Before CNI-103796-M] vDU [End CNI-103796-M] L1 L1 HWs, MMSE-IRC shrinkage receiver is supported for FR1 NR cells.
 
Below computations in IRC receiver algorithm are performed per PRB per slot:
 the covariance matrix of “disturbance” [image-0][image\_desc]The image contains the text "Rdd" with a circumflex accent above the "R". The text is in a dark blue color and is slightly blurry. The background is white.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_2036.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]
 shrinkage operation (including shrinkage coefficient calculation and scaling of  [image-1][image\_desc]The image contains the text "Rdd" in a blurry, stylized font. The letters are colored with a gradient of brown, orange, and purple. The background is white.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_2036.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1] entries),
 matrix inversion in IRC equalizer.
 
When there are multiple DMRS symbols, only the first DMRS symbol is used to calculate shrinkage coefficient. Reason behind:
 Simulation results show that performance of using the first DMRS symbol to calculate shrinkage coefficient is quite close to that of using multiple DMRS symbols (
 Use the first DMRS symbol only as it saves computation efforts
 
Refer to “5GMax-5G New Radio Algorithm Specification” section#2.3.6.2 - #2.3.6.4 for MMSE-IRC shrinkage algorithm specification.
 
Please refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_936\_replaced([11103003](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knp-qPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) (FR1 TDD) and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_940\_replaced([11103033](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knqltvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) (FR1 FDD) for supported number of antennas.

---

The DU (for Thor L1 HW) and the Radio (for Thor 7-2e) **L1 HW shall support MMSE-IRC receiver for FR1 NR cells and shall perform below computations per PRB per slot:**

 the covariance matrix of “disturbance” [image-0][image\_desc]The image contains the text "Rdd" with a circumflex accent above the "R". The text is in a dark blue color and is slightly blurred. The background is white, and the image has a black border.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_2044.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0],

 shrinkage operation (including shrinkage coefficient calculation and scaling of  [image-1][image\_desc]The image contains the text "Rdd" in a stylized font. The "R" is large and bold, while the "dd" is smaller and subscripted. The letters are colored with a gradient of blue, purple, and orange, giving them a somewhat iridescent appearance. The background is white.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_2044.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1] entries),

 matrix inversion in IRC equalizer.

When there are multiple DMRS symbols, the matrix of “disturbance” [image-2][image\_desc]The image contains the text "Rdd" with a circumflex accent above the "R". The text is in a dark blue color and is slightly blurred. The background is white, and the image has a black border.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_2044.001.png[/image\_path][/image-2] is the result of the averaging of the covariance matrix computed for each DMRS symbol.

When rdHybridIrcEnabledFlag=1 (cf. **[11110446](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpET8PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and**

* NRBTS/NRCELL/numberOfRxBeamforming=8Rx  
  or
* NRBTS/NRCELL/act8RXnonBeamforming = true

[CB008465-AA] or

- (NRBTS/NRCELL/nrCellType = 'xDL4UL' andNRBTS/NRCELL/actNrUlComp = 'Basic' and UE is configured for joint reception (see [12740825](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_G7GPo3v5EfCiUemk-K-C4g?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)))) [End CB008465-AA]

 The DU **shall apply Legalization + shrinkage** for PUSCH only. Operations and noise floor computation are described in [DynamicSwitchLegalizationAndshrinkage-CB009363-O.pptx](https://nokia.sharepoint.com/:p:/r/sites/ASCOP/AS%20Documents/FS1TAs-CFAMs/CB/CB009000%20-%20CB009499/CFAM/CB009363%208Rx%20IRC%20receiver%20ABIP%20FR1%20TDD/References-Simulations/IRC_legalization_shrinkage_whitening/Dynamic%20switch%20Legalization%20and%20shrinkage-CB009363-O.pptx?d=w94bbfeb91a2f4484a2a412614a249b05&csf=1&web=1&e=ICn8PO) .\*\*\*\*

 The DU shall always apply Legalization using noise floor multiplying factor rdHybridIrcBetaLegalization ([11110481](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpE6-PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). When Shrinkage coefficient is higher or equal to rdHybridIrcThrShrinkage ([11110465](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpE68_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), shrinkage occurs before legalization otherwise shrinkage operation is not performed.

When rdHybridIrcEnabledFlag= 0 and

* NRBTS/NRCELL/numberOfRxBeamforming=8Rx   
  or

- NRBTS/NRCELL/act8RXnonBeamforming = true,

[CB008465-AA] or

- (NRBTS/NRCELL/nrCellType = 'xDL4UL' and NRBTS/NRCELL/actNrUlComp = 'Basic' and UE is configured for joint reception (see [12740825](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_G7GPo3v5EfCiUemk-K-C4g?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9))) [End CB008465-AA]

* the DU ~~and the Radio~~ shall apply legacy IRC with shrinkage for PUSCH.

If NRBTS/NRCELL/numberOfRxBeamforming=16Rx, the Radio (for Thor 7-2e HW) shall support either 8Rx or 16Rx IRC for SU (Single User) receiver according to the algorithm decision in the DU, e.g. ([11113592](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpcHVfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

 For 8Rx IRC reception, Hybrid (Legalization + shrinkage) is enabled for PUSCH only with rdHybridIrcEnabledFlag (cf. **[11110446](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpET8PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and respective parameters rdHybridIrcThrShrinkage (cf.[11110465](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpE68_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and rdHybridIrcBetaLegalization (cf. [11110481](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpE6-PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).**

 For 16Rx IRC reception, Hybrid (Legalization + shrinkage) is enabled for PUSCH only with rdHybridIrcEnabledFlag16Rx ([11110455](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpE68PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and respective parameters rdHybridIrcThrShrinkage16Rx ([11110474](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpE69vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and rdHybridIrcBetaLegalization16Rx ([11110490](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpE7AfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Refer to “5GMax-5G New Radio Algorithm Specification” section#2.3.6.2 - #2.3.6.4 for MMSE-IRC shrinkage algorithm specification.

Please refer to [11103003](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knp-qPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) (FR1 TDD) and [11103033](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knqltvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) (FR1 FDD) for supported number of antennas.

If NRBTS/NRCELL/numberOfRxBeamforming=16RX and NRBTS\NRCELL\NRULMUMIMO\ulMaxMuMimoLayer **= 8, with TD/FD-PDP mixture (refer to [11102322](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kngNrfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) the Radio (for Thor 7-2e HW) shall compute Channel-based disturbance calculation for MU (Multi User) receiver as described in $2.3.6.2 "MMSE-IRC" section in 5GMax algorithm specification ([11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and shall use raw Channel Estimate (refer to [11102253](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knfmmfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for REs without DMRS transmission. This is expected to be mathematically equivalent to the legacy disturbance computation.**

For Thor**L1 HW, DU or Radio shall compute the covariance matrix of “disturbance” [image-0][image\_desc]The image contains the text "Rdd" in a blurry, stylized font. The letters are colored with a gradient of brown, orange, and blue, and have a slight shadow effect. The background is white, and the text is framed by a black border.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_2048.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0] which shall include a debiasing factor:**

* when NRBTS/NRCELL/actIrcRddDebiasing is enabled
* and only for PUSCH programmed with 8Rx IRC reception:
* for BeamForming (for DU or Radio);  
  **- or non-Beamforming (only for DU)  
  - [CB008465-AA] when (NRCELL.nrCellType = 'xDL4UL' and UL CoMP is enabled (NRCELL/actNrUlComp = 'basic' (see [12763065](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_dTd9AIOSEfCOGMMH109ICw?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) and UE is configured for joint reception (see [12740825](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_G7GPo3v5EfCiUemk-K-C4g?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) [End CB008465-AA]**

This factor aims to compensate an underestimation of the Rdd computation.

Refer to “5GMax-5G New Radio Algorithm Specification” section#2.3.6.2

Please refer to [11103003](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knp-qPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) (FR1 TDD) and [11103033](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knqltvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) (FR1 FDD) for supported number of antennas.

**2.4.2.4.6.2.0-4.0-4**  (ID: `12772114`)

For Thor **L1 HW, when NIDD NRBTS.NRCELL.IrcRddAveragingPRBsize>0, Radio shall compute the covariance matrix of “disturbance” [image-0][image\_desc]Rdd[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_2050.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]with PRB averaging across x PRBs where x=IrcRddAveragingPRBsize. This hidden NIDD parameter is supported only for PUSCH received with 16Rx IRC reception. This averaging aims to improve Rdd matrix stabilization before inversion. When the allocated PRB size is not a multiple of x, the remaining the last x  PRBs are averaged together i.e. a given PRB might contribute to 2 different groups. When value is x= 2 and when the allocated PRB size is not a multiple of 2, the last 2 PRBs are averaged together. Simulations results can be found here: [16rx\_Rddaveraging\_v1.pptx](https://nokia.sharepoint.com/:p:/r/sites/5Gdoc/_layouts/15/Doc.aspx?sourcedoc=%7B7A48DC8C-B73D-4D01-A2E4-0B655B6B373E%7D&file=16rx_Rddaveraging_v1.pptx&action=edit&mobileredirect=true)**

For Thor **L1 HW, when NIDD NRBTS/NRCELL/IrcRddAveragingPRBsize8Rx>1, DU shall compute the covariance matrix of “disturbance” [image-1][image\_desc]Rdd[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_2050.001.png[/image\_path][/image-1]with PRB averaging across y PRBs where y=IrcRddAveragingPRBsize8Rx. This NIDD parameter is supported for PUSCH received with 8Rx IRC reception in**[CB014128-E] CPRI FH only for FDD FR1 and \*\*[End CB014128-E] only for eCPRI 7-2a FH (i.e. it does not apply to 8Rx reception in 7-2e FH). This averaging aims to improve Rdd matrix stabilization before inversion. When the allocated PRB size is not a multiple of y, the last y PRBs are averaged together i.e. a given PRB might contribute to 2 different groups.

DU shall support NRBTS/NRCELL/IrcRddAveragingPRBsize8Rx in the following conditions:  
- when NRCELL.numberOfRxBeamforming=8RX   
- or when NRBTS/NRCELL/act8RXnonBeamforming = true,   
- [CB008465-AA] or when NRCELL.nrCellType = 'xDL4UL' and UL CoMP is enabled (NRCELL/actNrUlComp = 'basic' (see [12763065](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_dTd9AIOSEfCOGMMH109ICw?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) and UE is configured for joint reception (see [12740825](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_G7GPo3v5EfCiUemk-K-C4g?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)), [End CB008465-AA]**\***\*

Note: If only 1 PRB is scheduled, the averaging is not in effect.

Refer to “5GMax-5G New Radio Algorithm Specification” section#2.3.6.2

Please refer to [11103003](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knp-qPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) (FR1 TDD) and [11103033](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knqltvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) (FR1 FDD) for supported number of antennas.

Note: The parameter shall be carefully chosen because high values shall impact narrowband interference rejection capabilities.

For Thor **L1 HW, DU shall compute the covariance matrix of “disturbance” [image-0][image\_desc]The image contains the text "Rdd" in a blurry, stylized font. The letters are colored with a gradient of brown, orange, and blue, and have a slight shadow effect. The background is white, and the text is framed by a black border.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_2054.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0] while performing the matrix exponent alignment on group basis composed by neighbour PRBs instead of the whole allocation. The default size of each group is 3. When allocated PRB is not a multiple of 3, the one or two remaining PRBs are considered as the last group. The default size of each group is 3. When allocated PRB is not a multiple of 3, the one or two remaining PRBs are considered as the last group.**

This Rdd group scaling avoids to loose precision in case of narrow band interference.  
DU shall support Rdd groups scaling algorithm only when programmed to receive PUSCH ~~received~~ with 8Rx IRC order ~~and RCELL.numberOfRxBeamforming=8RX~~ in the following conditions:  
- when NRCELL.numberOfRxBeamforming=8RX   
- or when NRBTS/NRCELL/act8RXnonBeamforming = true,   
- [CB008465-AA] or when NRCELL.nrCellType = 'xDL4UL' and UL CoMP is enabled (NRCELL/actNrUlComp = 'basic' (see [12763065](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_dTd9AIOSEfCOGMMH109ICw?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) and UE is configured for joint reception (see [12740825](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_G7GPo3v5EfCiUemk-K-C4g?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)), [End CB008465-AA]

For PUSCH received with 16Rx IRC reception or 8Rx IRC reception with NRCELL.numberOfRxBeamforming=16, Radio shall support advanced PRB group solution called "OP5+". In this solution, the grouping shall not be restricted to neighbor PRBs and PRB group size is not restricted to fixed value.

Refer to “5GMax-5G New Radio Algorithm Specification” section#2.3.6.2

Please refer to [11103003](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knp-qPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) (FR1 TDD) and [11103033](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knqltvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) (FR1 FDD) for supported number of antennas.

---

###### 2.4.2.4.6.2.0-6 PUSCH IRC receiver PUSCH IRC Recevier algorithm for FR1 for GPU L1 HW (ID: 13336026)

**2.4.2.4.6.2.0-6.0-2**  (ID: `13457835`)

PUSCH IRC receiver
13457835:
PUSCH IRC receiver

PUSCH IRC receiver
For FR1 TDD, DU for GPU L1 HW shall support MMSE-IRC (Minimum Mean Square Error - Interference Rejection Combining) Equalization algorithm and perform per PRB per slot the following operations:
\* the covariance matrix of “disturbance” [image-0][image\_desc]Rdd[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_2036.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]computation based on the first DMRS symbol, even when programmed to receive PUSCH configured with multiple DMRS symbols. (See Rationale below)
\* the shrinkage procedure by computing the:
- shrinkage coefficient (**ρ) to control the balance of off-diagonal and the diagonal elements
- shrinkage Target** (F) Matrix as the covariance [image-1][image\_desc]Rdd[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_2036.001.png[/image\_path][/image-1]diagonal 
- scaling of [image-2][image\_desc]Rdd[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_2036.002.png[/image\_path][/image-2] entries,***\*
\* the matrix inversion in IRC equalizer
Rationale: 
- Simulation results show that performance of using the first DMRS symbol to calculate shrinkage coefficient is quite close to that of using multiple DMRS symbols ( 
- Using the first DMRS symbol only saves computation efforts.
[CB016061-B1] 
When rdHybridIrcEnabledFlag=1 (cf.* \*[11110446](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpET8PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and NRBTS/NRCELL/numberOfRxBeamforming=8Rx**
 The DU **shall apply Legalization + shrinkage** for PUSCH only. Operations and noise floor computation are described in [DynamicSwitchLegalizationAndshrinkage-CB009363-O.pptx](https://nokia.sharepoint.com/:p:/r/sites/ASCOP/AS%20Documents/FS1TAs-CFAMs/CB/CB009000%20-%20CB009499/CFAM/CB009363%208Rx%20IRC%20receiver%20ABIP%20FR1%20TDD/References-Simulations/IRC_legalization_shrinkage_whitening/Dynamic%20switch%20Legalization%20and%20shrinkage-CB009363-O.pptx?d=w94bbfeb91a2f4484a2a412614a249b05&csf=1&web=1&e=ICn8PO) .\*\*\*\*
 The DU shall always apply Legalization using noise floor multiplying factor rdHybridIrcBetaLegalization ([11110481](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpE6-PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). When Shrinkage coefficient is higher or equal to rdHybridIrcThrShrinkage ([11110465](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpE68_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), shrinkage occurs before legalization otherwise shrinkage operation is not performed.
When rdHybridIrcEnabledFlag= 0 and NRBTS/NRCELL/numberOfRxBeamforming=8Rx
\* the DU shall apply IRC with shrinkage for PUSCH. [End CB016061-B1]
Refer to “5GMax-5G New Radio Algorithm Specification” section#2.3.6.2 - #2.3.6.4 for MMSE-IRC shrinkage algorithm specification.
 
Please refer to [11103003](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knp-qPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) for supported number of antennas.

DU for GPU L1 HW shall support covariance matrix of “disturbance” [image-0][image\_desc]The image contains the text "Rdd" with a circumflex accent above the "R". The text is in a dark blue color and is slightly blurry. The background is white.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_2036.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]computation (refer to [13336026](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Sto_QdkwEfCmxLW1M7HMrQ) ) based on the averaging method.  
To improve the quality of Rdd matrix, DU for GPU L1 HW shall compute the averaging of Rdd matrices over 3 PRBs.  
For a given PRB, DU for GPU L1 HW shall use the average of Rdd matrices from 2 adjacent neighbouring PRBs.

Refer to “5GMax-5G New Radio Algorithm Specification (see [11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Please refer to ([11103003](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knp-qPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) (FR1 TDD).

##### 2.4.2.4.6.3 PUSCH receiver algorithm supported for SU receiver

###### 2.4.2.4.6.3.0-1 PUSCH receiver algorithm supported for SU receiver PUSCH receiver algorithm in FR1 TDD (ID: 11103003)

**2.4.2.4.6.3.0-1.0-12**  (ID: `13457834`)

PUSCH receiver algorithm supported for SU receiver
DU and Radio shall support the following receivers order for FR1 TDD are as below:

|  |  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  | **Loner** | **Loki** | **Thor** | **Thor** **(Radio****7-2e)**\*\*\*\* | **GPU** |
| 2Rx MMSE MRC | Supported | Supported | - | - | - |
| 4Rx MMSE MRC | Supported | - | - | - | - |
| 2Rx MMSE IRC | - | - | Supported (shrinkage) | - | - |
| 4Rx MMSE IRC |  | Supported (shrinkage) | Supported (shrinkage) | - | Supported (shrinkage) |
| 8Rx MMSE IRC | - | - | Supported (shrinkage, Hybrid) | Supported (shrinkage, Hybrid) | **[CB016061-B1] Supported** (shrinkage, Hybrid) **[End CB016061-B1]** |
| 16Rx MMSE IRC | - | - | - | Supported  (shrinkage, Hybrid) | - |
| **First Feature** |  |  | CB007487-D | CB007595-B | CB016066-C |

DU shall support 2Rx, 4Rx, 8Rx or 16RX PUSCH Receiver order, with 2 or 4 or 8 or 16RX antenna (or spatial) streams signal Receiver inputs, as per cell setup based on configured subcell type, please refer to [11094169](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmaBe_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

When NRCELL\numberOfRxBeamforming=16Rx, Radio shall support MMSE-IRC operations when UE PUSCH receiver programmed with either 8 or 16 Spatial Streams.

###### 2.4.2.4.6.3.0-2 PUSCH receiver algorithm supported for SU receiver PUSCH receiver algorithm in FR1 FDD (ID: 11103033)

**2.4.2.4.6.3.0-2.0-6**  (ID: `13323474`)

PUSCH receiver algorithm supported for SU receiver
The receiver supported in FR1 FDD are as below:

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  | **Loner** | **Loki** | **Thor** | GPU |
| 2Rx MMSE MRC | Supported | Supported | - | - |
| 4Rx MMSE MRC | Supported | - | - | - |
| 2Rx MMSE IRC | - | - | Supported  (shrinkage) | Supported (shrinkage) |
| 4Rx MMSE IRC | - | Supported  (shrinkage) | Supported  (shrinkage) | Supported (shrinkage) |
| 8Rx MMSE IRC | - | - | **Supported**(shrinkage, Hybrid)\*\*\*\* | - |
| **First Feature** |  |  | CB007487-D | CB016406-A |

DU shall support 2Rx, or 4Rx, or 8Rx PUSCH Receiver order, as per cell setup based on configured subcell type, please refer to [11094169](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmaBe_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

**[CB008465-AA] If 4Rx is configured at cell set-up phase, 8Rx can be selected if UL CoMP is activated (NRCELL.actNrUlComp = 'basic') and if the joint reception is configured (see [12740825](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_G7GPo3v5EfCiUemk-K-C4g?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)).**[End CB008465-AA]\*\*\*\*

DU shall support 8Rx PUSCH Receiver order with and without FDD Beamforming **[CB008465-AA] or for UL CoMP joint reception.**[End CB008465-AA]\*\*\*\*

##### 2.4.2.4.6.5 Equalization block update when Transform precoding is enabled

###### 2.4.2.4.6.5.0-1 Equalization block update when Transform precoding is enabled Equalization block update for SU receiver (ID: 11103073)

**2.4.2.4.6.5.0-1.0-3**  (ID: `11103080`)

Equalization block update when Transform precoding is enabled
\* For Loki, Loner, Thor [CB009514-F] and Thor(Radio 7-2e) [End CB009514-F] when Transform Precoding is enabled, before data layer de-mapping and after equalizer, 5G-L1-UL shall perform the inverse for transform precoder procedure described in 38.211 section 6.3.1.4.

###### 2.4.2.4.6.5.0-2 Equalization block update when Transform precoding is enabled Equalization block update for MU receiver (ID: 11103083)

**2.4.2.4.6.5.0-2.0-3**  (ID: `12812102`)

Equalization block update when Transform precoding is enabled
**[Before CB008148-C] For Loki and Thor only TDD FR1, MU receiver does not expect to receive signal with Transform Precoding enabled.**[End CB008148-C]   
[CB008148-C] For TDD FR1, DU for Loki and Thor L1HW, or Radio for Thor L1 HW shall support signal input to MU Receiver with Transform Precoding enabled. **[End CB008148-C]**\*\*\*\*

#### 2.4.2.4.7 LDPC decoder

##### 2.4.2.4.7.0-1 LDPC decoder LDPC (Low density parity check) decoder (ID: 11103094)

**2.4.2.4.7.0-1.0-4**  (ID: `13336054`)

LDPC decoder
The LDPC decoder algorithm is utilized for all data channels. The decoder deploys early termination of decoding iterations, for the case internal logic detects a correctly decoded block.

DU for Loner L1 HW shall support decoding with maximum 20 number of iterations, refer to <https://nokia.sharepoint.com/sites/LEKA005_Project/_layouts/15/DocIdRedir.aspx?ID=BRIRPOBIURMA-1992971631-82.>   
5GMAX simulations uses 15 as maximum value.

DU for Loki, Thor**[CB016066-C] and GPU**[End CB016066-C] L1 HW shall support decoding with the maximum number of iterations set to 10 ~~15.~~\*\*\*\*

Refer to Section “LDPC Decoder” of 5GMax algorithm specification (see [11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

DU an Radio shall support the PUSCH DMRS logical antenna port(s), the *antPort values in DCI for each transmission scheme as given below.*

When PTRS is activated, the ports 4, 5 cannot be used as the first port of the UE (cf. 38.214: 6.2.2 UE DM-RS transmission procedure).

| Single Tx Transmission | 2 | 0 | supported | supported | supported | **supported** | **supported** |
| 0 | 0 | supported | supported | supported | **supported** | **[CB016061-B1]** supported **[End CB016061-B1]** |  |
| 1 | 1 | NA | supported | supported | NA | **[CB016061-B2]** supported **[End CB016061-B2]** |  |
| 3 | 1 | NA | supported | supported | NA | **[CB016061-B2]** supported **[End CB016061-B2]** |  |
| 4 | 2 | NA | supported | supported | NA | **[CB016061-B2]** supported **[End CB016061-B2]** |  |
| 5 | 3 | NA | supported | supported | NA | **[CB016061-B2]** supported **[End CB016061-B2]** |  |
| Spatial multiplexing rank 1 | 2 | 0 | supported | supported | supported | **supported** | **supported** |
| 0 | 0 | supported | supported | supported | **supported** | **[CB016061-B1]** supported **[End CB016061-B1]** |  |
| 1 | 1 | NA | supported | supported | NA | **[CB016061-B2]** supported **[End CB016061-B2]** |  |
| 3 | 1 | NA | supported | supported | NA | **[CB016061-B2]** supported **[End CB016061-B2]** |  |
| 4 | 2 | NA | supported | supported | NA | **[CB016061-B2]** supported **[End CB016061-B2]** |  |
| 5 | 3 | NA | supported | supported | NA | **[CB016061-B2]** supported **[End CB016061-B2]** |  |
| Spatial multiplexing rank 2 | 0 | 0 and 1 | supported | supported | supported | **supported** | **[CB016061-B2]** supported **[End CB016061-B2]** |
| 1 | 0 and 1 | supported | supported | supported | **supported** | **[CB016061-B1]** supported **[End CB016061-B1]** |  |
| 3 | 0 and 2 | supported | supported | supported | ~~**supported**~~ | **[CB016061-A]** supported **[End CB016061-A]** |  |
|  | 2 | 2 and 3 | NA | supported | supported | NA | **[CB016061-B2]** supported **[End CB016061-B2]** |
| **First Feature** |  |  |  |  | CB007487-B CB007487-C | CB007595-B | CB016066-C |

**Note: The column "PUSCH DMRS logical antenna port(s)" corresponds to the signals at the input of "Precoding" block.**

| 3 | 1 | NA | NA |  | NA |  |

| 5 | 3 | NA |  | NA |  |  |

##### 2.4.2.5.1.0-8 PUSCH DMRS antenna ports for SU receiver Mapping to polarizations for maxLength = 1 (ID: 11103173)

**2.4.2.5.1.0-8.0-2**  (ID: `11103178`)

PUSCH DMRS antenna ports for SU receiver
The PUSCH antPort values in DCI, the number of layers  and the number of physical ports as well as the polarizations are given for each transmission scheme.

When PTRS is activated, the ports 4, 5 cannot be used as the first port of the UE.(cf. 38.214: 6.2.2 UE DM-RS transmission procedure).

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Transmission scheme | numOf Layers | antPort in UL DCI (38.212 table 7.3.1.1.2-8~11) | Number of physical ports & polarization(s) |
| Single Tx Transmission | 1 | 2 | 1, polarizations +45° |
| 0 | 1, polarizations +45° |  |  |
| Spatial multiplexing rank 1 | 1 | 2 | 2, polarizations +/-45° |
| 0 | 2, polarizations +/-45° |  |  |
| Spatial multiplexing rank 2 | 2 | 0 | 2, polarizations +/-45° |
| 1 | 2, polarizations +/-45° |  |  |
| 3 | 2, polarizations +/-45° |  |  |
| 2 | 2, polarizations +/-45° |  |  |

Note:

The number of PUSCH antenna ports (now after Precoding block) is equal to the number of SRS antenna ports. And the number of SRS/PUSCH ports are {1000,1001} for 2 SRS ports.

This mapping is valid whatever the technology FR1(TDD, FDD);  FR2 TDD.

[CB007595-B]

This requirement shall be also supported by Radio (L1) for eCPRI 7-2e.

[End CB007595-B]

---

| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Transmission scheme | numOf Layers | antPort in UL DCI (38.212 table 7.3.1.1.2-12 and 7.3.1.1.2-13) | Number of physical ports & polarization(s) | First feature |
| Single Tx Transmission | 1 | [2,3,4,5] | 1, polarizations +45° | CB008235-A |
| Spatial multiplexing rank 1 | 1 | [2,3,4,5] | 2, polarizations +/-45° | CB008235-A |
| Spatial multiplexing rank 2 | 2 | [1,2] | 2, polarizations +/-45° | CB008235-A |

Note: The number of PUSCH antenna ports (now after Precoding block) is equal to the number of SRS antenna ports.

Note : antPort =0,1 in UL DCI i.e. CDM group without data equal to 1 is not supported for MU receiver, corresponding 5GMAX results are available on the following link [5GMAX\_CB008360\_results\_related\_to\_FO](https://nokia.sharepoint.com/:x:/r/sites/5gmax/Shared%20Documents/SimulationReports/NR/mMIMO%20and%20BF/UL%20MU/CB008360/IntraFoCMu.xlsx?d=w6221471cd1b042b9aa5c169aba890258&csf=1&web=1&e=2aP3ke) . L2 requirement is [11137832](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krbravvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ). Rule is extended to antPort0 for rank2.

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Transmission scheme | numOf Layers | antPort in UL DCI (38.212 table 7.3.1.1.2-12 and 7.3.1.1.2-13) | Number of physical ports & polarization(s) | First feature |
| Single Tx Transmission | 1 | [6,…13] | 1, polarizations +45° | CB008235-A |
| Spatial multiplexing rank 1 | 1 | [6,…13] | 2, polarizations +/-45° | CB008235-A |
| Spatial multiplexing rank 2 | 2 | [4,5,6,7] | 2, polarizations +/-45° | CB008235-A |

Note: The number of PUSCH antenna ports (now after Precoding block) is equal to the number of SRS antenna ports.

For MU receiver support regarding L1 HW, please refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8756\_replaced([11103056](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knrMwPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

**2.4.2.5.3.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11103232`)

Overview PUSCH,PTRS,SRS antenna ports

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a block diagram illustrating the "UL Single Tx" process, likely within a telecommunications system. The diagram shows the flow of data and control signals through different processing blocks.
Here's a breakdown of the elements:
**Left Side:**
\* **PUSCH Layer mapping:** A blue block on the far left, labeled "PUSCH Layer mapping". An arrow labeled "data\_layer0" points from this block to the central green block.
\* **DMRS of PUSCH:** Text above a red arrow pointing to the central green block.
\* **DMRS ant port 0:** A red arrow originating from the text "DMRS ant port 0" and pointing to the central green block.
\* **PTRS ant port 0:** A purple arrow originating from the text "PTRS ant port 0" and pointing to the central green block.
**Center:**
\* **W = 1:** A large green block in the center, labeled "W = 1". Arrows from "DMRS ant port 0", "PTRS ant port 0", and "data\_layer0" point into this block. An arrow labeled "Pusch Port 1000" exits this block and points to the right-hand blue block.
**Right Side:**
\* **SRS Port 1000:** Text and an arrow indicating a signal labeled "SRS Port 1000" pointing to the right-hand blue block.
\* **RE MAPPING (BWP):** A large blue block on the right, labeled "RE MAPPING (BWP):". This block contains a list of sub-items with checkboxes:
\* **DMRS:**
\* Specific DMRS symbol when CDMgroup 2
\* **PTRS:**
\* 1 tone every LPT-RS symbols
\* 1 tone every KPT-RS RB
\* **SRS:**
\* Specific SRS symbol
\* Comb, cyclic shift, Bsrs, Csrs, bhop
\* **PUSCH:**
\* **H:** A large letter "H" is shown to the right of the blue block, with a bracket encompassing the output arrows from the blue block.
**Bottom:**
\* **UL Single Tx:** Text centered below the diagram.
The diagram depicts how different signals (DMRS, PTRS, data, SRS) are processed and mapped, likely for transmission in an uplink (UL) scenario. The "W = 1" block seems to be a key processing stage, and the "RE MAPPING (BWP)" block details the remapping operations performed on various signals. The final "H" might represent the output channel or a specific processing stage after remapping.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_2141.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]
Note (2) that the number of PUSCH antenna ports (now after Precoding block) is equal to the numofSRSPorts
 
[CB007595-B]
Note: This requirement shall be also supported by Radio (L1) for eCPRI 7-2e. But, PTRS and pSRS are not in scope.
[End CB007595-B]

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating the UL Spatial Multiplexing 2x2: rank 1 process. It shows the flow of data and control signals between different components.
On the left, there's a block labeled "PUSCH Layer mapping" which outputs "data\_layer0". This signal feeds into a green block labeled "W (TPMI)".
Above the "W (TPMI)" block, there are two inputs: "DMRS ant port 0" (red arrow) and "PTRS ant port 0" (purple arrow). The "DMRS of PUSCH" is also indicated.
The "W (TPMI)" block has two outputs labeled "Pusch Port 1000" and "Pusch Port 1001".
There are also two signals labeled "SRS Port 1000" and "SRS Port 1001" originating from above and feeding into the right side of the "W (TPMI)" block, with red arrows pointing towards a blue block on the right.
The blue block on the right is labeled "RE MAPPING (BWP):" and contains a list of items with checkboxes:
- DMRS
- Specific DMRS symbol when CDMgroup 2
- PTRS
- 1 tone every LPT-RS symbols
- 1 tone every KPT-RS RB
- SRS
- Specific SRS symbol
- Comb,cyclic shift, Bsrs, Csrs, bhop
- PUSCH
The "Pusch Port 1000" and "Pusch Port 1001" outputs from the "W (TPMI)" block feed into this blue "RE MAPPING (BWP)" block.
Finally, there are two arrows pointing from the "RE MAPPING (BWP)" block to a large letter "H" on the far right, enclosed by a curly bracket.
The title "UL Spatial Multiplexing 2x2: rank 1" is displayed at the bottom of the image.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_2144.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]
Note (2) that the number of PUSCH antenna ports (now after Precoding block) is equal to the numofSRSPorts
 
[CB007595-B]
Note: This requirement shall be also supported by Radio (L1) for eCPRI 7-2e. But, PTRS and pSRS are not in scope.
[End CB007595-B]
 
FILE: SU\_MIMO\_UL\_spatial\_multiplexing.pptx

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a block diagram illustrating the UL Spatial Multiplexing 2x2: rank 2 process. It shows the flow of data and control signals from the PUSCH Layer mapping to the RE MAPPING (BWP) block, which then outputs to a final stage represented by the letter 'H'.
Here's a breakdown of the components and their connections:
**Left Side:**
\* **DMRS of PUSCH:** This section indicates the Demodulation Reference Signal (DMRS) for the Physical Uplink Shared Channel (PUSCH).
\* **DMRS ant port 0:** An input signal related to antenna port 0 for DMRS.
\* **DMRS ant port 1 (or 2):** An input signal related to antenna port 1 or 2 for DMRS.
\* **PTRS ant port 0 (PTRS-DMRS association=0):** An input signal for Phase Tracking Reference Signal (PTRS) on antenna port 0, with a specific association to DMRS.
\* **PUSCH Layer mapping:** This block represents the mapping of PUSCH data.
\* **data\_layer0:** An output signal representing data layer 0.
\* **data\_layer1:** An output signal representing data layer 1.
**Middle Section:**
\* **W (TPMI):** A central processing block, likely representing a Transmit Precoding Matrix Indicator (TPMI) related function.
\* It receives inputs from the DMRS and PTRS sections on the left.
\* It receives `data_layer0` and `data_layer1` from the PUSCH Layer mapping.
\* It outputs to `Pusch Port 1000` and `Pusch Port 1001`.
\* It also interacts with SRS (Sounding Reference Signal) ports.
\* **SRS Port 1000:** Receives a signal from the RE MAPPING block and sends a signal to the W (TPMI) block.
\* **SRS Port 1001:** Receives a signal from the RE MAPPING block and sends a signal to the W (TPMI) block.
**Right Side:**
\* **RE MAPPING (BWP):** This block handles Resource Element (RE) mapping for the Bandwidth Part (BWP). It contains several sub-sections:
\* **DMRS:**
\* Ant port(0,1) same REs, different codes.
\* Ant port(0,2) different REs, same code.
\* Specific DMRS symbol when CDMgroup 2.
\* **PTRS:**
\* 1 tone every LPT-RS symbols.
\* 1 tone every KPT-RS RB.
\* **SRS:**
\* Specific SRS symbol.
\* Comb, cyclic shift, Bsrs, Csrs, bhop.
\* **PUSCH:** This is a general category for PUSCH related mapping.
\* **H:** This large letter 'H' on the far right likely represents the final output or the channel matrix in the context of spatial multiplexing. The arrows from the RE MAPPING block point towards this 'H', indicating the processed signals are used in this final stage.
**Connections and Flow:**
1. DMRS and PTRS signals from the top left feed into the W (TPMI) block.
2. PUSCH data layers (`data_layer0`, `data_layer1`) from the PUSCH Layer mapping block also feed into the W (TPMI) block.
3. The W (TPMI) block processes these inputs and outputs signals to `Pusch Port 1000` and `Pusch Port 1001`.
4. The RE MAPPING (BWP) block receives signals from the W (TPMI) block via `Pusch Port 1000` and `Pusch Port 1001`.
5. The RE MAPPING (BWP) block also interacts with SRS ports, with signals flowing both ways between the SRS ports and the W (TPMI) block.
6. The RE MAPPING (BWP) block's processed outputs are directed towards the final 'H' stage.
The diagram visually represents how different signals (DMRS, PTRS, PUSCH data, SRS) are processed and mapped to resource elements for uplink spatial multiplexing in a 2x2 MIMO configuration with a rank of 2.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_2147.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]
Note (2) that the number of PUSCH antenna ports (now after Precoding block) is equal to the numofSRSPorts
 
[CB007595-B]
Note: This requirement shall be also supported by Radio (L1) for eCPRI 7-2e. But, PTRS and pSRS are not in scope.
[End CB007595-B]

Supported spatial configuration for MU receiver
Supported configurations are as follow :

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **L1 HW** | **Single Tx ([11103235](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kntpAvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))** | **Rank1 spatial multiplexing ([11103246](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kntpBfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))** | **Rank2 spatial multiplexing ([11103258](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kntpFPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))** | **First Feature** |
| Loner L1 HW | - | - | - |  |
| Loki L1 HW | supported | supported | - | CB008360-A |
| Thor L1 HW | supported | supported | supported | CB009028-5G-A |
| Thor (Radio 7-2e) | - | - | supported | CB010708-C |
| GPU | supported | supported | supported | CB016061-B2 |

Important note : Rank2 is not supported for a single UE when only 4Rx IRC receiver is supported (cf. ([11103056](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knrMwPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) but a total of 2 layers is of course supported

(2UEs rank1). Please [11104119](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kn4oLvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

Note2: For number of layers support depending on subcell type please refer to [11102856](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knowhPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

12770632:
Overview
A8 subcell type supports SU (Single User) mode, if NRBTS\NRCELL.numberOfRxbeamforming=8Rx (only eCPRI fronthaul interface) in DU.
[Before CB008577-A] 
4 beams are build from the antenna panel split (horizontal & vertical).
Each antenna port consists of the antenna elements, belong to one quarter of the full panel. Antenna port provides one sector beam.
[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a block diagram illustrating a signal processing flow.
On the left, there is a large block labeled "UEO' Sector Beam". This block is divided into four smaller, equal-sized rectangular blocks stacked vertically. Each of these smaller blocks is labeled with "quarter antenna panel", with the first being "1 quarter antenna panel", the second "2 quarter antenna panel", the third "3 quarter antenna panel", and the fourth "4 quarter antenna panel".
To the right of the "UEO' Sector Beam" block, there are four horizontal lines originating from the four "quarter antenna panel" blocks, grouped together by curly braces. These lines then connect to a large rectangular block in the center, labeled "8RX IRC SU receiver".
To the right of the "8RX IRC SU receiver" block, there are two horizontal lines originating from the receiver block. These lines lead to two separate rectangular blocks. The top block is labeled "UEO Layer 0", and the bottom block is labeled "UEO Layer 1 \*".
The diagram depicts a system where a sector beam is processed by four antenna panels, which then feed into an 8RX IRC SU receiver, ultimately outputting to two layers, UEO Layer 0 and UEO Layer 1.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_2470.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]
[End CB008577-A] 
[CB008577-A] 
DU shall receive 8 streams for PUSCH, every 2 continuous streams corresponding to one beam.
DU will get directly 4 beams for the single UE from higher layer (see [12771852](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI__UhkgYlZEfCrqOdGJPoCRA)) [CB008577-C], or get 1 full panel beam for the single UE from higher layer and up to Radio to build 4 beams via panel split method (see [11354596](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_6rRT4PvfEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). [End CB008577-C]
[image-1][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating a receiver system. On the left, there are four rectangular boxes labeled "UEO's 1st beam", "UEO's 2nd beam", "UEO's 3rd beam", and "UEO's 4th beam". Each of these boxes has four horizontal lines extending to the right, connected by a curly brace. These lines represent input signals.
In the center, there is a large blue rectangular box labeled "8RX IRC SU receiver". The input lines from the left connect to this central box.
On the right side of the diagram, there are two rectangular boxes labeled "UEO Layer 0" and "UEO Layer 1". Two horizontal lines extend from the "8RX IRC SU receiver" box to these output boxes, representing output signals. The diagram depicts a system that receives multiple beams from a UEO (User Equipment), processes them through an 8RX IRC SU receiver, and outputs two layers.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/img-09031558.png[/image\_path][/image-1]
[End CB008577-A] 
A8 subcell type does not support MU (Multi User) mode.
File: MU vs SU.pptx

###### 2.4.2.5.6.1.0-2 Overview MU vs SU receiver : inputs vs outputs (A8) (ID: 11104243)

**2.4.2.5.6.1.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11104245`)

Overview
This requirement describes difference between SU (Single User) receiver or MU (Multi User) receiver regarding input streams and output streams. Reference to measurements is given for each receiver.

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Subcell Type=A8 | UE0 |  |
| Receiver input: number of streams per UE (FCP message) | Receiver output: number of antennas |  |
| SU receiver (cf. 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7236\_replaced([11109508](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko4tuvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) | 8 (4 beams) | 8 |
| MU receiver (cf. 5G\_UP\_ALG\_19621\_replaced([11109576](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko5Uz_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) | - | - |

Own UE beams and corresponding streams information can be found from FCP message (5G\_UP\_5222\_replaced([11378660](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_uecYa_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

##### 2.4.2.5.6.2 DMRS related information

###### 2.4.2.5.6.2.0-1 DMRS related information DMRS Ports allocation for UL SU MIMO (ID: 11104250)

**2.4.2.5.6.2.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11104253`)

DMRS related information
For SU (Single User) MIMO DMRS port mapping, A8 subcell type support DMRS port 0 and 1 (CDM group 0, internally represented by the parameter numOfDmrsCdmGroupWithoutData = 1, as described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_800\_replaced([11101782](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knb8MPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Codebook index is signalled to DU(L1) [CB007595-B] or Radio(L1) [End CB007595-B] by 5G-L2-PS and has to be taken into account for spatial multiplexing rank1 and rank2 transmissions. The DU(L1) [CB007595-B] or Radio(L1) [End CB007595-B] shall consider updates in UL codebook from 38211 2.0.0 (§6.3.1.5) as given in the table below:

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Codebook TPMI index | One-Layer | Two-Layer |
| 0 | [image-0][image\_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_2170.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0] | [image-1][image\_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_2170.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1] |
| 1 | [image-2][image\_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_2170.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2] | [image-3][image\_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_2170.004.png[/image\_path][/image-3] |
| 2 | [image-4][image\_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_2170.005.png[/image\_path][/image-4] | [image-5][image\_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_2170.006.png[/image\_path][/image-5] |
| 3 | [image-6][image\_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_2170.007.png[/image\_path][/image-6] | - |
| 4 | [image-7][image\_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_2170.008.png[/image\_path][/image-7] | - |
| 5 | [image-8][image\_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_2170.009.png[/image\_path][/image-8] | - |

Limitation in using codebook index exist depending on the pusch-TransCoherence UE capability. When set to fullCoherence all indices can be set, but when set to nonCoherent, only values specified in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_994\_replaced([11103365](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knu3IvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) can be used.

[CB007595-B]

Note: "5G-L1-UL" is changed to "DU(L1)" since this version as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_20317\_replaced([11094116](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmZaYfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). (Note kept only in this version)

[End CB007595-B]

---

For the UE corresponds to codebookSubset/pusch-TransCoherence = nonCoherent.

For non-coherent UE with Rel'15

The DU [CB007595-B] or Radio(L1) [End CB007595-B] shall also consider the nonCoherent UL codebook for 2 antenna ports from 38.212 Table 7.3.1.1.2-4, Table 7.3.1.1.2-5 and 38.211 Table 6.3.1.5-1 and Table 6.3.1.5-4, as given in the table below:

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Codebook TPMI index | One-Layer | Two-Layer |
| 0 | [image-0][image\_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_2173.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0] | [image-1][image\_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_2173.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1] |
| 1 | [image-2][image\_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_2173.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2] | - |

For non-coherent UE with Rel'16

The DU [CB007595-B] or Radio(L1) [End CB007595-B] shall also consider the nonCoherent UL codebook for 2 antenna ports from 38.212 Table 7.3.1.1.2-4, Table 7.3.1.1.2-4A, Table 7.3.1.1.2-5 and Table 7.3.1.1.2-5A and 38.211 Table 6.3.1.5-1 and Table 6.3.1.5-4, as given in the table below:

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Codebook TPMI index | One-Layer | Two-Layer | First Feature |
| 0 | [image-3][image\_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_2173.004.png[/image\_path][/image-3] | [image-4][image\_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_2173.005.png[/image\_path][/image-4] |  |
| 1 | [image-5][image\_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_2173.006.png[/image\_path][/image-5] | - |  |
| 2(\*) | [image-6][image\_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_2173.007.png[/image\_path][/image-6] | - | 5GC002396-B |

If the PUSCH power scaling ratio (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_6818\_replaced([11103724](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knzvrPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) ) is configured, the valid 1Layer TPMI for report is indicated by the valid PUSCH power scaling ratio.

Note(\*): 1Layer TPMI2 can be applied only when fullPowerMode1 is configured (see 5G\_UP\_IF\_1473\_replaced([11512549](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_tTta6vv1Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_3768\_replaced([11355416](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucQnHfvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

[CB007595-B]

"5G-L1-UL" is changed to "DU(L1)" since this version as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_20317\_replaced([11094116](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmZaYfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). (Note kept only in this version)

[End CB007595-B]

#### 2.4.2.6.0-3 Precoding matrix definition Codebook supported configurations TDD FR1 (ID: 11103400)

**2.4.2.6.0-3.0-4**  (ID: `13336065`)

Precoding matrix definition
DU and Radio shall support below configurations for different L1 HWs:

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  | coherent UEs ([11103324](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knuQH_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) | non-coherent UEs Rel15 ([11103365](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knu3IvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) | non-coherent UEs Rel16 ([11103365](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knu3IvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) |
| Loner L1 HW | supported | supported | supported |
| Loki L1 HW | supported | supported | supported |
| Thor L1 HW | supported | supported | supported |
| Thor (Radio 7-2e) | supported | supported | supported |
| GPU L1 HW | [CB016066-C] supported [End CB016066-C] | [CB016066-C] supported [End CB016066-C] | - |
| **First feature** |  |  | 5GC002396-B |

Precoding matrix definition

---

### 2.4.2.7 Decoder configuration

#### 2.4.2.7.1 Overview MCSs for 64-QAM (CP-OFDM)

##### 2.4.2.7.1.0-1 Overview MCSs for 64-QAM (CP-OFDM) MCSs for 64-QAM (CP-OFDM) (ID: 11103434)

**2.4.2.7.1.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11103437`)

Overview MCSs for 64-QAM (CP-OFDM)
MCS is used at the DU to select appropriate threshold inside phase noise estimation algorithm.

MCS indexes 0 to 28 are supported in 64-QAM table, 3GPP 38214 5.1.3.1-1. For L1 HW support, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1008\_replaced([11103440](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knveOfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| MCS Index | Modulation |
| 0 -> 9 | QPSK |
| 10 -> 16 | 16QAM |
| 17 -> 28 | 64QAM |

In practice, from the selected MCS value, 5G-L2-PS computes all code block segmentation parameters (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_6874\_replaced([11192598](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvlypfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and send them to 5G-L1-UL, together with the modulation Order, the number of layers and the redundancy version index.

---

#### 2.4.2.7.2 Supported MCSs for 64-QAM (CP-OFDM)

##### 2.4.2.7.2.0-1 Supported MCSs for 64-QAM (CP-OFDM) Supported MCSs for 64-QAM (CP-OFDM) for SU receiver (ID: 11103443)

**2.4.2.7.2.0-1.0-6**  (ID: `13465090`)

Supported MCSs for 64-QAM (CP-OFDM)
Supported configurations regarding 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1006\_replaced([11103434](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knveN_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) are as follow :

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **L1 HW** | **TDD FR1** | **FDD FR1** | **TDD FR2** | **First Feature** |
| Loner L1 HW | supported | supported | supported |  |
| Loki L1 HW | supported | supported | supported |  |
| Thor L1 HW | supported | Supported | Supported | CB007487-B CB007487-C CB010576-A |
| GPU L1 HW | supported | [CB016062-AD]  supported  [End CB016062-AD] | - | CB016066-C |

---

##### 2.4.2.7.2.0-2 Supported MCSs for 64-QAM (CP-OFDM) Supported MCSs for 64-QAM (CP-OFDM) for MU receiver (ID: 11103458)

**2.4.2.7.2.0-2.0-3**  (ID: `13398857`)

Supported MCSs for 64-QAM (CP-OFDM)
Supported configurations regarding 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1006\_replaced([11103434](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knveN_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) are as follow :

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **L1 HW** | **TDD FR1** | **FDD FR1** | **TDD FR2** | **First Feature** |
| Loner L1 HW | - | - | - |  |
| Loki L1 HW | supported | - | - | CB008360-A |
| Thor L1 HW | supported | **-** | - | CB009028-5G-A |
| GPU L1 HW | supported | - | - | CB016061-B2 |

---

#### 2.4.2.7.3 Overview reserved MCSs for 64-QAM (CP-OFDM)

##### 2.4.2.7.3.0-1 Overview reserved MCSs for 64-QAM (CP-OFDM) Reserved MCSs for 64-QAM (CP-OFDM) (ID: 11103469)

**2.4.2.7.3.0-1.0-4**  (ID: `11103479`)

Overview reserved MCSs for 64-QAM (CP-OFDM)
The DU shall use the MCS to select appropriate threshold inside phase noise estimation algorithm.

MCS indexes 29-31 are supported in 64-QAM table, 3GPP 38.214 5.1.3.1-1.

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| MCS Index | Modulation |
| 29 | QPSK |
| 30 | 16QAM |
| 31 | 64QAM |

For UL MU MIMO, reserved MCs shall be requested as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_9571\_replaced([11148009](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksI2APvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

For L1 HW support, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_10848\_replaced([11103482](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knwFRvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

NOTE: In practice, from the selected MCS value (see 5G\_UP\_8169\_replaced([11363792](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_udFGffvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)),5G\_UP\_7896\_replaced([11364127](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_udHiv_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), L2(DU) computes all code block segmentation parameters (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_6874\_replaced([11192598](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvlypfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and send them to L1(DU), together with the modulation Order, the number of layers and the redundancy version index.

[Before CB009514-F]

Note: Reserved MCSs is not supported in eCPRI 7-2e.

[End CB009514-F]

#### 2.4.2.7.5 Overview MCSs for 256-QAM (CP-OFDM)

##### 2.4.2.7.5.0-1 Overview MCSs for 256-QAM (CP-OFDM) MCSs for 256-QAM (CP-OFDM) (ID: 11103505)

**2.4.2.7.5.0-1.0-3**  (ID: `11103512`)

Overview MCSs for 256-QAM (CP-OFDM)
The DU shall use the MCS to select appropriate threshold inside phase noise estimation algorithm.

The selection of the table depends on MCS-Table-PUSCH at RRC level which is set depending on UE - capabilities and 256-QAM support at gNB side.

MCS indexes 0 to 27 are also supported in 256-QAM table,, 3GPP 38214 5.1.3.1-2. For L1 HW support, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1015\_replaced([11103515](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knwsVPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| MCS Index | Modulation |
| 0 -> 4 | QPSK |
| 5 -> 10 | 16QAM |
| 11 -> 19 | 64QAM |
| 20 -> 27 | 256QAM |

NOTE: In practice, from the selected MCS value (see 5G\_UP\_8169\_replaced([11363792](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_udFGffvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)),5G\_UP\_7896\_replaced([11364127](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_udHiv_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), L2(DU) computes all code block segmentation parameters (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_6874\_replaced([11192598](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvlypfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and send them to L1(DU), together with the modulation Order, the number of layers and the redundancy version index.

Note: No change to PTRS and DMRS configuration when 256QAM in enabled (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_795\_replaced([11101751](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knbVJ_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_UP\_ALG\_800\_replaced([11101782](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knb8MPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_UP\_ALG\_810\_replaced([11101852](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kncjQvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

[Before CB010708-A]

Note2: Uplink 256QAM is not supported in eCPRI 7.2e.

[End CB010708-A]

---

**2.4.2.7.7.0-1.0-4**  (ID: `11103545`)

Overview reserved MCSs for 256-QAM (CP-OFDM)
The DU shall use the MCS to select appropriate threshold inside phase noise estimation algorithm.

MCS indexes 28-31 are supported in 256-QAM table, 3GPP 38.214 5.1.3.1-2.

| **MCS Index** | **Modulation** |
| 28 | QPSK |
| 29 | 16QAM |
| 30 | 64QAM |
| 31 | 256QAM |

For UL MU MIMO, reserved MCs shall be requested as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_9571\_replaced([11148009](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksI2APvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

For L1 HW support, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_10856\_replaced([11103547](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knxTYfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

NOTE: In practice, from the selected MCS value (see 5G\_UP\_8169\_replaced([11363792](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_udFGffvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)),5G\_UP\_7896\_replaced([11364127](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_udHiv_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), L2(DU) computes all code block segmentation parameters (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_6874\_replaced([11192598](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvlypfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and send them to L1(DU), together with the modulation Order, the number of layers and the redundancy version index.

Note: **[Before CB009514-F]Reserved MCSs and**[End CB009514-F] UL MU MIMO is not supported in eCPRI 7-2e.\*\*\*\*

#### 2.4.2.7.9 Overview MCSs for 64-QAM (DFT-s-OFDM)

##### 2.4.2.7.9.0-1 Overview MCSs for 64-QAM (DFT-s-OFDM) MCSs for DFT-s-OFDM (ID: 11103572)

**2.4.2.7.9.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11103575`)

Overview MCSs for 64-QAM (DFT-s-OFDM)
The DU shall use the MCS to select appropriate threshold inside phase noise estimation algorithm.

when transform precoding is enabled 5G-L1-UL should support MCS 0-27 according 3GPP specification ( i.e. 38214 table 6.1.4.1-1, see 5G\_UP\_IF\_2260\_replaced([11518479](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_tUp2GPv1Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))). Please see supported configuration at 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1023\_replaced([11103577](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knxTbPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| **MCS Index** | **Modulation** |
| 0 -> 1 | Pi/2-BPSK or QPSK |
| 2 -> 9 | QPSK |
| 10 -> 16 | 16QAM |
| 17 -> 27 | 64QAM |

**Notes:**

* Modulation for MCS range0...1 depends on OAM configuration and UE Capability**. CP-RT notifies L2-PS which one to use with tp-pi2BPSK (see 5G\_CP\_IF\_RRC\_4026\_replaced([11349792](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_wXy8LfvjEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))**

---

**2.4.2.7.11.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11103601`)

The DU shall use the MCS to select appropriate threshold inside phase noise estimation algorithm.

MCS indexes 28-31 are supported in 64-QAM 3GPP 38.214 table 6.1.4.1-1 for PUSCH with transform precoding (for L1 HW support see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_11027\_replaced([11103603](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knx6d_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| MCS Index | Modulation |
| 28 | Pi/2-BPSK or QPSK |
| 29 | QPSK |
| 30 | 16QAM |
| 31 | 64QAM |

NOTE: In practice, from the selected MCS value (see 5G\_UP\_8169\_replaced([11363792](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_udFGffvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)),5G\_UP\_7896\_replaced([11364127](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_udHiv_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), L2(DU) computes all code block segmentation parameters (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_6874\_replaced([11192598](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvlypfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and send them to L1(DU), together with the modulation Order, the number of layers and the redundancy version index.

#### 2.4.2.7.13 Overview MCSs for qam64LowSE (CP-OFDM)

##### 2.4.2.7.13.0-1 Overview MCSs for qam64LowSE (CP-OFDM) MCSs for qam64LowSE (CP-OFDM) (ID: 11103616)

**2.4.2.7.13.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11103619`)

Overview MCSs for qam64LowSE (CP-OFDM)
The DU shall use the MCS to select appropriate threshold inside phase noise estimation algorithm.

When qam64LowSE MCS table is selected, MCS indexes 0 to 31 shall be supported according to Table Table 5.1.3.1-3 in 38.214. For L1 HW support, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_14966\_replaced([11103622](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knx6fvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| MCS Index | Modulation |
| 0 -> 14 | QPSK |
| 15 -> 20 | 16QAM |
| 21 -> 28 | 64QAM |
| 29 | QPSK |
| 30 | 16QAM |
| 31 | 64QAM |

Note: MCS 29~31 are the reserved MCS which could be used for retransmission only. When such MCS is configured, modulation order is still defined by this MCS index, but TBS and BG are derived from initial transmission.

Note: Table 6.1.4.1-2 in 38.214 is not supported, that is the qam64LowSE MCS table and transforming precoding (DFT-s\_OFDM) can not be configured simultanously for a UE, see 5G\_UP\_3904\_replaced([11360770](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucw9ZPvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for details.

NOTE: In practice, from the selected MCS value (see 5G\_UP\_8169\_replaced([11363792](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_udFGffvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)),5G\_UP\_7896\_replaced([11364127](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_udHiv_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), L2(DU) computes all code block segmentation parameters (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_6874\_replaced([11192598](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvlypfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and send them to L1(DU), together with the modulation Order, the number of layers and the redundancy version index.

---
