#### 2.4.2.7.15 TBS size vs number of layers

##### 2.4.2.7.15.0-1 TBS size vs number of layers TBS size vs number of layers for SU receiver (ID: 11103632)

**2.4.2.7.15.0-1.0-4**  (ID: `13336067`)

TBS size vs number of layers
DU shall use the number of layers (sent by higher layer, 5G-L2-PS), the MCS index and number of VRB to determine the TBS size for the demodulation on PUSCH.

DU shall support for

* Loner, Loki and Thor L1HW: Both 1 and 2 layers.

- **[CB016066-C] GPU L1 HW: Both 1 and 2 layers are supported for TDD FR1.**[End CB016066-C]\*\*\*\*

---

##### 2.4.2.7.15.0-2 TBS size vs number of layers TBS size vs number of layers for MU receiver (ID: 11103642)

**2.4.2.7.15.0-2.0-4**  (ID: `13398964`)

TBS size vs number of layers
~~The number of layers is sent from 5G-L2-PS. The number of layers is needed to get the TBS size for the demodulation on PUSCH, in addition to MCS and number of VRB.~~

For MU Receiver, DU shall use the number of layers (programmed by higher layer, 5G-L2-PS), the MCS index and number of VRB to determine the TBS size for the demodulation on PUSCH

For a given UE, DU shall support for:

* Loki and Thor L1 HW FR1 TDD: only 1 layer for MU receiver for C4 subcells (cf. [11103308](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knuQGfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

- Thor **[CB016061-B2] and GPU**[End CB016061-B2] L1 HW FR1 TDD: up to 2 layers are supported for MU receiver for C8 subcells (cf. [11103308](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knuQGfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).\*\*\*\*

---

### 2.4.2.8 Rank and TPMI computation on DMRS

#### 2.4.2.8.1 DMRS SU MIMO metrics for a given UE

##### 2.4.2.8.1.0-1 DMRS SU MIMO metrics for a given UE Rank computation (ID: 11103657)

**2.4.2.8.1.0-1.0-6**  (ID: `12504223`)

DMRS SU MIMO metrics for a given UE
The DU**or Radio**shall compute the UL rank. The rank metric computation is described in section 2.9 of the algorithm specification  (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_695\_replaced([11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) [11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) ).\*\*\*\*

Per RX antenna SNR is used in RI selection. The PBoost  is used for computation of the SNR see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_6716\_replaced([11103708](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knzvpfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) ([11103708](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knzvpfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) ).

When PUSCH is received with spatial multiplexing Rank 2:

DU or Radio shall compute short term rank that is used for Long Term UL Rank computation as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_19663\_replaced([11139919](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krq78PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), [11139919](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krq78PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9))

In case of MU receiver in C8, **[CB008235-D-UPD002], C16'4, and C16'8** [End CB008235-D-UPD002] when NRCELL.actPmiSinrUlMuMimo ([11355703](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucTDWfvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) is false, **[Before CB008235-D-UPD002], C16'4, and C16'8**[End CB008235-D-UPD002] , DU **or Radio shall compute rank :*****\****\*

* using noise from all DMRS REs
* using own UE antennas or UE beams

In case of MU receiver (cf. 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8756\_replaced([11103056](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knrMwPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for supported L1 HW) in C8, **[CB008235-D-UPD002], C16'4, and C16'8** [End CB008235-D-UPD002] when NRCELL.actPmiSinrUlMuMimo ([11355703](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucTDWfvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) is true DU **[CB008235-D-UPD002] or Radio**[End CB008235-D-UPD002] shall perform rank computation:***\****\*

* using noise from own DMRS REs only; *- using all antennas.*

In summary:

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| NRCELL.actPmiSinrUlMuMimo | true **[Before CB008235-D-UPD002]  (supported by DU only)** [End CB008235-D-UPD002]\*\*\*\* | false |
| Frequency domain | Use own DMRS REs only | Use all DMRS REs |
| Space domain | Use all UE beams | Use own UE beams only |

The codebook used for the computation of the metrics is either 5G\_UP\_ALG\_992\_replaced([11103324](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knuQH_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) or 5G\_UP\_ALG\_994\_replaced([11103365](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knu3IvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

##### 2.4.2.8.1.0-2 DMRS SU MIMO metrics for a given UE Rank computation: number of subcarriers used and RI threshold (ID: 11103673)

**2.4.2.8.1.0-2.0-8**  (ID: `13337251`)

DMRS SU MIMO metrics for a given UE
This requirement is applicable to PUSCH ([11103657](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knyhjPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and SRS (see [11106367](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koXJUfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

The DU for Loner, Loki, Thor, and Radio (Thor 7-2e) L1 HWs shall compute UL Rank Indicator metric with following inputs:

* 6 subcarriers to calculate UL rank for all the allocated subcarriers (downsampling).
* subcarriers which are distributed in equal distance to calculate UL rank.
* appropriate RI threshold L1 HW specific:

- DU for Loner L1 HW shall use \*rdUlRiSelectionThresh described in [11110270](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpB3rPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

- DU for Loki **and Thor L1 HWs shall support configured value (as per [11140077](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krrjEPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and shall**expect to be programmed with VALID value (i.e. not INVALID, see [11140077](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krrjEPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

* Radio (Thor  7-2e) shall use *rdUlRiSelectionThresh described in [11110270](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpB3rPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) except**for NRCELL\numberOfRxBeamforming= 16Rx**where Radio shall support configured value as per ([11140077](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krrjEPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). In this case Radio shall expect to be programmed with VALID value (i.e. not INVALID, see [11140077](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krrjEPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).***\***

The link level simulations are available on the link below: <https://nokia.sharepoint.com/sites/5Gdoc/AS_Tech_Spec/Forms/Tree_View.aspx?RootFolder=%2Fsites%2F5Gdoc%2FAS%5FTech%5FSpec%2FCFAMs%2F5GC0200%2D5GC0399%2F5GC000331%2D438%2DmMIMO%20Digital%20Beamforming%20%2D%2016%20stream%2Ftechnical%20material&FolderCTID=0x012000F0A0A3BA325F7648AD307E6959BA7376>

The simulation report is available at “[RI selection optimization.pptx](https://nokia.sharepoint.com/sites/5Gdoc/AS_Tech_Spec/CFAMs/5GC1800-5GC1999/5GC001981%20-%20L1%20algorithm%20changes%20-%205G19B/Simulation%20Reports/RI%20selection%20optimization.pptx)”.

The UL rank metric also depends on the IRC gain when available : for PUSCH refer to [11119457](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqKgGvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ), for SRS refer to [11119432](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqKgE_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)**. Simulations related to RI selection+IRC gain refer to: [PerformanceComparisonBetweenDifferentPMIRImethods.pptx](https://nokia.sharepoint.com/:p:/r/sites/ASCOP/AS%20Documents/FS1TAs-CFAMs/CB/CB009000%20-%20CB009499/CFAM/CB009363%208Rx%20IRC%20receiver%20ABIP%20FR1%20TDD/References/joint_RI_PMI_IRCgain/Performance%20comparison%20between%20different%20PMIRI%20methods.pptx?d=w9493ec4dab494ae3ba0cbf758b253f59&csf=1&web=1&e=YO1H2S)** .\*\*\*\*

[CB016066-C] DU for GPU L1 HW shall   
- compute and report UL Rank Indicator metric  
- support configured UL Rank Indicator value (as per [11140077](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krrjEPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))  
- support to be programmed with UL Rank Indicator VALID value (i.e. not INVALID, see [11140077](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krrjEPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) . [End CB016066-C]

---

##### 2.4.2.8.1.0-3 DMRS SU MIMO metrics for a given UE TPMI selection : metrics (ID: 11103694)

**2.4.2.8.1.0-3.0-6**  (ID: `12504324`)

DMRS SU MIMO metrics for a given UE
When PUSCH is received with spatial multiplexing Rank 2,

DU or Radio shall perform the computation of the TPMI.

DU**or Radio shall provide a short term rank:  
- a rank1 PMI and corresponding SINR,  
- a rank2 PMI and corresponding SINR  
so that 5G-L2-PS can schedule the appropriate PMI depending on the long term average rank.**

The rank1 PMI selection is the result of MECG metric. Supported values are [0..5].

The rank2 PMI selection is the result of Sum Layer Power (SLP) metric . Supported values are [0..2]

The codebook used for the computation of the metrics is either 5G\_UP\_ALG\_992\_replaced([11103324](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knuQH_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) or 5G\_UP\_ALG\_994\_replaced([11103365](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knu3IvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

The *P*Boost  is used for computation of the metrics see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_6716\_replaced([11103708](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knzvpfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).\*\*

When full power mode is configured for non-coherent UEs with Rel'16, PUSCH power scaling ratio is additional used for computation of the metrics see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_6818\_replaced([11103724](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knzvrPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Moreover, for non-coherent UEs with full power mode1 and under power limit radio conditions (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3231\_replaced([11139991](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krq7_fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), the SINR computation is updated as described in 5GMAX algorithm specification.

SNR calculation and TPMI selection are described in subclause 2.8 of 5GMax algorithm specification (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_695\_replaced([11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

In case of MU receiver in C8 [CB008235-D-UPD002], C16'4, and C16'8 [End CB008235-D-UPD002] when NRCELL.actPmiSinrUlMuMimo ([11355703](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucTDWfvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) is false**, [Before CB008235-D-UPD002] , C16'4** and C16'8 **[End CB008235-D-UPD002**], DU **or Radio shall compute TPMI:*****\****\*

* using noise from all DMRS REs
* using own UE antennas or UE beams

In case of MU receiver (cf. 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8756\_replaced([11103056](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knrMwPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for supported L1 HW) in C8 [CB008235-D-UPD002] , C16'4, and C16'8 [End CB008235-D-UPD002] when NRCELL.actPmiSinrUlMuMimo ([11355703](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucTDWfvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) is true DU [CB008235-D-UPD002] or Radio [End CB008235-D-UPD002] shall perform TPMI Computation:

* using noise from  own DMRS REs only;
* using all antennas or UE beams

In summary :

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| NRCELL.actPmiSinrUlMuMimo | true  [Before CB008235-D-UPD002]  (supported by DU only)  [End CB008235-D-UPD002] | false |
| Frequency domain | Use own DMRS REs only | Use all DMRS REs |
| Space domain | Use all UE beams | Use own UE beams only |

---

##### 2.4.2.8.1.0-4 DMRS SU MIMO metrics for a given UE RI/TPMI selection: Power Boosting (ID: 11103708)

**2.4.2.8.1.0-4.0-6**  (ID: `13365262`)

DMRS SU MIMO metrics for a given UE
For the SNR computation in and TPMI calculation,  the *P*Boost  corresponds to the linear ratio of received DMRS EPRE to the received PUSCH EPRE in the power domain is defined in 5GMax chapter 2.8.1 (see [11101303](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcoPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)),\*\*

DU shall determine  *P*Boost value only according to the number of DMRS groups without data (see 6.2.2 of 38.214), that equals to 2 in linear scaling for cdm group without data equals to 2 and to 1 in linear scaling for cdm group without data equals to 1.\*\*

With BeamForming Mode, for **NRCELL.numberOfRxBeamforming>= 4Rx**

or without BeamForming Mode: for 4RX PUSCH Receiver order **[CB014128-A, CB014128-B] or when NRBTS/NRCELL/act8RxNonBeamforming = true**[End CB014128-A, CB014128-B]\*\*\*\*

and only for IRC receiver(*), DU and Radio (for 7-2e) shall use value* P*Boost  ( linear scale) according to the algorithm in [11119457](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqKgGvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).*\*

(\*) For L1 HW supporting PUSCH IRC receiver, please refer to [11103001](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knp-p_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) for SU receiver and [11103053](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knqlvvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) for MU receiver.

---

##### 2.4.2.8.1.0-5 DMRS SU MIMO metrics for a given UE TPMI selection:  PUSCH power scaling (ID: 11103724)

**2.4.2.8.1.0-5.0-2**  (ID: `11103729`)

DMRS SU MIMO metrics for a given UE
Based on chapter 7.1 of 38.213, PUSCH power allocation for Rel15 requires PUSCH power to be scaled by a factor s,  which the ratio of a number of antenna ports with non-zero PUSCH transmission power over the maximum number of SRS ports, the resulting scaled power is then equally split amongst antenna ports transmitting PUSCH. For 2Tx UE, s=1 to 2 ports PUSCH transmission, s=1/2 to 1 port PUSCH transmission(resulting in a 3 dB power loss).

Based on 7.1 of 3GPP 38.213 R16, the PUSCH power scaling factor s =1 (doubled over 1/2) for 1 port PUSCH transmission when full power modes is configured on non-coherent UE Rel’16 (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_943\_replaced([11102886](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knpXkPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). In order to align full-power 1 port TPMIs with non-full power 1 port TPMIs and also indicate DU(L1) [CB007595-B] or Radio(L1) [End CB007595-B] the available TPMIs, the following ratio per 1Layer non-coherent TPMIs are applied on DU(L1) [CB007595-B] or Radio(L1) [End CB007595-B] for SNR calculation and TPMI selection,

PUSCH power scaling ratio = s\_fullPower/s\_nonFullPower,

with:

* s\_fullPower stands for the power scaling factor s of full power mode (see 38.213 section 7.1 and 5G\_UP\_3769\_replaced([11355420](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucQnHvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) for noncoherent 1Layer TPMI.
* s\_nonFullPower stands for the power scaling factor s of non-full power mode (see 38.213 section 7.1) for noncoherent 1Layer TPMI, including UE Rel'15 and UE Rel'16.

For different full power modes, the values of PUSCH power scaling ratio combining with the avialable 1Layer TPMI index are provided by L2-PS see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3231\_replaced([11139991](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krq7_fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

* PUSCH power scaling ratio is indicated by 5G-L2-PS with the value of {0, 1, 2}, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1037\_replaced([11103694](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knzInfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
* If PUSCH power scaling ratio for all 1Layer noncoherent TPMIs {0, 1, 2} are set to 0, then all of them are assummed to be invalid.

[CB007595-B]

Note : "5G-L1-UL" is changed to "DU(L1)" since this version as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_20317\_replaced([11094116](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmZaYfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). (Note kept only in this version)

[End CB007595-B]

---

##### 2.4.2.8.1.0-6 DMRS SU MIMO metrics for a given UE TPMI selection : number of subcarriers (ID: 11103732)

**2.4.2.8.1.0-6.0-5**  (ID: `13337404`)

DMRS SU MIMO metrics for a given UE
DU for Loner, Loki, Thor, Thor (Radio 7-2e) L1 HWs shall compute PMI metric based on 12 number of equidistant subcarriers (downsampling stage).

Note: the simulation report for rank2 PMI selection is available at [NR\_UplinkPMISelection.pptx](https://nokia.sharepoint.com/sites/5Gdoc/AS_Tech_Spec/CFAMs/5GC1800-5GC1999/5GC001981%20-%20L1%20algorithm%20changes%20-%205G19B/Simulation%20Reports/NR_UplinkPMISelection.pptx)

[CB016066-C] DU for GPU L1 HW shall compute PMI based on the third subcarrier from each PRB to reduce computation complexity. [End CB016066-C]

---

* and TPMI computation (5G\_UP\_ALG\_1037\_replaced([11103694](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knzInfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for PUSCH and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1521\_replaced([11106393](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koXwWPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for SRS)

#### 2.4.2.8.3 Supported metrics for MU receiver

##### 2.4.2.8.3.0-1 Supported metrics for MU receiver Rank and TPMI computation for FR1 TDD (ID: 11103779)

**2.4.2.8.3.0-1.0-7**  (ID: `13398973`)

Supported metrics for MU receiver
Supported configurations  are as follow for :

* Rank computation ([11103657](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knyhjPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) for PUSCH
* and TPMI computation ([11103694](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knzInfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for PUSCH

For a given UE,

 DU shall support only 1 layer in MU receiver for C4 subcells . Therefore rank and TPMI are not computed on DMRS of PUSCH.

 DU shall support up 2 layers is supported in MU receiver for C8 subcells. Therefore rank and TPMI are also computed on DMRS of PUSCH.

 Radio shall support up to **2 layers in MU receiver for C16’4** and C16'8 subcells. Therefore rank and TPMI are also computed on DMRS of PUSCH.\*\*\*\*

For MU receiver number of layers support depending on L1 board please refer to [11103308](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knuQGfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

For MU receiver number of layers support depending on subcell type please refer to [11102856](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knowhPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

* Rank computation ([11106370](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koXwUPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) for SRS) and TPMI computation ([11106393](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koXwWPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) for SRS)

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  | coherent UEs ([11103324](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knuQH_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) | non-coherent UEs Rel15 ([11103365](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knu3IvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) | non-coherent UEs Rel16 ([11103365](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knu3IvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) | **First feature** |
| Loner L1 HW | - | - | **-** |  |
| Loki L1 HW | supported | supported | supported | CB008360-A |
| Thor L1 HW | supported | supported | supported | CB009028-5G-A |
| Thor ( Radio 7-2e) | supported | supported | supported | CB010708-C |
| GPU L1 HW | supported | supported | - | CB016061-B2 |

---

### 2.4.2.9 UCI reporting on PUSCH

#### 2.4.2.9.1 Overview

##### 2.4.2.9.1.0-1 Overview Periodic CSI reporting (ID: 11103804)

**2.4.2.9.1.0-1.0-3**  (ID: `11103808`)

Overview
For FDD and TDD FR1, when the cell is configured with long PUCCH (i.e. NIDD parameter MRBTS/NRBTS/NRCELL/pucchModeSelect set to longPucch), the UE shall report its periodic CSI on PUSCH instead of PUCCH when this UE is sending data on PUSCH in the same slot, see [11350221](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ublRofvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) and [11350234](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ublRp_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ). For this P-CSI report, CSI part1 only or CSI part1 with HARQ-A/N are supported.

DU shall support Max UCI Payload for Channel State Information (CSI) framework ([11351771](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubw32fvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) as per 3gpp (refer to "CSI Only" in [11508536](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_tTJaM_v1Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for:  
- CSI Acquisition to obtain information for scheduling decisions and link adaptation (including selection of MIMO transmission parameters),   
- Beam Management (if beam management is based on UE measurement of gNB transmitted physical signals) - to obtain information on optimal UE beam (cf. [11351771](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubw32fvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

For P-CSI report the Max UCI on PUSCH Payload bits is:   
- detailed in Overview in [12763238](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_6rUdo4PbEfCOGMMH109ICw).   
- supported by L1 HW in DU as referred in [11103821](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kn09z_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

For the mapping of UCI on PUSCH to physical resources, refer to [11350268](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ublRrvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ). For the UCI on PUSCH channel coding and rate matching, refer to [11350246](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ublRqvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) and [11350256](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ublRrPvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) respectively.

~~The supported CSI and HARQ-A/N configurations, the number of CSI reports and the number of bits for each CSI report are given in the next sections.~~

For more details, please refer to 3GPP TS 38.212 section 6.3.2.3 and 5GMax-5G New Radio Algorithm Specification section 2.3.10  (see [11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

**[CB007595-B]**Note: This requirement is applicable also for eCPRI 7-2e in the same way as for the other FH configurations where UCI on PUSCH dedicated functions/algorithms are performed in the DU **[End CB007595-B]**

---

##### 2.4.2.9.1.0-2 Overview Aperiodic CSI reporting (ID: 11103811)

**2.4.2.9.1.0-2.0-2**  (ID: `11103817`)

Overview
For FDD/TDD FR1, A-CSI on PUSCH is supported as explained below. Please, refer to 5G\_UP\_655\_replaced([11351957](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubytCfvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for more details about this RRM functionality.

For each report, the following configurations are supported:

* CSI part 1 and CSI part2 in the same report.
* CSI part2 could be ommitted; which means report with CSI part1

For one CSI report, the mapping order of CSI fields of this report is:

* CSI part1 with following configurations:
* CRI, RI, Wideband CQI (refer to 5G\_UP\_IF\_1135\_replaced([11509425](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_tTQu-vv1Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)))
* gNB shall support table 6.3.2.1.2-4 (Mapping order of CSI fields of one CSI report, CSI part 2 wideband) with following configurations:
* Wideband PMI information X1 and X2,  Layer Indicator (refer to 5G\_UP\_IF\_1135\_replaced([11509425](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_tTQu-vv1Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

More details about A-CSI report is given in 5G\_UP\_675\_replaced([11352052](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubz7IfvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

For the mapping of UCI on PUSCH to physical resources, refer to 5G\_UP\_11229\_replaced([11350268](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ublRrvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). For the UCI on PUSCH channel coding and rate matching, refer to 5G\_UP\_11037\_replaced([11350246](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ublRqvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_11113\_replaced([11350256](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ublRrPvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) respectively.

The supported CSI and HARQ-A/N configurations, the number of CSI reports and the number of bits for each CSI report are given in the next sections.

The supported UCI configurations for each A-CSI on PUSCH report is provided in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1089\_replaced([11103934](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kn2y8PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) per L1 variant.

For more details, please refer to  3GPP TS 38.212 section 6.3.2.3 and 5GMax-5G New Radio Algorithm Specification section 2.3.10 (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_695\_replaced([11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

[CB007595-B]

Note:

This requirement is applicable also for eCPRI 7-2e in the same way as for the other FH configurations where UCI on PUSCH dedicated functions/algorithms are performed in the DU

[End CB007595-B]

---

#### 2.4.2.9.2 CSI reporting on PUSCH - Number of bits

##### 2.4.2.9.2.1 CSI Part 1

###### 2.4.2.9.2.1.0-1 CSI Part 1 TDD FR1 (ID: 11103824)

**2.4.2.9.2.1.0-1.0-6**  (ID: `13457832`)

CSI Part 1
DU shall support payload bits for L1 HW as in below table with System Inputs as detailed in [12763238](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_6rUdo4PbEfCOGMMH109ICw?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)

|  |  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **L1 HW** | **Number of bits for CSI part 1 per report** |  |  | **Number of CSI reports for a given UE**\* | **First Feature** |
| CSI part1 with nb. of bits ≤ 2 (repetition coding) | CSI part1 with nb. of bits in [3…11] (Reed-Muller coding) | CSI part1 with nb. of bits in [12…50] (Polar coding) |  |  |  |
| Loner L1 HW | - | supported\*\* | supported\*\* | 1 |  |
| Loki L1 HW | - | supported\*\* | supported\*\* | 1 |  |
| Thor L1 HW | - | supported\*\* | supported\*\* | 1 | CB007487-B |
| GPU L1 HW | - | supported\*\* | supported\*\* | 1 | CB016066-C |

**\*NOTE: For P-CSI reported on PUSCH (due the overlapping in time domain of P-CSI on long PUCCH with PUSCH), only 1 report is supported because CSI associated to multiple CCs are encoded within the same sequence.**

\*\*DU shall support P-CSI for CSI framework (see [11103804](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kn09yPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) with max UCI on PUSCH payload bits:  
- for Loner, Loki, Thor  and GPU   L1 HW with:

* no bundling
* up to 13bits or up to 19 bits for CSI Acquisition depending on System Inputs as detailed in [12764792](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_08wlAISvEfCOGMMH109ICw).
* up to 21 bits for Beam Management with System Inputs as detailed in [12764908](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_GGPRp4S0EfCOGMMH109ICw).
* for Loki and Thor L1 HW only, with bundling, up to 40 bits.

**\*\* DU for Loki and Thor [CB016061-B1] and GPU [End CB016061-B1] L1 HW shall support A-CSI (see also [11103811](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kn09y_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) and [11160598](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktPCOPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) with:**

* up to 8 bits: up to 2 CRI bits + up to 2 RI bits + up to 4 wideband CQI bits  (see 38.212 Table 6.3.2.1.2-3)

From E2E perspective, the DU for Loki **and Thor** **L1 HW shall support CSI Part1 processing if programmed (refer to [11197037](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwHXHPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) as function of "Minimum allowed PUSCH PRBs with UCI" (refer to [11197037](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwHXHPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) : with PUSCH grant of 1 or 2 PRBs number smaller than Minimum allowed PUSCH PRBs with UCI (e.g. 4), CSI Part1 report is not programmed to PUSCH Receiver in DU, for all L1 HWs.**

---

###### 2.4.2.9.2.1.0-2 CSI Part 1 FDD FR1 (ID: 11103838)

**2.4.2.9.2.1.0-2.0-9**  (ID: `13478662`)

CSI Part 1
DU shall support payload bits for L1 HW as in below table with System Inputs as detailed in [12763238](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_6rUdo4PbEfCOGMMH109ICw?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)

|  |  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **L1 HW** | **Number of bits for CSI part 1 per report** |  |  | **Number of CSI reports for a given UE**\* | **First Feature** |
| CSI part1 with nb. of bits ≤ 2 (repetition coding) | CSI part1 with nb. of bits in [3…11] (Reed-Muller coding) | CSI part1 with nb. of bits in [12…50] (Polar coding) |  |  |  |
| Loner L1 HW | - | supported\*\* | Supported\*\* | 1 | CB009854-B |
| Loki L1 HW | - | supported\*\* | Supported\*\* | 1 | CB009854-B |
| Thor L1 HW | - | supported\*\* | Supported\*\* | 1 | CB007487-D |
| GPU L1 HW | - | supported\*\* | supported\*\* | 1 | CB016406-A |

**\*NOTE: For P-CSI reported on PUSCH (due the overlapping in time domain of P-CSI on long PUCCH with PUSCH),  only 1 report is supported because CSI  associated to multiple CCs are encoded within the same sequence.**

**DU shall support P-CSI for CSI framework (see [11103804](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kn09yPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) with max UCI on PUSCH payload bits for Loner, Loki, Thor**[CB016062-AD**] and GPU**[End CB016062-AD**] L1HW:****\***\*

with no bundling:

* up to 11 bits or up to 19 bits**for CSI Acquisition depending on System Inputs as detailed in [12764792](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_08wlAISvEfCOGMMH109ICw).**
* up to 21 bits for Beam Management with System Inputs as detailed in [12764908](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_GGPRp4S0EfCOGMMH109ICw).

with bundling:

* up to 40 bits with 19 bits for CSI Acquisition and 21 bits for Beam Management.

***\**\*DU shall support for Loki, Loner, Thor**[CB016062-AD**] and GPU**[End CB016062-AD**] L1 HW, A-CSI (see also [11103811](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kn09y_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) and [11097374](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_km3UevvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) ) with:*****\****\*\*\*

* up to 6 bits: up to 2 RI bits + up to 4 wideband CQI bits (see 38.212 Table 6.3.2.1.2-3).

From E2E perspective, the DU  shall support CSI Part1 processing if programmed  (refer to [11197037](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwHXHPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) as function of "Minimum allowed PUSCH PRBs with UCI" (refer to [11197037](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwHXHPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)): with PUSCH grant of 1 or 2 PRBs number smaller than Minimum allowed PUSCH PRBs with UCI (e.g. 4), CSI Part1 report is not programmed to PUSCH Receiver in DU, for ~~al~~~~l~~ Loki and Thor L1 HWs.

11103866:
CSI Part 2
For FDD/TDD FR1:

When the UE reports A-CSI on PUSCH with both CSI part1 and CSI part2, 5G-L1-UL shall proceed as below for each A-CSI report. The maximum number of reports is given in the following.

* 5G-L1-UL starts decoding CSI part1 and determines the RI from the decoded bits (see 38.212 Table 6.3.2.1.2-3).
* 5G-L1-UL computes the number of CSI Part 2 bits ([image-0][image\_desc]The image contains a blurry, close-up view of the letters "A" and "(a)". The letters are in a blue and orange gradient, with a slight blur effect. The background is black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_2350.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]) taking into account the RI value from CSI part1 (see above bullet)
* Before CSI Part2 decoding, 5G-L1-UL shall ensure that CSI part2 is reported with coding rate in the expected range (Add reference later).

CSI Part 2
For FDD/TDD FR1, the UCI bit sequence is arranged as:

Let's denote:

* numOfCsiReports: the number of CSI reports for a given UE

The Jth CSI Part 2 report (with j = 1....numOfCsiReports) is constructed from wideband ([image-0][image\_desc]The image contains the text "Ocsi-2 wideband j". The text is in a dark color against a light background. The font is a sans-serif font. The letters are slightly blurred.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_2352.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]) parts. Then, the UCI bit sequence associated to the CSI Part2 reported by the UE is constructed of multiple CSI reports as shown in the below (see 38.212 Table 6.3.2.1.2-7):

Table 1

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| UCI bit sequence | CSI report number |
| [image-1][image\_desc]The image is blurry and pixelated, making it difficult to discern specific details. However, it appears to contain abstract shapes and colors, possibly representing a stylized illustration or graphic design. There are hints of what might be characters or figures, but they are too indistinct to identify. On the right side of the image, there are three distinct horizontal bars. Each bar is divided into two colored sections: a lighter, golden-brown color on the left and a blue color on the right. Where

* OCSI-2 wideband 1 corresponds to the wideband component of the of the CSI report number 1
* OCSI-2 wideband numOfCsiReports corresponds to the wideband component of the of the CSI report number

###### 2.4.2.9.2.2.0-3 CSI Part 2 TDD FR1 (ID: 11103894)

**2.4.2.9.2.2.0-3.0-4**  (ID: `13397688`)

CSI Part 2

|  |  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **L1 HW** | **Number of bits for CSI part 2 per report** **(see also [11103862](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kn1k3fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) and [11103869](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kn1k3_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))** |  |  | **Number of CSI reports for a given UE**\* | **First Feature** |
| CSI part2 with nb. of bits ≤ 2 (repetition coding) | CSI part2 with nb. of bits in [3…11] (Reed-Muller coding) | CSI part2 with nb. of bits in [12…50] (Polar coding) |  |  |  |
| Loner L1 HW | - | - | **-** | - |  |
| Loki L1 HW | supported for conformance tests   \*\* (Note1) | supported\*\* | ~~-~~ - | 1 | CB007220-A |
| Thor L1 HW | supported for conformance tests    \*\* (Note1) | supported\*\* | ~~-~~ | 1 | CB008325-5G-CA |
| GPU | supported for conformance tests  \*\* (Note1) | supported\*\* | ~~-~~ | 1 | CB016061-B1 |

**\*\* For Loki, Thor [CB016061-B1] and GPU [End CB016061-B1] L1 HW, A-CSI is also supported with:**

* **up to 11bits: up to 2 LI bits + up to 8 X1 bits (see below for more details) + up to 1X2 bit (see 38.212 Table 6.3.2.1.2-4)**

For X1 bits: up to i1,1 3bits + up to  i1,2 3bits + up to  i1,3 2bits

Note1: From E2E perspective,

* the number of bits for A-CSI part2 is always >=3 (see [11159329](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktI7lPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and UP\_ALG\_3185)

**Note2**: If *NRCELL.actEnhCtrlChannelTrans = True and if* NRCELL.enhCtrlChannelTransMode = **5 or 6** (see [11353784](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucB9oPvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), LI report is supported, see [11160044](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktN0EfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and [11171756](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuHL8PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).***\****\*\*\*

* **the DU shall support CSI Part2 processing if programmed (refer to [11197037](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwHXHPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) as function of "Minimum allowed PUSCH PRBs with UCI" (refer to [11197037](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwHXHPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))): with PUSCH grant of 1 or 2 PRBs number smaller than Minimum allowed PUSCH PRBs with UCI (e.g. 4 PRBs), CSI Part2 is not programmed to PUSCH Receiver in DU, for ~~all~~ Loki and Thor L1 HWs.**

Refer to [11160609](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktPCOvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) for more details

---

###### 2.4.2.9.2.2.0-4 CSI Part 2 FDD FR1 (ID: 11103905)

**2.4.2.9.2.2.0-4.0-4**  (ID: `13465094`)

CSI Part 2

|  |  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **L1 HW** | **Number of bits for CSI part 2 per report** **(see also [11103862](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kn1k3fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and [11103869](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kn1k3_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))** |  |  | **Number of CSI reports for a given UE**\* | **First Feature** |
| CSI part2 with nb. of bits ≤ 2 (repetition coding) | CSI part2 with nb. of bits in [3…11] (Reed-Muller coding) | CSI part2 with nb. of bits in [12…50] (Polar coding) |  |  |  |
| Loner L1 HW | supported | supported\*\* | - | 1 | CB007798-A |
| Loki L1 HW | supported | supported\*\* | **-** | 1 | 5GC001817-A |
| Thor L1 HW | supported | supported\*\* | **-** | 1 | CB008325-5G-BA |
| GPU L1 HW | supported | supported\*\* | - | 1 | CB016062-AD |

***\**\*For Loki, Loner  and Thor L1 HW, A-CSI is also supported with:**\*\*

* up to 5bits:  up to 3 X1 bits (see below for more details) + up to 2 X2 bit (see 38.212 Table 6.3.2.1.2-4)
* **If *NRCELL.actEnhCtrlChannelTrans = True and if *NRCELL.enhCtrlChannelTransMode = 5 or 6* *(see [11353784](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucB9oPvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)),* \*For  Loki  and Thor L1 HW only, up to 7 bits:  up to 2 LI bits,  up to 3 X1 bits (see below for more details) + up to 2 X2 bit (see 38.212 Table 6.3.2.1.2-4).*****\***

Refer to [11139272](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krmDcvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) and [11160609](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktPCOvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) for more details.

***\**\*For Loki, Loner and Thor L1 HW, A-CSI is also supported with:**\*\*

* up to 5 bits for FDD + FDD or TDD CA

**From E2E perspective, the DU ~~for Loki~~~~**L1 HW**~~ shall support CSI Part2 processing if programmed  (refer to [11197037](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwHXHPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) as function of "Minimum allowed PUSCH PRBs with UCI"  (refer to [11197037](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwHXHPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))  : with PUSCH grant of 1 or 2 PRBs number smaller than Minimum allowed PUSCH PRBs with UCI (e.g. 4), CSI Part2 is not programmed to PUSCH Receiver in DU, for ~~all~~ Loki**and Thor L1 HWs.\*\*\*\*

#### 2.4.2.9.5 Conformance tests for UCI multiplexed on PUSCH

##### 2.4.2.9.5.0-1 Conformance tests for UCI multiplexed on PUSCH TDD FR1 (ID: 11103955)

**2.4.2.9.5.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `11103960`)

Conformance tests for UCI multiplexed on PUSCH
There are two cases defined in 38.141:

* 7 bits:  5 bits in CSI part 1, 2 bits in CSI part 2,
* 40 bits: 20 bits in CSI part 1, 20 bits in CSI part 2.

Supported configurations are:

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| L1 HW | 7 bits:  5 bits in CSI part 1, 2 bits in CSI part 2 | 40 bits: 20 bits in CSI part 1, 20 bits in CSI part 2 |
| Loner | - | - |
| Loki | Supported | - |
| vDU\* | [Before CNI-103796-M] Supported [End CNI-103796-M] | - |
| Thor | [CB008325-5G-CA] Supported [End CB008325-5G-CA] |  |

[CNI-103796-M] \*vDU L1 HW is no more supported [End CNI-103796-M]

---

**2.4.2.9.5.0-2.0-2**  (ID: `11103968`)

Conformance tests for UCI multiplexed on PUSCH
There are two cases defined in 38.141:

* 7 bits:  5 bits in CSI part 1, 2 bits in CSI part 2,
* 40 bits: 20 bits in CSI part 1, 20 bits in CSI part 2.

Supported configurations are:

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| L1 HW | 7 bits:  5 bits in CSI part 1, 2 bits in CSI part 2 | 40 bits: 20 bits in CSI part 1, 20 bits in CSI part 2 |
| Loner | Supported | - |
| Loki | Supported | - |
| vDU | - | - |
| Thor | [CB008325-5G-BA] supported [End CB008325-5G-BA] |  |

### 2.4.2.10 UL HARQ management

#### 2.4.2.10.0-1 UL HARQ management UL HARQ (ID: 11103974)

**2.4.2.10.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11103976`)

UL HARQ management
UL HARQ Management shall be supported.

To avoid any UE context management in either 5G-L1-UL or 5G-L1-DL, but leaving some flexibility in the exact memory mapping to 5G-L1-UL, the responsibility split is defined as follows:

* UL HARQ context defines the mapping between HARQ process index and the memory address of a HARQ memory block in 5G-L1-UL memory within an L1 instance.
* UL HARQ context is stored in a memory buffer so that 5G-L1-UL can determine the address where to read/write the rate dematched softbits.
* 5G-L1-UL would decide the allocation strategy of the UL HARQ memory.
* 5G-L1-UL is expected to ignore the softbits stored in the memory if requested by 5G-L2-PS
* 5G-L1-UL is expected to combine received softbits of current retransmission, with the softbits of the HARQ memory.
* 5G-L1-UL is never expected to flush the content of the HARQ softbit memory.

5G-L1-UL shall support the HARQ combining of retransmissions.

---

HARQ reporting in UL conformance tests
As defined by 38.141-1/38.141-2, section D5 and D6, PUSCH HARQ feedback in UL performance tests (which are part of the UL conformance tests) shall be error free and done as a RF feedback or a digital feedback.

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a block diagram illustrating a test setup for a Base Station (BS) under test. The diagram shows the flow of signals and feedback mechanisms.
On the left, a "BS tester" block is connected to a splitter. The splitter directs the signal to two "Channel Simulator" blocks. Below the "Channel Simulator" blocks, there are two "AWGN Generator" blocks. The outputs of the "Channel Simulator" blocks and the "AWGN Generator" blocks are then fed into two separate splitters.
These splitters then direct the signals to the "Base Station under test". Specifically, one splitter's output goes to "RX A" of the base station, and the other splitter's output goes to "RX B".
There is also a feedback loop labeled "HARQ Feedback (only for PUSCH)" originating from the "Base Station under test" and connecting back to the "BS tester". This indicates a control or acknowledgment signal is sent back to the tester.
The overall diagram depicts a system where a BS tester simulates radio channel conditions and adds noise to signals before they are received by a Base Station under test. The HARQ feedback mechanism suggests a testing scenario involving hybrid automatic repeat request for the Physical Uplink Shared Channel (PUSCH).
--- OCR Start ---
Channel
Simulator
Base Station
under test
RX A
BS
tester
Channel
Simulator
RX B
AWGN
Generator
AWGN
Generator
HARQ Feedback
(only for PUSCH)
--- OCR End ---[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_2387.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

11103995:
HARQ reporting in UL conformance tests
If used Test Equpiment is from Rohde&Schwarz, 5G-L1-UL shall send HARQ feedback with serial model. When no feedback is available for the given slot (i.e. no PUSCH transmission in given slot), high voltage level signal is sent out.
Baud rates 115.2 kbps (bitrate115dot2k)  and 1.92 Mbps (bitrate1dot92M) shall be supported in this format.
HARQ-ACK shall be sent to the TE with signal sequence structure defined as below:
[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a timeline diagram illustrating data transmission or a communication protocol. The timeline progresses from left to right, indicated by the "Time" label at the bottom right. The diagram is segmented into colored blocks, each representing a specific state or data unit. A red line above the blocks indicates a signal level, which is high during the colored segments and low during the "Idle" periods.
The segments are labeled as follows:
\* **Idle**: These are periods where no data is being transmitted. They are represented by white space.
\* **SB**: Start Bit. These are light blue segments.
\* **Low**: These are yellow segments, likely representing data bits with a low value. There are four consecutive "Low" segments at the beginning.
\* **D0, D1, D2, D3**: These are orange segments, representing data bits D0 through D3.
\* **EB**: End Bit. These are light blue segments.
\* **D4, D5, D6**: These are orange segments, representing data bits D4 through D6.
\* **High**: These are yellow segments, likely representing data bits with a high value.
\* **D7, D8, D9, D10**: These are orange segments, representing data bits D7 through D10.
\* **D11, D12, D13**: These are orange segments, representing data bits D11 through D13.
\* **D14, D15**: These are orange segments, representing data bits D14 and D15.
\* **UN**: These are light gray segments, the meaning of which is not explicitly defined in the diagram but appears between data bits and an end bit.
The pattern observed is: Idle -> SB -> 4x Low -> D0-D3 -> EB -> Idle -> SB -> D4-D6 -> High -> D7-D10 -> EB -> Idle -> SB -> D11-D13 -> High -> D14-D15 -> UN -> UN -> EB -> Idle.
The diagram shows multiple cycles of data transmission, each starting with an "SB" (Start Bit) and ending with an "EB" (End Bit), with data bits in between. The "Idle" periods signify the absence of activity. The "High" and "Low" labels within the yellow segments suggest the state of the data bits. The "UN" segments are an anomaly in the otherwise consistent pattern of data bits and start/end bits.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_2389.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]
Where,
Idle = Always high
SB = Start bit, always low
EB = Stop bit, always high
Low = used for synchronization purposes
High = used for synchronization purposes
UN = unused
D0 to D15 = Data bits in the LSB-first manner. The meaning of each bits is described below:
 
| | | |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Bits | Description | Value |
| D15-D14 | BB selector which is used for baseband block selection. Maximum 4 HARQ ACK can be feedback to TE parallelly. | Set according to bbSelector  from Cell Setup. |
| D13-D11 | Message type selector. Indicate different HARQ ACK feedback mode. | Set to (D13, D12, D11) =(0, 0, 1) |
| D10-D2 | reserved | Set to 0 |
| D1-D0 | Redundancy Version | [0..3] - RV index associated to the current PUSCH grant. |
 
R&S user manual with the interface for 5G NR can be found here:
[image-1][image\_desc]The image displays a PDF file icon against a black background. The icon is a white rectangle with a red tab at the top left corner. The tab has the word "PDF" written in white capital letters. Below the tab, the Adobe Acrobat logo, a stylized red "A," is visible on the white rectangle.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_2389.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]
When officially available, it will be also visible under the link:
 
Note: Serial 3x8 Mode does not require Idle time (can be set to zero), since the first 4 LOW bits after the start bit are enough for synchronization.

For general description and signal sequence structure please see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1108\_replaced([11103990](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kn3aBPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
 
In case of Scenario X or Scenario Y, TAC (Timing Advance Command) shall be sent to Test Equipment together with HARQ-ACK. In this case following message format = 4 with below data format shall be used:

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bits | Description | Value | First Feature |
| D15-D14 | BB selector which is used for baseband block selection. Maximum 4 HARQ ACK can be feedback to TE parallelly. | Set according to bbSelector  from Cell Setup. |  |
| D13-D11 | Message type selector. Indicate different HARQ ACK feedback mode. | Set to (D13, D12, D11) =(1, 0, 0). |  |
| D10-D5 | Requested TA command for the baseband configured with the main BB selector. | Set to TAC value received in Pusch Receive Request from L2-PS. | CB008014-A |
| D4-D2 | reserved | Set to 0. |  |
| D1-D0 | Redundancy Version | [0..3] - RV index associated to the current PUSCH grant. |  |
|  |  |  |  |
| R&S user manual with the interface for 5G NR can be found here: |  |  |  |

The tab has the word "PDF" written in white capital letters. | When officially available, it will be also visible under the link: |  |  |  |
|  |  |  |  |

11104011:
HARQ reporting in UL conformance tests
If used Test Equipment is from Keysight, 5G-L1-UL shall send HARQ feedback with the signal with an LV TTL RS-232C type protocol. Baud rates 115.2 kbps (bitrate115dot2k) and 460.8 kbps (bitrate460dot8k) shall be supported in this format. Baud rate error of 2 to 3% is tolerated by the Test Equipment. 115.2 kpbs is used for FR1 TDD and FDD while 460.8 kpbs is used for FR2 TDD. Bit value of '1' is represented as 3.3V and bit value of '0' is represented as 0V.

HARQ report consists of 8 bits of payload and additional bits at 0V for the START and 3.3 V for the STOP of the transmission.

[image-0][image\_desc]The image depicts a timeline illustrating a sequence of events. The timeline is represented by a horizontal arrow labeled "Time" pointing to the right. Above the arrow, colored blocks represent different states or data segments.
From left to right, the timeline begins with a light blue block labeled "idle" in a vertical orientation. This is followed by a light blue block labeled "SB" in a vertical orientation. Next, there is a series of eight yellow blocks, each representing a data segment labeled "B0", "B1", "B2", "B3", "B4", "B5", "B6", and "B7", also in a vertical orientation. After "B7", there is another light blue block labeled "EB" in a vertical orientation. The timeline concludes with a final light blue block labeled "idle" in a vertical orientation.
The entire sequence of blocks is framed by thin red lines at the beginning and end, suggesting the start and end of a particular process or transmission. The blocks are arranged contiguously, indicating a continuous flow of time.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_2393.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

Where,

SB = Start bit, always low

EB = Stop bit, always

Payload is transmitted in LSB-first manner with following bits:

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Bits | Description | Value |
| b0 | New Data Indicator (NDI) | NDI \* For new transport block (successful reception of the previous one for given HARQ process ID): toggle bit compared to the previous report. \* For retransmission (unsuccessful reception of the previous one for given HARQ process iD): same value as in the previous report. |
| b1-b4 | Process ID | 0..15 |
| b5-b6 | Redundancy Version (RV) | 0..3 |
| b7 | Command type | '0' |

[image-1][image\_desc]The image displays a diagram illustrating the breakdown of an 8-bit byte, labeled B7 through B0. Below the byte, a red bracket indicates how these bits are grouped and what they represent.
The breakdown is as follows:
- **B7**: Labeled as "Fixed value (0)". This bit has a constant value of 0.
- **B6 and B5**: Grouped together and labeled as "RV (0 to 3)". These two bits represent a value ranging from 0 to 3.
- **B4, B3, B2, and B1**: Grouped together and labeled as "Process ID (0 to 15)". These four bits represent a Process ID, which can range from 0 to 15.
- **B0**: Labeled as "NDI". The specific meaning or range of this bit is not further elaborated in the diagram.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_2393.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]

---

[Before CNI-103796-M] For vDU following conformance test configuration is used for PUSCH performance scenario.
 
[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a block diagram illustrating a telecommunications system, likely related to 5G base station architecture.
The diagram shows the following components and their connections:
\* **eCPRI RU's**: Three "RRH" (Remote Radio Head) units are connected to a "ToRR switch (timing aware)".
\* **ToRR switch (timing aware)**: This switch receives connections from the RRHs and also connects to a "L1High/L2RT HARQ info generation and timing" block. It also receives "HARQ info" from an "SFP28" component.
\* **L1High/L2RT HARQ info generation and timing**: This block is labeled as "vDU" (virtualized Distributed Unit).
\* **SFP28**: This component receives "HARQ info" from the "ToRR switch". It is connected to a "LONER" component within an "ABIL" block.
\* **ABIL**: This block contains a "LONER" component and a "backplane". The backplane has "SIN" and "SOUT" ports. The "SIN" port receives "1PPS/ToD" (Pulse Per Second/Time of Day) from a "GNSS" (Global Navigation Satellite System) receiver via an "Airscale test cable". The "SOUT" port outputs "10MHz 100Hz HARQ" to the "ASIX" component.
\* **ASIX**: This component receives "HARQ" from the "ABIL" backplane and outputs "10MHz 100Hz HARQ" through the "Airscale test cable" to the "TEST EQUIPMENT".
\* **GM**: A "GM" (Grandmaster) clock is shown connected to a cloud symbol, which is labeled "PTP/SyncE" (Precision Time Protocol/Synchronous Ethernet). This GM clock is connected to the "ToRR switch".
\* **GNSS**: A GNSS receiver is shown connected to the "ABIL" backplane via the "Airscale test cable".
\* **TEST EQUIPMENT**: This is the final destination for the "10MHz 100Hz HARQ" signal.
The diagram uses different colored lines to represent data flow: blue lines for eCPRI connections, black lines for payload and HARQ info, and red lines for HARQ info and test signals.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_2397.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]
 
Note: In solution for vDU, L1Hi sends message with HARQ related information towards LONER where it is converted into serial model before delivered to Test Equipment.
File: Conformance test configuration for vDU.pptx
Folder:
 
[End CNI-103796-M]

---

##### 2.4.2.11.1.0-2 HARQ feedback for vDU HARQ feedback for Test Equpiment is from Rohde&amp;Schwarz (ID: 11104028)

**2.4.2.11.1.0-2.0-2**  (ID: `11104034`)

HARQ feedback for vDU
[Before CNI-103796-M] For vDU: If used Test Equpiment is from Rohde&Schwarz, 5G-L1-UL shall send HARQ info from vDU towards Test Equipment.

vDU Eth port is used to send HARQ info as depicted in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_6413\_replaced([11104017](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kn3aC_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Note: In solution for vDU, L1Hi sends message with HARQ related information towards LONER where it is converted into serial model before delivered to Test Equipment. More details on this mechanism are defined in CP3 requirements. [End CNI-103796-M]

---

##### 2.4.2.11.1.0-3 HARQ feedback for vDU HARQ feedback for Test Equpiment is from Keysight (ID: 11104035)

**2.4.2.11.1.0-3.0-2**  (ID: `11104041`)

HARQ feedback for vDU
[Before CNI-103796-M] For vDU: If used Test Equpiment is from Keysight, 5G-L1-UL shall send HARQ info from vDU towards Test Equipment.

vDU Eth port is used to send HARQ info as depicted in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_6413\_replaced([11104017](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kn3aC_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Note: In solution for vDU, L1Hi sends message with HARQ related information towards LONER where it is converted into serial model before delivered to Test Equipment. More details on this mechanism are defined in CP3 requirements.  [End CNI-103796-M]

Following timings towards Test Equipment shall be used when HARQ reporting in UL conformance tests is enabled.

R&S = Rohde & Schwarz

KS = Keysight

For FR1 TDD:

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a timing diagram illustrating different HARQ feedback options and latencies in a cellular network context, likely 5G NR. The diagram shows several horizontal timelines representing different processes or signals over time, marked by vertical dashed red lines indicating slot boundaries.
Here's a breakdown of the elements:
**Top Section:**
\* **AUD option 2 (slots):** A timeline indicating a duration labeled "AUD option 2 (slots)".
\* **AUD option 1 (slots):** A timeline below the previous one, labeled "AUD option 1 (slots)".
\* **k2 = 2 slots:** A label associated with the end of the "AUD option 1 (slots)" timeline.
\* **X = 1 slot (500 us) + T\_BB1ToAnt:** A timeline indicating a duration of one slot (500 microseconds) plus a transmission delay (T\_BB1ToAnt).
\* **Timing window for HARQ reponse for the NR-slot n+k2:** A longer timeline encompassing the "X = 1 slot..." duration, representing a HARQ response timing window.
\* **2 slots (1000 us) + T\_BB1ToAnt:** A timeline indicating a duration of two slots (1000 microseconds) plus the transmission delay.
**Middle Section (Green Text):**
This section details "R&S HARQ feedback" processes.
\* **R&S HARQ feedback timing window for the slot n+k2:** A timeline segment.
\* **0.1 slot:** A short duration within the timing window.
\* **H (option 2):** A label indicating a specific option for the HARQ feedback timing window.
\* **R&S HARQ feedback transmission latency:** A timeline segment.
\* **L:** A label representing latency.
\* **0.1 slot:** Another short duration.
\* **R&S HARQ feedback timing window for the slot n+k2:** Another timeline segment.
\* **H (option 1):** A label indicating a different option for the HARQ feedback timing window.
\* **R&S HARQ feedback transmission latency:** Another timeline segment.
\* **L:** Latency.
\* **0.1 slot:** Another short duration.
\* **NR-slot n (rio DCI over PDCCH):** A timeline indicating the current slot 'n', with a note about no DCI over PDCCH.
**Bottom Section (Blue Text):**
This section details "KS HARQ feedback" processes.
\* **KS HARQ feedback timing window for the slot n+k2:** A timeline segment.
\* **H (option 2):** A label indicating a specific option for the KS HARQ feedback timing window.
\* **KS k2 = k2 + 2:** A formula related to the timing.
\* **KS HARQ feedback transmission latency:** A timeline segment.
\* **L:** Latency.
\* **KS HARQ feedback timing window for the slot n+k2:** Another timeline segment.
\* **H (option 1):** A label indicating a different option for the KS HARQ feedback timing window.
\* **KS k2 = k2 + 1:** A formula related to the timing.
\* **KS HARQ feedback transmission latency:** Another timeline segment.
\* **L:** Latency.
**Rightmost Section:**
\* **OFDM symbol:** A visual representation of an OFDM symbol, depicted as a series of vertical bars.
\* **Slot boundary:** A label indicating the meaning of the dashed red lines.
The diagram visually compares different timing constraints and options for HARQ feedback, distinguishing between R&S and KS implementations, and between different options (option 1 and option 2) which affect the timing parameters like 'k2'. The diagram also shows the relationship between these feedback mechanisms and the overall NR slot structure.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_2406.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

Where:

"0.1 slot" is the time guard so that HARQ feedback can be properly assigned to a given slot in case of R&S TE.

T\_BB1ToAnt is assumed to be negligibly small (cable connection between gNB and RF of a few meters).

L (HARQ feedback transmission latency) for 16bits data and 14 bits overhead (assuming Idle period equal to 0);

H (HARQ feedback timing window length);

AUD is the Additional User Delay which is the configurable parameter and applicable only in Rohde&Schwarz Test Equipment.

KS k2 is the k2 parameter configurable at Keysight Test Equipment.

For Rohde&Schwarz, values of L, H and AUD are specified in the table below.

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  | TDD FR1 |  |
| L | 260.4 us |  |
| [(16 + 14) bits / 115.2 kbps] |  |  |
| H | 139.6 us |  |
| [500 - L - 2*0.1*slot] |  |  |
| AUD | Option 1 | Option 2 |
| -2 slots | -(2+1) = -3 slots |  |

Table below specifies the L, H and KS k2 values in case of Keysight TE usage.

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  | TDD FR1 |  |
| L | 86.8 us |  |
| [(8 + 2) bits / 115.2 kbps] |  |  |
| H | 313.2 us |  |
| [500 - L - 2*0.1*slot] |  |  |
| KS k2 | Option 1 | Option 2 |
| 3 | 4 |  |

---

Following timings towards Test Equipment shall be used when HARQ reporting in UL conformance tests is enabled.

R&S = Rohde & Schwarz

KS = Keysight

For FDD:

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a timing diagram illustrating HARQ feedback mechanisms in a wireless communication system. It depicts different time windows and latencies associated with HARQ feedback, comparing two options: "R&S HARQ" and "KS HARQ".
The diagram shows a timeline with several events and durations marked. Key elements include:
\* **Timing window for HARQ response for the NR-slot n+k2**: This is a general timing window.
\* **1000 us (1 slot) + T\_BB1ToAnt**: This represents a duration, likely related to processing or transmission.
\* **AUD option 1 (slots)**: This indicates a specific option for the AUD (Automatic Decode and Update) mechanism, measured in slots.
\* **500 us + T\_BB1ToAnt**: Another duration, possibly related to a different processing or transmission path.
\* **k2 = 2 slots**: This specifies a value for the parameter k2, indicating it's equal to 2 slots.
\* **0.1 slot**: A short duration of 0.1 slots is shown multiple times.
\* **R&S HARQ feedback timing window for the slot n+k2**: This is a specific timing window for the R&S HARQ option.
\* **H (option 1)**: This likely refers to a specific configuration or parameter within the R&S HARQ option.
\* **R&S HARQ feedback transmission latency L**: This represents the latency for R&S HARQ feedback transmission.
\* **NR-slot n (rio DCI over PDCCH)**: This indicates a specific slot 'n' and mentions DCI (Downlink Control Information) over PDCCH (Physical Downlink Control Channel).
\* **KS HARQ feedback timing window for the slot n+k2**: This is a specific timing window for the KS HARQ option.
\* **H**: Similar to the R&S HARQ, this might be a parameter.
\* **KS k2 = k2 + 1**: This shows a relationship for the KS HARQ's k2 parameter.
\* **KS HARQ feedback transmission latency L**: This represents the latency for KS HARQ feedback transmission.
\* **NR-slot n+k2 PUSCH channel**: This indicates the PUSCH (Physical Uplink Shared Channel) in slot n+k2, depicted as a series of vertical bars.
\* **OFDM symbol**: This is a label indicating an OFDM symbol, represented by a small rectangle.
\* **Slot boundary**: This is a label indicating the boundary of a slot, represented by a dashed vertical line.
The diagram uses dashed vertical lines to denote slot boundaries and event timings, and horizontal arrows to indicate durations and relationships between events. The two main HARQ feedback mechanisms, R&S HARQ and KS HARQ, are presented in parallel, highlighting their respective timing windows and latencies. The diagram also shows how these timings relate to specific slots (n and n+k2) and the transmission of data on the PUSCH channel.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_2408.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

Where:

"0.1 slot" is the time guard so that HARQ feedback can be properly assigned to a given slot in case of R&S TE.

T\_BB1ToAnt is assumed to be negligibly small (cable connection between gNB and RF of a few meters).

L (HARQ feedback transmission latency) for 16bits data and 14 bits overhead (assuming Idle period equal to 0);

H (HARQ feedback timing window length);

AUD is the Additional User Delay which is the configurable parameter and applicable only in Rohde&Schwarz Test Equipment.

KS k2 is the k2 parameter configurable at Keysight Test Equipment.

For Rohde&Schwarz, values of L, H and AUD are specified in the table below.

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  | FDD |  |
| L | 260.4 us |  |
| [(16 + 14) bits / 115.2 kbps] |  |  |
| H | 539.6 us |  |
| [1000 - L - 2*0.1*slot] |  |  |
| AUD | Option 1 | Option 2 |
| -2 slots | N/A |  |

Table below specifies the L, H and KS k2 values in case of Keysight TE usage.

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  | FDD |  |
| L | 86.8 us |  |
| [(8 + 2) bits / 115.2 kbps] |  |  |
| H | 713.2 us |  |
| [1000 - L - 2*0.1*slot] |  |  |
| KS k2 | Option 1 | Option 2 |
| 3 | N/A |  |

Following timings towards Test Equipment shall be used when HARQ reporting in UL conformance tests is enabled.

R&S = Rohde & Schwarz

KS = Keysight

For FR2:

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a timing diagram illustrating HARQ (Hybrid Automatic Repeat Request) feedback timing windows in a communication system. The diagram uses horizontal lines to represent different communication entities or processes and vertical dashed lines to denote time boundaries, likely slots. Arrows indicate the duration and timing of various events.
Key elements and their descriptions:
\* **Top Line:** Represents a timing window for HARQ response for "the NR-slot n+k2". It spans "X = 2 slots (250 us) + T\_BB1ToAnt".
\* **Second Line from Top:** Shows a duration of "3 slots (374 us) + T\_BB1ToAnt".
\* **Third Line from Top (Green Text):** Depicts "R&S HARQ feedback timing window for the slot n+k2". It has two options:
\* Option 1: "H (option 1, AUD = 0)"
\* Option 2: "H (option 2, AUD = -2)"
\* **Fourth Line from Top (Green Text):** Illustrates "R&S HARQ feedback transmission latency" followed by "0.1 slot". It also shows "AUD option 2 (slots)".
\* **Fifth Line from Top (Blue Text):** This line shows "Multiple KS HARQ feedback timing windows for the slot n+k2".
\* It includes "H (option 1)" with a timing of "KS k2 = k2 + x".
\* It also shows "KS HARQ feedback transmission latency" with a duration labeled "L".
\* **Sixth Line from Top (Blue Text):** Shows "KS HARQ feedback timing window for the slot n+k2 H (option 1)" with a timing of "KS k2 = 1". This is followed by "KS HARQ feedback transmission latency" with duration "L".
\* **Bottom Line (Blue Text):** This line shows multiple instances of "KS HARQ feedback timing window for the slot n+k2 H".
\* The first instance is "H (option 2)" with timing "KS k2 = k2 + 3", followed by "KS HARQ feedback transmission latency" with duration "L".
\* The second instance is "H (option 2)" with timing "KS k2 = k2 + 2", followed by "KS HARQ feedback transmission latency" with duration "L".
\* The third instance is "H (option 2)" with timing "KS k2 = k2 + 1", followed by "KS HARQ feedback transmission latency" with duration "L".
**Other Labels:**
\* "0.1 slot" is indicated with a small arrow.
\* "NR-slot n+k2 PUSCH channel" is shown as a shaded rectangular block with vertical lines, representing a channel.
\* "OFDM symbol" is depicted as a small rectangular block.
\* "Slot boundary" is indicated by a vertical dashed line.
The diagram appears to be comparing different timing configurations and latencies for HARQ feedback mechanisms, possibly between different vendors (R&S vs. KS) and different options within those mechanisms.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_2410.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

Where:

"0.1 slot" is the time guard so that HARQ feedback can be properly assigned to a given slot in case of R&S TE.

T\_BB1ToAnt is assumed to be negligibly small (cable connection between gNB and RF of a few meters).

L (HARQ feedback transmission latency) for 16bits data and 14 bits overhead (assuming Idle period equal to 0);

H (HARQ feedback timing window length);

AUD is the Additional User Delay which is the configurable parameter and applicable only in Rohde&Schwarz Test Equipment.

KS k2 is the k2 parameter configurable at Keysight Test Equipment.

For Rohde&Schwarz, values of L, H and AUD are specified in the table below.

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  | TDD FR2 |  |
| L | 15.6 us |  |
| [(16 + 14) bits / 1.92 Mbps] |  |  |
| H | Option 1 | Option 2 |
| 471.9 us | 333.4 us |  |
| [500 - L - 0.1\*slot] | [374 - L - 2*0.1*slot] |  |
| AUD | Option 1 | Option 2 |
| 0 | -2 slots |  |

Table below specifies the L, H and KS k2 values in case of Keysight TE usage.

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  | TDD FR2 |  |
| L | 21.7 us |  |
| [(8 + 2) bits / 460.8 kbps] |  |  |
| H | Option 1 | Option 2 |
| 465.8 us | 327.3 us |  |
| [500 - L - 0.1\*slot] | [374 - L - 2*0.1*slot] |  |
| KS k2 | See note below. |  |

Note: For FR2 TDD, k2 value set at the Keysight Test Equipment (KS k2) depends on the L1 implementation as HARQ feedback in FR2 tests can be sent during multiple slots (because of the DL/UL ratio = 4:1), and this affects used "KS k2" value.

---

Timings towards TE
Feedback to the Keysight's TE can be done in within any time in the slot and the PUSCH transmission will occur after "KS k2" slots, counting from the beginning of the slot then HARQ feedback has been sent. This means that HARQ feedback acts as DCI for Keysight TE, see diagram below.

[image-0][image\_desc]The image displays a timeline or sequence of events, indicated by vertical dashed red lines. The timeline progresses from left to right.
At the beginning of the timeline, there are three labels associated with specific points in time:
- "KS k2 = k2 + 3"
- "KS k2 = k2 + 2"
- "KS k2 = k2 + 1"
Between the second and third labels, there is a purple box labeled "HARQ feedback".
Further to the right on the timeline, there are two distinct visual elements:
- A thin, light gray vertical bar.
- A wider, light purple rectangular block composed of multiple thin vertical segments.
The overall image appears to be a diagram illustrating a process or communication protocol, likely related to wireless communication systems given the "HARQ feedback" label, which stands for Hybrid Automatic Repeat reQuest. The labels "KS k2 = k2 + n" suggest some form of indexing or counter incrementing over time.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_2412.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

Overview
This requirement describes when to expect SU (Single User) receiver or MU (Multi User) receiver when UL MU MIMO feature is enabled and nbRxbeamforming=4Rx (only eCPRI fronthaul interface).

MU receiver is expected to handle a UE pair (UE0, UE1) in a given subcell. MU receiver is illustrated with input beams as per below. In such case, each UE has 1 layer.

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating a communication system. On the left, there are two rectangular boxes. The top box contains the text "UE0' best beam" in black. The bottom box contains the text "UE1' best beam" in green.
From the "UE0' best beam" box, two blue arrows originate, pointing to the right. From the "UE1' best beam" box, two green arrows originate, also pointing to the right. These arrows are grouped by curly braces, with a blue brace encompassing the two blue arrows and a green brace encompassing the two green arrows.
To the right of these arrows is a large blue rectangular box. This box contains the text "4RX IRC MU receiver" in white. The arrows from the left connect to this receiver box. The diagram visually represents that the best beams from two different User Equipments (UE0 and UE1) are fed into a 4RX IRC MU receiver.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_2431.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

The same 4 streams are used by the MU receiver for all PRBs, including PRB region where only UE1 is scheduled.

[CB008360-D] For PRB regions where only UE1 is scheduled, either best beam of UE0 or second best beam of UE1 might be considered. For more details, please refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_11355\_replaced([11104143](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kn5PNfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). [End CB008360-D].

For MU receiver support regarding L1 HW, please refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8756\_replaced([11103056](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knrMwPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

SU receiver is expected to handle a single UE in a given subcell. SU receiver is illustrated with input beams as per below :

[image-1][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating a signal processing flow. On the left, there are two rectangular boxes, one above the other. The top box contains the text "UE0 best beam", and the bottom box contains the text "UE0 2nd best beam". To the right of these boxes, there are four blue arrows pointing towards a larger blue rectangular box. The arrows are grouped by curly braces, with two braces on the left, each encompassing two arrows. The large blue box on the right contains the text "4RX IRC SU receiver". The diagram suggests that the "UE0 best beam" and "UE0 2nd best beam" signals are processed by the "4RX IRC SU receiver".[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_2431.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]

[CB008360-B] For 2 C4 subcell case, more than one MU MIMO pair shall be received at a time (cf. 5G\_UP\_ALG\_9550\_replaced([11146266](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksDWfPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

Case with 2 MU pairs

* MU receiver in subcell0 for (UE0, UE1) where each UE has one layer
* MU receiver in subcell1 for (UE3, UE4) where each UE has one layer

Case with 3 UEs paired

* MU receiver in subcell0 for (UE0, UE1) where each UE has one layer
* SU receiver in subcell1 for UE3 where UE3 has one or two layers

Case with 1 MU pair

* SU receiver in subcell0 for UE0 where UE0 has one or two layers
* SU receiver in subcell1 for UE1 where UE1 has one or two layers

[End CB008360-B]

---

##### 2.4.2.12.1.0-2 Overview MU vs SU receiver : inputs vs outputs (ID: 11104129)

**2.4.2.12.1.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11104131`)

Overview
This requirement describes difference between SU (Single User) receiver or MU (Multi User) receiver regarding input streams and output streams. Reference to measurements is given for each receiver.

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Subcell Type=C4 | UE0 |  | UE1 |  |
| Receiver input: number of streams per UE (FCP message) | Receiver output: number of antennas | Receiver input: number of streams per UE (FCP message) | Receiver output: number of antennas |  |
| SU receiver (cf. 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7236\_replaced([11109508](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko4tuvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) | 4 | 4 | - | - |
| MU receiver (cf. 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8995\_replaced([11109559](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko5Uy_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) | 2 | 4 | 2 | 4 |
