Resource allocation related information
This requirement describes resource allocation expectation when UL MU MIMO feature is enabled (only eCPRI fronthaul interface).

L1 shall expect same start PRB for UEs received with MU receiver (cf. 5G\_UP\_ALG\_9420\_replaced([11104117](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kn4oLfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) for a given subcell. Let's call (UE0, UE1) the MU MIMO pair received in subcell0. If UE2 is FDM with UE0 and received in subcell0, L1 shall not expect start PRB of UE2 to overlap with end PRB of UE1.

For FCP message, please refer to 5G\_UP\_5222\_replaced([11378660](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_uecYa_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Example:

[image-0][image\_desc]The image displays a visual representation of two scenarios, labeled "OK" and "Not OK" at the bottom. Each scenario is divided into a grid and contains three colored blocks representing "UE0" (green), "UE1" (blue), and "UE2" (yellow).
In the "OK" scenario, UE0 and UE1 are positioned side-by-side in the upper section, with UE0 on the left and UE1 on the right. UE2 is located below them, centered horizontally.
In the "Not OK" scenario, the arrangement is similar, with UE0 and UE1 in the upper section and UE2 below. However, the relative positions of UE0 and UE1 appear to be shifted or misaligned compared to the "OK" scenario, suggesting a problem.
The text in the image is:
OK
Not OK
UE0
UE1
UE2[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_2436.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

For MU receiver :

- start PRB support is as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_782\_replaced([11101549](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knY45fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)),

- start symbol support is as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_743\_replaced([11101406](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knXDvPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)),

- number of symbols supported is as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_752\_replaced([11101457](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knXq0fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)),

- number of PRBs supported is as per5G\_UP\_ALG\_762\_replaced([11101605](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knZf-fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[CB008360-B] For 2 C4 subcell case, more than one MU MIMO pair shall be received at a time. Let's consider the case of a second MU MIMO pair (UE3, UE4) received in subcell1. L1 shall expect same start PRB for UE3 and UE4 UEs received with MU receiver in subcell1. If UE5 is FDM with UE3 and received in subcell1, L1 shall not expect start PRB of UE5 to overlap with end PRB of UE4.

Resource allocation (start PRB, start symbols, number of symbols, number of PRBs) is independent across subcells i.e. there are no constraints between (UE0,UE1,UE2) on subcell0 and (UE3, UE4, UE5) on subcell1.

Resource allocation (start PRB, start symbols, number of symbols, number of PRBs) being independent across subcells is true whatever receiver type used (cf. 5G\_UP\_ALG\_10445\_replaced([11104156](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kn5POvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) detailed pairing cases per rank):

* subcell0 : SU receiver , subcell1 : SU receiver
* subcell0 : SU receiver , subcell1 : MU receiver
* subcell0 : MU receiver , subcell1 : SU receiver
* subcell0 : MU receiver , subcell1 : MU receiver

[End CB008360-B]

For SU vs MU receiver illustration, please refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_9420\_replaced([11104117](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kn4oLfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

For MU receiver support regarding L1 HW, please refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8756\_replaced([11103056](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knrMwPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

##### 2.4.2.12.2.0-2 Resource allocation related information PRB allocation vs beam for MU receiver (ID: 11104143)

**2.4.2.12.2.0-2.0-2**  (ID: `12631864`)

Resource allocation related information
This requirement describes resource allocation expectation for beam used for MU receiver when UL MU MIMO feature is enabled (only eCPRI fronthaul interface).

For PRB regions where only UE1 is scheduled, [Before CNI-137616-B] either best beam of UE0 or [End CNI-137616-B] second best beam of UE1 might be considered. [Before CNI-137616-B] 5G-L2-PS shall decide this based on rdUlMuReceiverBeamUsedOverlappingPRB, as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_11359\_replaced([11149352](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksNHevvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). [End CNI-137616-B]

Let's consider UE0 is allocated with PRB [startPRB , lastPRB\_UE0] ; UE1 is allocated with PRB [startPRB , lastPRB\_UE1] ; lastPRB\_UE1 > lastPRB\_UE0.

[Before CNI-137616-B]

For [lastPRB\_UE0+1, lastPRB\_UE1] to be received with best beam of UE0, L1 shall expect second best beam indication regarding UE1 to be filled with INVALID value.

[End CNI-137616-B]

For [lastPRB\_UE0+1, lastPRB\_UE1] to be received with second best beam of UE1, then L1 shall expect second best beam indication regarding UE1 to be filled with a valid value within current beam index range.

For related FCP message sections, please refer to 5G\_UP\_5222\_replaced([11378660](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_uecYa_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

For MU receiver support regarding L1 HW, please refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8756\_replaced([11103056](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knrMwPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

#### 2.4.2.12.3 DMRS related information

##### 2.4.2.12.3.0-1 DMRS related information DMRS configuration for UL MU MIMO (ID: 11104151)

**2.4.2.12.3.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11104153`)

DMRS related information
This requirement describes DMRS configuration expectation when UL MU MIMO feature is enabled (only eCPRI fronthaul interface).

L1 shall expect same DMRS configuration (number of symbols and position) in a given subcell.

L1 shall be able to handle different DMRS configuration (number of symbols and position) in each subcell.

In a given subcell, please refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_795\_replaced([11101751](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knbVJ_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for supported DMRS configurations.

Hereafter, explicit cases are described. For 1 C4 subcell case, one MU MIMO pair (UE0, UE1) shall be received at a time.

L1 shall expect same number of DMRS symbols and same position for UE0 and UE1 received within same MU receiver (cf. 5G\_UP\_ALG\_9420\_replaced([11104117](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kn4oLfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) in subcell0.

[CB008360-B] For 2 C4 subcell case, more than one MU MIMO pair shall be received at a time. Let's call (UE3, UE4) the second MU MIMO pair or UE3 if only a third UE is paired at a given moment in time.

Same DMRS configuration (number of symbols and position) shall be expected by L1 in subcell 0 and subcell1.

Case with 2 MU pairs

* DMRS configuration X in subcell0 for (UE0, UE1) in MU receiver
* DMRS configuration X in subcell1 for (UE3, UE4) in MU receiver

Case with 3 UEs paired

* DMRS configuration X in subcell0 for (UE0, UE1) in MU receiver
* DMRS configuration X in subcell1 for UE3 in SU receiver

Case with 1 MU pair

* DMRS configuration X in subcell0 for UE0 in SU receiver
* DMRS configuration X in subcell1 for UE1 in SU receiver

[End CB008360-B]

For SU vs MU receiver illustration, please refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_9420\_replaced([11104117](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kn4oLfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

For MU receiver support regarding L1 HW, please refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8756\_replaced([11103056](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knrMwPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

##### 2.4.2.12.3.0-2 DMRS related information DMRS Ports allocation for UL MU MIMO per receiver type (ID: 11104156)

**2.4.2.12.3.0-2.0-3**  (ID: `13313618`)

DMRS related information
The DU shall handle receive request with DMRS port allocation as described in table in case of MU pairing. Table below corresponds to a same scrambling ID among paired UEs.

Please refer to:

- [11103109](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knrz1PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) for SU receiver Antenna Ports supported configuration

- [11103188](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kntB8PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) for MU receiver Antenna Ports supported configuration.

- 3gpp 38.212 Tables 7.3.1.1.2-8~11 for CDM group supported configurations and corresponding DCI antenna ports.

- [CB008148-C] [13313026](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_bQoZTs6WEfCVOOJMfwsFiw) DMRS waveform within CDM group. [End CB008148-C]

|  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Description | 1st C4 subcell |  |  | 2nd C4 subcell |  |  | First Feature |
| UE allocation | DMRS antenna ports |  | UE allocation | DMRS antenna ports |  |  |  |
| SU-Receiver | MU-Receiver | SU-Receiver | MU-Receiver |  |  |  |  |
| No pairing | 1 UE rank 1 | 0 | NA | NA | NA | NA |  |
| No pairing | 1 UE rank 2 | (0,1) | NA | NA | NA | NA |  |
| 2 UEs paired | 2 UE rank 1 | NA | 0 /1 | NA | NA | NA | CB008360-A |
| 2 UEs paired | 1 UE rank 1 | 0 | NA | 1 UE rank 1 | 2 | NA | CB008360-B |
| 2 UEs paired | 1 UE rank 2 | (0,1) | NA | 1 UE rank1 | 2 | NA | CB008360-B |
| 2 UEs paired | 1 UE rank 2 | (0,1) | NA | 1 UE rank2 | (2,3) | NA | CB008360-B |
| 3 UEs paired | 2 UEs rank1 | NA | 0 / 1 | 1 UE rank1 | 2 | NA | CB008360-B |
| 3 UEs paired | 2 UEs rank1 | NA | 0 / 1 | 1 UE rank 2 | (2,3) | NA | CB008360-B |
| 4 UEs paired | 2 UEs rank1 | NA | 0 / 1 | 2 UEs rank1 | NA | 2 / 3 | CB008360-B |

For SU vs MU receiver illustration, please refer to [11104117](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kn4oLfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

For MU receiver support regarding L1 HW, please refer to [11103056](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knrMwPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

Important Notes:

1) rank1 refers to both 2Tx 1layer and 1Tx to keep the table readable.

2) 2 rank1 in same subcell and 2 rank1 in different subcell are supported by L1, L2 shall prefer 2 rank1 in same subcell based on current simulations results

#### 2.4.2.12.4 Remaining algorithms

##### 2.4.2.12.4.0-1 Remaining algorithms Configuration and algorithms (generic) for MU receiver vs SU receiver (ID: 11104168)

**2.4.2.12.4.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11104170`)

Remaining algorithms
\* Summary of configuration and algorithms for MU receiver :
\* Resource allocation (additionnal constraints vs SU receiver) is as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8792\_replaced([11104136](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kn5PM_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
\* DMRS configuration (additionnal constraints vs SU receiver) is as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8806\_replaced([11104151](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kn5POPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
\* DMRS port allocation is as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_10445\_replaced([11104156](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kn5POvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
\* PTRS configuration : PTRS shall be disabled in NIDD when UL MU MIMO is enabled.
\* Receiver algorithm - Channel Estimation (same as SU receiver) is as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_846\_replaced([11102229](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knfmk_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
\* Receiver algorithm - TO Estimation and compensation (same as SU receiver) is as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_872\_replaced([11102411](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kniC0PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
\* Receiver algorithm - Mapping of antenna wise ST/LT CFO to spatial streams (modified vs SU receiver) is as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8992\_replaced([11102733](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knmUTvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
\* Receiver algorithm - Input for phase noise compensation (same as SU receiver) is as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_923\_replaced([11102776](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knm7YPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
\* Spatial configuration - Expected PUSCH DMRS antenna ports for MU MIMO pairs is as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_10445\_replaced([11104156](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kn5POvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
\* Precoding Matrix definition (same as SU receiver) is as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_991\_replaced([11103323](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knuQHvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
\* Decoder configuration - MCSs definition for 64-QAM  (same as SU receiver) is as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1006\_replaced([11103434](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knveN_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
\* Decoder configuration - Supported MCSs for 256-QAM  (same as SU receiver) is as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1013\_replaced([11103505](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knwsUPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
\* UCI reporting on PUSCH (same as SU receiver) is as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1049\_replaced([11103798](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kn09xvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

---

##### 2.4.2.12.4.0-2 Remaining algorithms Algorithms support per L1 HW for MU receiver vs SU receiver (ID: 11104172)

**2.4.2.12.4.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11104175`)

Remaining algorithms
\* L1 HW support for MU receiver :
\* PUSCH receiver algorithm, please refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8756\_replaced([11103056](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knrMwPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
\* DMRS port allocation, please refer to  5G\_UP\_ALG\_8810\_replaced([11103188](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kntB8PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
\* Receiver algorithm - Overview : Front End processing (modified vs SU receiver) is as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_826\_replaced([11101933](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kndKVfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_UP\_ALG\_15158\_replaced([11101922](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kndKUvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
\* Receiver algorithm - Overview : Reference Signal processing (same as SU receiver) is as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_832\_replaced([11102025](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kndxZvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_UP\_ALG\_15161\_replaced([11102011](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kndxY_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
\* Receiver algorithm - Overview : Data processing (modified vs SU receiver) is as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_840\_replaced([11102122](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kneYd_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_UP\_ALG\_15163\_replaced([11102109](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kneYdPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
\* Receiver algorithm - Channel Estimation (same as SU receiver) is as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_6561\_replaced([11102265](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knfmnPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
\* Receiver algorithm - TO Estimation and compensation (same as SU receiver) is as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_872\_replaced([11102411](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kniC0PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), please only consider L1 HW from 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8756\_replaced([11103056](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knrMwPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
\* Receiver algorithm - Antenna wise CFO estimation and compensation (modified vs SU receiver) is as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8991\_replaced([11102725](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knmUS_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
\* Receiver algorithm - CFO compensation (modified vs SU receiver) is as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8989\_replaced([11102619](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knkfHfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
\* Receiver algorithm - Phase Noise Estimation and compensation (same as SU receiver) is as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_922\_replaced([11102772](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knm7XvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)),please only consider L1 HW from 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8756\_replaced([11103056](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knrMwPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
\* Receiver algorithm - Equalization (modified vs SU receiver) is as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8752\_replaced([11102856](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knowhPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
\* Spatial configuration - Supported PUSCH DMRS antenna ports (modified vs SU receiver) is as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8810\_replaced([11103188](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kntB8PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
\* Spatial configuration - Supported spatial configuration (modified vs SU receiver) is as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8748\_replaced([11103306](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knuQGPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
\* Precoding Matrix support (same as SU receiver) is as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_996\_replaced([11103400](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knu3JfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for number of layers supported from 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8748\_replaced([11103306](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knuQGPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
\* Decoder configuration - Supported MCSs for 64-QAM  (modified vs SU receiver) is as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8773\_replaced([11103458](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knveP_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
\* Decoder configuration - Supported MCSs for 256-QAM  (modified vs SU receiver) is as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8776\_replaced([11103525](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knwsWPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
\* Rank and TPMI computation on DMRS -  (modified vs SU receiver) please refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8782\_replaced([11103777](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kn0WvvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

Overview
This requirement describes when to expect SU (Single User) receiver in DU (only eCPRI fronthaul interface) **[CB010708-A] and in Radio** [End CB010708-A]\*\*\*\*

or MU (Multi User) receiver when UL MU MIMO feature is enabled and NRBTS\NRCELL.nbRxbeamforming=8Rx in DU (only eCPRI fronthaul interface)

MU receiver is expected to handle a UE pair of one or several UEs in a given subcell. MU receiver is illustrated with input beams as per below.

In example below, each UE has 1 layer and uses one best beam.

[image-0][image\_desc]The image displays a block diagram illustrating a multi-user receiver system. On the left side, there are four rectangular boxes, each labeled with "UE" followed by a number (0, 1, 2, or 3) and "best beam". These represent the best beams for four different User Equipments (UEs). From each of these UE boxes, a set of two parallel arrows originates, indicating data streams or signals. These arrows are grouped together by a curly brace. All these grouped arrows converge towards a large blue rectangular block on the right. This block is labeled "8RX IRC MU receiver", signifying an 8-receive antenna, Interference Rejection Combining (IRC), Multi-User receiver. The diagram visually represents how the best beams from multiple UEs are processed by a sophisticated receiver.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_2454.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

The same 8 streams are used by the MU receiver for all PRBs, including PRB region where only UE1 is scheduled. For PRB allocation vs beam, please refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_20408\_replaced([11104212](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kn52T_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

For MU receiver support regarding L1 HW, please refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8756\_replaced([11103056](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knrMwPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

SU receiver is used for a single UE allocated with 4 different beams in a given subcell, UE has up to 2 layers (cf. [11103106: PUSCH DMRS antenna ports for SU receiver](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knrz0_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)).

Please refer to [Link to figures](https://nokia.sharepoint.com/:w:/r/sites/ASCOP/AS%20Documents/FS1TAs-CFAMs/CB/CB009000%20-%20CB009499/CFAM/CB009363%208Rx%20IRC%20receiver%20ABIP%20FR1%20TDD/CP2/cb009363_figures.docx?d=wfe6d7bb83a8548d49a63752c2b515742&csf=1&web=1&e=Mlb2YA) if needed.

##### 2.4.2.13.1.0-2 Overview MU vs SU receiver : inputs vs outputs (C8) (ID: 11104193)

**2.4.2.13.1.0-2.0-2**  (ID: `11104199`)

Overview
This requirement describes difference between SU (Single User) receiver or MU (Multi User) receiver regarding input streams and output streams. Reference to measurements is given for each receiver.

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Subcell Type=C8 | UE0 |  | paired UE i |  |
| Receiver input: number of streams per UE (FCP message) | Receiver output: number of antennas | Receiver input: number of streams per UE (FCP message) | Receiver output: number of antennas |  |
| SU receiver (cf. 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7236\_replaced([11109508](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko4tuvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) | 8 (4 beams) | 8 | - | - |
| MU receiver (cf. 5G\_UP\_ALG\_19621\_replaced([11109576](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko5Uz_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) | 2 (1 beam) or 4 (2 beams) [CB009363-OO-UPD001] or  6 (3 beams) [End CB009363-OO-UPD001] | 8 (\*) | 2 (1 beam) or 4 (2 beams) [CB009363-OO-UPD001] or  6 (3 beams) [End CB009363-OO-UPD001] | 8 (\*) |

Note (\*): Among 8 measures, For TOE and FOE on PUSCH only 2 and up to [Before CB009363-OO-UPD001] 4 [CB009363-OO-UPD001] 6 [End CB009363-OO-UPD001] measurements are reported on own UE beams, the remaining values are set to INVALID. Please refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_19621\_replaced([11109576](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko5Uz_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Own UE beams and corresponding streams information can be found from FCP message (5G\_UP\_5222\_replaced([11378660](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_uecYa_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

---

Example of paring with UEs :

For virtual UE concept, please refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7444\_replaced([11142531](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr4XXvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). Virtual UE in black box as legend shows.

[image-0][image\_desc]The image displays a spreadsheet-like grid with colored cells and text labels. The text labels on the left side describe different pairings and ports: "paired UE0, DMRS port 0", "paired UE1, DMRS port 1", "paired UE2, DMRS port (2,3)", and "FDM UE4".
To the right of these labels, there are colored cells arranged in a grid.
- The cells corresponding to "paired UE0, DMRS port 0" are colored yellow. There are four yellow cells in a column.
- The cells corresponding to "paired UE1, DMRS port 1" are colored orange. There are three orange cells in a column, positioned to the right of the yellow cells.
- The cells corresponding to "paired UE2, DMRS port (2,3)" are colored light green. There are four light green cells in a column, positioned to the right of the orange cells.
- The cells corresponding to "FDM UE4" are colored blue. There are four blue cells in a column, positioned below the yellow and orange cells, and to the left of the green cells.
The grid structure suggests a table, where the colored cells represent data or assignments related to the text labels. The grid extends further to the right with many empty white cells.
Here is the table representation:

| Label | Column 1 | Column 2 | Column 3 | Column 4 | Column 5 | Column 6 | Column 7 |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| paired UE0, DMRS port 0 | Yellow | Yellow | Yellow | Yellow |  |  |  |
| paired UE1, DMRS port 1 | Orange | Orange | Orange |  |  |  |  |
| paired UE2, DMRS port (2,3) | Green | Green | Green | Green |  |  |  |
| FDM UE4 | Blue | Blue | Blue | Blue |  |  |  |

To the right of these labels, there are colored cells that correspond to the labels.
- "paired UE0, DMRS port 0" is associated with a column of yellow cells.

- "paired UE2, DMRS port (2,3)" is associated with a block of green cells.
- "FDM UE4" is associated with a column of blue cells.

Resource allocation related information
12631967:
Resource allocation related information
This requirement describes resource allocation expectation for beam used for MU receiver when UL MU MIMO feature is enabled (only eCPRI fronthaul interface) and nbRxbeamforming=8Rx (only eCPRI fronthaul interface).

For unallocated PRB regions (e.g. blank PRBs in example below) , only best beam of corresponding UE shall be considered (e.g. best beam of UE1 and best beam of UE3 in example below). [Before CNI-137616-B] For NRBTS\NRCELL.nbRxbeamforming=8Rx,  5G-L2-PS has only one behavior which is same as  value false of rdUlMuReceiverBeamUsedOverlappingPRB (5G\_UP\_ALG\_11359\_replaced([11149352](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksNHevvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). This rdparameter is only used for NRBTS\NRCELL.nbRxbeamforming=4Rx. [End CNI-137616-B]

[image-0][image\_desc]The image displays a spreadsheet-like grid with text labels and colored cells. To the left of the grid, there are four lines of text, each associated with a colored rectangle.
The text reads:
- paired UE0, DMRS port 0 (associated with a yellow rectangle)
- paired UE1, DMRS port 1 (associated with an orange rectangle)
- paired UE2, DMRS port 2 (associated with a blue rectangle)
- paired UE3, DMRS port 3 (associated with a light green rectangle)
To the right of the text labels is a grid of cells. The grid has 8 rows and 4 columns. The cells are colored to correspond with the labels on the left.
The grid can be represented as follows:

| Column 1 | Column 2 | Column 3 | Column 4 |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Yellow | Yellow | Orange | Light Green |
| Yellow | Yellow | Orange | Light Green |
| Yellow | Yellow | Orange | Light Green |
| Yellow | Yellow | Orange | Light Green |
| Yellow | White | Blue | Light Green |
| Yellow | White | Blue | White |
| Yellow | White | Blue | White |
| Yellow | White | Blue | White |

For related FCP message sections, please refer to 5G\_UP\_5222\_replaced([11378660](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_uecYa_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

For MU receiver support regarding L1 HW, please refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8756\_replaced([11103056](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knrMwPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Note: Decision to use [Before CNI-137616-B] rdUlMuReceiverBeamUsedOverlappingPRB only for 4Rx receiver [CNI-137616-B] only best beam of corresponding UE [End CNI-137616-B] is taken for simplicity purpose due to several possibilities with more than 2 paired UEs. Other reason is one can expect higher gain from 8Rx receiver than from beamforming gain for unallocated PRBs. Number of unallocated PRB depends on ulMuMimoPrbThresholdRatio with 0.2 as default value.

Source of figures is [MU\_MIMO\_Cases.xlsx](https://nokia.sharepoint.com/:x:/r/sites/ASCOP/AS%20Documents/FS1TAs-CFAMs/CB/CB009000%20-%20CB009499/CFAM/CB009363%208Rx%20IRC%20receiver%20ABIP%20FR1%20TDD/References/MU_MIMO/MU_MIMO_Cases.xlsx?d=wb107db0e5f9b498f8384921a9d18ef35&csf=1&web=1&e=XOnAxZ)

---

#### 2.4.2.13.3 DMRS related information

##### 2.4.2.13.3.0-1 DMRS related information DMRS Ports allocation for UL MU MIMO (ID: 11104222)

**2.4.2.13.3.0-1.0-3**  (ID: `13313662`)

DMRS related information
The 5G-L1-UL shall handle PUSCH reception with DMRS port allocation as described in table in case of MU pairing. Table below corresponds to a same scrambling ID among paired UEs.

Please refer to:

- [11103109](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knrz1PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) for SU receiver Antenna Ports supported configuration

- [11103188](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kntB8PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) for MU receiver Antenna Ports supported configuration.

- 3gpp 38.212 Tables 7.3.1.1.2-8~11 for CDM group supported configurations and corresponding DCI antenna ports.

- [CB008148-C] [13313026](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_bQoZTs6WEfCVOOJMfwsFiw) DMRS waveform within CDM group [End CB008148-C]

|  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Description | 1st C8 subcell |  |  |  |  | First feature |
| UE allocation | DMRS antenna ports |  |  |  |  |  |
| UE0 | UE1 | UE2 | UE3 |  |  |  |
| No pairing | 1 UE rank 1 | 0 | NA | NA | NA |  |
| No pairing | 1 UE rank 2 | (0,1) | NA | NA | NA | CB009363-F |
| 2 UEs paired | 2 UE rank 1 | 0 | **2** | NA | NA | CB009363-F |
| 2 UEs paired | 2 UE rank 2 | (0,1) | (2,3) | NA | NA | CB009363-F |
| 2 UEs paired | 1 UE rank 2 +  1 UE rank1 | (0,1) | 2 | NA | NA | CB009363-F |
| 2 UEs paired | 1 UE rank 1 +  1 UE rank2 | 0 | (2,3) | NA | NA | CB009363-F |
| 3 UEs paired | 1 UEs rank2 +   2 UEs rank 1 | (0,1) | 2 | 3 | NA | CB009363-F |
| 3 UEs paired | 2 UEs rank1 + 1 UEs rank 2 | 0 | 1 | (2,3) | NA | CB009363-F |
| 3 UEs paired | 3 UEs rank 1 | 0 | 1 | 2 | NA | CB009363-F |
| 4 UEs paired | 4 UEs rank1 | 0 | 1 | 2 | 3 | CB009363-F |

For MU paring with candidate UE group ([11148139](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksI2BPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), DMRS port allocation of :

* the root UE will start with 0
* the candidate UE group will start with 2 (cf. illustration in [11104203](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kn52TfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) which represents a subset of above table for "UE1" DMRS ports.

Please refer to [11147413](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksGZyPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) for PUSCH reception DMRS ports allocation .

For SU vs MU receiver illustration, please refer to [11104182](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kn52RPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

For MU receiver support regarding L1 HW, please refer to [11103056](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knrMwPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

~~Note : 2nd rank1 paired DMRS port is changed from 1 to 2 after it was proposed for 16Rx but 25R1 branch off occured before UPD to revert it in 8Rx was approved. Intial tests show improvement therefore it shall be tracked in ACD.~~

---

11104266:
Overview
This requirement describes MU (Multi User) receiver when UL MU MIMO feature is enabled and NRBTS\NRCELL.nbRxbeamforming=16Rx in Radio (only eCPRI fronthaul interface)

MU receiver is expected to handle a UE pair in a given subcell. MU receiver is illustrated with input beams as per below.

Example below represents 2 UEs rank2 pairing.

[image-0][image\_desc]The image displays a block diagram illustrating a multi-user receiver system. On the left side, there are two columns of text boxes, each representing a User Equipment (UE) and its associated beams. The first column lists beams for "UE0" with labels "best beam", "2nd beam", "3rd beam", and "4th beam". The second column lists beams for "UE1" with similar labels: "best beam", "2nd beam", "3rd beam", and "4th beam".
Connecting these UE beams to the central block are several horizontal lines grouped by curly braces. Each group of four lines from the UE0 beams and each group of four lines from the UE1 beams are connected to the central block.
The central block is a large blue rectangle labeled "16RX IRC MU receiver". This indicates a receiver with 16 receive antennas, employing Interference Rejection Combining (IRC) for Multi-User (MU) reception.
On the right side of the central block, there are two output text boxes. The top box is labeled "UE0 layer0,1", and the bottom box is labeled "UE1 layer0,1". These represent the processed data layers for each respective user. The lines connecting the central receiver block to these output boxes show the flow of processed information.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_2479.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

The same 16 streams are used by the MU receiver for all PRBs, including PRB region where only UE1 is scheduled. For PRB allocation vs beam, please refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_20408\_replaced([11104212](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kn52T_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

For MU receiver support regarding L1 HW, please refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8756\_replaced([11103056](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knrMwPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

SU receiver is used for a single UE allocated with 8 different beams in a given subcell, UE has up to 2 layers (cf. [11103106: PUSCH DMRS antenna ports for SU receiver](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knrz0_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)).

---

##### 2.4.2.14.1.0-2 Overview MU vs SU receiver : inputs vs outputs (C16'2 or C16'4) (ID: 11104269)

**2.4.2.14.1.0-2.0-3**  (ID: `11104276`)

Overview
This requirement describes difference between SU (Single User) receiver or MU (Multi User) receiver regarding input streams and output streams. Reference to measurements is given for each receiver.

This requirement describes [CB010708-G-UPD001] up to 2 paired rank1 UEs in C16'2 or [End CB010708-G-UPD001] up to 4 paired UEs in [CB010708-G-UPD001] C16'4 for [End CB010708-G-UPD001] MU (Multi User) receiver regarding input streams and output streams.

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Subcell Type = C16’2  (SU [CB010708-G-UPD001] or MU) [End CB010708-G-UPD001] C16'4 (MU) | UE of interest (j=0) |  | paired UE (j=1,2,3) |  |
| Receiver input: number of streams per UE (PUSCH grant) | Receiver output: number of antennas | Receiver input: number of streams per UE (PUSCH grant) | Receiver output: number of antennas |  |
| SU receiver (cf. 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7236\_replaced([11109508](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko4tuvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) | 8 (4 beams) or 16 (8 beams) | 8  or 16 (\*) | - | - |
| MU receiver (cf. 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26119\_replaced([11109595](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko570_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) | 2\*N0 (N0 beams) | 16 (\*) | 2\*Nj (Nj beams) | 16 (\*) |

Nj corresponds to the number of beams of the UEj : Nj=1,2 where sum(Nj)=8 for j=0..3.

Note (\*): Among 16 measures, For TOE and FOE on PUSCH up to 8 measurements are reported on own UE beams, the remaining values are set to INVALID. Please refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26413\_replaced([11109603](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko571fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Own UE beams order for each UE can be found from PUSCH grant (5G\_UP\_12233\_replaced([11380715](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ueolp_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

Note: In initial subfeature CB010708-A, Radio shall use internal rdparameter to reduce to 8 streams for C16'2 subcell.

---

##### 2.4.2.14.1.0-3 Overview MU vs SU receiver : inputs vs outputs (C16'8) (ID: 11104279)

**2.4.2.14.1.0-3.0-2**  (ID: `11104285`)

Overview
This requirement describes up to 8 paired UEs in MU (Multi User) receiver regarding input streams and output streams.

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Subcell Type = C16’2 (SU) C16'8 (MU) | UE of interest (j=0) |  | paired UE (j=1,2,3,4,5,6,7) |  |
| Receiver input: number of streams per UE (PUSCH grant) | Receiver output: number of antennas | Receiver input: number of streams per UE (PUSCH grant) | Receiver output: number of antennas |  |
| SU receiver (cf. 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7236\_replaced([11109508](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko4tuvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) | 8 (4 beams) [CB008235-D] or 16 (8 beams) [End CB008235-D] | 8 [CB008235-D] or 16(\*) [End CB008235-D] |  |  |
| MU receiver (cf. 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26119\_replaced([11109595](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko570_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) | 2\*N0 (N0 beams) | 16 (\*) | 2\*Nj (Nj beams) | 16 (\*) |

Nj corresponds to the number of beams of the UEj : Nj=1,2 where sum(Nj)=8 for j=0..7.

Note (\*): Among 16 measures,  the Radio with Thor 7.2-e shall report for not own beams the PUSCH TOE and FOE measurements as INVALID. Please refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26413\_replaced([11109603](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko571fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Own UE beams order for each UE can be found from PUSCH grant (5G\_UP\_12233\_replaced([11380715](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ueolp_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

---

#### 2.4.2.14.2 Resource allocation related information

##### 2.4.2.14.2.0-1 Resource allocation related information Supported cases for C16'4 or C16'2 subcell (ID: 11104290)

**2.4.2.14.2.0-1.0-3**  (ID: `11104298`)

Resource allocation related information
List of supported cases is restricted to 2 paired rank2 UEs.  Such virtual UE multiplexing with another SU MIMO UE is not supported. Pairing with candidate UE group (5G\_UP\_ALG\_9690\_replaced([11148139](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksI2BPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is not supported.

[CB010708-G-UPD001] For subcell C16'4, [End CB010708-G-UPD001] The list of cases supported is extended to 4 UEs paired per PRB and illustarted in the following link : [MU\_MIMO\_Cases.xlsx](https://nokia.sharepoint.com/:x:/r/sites/ASCOP/AS%20Documents/FS1TAs-CFAMs/CB/CB009000%20-%20CB009499/CFAM/CB009363%208Rx%20IRC%20receiver%20ABIP%20FR1%20TDD/References/MU_MIMO/MU_MIMO_Cases.xlsx?d=wb107db0e5f9b498f8384921a9d18ef35&csf=1&web=1&e=XOnAxZ) including pairing with UE candidate group.

[CB010708-G-UPD001] For subcell C16'2, the list of cases supported is 2 rank1.  Pairing with candidate UE group (5G\_UP\_ALG\_9690\_replaced([11148139](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksI2BPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is supported where all UEs are rank1. [End CB010708-G-UPD001]

---

including pairing with UE candidate group.

12632002:
Resource allocation related information
This requirement describes resource allocation expectation for beam used for MU receiver when UL MU MIMO feature is enabled  and numberOfRxBeamforming=16Rx in Radio eCPRI 7-2e.

For unallocated PRB regions (e.g. blank PRBs in example below), only best beam of corresponding UE shall be considered : i.e. best beam of UE1 in 2 rank2 pairing  illustrated below.

Example of 2 rank2 paired UEs

[image-0][image\_desc]The image contains a spreadsheet with text and a table.
**Text:**
\* 2 Rank2
\* paired UE0, DMRS port (0,1)
\* paired UE1, DMRS port (2,3)
**Table:**
The table has 12 rows and 6 columns. The first two columns are empty. The third and fourth columns are filled with yellow cells. The fifth and sixth columns are filled with green cells. The last two rows in the fifth and sixth columns are empty.

|  |  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  |  |  |  |  |  |
|  |  |  |  |  |  |
|  |  |  |  |  |  |
|  |  |  |  |  |  |
|  |  |  |  |  |  |
|  |  |  |  |  |  |
|  |  |  |  |  |  |
|  |  |  |  |  |  |
|  |  |  |  |  |  |
|  |  |  |  |  |  |
|  |  |  |  |  |  |

The requirement applies for up to:

* **2 UEs rank1 paired per PRB** for subcell C16'2\*\*\*\*
* 4 UEs paired per PRB for subcell C16'4, please see below illustration of 4 rank1 UEs paired (DMRS ports 0,1,2,3):

[image-1][image\_desc]The image displays a grid with four columns labeled UE0, UE1, UE2, and UE3. Each column is divided into several rows, with the top rows colored yellow, orange, blue, and green, respectively, corresponding to the column labels.
Below the colored sections, there are white rows with arrows indicating movement or allocation.
- **UE0 (Yellow column):** The yellow section extends down to the bottom of the grid. There are no arrows in the white section of this column.
- **UE1 (Orange column):** The orange section stops a few rows above the bottom. The white section below it has an orange double-headed arrow pointing upwards and downwards, suggesting a range or flexibility.
- **UE2 (Blue column):** The blue section also stops a few rows above the bottom. The white section below it has a blue double-headed arrow pointing upwards and downwards, also indicating a range or flexibility.
- **UE3 (Green column):** The green section stops a few rows above the bottom. The white section below it has a green double-headed arrow pointing upwards and downwards, similar to UE1 and UE2.
The grid structure suggests a resource allocation or scheduling scenario, where different User Equipments (UEs) are assigned resources represented by the colored rows. The arrows in the white sections might represent dynamic adjustments or available buffer space within the allocated resources.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_2497.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]

For each UE, the color of the arrow indicates the UE’ beam(s) used to receive the unallocated PRBs.

For related FCP message sections, please refer to 5G\_UP\_12233\_replaced([11380715](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ueolp_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

For MU receiver support regarding L1 HW, please refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8756\_replaced([11103056](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knrMwPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[Before CNI-137616-B] Note : For NRBTS\NRCELL.numberOfRxBeamforming=16Rx,  DU has only one behavior corresponding to value false of rdUlMuReceiverBeamUsedOverlappingPRB (5G\_UP\_ALG\_11359\_replaced([11149352](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksNHevvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). This rdparameter is only used for NRBTS\NRCELL.numberOfRxBeamforming=4Rx. [End CNI-137616-B]

Source of figures is [[image-2][image\_desc]The image is a blurry, low-resolution icon that appears to be a Microsoft Excel file. The icon is predominantly green and white. A green square with a white "X" is overlaid on the left side of a white document icon. The document icon has a green rectangle in the upper right corner, suggesting a spreadsheet. 11104357:
Resource allocation related information
This requirement describes resource allocation expectation for beam used for MU receiver when UL MU MIMO feature is enabled and numberOfRxBeamforming=16Rx in Radio eCPRI 7-2e.

For unallocated PRB regions (e.g. blank PRBs in example below), only best beam of corresponding UE shall be considered : i.e. best beam of UE1 in 2 rank2 pairing  illustrated below.

Example of 2 rank2 paired UEs

[image-0][image\_desc]The image contains a spreadsheet with text and a table.
**Text:**
\* 2 Rank2
\* paired UE0, DMRS port (0,1)
\* paired UE1, DMRS port (2,3)
**Table:**
The table has 12 rows and 6 columns. The first two columns are empty. The third and fourth columns are filled with yellow cells. The fifth and sixth columns are filled with green cells. The last two rows in the fifth and sixth columns are empty.

|  |  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  |  |  |  |  |  |
|  |  |  |  |  |  |
|  |  |  |  |  |  |
|  |  |  |  |  |  |
|  |  |  |  |  |  |
|  |  |  |  |  |  |
|  |  |  |  |  |  |
|  |  |  |  |  |  |
|  |  |  |  |  |  |
|  |  |  |  |  |  |
|  |  |  |  |  |  |

The requirement applies for up to:

* 4 UEs paired (cf. 5G\_UP\_13964\_replaced([11355535](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucR1OvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_UP\_4977\_replaced([11355544](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucR1PPvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) per PRB, please see below illustration of 4 rank1 UEs paired (DMRS ports 0,1,2,3):

[image-1][image\_desc]The image displays a grid with four columns labeled UE0, UE1, UE2, and UE3. Each column is divided into several rows, with the top rows colored yellow, orange, blue, and green, respectively, corresponding to the column labels.
Below the colored sections, there are white rows with arrows indicating movement or allocation.
- **UE0 (Yellow column):** The yellow section extends down to the bottom of the grid. There are no arrows in the white section of this column.
- **UE1 (Orange column):** The orange section stops a few rows above the bottom. The white section below it has an orange double-headed arrow pointing upwards and downwards, suggesting a range or flexibility.
- **UE2 (Blue column):** The blue section also stops a few rows above the bottom. The white section below it has a blue double-headed arrow pointing upwards and downwards, also indicating a range or flexibility.
- **UE3 (Green column):** The green section stops a few rows above the bottom. The white section below it has a green double-headed arrow pointing upwards and downwards, similar to UE1 and UE2.
The grid structure suggests a resource allocation or scheduling scenario, where different User Equipments (UEs) are assigned resources represented by the colored rows. The arrows in the white sections might represent dynamic adjustments or available buffer space within the allocated resources.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_2500.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]

* or 8 UEs paired (cf. 5G\_UP\_13964\_replaced([11355535](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucR1OvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_UP\_4977\_replaced([11355544](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucR1PPvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) per PRB, please see below illustration of 8 rank1 UEs paired (DMRS ports 0,1,2,3,4,5,6,7):
* **[CB008235-C] without** enhanced UE Groups pairing (cf. 5G\_UP\_13281\_replaced([11355586](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucScQvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) algorithm ,   **[End CB008235-C]**\*\*\*\*

[image-2][image\_desc]The image displays a grid representing a schedule or allocation for eight User Equipments (UEs), labeled UE0 through UE7. Each UE column is filled with a distinct color, indicating different time slots or resource blocks.
UE0 is colored yellow.

UE3 is colored light blue.
UE4 is colored yellow.
UE5 is colored green.
UE6 is colored purple.
UE7 is colored blue.
The grid has 8 columns (UE0-UE7) and 10 rows. The top row contains the labels for each UE. The subsequent rows are filled with the respective colors.
At the bottom of the grid, there are visual indicators representing some form of allocation or activity for specific UEs:
\* Under UE0, there is a yellow double-headed arrow pointing upwards and downwards, spanning two cells.
\* Under UE1, there is an orange double-headed arrow pointing upwards and downwards, spanning two cells.
\* Under UE4, there is a yellow double-headed arrow pointing upwards and downwards, spanning one cell.
\* Under UE5, there is a green double-headed arrow pointing upwards and downwards, spanning two cells.
\* Under UE6, there is a purple double-headed arrow pointing upwards and downwards, spanning one cell.

[image-3][image\_desc]The image displays a grid-like structure representing resource allocation for different User Equipments (UEs). The grid is divided into columns labeled UE0, UE1, UE2, UE3, and UE4. Within these columns, various colored blocks represent allocated resources.
Here's a breakdown of the visible UEs and their resource allocations:
\* **UE0:** Occupies the first column, with resources allocated in yellow.
\* **UE1:** Occupies the second column, with resources allocated in orange.
\* **UE2:** Occupies the third column, with resources allocated in light green.
\* **UE3:** Occupies the fourth column, with resources allocated in light blue.
\* **UE4:** Occupies the fifth column, with resources allocated in orange.
Additionally, there are other UEs represented by colored blocks that span across multiple columns or are positioned within the grid:
\* **UE5:** Appears in the first column, with resources allocated in green.
\* **UE6:** Appears in the second column, with resources allocated in purple.
\* **UE7:** Appears in the fifth column, with resources allocated in blue.
There are also visual indicators of resource sharing or potential interference:
\* A green double-headed arrow is shown at the bottom of the UE0 column, indicating a potential interaction or resource usage within that area.
\* A purple double-headed arrow is shown at the bottom of the UE1 column, indicating a potential interaction or resource usage within that area.
\* A light blue double-headed arrow is shown at the bottom of the UE3 column, indicating a potential interaction or resource usage within that area.
The grid itself is composed of smaller cells, suggesting a granular allocation of resources. The colors are distinct and used to differentiate the resource blocks assigned to each UE.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_2500.004.png[/image\_path][/image-3]

This rdparameter is only used for NRBTS\NRCELL.numberOfRxBeamforming=4Rx. [End CNI-137616-B]
