For PUCCH Format3, DU (for Loki and Thor L1HW) shall support Release16 "Low-PAPR DMRS Type2" (5G\_UP\_10530\_replaced([11360966](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucyLjfvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) Pilot Sequence generation:
- with DMRS sequences modulated using [image-0][image\_desc]The image contains the mathematical expression "π/2" rendered in a blurry, stylized font. The colors appear to be a gradient of blue, orange, and yellow, with a black outline.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_2820.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]BPSK and DFT-s-OFDM,
[PR780757] - with Raw Channel Estimation Algorithm enhancement, needed to mitigate negative impacts of DMRS Type2 sequence generation on System Performance. For L1 Algorithm impact on Measurements (as referred by 5GMax algorithm specifications in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_695\_replaced([11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), §2.3.1.17 chapter) and System Performance metrics refer to 5G\_UP\_13354\_replaced([11361085](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucyymvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). Refer to NR5GMAX L1 Algorithm change simulations:
[End PR780757]
 
NOTE: DU shall support "Low-PAPR DMRS Type2" (5G\_UP\_10530\_replaced([11360966](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucyLjfvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) Pilot Sequence generation under assumption that the (FDSS) Frequency Domain Spectral Shaping (cf. 5G\_UP\_10567\_replaced([11361037](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucyylvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) technique is needed in System, implemented in UE Transmitter side and transparent to gNB receiver that is agnostic about FDSS usage in UE Transmitter.
 
Refer to PUCCH Format3 Receiver scheme in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_15881\_replaced([11104938](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koDnUPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

#### 2.4.3.4.5 Noise Power Estimation

##### 2.4.3.4.5.0-1 Noise Power Estimation Noise estimation for Format0 (ID: 11105226)

**2.4.3.4.5.0-1.0-3**  (ID: `11105234`)

Noise Power Estimation
For Loner, Loki  [CB010576-A] andThor [End CB010576-A] L1 HWs, the noise power is estimated per symbol and per antena for PUCCH Format 0 as specified in section 2.4.1.1.3 of the 5GMax algorithm specification (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_695\_replaced([11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

Noise Power Estimation is performed in PUCCH Format0 Signal processing as shown in Receiver scheme (refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_15878\_replaced([11104902](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koDnRfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

---

##### 2.4.3.4.5.0-2 Noise Power Estimation Noise estimation for Format1 (ID: 11105236)

**2.4.3.4.5.0-2.0-4**  (ID: `13341423`)

Noise Power Estimation
For Loner, Loki and Thor L1 HWs, Noise power estimation is computed using 'AllFreeRes' method with more reliable estimates as described in section 2.4.2.5 of 5GMax algorithm specification (see [11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

**[CB016066-C] For GPU L1 HWs, 'Rdd-based NPE' method is used for noise estimation as specified in section 2.4.2.5 of the 5GMax algorithm specification (see [11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).**[End CB016066-C]\*\*\*\*

**NOTE: For Format1, the noise is estimated on unused cyclic shifts within one PRB. This estimate includes both thermal noise + intra-cell interference since no orthogonality between the received signals associated to different cyclic shifts, in particular for adjacent cyclic shifts.**

Noise Power Estimation is performed in PUCCH Format1 Signal processing as shown in Receiver scheme (refer to [11104911](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koDnSPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

##### 2.4.3.4.5.0-3 Noise Power Estimation Noise estimation for Format2 (ID: 11105247)

**2.4.3.4.5.0-3.0-3**  (ID: `11105255`)

Noise Power Estimation
For Loner, Loki and [CB010576-A] Thor [End CB010576-A] L1 HWs, the per symbol noise power is estimated and used in PUCCH Format 2 equalization. The noise power estimation is described in section “2.4.3.1.3 Noise Power Estimation” of 5GMax algorithm specification (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_695\_replaced([11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

Noise Power Estimation is performed in PUCCH Format2 Signal processing as shown in Receiver scheme (refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_15880\_replaced([11104928](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koDnTfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

---

##### 2.4.3.4.5.0-4 Noise Power Estimation Noise estimation for Format3 (ID: 11105258)

**2.4.3.4.5.0-4.0-5**  (ID: `13341424`)

Noise Power Estimation
DU for Loner, Loki, Thor **[CB016066-C] and GPU**[End CB016066-C] L1HWs shall support the noise power estimated per antenna as specified in section 2.4.4.5 of the 5GMax algorithm specification (see [11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).\*\*\*\*

DU for Loki and Thor L1HW shall support Noise Power Estimation with Release16 "Low-PAPR DMRS Type2" ([11104870](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koDAOPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ),[11360966](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucyLjfvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) Pilot Sequence generation**, with Raw Channel ([11105217](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koH4tvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and Noise Estimation Algorithm enhancements, needed to mitigate negative impacts of DMRS Type2 Sequence on System Performance. For L1 Algorithm impact on Noise Measurements (as referred by 5GMax algorithm specifications in [11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ), §2.3.1.17 chapter refer to [11361085](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucyymvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ). Refer to NR5GMAX L1 Algorithm change simulations: <https://nokia.sharepoint.com/:p:/r/sites/ASCOP/AS%20Documents/FS1TAs-CFAMs/CB/CB007000%20-%20CB007199/CFAMs/CB007103%20Low%20PAPR%20DMRS%20for%20DFT-S-OFDM%20Pi2%20BPSK/CFAM/NPE%20optimization%20on%20Rel16%20low-PAPR%20DMRS%20sequence%20type%202%203.pptx?d=w24d793c8e01045b695cefa818e030e99&csf=1&web=1&e=lL0KBo>**

Noise Power Estimation is performed in PUCCH Format3 Signal processing as shown in Receiver scheme (refer to [11104938](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koDnUPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

#### 2.4.3.4.6 Signal Power Estimation

##### 2.4.3.4.6.0-1 Signal Power Estimation Signal Power estimation for Format0 (ID: 11105273)

**2.4.3.4.6.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11105276`)

Signal Power Estimation
The received signal is demodulated with all available cyclic shift (CS) based reference signal.

Knowing in advance which available cyclic shifts are used for received data demodulation of current UE,

the received power could be computed at available cyclic shift positions: the PUCCH Format0 signal power is estimated after cyclic shift detection, as detailed in section $2.4.1.1.4 ‘Signal Power Estimation’ of 5GMax algorithm specification (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_695\_replaced([11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

Signal Power Estimation is performed in PUCCH Format0 Signal processing as shown in Receiver scheme (refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_15878\_replaced([11104902](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koDnRfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

---

##### 2.4.3.4.6.0-2 Signal Power Estimation Signal Power estimation for Format1 (ID: 11105278)

**2.4.3.4.6.0-2.0-2**  (ID: `13467293`)

Signal Power Estimation
DU shall compute the Signal Power estimation ~~is so performed~~ subtracting Noise Power ([11105236](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koH4vfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) from average of Data and DMRS power.

DU shall compute the Data and DMRS Power:  
- [CB016066-C] as per sections ‘Noise Power and Signal Power Estimation’ and 'Measurement and Report' in 5GMax algorithm specification (see [11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).  
- based on different methods, depending on the receiver order and L1 HW (cf. [11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) [End CB016066-C] after initial processing steps: after the ZC (Zadoff-Chou) sequence removal, the de-Orthogonal Cover Code (OCC), the DFT operations, and the FOC operation.

To keep the power unchanged after de-OCC and DFT operations, the Signal Power needs to be normalized as detailed in section $2.4.2.5 ‘Noise Power and Signal Power Estimation’ of 5GMax algorithm specification (see [11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Signal Power Estimation is performed in PUCCH Format1 Signal processing as shown in Receiver scheme (refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_15879\_replaced([11104911](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koDnSPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

---

##### 2.4.3.4.6.0-3 Signal Power Estimation Signal Power estimation for Format2 (ID: 11105283)

**2.4.3.4.6.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11105286`)

Signal Power Estimation
PUCCH Format2 Signal power is estimated per PUCCH OFDM symbol based on raw channel estimate as described in section “2.4.3.1.4 Signal Noise Power Estimation” of 5GMax algorithm specification (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_695\_replaced([11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

Signal Power Estimation is performed in PUCCH Format2 Signal processing as shown in Receiver scheme (refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_15880\_replaced([11104928](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koDnTfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

---

##### 2.4.3.4.6.0-4 Signal Power Estimation Signal Power estimation for Format3 (ID: 11105288)

**2.4.3.4.6.0-4.0-2**  (ID: `11105294`)

Signal Power Estimation
DU shall estimate PUCCH Format3 Signal power per antenna as specified in section 2.4.4.5 of the 5GMax algorithm specification (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_695\_replaced([11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

**[CB007103-B] DU (~~for Loki and Thor L1 HW~~ for L1 HW supported as per [11104878](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koDAO_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) ) shall support Signal Power Estimation with Release16 "Low-PAPR DMRS Type2" (5G\_UP\_ALG\_21114\_replaced([11104870](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koDAOPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_UP\_10530\_replaced([11360966](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucyLjfvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) Pilot Sequence generation** [End CB007103-B], [PR780757]with Raw Channel (5G\_UP\_ALG\_29403\_replaced([11105217](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koH4tvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and Signal Estimation Algorithm enhancements, needed to mitigate negative impacts of DMRS Type2 sequence generation on System Performance. For L1 Algorithm impact on Measurements (as referred by 5GMax algorithm specifications in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_695\_replaced([11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), §2.3.1.17 chapter) and System Performance metrics refer to 5G\_UP\_13354\_replaced([11361085](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucyymvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). Refer to NR5GMAX L1 Algorithm change simulations: <https://nokia.sharepoint.com/:p:/r/sites/ASCOP/AS%20Documents/FS1TAs-CFAMs/CB/CB007000%20-%20CB007199/CFAMs/CB007103%20Low%20PAPR%20DMRS%20for%20DFT-S-OFDM%20Pi2%20BPSK/CFAM/NPE%20optimization%20on%20Rel16%20low-PAPR%20DMRS%20sequence%20type%202%203.pptx?d=w24d793c8e01045b695cefa818e030e99&csf=1&web=1&e=lL0KBo> **[End PR780757]**\*\*\*\*

Signal Power Estimation is performed in PUCCH Format3 Signal processing as shown in Receiver scheme (refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_15881\_replaced([11104938](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koDnUPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

---

#### 2.4.3.4.7 Frequency Offset Estimation and Compensation

##### 2.4.3.4.7.0-1 Frequency Offset Estimation and Compensation FOE and FOC on PUCCH Format0 (ID: 11105299)

**2.4.3.4.7.0-1.0-3**  (ID: `11105307`)

Frequency Offset Estimation and Compensation
For Loner, Loki and [CB010576-A] Thor [End CB010576-A] L1 HWs:

* No Frequency Offset Estimation and Compensation (see section 2.4.1.1.1 of the 5GMax algorithm specification (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_695\_replaced([11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))))

Refer to PUCCH Format0 Receiver scheme in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_15878\_replaced([11104902](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koDnRfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

##### 2.4.3.4.7.0-2 Frequency Offset Estimation and Compensation FOE and FOC on PUCCH Format1 (up to 24R3) (ID: 11105309)

**2.4.3.4.7.0-2.0-6**  (ID: `11105326`)

Frequency Offset Estimation and Compensation
[Before CNI-103796-A]

Frequency offset estimation (FOE)

For Loner, Loki and Thor L1 HWs : Frequency offset estimation (FOE): not supported because only configuration with frequency hopping and no additional DMRS is supported for PUCCH Format 1, see also section 2.4.2.1 of the 5GMax algorithm specification (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_695\_replaced([11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

Frequency offset compensation (FOC)

For Loner L1 HWs in case of NRBTS.NRCELL.actSupercellSingleSSB=False : Frequency offset compensation (FOC) is not supported.

For Loner in case of NRBTS.NRCELL.actSupercellSingleSSB=True, Loki and Thor L1 HW:

 Frequency offset compensation (inter-symbol FOC) is supported based on the Long term CFO metric computed at 5G-L2-PS for each Rx antenna stream for a given UE (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3331\_replaced([11151200](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksXfjfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_UP\_ALG\_9182\_replaced([11151225](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksYGkvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_UP\_ALG\_9183\_replaced([11151254](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksYGnvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_UP\_ALG\_9184\_replaced([11151331](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksYtpPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))))

* For each Rx antenna stream, 5G-L1-UL shall carry out a CFO compensation in frequency domain (post-FFT) for both data and DMRS as specified in section 2.4.2.1 of the 5GMax algorithm specification (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_695\_replaced([11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).
* Note:  FOC depends on the setting of the L2 R&D parameter rdLongPucchFocEnable (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_9423\_replaced([11151405](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksZUsfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

For THOR L1W when NRBTS/NRCELL/nbRxbeamforming = 8Rx, either with NRBTS/NRCELL/numberOfRxPucch= 8Rx or 4Rx, 5G-L1-UL shall apply LT CFO received from 5G-L2-PS as defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_9183\_replaced([11151254](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksYGnvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

For Loner in case of NRBTS.NRCELL.actSupercellSingleSSB=True, Loki and Thor L1 HW:

If the overall LT CFO (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3322\_replaced([11150515](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksT1LvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) received from 5G-L2-PS is 0 (i.e. 0+j0), 5G-L1-UL shall assume frequency offset is 0Hz.

- If the antenna wise LT CFO received from 5G-L2-PS is 0(i.e. 0+j0) for some or all antennas, 5G-L1-UL shall assume frequency offset is 0Hz for the corresponding antennas.

[End CNI-103796-A]

[CNI-103796-A]

For PUCCH Format1 Frequency Offset Estimation (FOE), refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_30236\_replaced([11105328](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koJt4fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

For PUCCH Format1 Frequency Offset Compensation (FOC), refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_30241\_replaced([11105333](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koJt4_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

For CFO Validation, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_30210\_replaced([11105394](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koKU-vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

Refer to PUCCH Format1 Receiver scheme in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_15879\_replaced([11104911](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koDnSPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[End CNI-103796-A]

---

##### 2.4.3.4.7.0-3 Frequency Offset Estimation and Compensation FOE on PUCCH Format1 (ID: 11105328)

**2.4.3.4.7.0-3.0-2**  (ID: `13341428`)

Frequency Offset Estimation and Compensation
For Loner, Loki, Thor **[CB016066-C] and GPU**[End CB016066-C]**L1 HWs the Frequency offset estimation (FOE) for PUCCH Format 1 is not supported because only configuration with frequency hopping and no additional DMRS is supported, see also section 2.4.2.1 of the 5GMax algorithm specification (see [11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))**\*\*\*\*

For PUCCH Format1 Frequency Offset Compensation (FOC), refer to [11105333](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koJt4_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

Refer to PUCCH Format1 Receiver scheme in [11104911](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koDnSPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

##### 2.4.3.4.7.0-4 Frequency Offset Estimation and Compensation FOC on PUCCH Format1 (ID: 11105333)

**2.4.3.4.7.0-4.0-6**  (ID: `13453140`)

Frequency Offset Estimation and Compensation
DU shall perform Frequency Offset Compensation (FOC) algorithm on each received PUCCH F1 resource (i.e. for a given UE), when it’s applicable, as specified in the following.

**Inputs:**

 MRBTS/NRBTS/NRCELL/actSupercellSingleSSB

 NRBTS/NRCELL/numberOfRxBeamforming, MRBTS/NRBTS/NRCELL/numberOfRxPucch

 FOC on PUCCH activation, see [11151200](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksXfjfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ), [11151225](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksYGkvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ), [11151254](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksYGnvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ), [11151331](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksYtpPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)

 Long Term CFO metric for each Rx antenna stream, longTermCfoMetricOfAnt[i], i=0, 1, ….,N, see [11151200](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksXfjfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ), [11151225](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksYGkvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ), [11151254](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksYGnvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ), [11151331](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksYtpPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)

with N stands for the max number of streams for PUCCH signal reception, see [11094176](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmaBffvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) and [11094230](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmaogfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)

**Algorithm:**

For a given UE/

* DU shall perform “LT CFO validation for value 0” for each Rx antenna stream, see [11105395](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koKU-_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)
* If the validation check is not passed, DU shall use frequency offset equals to 0Hz for the compensation (i.e. same as no compensation) of the corresponding antenna;
* For each Rx antenna stream i with validation check is passed,  
  DU (for Loner L1 HW only if actSupercellSingleSSB= True, Loki and Thor L1 HW and GPU L1 HW)  
  shall perform the compensation operation as below:

DU shall use longTermCfoMetricOfAnt[i] to compensate the Frequency Offset(FO) **only for Inter-symbol DMRS and DATA symbols, as specified in section 2.4.2.1 of the 5GMax algorithm specification (see [11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)),**

For Thor L1HW, longTermCfoMetricOfAnt[i] input is computed according to [11151254](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksYGnvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ):

* for eCPRI 7-2a with BeamForming**: with numberOfRxBeamforming = 8Rx, either with numberOfRxPucch= 8Rx or 4Rx**
* for **eCPRI 7-2e: with NRBTS/NRCELL/numberOfRxBeamforming = 16Rx and NRBTS/NRCELL/numberOfRxPucch = 8Rx.**
* for eCPRI 7.2a **[CB014128-E] and CPRI**[End CB014128-E] without BeamForming: with NRBTS/NRCELL/act8RXnonBeamforming = true.\*\*\*\*

**Output:**

 Data and DMRS with compensated Frequency Offset

For PUCCH Format1 Frequency Offset Estimation (FOE), refer to [11105328](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koJt4fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)

For CFO Validation, refer to [11105394](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koKU-vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)

Refer to PUCCH Format1 Receiver scheme in [11104911](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koDnSPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

---

##### 2.4.3.4.7.0-5 Frequency Offset Estimation and Compensation FOE and FOC on PUCCH Format2 (ID: 11105344)

**2.4.3.4.7.0-5.0-3**  (ID: `11105352`)

Frequency Offset Estimation and Compensation
For Loner, Loki and [CB010576-A] Thor [End CB010576-A] L1 HWs:

* No Frequency Offset Estimation and Compensation (see section 2.4.3.3 of the 5GMax algorithm specification (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_695\_replaced([11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))))

Refer to PUCCH Format2 Receiver scheme in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_15880\_replaced([11104928](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koDnTfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

##### 2.4.3.4.7.0-6 Frequency Offset Estimation and Compensation FOE and FOC on PUCCH Format3 (up to 24R3) (ID: 11105355)

**2.4.3.4.7.0-6.0-6**  (ID: `11105370`)

Frequency Offset Estimation and Compensation
[Before CNI-103796-A]

Frequency offset estimation (FOE)

For Loner and Thor L1 HWs: Frequency offset estimation (FOE): not supported because only configuration with frequency hopping and no additional DMRS is supported for PUCCH Format 3, see also section 2.4.4.1 of the 5GMax algorithm specification (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_695\_replaced([11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

For Loki and Thor L1 HWs: FOE based on PUCCH DMRS is supported when F3 additional DMRS is configured with intra-slot FH.

Additionally, Itv =4  is used by L1 for adjusting the time interval for short term correlation calculation between different DMRS symbols.

See also section 2.4.4.1 of the 5GMax algorithm specification (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_695\_replaced([11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

Frequency offset compensation (FOC)

For Loner L1 HWs in case of NRBTS.NRCELL.actSupercellSingleSSB=False : Frequency offset compensation (FOC) is not supported.

For Loner in case of NRBTS.NRCELL.actSupercellSingleSSB=True, Loki and Thor L1 HW:

 Frequency offset compensation (FOC) is supported based on the Long term CFO metric computed at 5G-L2-PS for each Rx antenna stream for a given UE (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3331\_replaced([11151200](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksXfjfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_UP\_ALG\_9182\_replaced([11151225](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksYGkvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_UP\_ALG\_9183\_replaced([11151254](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksYGnvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_UP\_ALG\_9184\_replaced([11151331](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksYtpPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

* FOC on PUCCH algorithm is specified in section 2.4.4.1 of of the 5GMax algorithm specification (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_695\_replaced([11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))):
* For each Rx antenna stream, 5G-L1-UL shall carry out a CFO compensation in frequency domain (post-FFT) for both data and DMRS as specified in section 2.4.4.1 of the 5GMax algorithm specification (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_695\_replaced([11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).
* FOC on PUCCH algorithm is specified in section 2.4.4.1 of of the 5GMax algorithm specification (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_695\_replaced([11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))):
* The FOC filter length for PUCCH is aligned with PUSCH (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_9433\_replaced([11102631](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knlGI_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). The length value supported by L1 HW is as in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_17374\_replaced([11105411](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koK8AfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

This applies also when NIDD MRBTS/NRBTS/NRCELL/actHighSpeedCell is enabled for Loki and Thor L1 HW.

* The FOC optimization method is aligned with PUSCH (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_9434\_replaced([11102653](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knlGK_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). The method supported by L1 HW is as in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_17376\_replaced([11105428](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koK8B_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

This applies also when NIDD MRBTS/NRBTS/NRCELL/actHighSpeedCell is enabled for Loki and Thor L1 HW.

NOTE: For Thor L1 HW, the FOC filter length and FOC optimization method are not as in Loki L1 HW.

Note: FOC depends on the setting of the L2 R&D parameter rdLongPucchFocEnable (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_9423\_replaced([11151405](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksZUsfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

When NIDD MRBTS/NRBTS/NRCELL/actHighSpeedCell is enabled, the FOC optimization method set to “ON” considers the impact of TO in FOC and the impact on FOC coefficients as described in section "2.3.3.6 Frequency Offset Compensation" of 5GMax algorithm specification (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_695\_replaced([11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). The existing simulation results for PUSCH are provided by the link from 5G\_UP\_ALG\_6704\_replaced([11102650](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knlGKvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

For Loner in case of NRBTS.NRCELL.actSupercellSingleSSB=True, Loki and Thor L1 HW:

If the overall LT CFO (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3322\_replaced([11150515](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksT1LvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) received from 5G-L2-PS is 0 (i.e. 0+j0), 5G-L1-UL shall assume frequency offset is 0Hz.

If the antenna wise LT CFO received from 5G-L2-PS is 0(i.e. 0+j0) for some or all antennas, 5G-L1-UL shall assume frequency offset is 0Hz for the corresponding antennas.

Refer to PUCCH Format3 Receiver scheme in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_15881\_replaced([11104938](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koDnUPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[End CNI-103796-A]

[CNI-103796-A]

For PUCCH Format3 Frequency Offset Estimation (FOE), refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_30203\_replaced([11105373](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koKU8vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

For PUCCH Format3 Frequency Offset Compensation (FOC), refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_30204\_replaced([11105381](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koKU9fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

For CFO Validation, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_30210\_replaced([11105394](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koKU-vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

[End CNI-103796-A]

---

##### 2.4.3.4.7.0-7 Frequency Offset Estimation and Compensation FOE on PUCCH Format3 (ID: 11105373)

**2.4.3.4.7.0-7.0-3**  (ID: `13341432`)

Frequency Offset Estimation and Compensation
For Loner **[CB016066-C] and GPU**[End CB016066-C]**L1 HWs**: Frequency offset estimation (FOE) for PUCCH Format 3 is not supported because only configuration with frequency hopping and no additional DMRS is supported, see also section 2.4.4.1 of the 5GMax algorithm specification (see [11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).***\****\*

DU for Loki, and Thor L1 HWs shall support FOE based on PUCCH DMRS when F3 additional DMRS is configured with intra-slot FH.

DU shall use Itv =4 for adjusting the time interval for short term correlation calculation between different DMRS symbols. See also section 2.4.4.1 of the 5GMax algorithm specification (see [11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).  
When the number of PUCCH symbols is lower than 10 (i.e. < 10), DU for Loki and Thor L1 HWs shall not perform FOE on PUCCH F3 and it shall report invalid FOE result (i.e. 0+j0, see [11109788](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko8YFfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for Carrier Frequency Offset Handling (see [11150515](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksT1LvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Refer to FOC on PUCCH Format3 in [11105381](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koKU9fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

Refer to PUCCH Format3 Receiver scheme in [11104938](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koDnUPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

---

##### 2.4.3.4.7.0-8 Frequency Offset Estimation and Compensation FOC on PUCCH Format3 (ID: 11105381)

**2.4.3.4.7.0-8.0-7**  (ID: `13453169`)

Frequency Offset Estimation and Compensation
DU shall perform Frequency Offset Compensation (FOC) algorithm on each received PUCCH F3 resource (i.e. for a given UE), when it’s applicable, as specified in the following.

**Inputs:**

 MRBTS/NRBTS/NRCELL/actSupercellSingleSSB

 MRBTS/NRBTS/NRCELL/actHighSpeedCell

 NRBTS/NRCELL/numberOfRxBeamforming, MRBTS/NRBTS/NRCELL/numberOfRxPucch

 MRBTS/NRBTS/NRCELL/actPucchAdditionalDmrs

 FOC on PUCCH activation, see [11151200](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksXfjfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ), [11151225](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksYGkvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ), [11151254](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksYGnvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ), [11151331](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksYtpPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)

 Long Term CFO metric for each Rx antenna stream, longTermCfoMetricOfAnt[i], i=0, 1, ….,N, see [11151200](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksXfjfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ), [11151225](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksYGkvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ), [11151254](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksYGnvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ), [11151331](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksYtpPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)

with N stands for the max number of streams for PUCCH signal reception, see [11094176](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmaBffvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) and [11094230](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmaogfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)

 PUCCH Format3 Short Term FOE, see [11105373](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koKU8vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)

 foeValid, see [11151157](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksXfhvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)

**Algorithm:**

For a given UE:

* DU shall perform “LT CFO validation for value 0” for each Rx antenna stream, see [11105395](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koKU-_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)
* If the validation check is not passed, DU shall use frequency offset equals to 0Hz for the compensation (i.e. same as no compensation) of the corresponding antenna;
* For each Rx antenna stream i with validation check is passed,  
  DU (for Loner L1 HW only if actSupercellSingleSSB= True, Loki, Thor and GPU**L1 HW) shall perform the compensation operation as below:**

DU shall use longTermCfoMetricOfAnt[i] to compensate the Frequency Offset(FO) for Intra and Inter-symbol DMRS and DATA symbols, as specified in section 2.4.4.1 of the 5GMax algorithm specification (see [11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)),

For Thor L1HW, longTermCfoMetricOfAnt[i] input is computed according to [11151254](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksYGnvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ):

 for eCPRI 7-2a with BeamForming: with NRBTS/NRCELL/numberOfRxBeamforming = 8Rx, either with numberOfRxPucch= 8Rx or 4Rx,

 for **eCPRI 7-2e: with NRBTS/NRCELL/**numberOfRxBeamforming = 16Rx and numberOfRxPucch = **8Rx.**

 for eCPRI 7.2a **[CB014128-E] and CPRI**[End CB014128-E] without BeamForming: with NRBTS/NRCELL/act8RXnonBeamforming = true.\*\*\*\*

 For Loki and Thor L1 HW only, when foeValid is FALSE and actPucchAdditionalDmrs is TRUE, DU shall:

 Use PUCCH Format3 **ST FOE only for Inter-symbol FOC and ignore longTermCfoMetricOfAnt.**

 Skip Compensation for Intra-symbols FOC

* DU (for Loner, for Loki  and Thor L1 HW, depending on actHighSpeedCell) shall use for intra-FOC symbols:

 FOC optimization “Method” for each L1 HW:  as per [11105411](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koK8AfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)

 FOC filter “Length” for each L1 HW: as per [11105428](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koK8B_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ), aligned with PUSCH (see [11102631](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knlGI_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

* DU shall skip FOC:
* for Loner L1 HW if actSupercellSingleSSB= FALSE
* For Loki, Thor L1 HW when foeValid is FALSE and actPucchAdditionalDmrs is FALSE.

**Output:**

 Data and DMRS with Compensated Frequency Offset

For all details refer to section §2.4.4.1" and §2.3.3.6 for Frequency Offset Compensation in 5GMax algorithm specification (see [11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

The existing simulation results for PUSCH are provided by the link from [11102650](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knlGKvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

Refer to PUCCH Format3 Receiver scheme in ([11104938](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koDnUPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

##### 2.4.3.4.7.0-9 Frequency Offset Estimation and Compensation CFO Validation (ID: 11105394)

**2.4.3.4.7.0-9.0-1**  (ID: `11105395`)

Frequency Offset Estimation and Compensation
LT CFO validation for value 0

---

**2.4.3.4.7.0-9.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `13341628`)

Frequency Offset Estimation and Compensation
**Inputs:**

* NRBTS.NRCELL.actSupercellSingleSSB
* LT CFO metrics: longTermCfoMetric and longTermCfoMetricOfAnt, see [11150515](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksT1LvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)

**Algorithm:**

DU (for Loner if NRBTS.NRCELL.actSupercellSingleSSB=True, **Loki and Thor** **[CB016066-C] and GPU** [End CB016066-C] L1 HW) shall perform LT CFO validation:\*\*\*\*

* If longTermCfoMetric is 0 (i.e. 0+j0), DU shall not pass the Validation check.
* Else If longTermCfoMetricOfAnt *is 0 (i.e. 0+j0) for some or all antennas, DU shall shall not pass the Validation check for the corresponding antennas.*

**Outputs:**

* Validation check output for each LT CFO metric.

---

**2.4.3.4.7.0-9.0-2**  (ID: `11105401`)

Frequency Offset Estimation and Compensation
ST CFO Protection

---

**2.4.3.4.7.0-9.0-2.0-4**  (ID: `13341629`)

Frequency Offset Estimation and Compensation
For TDD FR1, for Loki and Thor L1 HW  
For FDD, for Loki and Thor L1 **for all UEs in the cell: here are the inputs and the outputs that DU shall handle for PUCCH ST CFO Protection based on 5G\_UP\_ALG\_24682\_replaced([11151348](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksYtqPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)):**

**Inputs:**

* NIDD parameter MRBTS/NRBTS/NRCELL/actDisableFoeProtection (or rrmLargestFoeGap) as per [11151348](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksYtqPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)
* "rrmLargestFoeGap" as defined in [11151348](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksYtqPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)
* rad parameter **rdUlStFoeScaler (see [11110529](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpFiB_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))**

**Algorithm:**

 DU shall perform the Max value protection for the total FOE as described in section “2.3.3.3" in 5GMax algorithm specification (see [11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

**Outputs:**

 *shortTermCfoMetric and* shortTermCfoMetricOfAnt values as per [11150823](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksVqVPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)\*\*

**[CB016066-C] NOTE: for GPU L1 HW above ST CFO Protection requirement is not supported**[End CB016066-C]\*\*\*\*

---

##### 2.4.3.4.7.0-10 Frequency Offset Estimation and Compensation Supported FOC Filter length for PUCCH Format 3 (ID: 11105411)

**2.4.3.4.7.0-10.0-7**  (ID: `13427608`)

Frequency Offset Estimation and Compensation
The FOC taps filter length values supported can be summarized as follow **:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **FOC filter length** | **Loner** | **Loki** | **Thor** | **GPU** |
| TDD FR1 | - | 5, 11 (\*) | 5, 11 (\*) | 5 |
| FDD FR1 | 5 | 5, 11 (\*) | 5, 11 (\*) | [CB016406-A]  5  [End CB016406-A] |
| First Feature | CB009107-A | CB007449-D | CB007487-B, CB007487-D | CB016066-C |

(\*) with NIDD MRBTS/NRBTS/NRCELL/actHighSpeedCell enabled.

For Algorithm details refer to [11105355](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koJt6_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ);

---

##### 2.4.3.4.7.0-11 Frequency Offset Estimation and Compensation Supported FOC Optimization Method for PUCCH Format 3 (ID: 11105428)

**2.4.3.4.7.0-11.0-7**  (ID: `13427610`)

Frequency Offset Estimation and Compensation
The FOC Optimization method is supported as follow:

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **FOC Optimization Method** | **Loner** | **Loki** | **Thor** | **GPU** |
| TDD FR1 | - | OFF, ON (\*) | REAL, ON (\*) | OFF |
| FDD FR1 | OFF | OFF, **ON (\*)** | REAL, ON (\*) | [CB016406-A]  OFF  [End CB016406-A] |
| First Feature | CB009107-A | CB007449-D | CB007487-B, CB007487-D | CB016066-C |

(\*) with NIDD MRBTS/NRBTS/NRCELL/actHighSpeedCell enabled.

For Algorithm details refer to [11105355](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koJt6_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

## 2.4.4 PRACH Reception

### 2.4.4.0-1 PRACH Reception Document Owner Yuping Li (NSB) (ID: 11105446)

### 2.4.4.1 PRACH Formats

#### 2.4.4.1.0-1 PRACH Formats Supported PRACH Formats for TDD FR1 (ID: 11105451)

**2.4.4.1.0-1.0-6**  (ID: `13338060`)

PRACH Formats
~~For TDD FR1, all subcells belonging to the same cell have to apply the same configuration for PRACH.~~

DU shall support below PRACH formats:

|  |  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **PRACH format** | **PRACH SCS** | **Loner** | **Loki** | **Thor** | GPU |
| long format 0 | 1.25kHz | Supported | Supported | Supported | [CB016061-A]  Supported  [End CB016061-A] |
| short format A2 | 30kHz | Supported | Supported | Supported | - |
| short format B4 | 30kHz | Supported | Supported | Supported | supported |
| short format C2 | 30kHz | Supported | Supported | Supported | - |
| short format C2 | 15kHz | - | Supported | Supported | - |
| **First feature** |  |  |  | CB007487-B CB007487-C | CB016066-C |

Refer to [11275328](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpGAOfvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ), [11361351](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_uc1O0PvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) and [11361410](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_uc1O2_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) for PRACH format and PRACH SCS configuration.

#### 2.4.4.1.0-2 PRACH Formats Supported PRACH Formats for TDD FR2 (ID: 11105468)

**2.4.4.1.0-2.0-2**  (ID: `11105473`)

PRACH Formats
For TDD FR2, all subcells belonging to the same cell have to apply the same configuration for PRACH.

The supported PRACH formats are:

|  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| PRACH format | PRACH SCS | Loner | Loki | Thor | vDU | First Feature |
| short format A2 | 120kHz | Supported | Supported | [CB010576-A] Supported [End CB010576-A] | - | 5GC000425-Q |
| short format C2 | 120kHz | Supported | Supported | [CB010576-A] Supported  [End CB010576-A] | - | 5GC001300-A |
| short format C0 | 120kHz | - | Supported | [CB010576-A] Supported  [End CB010576-A] | - | 5GC001305-A |
| short format B4 | 120kHz | - | Supported | [CB010576-A] Supported  [End CB010576-A] | - | CB007723-A |

---

#### 2.4.4.1.0-3 PRACH Formats Supported PRACH Formats for FDD (ID: 11105476)

**2.4.4.1.0-3.0-6**  (ID: `13427633`)

PRACH Formats
For FDD, only one PRACH configuration index is supported per subcell and the value must be different for each subcell.

DU and FHE shall support the PRACH formats in the below table:

|  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **PRACH format** | **PRACH SCS** | **Loner** | **Loki** | **Thor** | **Thor (FHE)** | **GPU** |
| long format 0 | 1.25kHz | Supported | Supported | supported | supported | supported |
| long format 1 | 1.25kHz | Supported | Supported | Supported | **-** | [CB016062-BD]  supported  [End CB016062-BD] |
| **First feature** |  |  |  | CB007487-D | CB013976-C | CB016406-A |

### 2.4.4.2 PRACH Resource Allocation

#### 2.4.4.2.0-1 PRACH Resource Allocation Introduction (ID: 11105491)

**2.4.4.2.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `11105496`)

PRACH Resource Allocation
The PRACH time and frequency resource concepts and number of SSBs per Rach Occasion (RO) concept are defined in 5G\_UP\_99\_replaced([11347309](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubLpA_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_284\_replaced([11348700](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubX2TPvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

DU shall derive the PRACH format and time domain resource allocation according to the PRACH configuration index. The supported PRACH configuration index is described in 5G\_UP\_IF\_410\_replaced([11498096](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_tSO0O_v1Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

For TDD, DU shall decide the supported PRACH configuration index base on supported frame structure.

The supported time resource allocation per technology per L1 HW are described in:

* TDD FR1: 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1403\_replaced([11105499](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koMKIfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
* TDD FR2: 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1407\_replaced([11105510](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koMKJfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
* FDD: 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1411\_replaced([11105518](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koMKKPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

The supported frequency resource allocation is described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1414\_replaced([11105528](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koMKLPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

DU shall support one SSB [CB012192-A] or more than one SSBs [End CB012192-A] map to one RO via parameter NRCELL.nbrOfSsbPerRachOccasion (see 5G\_UP\_120\_replaced([11347397](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubMQGPvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

DU shall support one SSB map to one RO always for all technology and all L1 HWs.

[CB012192-A]

DU shall support more than one SSBs map to one RO in TDD FR1 when beamforming enabled (see 5G\_RAN\_OP\_3919\_replaced([11693562](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_D32e5v9nEe-D3seUwaI0tg))).

[End CB012192-A]

The supported number of SSBs per RO per L1 HW is decribed in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_28174\_replaced([11105533](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koMKMPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

#### 2.4.4.2.0-5 PRACH Resource Allocation Frequency Domain Resource Allocation (ID: 11105528)

**2.4.4.2.0-5.0-1**  (ID: `11105530`)

PRACH Resource Allocation
In frequency domain,

- the supported number of ROs (RACH Occasions) in frequency domain is 1.

- the supported PRB offset values are: [0 … maximum PRACH PRB offset]

The PRACH PRBs allocation and offset are counted in number of corresponding PUSCH PRBs base on PUSCH SCS, the “maximum PRACH PRB offset” is calculated as below:

“maximum PRACH PRB offset” = “maximum number of UL PRBs” – “number of PRACH PRBs”

- The “maximum number of UL PRBs” shall be derived according to the UL bandwidths supported in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_17\_replaced([11094258](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmaoiPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

- The “number of PRACH PRBs” shall be derived according to Table 6.3.3.2-1 of 38.211 for different PRACH format and scs as well as the PUSCH scs.

---

#### 2.4.4.2.0-6 PRACH Resource Allocation Number of SSBs per RO (ID: 11105533)

**2.4.4.2.0-6.0-3**  (ID: `13338110`)

PRACH Resource Allocation
DU shall support below number of SSBs per RO ([11105491](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koLjH_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)):

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **L1 HW** | **Number of SSBs per RO** | First Feature |
| **Loner** | 1 |  |
| **Loki** | 1, 2, 4 for TDD FR1 and BF enabled |  |
| **Thor** | 1 ,2, 4 for TDD FR1 and BF enabled |  |
| **GPU** | 1 | CB016066-C |

PRACH Resource Allocation
One PRACH time occasion is supported with starting symbol = 0 for PRACH format 0. There's 1 preamble repetition per occasion, refer to 3GPP 38.211 Table 6.3.3.1-1.
 
Note that the slot size for PRACH format 0 is based on SCS 30kHz.
 
[image-0][image\_desc]The image displays a diagram illustrating the structure of a PRACH (Physical Random Access Channel) format 0 sequence. The diagram is divided into two main sections.
The top section is a long, horizontal rectangle divided into numerous vertical segments. Most of these segments are filled with a light blue color and have thin black vertical lines separating them. There is a single, thicker black vertical line positioned roughly in the middle of this section, dividing the light blue segments into two groups. The rightmost segment in this section is filled with white.
The bottom section is a shorter, horizontal rectangle that is also divided into segments. From left to right, it begins with a small, light blue rectangular segment labeled "CP". To the right of "CP" is a larger, light blue rectangular segment containing the text "PRACH format 0 sequence". To the right of this text segment is a small, white rectangular segment labeled "GT".
The overall impression is a visual representation of data or signal structure, with different components clearly demarcated.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_2942.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

One PRACH time occasion is supported with starting symbol = 0 for PRACH format 0. There's 1 preamble repetition per occasion, refer to 3GPP 38.211 Table 6.3.3.1-1.
 
Note that the slot size for PRACH format 0 is based on SCS 15 kHz.
[image-0][image\_desc]The image displays two diagrams related to PRACH (Physical Random Access Channel) format 0.
The top diagram is a horizontal bar divided into 14 equal light blue sections, separated by thin black vertical lines. The rightmost section is white. This diagram appears to represent a sequence of time slots or resources.
The bottom diagram is a horizontal bar with two distinct sections. The leftmost section is a small, light blue rectangle labeled "CP". To the right of "CP" is a larger, light blue rectangle containing the text "PRACH format 0 sequence". To the right of this text is a small, white rectangle labeled "GT". This diagram illustrates the structure of PRACH format 0, indicating a Cyclic Prefix (CP) followed by the PRACH sequence and a Guard Time (GT).[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_2944.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

One PRACH time occasion is supported with starting symbol = 0 for PRACH format 1. There're 2 preamble repetitions per occasion, refer to 3GPP 38.211 Table 6.3.3.1-1.
 
Note that the slot size for PRACH format 1 is based on SCS 15 kHz.
 
[image-0][image\_desc]The image displays a diagram illustrating a communication signal structure. The top portion of the diagram shows a long rectangular bar divided into many vertical segments. The majority of these segments are filled with a light blue color, while the rightmost segments are white. The segments are separated by thin black lines, with a few thicker black lines interspersed, indicating divisions within the signal.
Below this bar, there is a second, shorter rectangular bar divided into three distinct sections. The leftmost section is a light gray rectangle labeled "CP". The middle section is a light blue rectangle labeled "PRACH format 1 sequence". The rightmost section is a light gray rectangle labeled "GT". This bottom bar represents the breakdown of the signal shown in the top bar into its constituent parts.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_2946.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]
 
File: Drawings for Prach Formats.vsd

Three PRACH time occasions are supported in one PRACH slot with starting symbol = 0. There're 4 preamble repetitions per occasion, refer to 3GPP 38.211 Table 6.3.3.1-2.
 [image-0][image\_desc]The image displays a horizontal arrangement of vertical rectangular cells. The cells are predominantly light blue, with thin black lines separating them. There are three prominent, thicker black lines that divide the arrangement into four larger sections.
The first large section contains three light blue cells, with the text "A2" centered within the middle cell.
The second large section also contains three light blue cells, with the text "A2" centered within the middle cell.
The third large section contains three light blue cells, with the text "A2" centered within the middle cell.
The fourth and final section contains two white cells.
The overall impression is that of a grid or a table with some cells containing text and others being blank.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_2948.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]
 
One PRACH time occasion is also supported with starting symbol = 9.               
 [image-1][image\_desc]The image displays a grid of rectangles. The grid is arranged horizontally, with vertical lines dividing the space into individual cells. The majority of the cells are white. However, starting from the 11th cell from the left, there are four consecutive cells that are filled with a light blue color. Within the second of these blue cells (the 12th cell from the left), the text "A2" is prominently displayed in black. The remaining cells to the right of the blue section are white. The overall impression is that of a segmented layout, possibly representing a table or a visual organizational structure.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_2948.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]
Note, the figures provide a view from 3GPP definition, refer to [11347318](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubLpB_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) for the valid ROs (from UE POV), and refer to [11152282](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kse0TvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) for the ROs to be received per PRACH slot.

Three PRACH time occasions are supported in one PRACH slot with starting symbol = 0. There're 4 preamble repetitions per occasion, refer to 3GPP 38.211 Table 6.3.3.1-2.
 [image-0][image\_desc]The image displays a horizontal arrangement of vertical rectangular cells. The cells are predominantly light blue, with thin black lines separating them. There are three prominent, thicker black lines that divide the arrangement into four larger sections.
The first large section contains three light blue cells, with the text "A2" centered within the middle cell.
The second large section also contains three light blue cells, with the text "A2" centered within the middle cell.
The third large section contains three light blue cells, with the text "A2" centered within the middle cell.
The fourth and final section contains two white cells.
The overall impression is that of a grid or a table with some cells containing text and others being blank.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_2950.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]
 
 
Two PRACH time occasions are also supported with starting symbol = 5.               
 [image-1][image\_desc]The image displays a horizontal arrangement of vertical rectangular cells. The cells are divided into two distinct sections by a thick black vertical line.
The left section consists of eight white cells. The first seven cells are entirely white, while the eighth cell has a thin horizontal black line at the top, suggesting it might be a header or a separator.
The right section is further divided into two sub-sections by another thick black vertical line. The first sub-section contains four light blue cells. The first two cells in this sub-section are labeled with the text "A2". The second sub-section also contains four light blue cells, and the first two cells in this sub-section are also labeled with the text "A2". The last cell in the entire right section is white.
The overall impression is that of a structured layout, possibly representing data or a visual representation of a process or system. The repetition of "A2" in the blue cells suggests a categorization or grouping.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_2950.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]
Note, the figures provide a view from 3GPP definition, refer to [11347318](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubLpB_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) for the valid ROs (from UE POV), and refer to [11152282](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kse0TvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) for the ROs to be received per PRACH slot.

One PRACH time occasion is supported in one PRACH slot with starting symbol = 0 and 2. There're 12 preamble repetitions per occasion, refer to 3GPP 38.211 Table 6.3.3.1-2.
 
 [image-0][image\_desc]The image displays a grid of 18 vertical rectangles. The first two rectangles on the left are white, separated by a thin black line. The remaining 16 rectangles are filled with a light blue color and are also separated by thin black lines. The text "B4" is centrally located within the 9th rectangle from the left. The background of the image is black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_2952.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]
 [image-1][image\_desc]The image displays a horizontal bar divided into multiple vertical segments. The majority of these segments are filled with a light blue color, and they are separated by thin black lines. Within one of the light blue segments, the text "B4" is visible. Towards the right end of the bar, there are two segments that are filled with white. These white segments are also separated by a thin black line. The overall impression is that of a segmented bar, possibly representing a progress bar or a series of categories, with a label in one of the segments.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_2952.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]
Note, the figures provide a view from 3GPP definition, refer to [11347318](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubLpB_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) for the valid ROs (from UE POV), and refer to [11152282](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kse0TvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) for the ROs to be received per PRACH slot.

Two PRACH time occasions are supported in one PRACH slot with starting symbol = 2 and one PRACH time occasion is supported with starting symbol = 8. There're 4 preamble repetitions per occasion, refer to 3GPP 38.211 Table 6.3.3.1-2.
 
 [image-0][image\_desc]The image displays a series of vertical rectangular panels arranged horizontally. The leftmost three panels are white, while the remaining panels are a light blue color. Within the light blue panels, the text "C2" is visible in two separate panels, centered horizontally and vertically within each. The panels are separated by thin black vertical lines, and the entire arrangement is bordered by a thick black outline. The overall composition suggests a segmented or compartmentalized structure.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_2954.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]
 [image-1][image\_desc]The image displays a horizontal arrangement of two distinct sections. The left section is composed of ten equally sized, vertical white rectangular cells, separated by thin black lines. The right section is a single, larger light blue rectangular area. This blue area is further subdivided by thin black lines into five equally sized, vertical cells. The text "C2" is prominently displayed in the center of the middle cell within the blue section. The background of the entire image is black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_2954.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]
Note, the figures provide a view from 3GPP definition, refer to [11347318](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubLpB_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) for the valid ROs (from UE POV), and refer to [11152282](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kse0TvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) for the ROs to be received per PRACH slot.

Two PRACH time occasions are supported in one PRACH slot with starting symbol = 0 or 2, and one PRACH time occasion is supported with starting symbol = 7. There're 4 preamble repetitions per occasion, refer to 3GPP 38.211 Table 6.3.3.1-2.
 
 [image-0][image\_desc]The image displays a grid-like structure composed of vertical rectangular cells. The cells are predominantly light blue, with thin black lines separating them. There are two distinct sections of these blue cells, separated by a thicker black vertical line.
Within the first section of blue cells, the text "C2" is centered within one of the cells. The second section of blue cells also contains the text "C2" centered within one of its cells.
To the right of the blue cells, there are three white cells, also separated by thin black vertical lines. The leftmost of these white cells is slightly narrower than the other two.
The overall arrangement suggests a visual representation of data or a layout, possibly for a user interface or a diagram.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_2956.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]
 [image-1][image\_desc]The image displays a series of vertical rectangular panels arranged horizontally. The leftmost three panels are white, while the remaining panels are a light blue color. Within the light blue panels, the text "C2" is visible in two separate panels, centered horizontally and vertically within each. The panels are separated by thin black vertical lines, and the entire arrangement is bordered by a thick black outline. The overall composition suggests a segmented or compartmentalized structure.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_2956.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]
 [image-2][image\_desc]The image displays a horizontal arrangement of vertical rectangular cells. The cells are divided into three distinct sections. The leftmost section consists of ten white cells. The middle section, which is highlighted with a light blue background, contains six cells. The text "C2" is centered within the middle cell of this blue section. The rightmost section consists of two white cells, one on each side of the blue section. The overall impression is that of a segmented bar or a visual representation of data categories.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_2956.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2]
Note, the figures provide a view from 3GPP definition, refer to [11347318](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubLpB_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) for the valid ROs (from UE POV), and refer to [11152282](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kse0TvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) for the ROs to be received per PRACH slot.

Seven PRACH time occasions are supported in one PRACH slot with starting symbol = 0 and three PRACH time occasions is supported with starting symbol = 8. There're 2 preamble repetitions per occasion, refer to 3GPP 38.211 Table 6.3.3.1-2.
 
 [image-0][image\_desc]The image displays a series of seven vertical rectangular panels, arranged horizontally. Each panel is light blue and has a thick black border. Inside each panel, a thin black vertical line runs down the center. On the left side of this central line, the text "C0" is present. The "C" is positioned above the "0", with a small horizontal line separating them. This "C0" text and the separating line are repeated identically in each of the seven panels. The background of the image is black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_2958.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]
 
 [image-1][image\_desc]The image displays a series of vertical bars, arranged in a grid-like structure. The bars are divided into two distinct sections. The left section consists of eight white bars, separated by thin black lines. The right section contains three light blue bars, also separated by black lines. Each of these blue bars has the text "C0" written vertically in the center, with a horizontal line above and below the text. The background of the image is black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_2958.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]
Note, the figures provide a view from 3GPP definition, refer to [11347318](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubLpB_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) for the valid ROs (from UE POV), and refer to [11152282](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kse0TvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) for the ROs to be received per PRACH slot.

### 2.4.4.3 PRACH Configuration

#### 2.4.4.3.0-1 PRACH Configuration Introduction of PRACH Root Sequence and Cyclic Shift (ID: 11105620)

**2.4.4.3.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11105623`)

PRACH Configuration
The first logical root sequence index and the cyclic shift used for preamble generation is defined in 3GPP 38.211 section 6.3.3.1.

The cyclic shift is dependent on Ncs which is determined by the combination of restrictedSet configuration and zeroCorrelationZone configuration.

The first logical root sequence index is configurable via NIDD parameter MRBTS/NRBTS/NRCELL/prachRootSequenceIndex.

The zeroCorrelationZone is configurable via NIDD parameter MRBTS/NRBTS/NRCELL/zeroCorrelationZoneConfig.

The restrictedSet is configurable via NIDD parameter MRBTS/NRBTS/NRCELL/prachSequenceType.

The supported configurations per technology per L1 HW are described in:

* TDD FR1: 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1437\_replaced([11105624](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koNYTPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
* TDD FR2: 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1441\_replaced([11105639](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koNYUfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
* FDD: 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1444\_replaced([11105647](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koNYVPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

---

#### 2.4.4.3.0-2 PRACH Configuration PRACH Root Sequence and ZeroCorrelationZone Configuration For TDD FR1 (ID: 11105624)

**2.4.4.3.0-2.0-6**  (ID: `13463639`)

PRACH Configuration
The supported first logical root sequence indices (see [11105620](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koNYSvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) are:

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **PRACH formats** | **first PRACH root sequence index** |  |  |  |
| **Loner** | **Loki** | **GPU** | **Thor** |  |
| Short formats | [0..137] | [0..137] | 0 | [0..137] |
| Long formats | [0..837] | [0..837] | **[CB016061-A] [0..837]** [End CB016061-A]\*\*\*\* | [0..837] |
| **First feature** |  |  | CB016066-C | CB007487-B CB007487-C |

The supported zeroCorrelationZoneConfig values (see [11105620](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koNYSvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) are:

|  |  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **PRACH formats** | **zeroCorrelationZoneConfig** |  |  |  | **Ncs** |
| **Loner** | **Loki** | **GPU** | **Thor** |  |  |
| Short formats | {0, 4..15} for Unrestricted set\* | {0, 4..15} for Unrestricted set\* | 14 for Unrestricted set\* | {0, 4..15} for Unrestricted set\* | see Table 6.3.3.1-7 in 38.211 |
| Long format 0 | {1..15} for Unrestricted set {0..14} for restricted set **A** | {1..15} for Unrestricted set {0..14} for restricted set **A** {0..12} for restricted set **B** | **[CB016061-A] {1..15} for Unrestricted set** [End CB016061-A]\*\*\*\* | {1..15} for Unrestricted set {0..14} for restricted set **A** {0..12} for restricted set **B** | see Table 6.3.3.1-5 in 38.211 |
| **First feature** |  |  | CB016066-C | CB007487-B CB007487-C | - |

\*For more details about zeroCorrelationZoneConfig value restriction, refer to [11105853](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koQbm_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

#### 2.4.4.3.0-3 PRACH Configuration PRACH Root Sequence and ZeroCorrelationZone Configuration For TDD FR2 (ID: 11105639)

**2.4.4.3.0-3.0-2**  (ID: `11105645`)

PRACH Configuration
For L1 Loner, Loki and [CB010576-A] Thor [End CB010576-A] HWs:

The supported first logical root sequence indices (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1435\_replaced([11105620](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koNYSvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) are: [0..137] for short formats.

The supported zeroCorrelationZoneConfig values (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1435\_replaced([11105620](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koNYSvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) are: {0, 4..15} for short formats Unrestricted set. For more details about zeroCorrelationZoneConfig value restriction, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_16688\_replaced([11105853](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koQbm_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

The supported zeroCorrelationZoneConfig value is 0 for preamble detection with extended cell range algorithm enabled (5G\_UP\_ALG\_1473\_replaced([11105938](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koRpu_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

#### 2.4.4.3.0-5 PRACH Configuration PRACH UL Buffering for TDD (ID: 11105662)

**2.4.4.3.0-5.0-1**  (ID: `11105664`)

PRACH Configuration
For L1 Loner HW TDD, PRACH can be processed during UL slots in real time, or can be buffered and processed during later DL slots in non-real-time.

### 2.4.4.4 PRACH Configuration Online Modification

#### 2.4.4.4.0-1 PRACH Configuration Online Modification PRACH Root Sequence Online Modification (ID: 12495459)

**2.4.4.4.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `12495489`)

PRACH Configuration Online Modification
**Purpose:**

DU shall support the PRACH first logical root sequence index parameter online modification via cell reconfiguration (see [12504043: PRACH parameters support online modification](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_B-o_whSKEfCph-OiSyk16Q?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Frm%2Fcm%2Fstream%2F_KjDNNu3wEe-rL47x_rCCmw)).

**Input:**

- the existing first logical root sequence index in use

- a valid new first logical root sequence index from cell reconfiguration

**Algorithm:**

DU shall compare the new first logical root sequence index with the existing first logical root sequence index. Only if they are different, DU shall replace the first logical root sequence index with the new value and re-generate the reference preamble sequence (see [12504387: Reference Preamble Generation and Conjugate Multiplication](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_rewD8BTZEfCV0v_Ffc8sJw?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)).

After the re-generation, DU shall start to use the new reference preamble sequence from the next RACH slot reception.

**Output:**

- DU generates new reference preamble sequence if the first logical root sequence index is modified.

For the supported L1 HWs, refer to [12504203: Supported Online Modification HWs](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_diD0URSREfCph-OiSyk16Q?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9).

---

#### 2.4.4.4.0-2 PRACH Configuration Online Modification PRACH zeroCorrelationZone Online Modification (ID: 12514619)

**2.4.4.4.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `12514620`)

PRACH Configuration Online Modification
**Purpose:**

DU shall support the PRACH zeroCorrelationZone parameter online modification via cell reconfiguration (see [12504043: PRACH parameters support online modification](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_B-o_whSKEfCph-OiSyk16Q?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Frm%2Fcm%2Fstream%2F_KjDNNu3wEe-rL47x_rCCmw)).

**Input:**

- the existing zeroCorrelationZone in use

- a valid new zeroCorrelationZone from cell reconfiguration

**Algorithm:**

DU shall compare the new zeroCorrelationZone with the existing zeroCorrelationZone. Only if they are different:

* DU shall replace the existing zeroCorrelationZone with the new value and re-generate the reference preamble sequence (see [12504387: Reference Preamble Generation and Conjugate Multiplication](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_rewD8BTZEfCV0v_Ffc8sJw?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)).
* DU shall re-calculate the PRACH DTX threshold (see [11105812: PRACH DTX Threshold](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koP0jvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9))

After the re-generation and re-calculation completed, DU shall start to use the new preamble sequence from the next RACH slot reception.

**Output:**

- DU generates new reference preamble sequence if the zeroCorrelationZone is modified.

- DU calculates new DTX threshold for preamble detection if the zeroCorrelationZone is modified.

For the supported L1 HWs, refer to [12504203: Supported Online Modification HWs](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_diD0URSREfCph-OiSyk16Q?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9).

---

#### 2.4.4.4.0-3 PRACH Configuration Online Modification PRACH pfaTargetPRACH Online Modification (ID: 12694173)

**2.4.4.4.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `12694174`)

PRACH Configuration Online Modification
**Purpose:**

DU shall support the PRACH pfaTargetPRACH parameter online modification via cell reconfiguration (see [12504043: PRACH parameters support online modification](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_B-o_whSKEfCph-OiSyk16Q?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Frm%2Fcm%2Fstream%2F_KjDNNu3wEe-rL47x_rCCmw)).

**Input:**

- the existing pfaTargetPRACH in use

- a valid new pfaTargetPRACH from cell reconfiguration

**Algorithm:**

DU shall compare the new pfaTargetPRACH with the existing one. Only if they are different:

* DU shall replace the existing pfaTargetPRACH with the new value, and re-calculate the PRACH DTX threshold (see [11105812: PRACH DTX Threshold](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koP0jvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9))

After the re-calculation ready, DU shall start to use the new PRACH DTX threshold from the next RACH slot reception. The PRACH false alarm and miss detection performance shall be guaranteed based on the new pfaTargetPRACH (see [11105791](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koP0hvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

**Output:**

- DU calculates new DTX threshold for preamble detection if the pfaTargetPRACH is modified.

For the supported L1 HWs, refer to [12504203: Supported Online Modification HWs](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_diD0URSREfCph-OiSyk16Q?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9).

---

#### 2.4.4.4.0-4 PRACH Configuration Online Modification PRACH adjustPrachThresholdOffset Online Modification (ID: 12694178)

**2.4.4.4.0-4.0-1**  (ID: `12694179`)

PRACH Configuration Online Modification
**Purpose:**

DU shall support the PRACH adjustPrachThresholdOffset parameter online modification via cell reconfiguration (see [12504043: PRACH parameters support online modification](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_B-o_whSKEfCph-OiSyk16Q?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Frm%2Fcm%2Fstream%2F_KjDNNu3wEe-rL47x_rCCmw)).

**Input:**

- the existing adjustPrachThresholdOffset in use

- a valid new adjustPrachThresholdOffset from cell reconfiguration

**Algorithm:**

DU shall compare the new adjustPrachThresholdOffset with the existing one. Only if they are different:

* DU shall replace the existing adjustPrachThresholdOffset with the new value

DU shall start to use the new adjustPrachThresholdOffset value for PRACH DTX threshold adjustment from the next PRACH slot reception, see [11105826](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koQbkfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

**Output:**

- DU adjusts DTX threshold using the new configured adjustPrachThresholdOffset.

For the supported L1 HWs, refer to [12504203: Supported Online Modification HWs](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_diD0URSREfCph-OiSyk16Q?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9).

---

#### 2.4.4.4.0-5 PRACH Configuration Online Modification Supported Online Modification HWs (ID: 12504203)

**2.4.4.4.0-5.0-3**  (ID: `12694182`)

PRACH Configuration Online Modification
The PRACH parameters online modification is supported in below L1 HWs:

| Loki | Supported | Supported | Supported | Supported |
| Thor | Supported | Supported | Supported | Supported |
| First Feature | CNI-142789-A | CB014093-B | CB014330-A | CB014330-A |

The PRACH Receiver [CB008577-A] or sub-receiver [End CB008577-A] Structure:
[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a flowchart illustrating a signal processing pipeline. The pipeline is divided into two main sections: "Frequency Domain" and "Time Domain".
**Frequency Domain Section:**
This section begins with two input streams labeled "RX stream 1" and "RX stream M". These streams are processed by a block labeled "CP Removal & Decimation & FFT (1)". The output of this block goes into a "De-mapping" block. Following "De-mapping" is "Coherent Combining (2)", which then feeds into "FD AGC". The output of "FD AGC" is then subjected to "Conjugate Multiplication". Finally, an "IFFT" block transforms the signal back to the time domain.
**Time Domain Section:**
This section starts with the output from the "IFFT" block, which is fed into a "Power Metric |.|²" block. The output of this block then goes into a "Non-coherent Combining" block. This is followed by "Harmonic Combining (4)". The output of "Harmonic Combining (4)" is then processed by "Threshold Calculation & MAD & Preamble Peak Detection". The final block in this section is "High Power Mitigation & Harmonic Removal".
An output arrow from the "High Power Mitigation & Harmonic Removal" block indicates "Preamble detected & Time Offset estimated".
The overall flowchart depicts a process for detecting a preamble and estimating a time offset in received signal streams, likely in a wireless communication system.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/img-03041446.png[/image\_path][/image-0]
Notes:
(1) The CP Removal & Decimation & FFT are located on DU for CPRI FH, and located on Radio for eCPRI FH.
(2) Coherent Combining is only used for short formats, see [11105701](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koOmYfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).
(3) FD AGC usage is dependent on L1 HW types, see [11105715](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koOmZvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).
(4) Harmonic Combining is only used for long formats with Restricted type, see [11105762](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koPNefvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).
(5) One PRACH Receiver includes one or multiple PRACH Sub-receivers, and the number of streams (Nrx) input for one PRACH Sub-receiver is described in [13420911](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_LQpwUReZEfGFMKAOrP5qqw?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9):
\* For TDD FR1 beamforming, if multiple SSBs associated with one RO (NRCELL.nbrOfSsbPerRachOccasion higher than one), one PRACH Receiver includes multiple PRACH Sub-receivers, the number of PRACH sub-receivers is given in [11106057](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koTe5fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ). Refer to [12772147](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_0bEpgYlrEfCrqOdGJPoCRA) for the PRACH Receiver overall structure.
\* [CB008577-A] For FDD, if NRCELL.actPrachProbing = True, one PRACH Receiver includes multiple PRACH Sub-receivers, the above structure corresponds to one PRACH sub-receiver structure, the number of PRACH sub-receivers is given in [11106062](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_lfj4APvHEe-AqvopbP1qhQ), each sub-receiver receive preambles from one beam.
[End CB008577-A]
\* Otherwise, the PRACH Receiver includes only one PRACH Sub-receiver, as such the PRACH Sub-reciever is equal to the PRACH Receiver.
The figure is available at folder:
