The PRACH Receiver Structure for multiple SSBs per RO:
[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a block diagram illustrating a signal processing pipeline for preamble detection and time offset estimation in a wireless communication system, likely for 5G NR (New Radio) or similar technologies. The diagram is divided into two main sections: "Frequency Domain" and "Time Domain".
The pipeline processes multiple receive (RX) streams, denoted as "RX stream 1" to "RX stream 2M". The processing is shown in parallel for two groups of RX streams, suggesting a system with multiple receive antennas or diversity.
**Frequency Domain Processing:**
Each parallel processing branch begins with:
\* **De-mapping:** This block likely separates the received signal into different components or channels.
\* **Coherent Combining (1):** This step combines signals from different RX streams coherently, aiming to improve signal quality.
\* **FD AGC (2):** Frequency Domain Automatic Gain Control adjusts the signal power in the frequency domain.
\* **Conjugate Multiplication:** This operation is often used in synchronization and channel estimation.
**Time Domain Processing:**
The output from the Frequency Domain section is then processed in the Time Domain:
\* **IFFT:** An Inverse Fast Fourier Transform converts the signal back to the time domain.
\* **Power Metric |.|²:** This block calculates the squared magnitude of the signal, which is a common metric for detecting signal presence.
\* **Non-coherent Combining:** This step combines signals non-coherently, which is more robust to phase variations than coherent combining.
\* **Harmonic Combining (3):** This block likely combines harmonic components of the signal, which can be useful for preamble detection.
\* **Threshold Calculation & MAD & Preamble Peak Detection:** This stage involves calculating detection thresholds, using Median Absolute Deviation (MAD) for robustness, and identifying peaks that correspond to preamble occurrences.
\* **High Power Mitigation & Harmonic Removal:** This block addresses potential issues with high power interference and removes unwanted harmonic components.
\* **Select preambles per SSB:** This final block selects the detected preambles for each Synchronization Signal Block (SSB).
**Outputs:**
The outputs of the pipeline are:
\* **Preamble detected & Time Offset estimated:** This indicates the successful detection of a preamble and the estimation of the signal's arrival time.
\* **Report to L2 per slot:** The detected information is reported to Layer 2 (L2) for further processing on a per-slot basis.
A key processing note indicated below the Frequency Domain section states: "Process number of preambles per root sequence \* number of root per SSB every 2RX streams". This suggests that the processing is organized around root sequences and SSBs, and that every two RX streams are processed together in this manner.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/img-03041444.png[/image\_path][/image-0]
Notes:
(1) Coherent Combining is only used for short formats, see [11105701](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koOmYfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).
(2) FD AGC usage is dependent on L1 HW types, see [11105715](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koOmZvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).
(3) Harmonic Combining is only used for long formats with Restricted type, see [11105762](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koPNefvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).
(4) There're M sub-receivers where M is the number of SSBs mapped to each RO, see [11106057](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koTe5fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).
The figure is available at folder:

#### 2.4.4.5.2 Decimation

##### 2.4.4.5.2.0-1 Decimation Overview (ID: 11105682)

**2.4.4.5.2.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11105685`)

Decimation
For PRACH long formats and short formats with Δf/ΔfRA >1, where Δf is the PUSCH SCS, and ΔfRA is the PRACH SCS, the PRACH receiver FFT size will be larger compare to PUSCH channels, especially for PRACH long formats, the FFT size will be much larger and complex to implement in practice, so decimation shall be performed to reduce PRACH FFT size.

For PRACH short formats with Δf/ΔfRA =1, decimation can also be performed to reduce FFT size and save HW resources.

Dependent on the decimation filters chain that L1 and RU HWs can support, the reduced FFT size will be different but must bigger than the PRACH sequence length.

The detail introduction is described in section “2.5.2 Decimation filter” of the 5GMax algorithm specification (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_695\_replaced([11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

The decimation supported in different L1 HWs can be found in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_16312\_replaced([11105687](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koN_XPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

##### 2.4.4.5.2.0-2 Decimation Configurations Support Decimation (ID: 11105687)

**2.4.4.5.2.0-2.0-4**  (ID: `11105698`)

Decimation
The PRACH decimation (5G\_UP\_ALG\_15551\_replaced([11105682](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koN_WvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is supported in below L1 HWs:

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| L1 HW | Decimation |  | First Feature |
| Long Formats | Short Formats |  |  |
| Loner | Supported | - |  |
| Loki | Supported | Supported for CPRI only |  |
| Thor | Supported | [CB010575-A] Supported for CPRI only [End CB010575-A] | CB007487-B CB007487-C CB007487-D |

#### 2.4.4.5.3 Whitening Sub-Band for GPU

##### 2.4.4.5.3.0-1 Whitening Sub-Band for GPU Whitening and Sub-Band spread (ID: 13491924)

**2.4.4.5.3.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `13494339`)

Whitening Sub-Band for GPU
DU for GPU L1 HW shall perform:

* Whitening algorithm defined as process of normalization and decorrelation of the received signal within individual frequency sub-bands.
* Rational: DU shall compensate for the non-uniform channel characteristics,resulting in a signal with uniform power distribution.
* Sub-band Spread algorithm defined as process of dispersing the signal energy with in a frequency sub-band.
* Rational: DU shall average out the impact of localized channel fades and narrow band interference to improve PRACH detection robustness and the stability of signal estimation.

Refer to section §2.5.5 "Sub-Band Whitening" of 5GMax algorithm specification ([11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

#### 2.4.4.5.4 Coherent Combining

##### 2.4.4.5.4.0-1 Coherent Combining (ID: 11105704)

**2.4.4.5.4.0-1.0-4**  (ID: `13338470`)

Coherent Combining
In TDD, for Loner, Loki, Thor **[CB016066-C] and GPU** [End CB016066-C] L1 HW, to improve the PRACH detection performance and reduce the computation resources for short formats only, the PRACH sequence repetitions could be divided into several groups.\*\*\*\*

DU shall perform the coherent combining operation in each group before non-coherent combining of the groups.

The PRACH coherent combining algorithms are described in section 2.5.5 of the 5GMax algorithm specification (see [11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

The coherent combining length for each PRACH format is described in [11275328](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpGAOfvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) according to 5GMax algorithm specification. Note, for Loki, Thor **[CB016066-C] GPU** [End CB016066-C] L1 HW,  the value is hardcoded in L1 for all PRACH short formats instead of derived from [11275328](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpGAOfvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ). This hardcoding is decided by L1 team to optimize the resources in the HW.\*\*\*\*

DU for Loki and Thor**L1 HW shall apply the shorter coherent combining lengths on the high-speed cells to avoid the performance degradation that results from higher FO.**

---

#### 2.4.4.5.5 FDAGC Algorithm

##### 2.4.4.5.5.0-1 FDAGC Algorithm Overview (ID: 11105718)

**2.4.4.5.5.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `11105723`)

FDAGC Algorithm
FDAGC (Frequency Domain Automatic Gain Control) algorithm is introduced to mitigate the effects of strong interference (from same cell or neighbor cells) near to the PRACH subband in frequency domain by ‘whitening’ the PRACH subband, so the false alarm performance can be improved.

[CB012261-A]When the cell parameter NRBTS/NRCELL/actPrachMultiplexingLevel is set to activate as per 5G\_RAN\_OP\_3686\_replaced([11693118](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_D3vKKP9nEe-D3seUwaI0tg)), the DU shall support FDAGC algorithm for additional scenarios as given in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_13863\_replaced([11105726](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koOmavvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) [End CB012261-A]

The FDAGC algorithm is described in section "2.5.1 General Receiver Structure" of the 5GMax algorithm specification (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_695\_replaced([11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

The FDAGC supported in different L1 HWs can be found in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_13863\_replaced([11105726](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koOmavvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

##### 2.4.4.5.5.0-2 FDAGC Algorithm Configurations Support FDAGC (ID: 11105726)

**2.4.4.5.5.0-2.0-5**  (ID: `13491921`)

FDAGC Algorithm
DU shall support the FDAGC algorithm (5G\_UP\_ALG\_13861\_replaced([11105718](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koOmZ_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) ~~is supported~~ in below L1 HWs:

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Loki | Supported for long and short PRACH format in FR1; Supported for short PRACH format in FR2 TDD (except when parameter MRBTS/NRBTS/NRCELL/rangeExtension is enabled, see [11105938](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koRpu_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) ), see below\*. | PR649452 |
| Thor | Supported for long and short PRACH format in FR1; Supported for short PRACH format in FR2 TDD (except when parameter MRBTS/NRBTS/NRCELL/rangeExtension is enabled, see [11105938](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koRpu_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), see below\*\*. | CB007487-B CB007487-C CB007487-D |

* + For Loki L1 HW: the DU shall always enable FDAGC regardless the configuration of NRBTS/NRCELL/actPrachMultiplexingLevel for:
* HST with PRACH short formats only in TDD FR1
* FDD FR1 for Ecpri.

For other cases, the activation of FDAGC depends on this cell parameter actPrachMultiplexingLevel configuration

* \*\* For Thor L1 HW, the DU shall always enable FDAGC for all the configurations when it’s applicable regardless of NRBTS/NRCELL/actPrachMultiplexingLevel configuration.

~~That’s why, only additional details are added in the above table for Thor, without functional impact. This last sentence could be removed in next version.~~

**[CB016061-E] NOTE: For GPU L1 HW, the DU shall always enable PRACH whitening algorithm:  
- before "Coherent Combining" step processing (refer to [11105701](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koOmYfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)),  
- specifically referred as "Sub-Band Whitening" step processing (refer to [13491922](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_XZXu4EM5EfGy8ZlFziQwLA?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)),  
- regardless of NRBTS/NRCELL/actPrachMultiplexingLevel configuration.**[CB016061-E]\*\*\*\*

#### 2.4.4.5.6 Reference Preamble Generation and Conjugate Multiplication

##### 2.4.4.5.6.0-1 Reference Preamble Generation and Conjugate Multiplication Overview (ID: 12504388)

**2.4.4.5.6.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `12504389`)

Reference Preamble Generation and Conjugate Multiplication
Purpose:

DU shall generate the reference preamble sequence and perform conjugate multiplication.

Input:

* the preamble format (see [11105449: PRACH Formats](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koK8D_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9))
* the total number of preambles per RACH occasion (see [11347388: totalNumberOfRA-Preambles supported values](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubMQFvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Frm%2Fcm%2Fstream%2F_KjDNNu3wEe-rL47x_rCCmw))
* the zeroCorrelationZone, restrictedSet and first logical root sequence index configuration(see [11105620: Introduction of PRACH Root Sequence and Cyclic Shift](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koNYSvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9))
* the received signal after FDAGC (see [11105715: FDAGC Algorithm](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koOmZvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9))

Algorithms:

DU shall generate the reference preamble sequence in frequency domain according to the preamble format, the total number of preambles per RACH occasion, the zeroCorrelationZone, the restrictedSet and the first logical root sequence index configured.

DU shall multiply the received signal after FDAGC with the conjugate reference preamble sequence in frequency domain.

Output:

* The multiplication result of the received signal and the reference preamble sequence in frequency domain.

For the supported L1 HWs, refer to

---

#### 2.4.4.5.7 IFFT, Power Metric and Non-coherent Combining

##### 2.4.4.5.7.0-1 IFFT, Power Metric and Non-coherent Combining IFFT and Power Metric Calculation (ID: 11105741)

**2.4.4.5.7.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11105743`)

IFFT, Power Metric and Non-coherent Combining
IFFT is performed to get the time domain correlation result between the received signal and the reference Zadoff-Chu sequence. The IFFT size (NIFFT) is different depends on the PRACH short or long formats, technologies, and L1 HW types.

The output of the IFFT is used to get NIFFT power metrics.

The IFFT and power metric calculation is described in section “2.5.6 IFFT and Power Metric Calculation” of the 5GMax algorithm specification (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_695\_replaced([11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

The IFFT and power metric calculation supported in different L1 HWs can be found in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_16316\_replaced([11105751](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koPNdfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

##### 2.4.4.5.7.0-2 IFFT, Power Metric and Non-coherent Combining Non-coherent Combining (ID: 11105746)

**2.4.4.5.7.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11105748`)

IFFT, Power Metric and Non-coherent Combining
Then the non-coherent combining simply averages the power metrics from different antennas and sequence repetitions.

The Non-coherent combining algorithm is described in section "2.5.9 Non-coherent Combining" of the 5GMax algorithm specification (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_695\_replaced([11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

The Non-coherent combining supported in different L1 HWs can be found in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_16316\_replaced([11105751](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koPNdfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

**2.4.4.5.7.0-3.0-4**  (ID: `13338472`)

IFFT, Power Metric and Non-coherent Combining
The IFFT, Power Metric Calculation and Non-coherent Combining ([11105741](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koPNcfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) and [11105746](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koPNc_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) are supported in below L1 HWs:

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |

| **Loner** | Supported |  |
| **Loki** | Supported |  |
| **Thor** | Supported | CB007487-B,  CB007487-C,  CB007487-D |
| **GPU** | Supported | CB016066-C |

#### 2.4.4.5.8 Harmonic Combining

##### 2.4.4.5.8.0-1 Harmonic Combining Overview (ID: 11105765)

**2.4.4.5.8.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `11105770`)

Harmonic Combining
For PRACH long formats with restricted set type, the harmonic peaks caused by frequency offsets fall outside search windows, in order to improve detection performance, the detection is performed on harmonic combining window, 3 or 5 windows are combined depends on restricted set type. For restricted set type A, 3 windows are combined for detection. [CB007491-B] For restricted set type B, 5 windows are combined for detection. [End CB007491-B]

The harmonic combining algorithm is described in section "2.5.10 Harmonic combining" of the 5GMax algorithm specification (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_695\_replaced([11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

The harmonic combining algorithm supported in different L1 HWs can be found in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_15665\_replaced([11105772](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koPNfvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

**2.4.4.5.8.0-2.0-3**  (ID: `11105780`)

Harmonic Combining
The harmonic combining algorithm (5G\_UP\_ALG\_15663\_replaced([11105765](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koPNevvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is supported in below L1 HWs:

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Harmonic combining for long formats with restricted set | Loner | Loki | vDU | Thor |
| restricted set type A | supported | supported | - | supported |
| restricted set type B | - | [CB007491-B] supported [End CB007491-B] | - | [CB007491-B] supported [End CB007491-B] |
| First feature | CB006662-A CB006662-B | CB006662-A CB006662-B | - | CB009027-5G-A |

##### 2.4.4.5.9.0-2 Preamble Detection Threshold PRACH False Alarm and Miss Detection (ID: 11105791)

**2.4.4.5.9.0-2.0-3**  (ID: `12694194`)

Preamble Detection Threshold
DU shall guarantee the PRACH false alarm and miss detection performance as described in section "8.4 Performance requirements for PRACH" of 3GPP 38.104.

The probability of false alarm target is configurable via NIDD parameter [Before CB014330-A] MRBTS/NRBTS/pfaTargetPRACH [End CB014330-A] [CB014330-A] MRBTS/NRBTS/NRCELL/pfaTargetPRACH [End CB014330-A].

[CB014330-A] DU shall support online modification of MRBTS/NRBTS/NRCELL/pfaTargetPRACH, see [12694173](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_mgBgwFjgEfCIAJ9nOfeS0w). [End CB014330-A]

For the supported L1 HWs, refer to [11105786](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koP0hPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

---

Preamble Detection Threshold
[Before CNI-103796-J]

[End CNI-103796-J]

##### 2.4.4.5.9.0-4 Preamble Detection Threshold PRACH DTX Threshold (ID: 11105812)

**2.4.4.5.9.0-4.0-5**  (ID: `13339715`)

Preamble Detection Threshold
**Purpose:**

DU shall calculate the theoretical PRACH DTX threshold for preamble detection based on normalized AWGN noise model and guarantee the 3GPP performance requirements (see [11105791](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koP0hvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

**Input:**

- the false alarm performance target (see [11105791](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koP0hvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

- the preamble format (see [11105449](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koK8D_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

- the zeroCorrelationZone configuration (see [11105624](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koNYTPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ), [11105639](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koNYUfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ), [11105647](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koNYVPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

- the number of valid RX antennas (see [11105836](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koQblfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

- the total number of preambles of the PRACH occasion (see [11347388](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubMQFvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

**Algorithm:**

For Loner, Loki, Thor **[CB016066-C] and GPU** [End CB016066-C] L1 HW, DU shall derive the theoretical PRACH DTX threshold according to section "2.5.12 Detection Threshold Set" of the 5GMax algorithm specification (see [11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).\*\*\*\*

**Output:**

* the theoretical PRACH DTX threshold

For the supported L1 HWs, refer to [11105786](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koP0hPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

---

##### 2.4.4.5.9.0-5 Preamble Detection Threshold PRACH DTX Threshold Adjustment (ID: 11105826)

**2.4.4.5.9.0-5.0-3**  (ID: `11105833`)

Preamble Detection Threshold
[Before CNI-103796-J]

For Loner, Loki and Thor L1 HWs, an offset is introduced to adjust the DTX threshold, the offset is configurable in 5G-L1-UL and through the cell parameter NRBTS/NRCELL/adjustPrachThresholdOffset.

For Loner L1 HW, NRBTS/NRCELL/adjustPrachThresholdOffset is mapped to either PrachThresholdOffsetDbAdjustT1 or  PrachThresholdOffsetDbAdjustTN or both, depends on the configuration e.g. 2RX, 4RX, TDD, FDD, etc.

For Loki and Thor L1 HWs, NRBTS/NRCELL/adjustPrachThresholdOffset is mapped to both PrachThresholdOffsetDbAdjustT1 and PrachThresholdOffsetDbAdjustTN.

The PrachThresholdOffsetDbAdjustT1 and PrachThresholdOffsetDbAdjustTN are described in section 2.5.14 of 5GMax algorithm specification.

[End CNI-103796-J]

[CNI-103796-J]

Purpose:

DU shall adjust the calculated PRACH DTX threshold (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1466\_replaced([11105812](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koP0jvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) to reach requirements from different customers or diverse field configurations (see 5G\_UP\_6358\_replaced([11361420](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_uc1O3fvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

Input:

* the PRACH DTX threshold adjust offset in dB, the offset is configurable through the cell parameter NRBTS/NRCELL/adjustPrachThresholdOffset.

Algorithm:

For Loner L1 HW, DU shall map NRBTS/NRCELL/adjustPrachThresholdOffset to either PrachThresholdOffsetDbAdjustT1 or PrachThresholdOffsetDbAdjustTN or both, depends on the configuration e.g. 2RX, 4RX, TDD, FDD, etc.

For Loki and Thor L1 HWs, DU shall map NRBTS/NRCELL/adjustPrachThresholdOffset to both PrachThresholdOffsetDbAdjustT1 and PrachThresholdOffsetDbAdjustTN.

Refer to section 2.5.14 of 5GMax algorithm specification (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_695\_replaced([11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) for the PrachThresholdOffsetDbAdjustT1 and PrachThresholdOffsetDbAdjustTN.

Output:

* the adjusted PRACH DTX threshold

For the supported L1 HWs, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_28831\_replaced([11105786](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koP0hPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[End CNI-103796-J]

---

##### 2.4.4.5.9.0-6 Preamble Detection Threshold Valid RX Antennas for PRACH Reception (ID: 11105836)

**2.4.4.5.9.0-6.0-6**  (ID: `13417495`)

Preamble Detection Threshold
[Before CB014128-A, CB014128-B]

For non-Beamforming, DU shall derive the number of valid RX antennas for PRACH reception according to the number of RX antennas configuration supported in [11094165](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmaBcPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

[End CB014128-A, CB014128-B]

[CB014128-A, CB014128-B]

For non-Beamforming, DU shall derive the number of valid RX antenna for PRACH reception according to [13420973](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_t-83MxegEfGFMKAOrP5qqw?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9).

[End CB014128-A, CB014128-B]

For Beamforming, DU shall derive the number of valid RX ~~antenna~~ spatial streams for PRACH reception:

- For TDD FR1, according to the number of spatial streams processed per PRACH sub-receiver defined in [13420912](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ViZF6ReZEfGFMKAOrP5qqw?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9).

- For TDD FR2, according the number of spatial streams processed per PRACH sub-receiver defined in [13420917](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_bdhLEBeaEfGFMKAOrP5qqw?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9).

- For FDD, according the number of spatial streams processed per PRACH sub-receiver defined in [13420913](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ajQXvBeZEfGFMKAOrP5qqw?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9).

---

##### 2.4.4.5.9.0-7 Preamble Detection Threshold Preamble Search Window (ID: 11105853)

**2.4.4.5.9.0-7.0-2**  (ID: `11105858`)

Preamble Detection Threshold
**[Before CNI-103796-J]**

The search windows for preamble detection are set according to zero correlation zone configuration (NRBTS/NRCELL.zeroCorrelationZoneConfig, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1435\_replaced([11105620](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koNYSvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). The search window size is decided according to cyclic shift value Ncs (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1435\_replaced([11105620](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koNYSvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and IFFT size (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_15557\_replaced([11105741](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koPNcfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

A negative search window offset is introduced to avoid the earliest path of UE close to gNB is out of search window due to timing error. The timing error usually includes 2 parts:

- The UE initial transmission timing error ±Te, refer to 3GPP 38.133 Table 7.1.2-1.

- The gNB internal timing error, main timing error come from RU and current estimation is ±100ns shall be take into consideration.

The worst case when both UE initial transmission timing error and gNB internal timing error in same negative direction will lead to maximum negative search window offset.

In the other side, a protection guard sample is introduced between adjacent search windows to avoid side lobes causing false alarm in adjacent search window.

As the search window size is fix for each Ncs, the supported positive delay is reduced due to the negative search window offset and guard sample, which leads to supported cell range reduce. The cell range reduce amount depends on the negative search window offset values and guard sample values. Refer to below link for the cell size calculation with negative search window offset and guard sample can be found in <https://nokia.sharepoint.com/:x:/r/sites/5Gdoc/AS_Tech_Spec/Drawings%20and%20diagrams%20for%205G/User%20Plane/Drawings%20for%20PRACH%20formats/RACH_cell_sizes_5gmax.xls?d=wd5b9c29b127d4fa5af2d91e451d3b66f&csf=1&web=1&e=qKx62L>

In the other side, small non-zero Ncs has small search window size, especially for PRACH short formats with small non-zero Ncs, the corresponding search window size are very small, with negative search window offset and guard sample the preamble will be not detected or detected in wrong search window and lead to high false alarm. According to “5GMax small Ncs simulation”, this issue is common for PRACH short formats, but depends on the negative search window offset values, guard sample values, timing offset and channel models the impact will be different for different L1 HWs. To avoid high false alarm for PRACH short formats, zeroCorrelationZoneConfig value 1, 2 and 3 with small non-zero Ncs values are not supported.

For more details about search window size, search window offset values, guard sample values and “[5GMax small Ncs simulation](https://nokia.sharepoint.com/:p:/r/sites/5gmax/Shared%20Documents/GeneralDocuments/5G%20Algorithm%20Specifications/5GMax-5G%20New%20Radio%20Algorithm%20Specification%20-%20background%20information/Simulations%20for%20NR%20PRACH%20short%20format%20with%20small%20Ncs%20configurations.pptx?d=w07477580a2784c42836cee4f9361e294&csf=1&web=1&e=KZePac)”, refer to “2.5.12 Search Window Set” of the 5GMax algorithm specification (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_695\_replaced([11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

The negative search window offset and guard sample supported in different L1 HWs can be found in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_16712\_replaced([11105861](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koQbnvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

The zeroCorrelationZoneConfig supported in different L1 HWs can be found in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1437\_replaced([11105624](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koNYTPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for TDD FR1 and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1441\_replaced([11105639](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koNYUfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for TDD FR2 PRACH shot formats.

**[End CNI-103796-J]**

**[CNI-103796-J]**

**Purpose:**

DU shall set the search windows for preamble detection with proper search window size.

DU shall introduce a negative search window offset to avoid the earliest path of UE close to gNB is out of search window due to timing error.

DU shall also introduce a protection guard sample between adjacent search windows to avoid side lobes causing false alarm in adjacent search window.

**Input:**

- the cyclic shift value Ncs (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1435\_replaced([11105620](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koNYSvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

- the IFFT size (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_15557\_replaced([11105741](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koPNcfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

**Algorithm:**

• DU shall decide the search window size for preamble detection according to cyclic shift value Ncs and IFFT size.

• DU shall introduce a negative search window offset to avoid the earliest path of UE close to gNB is out of search window due to timing error. The timing error usually includes 2 parts:

- The UE initial transmission timing error ±Te, refer to 3GPP 38.133 Table 7.1.2-1.

- The gNB internal timing error, main timing error come from RU and current estimation is ±100ns shall be take into consideration.

The worst case when both UE initial transmission timing error and gNB internal timing error in same negative direction will lead to maximum negative search window offset.

• DU shall introduce a protection guard sample between adjacent search windows to avoid side lobes causing false alarm in adjacent search window.

Rationale:

As the search window size is fix for each Ncs, the supported positive delay is reduced due to the negative search window offset and guard sample, which leads to supported cell range reduce. The cell range reduce amount depends on the negative search window offset values and guard sample values.

In the other side, small non-zero Ncs has small search window size, especially for PRACH short formats with small non-zero Ncs, the corresponding search window size are very small, with negative search window offset and guard sample the preamble will be not detected or detected in wrong search window and lead to high false alarm. According to “5GMax small Ncs simulation”, this issue is common for PRACH short formats, but depends on the negative search window offset values, guard sample values, timing offset and channel models the impact will be different for different L1 HWs. To avoid high false alarm for PRACH short formats, zeroCorrelationZoneConfig value 1, 2 and 3 with small non-zero Ncs values are not supported.

For more details about search window size, search window offset values, guard sample values and “[5GMax small Ncs simulation](https://nokia.sharepoint.com/:p:/r/sites/5gmax/Shared%20Documents/GeneralDocuments/5G%20Algorithm%20Specifications/5GMax-5G%20New%20Radio%20Algorithm%20Specification%20-%20background%20information/Simulations%20for%20NR%20PRACH%20short%20format%20with%20small%20Ncs%20configurations.pptx?d=w07477580a2784c42836cee4f9361e294&csf=1&web=1&e=KZePac)”, refer to “2.5.12 Search Window Set” of the 5GMax algorithm specification (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_695\_replaced([11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

For extended cell, there could be multiple search windows, refer to [11105938](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koRpu_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) for more details.

For ring cell, the search window start could depends on external delay, refer to [11105955](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koSQwPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) for more details.

**Output:**

- search window size

- search window offset

- guard sample values

For the supported L1 HWs, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_28831\_replaced([11105786](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koP0hPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

**[End CNI-103796-J]**

---

##### 2.4.4.5.9.0-8 Preamble Detection Threshold Configurations Support Negative Search Window Offset and Guard Sample (ID: 11105861)

**2.4.4.5.9.0-8.0-3**  (ID: `11105869`)

Preamble Detection Threshold
[Before CNI-103796-J]

The negative search window offset and guard sample (5G\_UP\_ALG\_16688\_replaced([11105853](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koQbm_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) are supported in below L1 HWs:

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| L1 HW | Negative Search Window Offset and Guard Sample | First Feature |
| Loner | Supported |  |
| Loki | Supported |  |
| vDU | - |  |
| Thor | Supported | CB007487-B CB007487-C CB007487-D |

[End CNI-103796-J]

---

##### 2.4.4.5.9.0-9 Preamble Detection Threshold Preamble Peak Detection (ID: 11105872)

**2.4.4.5.9.0-9.0-1**  (ID: `11105874`)

Preamble Detection Threshold
Purpose:

DU shall perform the preamble detection.

Input:

- the NIFFT power metrics (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_15409\_replaced([11105738](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koPNcPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

- the PRACH DTX threshold (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1466\_replaced([11105812](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koP0jvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1469\_replaced([11105826](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koQbkfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_15426\_replaced([11105877](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koRCpPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

- the search window setting (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_16688\_replaced([11105853](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koQbm_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

Algorithm:

DU shall divide the NIFFT power metrics after non-coherent combining into different groups according to search window setting. If the peak value of power metrics in corresponding search window exceeds the PRACH DTX threshold, DU shall announce the corresponding preamble as detected and calculate the time offset & peak metric.

Refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_15668\_replaced([11105969](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koSQxfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for more details about the detector types.

Refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1614\_replaced([11107905](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koj9m_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) about the time offset calculation and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_28482\_replaced([11108880](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kowx6PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) about the peak metric calculation.

The preamble peak detection algorithm is defined in section "2.5.11 Peak Detection" of the 5GMax algorithm specification (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_695\_replaced([11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

Output:

- the peak value for the detected preambles

- the detected preamble indices

For the supported L1 HWs, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_28831\_replaced([11105786](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koP0hPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

#### 2.4.4.5.10 MAD Algorithm

##### 2.4.4.5.10.0-1 MAD Algorithm Overview (ID: 11105879)

**2.4.4.5.10.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11105882`)

MAD Algorithm
The detection threshold (5G\_UP\_ALG\_1466\_replaced([11105812](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koP0jvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is determined base on normalized AWGN noise model. In the situation where the signals are significantly correlated, e.g. due to strong interference, it will lead to a different distribution function of the noise signal part in the detection, so that the original detection threshold does not match anymore, and false alarm rate will increase. To improve the false alarm performance with interference, the MAD (mean absolute deviation) algorithm is introduced.

The MAD algorithm takes into account the antenna correlation effect by calculating the mean absolute deviation (MAD) statistic when determine the threshold.

The MAD algorithm can be enabled and disabled in 5G-L1-UL, by default it is enabled.

T1\_MAD and TN\_MAD are the two calculated thresholds adjusted by L1 internal offsets and NIDD offset parameters (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1469\_replaced([11105826](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koQbkfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) respectively. If TN\_MAD is larger than or equal to T1\_MAD, MAD algorithm is disabled, and L1 internal warning will be printed.

The L1 internal offsets are dependent on NRBTS/NRCELL/actAdjustPrachThresholdOption (5G\_UP\_ALG\_15428\_replaced([11105885](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koRCp_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), L1 HW types, technologies, PRACH formats and number of RX antennas (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_6372\_replaced([11105836](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koQblfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), etc.

The MAD algorithm and L1 internal offsets are described in section "2.5.14 MAD algorithm" of the 5GMax algorithm specification (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_695\_replaced([11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

The MAD algorithm supported in different L1 HWs can be found in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_15428\_replaced([11105885](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koRCp_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

##### 2.4.4.5.10.0-2 MAD Algorithm Configurations Support MAD Algorithm (ID: 11105885)

**2.4.4.5.10.0-2.0-6**  (ID: `13339935`)

MAD Algorithm
The DU shall support MAD algorithm ([11105879](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koRCpfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for below L1 HWs:

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **L1 HW** | **MAD Algorithm** | **First Feature** |
| Loner | Supported |  |
| Loki | Supported |  |
| Thor | Supported | CB007487-B CB007487-C CB007487-D CB010576-A |
| GPU | Supported ~~(TBC)~~ | CB016066-C |

Notes:

1. For Loner L1 HW, the MAD statistic is derived from each available root sequence.
2. For Loki and Thor L1 HWs with short formats (normal speed cells and high speed cells) and NRBTS/NRCELL/actPrachMultiplexingLevel is set to 0 (see [11105718](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koOmZ_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) :

. When R&D parameter rdShortPrachNoMuxMaxMadFlag (see [11110554](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpFiD_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) is set to FALSE the MAD statistic is derived from first available root sequence.

. When R&D parameter rdShortPrachNoMuxMaxMadFlag is set to TRUE, the MaxMAD is used where MAD statistic is derived from maximum of all available root sequence.

[CB016066-C] R&D parameter rdShortPrachNoMuxMaxMadFlag (see [11110554](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpFiD_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) parameter is not supported in DU for GPU L1 HW: DU for GPU L1 HW shall behave as with 'disabled' value. [End CB016066-C]

1. For Loki and Thor L1 HWs with long formats or when NRBTS/NRCELL/actPrachMultiplexingLevel is set to activate (see [11105718](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koOmZ_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), the MaxMAD is used where MAD statistic is derived from maximum of all available root sequence.
2. **For TDD FR1, 4RX, non BF , both long and short PRACH format in Loner L1 HW:**

NRBTS/NRCELL/actAdjustPrachThresholdOption (see [11361420](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_uc1O3fvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) can be set to TRUE or FALSE.

***\*For other configurations or L1 HW platforms:***\*

NRBTS/NRCELL/actAdjustPrachThresholdOption can only be set to FALSE.

---

#### 2.4.4.5.11 HPM Algorithm

##### 2.4.4.5.11.0-1 HPM Algorithm Overview (ID: 11105906)

**2.4.4.5.11.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11105908`)

HPM Algorithm
Preambles with high power may cause false alarms due to filter side lobes, the HPM (High Power Mitigation) algorithm is introduced to process the PDP (Power Delay Profile) region around the main peaks. For each root sequence, if the number of detected preambles on this root sequence is large than 1, then some preambles will be discarded according to HPM algorithm.

The HPM algorithm is described in section "2.5.16 High Power Mitigation" of the 5GMax algorithm specification (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_695\_replaced([11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

The HPM algorithm supported in different L1 HWs can be found in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_15660\_replaced([11105911](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koRpsPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

##### 2.4.4.5.11.0-2 HPM Algorithm Configurations Support HPM Algorithm (ID: 11105911)

**2.4.4.5.11.0-2.0-2**  (ID: `11105917`)

HPM Algorithm
The HPM algorithm (5G\_UP\_ALG\_15658\_replaced([11105906](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koRCrvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is supported in below L1 HWs:

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| L1 HW | HPM Algorithm | First Feature |
| Loner | Supported |  |
| Loki | Supported |  |
| vDU | - |  |
| Thor | Supported | CB007487-B CB007487-C CB007487-D |

---

#### 2.4.4.5.12 Harmonic Removal

##### 2.4.4.5.12.0-1 Harmonic Removal Overview (ID: 11105922)

**2.4.4.5.12.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `11105927`)

Harmonic Removal
For the cell configured with PRACH long formats and Unrestricted type, the false alarm performance could be degraded as caused by frequency offset more than 0.24SCS (e.g. for PRACH long format 0, PRACH SCS is 1.25kHz, then 0.24\*1.25kHz=300Hz), such frequency offset could be reached even at low or medium speed mobility based on carrier frequency and UE error frequency.

The harmonic removal algorithm described by 5GMax algorithm specification (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_695\_replaced([11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) aims to suppress harmonic peaks and reduce false alarm probability. Though the main use case is for PRACH long formats, but the algorithm can be generally supported for all PRACH formats and all technology.

The harmonic removal algorithm is defined for normal receiver (5G\_UP\_ALG\_10818\_replaced([11105931](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koRpuPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) or for highspeed receiver (5G\_UP\_ALG\_9049\_replaced([11105996](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koSQz_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and allow to remove:

* either only 1st hamonic order
* or 1st and 2nd hamonics order

For further details refer to 5GMax algorithm specification section [Before CB007487-B, CB007487-C, CB007487-D] "2.5.16 Harmonic removal". [CB007487-B, CB007487-C, CB007487-D] "2.5.17 Harmonic removal". [End CB007487-B, CB007487-C, CB007487-D] (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_695\_replaced([11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

The supported normal receiver detector with harmonic removal for unrestricted type in different L1 HWs can be found in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_10818\_replaced([11105931](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koRpuPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[CB007487-B, CB007487-C, CB007487-D] NOTE: There is only update of 5Gmax algorithm specification without algorithm change. Note should be kept only for this version [End CB007487-B, CB007487-C, CB007487-D]

---

##### 2.4.4.5.12.0-2 Harmonic Removal Support normal receiver harmonic removal for unrestricted type (ID: 11105931)

**2.4.4.5.12.0-2.0-2**  (ID: `11105935`)

Harmonic Removal
The supported normal receiver detector with harmonic removal for unrestricted type in different L1 HWs are as below:

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Normal Receiver Harmonic Removal  (5G\_UP\_ALG\_10817\_replaced([11105922](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koRptPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) | Loner | Loki | vDU | Thor |
| 1st hamonic order | supported   for TDD FR1 only | supported | - | supported |
| 1st and 2nd hamonics order | - | - | - |  |
| First feature | PR633808 |  |  | CB007487-B CB007487-C CB007487-D |

#### 2.4.4.5.13 PRACH Extend Cell Range

##### 2.4.4.5.13.0-1 PRACH Extend Cell Range PRACH Extend Cell Range for TDD FR2 (ID: 11105942)

**2.4.4.5.13.0-1.0-3**  (ID: `13479618`)

PRACH Extend Cell Range
The extended cell range algorithm is introduced to allow preamble detection range exceeding the legacy cell size, with acceptable performance degradation, see [11105946](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koRpvvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) for the performance reference.

The extended cell range algorithm performs multiple normal peak detection in multiple sliding window for each preamble, see [11105946](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koRpvvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) for the number of sliding windows. If multiple peak detected in multiple sliding windows for one preamble, one of the detected peak shall be selected.

The zeroCorrelationZoneConfig value 0 shall be used in extended cell range algorithm. Since the zeroCorrelationZoneConfig value 0 means whole root sequence belongs to one preamble and corresponding to the maximum regular cell size.

[PR854254]

In extended cells, PRACH symbols may spill over to the consequent slot due to large delay and interfere with that slot reception if it is an UL slot. Therefore, DU shall avoid configurations, where PRACH slot is followed by another UL slot.

[End PR854254]

The extended cell range algorithm shall be enabled or disabled via parameter MRBTS/NRBTS/NRCELL/rangeExtension (see [11361363](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_uc1O0vvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) 'Extended cell range activation' for details).

The legacy algorithm for regular cell range is used by default, so that the extended cell range algorithm is disabled by default.

The configurations support extend cell range in differnt L1 HWs can be found in [11105946](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koRpvvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

---

#### 2.4.4.5.14 PRACH Support Ring Cell Algorithm

##### 2.4.4.5.14.0-1 PRACH Support Ring Cell Algorithm PRACH Support Ring Cell Algorithm for TDD FR1 (ID: 11105958)

**2.4.4.5.14.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11105960`)

PRACH Support Ring Cell Algorithm
PRACH ring cell algorithm support preamble detection with a configurable cell start, while the cell size is not changed and keep the normal cell size.

PRACH ring cell support below scenarios:

 the ring cell start refer to air delay from the gNB, as described in 5G\_UP\_7842\_replaced([11361663](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_uc3EBfvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

 the ring cell start refer to external DAS delay from the gNB (BBU+RU, exclude remote antenna), as described in 5G\_UP\_7849\_replaced([11361641](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_uc3EAfvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

From PRACH algorithm POV, they are consdiered commonly as PRACH ring cell algorithm.

The preamble detection algorithm to support the ring cell is achieved by adding the configurable cell start delay to a time offset which indicate the PRACH sequence data start, then after preamble detected, the initial TA need to adjust as well by adding the configurable cell start delay.

The PRACH ring cell algorithm is described in section "2.5.26 Processing for CB010784" of the 5GMax algorithm specification (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_695\_replaced([11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

If the configured cell start exceeds certain distance, performance degradation is expected, see details in [the performance degradation for UE with far distance in CB010784 v8.pptx](https://nokia.sharepoint.com/:p:/r/sites/ASCOP/AS%20Documents/FS1TAs-CFAMs/CB/CB010500%20-%20CB010999/CFAM/CB010784%20FR1%20TDD%20cell%20support%20for%203rd%20party%20delay%20compensation/Additional_materials/the%20performance%20degradation%20for%20UE%20with%20far%20distance%20in%20CB010784%20v8.pptx?d=wd8401aba657e449a845ee0a603a84220&csf=1&web=1&e=HWh10G).

The ring cell mode shall be enabled or disabled via parameter MRBTS/NRBTS/NRCELL/addExtDelayCompensation. (see 5G\_UP\_7840\_replaced([11361372](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_uc1O1PvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) & 5G\_UP\_7847\_replaced([11361383](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_uc1O1vvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

The configurations support ring cell in different L1 HWs for specific PRACH formats can be found in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_21124\_replaced([11105963](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koSQw_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

| vDU | - | - |  |
| Thor | B4 | Supported | CB010784-D |

#### 2.4.4.5.16 Normal preamble detector

##### 2.4.4.5.16.0-1 Normal preamble detector Normal preamble detector introduction (ID: 11105979)

**2.4.4.5.16.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11105983`)

Normal preamble detector
Normal preamble detector is the default detector for PRACH receiver, it is introduced for the cell which is configured with unrestricted set type, and harmonic combining (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_15411\_replaced([11105762](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koPNefvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is not applicable.

The normal preamble detector is described in section 2.5.20.1 of 5GMax algorithm specification (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_695\_replaced([11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

The normal preamble detector supported in different L1 HWs can be found in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_16359\_replaced([11105984](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koSQy_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

##### 2.4.4.5.16.0-2 Normal preamble detector Configurations Support Normal preamble detector (ID: 11105984)

**2.4.4.5.16.0-2.0-4**  (ID: `13338500`)

Normal preamble detector
The normal preamble detector ([11105979](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koSQyfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) is supported in below L1 HWs:

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **L1 HW** | **Normal preamble detector** | **First Feature** |
| **Loner** | Supported |  |
| **Loki** | Supported |  |
| **Thor** | **Supported** | CB007487-B CB007487-C CB007487-D |
| GPU | Supported | CB016066-C |

---

#### 2.4.4.5.17 Highspeed preamble detector for restricted set

##### 2.4.4.5.17.0-1 Highspeed preamble detector for restricted set Overview (ID: 11105998)

**2.4.4.5.17.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `11106004`)

Highspeed preamble detector for restricted set
Highspeed preamble detector is introduced for the cell which is configured with restricted set type since there are harmonic peaks in case of high frequency offset. The combination over main and harmonic windows (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_15411\_replaced([11105762](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koPNefvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is implemented to improve missed detection performance, and the harmonic removal (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_10813\_replaced([11105920](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koRps_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is implemented to improve false alarm performance.

* For the combination window, in case of restricted set A, the main peak and 1st harmonics are combined. [CB007491-B] In case of restricted set B, the main peak, 1st harmonics and 2nd harmonics are combined. [End CB007491-B] The detailed algorithms are described in section 2.5.20.3 for Loner, 2.5.20.6 for Loki and 2.5.20.7 for Thor in 5GMax algorithm specification (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_695\_replaced([11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).
* For the harmonic removal, in case of restricted set A, the 2nd and 3rd harmonics are removed. [CB007491-B] In case of restricted set B, the 5th harmonics are removed. [End CB007491-B]The detailed algorithms are described in section 2.5.17 of the 5GMax algorithm specification (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_695\_replaced([11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

The supported highspeed preamble detector for restricted set for different L1 HWs can be found in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_9057\_replaced([11106007](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koS30_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for TDD FR1, and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_9059\_replaced([11106018](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koS31_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for FDD.

---

### 2.4.4.6 PRACH Reception with Beamforming

#### 2.4.4.6.0-1 PRACH Reception with Beamforming PRACH Reception with Beamforming for TDD FR1 and one SSB per RO (ID: 11106032)

**2.4.4.6.0-1.0-10**  (ID: `13339943`)

PRACH Reception with Beamforming
For one SSB per RO (see [11105491](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koLjH_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), beamforming and C4 subcell type or C8’2(DU)/C16'2(Radio) or C8 subcell type (reception with 4 spatial ~~antenna~~ streams) case:

* PRACH signal received from the 2 spatial streams associated to the 2nd beam are duplication of the 2 spatial streams associated to 1st beam (more precisely the PRACH associated SSB beam).

Due to the signal duplication, the PRACH reception via 4 spatial ~~antenna~~ streams could be seen as reception with 2RX antennas.

The processing of the signal associated to the 2nd **beam depends on the L1 HWs as given in the following table:**

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **L1 HW** | **PRACH received in the spatial streams associated to the 1**st **beam**\*\*\*\* | **PRACH received in the spatial streams associated to the 2**nd **beam**\*\*\*\* | **First Feature** |
| Loner | Processed | Processed for unrestricted set, ignored for restricted set A |  |
| Loki | Processed | Ignored |  |
| Thor | Processed | Ignored | CB007487-C |
| GPU | Processed | Ignored | CB016066-C |

So for TDD FR1 with BF enabled, the number of valid RX ~~antennas~~ for PRACH DTX threshold decision is 2 RX ~~antennas~~.

Note : C8 UL subcell type has been introduced without increase of PRACH number of streams, as C4 only 4 streams are created until real 8Rx PRACH support in future.

**Note2: For eCPRI 7-2e, the duplication of the 1st beam is done in Radio and sent to DU in 2 spactial streams associated to the 2nd beam, which are ignored in DU as what is done in eCPRI 7-2a.**

---

#### 2.4.4.6.0-2 PRACH Reception with Beamforming PRACH Reception with Beamforming for TDD FR1 and multiple SSBs per RO (ID: 11106057)

**2.4.4.6.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11106060`)

PRACH Reception with Beamforming
For TDD FR1 Loki and Thor L1 HWs, DU shall support more than one SSBs map to one RO (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1401\_replaced([11105491](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koLjH_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) when beamforming enabled.

DU shall receive PRACH signal from up to 2\*N spatial streams, where N is the maximum number of SSBs per RO (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_28174\_replaced([11105533](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koMKMPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

Based on the SSB and RO association result (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1970\_replaced([11111961](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpRvS_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), for each RO, DU shall receive PRACH signal from 2\*M spatial streams, where M is the number of SSBs mapped to each RO, and M is smaller than or equal to N. It is possible M is different value for each RO, e.g. for some ROs, M=N and for other ROs, M<N.

[Before CB012192-A-UPD001] Each 2 continuous spatial streams receive totalNumberOfRAPreambles/N preambles which associated to one SSB beam (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3378\_replaced([11152306](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksfbU_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), with preamble range: m*(totalNumberOfPreambles/N) … (m+1)*(totalNumberOfPreambles/N)-1, where m=0,…,M-1, and refer to 5G\_UP\_118\_replaced([11347388](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubMQFvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for the totalNumberOfPreambles. [End CB012192-A-UPD001]

[CB012192-A-UPD001]

As such, there’re M PRACH sub-receivers, each sub-receiver with 2 continuous spatial streams shall detect the preambles associated to one SSB.

There’re totalNumberOfRAPreambles/N preambles associated to one SSB beam, with preamble range: m*(totalNumberOfPreambles/N) … (m+1)*(totalNumberOfPreambles/N)-1, where m=0,…,M-1, and refer to 5G\_UP\_118\_replaced([11347388](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubMQFvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for the totalNumberOfPreambles.

Each sub-receiver shall find the related roots which generate the totalNumberOfRAPreambles/N preambles according to the root sequence and cyclic shift configuration (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1435\_replaced([11105620](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koNYSvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), these roots can be considered as “the number of roots map to one SSB beam”. The sub-receiver shall perform PRACH reception for all the preambles related to these roots, where “the number of preambles per sub-receiver” = “the number of preambles per root” \* “the number of roots map to one SSB beam”. Refer to section "8.1.2 Sector Beam Based Beamforming" of 5GMax Massive MIMO Algorithm Specification (5G\_UP\_ALG\_700\_replaced([11101361](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcsvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

Since the number of preambles per sub-receiver is more than or equal to totalNumberOfRAPreambles/N, so the sub-receiver shall finally filter out totalNumberOfRAPreambles/N preambles associated to the SSB beam.

[End CB012192-A-UPD001]

The number of valid RX antennas is 2 RX for preamble reception, corresponding to the 2 continuous spatial streams associated to one SSB beam.

---

#### 2.4.4.6.0-3 PRACH Reception with Beamforming PRACH Reception with Beamforming for FDD (ID: 11106062)

**2.4.4.6.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `12772224`)

PRACH Reception with Beamforming
[Before CB008577-A]

PRACH reception for A8 (DU) subcell type, shall be proceeded as follow:

- Receiver processes 2 streams belong to Beam1, and ignore rest of the streams, if exists.

- Beam1 is associated with a single SSB beam (sector beam).

As such, the number of valid RX antennas is 2 RX for preamble reception (see [11105836](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koQblfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)).

**[CB008577-A]**

Purpose:

DU shall perform PRACH reception with beamforming for A8 subcell type (see [11094230](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmaogfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)) in FDD Thor L1 HW.

Input:

* NRCELL.actPrachProbing
* PRACH spatial streams from Radio
* 4 beams for PRACH probing reception, defined as beamset #4 (see [11111686](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpPTD_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

Algorithm:

If PRACH probing is enabled (NRCELL.actPrachProbing = True), DU shall perform PRACH beam probing reception per RO (RACH Occasion, each RO has same time and frequency resource) as below:

* DU shall receive PRACH preambles in parallel from 4 beams with 4 PRACH sub-receivers (see [11105672](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koN_V_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9 "https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koN_V_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9")).
* Each sub-receiver with 2 continuous spatial streams shall receive all the totalNumberOfPreambles (see [11347388](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubMQFvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) preambles from one beam, so totally 8 PRACH spatial streams for the 4 beams.
* DU may detect same preamble index from multiple beams in multiple sub-receivers, or only detect same preamble index from one beam in one sub-receiver. As such, DU shall estimate measurement(s) from one or multiple beams of each detected preamble. Note, L2(DU) will use the measurements for best beam selection (see [11152417](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksgCZPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
* Refer to section “8.1.2 Sector Beam Based Beamforming” of 5GMAX Massive MIMO Algorithm specification (see [11101361](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcsvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for the PRACH probing receiver algorithm.
* The number of valid RX antennas is 2 RX for preamble reception (see [11105836](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koQblfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)), corresponding to the 2 continuous spatial streams associated to one of the beams in beamset #4.

Else if PRACH probing is disabled (NRCELL.actPrachProbing = False), DU shall perform PRACH reception per RO as below:

* DU shall receive PRACH preambles from one SSB beam configured with 2 spatial streams, and ignore the rest of the streams if exist.
* DU shall report measurement from one SSB beam of each detected preamble.
* As such, the number of valid RX antennas is 2 RX for preamble reception (see [11105836](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koQblfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)).

Output:

* The number of valid RX antennas。
* The peak metric, initial timing offset [CB008577-E] and TADV [End CB008577-E] measurements from one or multiple beams for each detected preamble.

Refer to [12801091](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_s80MkpqgEfCahcI91JQtLg) for the supported L1 HWs.

[End CB008577-A]

PRACH Reception with Beamforming
DU shall support the PRACH Reception with beamforming for FDD in below L1 HWs:

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |

| Loner | - |  |
| Loki | - |  |
| Thor | Supported | CB013442-D |

### 2.4.4.7 PRACH Receiver Spatial Streams

#### 2.4.4.7.0-1 PRACH Receiver Spatial Streams PRACH Receiver Spatial Streams for TDD FR1 BF (ID: 13420912)

**2.4.4.7.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `13428184`)

PRACH Receiver Spatial Streams
DU shall support to process data received from multiple PRACH spatial streams for each PRACH sub-receiver as the below table:

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| L1 HW | 2 spatial streams | 4 spatial streams | First Feature |
| Loner | Supported | Supported |  |
| Loki | Supported | - |  |
| Thor | Supported | - |  |
| GPU | Supported (\*) | - | CB016066-C |

DU shall support the total number of streams input to one PRACH receiver as defined in [11094176](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmaBffvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9).

[CB016066-C] (\*) DU for GPU L1 HW shall support Active RX (refer to [13446610](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_HH2g4yedEfGxldjOZQLezQ)) algorithm when RX streams number in the table is as per supported [11094176](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmaBffvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) configuration. [End CB016066-C]

For TDD FR1, one PRACH receiver can include one or multiple PRACH sub-receivers (depending on NRCELL.nbrOfSsbPerRachOccasion), and the PRACH sub-receiver is equal to the PRACH Receiver when the PRACH Receiver includes only one PRACH sub-receiver, see [11105672](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koN_V_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9).

The PRACH related algorithm details:

* for TDD FR1 BF with one SSB per RO, refer to [11106032](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koS33PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)
* for TDD FR1 BF with multiple SSB per RO, refer to [11106057](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koTe5fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)

**2.4.4.7.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `13428207`)

PRACH Receiver Spatial Streams
DU shall support to process data received from multiple PRACH spatial streams for each PRACH sub-receiver as the below table:

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| L1 HW | 2 spatial streams | First Feature |
| Loner | Supported |  |
| Loki | Supported |  |
| Thor | Supported |  |

DU shall support the total number of streams input to one PRACH receiver as defined in [11094219](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmaBkfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9).

For TDD FR2, the PRACH sub-receiver is equal to the PRACH Receiver since the PRACH Receiver includes only one PRACH sub-receiver, see [11105672](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koN_V_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9).

**2.4.4.7.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `13428209`)

PRACH Receiver Spatial Streams
DU shall support to process data received from multiple PRACH spatial streams for each PRACH sub-receiver as the below table:

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| L1 HW | 2 spatial streams | First Feature |
| Loner | - |  |
| Loki | - |  |
| Thor | Supported | CB013442-D |

DU shall support the total number of streams input to one PRACH receiver as defined in [11094230](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmaogfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9).

[Before CB008577-A]

For FDD, the PRACH sub-receiver is equal to the PRACH Receiver since the PRACH Receiver includes only one PRACH sub-receiver, see [11105672](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koN_V_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9).

[End CB008577-A]

[CB008577-A]

For FDD, one PRACH receiver can include one or multiple PRACH sub-receivers (depending on NRCELL.actPrachProbing), and the PRACH sub-receiver is equal to the PRACH Receiver when the PRACH Receiver includes only one PRACH sub-receiver, see [11105672](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koN_V_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9).

[End CB008577-A]

* for FDD BF, refer to [11106062](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koTe5_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9).

#### 2.4.4.7.0-4 PRACH Receiver Spatial Streams PRACH Receiver Spatial Streams for non-BF (ID: 13420973)

**2.4.4.7.0-4.0-2**  (ID: `13412032`)

PRACH Receiver Spatial Streams
~~DU shall support PRACH receiver order as referred at the table below:~~

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| ~~L1 HW~~ | ~~2 Rx\*~~ | ~~4Rx\*~~ | ~~8Rx\*~~ |
| ~~Loner~~ | ~~Supported~~ | ~~Supported~~ | ~~-~~ |
| ~~Loki~~ | ~~Supported~~ | ~~Supported~~ | ~~-~~ |
| ~~Thor~~ | ~~Supported~~ | ~~Supported~~ | ~~-~~ |
| ~~GPU~~ | ~~-~~ | ~~[CB016406-A] Supported  [End CB016406-A]~~ | ~~-~~ |

~~(\*) Number of streams input to PRACH receiver defined in [11094230](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmaogfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).~~

~~[CB014128-A, CB014128-B] When NRBTS/NRCell.act8RxNonBeamforming is enabled, DU for L1 Thor shall support only 4Rx pRACH receiver order on first four spatial streams and ignoring the last four ones.[End CB014128-A, CB014128-B]~~

DU shall support to process data received from multiple PRACH spatial streams for each PRACH sub-receiver as the below table:

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| L1 HW | 2 spatial streams | 4 spatial streams | First Feature |
| Loner | Supported | Supported | - |
| Loki | Supported | Supported | - |
| Thor | Supported | Supported | - |
| GPU | - | Supported (\*) | CB016406-A |

For non-BF, the PRACH sub-receiver is equal to the PRACH Receiver since the PRACH Receiver includes only one PRACH sub-receiver, see [11105672](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koN_V_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9).

DU shall support the total number of streams input to one PRACH receiver as defined in [11094165](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmaBcPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9).

[CB016406-A] (\*) DU for GPU L1 HW shall support Active RX (refer to [13446610](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_HH2g4yedEfGxldjOZQLezQ)) algorithm when RX streams number in the table is as per supported [11094165](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmaBcPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) configuration. [End CB016406-A]

[CB014128-A, CB014128-B] When NRBTS/NRCell.act8RxNonBeamforming is enabled, DU for L1 Thor shall support PRACH reception from the first four spatial streams and ignore the last four spatial streams.[End CB014128-A, CB014128-B]

### 2.4.4.8 PRACH Preamble Report

#### 2.4.4.8.0-1 PRACH Preamble Report Detected PRACH preambles report (ID: 11106070)

**2.4.4.8.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `11106075`)

PRACH Preamble Report
Purpose:

DU shall prepare the PRACH report related information for detected preambles, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3381\_replaced([11152328](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksfbVvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for more details about the usage of the information.

**Input:**

PRACH format and SCS (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1388\_replaced([11105449](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koK8D_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) & 5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_2005\_replaced([11275328](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpGAOfvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

PRACH time and frequency occasion (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1400\_replaced([11105488](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koLjHvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) & 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3375\_replaced([11152277](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kse0TfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

PRACH Root Sequence and Cyclic Shift configuration (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1434\_replaced([11105617](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koNYSfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) & 5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_2005\_replaced([11275328](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpGAOfvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

Valid RX Antennas for PRACH Reception (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_6372\_replaced([11105836](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koQblfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

PRACH IQ data from Radio.

**Algorithm:**

DU shall perform PRACH reception according to the algorithm defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1459\_replaced([11105668](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koN_VfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

DU shall detect the preamble index according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_15412\_replaced([11105783](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koP0gvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

DU shall derive the peak metric (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_28482\_replaced([11108880](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kowx6PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), noise power (5G\_UP\_ALG\_1661\_replaced([11108462](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_korSV_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), initial timing offset (5G\_UP\_ALG\_1614\_replaced([11107905](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koj9m_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) **[CB013507-A] and TADV measurement (5G\_UP\_ALG\_31688\_replaced([11109832](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko8_LPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))** [End CB013507-A] for PRACH preambles.\*\*\*\*

**Output:**

- Detected preamble indices and corresponding time & frequency occasion indices.

- Peak metric, noise power, initial timing offset **[CB013507-A] and TADV** [End CB013507-A] measurements.\*\*\*\*

**2.4.5.0-3.0-7**  (ID: `13422903`)

For TDD FR1 with eCPRI fronthaul interface, in addition to SRS SUMIMO block ([11106114](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koUF9vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)), DU and Radio shall support SRS Long-term BM block ([11106868](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kocBwvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and Short-term BM block ([11107356](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koeeA_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for Beam Management. **In addition, DU shall support SRS HP Positioning block([11107759](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koiIb_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for HP(high precision) positioning** as below,

· **Long-term BM block: it is enabled via the NIDD parameter *NRCELL/actDlSrsBm or \*NRCELL/actUlSrsBm (see [11354194](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucGPAfvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). Details about DU/Radio processing are given in [11106868](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kocBwvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).***\*

· **Short-term BM block: it is enabled via *NRCELL/actDlSrsSubbandBm\*\*. Details about DU/Radio processing are given in [11107356](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koeeA_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).***\*\*

· **HP Positioning block: it is enabled via NRCELL.actHPPosMethodOptList. It's only supported in DU. Details about DU processing are given in [11107759](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koiIb_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).**

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a flowchart illustrating the components of an SRS (Sounding Reference Signal) Receiver. The flowchart starts with a single block on the left labeled "SRS Channel Demapper". From this block, four arrows point to four separate blocks on the right, each representing a different function or component of the SRS Receiver.
The four blocks on the right are:
1. **SRS SUMIMO**: This block has no further sub-points.
2. **SRS Long-term BM**: This block has two sub-points indicated by bullet points:
\* "Co-Ma calculation in L1(BB) or L1(Radio)"
\* "Long-term beam computer in L2(DU)"
3. **SRS Short-term BM**: This block also has two sub-points indicated by bullet points:
\* "Short-term beam computer in L1(BB) or L1(Radio)"
4. **SRS HP Positioning**: This block has one sub-point indicated by a bullet point:
\* "TOE estimation in L1(BB)"
At the bottom of the image, centered, is the text "SRS Receiver". The overall diagram is presented on a gray background.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_3135.002.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

If *NRCELL/actDlSrsSubbandBm enabled, Short-term BM, Long-term BM and* *SUMIMO can be coexist in the same DU or Radio subcell on the same resource for the same UE.*\*\*

For UE capable of more than 1 MIMO layer for PUSCH, SRS reception for both Long-term beam management and SU-MIMO shall use the same one symbol two-port SRS resource. **When Short-term BM is active, SU-MIMO shall use the same one symbol two-port SRS resource for Short-term BM.**

SRS reception for long-term beamforming management is using one symbol single port SRS resource.

[Before CB014040-A]

For UE allocated with pSRS for positioning, DU shall support SRS reception for both SU MIMO **and Positioning using the same one symbol two-port SRS resource. DU shall also support** One-port SRS if it's only used for positioning.\*\*\*\*

[End CB014040-A]

[CB014040-A]

For UE allocated with periodic SRS for positioning:

* For normal cell (on RU-ACCESS, see the nrCellType in [11390858](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ufVwTfvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)),
* If NRCELL.actHPPosMethodOptList is true, and NRCELL.NRPOSINFO.activateTDoAMeas is true, DU shall receive SU MIMO and Positioning SRS together. As such, DU shall support SRS reception for both SU MIMO **and Positioning using the same one symbol two-port SRS resource. DU shall also support** One-port SRS if it's only used for positioning.\*\*\*\*
* If NRCELL.actHPPosMethodOptList is true, and NRCELL.NRPOSINFO.activateTDoAMeas is false, DU shall receive SU MIMO SRS only, the Positioning SRS process will be handled by the associated positioning cell. Note, the multi UEs SU MIMO receiver needs to know if there is Positioning SRS is present in the same comb of the SU-MIMO SRS resource (e.g., SRS Comb 0,  CS 1&3 are used for SU MIMO while CS 5&7 are used for Positioning).
* For positioning cell (on RU-SRS, see [11370296](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_udvNwvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) and the nrPosCellType in [11390858](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ufVwTfvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)),
* DU shall receive Positioning SRS only.

[End CB014040-A]

Note: For eCPRI 7-2e, See [11693050](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_D3t8DP9nEe-D3seUwaI0tg) for the activation details of eCPRI 7-2e.

- Radio shall enable SRS SUMIMO block support.

- Radio shall support SRS Long-term BM block. **The receiver algorithm difference between DU and Radio shall refer to [11106894](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kocBz_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) and [11107174](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koco2vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).**

- Radio shall support SRS Short-term BM block. **The receiver algorithm difference between DU and Radio shall refer to [11107358](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koeeBPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) and [11107565](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kog6SPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).**

- Radio allow the SRS Long-term BM block, SRS Short-term BM block and SRS SUMIMO block coexistence when periodical SRS resources are used for SU-MIMO, Short-term BM and LT BM functions.

File: Drawings see the folder: [RAN Reference Documentation - UL - SRS Reception - Tree\_View (sharepoint.com)](https://nokia.sharepoint.com/sites/5Gdoc/AS_Tech_Spec/Forms/Tree_View.aspx?csf=1&web=1&e=roJEom&cid=933d18ee%2Df570%2D40b2%2D916c%2D3db2886f5b4d&FolderCTID=0x012000F0A0A3BA325F7648AD307E6959BA7376&id=%2Fsites%2F5Gdoc%2FAS%5FTech%5FSpec%2FDrawings%20and%20diagrams%20for%205G%2FUser%20Plane%2F5G%20UP%20Algorithms%2F2%20L1%20Algorithms%20and%20Configurations%2FUL%20%2D%20SRS%20Reception&viewid=967508b3%2D8e81%2D461c%2Db99d%2D145065cf0c1c)

---

### 2.4.5.1 SRS for SU-MIMO

#### 2.4.5.1.0-1 SRS for SU-MIMO Overview (ID: 11106117)

**2.4.5.1.0-1.0-6**  (ID: `13467240`)

SRS for SU-MIMO
DU (for Loner, Loki, Thor **and GPU L1 HW) or Radio (for Thor 7-2e L1 HW) shall support SRS reception with SU-MIMO usage as described in the following chapter. DU or Radio shall support Aperiodic SRS is supported for SU-MIMO (see [11351229](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubr_XvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).**

For TDD FR1 only, eCPRI FH only,  
when the NIDD MRBTS/NRBTS/NRCELL/actUlSrsBm is true,  
DU (for Loki, Thor L1 HW **[CB016061-B1]  and GPU**[End CB016061-B1]) and Radio (for Thor 7-2e L1 HW) shall support SRS reception in the same cell for some users configured with Aperiodic SRS and other users in the same cell configured with Periodic SRS by L3 (see [11351237](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubsmYfvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).\*\*\*\*

For TDD FR1, eCPRI 7-2a, Non-BF deployment,  
when NIDD MRBTS/NRBTS/NRCELL/actHPPosMethodOptList is NOT disabled,  
DU for Thor L1 HW shall support the mix of UEs configured with Aperiodic SRS and with Periodic SRS per one cell (see [11351374](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubt0gvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Note: For eCPRI 7-2e, SRS reception with SU-MIMO and SRS Long-term BM usage is done in Radio.

---

#### 2.4.5.1.1 Receiver algorithm

##### 2.4.5.1.1.1 Overview

###### 2.4.5.1.1.1.0-1 Overview Receiver Scheme reference (ID: 11106136)

**2.4.5.1.1.1.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `11106142`)

Overview
In the whole overview subsection, the source of the Receiver Structure scheme is available on the link below:

<https://nokia.sharepoint.com/sites/5Gdoc/AS_Tech_Spec/Forms/Tree_View.aspx?csf=1&web=1&e=roJEom&cid=a17f47e7%2Da635%2D4f31%2Dae2a%2Db31c90fbfa44&FolderCTID=0x012000F0A0A3BA325F7648AD307E6959BA7376&OR=Teams%2DHL&CT=1656399277106&clickparams=eyJBcHBOYW1lIjoiVGVhbXMtRGVza3RvcCIsIkFwcFZlcnNpb24iOiIyNy8yMjA2MDYxNDgwNSIsIkhhc0ZlZGVyYXRlZFVzZXIiOmZhbHNlfQ%3D%3D&id=%2Fsites%2F5Gdoc%2FAS%5FTech%5FSpec%2FDrawings%20and%20diagrams%20for%205G%2FUser%20Plane%2F5G%20UP%20Algorithms%2F2%20L1%20Algorithms%20and%20Configurations&viewid=967508b3%2D8e81%2D461c%2Db99d%2D145065cf0c1c>
