The scheme illustrates the Signal Processing for SRS Receiver:

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a flowchart illustrating a signal processing chain. The flowchart begins with "FFT & RB de-mapping" on the left, followed by "TOC & FOC", and then "RAW Channel Estimation". From "RAW Channel Estimation", a line goes to "Pilot Separation".
There is a separate block labeled "TOE & DTX detection" at the top, with an upward arrow indicating "DTX status". This block receives input from "Pilot Separation" and also has feedback loops from "Noise Power Estimation" and "Signal Power Estimation".
"Pilot Separation" feeds into "Noise Power Estimation" and "Signal Power Estimation". Both "Noise Power Estimation" and "Signal Power Estimation" have feedback loops to "TOE & DTX detection".
"Signal Power Estimation" also feeds into "Channel Smoother Selection". "Noise Power Estimation" also feeds into "Channel Smoother Selection".
"Channel Smoother Selection" then feeds into "Channel Smoothing (MMSE Filtering)".
"Channel Smoothing (MMSE Filtering)" has two output paths. One goes to "RI Selection", and the other goes to "PMI Selection".
"SNR Measurement" is positioned above and to the right of "Channel Smoothing (MMSE Filtering)". It receives input from "Signal Power Estimation".
"RI Selection" receives input from "SNR Measurement" and "Channel Smoothing (MMSE Filtering)". The output of "RI Selection" is labeled "RI".
"PMI Selection" receives input from "Channel Smoothing (MMSE Filtering)". The output of "PMI Selection" is labeled "PMI".
The overall flow suggests a process for channel estimation and selection of relevant information (RI and PMI) based on signal and noise characteristics, with a DTX detection mechanism integrated.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_3150.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

Please refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_16062\_replaced([11106136](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koUGCfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for the source link of the scheme.

For each processing step supported by L1 HWs refer to:

* 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1483\_replaced([11106160](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koUtBvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for Receiver Modes
* 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1495\_replaced([11106236](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koVUJ_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for TOC and TOE
* 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1504\_replaced([11106276](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koV7LvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for Raw Channel Estimation
* 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1508\_replaced([11106311](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koWiO_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for Noise Power Estimation
* 5G\_UP\_ALG\_16181\_replaced([11106334](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koXJQ_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for Signal Power Estimation
* 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1515\_replaced([11106356](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koXJTfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for Rank Indicator (RI) metric
* 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1515\_replaced([11106356](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koXJTfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for Precoding Matrix Index (PMI) metric

[CB007595-C]

Note:

- Add link to NE 5G-L1-UL (Radio) as SRS receiver is implemented in Radio(L1) for eCPRI 7-2e. (Note kept only for this version)

[End CB007595-C]

##### 2.4.5.1.1.2 Receiver modes

###### 2.4.5.1.1.2.0-1 Receiver modes Overview (ID: 11106163)

**2.4.5.1.1.2.0-1.0-10**  (ID: `13342914`)

Receiver modes
For SU-MIMO usage, the DU (for L1 Loner, Loki, Thor, [CB016066-C] GPU [End CB016066-C] ) and the Radio (for Thor 7-2e) shall perform the SRS detection algorithm as described in section 2.6 of the algorithm specification, please see [11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

Refer to SRS Receiver Scheme Overview in [11106134](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koUGB_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

Receivers with up to N antenna streams are supported, where N values are given in [11106189](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koUtEPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ), [11106213](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koVUH_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) and [11106222](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koVUIvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

~~Use of 2Rx, 4Rx, 8Rx etc is configurable per cell during cell setup phase.~~

DU shall derive the (2Rx, or 4Rx, or 8Rx, etc.) order of the SRS receiver during cell setup phase based on the supported configuration, refer to [11094165](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmaBcPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ). The corresponding AxC mapping can be found in [11389618](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ufPCk_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

When NRBTS/NRCELL/numberOfRxBeamforming=16Rx, Radio shall support configured ~~number of Rx for~~ SRS (8 or 16) receiver order for each UE SRS reception.

---

###### 2.4.5.1.1.2.0-2 Receiver modes TDD FR1 - Receiver mode (ID: 11106189)

**2.4.5.1.1.2.0-2.0-10**  (ID: `13457831`)

Receiver modes
Following receiver modes are supported (see also [11106163](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koUtB_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) with 2, 4 or 8 antenna streams. Use of 2Rx or 4Rx or 8RX is configurable per cell during cell setup phase, refer to [11094176](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmaBffvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

|  |  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **L1 HW** | **2RX** | **4RX** | **8RX** | **16RX** | **First Feature** |
| Loner | supported | supported | - | - |  |
| Loki | supported | supported | - | - |  |
| Thor | supported | supported | supported | supported | CB008325-5G-AA |
| Thor (Radio 7-2e) | - | - | supported | supported | CB007595-C |
| GPU | - | supported | **[CB016061-B1] supported** **[End CB016061-B1]** | **[CB016354-A] supported** **[End CB016354-A]** | CB016066-C |

SRS Receiver processing supported by L1 HW is as in functional blocks scheme view in [11106147](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koUtAfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

For eCPRI 7-2e, for SRS SU-MIMO the Radio shall support, 8RX with C16'2 and with C16'4, 16Rx **with C16'4** subcell type.***\** \*When NRCELL\numberOfRxBeamforming=16Rx, radio shall support configured number of Rx for SRS (8 or 16) for each UE SRS reception.**

---

###### 2.4.5.1.1.2.0-3 Receiver modes TDD FR2 - Receiver mode (ID: 11106213)

**2.4.5.1.1.2.0-3.0-2**  (ID: `11106219`)

Receiver modes
Following receiver modes are supported (see also 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1484\_replaced([11106163](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koUtB_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| L1 HW | 2RX | 4RX | First Feature |
| Loner | supported | - |  |
| Loki | supported | - | CB007596-A |
| Thor | supported | - | CB010576-A |
| vDU | - | - |  |

\*MLSE: Maximum Likelihood Sequence Estimation

---

###### 2.4.5.1.1.2.0-4 Receiver modes FDD FR1 - Receiver mode (ID: 11106222)

**2.4.5.1.1.2.0-4.0-5**  (ID: `13369087`)

Receiver modes
Following receiver modes are supported (see also 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1484\_replaced([11106163](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koUtB_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **L1 HW** | **2RX** | **4RX** | **8RX** | **First Feature** |
| Loner | supported | supported | - |  |
| Loki | supported | supported | - |  |
| Thor | **supported** | supported | supported | CB007487-D |
| GPU | - | supported | - | **CB016406-A** |

DU shall support 8RX SRS Receiver order programmed **[Before CB014128-A] only** [End CB014128-A]:  
- with Beamforming, when “rdFddBfL3Call (see [11329923](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TtGWePvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) = true” or (NRCELL.numberOfRxBeamforming = 8Rx and NRCELL/numberOfRxPucch=8Rx)  
- **[CB014128-A, CB014128-B] or without BeamForming when NRBTS/NRCELL/act8RxNonBeamforming = true.**[End CB014128-A, CB014128-B]***\****\*

SRS Receiver processing supported by L1 HW is as in functional blocks scheme view in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_16065\_replaced([11106147](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koUtAfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

---

##### 2.4.5.1.1.3 TO Estimation and Compensation

###### 2.4.5.1.1.3.0-1 TO Estimation and Compensation Timing Offset Estimation for Loner, Loki and Thor L1 HW (ID: 11106238)

**2.4.5.1.1.3.0-1.0-3**  (ID: `11106247`)

TO Estimation and Compensation
For L1 Loner, Loki, Thor [CB007595-C]and Thor (Radio 7-2e)[End CB007595-C] HWs:

* TO estimation is performed in Time Domain after IFFT.
* The IFFT size used in TO estimation (64, 128,256) depends the SRS RE number.
* The search window length for time offset estimation is changed to 2/3 CP length, and the search window range is also changed to be symmetric.

The timing offset estimation algorithm for PUSCH is described in section "2.6.4 Time Offset Estimation" of the 5GMax algorithm specification (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_695\_replaced([11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

TOE operation is performed in SRS Signal processing as shown in Receiver scheme view in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_16065\_replaced([11106147](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koUtAfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

---

###### 2.4.5.1.1.3.0-2 TO Estimation and Compensation Timing Offset Estimation for vDU L1 HW (ID: 11106250)

**2.4.5.1.1.3.0-2.0-2**  (ID: `11106255`)

TO Estimation and Compensation
[Before CNI-103796-M]

For vDU L1 HW TDD-FR1:

* TO estimation is performed in Frequency Domain with use of the time-frequency domain relation which introduces time offset dependent phase shifts on the frequency domain OFDM or SC-FDM symbols
* TO estimation is performed separately for each PRB per antenna-wise, and final TO estimation is a mean value over all PRBs per antenna-wise
* Raw CE PRB-based is used with 4 samples per PRB without layer separation
* Relative SC distance is 1

The time offset estimation algorithm on SRS is described in section "2.6.4.1 ACF TO estimation" of the 5GMax algorithm specification (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_695\_replaced([11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). [End CNI-103796-M]

---

###### 2.4.5.1.1.3.0-3 TO Estimation and Compensation Timing Offset Estimation for GPU L1 HW (ID: 13342917)

**2.4.5.1.1.3.0-3.0-2**  (ID: `13467287`)

TO Estimation and Compensation
DU for GPU L1 HW [Before CB016406-A] in TDD-FR1 [End CB016406-A] shall perform

* TO estimation in Frequency Domain with the time-frequency domain relation which introduces time offset dependent phase shifts on the frequency domain OFDM or SC-FDM symbols
* TO estimation separately for each PRB per antenna-wise, and final TO estimation is a mean value over all PRBs per antenna-wise
* Raw CE PRB-based with 4 samples per PRB without layer separation
* with Relative SC distance is 1

The time offset estimation algorithm on SRS is described in section "2.6.4.1 ACF TO estimation" of the 5GMax algorithm specification (see [11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

###### 2.4.5.1.1.3.0-4 TO Estimation and Compensation TO compensation for Loner, Loki and Thor L1 HW (ID: 11106257)

**2.4.5.1.1.3.0-4.0-3**  (ID: `11106265`)

TO Estimation and Compensation
For Loner, Loki, Thor [CB007595-C]and Thor (Radio 7-2e)[End CB007595-C] L1 HW, the time offset compensation (TOC) is moved to front end, it is performed before channel estimation.

For Loner, Loki, Thor[CB007595-C]and Thor (Radio 7-2e)[End CB007595-C] L1 HWs, The time offset compensation algorithm on SRS is described in section "2.6.1 Time Offset Compensation" of the 5GMax algorithm specification (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_695\_replaced([11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

TOC operation is performed in SRS Signal processing as shown in Receiver scheme view in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_16065\_replaced([11106147](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koUtAfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

---

###### 2.4.5.1.1.3.0-5 TO Estimation and Compensation TO compensation for vDU (ID: 11106268)

**2.4.5.1.1.3.0-5.0-2**  (ID: `11106273`)

TO Estimation and Compensation
[Before CNI-103796-M] For vDU L1 HW the time offset compensation algorithm on SRS is described in section "2.6.1 Time Offset Compensation" of the 5GMax algorithm specification (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_695\_replaced([11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

TOC by FFT time shift is performed on the reference sequence.

[End CNI-103796-M]

---

###### 2.4.5.1.1.3.0-6 TO Estimation and Compensation TO compensation for GPU (ID: 13342920)

**2.4.5.1.1.3.0-6.0-1**  (ID: `13342921`)

TO Estimation and Compensation
DU for GPU L1 HW shall perform the time offset compensation algorithm on SRS is described in section "2.6.1 Time Offset Compensation" of the 5GMax algorithm specification (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_695\_replaced([11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

TOC by FFT time shift is performed on the reference sequence.

---

##### 2.4.5.1.1.4 Channel estimation

###### 2.4.5.1.1.4.0-1 Channel estimation Raw channel estimation (ID: 11106279)

**2.4.5.1.1.4.0-1.0-5**  (ID: `13342923`)

Channel estimation
DU (for Loner, Loki, Thor **[CB016066-C] and GPU**[End CB016066-C] L1 HW) or Radio (for Thor 7-2e L1 HW) shall support the channel estimation algorithm on SRS as described in section "2.6.3 Raw channel estimation" of the 5GMax algorithm specification (see [11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).\*\*\*\*

Channel estimation operation is performed in SRS Signal processing as shown in Receiver scheme view in [11106147](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koUtAfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

---

###### 2.4.5.1.1.4.0-2 Channel estimation Pilot separation (ID: 11106292)

**2.4.5.1.1.4.0-2.0-8**  (ID: `13342955`)

Channel estimation
DU (for Loner, Loki, Thor **[CB016066-C] and GPU**[End CB016066-C] L1 HW) or Radio (for Thor 7-2e L1 HW) shall support the non-overlapping pilot separation on SRS as described in section "2.6.4  Pilot separation" of the 5GMax algorithm specification (see [11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).\*\*\*\*

The DU (for Loki and Thor L1 HWs) and the Radio (for Thor 7-2e L1 HW) shall use overlapping pilot separation for SRS comb4 (K*TC=4)**in case 4 cycle shift per comb is configured, see section "2.6.4 Pilot separation" of the 5GMax algorithm specification (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_695\_replaced([11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).*\*\*

---

##### 2.4.5.1.1.5 Noise power estimation

###### 2.4.5.1.1.5.0-1 Noise power estimation Noise Power Estimation for Loner, Loki and Thor L1 HW (ID: 11106314)

**2.4.5.1.1.5.0-1.0-3**  (ID: `11106323`)

Noise power estimation
For Loner, Loki, Thor **[CB007595-C] and Thor (Radio 7-2e)**[End CB007595-C] L1 HW, the noise is computed on the frequency domain. The computation includes only the used resource elements (i.e. comb assigned to the UE) and it is similar to PUSCH noise estimation on RE allocated to the UE.\*\*\*\*

Noise Power Estimation is described in section "2.6.6 Noise Power Estimation"of the 5Gmax algorithm specification (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_695\_replaced([11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

Noise Power Estimation is performed in SRS Signal processing as shown in Receiver scheme view in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_16065\_replaced([11106147](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koUtAfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

###### 2.4.5.1.1.5.0-2 Noise power estimation Noise Power Estimation for vDU L1 HW (ID: 11106326)

**2.4.5.1.1.5.0-2.0-2**  (ID: `11106331`)

Noise power estimation
[Before CNI-103796-M] For vDU L1 HW, the noise is computed on the frequency domain. The computation includes only unused resource elements i.e. unallocated comb(s).

This method relies on the assumption that one comb is free which suits to aperiod SRS scheduling for a single UE but not for period SRS scheduling for several UEs using all combs (e.g. 5GC001086).

Noise Power Estimation is described in section "2.6.6 Noise Power Estimation"of the 5Gmax algorithm specification (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_695\_replaced([11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

[End CNI-103796-M]

---

###### 2.4.5.1.1.5.0-3 Noise power estimation Noise Power Estimation for GPU L1 HW (ID: 13342956)

**2.4.5.1.1.5.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `13342957`)

Noise power estimation
DU for GPU L1 HW shall perform the noise power on the frequency domain and shall include only unused resource elements i.e. unallocated comb(s).

This method relies on the assumption that one comb is free which suits to Aperiodic SRS scheduling for a single UE but not for period SRS scheduling for several UEs using all combs ~~(e.g. 5GC001086)~~.

Noise Power Estimation is described in section "2.6.6 Noise Power Estimation"of the 5Gmax algorithm specification (see [11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

##### 2.4.5.1.1.6 Signal power estimation

###### 2.4.5.1.1.6.0-1 Signal power estimation Signal Power Estimation (ID: 11106337)

**2.4.5.1.1.6.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `11106342`)

Signal power estimation
The SRS signal power estimation is similar to PUSCH Signal Power estimation in the assumption that:

* PUSCH number of layers corresponds to the number of Tx antenna ports for SRS
* PUSCH pilot spacing is equivalent to the number of combs for SRS.

Signal Power Estimation is described in section "2.6.9 TO phase rotation/de-rotation and signal power estimation "of the 5Gmax algorithm specification (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_695\_replaced([11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

Signal Power Estimation is performed in SRS Signal processing as shown in Receiver scheme view in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_16065\_replaced([11106147](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koUtAfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

[CB007595-C]

Note:

- Link to NE 5G-L1-UL (Radio) is added as SRS receiver is implemented in Radio(L1) for eCPRI 7-2e. (Note kept only for this version)

[End CB007595-C]

---

##### 2.4.5.1.1.7 Frequency Offset Compensation

###### 2.4.5.1.1.7.0-1 Frequency Offset Compensation Frequency Offset Compensation on SRS (ID: 11106348)

**2.4.5.1.1.7.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `11106353`)

Frequency Offset Compensation
FOC on SRS is not supported since only 1 SRS symbol configuration is supported

[CB007595-C]

Note:

- Link to NE 5G-L1-UL (Radio) is added as SRS receiver is implemented in Radio(L1) for eCPRI 7-2e. (Note kept only for this version)

[End CB007595-C]

---

#### 2.4.5.1.2 SRS based metrics for SU-MIMO

##### 2.4.5.1.2.0-1 SRS based metrics for SU-MIMO Overview (ID: 11106359)

**2.4.5.1.2.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `11106365`)

SRS based metrics for SU-MIMO
The SRS based metrics for SU-MIMO usage are defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1518\_replaced([11106367](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koXJUfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). The supported metrics per L1 HW are provided in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1525\_replaced([11106411](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koXwXvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[CB007595-C]

Note:

- Link to NE 5G-L1-UL (Radio) is added as SRS receiver is implemented in Radio(L1) for eCPRI 7-2e. (Note kept only for this version)

[End CB007595-C]

---

##### 2.4.5.1.2.1 SRS metrics for SU-MIMO

###### 2.4.5.1.2.1.0-1 SRS metrics for SU-MIMO UL rank computation (ID: 11106370)

**2.4.5.1.2.1.0-1.0-6**  (ID: `13371292`)

SRS metrics for SU-MIMO
Based on SRS channel estimates, the DU**or Radio shall compute the UL rank, needed when PUSCH is scheduled with rank1.**

The computed UL rank shall be sent to 5G-L2-PS (see [11139919](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krq78PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Per RX antenna (or spatial streams) SNR is used in RI selection.

DU**or Radio shall ~~consider~~ use the value of the parameter P\_boost (P\_SRS\_boost) for TPMI selection provided by higher layers, (see [11119429](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqKgEvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). This parameter corresponds to the linear ratio of SRS EPRE and PUSCH EPRE in the power domain, but only where SRS REs are actually allocated.**

DU shall include IRC gain ([11140120](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krsKE_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) from P\_boost ([11140073](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krrjD_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), so that it is taken into account during Rank selection:   
**-** without BeamForming Mode: for 4RX PUSCH Receiver order **[CB014128-A,** CB014128-B**] or when NRBTS/NRCELL/act8RxNonBeamforming = true**[End CB014128-A, **CB014128-B**]  
- or with BeamForming Mode: when NRBTS/NRCELL/numberOfRxBeamforming >= 4 **Rx*****\*******\****\*\*

and only for IRC receiver (\*).

(\*) For L1 HW supporting PUSCH IRC receiver (SRS measurements are predictions for PUSCH whatever UE is paired or not), please refer to [11103001](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knp-p_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) for SU receiver and [11103053](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knqlvvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) for MU receiver.

RI selection is described in subclause 2.9 of 5GMax algorithm specification (see [11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

###### 2.4.5.1.2.1.0-2 SRS metrics for SU-MIMO Rank computation: number of subcarriers used and RI threshold (ID: 11106387)

**2.4.5.1.2.1.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11106390`)

SRS metrics for SU-MIMO
Number of subcarriers and RI threshold for rank computation follows same rules as 5G\_UP\_ALG\_6449\_replaced([11103673](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knzIlfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

###### 2.4.5.1.2.1.0-3 SRS metrics for SU-MIMO TPMI computation (ID: 11106393)

**2.4.5.1.2.1.0-3.0-6**  (ID: `13371305`)

SRS metrics for SU-MIMO
Based on SRS channel estimates, TPMI selection shall be performed.  It is needed when PUSCH is scheduled with rank1.

Based on the computed rank ([11106370](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koXwUPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) DU**or Radio**shall use the following methods:\*\*\*\*

- For rank1 TPMI selection, the DU**or Radio shall apply MECG metric (Maximal Effective Channel Gain).**

- For rank 2 TPMI selection, the DU**or Radio shall apply SLP metric (Sum Layer Power).**

The rank1 and rank2 codebook used for the computation of the metric is described in [11106370](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koXwUPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ). The computed TPMIs shall be sent to 5G-L2-PS.

The codebook used for the computation of the metrics also depends on the capability of the UE supported codebook subset (*puschTransCoherence). Supported values are* nonCoherent and *fullCoherent and given in [11103324](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knuQH_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) and [11103365](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knu3IvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ), respectively.*\*\*

DU **or Radio shall consider the value of the parameter P\_boost (P\_SRS\_boost) for TPMI selection provided by higher layers, see [11119429](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqKgEvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ). This parameter corresponds to the linear ratio of SRS EPRE and PUSCH EPRE in the power domain, but only where SRS REs are actually allocated.**

DU or Radio shall support values P\_boost including IRC gain ([11140120](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krsKE_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), so that it is taken into account during TPMI selection:   
**-** without BeamForming Mode: for 4RX PUSCH Receiver order **[CB014128-A,** CB014128-B**] or when NRBTS/NRCELL/act8RxNonBeamforming = true**[End CB014128-A, **CB014128-B**]  
- or with BeamForming Mode: when NRBTS/NRCELL/numberOfRxBeamforming >= 4 **Rx*****\*******\****\*\*

and only for IRC receiver (\*).

(\*) For L1 HW supporting PUSCH IRC receiver (SRS measurements are predictions for PUSCH whatever UE is paired or not), refer to [11103001](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knp-p_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) for SU receiver and [11103053](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knqlvvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) for MU receiver.

The full power modes are introduced for non-coherent UE when MRBTS/NRBTS/NRCELL/*actFullPwrMode is active, PUSCH power scaling multiple ratio is introduced for  SNR calculation and TPMI selection see [11103724](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knzvrPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)**.*\*\*

TPMI selection is described in subclause 2.8 of 5GMax algorithm specification for each L1 HW (see [11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

##### 2.4.5.1.2.2 Supported SRS metrics for SU-MIMO receiver

###### 2.4.5.1.2.2.0-1 Supported SRS metrics for SU-MIMO receiver UL rank (ID: 11106413)

**2.4.5.1.2.2.0-1.0-5**  (ID: `13342960`)

Supported SRS metrics for SU-MIMO receiver
DU (for Loner, Loki, Thor [CB016066-C] and GPU [End CB016066-C] L1 HW) and Radio (for Thor 7-2e L1 HW) shall support the configurations for rank computation ([11106370](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koXwUPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) given in:

* ([11103747](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kn0Ws_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) (FR1 TDD)
* ([11103759](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kn0Wt_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) (FR1 FDD)
* ([11103769](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kn0Wu_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) (FR2 TDD)

---

###### 2.4.5.1.2.2.0-2 Supported SRS metrics for SU-MIMO receiver TPMI (ID: 11106427)

**2.4.5.1.2.2.0-2.0-5**  (ID: `13342961`)

Supported SRS metrics for SU-MIMO receiver
DU and Radio shall support the configurations for TPMI computation ([11106393](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koXwWPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) given in:

* ([11103747](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kn0Ws_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))(FR1 TDD)
* ([11103759](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kn0Wt_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))(FR1 FDD)
* ([11103769](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kn0Wu_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))(FR2 TDD)

Rank selection is described in subclause 2.8 of 5GMax algorithm specification (see [11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

DU (for Loner, Loki, Thor L1 HW) or Radio (for Thor 7-2e L1 HW) shall support:

* The number of equidistant Subcarriers for PMI evaluation on SRS = 12.

The link level simulations are available on the link below:

* <https://nokia.sharepoint.com/sites/5Gdoc/AS_Tech_Spec/Forms/Tree_View.aspx?RootFolder=%2Fsites%2F5Gdoc%2FAS%5FTech%5FSpec%2FCFAMs%2F5GC0400%2D5GC0599%2F5GC000532%20%2D%20UL%202x2%20SU%20MIMO%2FSimulations%5Freports&FolderCTID=0x012000F0A0A3BA325F7648AD307E6959BA7376>
* The simulation report is available at “[NR\_UplinkPMISelection.pptx](https://nokia.sharepoint.com/sites/5Gdoc/AS_Tech_Spec/CFAMs/5GC1800-5GC1999/5GC001981%20-%20L1%20algorithm%20changes%20-%205G19B/Simulation%20Reports/NR_UplinkPMISelection.pptx)”.

[CB016066-C] DU for GPU L1 HW shall support PRB-wise processing. [End CB016066-C]

---

#### 2.4.5.1.3 SRS SU-MIMO receiver using the same periodic SRS resource as SRS_BM

##### 2.4.5.1.3.1 Overview

##### 2.4.5.1.3.1-1 Overview

**2.4.5.1.3.1-1.0-5**  (ID: `11106506`)

Overview
For TDD FR1 with eCPRI fronthaul interface, DU or Radio shall perform SRS beamforming using the beam Id selected for each received SRS signal. DU or Radio shall expect two-port SRS resource in this SU-MIMO usage case.

For SRS reception for SU MIMO same as PUSCH cf. 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2032\_replaced([11112298](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpUymPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)):

* For eCPRI 7-2a,  DU shall use 4 beam Ids when NRBTS\NRCELL.numberOfRxbeamforming=8Rx;
* For eCPRI 7-2e, Radio shall use 4 [CB010708-D] or 8 [End CB010708-D] beam Ids when NRBTS/NRCELL/numberOfRxBeamforming = [Before CB010708-D] 8Rx [CB010708-D] 16RX. [End CB010708-D]. [CB010708-D] The number of beam Ids is per UE based and depends on the configuration of the R&D parameter in the DU rdSuUeReceiverSize (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_27595\_replaced([11114156](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpiN9vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) [End CB010708-D].

Note (informative only):

1. L1 expects a 32x1 beam weight vector for 64TRX RU, 16x1 beam weight vector for 32 TRX RU and 8x1 beam weight vector for 16 TRX RU, respectively.

---

##### 2.4.5.1.3.1-2 Overview

###### 2.4.5.1.3.1-2.0-2 Overview For TDD FR1 with eCPRI fronthaul interface, after beamforming, DU(L1) [CB010887-C] or Radio(L1) [End CB010887-C]shall perform the same metrics (TPMI/RI/SINR) computation steps as that specified in chapter 5G_UP_ALG_1482_replaced( 11106131 ) (Receiver algorithm) and 5G_UP_ALG_1515_replaced( 11106356 ) (Metrics computation for SU-MIMO). (ID: 11106515)

##### 2.4.5.1.3.1-3 Overview

**2.4.5.1.3.1-3.0-8**  (ID: `13394575`)

Overview
DU(for 7-2a) and Radio(for 7-2e) shall support SRS SU-MIMO receiver using the same periodic SRS resource as either Long-term SRS\_BM (see overview in [11106444](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koYXavvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ), beamforming in [11106491](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koY-c_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ), Channel estimation and metrics computation in [11106509](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koY-efvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) or **short term SRS BF per technology and per L1 HW are given in below table:**

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **L1 HW** | **TDD FR1** **Long-term BF** | **TDD FR1** **Short-term BF** | **First Feature** |
| Loner | - | - |  |
| Loki | supported | supported |  |
| Thor | supported | supported |  |
| Thor (Radio 7-2e) | supported | supported | CB010887-C |
| GPU | supported | [CB016062-BD]  supported  [End CB016062-BD] | CB016061-B1 |

DU (for Loki and Thor and GPU L1 HWs) and Radio (for Thor 7-2e L1 HW) shall support online modifiable beam set as per [11106444](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koYXavvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

---

12642745:
Overview
For two-SRS port only, DU shall precombine SRS data streams, perform SRS reception and calculate SU-MIMO metrics including TPMI/RI/SINR, using beam weight Id and SRS resource parameters indicated by L2PS.

Red box in figure below indicates L1 functional blocks to support SRS SU-MIMO receiver in SRS based digital beamforming for UL on eCPRI connected RUs for TDD FR1.

DU shall receive beam set with associated pattern ID during cell setup phase or during antenna down tilt online change (see 5G\_CP\_OTH\_21315\_replaced([11611721](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Y5PYcfwOEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). **[CB014301-A] or during online modification of BF NIDD parameters (eg. NRBTS/NRCELL/beamSet.leftEdgeAngle, rightEdgeAngle, upperEdgeAngle, lowerEdgeAngle, etc) via cell reconfiguration (see [11611748](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Y5PYefwOEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))**[End CB014301-A]\*\*\*\*

When *NRCELL/actDlSrsSubbandBm is false,* *the same two-port SRS resource is used not only for SU-MIMO purpose here, but also for SRS-BM purpose. Refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_13922\_replaced([11106083](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koTe-vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).*\*\*

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a block diagram illustrating a signal processing flow, likely in a wireless communication system. The diagram is divided into three main sections: RFU, L1, and L2.
**RFU (Radio Frequency Unit):**
This section is represented by a gray rectangle labeled "RFU". An arrow originates from it, labeled "NDM SRS (MxNx2)", pointing towards the L1 section.
**L1 (Layer 1):**
This section is enclosed in a blue rounded rectangle and contains several processing blocks connected by green arrows.
\* **SRS pre-combine:** Receives the "NDM SRS (MxNx2)" input. An arrow labeled "SRS" exits this block, pointing to "SRS channel estimation".
\* **SRS channel estimation:** Receives "SRS" from the previous block. An arrow labeled "H" exits this block, pointing to "CoMa".
\* **CoMa:** Receives "H" from the previous block. An arrow labeled "Cov" exits this block, pointing towards the L2 section.
Below the main L1 processing chain, there is a parallel or alternative processing path highlighted by a red rectangle. This path also starts with an input from the RFU, indicated by a dashed green arrow.
\* **beamforming:** Receives the dashed green arrow input. An arrow labeled "Beam set" points upwards into this block. An arrow exits this block, pointing to "SRS Channel estimation".
\* **SRS Channel estimation:** Receives input from "beamforming". An arrow exits this block, pointing to "RANK,PMI estimation".
\* **RANK,PMI estimation:** Receives input from "SRS Channel estimation".
**L2 (Layer 2):**
This section is also enclosed in a blue rounded rectangle and contains two main blocks.
\* **Beam determination:** Receives "Cov" from the L1 section. An arrow labeled "Beam set" points upwards into this block. An arrow labeled "Best Beam(s)" exits this block.
The diagram shows data flow and processing steps within different layers of a communication system, focusing on Signal-to-Noise Ratio (SNR) related processing, beamforming, and beam selection.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_3256.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

When short-term beamforming active flag *NRCELL/actDlSrsSubbandBm is true, the same two-port is used for SU-MIMO, Long-term BM and* *short-term BM purpose, but only some PRBs from wideband SRS are extracted for SU-MIMO and Long-term BM.*\*\*

[image-1][image\_desc]The image is a block diagram illustrating a signal processing flow, likely for a wireless communication system. The diagram is organized into three main sections labeled "L1" and "L2", with an initial processing block above "L1". A large gray block labeled "RF" is positioned to the left, indicating the radio frequency interface.
The top processing block, which appears to be a parallel or alternative path, takes "NDM SRS MXNX2" as input, processes it through "SRS channel estimation" and then "MMSE", outputting "BWVS".
The "L1" section starts with "NDM SRS (MxNx2)" as input, which is then fed into "SRS pre-combine". The output of "SRS pre-combine" is labeled "SRS" and goes into "SRS channel estimation". The output of this block is labeled "H" and feeds into "CoMa".
Below the "L1" section, enclosed in a red rectangle, is another processing path. It receives input from the "RF" block via a dashed arrow labeled "NDM SRS (MxNx2)". This input goes into a "beamforming" block. The output of "beamforming" is fed into "SRS Channel estimation", and its output then goes into "RANK,PMI estimation". An arrow labeled "Beam set" points upwards into the "beamforming" block.
The "L2" section takes "Cov" as input from the "CoMa" block in "L1". It then proceeds to "Beam determination", which outputs "Best Beam(s)". An arrow labeled "Beam set" points upwards into the "Beam determination" block.
The overall flow suggests a process of channel estimation, pre-combining, beamforming, and beam determination, likely for optimizing wireless transmission.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_3256.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]

Overview
For TDD FR1 eCPRI 7-2e:
When NRCELL/actDlSrsSubbandBm is false, Radio shall be able to use the same two-port SRS resource for UL SU-MIMO and Long-term BM (see [11107165](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koco1_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) purposes.
When NRCELL/actDlSrsSubbandBm is true, Radio shall be able to use one of the two-port SRS resources for UL SU-MIMO, Long-term BM (see [11107165](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koco1_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and short-term BM (see [11107565](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kog6SPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) purposes, and only a subset of PRBs from SRS are extracted for UL SU-MIMO and Long-term BM (see [11155560](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksv6DPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)).
**Purple box in figure below indicates Radio functional blocks to support SRS SU-MIMO receiver in SRS based digital beamforming for UL.**
Radio shall receive beam set with associated pattern ID during cell setup phase or during antenna down tilt online change (see [11611721](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Y5PYcfwOEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). [CB014301-A] or during online modification of BF NIDD parameters (eg. NRBTS/NRCELL/beamSet.leftEdgeAngle, rightEdgeAngle, upperEdgeAngle, lowerEdgeAngle, etc) via cell reconfiguration (see [11611748](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Y5PYefwOEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) [End CB014301-A]
[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a block diagram illustrating the process of Sounding Reference Signal (SRS) reception and processing in a 5G network, specifically focusing on the uplink (UL) path. The diagram is divided into two main sections: "Radio" and "DU" (Digital Unit).
**Radio Section:**
This section details the initial processing of SRS signals received from a User Equipment (UE).
- **UE Transceiver:** The UE transmits TAS SRS or Codebook SRS.
- **SRS Reception per TRX:** Raw SRS data is received per Transceiver (TRX). This data is represented as (64, 4x2).
- **SRS Channel Estimation:** This block performs channel estimation based on the received SRS.
- **Calculate SRS power, SINR and decide DTX status:** This process calculates the power and Signal-to-Interference-plus-Noise Ratio (SINR) of the SRS and determines the Discontinuous Transmission (DTX) status.
- **Store SRS CE per SB in memory for SB BF users:** Channel Estimation (CE) results are stored per Spatial Beam (SB) in memory for users intended for Spatial Beamforming (BF).
- **Calculate user correlation and singular values:** This block calculates user correlation and singular values, likely for beamforming or user grouping.
- **Calculate SRS channel correlation:** This process calculates the correlation of the SRS channel.
- **BF subband interference detection:** This block detects interference within specific subbands related to beamforming.
- **LT BF:** This sub-section deals with Long-Term Beamforming.
- **SRS Channel Estimation:** Channel estimation is performed again, possibly with different parameters or for a different purpose.
- **Calculation of H and V Gramian matrices:** Gramian matrices (H and V) are calculated, which are often used in channel estimation and beamforming.
**DU Section:**
This section outlines the processing performed within the Digital Unit, which is further divided into "5G-L1-UL (BB)" (Baseband) and "L2 (DU)" (Layer 2).
- **5G-L1-UL (BB):**
- **Proxy to pass the Measurement and Covariance matrix result to L2:** This block acts as an intermediary to transfer measurement and covariance matrix results to Layer 2.
- **L2 (DU):**
- **Long-term correlation table for DL MU-MIMO:** A table containing long-term correlation information for Downlink Multi-User MIMO (MU-MIMO) is generated.
- **DL MU-MIMO Pairing:** This process is responsible for pairing users for DL MU-MIMO.
- **BF Method determination:** The appropriate beamforming method (STBF or LTBF) is determined.
- **Cov:** Covariance information is passed to the Beam determination block.
- **Beam determination:** This block determines the optimal beam based on the received information. It outputs "Best (Indices)".
- **Beam set:** A beam set is provided as input to the Beam determination block.
- **Rank and PMI determination:** This process determines the Rank and Precoding Matrix Indicator (PMI). It outputs "Rank PMI".
The diagram shows data flow using green arrows, with labels indicating the type of data or process. The "H" label likely signifies channel information, and "Cov" signifies covariance. The overall diagram illustrates a complex workflow involving SRS reception, channel estimation, correlation calculations, and beamforming decisions in a 5G uplink scenario.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/img-05161131.png[/image\_path][/image-0]
File: Short and Long-term Beam Management.pptx
Folder:

SRS SU-MIMO receiver using the same periodic SRS resource for positioning
11106542:
SRS SU-MIMO receiver using the same periodic SRS resource for positioning
When NRCELL.actHPPosMethodOptList is set to Non disabled:
For Non BF only, and only two SRS ports are supported for SU MIMO. DU shall perform SRS reception and calculate SU-MIMO metrics including TPMI/RI/SINR .
The same two ports SRS resource is used not only for SU-MIMO, but also for positioning. Refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_27056\_replaced([11107759](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koiIb_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
 
[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a flowchart illustrating a signal processing pipeline. The pipeline starts with a block labeled "RF" in gray. An arrow, labeled "NDM SRS" in green, originates from the "RF" block and points to a blue rectangular block labeled "Raw Channel Estimation". This block is enclosed within a larger blue outlined area labeled "L1(BB)".
Following the "Raw Channel Estimation" block, another green arrow points to a second blue rectangular block labeled "RANK, PMI estimation". This block is also within the "L1(BB)" area.
Finally, a green arrow extends from the "RANK, PMI estimation" block to a third blue outlined area labeled "L2(BB)", which is empty. The overall flow indicates data processing from RF to L1 and then to L2.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_3278.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]
 
 
Source figure: (SRS for positioning architecture.pptx)

---

##### 2.4.5.1.4.0-2 SRS SU-MIMO receiver using the same periodic SRS resource for positioning Channel estimation and metrics computation (ID: 11106545)

**2.4.5.1.4.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11106547`)

SRS SU-MIMO receiver using the same periodic SRS resource for positioning
For TDD FR1 Non BF with eCPRI 7-2a fronthaul interface, after receiving the raw SRS data, DU shall perform the same metrics (TPMI/RI/SINR) computation steps as that specified in chapter 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1482\_replaced([11106131](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koUGBvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) (Receiver algorithm) and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1515\_replaced([11106356](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koXJTfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) (Metrics computation for SU-MIMO).

---

##### 2.4.5.1.4.0-3 SRS SU-MIMO receiver using the same periodic SRS resource for positioning Supported SRS SU-MIMO receiver using the same periodic SRS resource for positioning (ID: 11106549)

**2.4.5.1.4.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11106552`)

SRS SU-MIMO receiver using the same periodic SRS resource for positioning
The supported SRS SU-MIMO receiver using the same periodic SRS resource as Precise Positioning(see overview in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_27142\_replaced([11106538](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koZlg_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), Channel estimation and metrics computation in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_27144\_replaced([11106545](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koZlhfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) per L1 HW are given in below table:

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| L1 HW | TDD FR1 Precise Positioning | First Feature |
| Thor | supported | CB010332-A |

---

Resource allocation
This chapter illustrates the SRS resource configurations for SRS SU-MIMO usage, in time and frequency domains per slot. The supported configurations per technology and per L1 HW are given in the next sections in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1545\_replaced([11106598](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koZljfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1557\_replaced([11106693](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koaMoPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Following 3GPP variables are referred to in this chapter.

* Time domain SRS parameters:
* [image-0][image\_desc]The image contains the text "N SRS" above "symb". The text is blurry and appears to be in a sans-serif font. The letters are colored in shades of blue and yellow, with a gradient effect. The background is white.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_3286.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0], see 38.211 section 6.4.1.4.1 (number of symbols per slot on the air interface)
* [image-1][image\_desc]The image is a close-up, abstract, and colorful depiction of what appears to be a stylized letter "L" and a smaller, similarly stylized element to its right. The colors are a blend of blues, browns, yellows, and whites, creating a gradient effect that gives the shapes a sense of depth and luminescence. The lighting seems to be coming from the top left, casting subtle shadows and highlights. The overall impression is one of digital art or a graphic design element, with a smooth, almost metallic or glass-like texture. There is no discernible text or table in the image.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_3286.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1], see 38.211 section 6.4.1.4.1
* Frequency domain SRS variables:
* [image-2][image\_desc]The image contains the text "BSRS" in a stylized font. The letters are colored with a gradient effect, showing shades of blue, orange, and purple. The background is white.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_3286.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2], see 38.211 section 6.4.1.4.3. Its value defines the number of subbands for a given SRS resource
* When BSRS equals to 0, SRS wideband is configured (see 38.211 section 6.4.1.4.3)
* When BSRS ≠ 0, SRS subband is configured (see 38.211 section 6.4.1.4.3)
* [image-3][image\_desc]The image contains the text "Csrs" in a stylized font. The letters are colored with a gradient effect, transitioning from blue to yellow and then to red. The background is white, and the text is positioned on the left side of the image.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_3286.004.png[/image\_path][/image-3], see 38.211 section 6.4.1.4.3
* [image-4][image\_desc]The image contains blurry, colorful text that appears to be "n shift". The text is in a light blue and orange hue, with a slight rainbow effect. The background is white.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_3286.005.png[/image\_path][/image-4] (SRS allocation with respect to the reference point grid), see 38.211 section 6.4.1.4.3
* [image-5][image\_desc]The image contains the text "KTC" in a stylized font. The letters are colored with a gradient effect, transitioning from a reddish-orange to a blueish-purple. The text is slightly blurred, giving it a soft, almost glowing appearance. The background is white, and the text is centered within the frame.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_3286.006.png[/image\_path][/image-5] (Transmission Comb), see 38.211 section 6.4.1.4.2
* [image-6][image\_desc]The image is blurry and contains text. The text appears to be "11 CS" and "11 SR.S".[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_3286.007.png[/image\_path][/image-6] cyclic shift index, see 38.211 section 6.4.1.4.2
* nRRC (higher parameter freqDomainPosition), see 38.211 section 6.4.1.4.2

The frequency-domain starting position is computed according to the formula in 38.211 section 6.4.1.4.2 using the above 3GPP parameters.

* Spatial domain SRS variables:
* [image-7][image\_desc]The image contains the text "N SRS ap" in a blurry, stylized font with a rainbow gradient effect. The letters are outlined in black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_3286.008.png[/image\_path][/image-7], see 38.211 section 6.4.1.4.1

[CB007595-C]

Note:

- Link to NE 5G-L1-UL (Radio) is added as SRS receiver is implemented in Radio(L1) for eCPRI 7-2e. (Note kept only for this version)

[End CB007595-C]

The supported configurations for the following 3GPP variables (see ([11106557](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koZlivvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) per L1 HW are summarized in the below table.

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **L1 HW** | **Starting symbol**\* ([image-0][image\_desc]The image contains the characters "lo" with a subscript "o" after the "l". The characters appear to be stylized and have a gradient effect.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_3292.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]  ) | **Number of symbols ([image-1][image\_desc]N\_symb^{SRS}[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_3292.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]  )** | **Number of Antenna Ports** ([image-2][image\_desc]N\_{ap}(SRS)[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_3292.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2] | **First Feature** |
| Loner | [11..13] | 1 | 2 |  |
| Loki | [10…13] | 1 | 2 |  |
| Thor | [10…13] | 1 | 2 | CB008325-5G-AA |
| Thor (Radio 7-2e) | [10…13] | 1 | 2 | CB007595-C |
| GPU | **[CB016061-B1] 10** **[End CB016061-B1]** [11..13] | 1 | 2 | CB016066-C |

**NOTE :  Reception of 2 OFDM symbols is not required for the SRS\_SU MIMO block. It shall be considered by a dedicated feature.**

\*Starting symbol depends on the configured Frame structure type via the NIDD parameter MRBTS/NRBTS/NRCELL/tddFrameStructure.frameStructureType, see ([11364454](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_wZygM_vjEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and ([11346921](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubIls_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

When MRBTS/NRBTS /NRCELL/actHPPosMethodOptList is NOT disabled, only the last symbol in special slot is used for positioning. Refer to ([11106549](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koZlh_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for the support L1 HW.

**NOTE: For Loki, Thor and Thor (Radio 7-2e), only Semi-Static and tdLTE frame structures are supported**

| **L1 HW** | **Starting symbol** ([image-0][image\_desc]The image contains the characters "lo" with a subscript "o" after the "l". The characters appear to be stylized and have a gradient effect.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_3299.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]  ) | **Number of symbols ([image-1][image\_desc]N\_symb^{SRS}[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_3299.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]  )** | **Antenna port** ([image-2][image\_desc]N\_{ap}^{SRS})[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_3299.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2] | **First Feature** |
| Loner | {12, 13} | 1 | 2 |  |
| Loki | {12, 13} | 1 | 2 |  |
| Thor | {12, 13} | 1 | 2 | CB007487-D |
| GPU | {12, 13} | 1 | 2 | CB016406-A |

**NOTE :  Reception of 2 OFDM symbols is not required for the SRS\_SU MIMO block. It shall be considered by a dedicated feature.**

DU(for 7-2a) and Radio(for 7-2e) shall support configurations for the following 3GPP variables (see ([11106557](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koZlivvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) per bandwidth and per L1 HW as summarized in the below table.
 
For all the UL Bandwidth sizes (see [11094261](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmaoifvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), following configurations are supported. Any restriction to a given bandwidtdh size is given in the table.
 
When long PUCCH is enabled (i.e. NRBTS/NRCELL/pucchModeSelect is set to longPucch), the number of frequency resources reserved for SRS are reduced (see [11307417](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Tq8aWPvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). This is controlled through the parameter nshift which can take different values as provided in the below table. The maximum value for nshift is computed with the minimum supported Csrs value (see [11106698](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koaMovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) using the following formula:
nshif, max = MAX\_SRS\_BW\_size - mSRS,0 (MIN(supported Csrs))
with MAX\_SRS\_BW\_size is the maximum number of SRS PRBs in 38.211 Table 6.4.1.4.3-1 for a given UL bandwidth size (see [11094261](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmaoifvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and mSRS,0 is specified in 38.211 Table 6.4.1.4.3-1.
For example, for 20MHz bandwidth, MIN(supported Csrs) is equal to 1 in [11106698](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koaMovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
 
| | | | | | | |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **L1 HW** | **B**SRS***\* |* \*n**shift***\* |* \*n**RRC***\* |* \*K**TC***\* | [image-0][image\_desc]11.cr.s[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_3318.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0] |* \*First Feature** |
| **Loner** | 0 | For short PUCCH: 0 For long PUCCH: 20MHz: {0..40}; 30MHz: {0..28}; 40MHz: {0..56}; 50MHz: {0..84}; 60MHz: {0..112}; 70MHz: {0..136}; 80MHz: {0..168}; 90MHz: {0..192}; 100MHz: {0..224} | 0 | 2 | [0..7] | |
| **Loki** | {0, 1, 2, 3} | For short PUCCH: 0 For long PUCCH: 10MHz: {0..16}; 15MHz: {0..28}; 20MHz: {0..40}; 30MHz: {0..28}; 40MHz: {0..56}; 50MHz: {0..84}; 60MHz: {0..112}; 70MHz: {0..136}; 80MHz: {0..168}; 90MHz: {0..192}; 100MHz: {0..224} | For aperiodic SRS*: 0 For periodic SRS*: 10MHz: {0..5};  15MHz: {0..8}; 20MHz {0..11}; 30MHz:{0..18}; 40MHz:{0..25}; 50MHz:{0..32}; 60MHz:{0..39}; 70MHz:{0..45}; 80MHz:{0..53}; 90MHz:{0..59}; **100MHz: [0..67]** | {2,4} | [0..7] for KTC=2,    [0..11] for **KTC=4** | |
| **Thor** | {0, 1, 2, 3} | For long PUCCH: 10MHz: {0..16}; 15MHz: {0..28}; 20MHz: {0..40}; 30MHz: {0..28}; 40MHz: {0..56}; 50MHz: {0..84}; 60MHz: {0..112}; 70MHz: {0..136}; 80MHz: {0..168}; 90MHz: {0..192}; 100MHz: {0..224} | For aperiodic SRS*: 0 For periodic SRS*: 10MHz: {0..5};  15MHz: {0..8}; 20MHz {0..11}; 30MHz:{0..18}; 40MHz:{0..25}; 50MHz:{0..32}; 60MHz:{0..39}; 70MHz:{0..45}; 80MHz:{0..53}; 90MHz:{0..59}; 100MHz: [0..67] | {2,4} | [0..7] for KTC=2 [0..11] for **KTC=4** | CB008325-5G-AA |
| **Thor** **(Radio 7-2e)** | {0, 1, 2, 3} | For long PUCCH: 10MHz: {0..16}; 15MHz: {0..28}; 20MHz: {0..40}; 30MHz: {0..28}; 40MHz: {0..56}; 50MHz: {0..84}; 60MHz: {0..112}; 70MHz: {0..136}; 80MHz: {0..168}; 90MHz: {0..192}; 100MHz: {0..224} | For aperiodic SRS*: 0 For periodic SRS*: 10MHz: {0..5}; 15MHz: {0..8}; 20MHz {0..11}; 30MHz:{0..18}; 40MHz:{0..25}; 50MHz:{0..32}; 60MHz:{0..39}; 70MHz:{0..45}; 80MHz:{0..53}; 90MHz:{0..59};  100MHz: [0..67] | 2 ,4 | [0..7]  for KTC=2, [0..11] for **KTC=4** | CB007595-C |
| **GPU** | 0 , 1, 2, 3 | For long PUCCH:  40MHz: {0..56}; 90MHz: {0..192}; 100MHz: {0..224} | For aperiodic SRS*: 0  40MHz:{0..25} 90MHz:{0..59};  For periodic SRS*: 100MHz: [0..67] | 2  **[CB016062-BD] 4**[End CB016062-BD]***\* | [0..7] for KTC=2,* \*[CB016062-BD] [0..11] for** KTC=4  **[End CB016062-BD]*****\* | CB016066-C |*For periodic/aperiodic SRS, refer to [11106117](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koUF-fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)
 
For periodic SRS, refer to:
\* ([11364756](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_wZ08c_vjEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for the supported nShift values,
\* ([11306608](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Tq4I7vvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for nRRC computation algorithm
\* ([11351366](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubt0gPvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for PUCCH mode
\* ([11351237](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubsmYfvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for cyclic shift [image-1][image\_desc]71.cs.sr.s[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_3318.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]
\* ([11351237](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubsmYfvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for transmission comb KTC
 
For periodic SRS used also for positioning in Non BF cell, refer to ([11107841](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kojWlfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for the supported SRS configuration per L1 HW**NOTE1: For Cyclic Shift and Combs configuration, refer to ([11106780](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koazrfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).\*\*
 
More details about nShift and nRRC max value for each UL bandwidth size are here:

###### 2.4.5.1.5.2.1.0-3 SRS Parameters for TDD FR1 Cyclic Shift and Combs configuration (ID: 11106780)

**2.4.5.1.5.2.1.0-3.0-8**  (ID: `13391562`)

SRS Parameters for TDD FR1

SRS Parameters for TDD FR1
The DU shall support any combinations of cyclic shift [image-0][image\_desc]71. CRS[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_3323.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0] and number of combs KTC in the right range.

The DU for GPU L1 HW shall support aperiodic SRS for SU-MIMO usage (see *[11351221](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubr_XPvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) with KTC=2 and CS either {0,2,4,6} or {1,3,5,7}. (Note1)*

The DU (for Loki and Thor and GPU L1 HWs) and the Radio (for Thor 7-2e L1 HW)**shall support periodic SRS Comb2 for SU-MIMO usage with periodic/aperiodic SRS (Note 1). For the CS combination per comb, refer to [11351237](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubsmYfvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).**

The DU (for Loki and Thor  **[CB016062-BD] and GPU**[End CB016062-BD] L1 HWs) and the Radio (for Thor 7-2e L1 HW)**shall support periodic SRS with Comb4 (KTC=4)** with below limitation,***\****\*

a) For the CS combination per comb, refer to [11351295](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubsmb_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) (Note1)

b) SRS subband length *mSRS shall not be less than 8 PRBs, up to 4 CS can be supported per comb.* *Where* mSRS is given by {*C*srs*, B*srs} according to Table 6.4.1.4.3-1 of TS 38.211**.*****\****\*

c) up to 4 transmission combs per symbol per cell for KTC=4, but 2 combs in maximum would be configured from end to end.

For periodic SRS used also for positioning in Non BF, refer to [11107841](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kojWlfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) for the supported SRS configuration. DU(for Thor L1 HWs) shall support up to 4 CS per Comb2 or Comb4.

Note1: There is a limitation for cyclic shift from higher layers configuration to avoid inter-UE interference, due to simulations indicate cyclic shift interval less than ¼ would degrade the performance.

---

###### 2.4.5.1.5.2.2 SRS Parameters for FDD FR1

###### 2.4.5.1.5.2.2.0-1 SRS Parameters for FDD FR1 SRS Parameters, Csrs and nshift (ID: 11106806)

**2.4.5.1.5.2.2.0-1.0-7**  (ID: `13455074`)

SRS Parameters for FDD FR1
The supported configurations for the following 3GPP variables (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1543\_replaced([11106557](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koZlivvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) per bandwidth and per L1 HW are summarized in the below tables (see also 5G\_UP\_IF\_546\_replaced([11500743](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_tSYlMfv1Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

For more details about the supported configurations for short and long PUCCH for Csrs, you can refer to 5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_2792\_replaced([11307311](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Tq7zR_vcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

When long PUCCH is enabled (i.e. NRBTS/NRCELL/pucchModeSelect is set to longPucch), the number of frequency resources reserved for SRS are reduced (see 5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_2799\_replaced([11307417](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Tq8aWPvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). This is controlled through the parameter nshift which can take different values as provided in the below table. The maximum value for nshift is computed with the minimum supported Csrs value (see 5G\_UP\_IF\_546\_replaced([11500743](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_tSYlMfv1Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))) using the following formula:

nshif, max = MAX\_SRS\_BW\_size - mSRS,0 (MIN(supported Csrs))

with MAX\_SRS\_BW\_size is the maximum number of SRS PRBs in 38.211 Table 6.4.1.4.3-1 for a given UL bandwidth size (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_18\_replaced([11094261](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmaoifvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and mSRS,0 is specified in 38.211 Table 6.4.1.4.3-1.

For example, for 5MHz bandwidth, MIN(supported Csrs) is equal to 1 in 5G\_UP\_IF\_549\_replaced([11500792](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_tSYlNvv1Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

|  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **BW [MHz]** | **C**srs\*\*\*\* |  |  |  | **n**shift\*\*\*\* |  |  |  |  |  |  | **First Feature** |
| **Loner** | **Loki** | **Thor** | **GPU** | **Short PUCCH** |  |  | **Long PUCCH** |  |  |  |  |  |
|  |  |  |  | **Loner** | **Loki** | GPU | **Loner** | **Loki** | **Thor** | **GPU** |  |  |
| 5 | 1, 7 | 1, 7 | 1, 7 | - | 0 | 0 | - | 0..16 | 0..16 | 0..16 | - |  |
| 10 | 14, 7 | 14, 7 | 14, 7 | [CB016406-A] 14 [End CB016406-A] | 0 | 0 | - | 0..28 | 0..28 | 0..28 | [CB016406-A] 0..28 [End CB016406-A] |  |
| 15 | 20, 7, 14 | 20, 7, 14 | 20, 7, 14 | - | 0 | 0 | - | 0..52 | 0..52 | 0..52 | - |  |
| 20 | 25, 7, 14, 20 | 7, 14, 20, 25 | 7, 14, 20, 25, | [CB016406-A] 25 [End CB016406-A] | 0 | 0 | - | 0..80 | 0..80 | 0..80 | [CB016406-A] 0..80 [End CB016406-A] |  |
| 25 | - | 7, 14, 20, 25, 33 | **7, 14, 20, 25, 33** | - | - | - | - | - | 0..108 | **0..108** | - | CB008229-A |
| 30 | **-** | 7, 14, 20, 25, 40 | 7, 14, 20, 25, 40 | - | **-** | - | - | - | 0..136 | 0..136 | - |  |
| 35 | - | 7, 14, 20, 25, 33, 40, 43, 44, 45 | 7, 14, 20, 25, 33, 40, 43, 44, 45 | - | - | - | - | - | 0..164 | 0..164 | - | CB008152-A |
| 40 | **-** | 7, 14, 20, 25, 40,51 | **7, 14, 20, 25, 40, 51** | - | **-** | - | - | - | 0..192 | 0..192 | - | CB007146-A CB007146-B |
| 50 | **-** | 7, 14, 20, 25, 33, 40, 43, 44, 45, 46, 50, 52, 53, 57 | 7, 14, 20, 25, 33, 40, 43, 44, 45, 46, 50, 52, 53, 57 | - | **-** | - | - | - | 0..232 | 0..232 | - | CB008935-A |

**Table1. Configurations when PRB blanking is disabled**

When PRB blanking is enabled (see 5G\_UP\_360\_replaced([11349064](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubbgpPvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), the number of frequency resources reserved for 5G are reduced. Under this scenario, the supported configurations for Csrs and nshift are provided in the below table. The range for nshift is computed with Csrs = 1 using the following formula:

nshif, max = MAX\_SRS\_BW\_size - mSRS,0 (Csrs=1)

with MAX\_SRS\_BW\_size is the maximum number of SRS PRBs in 38.211 Table 6.4.1.4.3-1 for a given UL bandwidth size (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_18\_replaced([11094261](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmaoifvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and mSRS,0 is specified in 38.211 Table 6.4.1.4.3-1.

|  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **BW [MHz]** | **C**srs\*\*\*\* |  |  |  | **n**shift\*\*\*\* |  |  |  |  |  |  | **First Feature** |
| **Loner** | **Loki** | **Thor** | **GPU** | **Short PUCCH** |  |  | **Long PUCCH** |  |  |  |  |  |
|  |  |  |  | **Loner** | **Loki** | **vDU** | **Loner** | **Loki** | **Thor** | GPU |  |  |
| 5 | 7 | 1 | **1** | - | 0 | - | - | 0..16 | 0..16 | 0..16 | - |  |
| 10 | 1, 7, 9 | 1, 7, 9 | 1, 7, 9 | - | 0 | - | - | 0..44 | 0..44 | 0..44 | - |  |
| 15 | 1, 7, 9, 14 | 1, 7, 9, 14 | 1, 7, 9, 14 | - | 0 | - | - | 0..68 | 0..68 | 0..68 | - |  |
| 20 | 1, 7, 9, 14, 20 | 1, 7, 9, 14, 20 | 1, 7, 9, 14, 20 | - | 0 | - | - | 0..96 | 0..96 | 0..96 | - |  |
| 25 | - | 1, 7, 9, 14, 20, 25 | **1, 7, 9, 14, 20, 25** | - | - | - | - |  | 0..124 | **0..124** | - | CB008229-A |
| 30 | **-** | 1, 7, 9, 14, 20, 25 | 1, 7, 9, 14, 20, 25 | - | - | - | - | **-** | 0..152 | 0..152 | - |  |
| 35 | - | 1, 7, 9, 14, 20, 25, 33, 40, 43, 44 | 1, 7, 9, 14, 20, 25, 33, 40, 43, 44 | - | - | - | - | - | 0..180 | 0..180 | - | CB008152-A |
| 40 | **-** | 1, 7, 9, 14, 20, 25, 40 | 1, 7, 9, 14, 20, 25, 40 | - | - | - | - | **-** | 0…208 | 0…208 | - | CB007145-A |
| 50 | **-** | 1, 7, 9, 14, 20, 25, 33, 40, 43, 44, 45, 46, 50, 52, 53, 57 | 1, 7, 9, 14, 20, 25, 33, 40, 43, 44, 45, 46, 50, 52, 53, 57 | - | - | - | - | **-** | 0...248 | 0...248 | - | CB008935-A |

**Table2 Configurations when PRB blanking is enabled**

More details about nShift max value for each UL bandwidth size are here: <https://nokia.sharepoint.com/:x:/r/sites/5Gdoc/AS_Tech_Spec/Drawings%20and%20diagrams%20for%205G/User%20Plane/5G%20UP%20Algorithms/2%20L1%20Algorithms%20and%20Configurations/UL%20-%20SRS%20Reception/SRS%20configurations.xlsx?d=wb3dd70f5275048e992617986f0e87435&csf=1&web=1&e=WV4REF>

For all the UL Bandwidth sizes (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25\_replaced([11094320](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmbPmPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) (with and without PRB blanking), following configurations are supported. Any restriction to a given bandwidth size is given in the table.

|  |  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **L1 HW** | **B**SRS\*\*\*\* | **n**RRC\*\*\*\* | **K**TC\*\*\*\* | [image-0][image\_desc]n.cr.s[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_3334.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0] | **First Feature** |

| **GPU** | 0 | 0 | 2 | [0..7] | CB016406-A |

**NOTE: for SRS using the same comb value in a given srs band, there is a limitation for cyclic shift:**

-either {0,2,4,6} or {1,3,5,7} shall be configured by higher layers to avoid inter-UE interference. In L1, any combination in the right range is supported.

If DSS is disabled, wideband SRS is supported, and the number of SRS subbands is 1.

NOTE: SRS is not supported for DSS

### 2.4.5.2 SRS for Long-term BM

#### 2.4.5.2.0-1 SRS for Long-term BM Supported SRS beamforming algorithms -TDD FR1 (ID: 11106871)

**2.4.5.2.0-1.0-5**  (ID: `13371141`)

SRS for Long-term BM
The following L1 HW shall support the below algorithms for SRS long-term BM:

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Algorithm | Loki | Thor | Thor (Radio 7-2e) |
| Inputs/Outputs/Algorithm | [11106886](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kocByfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) | [11106886](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kocByfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) | [11107168](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koco2PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) |
| Receiver algorithm overview | [11106894](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kocBz_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) | [11106894](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kocBz_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) | [11107174](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koco2vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) |
| Channel estimation\* | [11107130](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koco0vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) | [11107130](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koco0vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) | [11107182](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koco4vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) |
| Precombining | [11106901](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kocB0fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) | [11106901](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kocB0fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) | - |
| Covariance matrix computation | [11107143](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koco1fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) | [11107143](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koco1fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) | - |
| Gramian matrix computation | [CB015122-A] [13367271](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_R9b-0fbTEfC0Z6kKit-MzQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) [End CB015122-A] | [CB015122-C] [13367271](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_R9b-0fbTEfC0Z6kKit-MzQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) [End CB015122-C] | [11107195](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koco5fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) |
| Gramian matrices validation | [CB015122-A] [13367270](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Lj97U_bTEfC0Z6kKit-MzQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) [End CB015122-A] | [CB015122-C] [13367270](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Lj97U_bTEfC0Z6kKit-MzQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) [End CB015122-C] | [11107200](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kodP4fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) |
| First Feature |  |  | CB010887-A |

Note: \* Refer to [11107223](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kodP6PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) for the supported SRS ports

DU (for Loki and Thor L1 HWs) shall support online modification of parameters for precombiner weight vectors, see [11106886](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kocByfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) and [11106901](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kocB0fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

[CB016061-B1] DU for GPU L1 HW shall support SRS long-term BM algorithm as described in:   
- "Supported SRS Beamforming algorithm for GPU - TDD FR1" in [13473056](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_1WvaIjo8EfG0jPRAdLhzvA?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)   
- "Receiver algorithm - DU for GPU L1 HW"s in [13472937](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_hQ9VhDotEfG0jPRAdLhzvA?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) [End CB016061-B1]

#### 2.4.5.2.0-2 SRS for Long-term BM Supported SRS beamforming algorithms for GPU -TDD FR1 (ID: 13473056)

**2.4.5.2.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `13473057`)

SRS for Long-term BM
The following L1 HW shall support the below algorithms for SRS long-term BM for GPU:

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Algorithm | GPU |
| Inputs/Outputs/Algorithm | [13472937](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_hQ9VhDotEfG0jPRAdLhzvA?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) |
| Receiver algorithm overview | [13472942](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_hQ9VezotEfG0jPRAdLhzvA?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) |
| Channel estimation\* | [13472949](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_hQ9VdDotEfG0jPRAdLhzvA?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) |
| Covariance matrix computation | [13472952](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_hQ9VeDotEfG0jPRAdLhzvA?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) |
| First Feature | CB016061-B1 |

Note: \* Refer to [11107223](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kodP6PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) for the supported SRS ports

---

#### 2.4.5.2.1 Receiver algorithm - DU(L1)

##### 2.4.5.2.1.0-1 Receiver algorithm - DU(L1) L1 SRS receiver Inputs/Outputs/Algorithm (ID: 11106886)

**2.4.5.2.1.0-1.0-3**  (ID: `12642904`)

Receiver algorithm - DU(L1)
DU shall consider the inputs and the output of the whole L1 SRS for Long-term beaforming receiver algorithm as below:

**Inputs:**

 Number of TRX during cell setup phase (see 5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_2499\_replaced([11289661](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TqB0X_vcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), which is equal to M*N*P where:

* M= ruNumberOfRowsTRX;
* N = ruNumberOfColumns
* P = 2 is number of Polarizations *(hardcoded in the DU)*

 Raw SRS received signal coming from the Radio (see 5GMAX  Massive MIMO Algorithm specification §4.1.1.1)

* Precombiner weight vectors during cell setup phase (see 5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_4843\_replaced([11294532](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TqEQkfvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) or during antenna down tilt online change (see 5G\_CP\_OTH\_21315\_replaced([11611721](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Y5PYcfwOEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). **[CB014301-A] or during online modification of BF NIDD parameters (eg. NRBTS/NRCELL/beamSet.leftEdgeAngle, rightEdgeAngle, upperEdgeAngle, lowerEdgeAngle, etc) via cell reconfiguration (see [11611748](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Y5PYefwOEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) ) [End CB014301-A] :**
* For row direction: Wh,
* For column direction, Wvrd

**Algorithm:**

 refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1580\_replaced([11106894](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kocBz_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

**Output:**

 Covariance Matrix (CoMa):

* For horizontal direction: Rh with size MxM
* For vertical direction: Rv with size NxN

---

Receiver algorithm - DU(L1)
Red box in figure below indicates L1 functional blocks to support SRS based digital beamforming for UL on eCPRI connected RUs for TDD FR1.

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a block diagram illustrating a signal processing flow, likely within a wireless communication system. The diagram is divided into three main sections: RFU, L1, and L2.
The RFU (Radio Frequency Unit) is a gray block on the left, receiving input labeled "NDM SRS MxNx2".
The L1 section, enclosed in a red rectangle, contains several interconnected blue blocks. From the RFU, an input labeled "NDM SRS MxNx2" feeds into "SRS pre-combine". The output of "SRS pre-combine" is labeled "SRS" and goes to "SRS channel estimation". The output of "SRS channel estimation" is labeled "H" and feeds into "CoMa".
Below the main L1 flow, there's another set of interconnected blue blocks. "RFU" also has a dashed arrow pointing to "beamforming". An input labeled "Beam set" points to "beamforming". The output of "beamforming" feeds into "SRS Channel estimation". The output of this "SRS Channel estimation" feeds into "RANK,PMI estimation".
The L2 section, to the right of L1, contains a single blue block labeled "Beam determination". An input labeled "Cov" comes from "CoMa" in L1 and feeds into "Beam determination". An input labeled "Beam set" points to "Beam determination". The output of "Beam determination" is labeled "Best Beam (s)".
The overall flow suggests that the RFU receives data, which is then processed in L1 through steps like pre-combining, channel estimation, and beamforming. The results from L1, particularly "Cov" and "Beam set", are used in L2 for "Beam determination" to select the "Best Beam".[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_3349.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

Main functions of L1 to support SRS based digital beamforming for UL:

 L1 expects to receive 1 or 2 or 4 port (refer to 5G\_UP\_4089\_replaced([11354191](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucGPAPvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for RRM concept on SRS based DL beam management) raw SRS data streams from RU via Non Delay Managed (NDM) eAxCs. Refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1600\_replaced([11107223](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kodP6PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for supported ports configuration.

 L1 expects periodical one symbol\* SRS resource and this SRS symbol located in special slot of TDD FR1 frame structure.

 L1 shall consider RU TRX array layout and use one R&D parameter from high layers to decide whether pre-combine raw SRS data or not, and if yes, how to perform pre-combining. L1 shall perform channel estimation for those pre-combined/no-combined SRS data. See 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1584\_replaced([11106901](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kocB0fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for details on pre-combining.

 L1 shall construct covariance matrices based on SRS channel estimation result, and then send those instantaneous covariance matrixes to L2PS.

\*Note: depending on the configured TDD FR1 frame structure MRBTS/NRBTS/NRCELL/tddFrameStructure.frameStructureType (semiStatic or tdLte) and configured MRBTS/NRBTS/NRCELL/tddFrameStructure.guardPeriodLength,  this SRS symbol position in special slots maybe any position among symbol index {10,11,12,13}.

Receiver algorithm - DU(L1)
For TDD FR1 with eCPRI fronthaul interface, DU shall expect to receive both horizontal and vertical pre-combination weight vectors during cell setup phase from high layers  or during antenna down tilt online change (see 5G\_CP\_OTH\_21315\_replaced([11611721](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Y5PYcfwOEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) **[CB014301-A] or during online modification of BF NIDD parameters (eg. NRBTS/NRCELL/beamSet.leftEdgeAngle, rightEdgeAngle, upperEdgeAngle, lowerEdgeAngle, etc) via cell reconfiguration (see [11611748](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Y5PYefwOEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) ) [End CB014301-A]. DU shall validates the necessity of pre-combination and shall start pre-combination when SRS raw data streams from at least one polarization are completely received. Refer to [11294532](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TqEQkfvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) for how to generate pre-combination weight.**
 
For all supported mMIMO RU with N columns x M rows TRX array, DU shall select one column’s SRS raw data from TRX array to validate the necessity to perform pre-combination in vertical direction. Only 1 column is selected to save DU(L1) resources such as channel estimation computations. One option is to always select the 1st column to do this validation.
i. For the selected TRX column [image-0][image\_desc]The image is a blurry, abstract composition of vertical and horizontal bands of color and light. This is followed by a vertical band of light blue and purple, with a darker, almost black area in the center, suggesting a shadow or a more intense color. To the right of this, there's a bright white vertical band, which then fades into a soft gray and finally a sharp black edge on the far right. The overall impression is one of soft, blended colors and light, with a strong contrast between the bright areas and the dark edges. a) DU shall perform channel estimation and gets raw channel estimates [image-6][image\_desc]The image contains the text "h\_{i,n,l,k,p}". The text is in a mathematical notation, with the letter "h" followed by subscripts "i", "n", "l", "k", and "p". The subscripts are arranged in a sequence, separated by commas.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_3353.007.png[/image\_path][/image-6]. After group partition and pilot separation, for each TRX index [image-7][image\_desc]The image is a close-up, blurry shot of a person's face, focusing on their eye and cheek. b) Using vertical direction weight vector [image-13][image\_desc]The image is too blurry to describe in detail or extract any text.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_3353.013.png[/image\_path][/image-13] received from the higher layer, DU shall also pre-combine [image-14][image\_desc]The image contains the text "i,n,l,k,p" with a superscript "r" above the "i". Then, DU shall perform SRS channel estimation for the pre-combined SRS signal [image-16][image\_desc]The image contains the text "T*pre" followed by "l,n,l,k".
