After raw channel estimation, group partition and pilot separation, DU shall get pre-combined channel estimate [image-17][image\_desc]The image contains the text "h\_{pro}\_{n,l,k}". The text is in a subscript format, with "pro" as a subscript to "h", and "n,l,k" as a subscript to "pro". The text is blurry and difficult to read.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_3353.016.png[/image\_path][/image-17] per RBG. If there is more than 1 SRS RBG configured for one user, DU shall get [image-18][image\_desc]The image contains the text "h\_pro" in a stylized font. The letters are colored with a gradient from a light yellow to a darker orange, with a slight blueish tint at the edges. The background is white.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_3353.017.png[/image\_path][/image-18] **with size N\_RBG x 1.**
 
Example: for 64TRX RU with 4 rows x 8 columns, SRS comb=2, SRS bandwidth size=2 RBG (8 PRBs)
Table TRX indexes for 64TRX RU in one polarization

|  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 0 | 4 | 8 | 12 | 16 | 20 | 24 | 28 |
| 1 | 5 | 9 | 13 | 17 | 21 | 25 | 29 |
| 2 | 6 | 10 | 14 | 18 | 22 | 26 | 30 |
| 3 | 7 | 11 | 15 | 19 | 23 | 27 | 31 |
|  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| - DU receives a 1x4 vertical direction pre-combination weight vector [image-19][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical expression written in white text against a black background. The expression is: w = [w0, w1, w2, w3]. The text is clear and legible.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_3353.018.png[/image\_path][/image-19] from higher layers. |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| - With received  [image-20][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is: |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| r = [r\_{i,n,k,0}, r\_{i,n,k,1}, r\_{i,n,k,2}, r\_{i,n,k,3}]^T,[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_3353.019.png[/image\_path][/image-20]DU shall calculate the pre-combined SRS signal[image-21][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is: |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| $\Gamma\_{\text{pre}*{f,n,l,k}} = W*{1 \times 4} \* \Gamma\_{4 \times 1}$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_3353.020.png[/image\_path][/image-21]. |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| - DU shall perform channel estimation steps based on above pre-combined signal and gets 2x1 vector  [image-22][image\_desc]The image contains the text "h\_pro" in a stylized font. The letters are colored with a gradient from a light yellow to a darker orange-brown, with a slight blueish tint on the edges. The "h" is larger and more prominent than the "pro" which is smaller and positioned below and to the right of the "h".[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_3353.021.png[/image\_path][/image-22] (2 RBGs in this example and thus size of [image-23][image\_desc]The image contains the text "h\_pro" in a stylized font. The letters are colored with a gradient from blue to orange, and the text is slightly blurred.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_3353.022.png[/image\_path][/image-23]is 2x1.) |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| - DU shall perform channel estimation on 1st column of TRX and shall get [image-24][image\_desc]The image contains the text "hno" in a blurry, pixelated font. The letters are colored with shades of blue, brown, and yellow, with a white background and a black border.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_3353.010.png[/image\_path][/image-24], [image-25][image\_desc]The image contains the mathematical expression "h₁, ..., hM-1". The expression consists of the letter "h" followed by a subscript "1", an ellipsis, the letter "h" followed by a subscript "M-1".[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_3353.023.png[/image\_path][/image-25]**, all of which have size 2x1.** |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| ii. DU shall calculate square value of L2-Norm for [image-26][image\_desc]The image contains the text "h\_pre, h\_0". The text is in a dark color against a white background. The letters are slightly blurred.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_3353.024.png[/image\_path][/image-26], [image-27][image\_desc]The image contains the mathematical expression "h₁, ..., hM-1," written in a light gray color against a white background. The text is slightly blurred.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_3353.025.png[/image\_path][/image-27]I.e., DU(L1) calculates and gets [image-28][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in a blurry, light-colored font on a white background. The equation appears to be: |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| ( |  | hpre |  | )2 = conj (hpre) \* hpre, ( |  | h0 |  |

The asterisk (*) symbol likely denotes multiplication.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_3353.026.png[/image\_path][/image-28]*\*…,[image-29][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in white text against a white background. The equation is: |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |

| The equation appears to be related to signal processing or linear algebra, where |  | hM-1 |  | 2 represents the L2 norm of a vector hM-1, and "conj" denotes the complex conjugate. The asterisk (*) likely represents multiplication.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_3353.027.png[/image\_path][/image-29]**.*\*\*\* |  |  |  |
| iii. In horizontal (row) direction, DU shall always perform pre-combination per row per polarization. Refer to Massive MIMO Algorithm Specification Chapter 4.2.4. |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| iv. In the vertical (column) direction, |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| a) If [image-30][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical inequality. The inequality states that the squared L2 norm of "h\_pre" is greater than the maximum of the squared L2 norms of "h\_0", "h\_1", ..., "h\_{M-1}". The text is rendered in a mathematical font.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_3353.028.png[/image\_path][/image-30] DU shall perform pre-combination per column per row per polarization. Refer to Massive MIMO Algorithm Specification Chapter 4.2.4. |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| b) If [image-31][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical inequality. The inequality states that the squared L2 norm of `h_pre` is less than or equal to the maximum of the squared L2 norms of `h_0`, `h_1`, ..., `h_{M-1}`. |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| The text in the image is: |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| ( |  | hpre |  | ₂)2≤ max[( |  | ho |  |
| The text in the image is: |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| max[( |  | ho |  | 2)², ( |  | h₁ |  |
| Where [image-34][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical expression with vertical bars and a number. The expression appears to be |  |  |  | ₂. The vertical bars are colored in shades of brown, light blue, and purple. The number "2" is positioned to the right of the second set of vertical bars, slightly above the baseline. The background is white, and the image has a black border.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_3353.032.png[/image\_path][/image-34] is L2-norm value, and *h bold lower-case letter represents a column or row vector.* |  |  |  |

11107140:
Receiver algorithm - DU(L1)
For TDD FR1 with eCPRI fronthaul interface, per configured subband per comb per cyclic shift per SRS symbol, L1 shall perform channel estimation according to Massive MIMO Algorithm Specification Chapter 4.1.1.

Input:

 Raw SRS received signals coming from L1-LO or pre-combined SRS signals

Algorithm:

 L1 shall perform raw channel estimation according to 5GMAX Massive MIMO Algorithm specification §4.1.1.1

 L1 shall perform SRS RBG-wise group partition according to 5GMAX Massive MIMO Algorithm specification §4.1.1.2. All RBGs (a.k.a. PRB groups) are non-overlapping

 L1 shall perform SRS RBG-wise pilot separation according to 5GMAX Massive MIMO Algorithm specification §4.1.1.3. Channel estimation per PRB group is calculated by multiplying channel estimates [image-0][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical expression written in a stylized font. The expression appears to be $\overline{h}\_{n,l,k}$. The overline indicates a complex conjugate or an average. The subscripts $n$, $l$, and $k$ are likely indices or parameters. The font used is a bold, serif font with a slight italic slant. The background is white.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_3356.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0] with window function. The window function length is 24 for comb2 [CB008322-D]and 12 for comb4[End CB008322-D].

Output:

 If the input is raw SRS received signals, output are channel estimation vectors per polarization per TRX

 If the input is pre-combined SRS signals, output are channel estimation vectors per polarization per H/V direction of TRXs

For TDD FR1 with eCPRI fronthaul interface, 5G-L1-UL shall compute instantaneous Covariance Matrices (CoMa) computations, and report to L2PS CoMa per indicated SRS symbol per comb per cyclic shift per polarization per H/V direction according to Massive MIMO Algorithm Specification Chapter 4.2.1.

Input:

 Channel estimation vectors per polarization per TRX, or channel estimation vectors per polarization per H/V direction of TRX.

Algorithm:

 If input is channel estimation vectors per polarization per TRX, L1 shall compute subband independent CoMa according to 5GMAX Massive MIMO Algorithm specification §4.2.1.3. Per sub-band per comb per cyclic shift per polarization per direction, L1 shall construct and report the computed CoMa to L2PS (Note: For Loki, this scenario corresponds to either RUs with only 1 row TRX or RUs without vertical pre-combining, thus the computed CoMa here is horizontal [image-0][image\_desc]The image contains the text "R\_{inst,h}" in a dark font against a white background. The text is slightly blurry.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_3358.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0] only).

 If input is channel estimation vectors per polarization per H/V direction of TRX, L1 shall compute subband instantaneous CoMa according to 5GMAX Massive MIMO Algorithm specification §4.2.1.2. L1 shall construct and report multiple covariance matrices per sub-band per comb per cyclic shift per polarization per direction [image-1][image\_desc]The image contains the text "R\_{inst,h}" in a dark font against a white background. The text is slightly blurry.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_3358.001.png[/image\_path][/image-1] and/or [image-2][image\_desc]The image contains the text "R\_inst,v" in a stylized font. The text is black and appears to be on a white background. The "R" is larger and bolder than the rest of the text, and it is followed by a subscript "inst,v". The subscript is smaller and appears to be in a lighter shade of black. The overall image is blurry and pixelated, making it difficult to discern fine details.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_3358.002.png[/image\_path][/image-2] to L2PS (Note: For Loki, this scenario corresponds to pre-combining operations performed in H and/or V directions, thus the computed CoMa here are [image-3][image\_desc]The image contains the text "R\_{inst,h}" in a dark font against a white background. The text is slightly blurry.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_3358.001.png[/image\_path][/image-3] and/or [image-4][image\_desc]The image contains the text "R\_inst,v" in a stylized font. The text is black and appears to be on a white background. The "R" is larger and bolder than the rest of the text, and it is followed by a subscript "inst,v". The subscript is smaller and appears to be in a lighter shade of black. The overall image is blurry and pixelated, making it difficult to discern fine details.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_3358.002.png[/image\_path][/image-4]).

Output:

  [Image: Embedded image] and [image-5][image\_desc]The image contains the text "R\_inst,v" in a stylized font. The text is black and appears to be on a white background. The "R" is larger and bolder than the rest of the text, and it is followed by a subscript "inst,v". The subscript is smaller and appears to be in a lighter shade of black. The overall image is blurry and pixelated, making it difficult to discern fine details.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_3358.004.png[/image\_path][/image-5] (Note: [image-6][image\_desc]The image contains the mathematical expression $R\_{inst,v} = NULL$. The expression is written in a dark font on a light background. The letter R is capitalized and has a subscript "inst,v". The equals sign is centered between the subscript and the word NULL. The word NULL is capitalized.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_3358.005.png[/image\_path][/image-6] for RUs with only 1 row TRX. E.g., 32TRX in RF sharing/split mode).

##### 2.4.5.2.1.0-6 Receiver algorithm - DU(L1) Instantaneous CoMa validation (ID: 13367270)

**2.4.5.2.1.0-6.0-1**  (ID: `13367273`)

Receiver algorithm - DU(L1)
Input:

* NRBTS/NRCELL.actInternalImprovements1.Bit1 set to 1
* NCELL.actEcpri72e is set to false (that is eCPRI 7-2a)
* Rh (resp. Rv) is the Rinst,h  (resp. Rinst,v) instantaneous CoMa for each polarization (+45/-45) per SRS port per direction (H and V) for a given UE, see [11107143](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koco1fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)
* NRCELL.srsForBeamMgmt/srsPowerThresholdForUlBm
* NRCELL.srsForBeamMgmt/srsPowerThresholdForDlBm
* gainCorrection parameter (see [11689713](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_D3D0sv9nEe-D3seUwaI0tg?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)).
* rxScalingFactorEcpriDbm is the default RX power scaling over eCPRI interface (see [11380532](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_uenXgvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Algorithm:

If NRBTS/NRCELL.actInternalImprovements1.Bit1 is set to 1 and if NRBTS.actEcpri72e is set to ‘disabled’, DU (LT beam computer see [13397952](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_1qdpoQizEfGu_4itz1o3RA?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)) shall validate the instantaneous CoMa for each polarization per SRS port.

1). To calculate SRS power (dBm unit) for each polarization according to below equations :

rrmSrsPowerrelative = 10 \* log10 ((Tr(Rh)+Tr(Rv)) / (Nh+Nv))

rrmSrsPowerdBm = rrmSrsPowerrelative + rxScalingFactorEcpriDbm - gainCorrection

where:

* Tr() is a Trace of matrix - sum of its diagonal elements
* Nh is the number of columns in the matrix Rh. If Nh = 1, values of Tr(Rh) and Nh shall be skipped in above equation.

- Nv is the number of columns in the matrix Rv. If Nv = 1, values of Tr(Rv) and Nv shall be skipped in above equation.

-  rxScalingFactorEcpriDbm is the default RX power scaling over eCPRI interface, its value is -152 (see [11380532](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_uenXgvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

* gainCorrection is the scaler (see [11689713](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_D3D0sv9nEe-D3seUwaI0tg?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) during the cell setup.

2). If the value of rrmSrsPowerdBm is higher than SrsPowerThreshold, DU (LT beam computer see [13397952](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_1qdpoQizEfGu_4itz1o3RA?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)) shall set the instantaneous CoMas Rh and Rvfor this polarization as valid, otherwise they are not valid,

where  SrsPowerThreshold = min(NRCELL.srsForBeamMgmt.srsPowerThresholdForUlBm, NRCELL.srsForBeamMgmt.srsPowerThresholdForDlBm)

3). DU (LT beam computer see [13397952](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_1qdpoQizEfGu_4itz1o3RA?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)) shall derive the DTX status per SRS port according to the value of rrmSrsPowerdBm and SrsPowerThresholdDtx for UL BM and DL BM separately. If the rrmSrsPowerdBm value of both polarization for this port is lower than SrsPowerThresholdDtx, DU (LT beam computer see [13397952](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_1qdpoQizEfGu_4itz1o3RA?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)) shall set the instantaneous CoMa for this SRS port as DTX, otherwise DU (LT beam computer see [13397952](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_1qdpoQizEfGu_4itz1o3RA?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)) shall set the instantaneous CoMa of this SRS port as non-DTX, where:

* SrsPowerThresholdDtx = NRCELL.srsForBeamMgmt.srsPowerThresholdForUlBm for UL BM
* SrsPowerThresholdDtx  = NRCELL.srsForBeamMgmt.srsPowerThresholdForDlBm for DL BM.

Output:

-    rrmSrsPowerdBm and reported to upper layer (L2(DU) see [13397952](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_1qdpoQizEfGu_4itz1o3RA?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)) for each polarization;

-    validation result for each instantaneous CoMa per polarization

-    DTX or non-DTX result per SRS port and reported to upper layer (L2(DU) see [13397952](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_1qdpoQizEfGu_4itz1o3RA?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9))

Note: DU (LT beam computer see [13397952](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_1qdpoQizEfGu_4itz1o3RA?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)) reports the instantaneous CoMas per polarization to upper layer (L2(DU) see [13397952](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_1qdpoQizEfGu_4itz1o3RA?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)) for troubleshooting purpose.

For more information, please refer to the document at the below link:

<https://nokia.sharepoint.com/:p:/r/sites/5Gdoc/AS_Tech_Spec/CFAMs/5GC1000-5GC1199/5GC001086%20Eigenmode%20based%20digital%20beamforming%20for%20sub%206GHz/technical%20material/Coma%20DTX%20Issue%20Analysis.pptx?d=w57d2386fc48f4c0c8310f737804f4df9&csf=1&web=1&e=84AU9a>

---

##### 2.4.5.2.1.0-7 Receiver algorithm - DU(L1) Average valid instantaneous CoMa (ID: 13367271)

**2.4.5.2.1.0-7.0-1**  (ID: `13367280`)

Receiver algorithm - DU(L1)
Input:

* NRBTS/NRCELL.actInternalImprovements1.Bit1 is set to 1
* NRCELL.actEcpri72e is set to false (that is eCPRI 7-2a)
* rrmSrsPowerdBm  for each polarization and DTX/ non DTX result for both polarizations (see [13367270](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Lj97U_bTEfC0Z6kKit-MzQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) )
* Rinst,h(resp.Rinst,v ) instantaneous CoMa for each polarization per SRS port per direction (H and V), they can be Rh,+45/ Rv,+45 or Rh,-45/ Rv,-45, see  [11107143](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koco1fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)

Algorithm:

If NRBTS/NRCELL.actInternalImprovements1.Bit1 is set to 1 and if NRBTS.actEcpri72e is set to ‘disabled’,  for each SRS port and each subband (RBG see [11107130](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koco0vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)), DU (LT beam computer see [13397952](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_1qdpoQizEfGu_4itz1o3RA?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)) shall average the valid instantaneous H/V CoMa from different polarizations.

If the instantaneous CoMas coming from the both polarizations are valid (see [13367270](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Lj97U_bTEfC0Z6kKit-MzQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)),

* Cov(H) = (Rh,+45 + Rh,-45) / 2
* Cov(V) = (Rv,+45 + Rv,-45) / 2

Else (that is only one polarization is valid)

* Cov(H) = Rh,+45, or Rh,-45 where Rinst,h is the instantaneous CoMa selected for the polarization where the rrmSrsPowerdBm has the highest value.
* Cov(V) = Rv,+45, or Rv,-45 where Rinst,v is the instantaneous CoMa selected for the polarization where the rrmSrsPowerdBm has the highest value.

OutPut:

* DU (LT beam computer see [13397952](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_1qdpoQizEfGu_4itz1o3RA?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)) shall generate instantaneous CoMas Cov(H) and Cov(V) for each SRS port, per direction (H and V) and per subband (RBG see [11107130](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koco0vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)), and report to upper layer (see L2(DU) component see [13397952](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_1qdpoQizEfGu_4itz1o3RA?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)).

Note: the instantaneous CoMa is still reported to upper layer (L2(DU) see [13397952](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_1qdpoQizEfGu_4itz1o3RA?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)) for debug usage even when DTX is detected. DTX indication to upper layers is per subband (RBG see [11107130](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koco0vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)), and per port.

---

#### 2.4.5.2.2 Receiver algorithm - DU for GPU L1 HW

##### 2.4.5.2.2.0-1 Receiver algorithm - DU for GPU L1 HW SRS receiver Inputs/Outputs/Algorithm - GPU (ID: 13472938)

**2.4.5.2.2.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `13472941`)

Receiver algorithm - DU for GPU L1 HW
DU shall consider the inputs and the output of the whole L1 SRS for Long-term beaforming receiver algorithm as below:

**Inputs:**

 Number of TRX during cell setup phase (see 5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_2499\_replaced([11289661](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TqB0X_vcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), which is equal to M*N*P where:

* M= ruNumberOfRowsTRX;
* N = ruNumberOfColumns
* P = 2 is number of Polarizations *(hardcoded in the DU)*

 Raw SRS received signal coming from the Radio (see 5GMAX  Massive MIMO Algorithm specification §4.1.1.1)

* Precombiner weight vectors during cell setup phase (see 5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_4843\_replaced([11294532](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TqEQkfvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) or during antenna down tilt online change (see 5G\_CP\_OTH\_21315\_replaced([11611721](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Y5PYcfwOEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) ~~**or during online modification of BF NIDD parameters (eg. NRBTS/NRCELL/beamSet.leftEdgeAngle, rightEdgeAngle, upperEdgeAngle, lowerEdgeAngle, etc) via cell reconfiguration (see [11611748](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Y5PYefwOEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) )  :**~~
* For row direction: Wh,
* For column direction, Wvrd

**Algorithm:**

 refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1580\_replaced([11106894](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kocBz_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

**Output:**

 Covariance Matrix (CoMa):

* For horizontal direction: Rh with size MxM
* For vertical direction: Rv with size NxN

---

##### 2.4.5.2.2.0-2 Receiver algorithm - DU for GPU L1 HW Receiver algorithm overview - GPU (ID: 13472942)

**2.4.5.2.2.0-2.0-2**  (ID: `13481005`)

Receiver algorithm - DU for GPU L1 HW
13481005:
Receiver algorithm - DU for GPU L1 HW
13481005:
Receiver algorithm - DU for GPU L1 HW
13481005:
Receiver algorithm - DU for GPU L1 HW
13481005:
Receiver algorithm - DU for GPU L1 HW
Main functional steps here are below provided to describe L1 processing support of UL SRS based digital beamforming with on eCPRI connected RUs for TDD FR1.  
[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a block diagram illustrating a process related to Sounding Reference Signals (SRS) in a wireless communication system. The diagram shows data flow and processing steps across different layers, labeled L1 and L2.
On the left side, a "Radio Unit" is depicted, receiving "SRS reception per TRX". Below this, a mobile device icon labeled "UE Transmits" indicates that the UE transmits either "TAS SRS or Codebook SRS".
The main processing flow starts with "RAW SRS (MxNx2)" entering a block labeled "SRS Channel Estimation" within Layer 1 (L1). The output of this block, denoted by "H", feeds into a "Calculation of H and V Gramian matrices" block. The output of this latter block is labeled "Cov" and proceeds to Layer 2 (L2).
A parallel processing path is shown within a dashed box labeled "UL SU MIMO". This path begins with "SRS Beam Forming", which receives a "Beam set" as input. The output of "SRS Beam Forming" is labeled "SRS" and goes to "SRS Channel estimation". The output of this estimation block, also labeled "H", feeds into a "Rank, PMI Estimation" block. The output of this block is labeled "RI, PMI" and also proceeds to Layer 2 (L2).
In Layer 2 (L2), there are two main processing blocks. The "Cov" output from L1 enters a "Beam determination" block, which also receives a "Beam set" as input. The output of this block is "Best beam(s)". The "RI, PMI" output from L1 enters a "Rank and PMI determination" block, which outputs "Rank" and "PMI".[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/img-04171717.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

Main functions of L1 to support SRS based digital beamforming for UL:

 DU shall expect to receive 1 or 2 **[CB016062-BD] or 4**[End CB016062-BD] port (refer to [11354191](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucGPAPvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) for RRM concept on SRS based DL beam management) **raw SRS data streams from RU via Non Delay Managed (NDM) eAxCs. Refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1600\_replaced([11107223](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kodP6PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for supported ports configuration.**\*\*\*\*

 DU shall expect to be programmed with periodical one symbol\* SRS resource and this SRS symbol located in special slot of TDD FR1 frame structure.

 DU shall take input RU TRX (Rows, Columns) array layout input ~~and use one R&D parameter from high layers to decide whether pre-combine raw SRS data or not, and if yes, how to perform pre-combining. L1 shall perform channel estimation for those pre-combined/no-combined SRS data. See 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1584\_replaced([11106901](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kocB0fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for details on pre-combining.~~

* DU shall construct H Gramian matrices (CoMa) for each Row, then average H CoMa over Rows; similarly, V CoMa for each Column is constructed, then average V CoMa over Columns, see [13472952](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_hQ9VeDotEfG0jPRAdLhzvA?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9).
* DU shall validate the H and V CoMa per polarization for each SRS port.  see [13472954](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_hQ9VczotEfG0jPRAdLhzvA?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9).
* DU shall combine CoMa for H and V for each port, and report them to L2, see [13472956](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_hQ9VfzotEfG0jPRAdLhzvA?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)

Note:

* Those algorithms are performed per subband per comb per SRS symbol.
* The source of the figures are available on the link below:

[Short and Long-term Beam Management.pptx](https://nokia.sharepoint.com/:p:/r/sites/5Gdoc/AS_Tech_Spec/Drawings%20and%20diagrams%20for%205G/User%20Plane/5G%20UP%20Algorithms/2%20L1%20Algorithms%20and%20Configurations/UL%20-%20SRS%20Reception/Short%20and%20Long-term%20Beam%20Management.pptx?d=wa7f0db9326d54ccbb7c754d6521431fa&csf=1&web=1&e=LYkWTr)

*Note: depending on the configured TDD FR1 frame structure* MRBTS/NRBTS/NRCELL/tddFrameStructure.frameStructureType (semiStatic or *tdLte) and* configured *MRBTS/NRBTS/NRCELL/tddFrameStructure.guardPeriodLength,*this SRS symbol position in special slots maybe any position among symbol index {10,11,12,13}.**\***

---

##### 2.4.5.2.2.0-3 Receiver algorithm - DU for GPU L1 HW Channel estimation - GPU (ID: 13472949)

**2.4.5.2.2.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `13472951`)

Receiver algorithm - DU for GPU L1 HW
13472951:
Receiver algorithm - DU for GPU L1 HW
13472951:
Receiver algorithm - DU for GPU L1 HW
13472951:
Receiver algorithm - DU for GPU L1 HW
13472951:
Receiver algorithm - DU for GPU L1 HW
For TDD FR1 with eCPRI fronthaul interface, per configured subband per comb per cyclic shift per SRS symbol, L1 shall perform channel estimation according to Massive MIMO Algorithm Specification Chapter 4.1.1.

**Input:**

 Raw SRS received signals coming from L1-LO ~~or pre-combined SRS signals~~

**Algorithm:**

 DU shall perform raw channel estimation according to 5GMAX Massive MIMO Algorithm specification §4.1.1.1

 DU shall perform SRS RBG-wise group partition according to 5GMAX Massive MIMO Algorithm specification §4.1.1.4. All RBGs (a.k.a. PRB groups) are non-overlapping

 DU shall perform SRS RBG-wise pilot separation according to 5GMAX Massive MIMO Algorithm specification §4.1.1.5. Channel estimation per PRB group is calculated by multiplying channel estimates [image-0][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical expression written in a stylized font. The expression appears to be $\overline{h}\_{n,l,k}$. The overline indicates a complex conjugate or an average. The subscripts $n$, $l$, and $k$ are likely indices or parameters. The font used is a bold, serif font with a slight italic slant. The background is white.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_3356.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0] with window function. The window function length is 24 for comb2.

**Output:**

 If the input is raw SRS received signals, output are channel estimation vectors per polarization per TRX

 If the input is pre-combined SRS signals, output are channel estimation vectors per polarization per H/V direction of TRXs

---

##### 2.4.5.2.2.0-4 Receiver algorithm - DU for GPU L1 HW Covariance matrix computation - GPU (ID: 13472952)

**2.4.5.2.2.0-4.0-1**  (ID: `13472953`)

Receiver algorithm - DU for GPU L1 HW
13472953:
Receiver algorithm - DU for GPU L1 HW

13472953:
Receiver algorithm - DU for GPU L1 HW

Receiver algorithm - DU for GPU L1 HW
13472953:
Receiver algorithm - DU for GPU L1 HW
13472953:
Receiver algorithm - DU for GPU L1 HW
For TDD FR1 with eCPRI fronthaul interface, 5G-L1-UL shall compute instantaneous Covariance Matrices (CoMa) computations, and report to L2PS CoMa per indicated SRS symbol per comb per cyclic shift per polarization per H/V direction according to Massive MIMO Algorithm Specification Chapter 4.2.1.

**Input:**

 Channel estimation vectors per polarization per TRX, or channel estimation vectors per polarization per H/V direction of TRX.

**Algorithm:**

 If input is channel estimation vectors per polarization per TRX, L1 shall compute Independent CoMa according to 5GMAX Massive MIMO Algorithm specification §4.2.1.3. Per sub-band per comb per cyclic shift per polarization per direction, L1 shall construct and report the computed CoMa to L2PS.

 ~~If input is channel estimation vectors per polarization per H/V direction of TRX, L1 shall compute instantaneous CoMa according to 5GMAX Massive MIMO Algorithm specification §4.2.1.2. L1 shall construct and report multiple covariance matrices per sub-band per comb per cyclic shift per polarization per direction [image-0][image\_desc]The image contains the text "R\_{inst,h}" in a dark font against a white background. #### 2.4.5.2.3 Receiver algorithm - Radio(L1)

##### 2.4.5.2.3.0-1 Receiver algorithm - Radio(L1) Radio(L1) SRS receiver Inputs/Outputs/Algorithm (ID: 11107168)

**2.4.5.2.3.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11107171`)

Receiver algorithm - Radio(L1)
Here are the inputs and the output of the whole L1 SRS for Long-term beaforming receiver algorithm on Radio(L1).

Inputs:

 number of TRX (which is equal to M*N*P where M=number of TRX rows; N = number of TRX columns and P = 2 is number of Polarizations,  see 5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_2499\_replaced([11289661](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TqB0X_vcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) from Cplane during cell setup phase

 raw SRS received signal (see 5GMAX  Massive MIMO Algorithm specification §4.1.1.1)

Algorithm:

 refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23222\_replaced([11107174](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koco2vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

Output:

 Covariance Matrix (CoMa) for horizontal (Rh: MxM) and vertical (Rv: NxN) directions sent to higher layers.

---

Below figure indicates Radio(L1) functional blocks to support SRS based digital beamforming in eCPRI 7-2e for TDD FR1.

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a block diagram illustrating a process related to wireless communication, specifically focusing on Sounding Reference Signals (SRS) and beamforming. The diagram is divided into several sections: "UE Transmits:", "Radio(L1)", "FH", "DU(L1)", and "L2".
**UE Transmits:** This section indicates that the User Equipment (UE) transmits either TAS SRS or Codebook SRS.
**Radio(L1):** This is the main processing block.
\* **SRS reception per TRX:** Receives SRS signals.
\* **RAW SRS (MxNx2):** The raw SRS data, with dimensions M x N x 2.
\* **SRS channel estimation:** Estimates the channel based on the SRS. This block receives input from "SRS reception per TRX" and outputs 'H' (likely representing the channel matrix).
\* **Calculation of H and V Gramian matrices:** Calculates Gramian matrices H and V, taking 'H' from the SRS channel estimation as input.
\* **SRS Beamforming:** This block receives a "Beam set" as input and outputs SRS.
\* **SRS Channel estimation:** Another SRS channel estimation block, likely for a different path or purpose, receiving SRS and outputting 'H'.
\* **PMI, RI calculation:** Calculates Precoding Matrix Indicator (PMI) and Rank Indicator (RI), taking 'H' from the second SRS channel estimation as input.
**FH:** This section appears to be a functional split or interface, connecting the Radio(L1) block to the DU(L1) block.
**DU(L1):** This block acts as a proxy to pass measurement and covariance matrix results to L2. It reuses the existing L2-L1(Radio) interface. It receives input from the "Calculation of H and V Gramian matrices" and outputs 'Cov' (likely representing covariance) and another output to L2.
**L2:** This is the higher layer processing block.
\* **Beam determination:** This block receives 'Cov' from the DU(L1) block and a "Beam set" as input, and outputs a result.
The diagram uses blue boxes for processing blocks and green arrows for data flow. A blue upward arrow labeled "Beam set" indicates input to the "SRS Beamforming" and "Beam determination" blocks.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_3363.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

Main functions of Radio(L1) to support SRS based digital beamforming:

* Radio(L1) expects to receive 1 or 2 or 4 ports raw SRS data per SRS symbol in special slot. Refer to [11107223](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kodP6PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) for supported ports configuration.
* Radio(L1) shall perform channel estimation for full TRX dimension SRS data, see [11107182](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koco4vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).
* Radio(L1) shall construct H Gramian matrices (CoMa) for each row, then average H CoMa over rows; similarly, V CoMa for each column is constructed, then average V CoMa over columns, see [11107195](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koco5fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)
* Radio(L1) shall validate the H and V CoMa per polarization for each SRS port. [Before CB011026-A] If both polarizations are invalid, report DTX to L2. [End CB011026-A] see [11107200](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kodP4fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).
* [CB011026-A] Radio(L1) shall derive the DTX status for each port, and report to DU(L2), see [11107200](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kodP4fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ). [End CB011026-A]
* Radio(L1) shall combine CoMa for H and V for each port, and report them to L2. see [11107209](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kodP4_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

Note:

* Those algorithms are performed per subband per comb per SRS symbol.
* The source of the figures are available on the link below:

[Short and Long-term Beam Management.pptx](https://nokia.sharepoint.com/:p:/r/sites/5Gdoc/AS_Tech_Spec/Drawings%20and%20diagrams%20for%205G/User%20Plane/5G%20UP%20Algorithms/2%20L1%20Algorithms%20and%20Configurations/UL%20-%20SRS%20Reception/Short%20and%20Long-term%20Beam%20Management.pptx?d=wa7f0db9326d54ccbb7c754d6521431fa&csf=1&web=1&e=LYkWTr)

Receiver algorithm - Radio(L1)
For TDD FR1 with eCPRI 7-2e fronthaul interface, Radio shall perform channel estimation according to Massive MIMO Algorithm Specification Chapter 4.1.1 and 4.1.2.

Input:

*               Raw SRS received signals per TRX, refer to 5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_2499\_replaced([11289661](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TqB0X_vcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

Algorithm:

 Radio shall perform raw channel estimation according to 5GMAX Massive MIMO Algorithm specification §4.1.1.1.

 Radio shall perform SRS RBG-wise group partition according to 5GMAX Massive MIMO Algorithm specification §4.1.1.24. All RBGs (a.k.a. PRB groups) are non-overlapping. One PRB group consists of 24 consecutive SRS RE for comb2 [CB009527-A] and 12 consecutive SRS RE for comb4 [End CB009527-A].

 Radio shall perform SRS RBG-wise pilot separation according to 5GMAX Massive MIMO Algorithm specification §4.1.1.35. Channel estimation per PRB group is calculated by multiplying channel estimates [image-0][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical expression written in a stylized font. The expression appears to be $\overline{h}\_{n,l,k}$. The overline indicates a complex conjugate or an average. The subscripts $n$, $l$, and $k$ are likely indices or parameters. The font used is a bold, serif font with a slight italic slant. The background is white.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_3366.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0] with window function. The window function length is 24 for comb2 [CB009527-A] and 12 for comb4 [End CB009527-A].

Output:

 output are channel estimation vectors per RBG per SRS port

---

##### 2.4.5.2.3.0-4 Receiver algorithm - Radio(L1) CoMa computation (ID: 11107195)

**2.4.5.2.3.0-4.0-1**  (ID: `11107198`)

Receiver algorithm - Radio(L1)
For TDD FR1 with eCPRI 7-2e fronthaul interface, Radio(L1) UL shall compute instantaneous CoMa independently in horizontal and vertical domain for each antenna polarization

Input:

* [Before CB010887-G-UPD001]R&D parameter rdActHwBfChest, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23597\_replaced([11110513](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpFiAvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))[End CB010887-G-UPD001]
* Channel estimation vectors per RBG each polarization each SRS port [Before CB010887-G-UPD001][CB010887-G] when rdActHwBfChest =0; Or Channel estimation vectors per PRB each polarization each SRS port when rdActHwBfChest =1 [End CB010887-G][End CB010887-G-UPD001], see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23219\_replaced([11107182](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koco4vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Algorithm:

[Before CB010887-G-UPD001][CB010887-G] If rdActHwBfChest =0 then [End CB010887-G][End CB010887-G-UPD001]

* Radio (L1) shall compute the horizontal CoMa Rinst,h by averaging each row of the CoMa and each RBG of this SRS subband, according to 5GMAX Massive MIMO Algorithm specification §4.2.1.3.
* Radio (L1) shall compute the vertical CoMa Rinst,v by averaging each column of the CoMa and each RBG of this SRS subband, according to 5GMAX Massive MIMO Algorithm specification §4.2.1.3.

[Before CB010887-G-UPD001][CB010887-G] Elseif rdActHwBfChest =1 then

* Radio (L1) shall compute the horizontal CoMa Rinst,h by averaging each row of the CoMa and each PRB of this SRS subband, according to 5GMAX Massive MIMO Algorithm specification §4.2.1.3.
* Radio (L1) shall compute the vertical CoMa Rinst,v by averaging each column of the CoMa and each PRB of this SRS subband, according to 5GMAX Massive MIMO Algorithm specification §4.2.1.3. [End CB010887-G][End CB010887-G-UPD001]

Output:

* Rinst,h / Rinst,v for each antenna polarization each SRS port.

Note:

* Rinst,v = NULL for RUs with only 1 row TRX. E.g., 32TRX in RF sharing/split mode.
* [Before CB010887-G-UPD001]The biggest difference between different values of this rdActHwBfChest is: Rinst,h / Rinst,v  are averaging over RBG vs. averaging over PRB.[End CB010887-G-UPD001]

---

##### 2.4.5.2.3.0-5 Receiver algorithm - Radio(L1) CoMa validation (ID: 11107200)

**2.4.5.2.3.0-5.0-2**  (ID: `13423004`)

Receiver algorithm - Radio(L1)

13423004:

13423004:
Receiver algorithm - Radio(L1)
13423004:
Receiver algorithm - Radio(L1)
13423004:
Receiver algorithm - Radio(L1)
13423004:
Receiver algorithm - Radio(L1)
Radio shall validate Gramian matrix Cov(H) and Cov(V) for each polarization per SRS port
**Input:**
-     *Rh / Rv is the* R*inst,h* / **R*inst,v covariance matrix for each polarization, see [11107195](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koco5fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ);******\*
-     gainCorrection is the scaler indicated from L3 interface during the cell setup, see [11379940](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_uejtIPvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9);*-     [CB011026-A] \*NRCELL.srsForBeamMgmt.srsPowerThresholdForUlBm,***NRCELL.srsForBeamMgmt.srsPowerThresholdForDlBm, see [11154516](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kssPr_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ). [End CB011026-A]*
 
**Algorithm:**
1. To calculate SRS power per virtual antenna for each polarization according to below equations:
      [image-0][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical formula. The formula is:
$rrmsSrsPower\_{relative} = 10 \cdot \log\_{10}\left(\frac{Tr(R\_h) + Tr(R\_v)}{N\_h + N\_v}\right)$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_3370.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]
 [image-1][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in a sans-serif font. The equation is:
`rrmSrsPower_dm = rrmSrsPower_relative + rxScalingFactorEcpriDbm - gainCorrection / 10.0`[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_3370.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]
 
where
 -    Nh is the number of columns in the matrix Rh. If Nh = 1, values of Tr(Rh) and Nh shall not be considered in above equation.
- Nv is the number of columns in the matrix Rv. If Nv = 1, valuess of Tr(Rv) and Nv shall not be considered in above equation.
-     *rxScalingFactorEcpriDbm is the default RX power scaling over eCPRI interface, its value is -152 (see [11379940](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_uejtIPvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9));*
 
2. If the value of rrmSrsPowerdBm is higher than *SrsPowerThreshold, the covariance matrix Rh/Rv for this polarization are valid, otherwise they are not valid, where*SrsPowerThreshold = min {*NRCELL.srsForBeamMgmt.srsPowerThresholdForUlBm, NRCELL.srsForBeamMgmt.srsPowerThresholdForDlBm} ~~is given by L2(DU)~~.***[CB011026-A]
3. DU shall derive the DTX status according to the value of rrmSrsPowerdBm and *SrsPowerThresholdDtx for UL BM and DL BM separately. If the rrmSrsPowerdBm value of both polarization for this port is lower than *SrsPowerThresholdDtx, the covariance matrix of this port shall be DTX, otherwise covariance matrix of this port shall be non-DTX, where*SrsPowerThresholdDtx =*** NRCELL.srsForBeamMgmt.srsPowerThresholdForUlBm*for UL BM****\**SrsPowerThresholdDtx = \*NRCELL.srsForBeamMgmt.srsPowerThresholdForDlBm for DL BM.**
[End CB011026-A]
**Output:**
-    Reports *rrmSrsPower*dBm to  L2(DU) for each polarization;\*\*
[Before CB011026-A]
-    Reports DTX to L2(DU) if neihter polarization for this port is valid. Otherwise (if either polarization is valid), reports non-DTX for this port to L2(DU).
[End CB011026-A]
[CB011026-A]
-    Reports DTX to L2(DU) per port.
-    The covariance matrix validation result of each polarization.
[End CB011026-A]
 
Note:
- This requirement shall keep aligning with [11154516](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kssPr_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).
- the reports towards L2(DU) is done via L1(BB).
 
For more information, please refer to the document at the below link:

---

##### 2.4.5.2.3.0-6 Receiver algorithm - Radio(L1) Combine valid CoMa (ID: 11107209)

**2.4.5.2.3.0-6.0-1**  (ID: `11107212`)

Receiver algorithm - Radio(L1)
For each SRS port and each subband, the valid **H/V CoMa from different polarizations shall be combined.**

Input:

-    *rrmSrsPower*dBm for each polarization;\*\*

-    *R*inst,h */* *R*inst,v covariance matrices for each polarization, they can be **Rh,+45 /** Rv,+45 or **Rh,-45 /** Rv,-45, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23220\_replaced([11107195](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koco5fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))***\*******\****

**Algorithm:**

If both polarizations are valid (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23226\_replaced([11107200](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kodP4fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))),

-   Cov(H) = (**Rh,+45 +** Rh,-45) / 2\*\*\*\*

-   Cov(V) = (**Rv,+45 +** Rv,-45) / 2\*\*\*\*

else

-   Cov(H) = max {**Rh,+45,** Rh,-45} //*R*inst,h links to the polarization of higher *rrmSrsPower*dBm would be used***\****\*

-   Cov(V) = max {**Rv,+45,** Rv,-45} **//*R*inst,v links to the polarization of higher* rrmSrsPower\*dBm would be used******\****

**OutPut:**

-  Radio(L1) shall report Cov(H) and Cov(V) to L2(DU) for each SRS port per subband; Note: CoMa is still reported for debug usage when DTX is detected. DTX indication to L2PS is per subband per port.

Note: the reports towards L2(DU) is done via L1(BB).

---

##### 2.4.5.2.4.0-5 Resource allocation Cyclic Shift and Combs configuration (ID: 11107338)

**2.4.5.2.4.0-5.0-6**  (ID: `13396370`)

Resource allocation
The DU (for Loki, Thor**and GPU L1 HWs) and the Radio (for Thor 7-2e L1 HW)** shall support periodic SRS Comb2. For the CS combination per comb, refer to [11351295](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubsmb_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).\*\*\*\*

The DU (for Loki, Thor **[CB016062-BD] and GPU**[End CB016062-BD] L1 HWs) and the Radio (for Thor 7-2e L1 HW)**shall support periodic SRS with Comb4 (KTC=4)** with below limitation,***\****\*

a) For the CS combination per comb, refer to [11351295](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubsmb_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) (Note1)

b) SRS subband length *mSRS shall not be less than 8 PRBs, up to 4 CS can be supported per comb.* *Where* mSRS is given by {*C*srs*, B*srs} according to Table 6.4.1.4.3-1 of TS 38.211**.*****\****\*

c) up to 4 transmission combs per symbol per cell for KTC=4, but 2 combs in maximum would be configured from end to end.

Note1: There is a limitation for cyclic shift from higher layers configuration to avoid inter-UE interference, due to simulations indicate cyclic shift interval less than ¼ would degrade the performance.

---

For TDD FR1 and eCPRI, below figure indicates SRS based short-term sub-band BWVs calculation in the granularity of per UE per SRS period.
[Before CB008322-A]
[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a block diagram illustrating a communication system. The diagram is divided into several blocks, each representing a functional unit.
On the left side, there is a block labeled "RU" (likely Radio Unit). An arrow labeled "Raw SRS" points from "RU" to a block labeled "L1" (Layer 1). Another arrow labeled "BWVs W" points from "L1" to "RU".
The "L1" block is further divided into two sub-blocks: "L1 UL" (Uplink) and "L1 DL" (Downlink).
Within "L1 UL", there are two connected sub-blocks: "Chanel Estimation" and "Short-term beam computer". An arrow labeled "Raw SRS" points from "L1" to "Chanel Estimation". An arrow labeled "H" points from "Chanel Estimation" to "Short-term beam computer". An arrow labeled "BWVs W for PDSCH" points from "L1 UL" to "FH".
The "Short-term beam computer" block has outgoing arrows to a block labeled "L2-RT" (Layer 2 - Real Time). These arrows are labeled "SRS Power", "BWV Status", and "Num of SRS ports". There is also a curved arrow originating from "L2-RT" and pointing towards "Short-term beam computer", labeled "Start pattern ID" and "No. of beam K".
The "L1 DL" block has an arrow labeled "PDSCH Data" pointing from it to "L2-RT".
There is a block labeled "FH" (likely Front-haul) positioned between "RU" and "L1". Arrows labeled "BWVs W" and "FH" connect "RU" and "FH", and "FH" and "L1" respectively.
The overall diagram depicts the flow of data and control signals between different components of a communication system, likely involving uplink and downlink processing, channel estimation, beam computation, and data transmission.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_3410.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]
[CB008322-A,CB008322-C]
[image-1][image\_desc]The image is a block diagram illustrating a communication system architecture. It depicts the interaction between different components labeled RU, FH, L1 UL, L1 DL, and L2-RT.
The RU (Radio Unit) receives and transmits "Raw SRS" and "BWVs W".
The FH (Front Haul) interfaces with the RU and L1. It receives "BWVs W" from the RU and sends "Raw SRS" to L1. It also receives "BWVs W for PDSCH" and "BWVs for UE specific CSI-RS beam" from L1.
The L1 (Layer 1) is divided into two sub-layers: L1 UL (Uplink) and L1 DL (Downlink).
L1 UL contains three main processing blocks:
1. **Chanel Estimation**: Receives "Raw SRS" from FH and outputs "H" (channel estimate).
2. **Short-term beam computer**: Receives "H" from Chanel Estimation and "Raw SRS". It outputs "SRS Power", "BWV Status", and "Num of SRS ports" to L2-RT. It also sends "Raw SRS" to the Long-term Beam calculation and SU-MIMO calculation blocks.
3. **Long-term Beam calculation**: Receives "Raw SRS" from the Short-term beam computer and outputs "H-V Coma" to L2-RT.
4. **SU-MIMO calculation**: Receives "Raw SRS" from the Short-term beam computer and outputs "RI/PMI" to L2-RT.
L1 DL handles "PDSCH / CSIRS" and "Data".
L2-RT (Layer 2 - Real Time) receives inputs from L1 UL, including "SRS Power", "BWV Status", "Num of SRS ports", "H-V Coma", and "RI/PMI". It also receives "Start pattern ID" and "No. of beam K" from an external source.
Arrows indicate the flow of data and control signals between these components.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_3410.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]
[End CB008322-A,CB008322-C]
 
As figure, the main functions are listed in below items:
\* Receive 1-, 2-, or 4-port raw SRS data streams from RU via Non Delay Managed (NDM) eAxCs. Refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1600\_replaced([11107223](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kodP6PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for supported ports configuration.
\* Receive SRS symbol on special slot for tdLte 1116 frame structure [CB008322-A] and all semi-static frame structures [End CB008322-A], see 5G\_UP\_57\_replaced([11346935](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubIltvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
\* Receive raw SRS and perform PRB-wise channel estimation, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_14126\_replaced([11107396](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koeeD_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
\* Calculate BWVs per BF sub-band on short-term beam computer, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_13929\_replaced([11107444](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kofFHvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
\* [CB008322-A] Calculate BWVs for CSI-RS beam on short-term beam computer, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_13929\_replaced([11107444](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kofFHvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).[End CB008322-A]
\* [CB009496-B] In case of broken antenna DU(L1) shall set value of 0.003 for relevant BWV at related position(s) of broken antenna(s), see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_13929\_replaced([11107444](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kofFHvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). [End CB009496-B]
\* [CB008322-A] Calculate H-V CoMa on Long-term beam computer, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1578\_replaced([11106868](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kocBwvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))[End CB008322-A]
\* [CB008322-C]Calculate SU-MIMO metrics (TPMI/RI/SINR), see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1535\_replaced([11106441](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koYXafvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))[End CB008322-C]
\* Report measurements to DU(5G-L2-PS) see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_14363\_replaced([11107520](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kogTOvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), and download BWVs to RU, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_13927\_replaced([11107557](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kog6RPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
 
For the supported L1 HWs, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_21343\_replaced([11107360](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koeeBfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
 
File: Short and Long-term Beam Management.pptx

##### 2.4.5.3.1.0-3 Receiver algorithm - DU Inputs/Outputs/Algorithm (ID: 11107388)

**2.4.5.3.1.0-3.0-2**  (ID: `11107393`)

Receiver algorithm - DU
Here are the inputs/output/algorithm for SRS short-term beamforming (BF) per UE per SRS period.

Inputs:

* For each Cell during cell setup phase given by Cplane

- Beamforming Sub-band size, BWV calculation method and [CB008322-A]SRS quality threshold[End CB008322-A], see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_13821\_replaced([11094605](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmeS5vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

- Number of TRX NTRX ∈ {64, [CB008322-B] 32 [End CB008322-B]}.  NTRX is equal to M*N*P, where M=number of TRX rows, N = number of TRX columns and P = 2 is number of TRX Polarizations, see 5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_2499\_replaced([11289661](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TqB0X_vcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

 For each UE and each SRS resource measurement,

Provided by RU:

- Raw SRS signal per SRS symbol, see 5GMAX Massive MIMO Algorithm specification §4.1.1.1

Provided by DU(5G-L2-PS), see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_13880\_replaced([11156626](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ks2Ap_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

- SRS resource info, Number of BWVs R  (which is aligned with CSI reported Rank), start pattern ID

- [CB008322-A] Wideband CSI-RS beams BWVs calculation flag and its BF subband index

- H-V CoMa calculation request, its start PRB and number of PRB, it is used for long-term BM [End CB008322-A]

-    [CB008322-C] SU-MIMO metrics calculation flag, its start PRB and number of PRB [End CB008322-C]

-     [CB008322-E]Broken antenna Bit mask from L2-PS, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_22217\_replaced([11156533](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ks1ZlfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) [End CB008322-E]

Algorithm:

 Channel estimation, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_14126\_replaced([11107396](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koeeD_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

* SRS quality Estimation, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_14919\_replaced([11107414](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kofFF_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

 Short-term beam computation, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_14086\_replaced([11107441](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kofFHfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

 [CB008322-A] BWVs computation for CSI-RS beams if requested, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_14086\_replaced([11107441](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kofFHfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

 H-V CoMa computation if requested, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1578\_replaced([11106868](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kocBwvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))[End CB008322-A]

* [CB008322-C] SU-MIMO metrics (TPMI/RI/SINR) calculation, 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1535\_replaced([11106441](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koYXafvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))[End CB008322-C]

Output:

* Measurement report to DU(5G-L2-PS) per UE per SRS period, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_14363\_replaced([11107520](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kogTOvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

- SRS quality for each ports, number BWVs per BF subband and calculation result refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_14363\_replaced([11107520](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kogTOvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

* [CB008322-A] H-V CoMa [End CB008322-A]

-     [CB008322-C] SU-MIMO metrics (TPMI/RI/SINR) [End CB008322-C]

* BWVs downloading to RU, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_13927\_replaced([11107557](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kog6RPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

- BWVs per BF sub-band per UE per SRS period,

* [CB008322-A] BWVs for CSI-RS beams [End CB008322-A]

For the supported L1 HWs, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_21343\_replaced([11107360](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koeeBfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).s

---

##### 2.4.5.3.1.0-4 Receiver algorithm - DU Channel estimation (ID: 11107396)

**2.4.5.3.1.0-4.0-3**  (ID: `11107405`)

Receiver algorithm - DU
Input:

 SRS IQ data received from RU

 rdSubbanfBfMethod to select BWVs calculation method, only MMSE is used

 Broken antenna bit mask from L2-PS

 [CB011117-D] Beam weight vectors for columns and rows from beam generation for pre-combination. Refer to 5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_4843\_replaced([11294532](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TqEQkfvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) [End CB011117-D]

Algorithm:

 DU shall perform raw channel estimation for all SRS PRBs according to Massive MIMO Algorithm Specification Chapter §4.1.1.1

 [Before CNI-137131][CB011117-D] DU shall support time offset estimation (TOE) and time offset compensation (TOC) according to Massive MIMO Algorithm Specification §4.1.1.2 and §4.1.1.3. DU shall apply Kronecker product for row beam weight vector and column beam weight vector from beam generation for pre-combination. Note: the support of this functionality depends on L1 Hw as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_21343\_replaced([11107360](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koeeBfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). [End CB011117-D][End CNI-137131]

 DU shall perform PRB downsampling: middle PRB per BF sub-band is selected if rdDownSampleSrsForBf =0 (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_14403\_replaced([11110332](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpDFxfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). The middle PRB is defined in 5GMAX Massive MIMO Algorithm specification §5.1.2.2.

 DU shall apply “PRBs partition” into PRB groups. Each PRB group (or RBG) is centred around a selected/given SRS PRB. One PRB group consists of 24 consecutive SRS RE for comb2 and comb4, according to 5GMAX Massive MIMO Algorithm specification §4.1.1.4. Note that, PRB groups might be partially overlapped.

 DU shall perform SRS PRBs pilot separation with window function length of 24 for comb2 and comb4 according to 5GMAX Massive MIMO Algorithm specification §4.1.1.5. Then it shall get Hs per BF sub-band.

 If any broken antenna bit mask is set to 1,then DU shall set the channel estimation result to 0 for the corresponding receiver antenna.

Output:

* Channel estimation Hs with the size of Psrs x NTRX where,
* s=[0, ..., Nsubband -1] is beamforming subband index see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_13821\_replaced([11094605](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmeS5vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)),

-  NTRX is the number of TRX of both polarizations on gNB receiver

-  Psrs is the number of SRS ports (Psrs = 4)

For the supported L1 HWs, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_21343\_replaced([11107360](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koeeBfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Rationale: Average PRB solution is not used due to computation resource reasons.[Before CB011117-D] TOC is not used because only narrow size PRB (4 middle PRBs) is used in SRS channel estimation. [End CB011117-D]

Note: L2 shall also implement this requirement (5G\_UP\_ALG\_21412\_replaced([11156825](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ks310_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) for small PRB scheduling.

---

##### 2.4.5.3.1.0-5 Receiver algorithm - DU Channel Estimation Correlation (ID: 11107408)

**2.4.5.3.1.0-5.0-1**  (ID: `11107411`)

Receiver algorithm - DU
DU(L1) needs to calculate SRS CE correlations for the UE configured short-term BM, based on the same single PRB between two latest SRS periods, and report the results to 5G-L2-PS. This metric is for monitoring the UE speed, UE with high speed would shows  the lower correlation.

Input:

* Nsubband is the number of beamforming sub-bands, see 5G\_UP\_ALG13924
* Raw SRS channel estimation, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_14126\_replaced([11107396](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koeeD_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

Algorithm:

* Middle beamforming subband index m =Floor (Nsubband /2)
* DU(L1) shall store two latest channel estimation results Hmn-1, Hmn  of the mid PRB in middle beamforming subband, n is current SRS period index
* DU(L1) shall calculate the correlation for each SRS port via Hmn-1 and Hmn . Refer to Massive MIMO Algorithm Specification §5.1.2.3.2 for more detail.

Output:

* DU(L1) shall report 4 correlations (one correlation per SRS port) to 5G-L2-PS together with BWVs calculation, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_21817\_replaced([11157124](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ks5rDvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for correlations averaging in 5G-L2-PS.

---

11107429:
Receiver algorithm - DU
DU shall **estimate SRS power and Sinr as below,**

**Input**

*-**NRCELL/srsForSubbandBm/srsSinrThreshold*given by Cell setup **[CB014094-A] or Cell Parameter Reconfiguration.** [End CB014094-A], see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_13821\_replaced([11094605](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmeS5vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))***\****\*

-    SRS raw CE, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_14126\_replaced([11107396](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koeeD_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

**Algorithm**

-  DU shall **estimate SRS port power *P*p for MMSE by gathering channel estimate for SRS PRBs according to Massive MIMO Algorithm Specification §5.1.2.2.1; Then average power* P*p is input per SRS subcarriers and per TRX as  [image-0][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is:
$\overline{P\_p} = P\_p \* K\_{tc} / (12 \* M \* N\_{TRX})$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_3423.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0] where* M is number of PRB for SRS.***\*\*\*\*

-   If *srsSinrThreshold =  -40,* SRS Power would be used for SRS ports validation and downgrading, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_13935\_replaced([11107463](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kofsJfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).\*\*

-    DU needs to report SRS port power (dB) to 5G-L2-PS.

**-**If *srsSinrThreshold  > -40, SRS power and* *SRS simplified Sinr shall be estimate for MMSE according to Massive MIMO Algorithm Specification §4.1.1.6;***\***\*

-     SRS Power and SRS SINR would be jointly appiled for SRS ports validation and downgrading, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_13935\_replaced([11107463](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kofsJfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

- DU shall report SRS port Sinr (dB) to 5G-L2-PS.

**OutPut**

-    SRS Power for each SRS port.

-   **If *srsSinrThreshold>-40, SRS SINR for each SRS port***

For the supported L1 HWs, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_21343\_replaced([11107360](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koeeBfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

**2.4.5.3.1.0-7.0-2**  (ID: `12798751`)
