12798751:
Receiver algorithm - DU
DU(L1) shall **detect the interference for each beamforming subband based on SRS IQ power as below.**
This function can be enabled via R&D parameter *rdSubbandInterferDetFlag defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_24395\_replaced([11110519](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpFiBPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))**.*Input:** SRS IQ data for each symbol and comb from Radio;
 *N*subband is the total number of subbands, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_13924\_replaced([11107540](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kog6QPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for definition***Slot is the system slot number, given by each SRS receive request.* R&D parameter* rdIQSPikeTshForInterfer, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23233\_replaced([11110409](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpET4vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))* R&D parameter* rdIQSPikeTshForUninterfer, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23844\_replaced([11110420](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpET5vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))* R&D parameter* rdRatioTshOfInterferSb, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23234\_replaced([11110429](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpET6_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))* R&D parameter* rdTshRatioOfInterferedTxPorts, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25013\_replaced([11110542](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpFiC_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).*Algorithm:**Step 1: Du(L1) shall select two pair of normal TRX (both polarizations of each pair, 4 TRX in total)** The first pair of TRX indexes are {Slot mod NTrx/4, Slot mod NTrx/4+ NTrx/2}; In case either TRX is broken, Slot = Slot +1 shall be used until normal pair are found.
 The second pair of TRX index are {(Slot +4} mod NTrx/4 + NTrx/4, (Slot +4} mod NTrx/4 + NTrx/4 + NTrx/2}; In case either TRX is broken, Slot = Slot +1 shall be used until normal pair are found. NTrx  is total TRX number of both polarizations.
 If neither pair is detected, L1 shall bypass interference detection.**Step 2: For each UE’s SRS symbol (or occasion) of a given BF subband, DU(L1) shall detect whether this SRS symbol/occasion is interfered or not.  These intermediate per occasion interference detection results are used as input to determine per subband interference detection results.**For every SRS symbol/occasion, DU(L1) shall
 Estimate the SRS IQ [Before PR852969] linear [End PR852969] power* P*IQ,n [PR852969] in dBm [End PR852969] for each BF subband n* by averaging linearly power of the middle \**24 SRS subcarriers for all selected TRXs, n=1..*N\*subband .******\*
 Estimate the average SRS IQ [Before PR852969] linear [End PR852969] power [PR852969] in dBm [End PR852969] over all BF subbands [image-0][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is: $\overline{P}*{IQ} = \sum*{n} P\_{IQ,n} / N\_{subband}$. The equation is written in black text on a white background. The text is clear and legible.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_3425.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]
 Estimate the [Before PR852969]*IQSpike\_n=*10*log10(P*IQ,n*/ [image-1][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical expression. The expression is written in a stylized font and appears to be in a digital format. The expression consists of the letter "p" with a bar over it, followed by the subscript "10". The "p" is in a bold, sans-serif font, and the bar over it is a thin, horizontal line. The subscript "10" is smaller than the "p" and is positioned below and to the right of it. The colors in the image are muted and appear to be a gradient of brown, yellow, and blue, suggesting a digital rendering or a stylized photograph of the expression. The background is white, and the expression is centered within the frame.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_3489.003.png[/image\_path][/image-1]) [PR852969]*IQSpike\_n=P*IQ,n *- [image-2][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical expression. The expression is written in a stylized font and appears to be in a digital format. The expression consists of the letter "p" with a bar over it, followed by the subscript "10". The "p" is in a bold, sans-serif font, and the bar over it is a thin, horizontal line. The subscript "10" is smaller than the "p" and is positioned below and to the right of it. The colors in the image are muted and appear to be a gradient of brown, yellow, and blue, suggesting a digital rendering or a stylized photograph of the expression. The background is white, and the expression is centered within the frame.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_3489.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2][End PR852969]* for each BF subband n.*****\*** Go through all BF subbands, for any subband with* IQSpike\_n rdIQSPikeTshForInterfer[PR852969] *P*IQ,n*- AveIqSpikeOfNonInterfer> rdIQSPikeTshForInterfer [End PR852969]* , then corresponding subband n shall in this symbol/occasion be recognized as interfered.*****\****Step 3: Maintain and mark interfered subbands in a bitmap for each UE and SRS port.** L1 shall maintain an interfered subband bitmap for each wideband SRS resource. The interfered subband is marks as 1;
 After CE (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_14216\_replaced([11126438](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqxkG_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), L1 shall map the bitmap to the corresponding UE and ports.**Step 4: DU(L1) shall determine whether a BF subband n is interfered for each UE every BWV computation period,** Collect and calculate ratio of interfered SRS Tx ports *ratioOfInterferedTxPorts of this subband n, based on bitmap in Step3 when all SRS ports are collected.*
 If *ratioOfInterferedTxPorts* >=rdTshRatioOfInterferedTxPorts, then corresponding subband n shall be recognized as interfered.**
 
**Step 5: DU(L1) shall calculate the ratio of interference BF subbands for each UE every BWV computation period,**
 Calculate the interfered subband ratio for each UE;
If the ratio for each UE > *rdRatioTshOfInterferSb,*
o L1 shall bypass the BWV calculation and report failure to L2-PS.
else
o For each SRS resource, BWV of interfered BF subband shall be replaced by the closest uninterfered neighbor subband.
endif
**OutPut:**
-   Non Interfered BWVs for each BF subband.
-   Or the decision of bypass the BWV calculation considering the interference ratio.
 
Example: CB008322 interfered subbband detection.xlsx
Link:
 
For the supported L1 HWs, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_21343\_replaced([11107360](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koeeBfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

##### 2.4.5.3.1.1 Short-term Beam computer

###### 2.4.5.3.1.1-1 FEBF Algorithm

**2.4.5.3.1.1-1.0-1**  (ID: `11107531`)

FEBF Algorithm
DU(L1) shall calculate coefficient for all 2D-DFT beams and the real time BWV,

Input

- DFT beams weight, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_21903\_replaced([11094630](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmeS7fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

- DFT expansion coefficient flag and corresponding beamforming subband index indicated by DU(L2-PS), see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_22030\_replaced([11118888](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqG1sPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

- Short term BWV matrix Ws  see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_13929\_replaced([11107444](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kofFHvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

- Number of BWV R within the BWV matrix see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_13929\_replaced([11107444](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kofFHvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

-    BF subband index from SRS receive request; if not requested, invalid value shall be provided.

Algorithm:

- DU(L1) need to calculate DFT expansion coefficient for each BF BWVs with all DFT beams. Refer to 5GMAX Massive MIMO Algorithm specification section 5.1.2.3.3

Output:

- DFT expansion coefficient array with the size of R*P*M\*N, and report to DU(L2-PS).

where R is the number of BWV per BF subband, P = 2 is number of Polarizations, M=number of TRX rows; N = number of TRX columns; For example, for 64TRX, there are R\* 64 EIRP need to be report to DU(L2-PS);

---

###### 2.4.5.3.1.1-2 FEBF Algorithm

**2.4.5.3.1.1-2.0-1**  (ID: `11107537`)

FEBF Algorithm
DU(L1) need to calculate beamforming gain with short-term BWVs for MUMIMO UE,

Input

-   hk is Channel estimate corresponding to the SRS port k with the size of NTRX X 1. It is obtained from HsR,see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_14126\_replaced([11107396](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koeeD_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

-   wk  is single BWV corresponding to the SRS beam k with the size of NTRX X 1, It is obtained from Ws , see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_13929\_replaced([11107444](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kofFHvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

-    BF subband index from SRS receive request; if not requested, invalid value shall be provided.

Algorithm:

-   The Beamforming gain with short-term beam Gk can be calculated with equation:Gk = ||wkThk||2 = (wkThk)\*(wkThk)H

Output:

-   Beamforming gain Gk with the size of 1 x 1, where k ∈ [0,…,R], R is the number of BWV per BF subband

---

###### 2.4.5.3.1.1-3 FEBF Algorithm

**2.4.5.3.1.1-3.0-3**  (ID: `11107553`)

FEBF Algorithm
Below are input/output/algorithm of number of BWV NBwv per SRS period for each UE

Input:

For each cell during the cell set up phase, below configurations are given,

* NBW is the number of PRBs of cell bandwidth, 5G\_UP\_ALG\_18\_replaced([11094261](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmaoifvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
* Nsrs is the number of SRS PRBs, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_13942\_replaced([11107726](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koiIZvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
* Ssubband is the beamforming sub-band size, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_13821\_replaced([11094605](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmeS5vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
* R={1,2,3,4} is number of beam in final see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_13935\_replaced([11107463](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kofsJfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
* n is Subband index starting with value 1. Lowest Subband index n=1 starts with lowest PRB (PRB 0) of the NRBTS.NRCELL.chBw.

Algorithm:

* Nsubband = Ceil(NBW / Ssubband) is the total number of beamforming subbands when subband beamforming feature is enabled.
* NBWV = Nsubband*R ; Or NBwv = Nsubband*R + 4, if CSI-RS beam BWVs are also requested
* PRB offset to the middle PRB of each subband shall be calculated expect the last BF subband is less than minmum BF subband size ([Before PR773514] [Before CB011116-C] 8 PRBs [CB011116-C] 4 PRBs [End CB011116-C] [PR773514] SubbandSizeThreshold PRBs)

SubbandSizeThreshold depends on configured beamforming sub-band size Ssubband.

• SubbandSizeThreshold = 8 PRBs when Ssubband >=8PRBs;

• SubbandSizeThreshold = Ssubband PRBs when Ssubband <=4PRBs [End PR773514],

For subband n in 1: Nsubband

If BF subband n is such that n\* Ssubband ≤ NRBTS.NRCELL.nbrOfBlankedPRBsLowEdgeUL

or (n-1)\* Ssubband  ≥ NBW - NRBTS.NRCELL.nbrOfBlankedPRBshighEdgeUL

midPrbOffset(n) = 0

No need to calculate BWV. Instead, use a value of BWV copied from adjacent valid BWV

Elseif BF subband n is such that (n-1)*Ssubband < NRBTS.NRCELL.nbrOfBlankedPRBsLowEdgeUL and n* Ssubband > NRBTS.NRCELL.nbrOfBlankedPRBsLowEdgeUL

If n\* Ssubband –NRBTS.NRCELL.nbrOfBlankedPRBsLowEdgeUL ≥ [Before PR773514] [Before CB011116-C] 8 PRBs [CB011116-C] 4 PRBs [End CB011116-C] [PR773514] SubbandSizeThreshold PRBs [End PR773514]

midPrbOffset(n) = Floor((n\* Ssubband – NRBTS.NRCELL.nbrOfBlankedPRBsLowEdgeUL)/2)

Else

midPrbOffset(n) = midPrbOffset(n+1)

Elseif BF subband n is such that (n-1)*Ssubband  < NRBTS.NRCELL. nbrOfBlankedPRBsLowEdgeUL + NSRS and NRBTS.NRCELL. nbrOfBlankedPRBsLowEdgeUL +NSRS  < n* Ssubband

If (NRBTS.NRCELL. nbrOfBlankedPRBsLowEdgeUL + NSRS)-(n-1)\* Ssubband ≥ [Before PR773514] [Before CB011116-C] 8 PRBs [CB011116-C] 4 PRBs [End CB011116-C] [PR773514] SubbandSizeThreshold PRBs [End PR773514]

midPrbOffset(n) = (n-1)*Ssubband - NRBTS.NRCELL.nbrOfBlankedPRBsLowEdgeUL  + Ceil(((NRBTS.NRCELL.nbrOfBlankedPRBsLowEdgeUL + NSRS)-(n-1)* Ssubband)/2)

Else

midPrbOffset(n) = midPrbOffset(n-1)

Elseif BF subband n is such that  (n-1)*Ssubband  >= NRBTS.NRCELL. nbrOfBlankedPRBsLowEdgeUL + NSRS  and  (n-1)* Ssubband < NBW - NRBTS.NRCELL.nbrOfBlankedPRBshighEdgeUL

midPrbOffset(n) = midPrbOffset(n-1)

Else

midPrbOffset(n)= (n-1)\*Ssubband  - NRBTS.NRCELL.nbrOfBlankedPRBsLowEdgeUL +Floor(Ssubband /2)

End if

End for

Link to figure to describe requirements above: <https://nokia.sharepoint.com/:x:/r/sites/5Gdoc/AS_Tech_Spec/Drawings%20and%20diagrams%20for%205G/User%20Plane/5G%20UP%20Algorithms/2%20L1%20Algorithms%20and%20Configurations/UL%20-%20SRS%20Reception/Figure_CB009915_interworking_with_CB008322.xlsx?d=w6ba1f0206d6f4e3f81bd35431908827f&csf=1&web=1&e=y2n9ND>

Output:

* Nsubband is the number of BF sub-band
* NBWV is the number of BWVs need to be calculated per UE
* midPrbOffset(n) if the PRB offset from the start of wide band SRS to the middle PRB of BF subband n

For the supported L1 HWs, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_21343\_replaced([11107360](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koeeBfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Note: L2 also implements this requirement (5G\_UP\_ALG\_21412\_replaced([11156825](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ks310_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) for small PRB scheduling

---

###### 2.4.5.3.1.1-4 FEBF Algorithm

**2.4.5.3.1.1-4.0-2**  (ID: `11107562`)

FEBF Algorithm
[Before CB009496-A]

The output BWVs Ws with the size R x NTRX, contains R BWVs, each one with size of 1 x NTRX (NTRX=64) is transmitted to RU in a pair of streams via FCP, each stream is for one polarization containing a split BWV in dimension of 1 x NTRX/2, where R is the number of beams. As for the total number of BWVs per UE refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_13924\_replaced([11107540](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kog6QPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

For the more detail of BWVs download, refer to 5G\_UP\_5701\_replaced([11379419](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_uegp1fvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

For the supported L1 HWs, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_21343\_replaced([11107360](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koeeBfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[CB009496-A]

Input

- NRBTS/NRCELL/actDlSrsSubbandBm is set to TRUE during Cell Setup procedure.

Algorithm

DU (L1) shall download Real time SRS based adaptive sub-band beamforming weights vectors (BWVs), Ws and Ws,csi, to the Radio for DL beamforming over eCPRI 7-2a Fronthaul using FCP messages (adaptive beams).

Note:

- For the more detail of BWVs download, refer to 5G\_UP\_5701\_replaced([11379419](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_uegp1fvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

- For the supported L1 HWs, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_21343\_replaced([11107360](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koeeBfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

- For the total number of BWVs per UE refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_13924\_replaced([11107540](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kog6QPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

Output

- The output BWVs Ws with the size R x NTRX, contains R BWVs, each one with size of 1 x NTRX (NTRX=64) and Ws,csi (for the size please see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_13929\_replaced([11107444](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kofFHvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) are transmitted to RU in a streams via FCP, each stream contains both polarizations containing BWV in dimension of 1 x NTRX, where R is the number of beams.

[End CB009496-A]

---

###### 2.4.5.3.1.1.0-1 Short-term Beam computer Inputs/Outputs (ID: 11107444)

**2.4.5.3.1.1.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `11107450`)

Short-term Beam computer
Here are the inputs/output/algorithm of Short-term Beam computer. This beam computer is per UE per period. The period is every time all ports of TAS SRS are received.

This beam computer is done per UE upon request from L2PS.

Inputs:

* From Cplane when cell setup

-    rdSubbanfBfMethod to select BWVs calculation method. [CB008322-A] When NRCELL/actDlSrsSubbandBm is enabled, only MMSE is supported [End CB008322-A]

* From DU(5G-L2-PS) per SRS receive, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_13873\_replaced([11156413](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ks0LfPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

- Start pattern ID, Buffer identifier, number of BWVs R

- [CB008322-A] BF subband index dedicate for UE specific CSI-RS beam BWV calculation, if CSI beam is not requested, invalid value shall be indicated [End CB008322-A]

-     [CB008322-E]Broken antenna Bit mask from L2-PS, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_22217\_replaced([11156533](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ks1ZlfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) [End CB008322-E]

* From DU(L1),

- Raw channel estimation Hs, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_14126\_replaced([11107396](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koeeD_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Algorithm overview:

* SRS port validation/downgrade, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_13935\_replaced([11107463](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kofsJfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
* BWVs are computed for each BF sub-band by MMSE (5G\_UP\_ALG\_13931\_replaced([11107453](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kofsIvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) or FEBF (5G\_UP\_ALG\_14913\_replaced([11107492](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kofsMPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))
* [CB008322-A] BWVs for CSI-RS beam are computed with MMSE algorithm refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_13931\_replaced([11107453](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kofsIvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). [End CB008322-A]

Output:

* To DU(5G-L2-PS), see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_14363\_replaced([11107520](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kogTOvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

-  The BWV calculation result

-   SRS quality for each SRS port

* To RU, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_13927\_replaced([11107557](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kog6RPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
* Ws  are BWVs for each BF subband

-  Ws  is BWVs with the size of NTRX x R,

* s=[0, ..., Nsubband - 1] is beamforming sub-band index.

-  NTRX is the number of TRX of both polarizations on gNB

-  R is the number of beams selected, R ∈ {0, 1, 2, 3, 4}

-  [CB009496-B] In case of given TRX is indicated as broken by 5G-L2-PS then DU(L1) shall set value of 0.003 for relevant BWV at related position(s) of broken antenna(s). [End CB009496-B]

* [CB008322-A] Ws\_csi is BWVs on a dedicated BF subband for wideband short-term CSI-RS beam

-  Ws\_csi is BWVs with size of NTRX x 4,

* s\_csi=[0, ..., Nsubband - 1] is beamforming sub-band index, which is picked by DU(5G-L2-PS)

-  NTRX is the number of TRX of both polarizations on gNB  [End CB008322-A]

-  [CB009496-B] In case of given TRX is indicated as broken by 5G-L2-PS then DU(L1) shall set value of 0.003 for relevant BWV at related position(s) of broken antenna(s). [End CB009496-B]

For the supported L1 HWs, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_21343\_replaced([11107360](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koeeBfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

MMSE Algorithm
The below figure show functions of short-term beam computation,
[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a block diagram illustrating a "Short-term beam computer (MMSE)". The diagram shows a process flow with several interconnected blocks.
Inputs to the system are:
- "SRS power" with a label "HPxNx".
- "Num beam K" and "Start Pattern ID" are provided from above.
The process flow consists of three main blocks within the "Short-term beam computer (MMSE)" boundary:
1. **SRS ports Select**: This block receives "SRS power" and "Num beam K" / "Start Pattern ID". It outputs "HRxNTRx".
2. **MMSE**: This block receives "HRxNTRx" from the previous block and outputs "WNTKXXR".
3. **BWV W normalization**: This block receives "WNTKXXR" from the MMSE block. It also has an input labeled "BWVS WNTRXX R" coming from below. This block outputs "SRS Power" and "EWW status".
The overall diagram is enclosed in a rounded rectangle. The background is a light gray color. The blocks are outlined in blue, and the text labels are in black. Arrows indicate the direction of data flow between the blocks and inputs/outputs.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_3432.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]
Below are the main steps of short-term BWV:
Step#1. Select R SRS ports from Hs according to SRS ports energy,  refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_13935\_replaced([11107463](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kofsJfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
Step#2. Calculate Ws per beamforming sub-band, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_13937\_replaced([11107475](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kofsKfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
Step#3. Normalize Ws refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_13938\_replaced([11107483](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kofsLfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), and then download to RU refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_13927\_replaced([11107557](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kog6RPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
 
File: Short and Long-term Beam Management.pptx

###### 2.4.5.3.1.1.1.0-2 MMSE Algorithm SRS ports validation and downgrading (ID: 11107463)

**2.4.5.3.1.1.1.0-2.0-3**  (ID: `11107472`)

MMSE Algorithm
DU needs to validate SRS quality and downgrade SRS ports ahead of BWV calculation,

Input

 *H*s*is SRS Raw CE* *from 5G\_UP\_ALG\_14126\_replaced([11107396](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koeeD_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))*\*\*\*\*

 number of BWVs requested by DU(5G-L2-PS), see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_14086\_replaced([11107441](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kofFHfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

 *NRCELL/srsForSubbandBm/srsPowerThreshold*given by Cell setup**[CB014094-A] or Cell Parameter Reconfiguration.** [End CB014094-A]**\***\*

 *NRCELL/srsForSubbandBm/srsSinrThreshold given by Cell setup* *[CB014094-A] or Cell Parameter Reconfiguration.* *[End CB014094-A].*\*\*\*\*

**Algorithm**

If *srsSinrThreshold = - 40,*

*case0:* rdRtBwvDowngradeMethod =0\*\*

- Remove ports if SRS power lower *srsPowerThreshold*

- Calculate R =min {number of valid SRS ports, the number of BWV per subband requested by L2-PS}

*case1* rdRtBwvDowngradeMethod =1\*\*

- R ports with highest Power are selected, R is the number of BWV per subband requested by L2-PS. If average power of the selected R SRS ports < srsPowerThreshold  then R=0;

*case2/3/4:* rdRtBwvDowngradeMethod =2/3/4\*\*

- *R ports with highest power are selected, R=* rdRtBwvDowngradeMethod. If average power of the selected R SRS ports *srsPowerThreshold*then R=0.\*\*\*\*

Elseif *srsSinrThreshold > - 40,*

*case0:* rdRtBwvDowngradeMethod =0\*\*

- For each SRS port if its power< *srsPowerThreshold Or SINR<* srsSinrThreshold, then corresponding SRS ports shall be droped.\*\*

- Calculate R =min {number of valid SRS ports, the number of BWV per subband requested by L2-PS}

*case1* rdRtBwvDowngradeMethod =1\*\*

- *R ports with highest SINR are selected, R is the number of BWV per subband requested by L2-PS. If average Power of the selected R SRS ports <* srsPowerThreshold Or average Sinr of the selected R SRS ports< *srsSinrThreshold then R=0;*\*\*

*case2/3/4:* rdRtBwvDowngradeMethod =2/3/4\*\*

- *R ports with highest SINR are selected, R= rdRtBwvDowngradeMethod. If average Power of the selected R SRS ports < srsPowerThreshold Or average Sinr of the selected R SRS ports< srsSinrThreshold then R= 0;*

If R=0;

Bypass BWVs calculation for PDSCH and UE specific CSI-RS and report failure to 5G-L2-PS, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_13937\_replaced([11107475](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kofsKfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Else

* BWVs *H*s*R with size of* R x N*TRX are fed to MMSE and* H*s*4 with size of *4 x N*TRX are fed to MMSE***\****\*\*\*

Endif

Output

Below data shall be fed to MMSE:

 The number of BWV R of one subband

 SRS Raw CE ***H*s\*4  for UE specific CSI-RS beam calculation**\*\*\*

 SRS Raw CE *H*s*R for PDSCH beam calculation*\*\*

 calculation result, {failure, OK}

For the supported L1 HWs, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_21343\_replaced([11107360](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koeeBfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

###### 2.4.5.3.1.1.1.0-3 MMSE Algorithm MMSE Calculation (ID: 11107475)

**2.4.5.3.1.1.1.0-3.0-2**  (ID: `11107480`)

MMSE Algorithm
The BWVs calculation (Step# 2) by MMSE is performed for each UE per SRS period. This requirement would be skipped if SRS channel quality isn’t eligible see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_13935\_replaced([11107463](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kofsJfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

For each beamforming sub-band s, channel estimate HsR shall be fed for MMSE, acquires BWVs WsR, it would be applied for PDSCH decoding.

[CB008322-A] If BF subband s\_csi  given by L2-PS is valid, channel estimate Hs4 shall be fed for MMSE, acquires BWVs Ws\_csi., it would be applied for 4 ports UE specific CSI-RS beams. [End CB008322-A]

A noise term is introduced for MMSE by choosing from R&D parameter rdThermNoisFlag, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_19841\_replaced([11110389](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpDs2_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

If rdThermNoisFlag =0 then

SRS power trace factor by rdMmsePowerFactor = {0.0001....0.1} (5G\_UP\_ALG\_19840\_replaced([11110396](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpDs3vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is applied, see MMSE formula option 0

elseif rdThermNoisFlag =1 then

thermal noise is used as MMSE regularization term, see MMSE formula option1

[CB008322-G]Elseif rdThermNoisFlag=2 then

a factor given by L2-PS is used for MMSE regularization term, see MMSE formula option2 [End CB008322-G]

Endif

The more detail of MMSE algorithm and [CB008322-G] MMSE formula options {option0,option1,option2}[End CB008322-G] please refer to 5GMAX Massive MIMO Algorithm specification §5.1.2.3.

For the supported L1 HWs, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_21343\_replaced([11107360](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koeeBfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

###### 2.4.5.3.1.1.1.0-4 MMSE Algorithm BWV normalization (ID: 11107483)

**2.4.5.3.1.1.1.0-4.0-2**  (ID: `11107489`)

MMSE Algorithm
DU(L1) shall normalize Ws  (Step# 3) according R&D parameter rdMmseNorm, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_19843\_replaced([11110379](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpDs1_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

if rdMmseNorm =0, Ws  is normalized per layer-wise and per TRX-wise.

If rdMmseNorm =1, Ws  is normalize per TRX-wise only.

[CB008322-A] Both WsR and Ws\_csi need to be normalized [End CB008322-A]

Please refer to the algorithm in the subsection 5.1.2.2.2 of 5Gmax Massive MIMO Algorithm specification.

The normalization per TRX-wise to secure each TRX doesn't exceed maximum transmit power.

For the supported L1 HWs, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_21343\_replaced([11107360](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koeeBfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

###### 2.4.5.3.1.1.2 FEBF Algorithm

###### 2.4.5.3.1.1.2-1 FEBF Algorithm

###### 2.4.5.3.1.1.2-1.0-2 FEBF Algorithm DU(L1) shall report below metrics to DU(5G-L2-PS) within message BWVs response, Number of valid SRS ports R see 5G_UP_ALG_13935_replaced( 11107463 ) step #1-2 on 5G_UP_ALG_13935_replaced( 11107463 ) for MMSE Number of Beamforming subband see 5G_UP_ALG_13924_replaced( 11107540 ) BWVs computation result (is failure or success). [Before CB008322-A] SRS power per port. [CB008322-A] SRS quality per port, see 5G_UP_ALG_14919_replaced( 11107414 ) [End CB008322-A] [Before CB008322-A] SRS time offset averaged all ports [End CB008322-A] For the supported L1 HWs, refer to 5G_UP_ALG_21343_replaced( 11107360 ). (ID: 11107526)

[Before CNI-99771]The below figure show functions of short-term beam computation on FEBF, it is performed per beamforming sub-band for each UE per SRS period.
[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a flowchart illustrating the "Short-term beam computer (FEBF)". The flowchart depicts a process with three main stages enclosed within a larger box.
Input to the system includes:
- "Num beam K = R" and "Start Pattern ID" from an arrow pointing downwards.
- "H P srs x N trx" from an arrow pointing from the left.
The three stages are:
1. **Eigen-vector calculation**: This is the first stage, receiving "H P srs x N trx" as input. It outputs "W N trx x R" to the next stage.
2. **Power balance**: This is the second stage, receiving "W N trx x R" as input. It outputs "W N trx x R" to the next stage.
3. **BWV W normalization**: This is the third stage, receiving "W N trx x R" as input. It outputs "BWV status" to the right and "BWVs W N trx x R" downwards.
The overall flowchart is contained within a larger rounded rectangle labeled "Short-term beam computer (FEBF)".[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_3445.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]
For each beamforming subband s, the corresponding channel estimate HsR shall be fed for FEBF algorithm according to 5GMAX Massive MIMO Algorithm specification §5.1.2.1. Below are the main steps of FEBF for short-term BWV:
 
Step#1. Calculate Eigen Vector based on raw SRS channel estimation Hs
Step#2. Perform Power Balance selected between max-min and mcs-specific algorithm
Step#3. Normalize Ws per TRX-wise, refer to subsection 5.1.2.1.3 of 5Gmax Massive MIMO Algorithm specification.
 
File: CB009459 Short-term Beam Computer.pptx
Folder:
[CNI-99771] Note: this requirement is not supported from E2E perspective. But, SW codes for this requirement still need to be inherited with deactivation.[End CNI-99771]

---

###### 2.4.5.3.1.1.2.0-2 FEBF Algorithm SRS ports validation (ID: 11107503)

**2.4.5.3.1.1.2.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11107506`)

FEBF Algorithm
[Before CNI-99771] If averaged power of all SRS ports is lower than the threshold, then FEBF computer (Step #1,#2,#3) shall be bypass, and report failure (number of beam R=0) to L2-PS.

else (the averaged power of all SRS ports is lower than the threshold)

The BWVs HsR with the size of R x NTRX should be fed to FEBF.

end

[CNI-99771] Note: this requirement is not supported from E2E perspective. But, SW codes for this requirement still need to be inherited with deactivation.[End CNI-99771]

---

###### 2.4.5.3.1.1.2.0-3 FEBF Algorithm Eigen Vector calculation (ID: 11107509)

**2.4.5.3.1.1.2.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11107511`)

FEBF Algorithm
[Before CNI-99771]There are two approaches for eigen vector calculation,

1. Singular Value Decomposition (SVD) is perform if rdEigenVectorMethod (5G\_UP\_ALG\_15230\_replaced([11110369](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpDs0_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is equal to "SVD". Please refer to subsection 5.1.2.1.1.1 of 5Gmax Massive MIMO Algorithm specification.

2. Eigen Value Decomposition (EVD) is perform if rdEigenVectorMethod is equal to "EVD". Please refer to subsection 5.1.2.1.1.2 of 5Gmax Massive MIMO Algorithm specification.

Among it the Power Iteration Method shall be applied, see description of 4.3.1 of 5Gmax Massive MIMO Algorithm specification. The initial vectors for power iteration method are selected by initVectorPiEvd.

After eigen vector calculation, L1 picks R (R=k) BWVs according to the rank K input from L2-PS.

If no SRS PRB is allocated on last beamforming subband, then Ws for second last subband shall be reused.

[CNI-99771] Note: this requirement is not supported from E2E perspective. But, SW codes for this requirement still need to be inherited with deactivation.[End CNI-99771]

---

###### 2.4.5.3.1.1.2.0-4 FEBF Algorithm Rx Power Balancing (ID: 11107514)

**2.4.5.3.1.1.2.0-4.0-1**  (ID: `11107517`)

FEBF Algorithm
[Before CNI-99771] Power balance is performed as below options,

a). Power adjustment factor will be 1 if rdPowerBalanceMethod is set to deactivate

b). Perform max-min algorithm if rdPowerBalanceMethod (5G\_UP\_ALG\_14934\_replaced([11110340](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpDFyPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is equal to "max-min".

* If SINR difference value is not invalid value (50dB) in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_15631\_replaced([11156565](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ks1Zm_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), power adjustment factor shall be calculated according to subsection 5.1.2.1.2.1 of 5Gmax Massive MIMO Algorithm specification respectively
* if SINR difference is invalid value, power adjustment factor will 1,

c).Perform MCS-specific algorithm if rdPowerBalanceMethod (5G\_UP\_ALG\_14934\_replaced([11110340](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpDFyPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is equal to "code rate”.

* If SINR difference value is not invalid value (50dB) in 5G\_ALG\_15631 according to subsection 5.1.2.1.2.2 of 5Gmax Massive MIMO Algorithm specification
* If SINR difference is invalid value, power adjustment factor will be 1

[CNI-99771] Note: this requirement is not supported from E2E perspective. But, SW codes for this requirement still need to be inherited with deactivation.[End CNI-99771]

---

#### 2.4.5.3.2 Receiver algorithm - Radio

##### 2.4.5.3.2.0-1 Receiver algorithm - Radio Supported short term Beamforming algorithm (ID: 11107567)

**2.4.5.3.2.0-1.0-5**  (ID: `13471626`)

Receiver algorithm - Radio
Radio shall support the below algorithms for SRS short-term BM in TDD FR1 eCPRI 7-2e:

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| **Algorithm** | **Thor (Radio 7-2e)** |
| Algorithm overview | see [11107579](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kog6TfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) |
| Inputs/Outputs/Algorithm | see [11107602](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kohhVPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) |
| Channel estimation | see [11107613](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kohhWPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) |
| SRS quality estimation | see [11107630](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kohhXfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) |
| Channel estimation correlation | see [11107622](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kohhW_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) |
| Number of beamforming subbands | see [11107635](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kohhX_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) |
| BF subband interference detection | see [11107641](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kohhYfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) |
| Angular Power Spectrum (APS) for user correlation ~~algorithm~~ | see [11107660](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kohhY_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) |
| [CB014315-B] APS metric [End CB014315-B] | see [13480974](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_j5kkED4rEfG0jPRAdLhzvA) |
| Singular value calculation | see [12580757](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_W-434TIBEfCfjdcwDn2YOQ) |
| **First Feature** | CB009527-A |

##### 2.4.5.3.2.0-2 Receiver algorithm - Radio Algorithm Overview (ID: 11107579)

**2.4.5.3.2.0-2.0-5**  (ID: `13485801`)

Receiver algorithm - Radio
For TDD FR1, the dashed orange box in below figure indicates Radio functional blocks to support SRS based Short-term BF in eCPRI 7-2e (see [11354249](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucGPDvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) and [11380288](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ueliXPvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[Before CB014315-B]

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a block diagram illustrating the architecture and data flow of a 5G radio system, specifically focusing on the interaction between the Radio Unit (RU), the Distributed Unit (DU), and the Centralized Unit (CU) which is not explicitly shown but implied by the L2 DU block. The diagram is divided into several sections: "Radio", "FH" (likely Fiber Home or Front Haul), and "DU".
Within the "Radio" section, there are two sub-sections: "5G-L1-DL (Radio)" for downlink and "5G-L1-UL (Radio)" for uplink.
The "5G-L1-DL (Radio)" section handles:
- **ST BF (Single-User Beamforming)**:
- **CSI-RS Beamforming**: Receives "data" and outputs "W" (Weight vector).
- **PDSCH Beamforming**: Receives "data" and outputs "W".
- **BWV Calculation**: Receives "W" from CSI-RS and PDSCH Beamforming, and outputs to EIRP Coefficient Calculation.
- **EIRP Coefficient Calculation**: Receives "W" from BWV Calculation and outputs to the "FH" section.
The "5G-L1-UL (Radio)" section handles:
- **SRS reception per TRX**: Receives signals from a UE and outputs "RAW SRS (MxNx2)".
- **LT BF (Long-Term Beamforming)**:
- **SRS channel estimation**: Receives "RAW SRS" and outputs "H" (Channel matrix).
- **Calculation of H and V Gramian matrices**: Receives "H" and outputs to the "FH" section.
- **UL SU MIMO (Uplink Single-User MIMO)**:
- **SRS Beamforming**: Receives "Beam set" and outputs "SRS".
- **SRS Channel estimation**: Receives "SRS" and outputs "H".
- **PMI, RI calculation**: Receives "H" and outputs "PMI, RI" to the "FH" section.
- **Store SRS CE per SB in memory for SB BF users**: Receives "H" from SRS channel estimation (likely related to a different path or context not fully detailed).
- **Calculate SRS power, SINR and decide DTX status**: Receives "H" and outputs to "BF subband interference detection".
- **Calculate user correlation and singular values**: Receives "H" and outputs to the "FH" section.
- **Calculate SRS channel correlation**: Receives "H" and outputs to the "FH" section.
- **BF subband interference detection**: Receives output from "Calculate SRS power, SINR and decide DTX status" and outputs to the "FH" section.
The "FH" section acts as an interface between the Radio and DU. It contains blocks for:
- **5G-L1-DL (BB)**:
- **CSI-RS scheduling request from L2**: Receives input from L2.
- **PDSCH scheduling and UE pairing request from L2**: Receives input from L2.
- **PDSCH scheduling response to L2**: Outputs to L2.
- **5G-L1-UL (BB)**:
- **Proxy to pass the Measurement and Covariance matrix result to L2**: Receives "H", "Cov" (Covariance), and other results from the Radio section and passes them to L2.
The "DU" section is further divided into "5G-L1-DL (BB)", "L2 (DU)", and "5G-L1-UL (BB)".
The "5G-L1-DL (BB)" section in the DU receives:
- **CSI-RS scheduling request from L2**.
- **PDSCH scheduling and UE pairing request from L2**.
- **PDSCH scheduling response to L2** is sent to the "FH" section.
The "L2 (DU)" section contains:
- **DL scheduler**: Receives scheduling requests from L2 and outputs to the "5G-L1-DL (BB)" section.
- **DL MU-MIMO Pairing**: Receives input from "Long-term correlation table for DL MU-MIMO".
- **Long-term correlation table for DL MU-MIMO**: Receives input from the "5G-L1-UL (BB)" section.
- **BF Method determination**: Receives "ST BF or LT BF" and outputs to the "5G-L1-DL (BB)" section.
- **Beam determination**: Receives "Cov" from the "FH" section and "Beam set" as input, outputs "Best beam(s)".
- **Rank and PMI determination**: Receives "Rank" and "PMI" as input.
The "5G-L1-UL (BB)" section in the DU receives:
- **Proxy to pass the Measurement and Covariance matrix result to L2** from the "FH" section.
- Outputs to "Long-term correlation table for DL MU-MIMO" and "BF Method determination".
The diagram also shows a UE transmitting "TAS SRS or Codebook SRS".
Arrows indicate the direction of data flow, with labels like "data", "W", "H", "Cov", "SRS", "Beam set", "PMI", "RI", "Rank", "ST BF or LT BF", "Best beam(s)". The overall diagram depicts a complex system for managing downlink and uplink communications, including beamforming, scheduling, channel estimation, and user pairing in a 5G network.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/img-12251451.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

[End CB014315-B]

[CB014315-B]

[image-1][image\_desc]The image is a block diagram illustrating the functionalities of a 5G base station, specifically focusing on the radio and baseband processing units for both downlink (DL) and uplink (UL) communications. The diagram is divided into several sections: "Radio", "FH" (Fronthaul), "5G-L1-DL (BB)" (Downlink Layer 1 Baseband), "L2 (DU)" (Layer 2 Distributed Unit), and "5G-L1-UL (BB)" (Uplink Layer 1 Baseband).
The "Radio" section is further divided into "5G-L1-DL (Radio)" and "5G-11-UL (Radio)".
In the "5G-L1-DL (Radio)" section, there are two main processes: "CSI-RS Beamforming" and "PDSCH Beamforming". Both receive "data" and output "W" to "BWV Calculation" blocks. There are two "BWV Calculation" blocks, each receiving input from one of the beamforming blocks. These calculations also involve "H" and "W" inputs and outputs, leading to an "EIRP Coefficient Calculation" block.
The "5G-11-UL (Radio)" section deals with uplink signals. It starts with "SRS reception per TRX" which receives "RAW SRS (MxNx2)" and feeds into "LT BF" (Long Term Beamforming) and "UL SU MIMO" (Uplink Single User MIMO) paths.
The "LT BF" path involves "SRS channel estimation" and then "Calculation of H and V Gramian matrices".
The "UL SU MIMO" path involves "SRS Beamforming", "SRS Channel estimation", and "PMI, RI calculation".
Both "5G-11-UL (Radio)" paths also involve "SRS channel estimation", "Calculate SRS power, SINR and decide DTX status", and "BF subband interference detection". The "Store SRS CE per SB in memory for SB BF users" block receives input from the "Calculate SRS power, SINR and decide DTX status" block and also receives "H" input.
The "FH" (Fronthaul) section acts as an interface between the Radio and Baseband units. It shows requests and responses related to scheduling and beamforming. Specifically, it handles "CSI-RS scheduling request from L2", "PDSCH scheduling and UE pairing request from L2", and "PDSCH scheduling response to L2".
The "5G-L1-DL (BB)" section receives scheduling requests from L2 and interacts with the "DL scheduler" in the "L2 (DU)" section. It also outputs information to the "Long-term correlation table for DL MU-MIMO" and "BF Method determination" blocks.
The "L2 (DU)" section contains the "DL scheduler", which receives scheduling requests from L2. It also has blocks for "DL MU-MIMO Pairing", "BF Method determination" (which outputs "ST BF or LT BF"), and "Beam determination" (which receives "Cov" and outputs "Best beam(s)" based on a "Beam set" input).
The "5G-L1-UL (BB)" section is responsible for processing uplink signals. It includes a "Proxy to pass the Measurement and Covariance matrix result to L2" block. It also interacts with the "Rank and PMI determination" block, which receives "Rank" and "PMI" as outputs.
The diagram also shows a UE (User Equipment) transmitting "TAS SRS or Codebook SRS".
The arrows indicate the flow of data and control signals between the different blocks. Labels on the arrows, such as "W", "H", "Cov", "SRS", "data", "Rank", "PMI", "Beam set", "ST BF or LT BF", and "Best beam(s)", specify the type of information being exchanged.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/img-04281734.png[/image\_path][/image-1]

[End CB014315-B]

The main functions are listed below:

* Radio shall receive 1-port or 2-port raw SRS data streams. Refer to [11107696](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koiIYvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) for supported configuration.
* Radio shall perform PRB-wise channel estimation, refer to [11107613](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kohhWPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ). Note, Radio still not support TOE and TOC during SRS channel estimation.
* Radio shall calculate SRS power, SINR and decide DTX status, and store SRS power together with SRS CE per SB in memory for SB BF users, refer to [11107622](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kohhW_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).
* Radio in downlink shall perform the BWV calculation with the stored SRS CE when PDSCH and/or CSI-RS scheduled, refer to [11099691](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knGlAvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)**and [11100814](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knRkIvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ). And then Radio in downlink shall apply the BWV to the PDSCH and/or CSI-RS data.**
* Radio in downlink shall perform EIRP expansion coefficient calculation if requested, refer to [11099828](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knHzJ_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).
* Radio shall calculate SRS channel correlation in time domain, refer to [11107630](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kohhXfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).
* Radio shall calculate BF subband interference detection, refer to [11107641](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kohhYfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).
* Radio shall calculate the user correlation [CB014315-B] or APS metric [End CB014315-B] with APS algorithm, refer to [11107660](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kohhY_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) [CB014315-B] and [13480974](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_j5kkED4rEfG0jPRAdLhzvA) [End CB014315-B].
* Radio shall calculate singular values for selected users, refer to [12580757](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_W-434TIBEfCfjdcwDn2YOQ).
* Radio shall calculate H-V CoMa if requested, see [11106868](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kocBwvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).
* Radio shall calculate UL SU-MIMO metrics (TPMI/RI/SINR) if requested, see [11106441](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koYXafvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).
* Radio shall report measurements to DU, see [11107622](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kohhW_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ), [11107630](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kohhXfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) and [11107641](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kohhYfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

File: Short and Long-term Beam Management.pptx

Folder: <https://nokia.sharepoint.com/:p:/r/sites/5Gdoc/AS_Tech_Spec/Drawings%20and%20diagrams%20for%205G/User%20Plane/5G%20UP%20Algorithms/2%20L1%20Algorithms%20and%20Configurations/UL%20-%20SRS%20Reception/Short%20and%20Long-term%20Beam%20Management.pptx?d=wa7f0db9326d54ccbb7c754d6521431fa&csf=1&web=1&e=PUzwc4>

##### 2.4.5.3.2.0-3 Receiver algorithm - Radio Inputs/Outputs/Algorithm (ID: 11107602)

**2.4.5.3.2.0-3.0-5**  (ID: `13486155`)

Receiver algorithm - Radio
Here are the inputs/output/algorithm for SRS short-term BF per UE per SRS period.

**Inputs:**

 For each Cell during cell setup phase from DU (see [11094702](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kme6AfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

- Beamforming Sub-band size, SRS quality threshold, see [11094605](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmeS5vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)**.**

 For each UE and each SRS resource measurement,

From antenna:

- Raw SRS signal per SRS symbol.

From DU:

- Buffer identifier, SRS resource info, see [11156418](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ks0LffvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)

- H-V CoMa calculation flag, its start PRB and number of PRB, it is used for long-term BM, see [11106868](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kocBwvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)

- UL SU-MIMO metrics calculation request, its start PRB and number of PRB, see [11106441](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koYXafvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)

- A list of correlation users for which the correlation with the given user shall be calculated, and a selected SRS sub-band index for each correlation user, see [11143715](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr6zlfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

From Radio:

- Number of TRX NTRX ∈ {64, 32}.  NTRX is equal to M*N*P, where M=number of TRX rows, N = number of TRX columns and P = 2 is number of TRX Polarizations.

**Algorithm:**

 Channel estimation, refer to [11107613](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kohhWPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)

* SRS quality Estimation, refer to [11107622](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kohhW_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)
* SRS channel correlation in time domain, refer to [11107630](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kohhXfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)
* User correlation [CB014315-B] or APS metric [End CB014315-B] **calculation with APS algorithm, refer to [11107660](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kohhY_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) [CB014315-B] or [13480974](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_j5kkED4rEfG0jPRAdLhzvA). Note, the user correlation and APS metric calculation will not happen together, DU shall request either user correction or APS metric. [End CB014315-B]**
* User singular values calculation, refer to [12580757](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_W-434TIBEfCfjdcwDn2YOQ)
* H-V CoMa computation if requested, refer to [11106868](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kocBwvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)
* UL SU-MIMO metrics (TPMI/RI/SINR) calculation if requested, refer to [11106441](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koYXafvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)

**Output:**

* Radio shall report below measurement to DU per UE per SRS period:

- SRS quality for each port, refer to [11107622](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kohhW_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)

- SRS channel correlation result, refer to [11107630](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kohhXfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)

* H-V CoMa

-     UL SU-MIMO metrics (TPMI/RI/SINR)

* Radio shall store "SRS CE per BF SB per TRX and SRS power" per port per UE in memory according to buffer identifier for SB BF users.
* Radio shall report the user correlation [CB014315-B] or APS metric [End CB014315-B] results of each user pairs, refer to [11107660](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kohhY_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) [CB014315-B] or [13480974](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_j5kkED4rEfG0jPRAdLhzvA) [End CB014315-B].
* Radio shall report the selected user's singular values, refer to [12580757](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_W-434TIBEfCfjdcwDn2YOQ).

For the supported L1 HWs, refer to [11107567](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kog6SfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

---

##### 2.4.5.3.2.0-4 Receiver algorithm - Radio Channel estimation (ID: 11107613)

**2.4.5.3.2.0-4.0-2**  (ID: `11107619`)

Receiver algorithm - Radio
For TDD FR1 eCPRI 7-2e, Radio shall perform channel estimation according to Massive MIMO Algorithm Specification Chapter 4.1.1. This functionality is per port per UE (per comb per cyclic shift) per SRS symbol.

**Input:**

• SRS IQ data received from antenna

• Buffer identifier, SRS resource info from DU, SRS CE operation indicator from DU, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_13886\_replaced([11156418](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ks0LffvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

• **[Before CB009527-E-UPD001] [CB009527-E] Beam generation for pre-combination from DU, see 5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_4843\_replaced([11294532](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TqEQkfvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23308\_replaced([11094702](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kme6AfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). [**End CB009527-E] **[End CB009527-E-UPD001]**\*\*\*\*

**Algorithm:**

• Radio shall perform raw channel estimation for all SRS PRBs according to Massive MIMO Algorithm Specification Chapter §4.1.1.1.

 **[Before CB009527-E-UPD001] [CB009527-E] Radio shall support time offset estimation (TOE) and time offset compensation (TOC) according to Massive MIMO Algorithm Specification §4.1.1.2 and §4.1.1.3. Radio shall derive the sector beam weight *w via Kronecker product for the pre-combination weight vectors of vertical direction and horizontal direction from the beam generation for pre-combination. [**End CB009527-E]** [End CB009527-E-UPD001]***\*\*\*\*

• Radio shall perform PRB downsampling: middle PRB per BF subband is selected.

• Radio shall apply “PRBs partition” into PRB groups. Each PRB group (or RBG) is centred around a selected/given SRS PRB. One PRB group consists of **24 consecutive SRS RE for comb2 and comb4, according to 5GMAX Massive MIMO Algorithm specification §4.1.1.4. Note that, PRB groups might be partially overlapped.**

• Radio shall perform SRS PRBs pilot separation with window function length of 24 for comb2 and comb4 **according to 5GMAX Massive MIMO Algorithm specification §4.1.1.5.** Then it shall get channel estimation per BF subband**.**\*\*\*\*

• Radio shall handle the BF subband CE at starting PRB(s) or ending PRB(s) which are not covered by SRS subband, according to SRS CE operation indicator, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_13879\_replaced([11156605](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ks2Ao_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

• **[CB009527-B] If BF subband interference detection is enabled, Radio shall replace the CE of interfered BF subband with the CE of the closest non-interfered subband, according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26920\_replaced([11107641](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kohhYfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).**[End CB009527-B]\*\*\*\*

**Output:**

• Channel estimation per BF sub-band.

For the supported L1 HWs, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26944\_replaced([11107567](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kog6SfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Rationale: Average PRB solution is not used due to computation resource reasons. TOC is not used because only narrow size PRB (4 middle PRBs) is used in SRS channel estimation.

---

11107627:
Receiver algorithm - Radio
11107627:
Receiver algorithm - Radio
11107627:
Receiver algorithm - Radio
11107627:
Receiver algorithm - Radio
11107627:
Receiver algorithm - Radio
Radio shall **estimate SRS power and Sinr as below,**

**Input**

• srsPowerThreshold and srsSinrThreshold from DU, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_13821\_replaced([11094605](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmeS5vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23308\_replaced([11094702](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kme6AfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

• SRS CE operation indicator from DU, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_13879\_replaced([11156605](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ks2Ao_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

• Raw SRS channel estimation, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26688\_replaced([11107613](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kohhWPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

**Algorithm**

• Radio shall **estimate SRS port power *P*p for MMSE by gathering channel estimate for SRS PRBs according to Massive MIMO Algorithm Specification §5.1.2.1; Then average power per SRS subcarriers and per TRX is calculated as  [image-0][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is:
$\overline{P\_p} = P\_p \* K\_{cc} / (12 \* M \* N\_{TRX})$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_3483.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0] where* M is number of PRB for SRS.***\*\*

•  If srsSinrThreshold = -40dB, Radio shall use SRS Power for DTX status decision.

-  If SRS power per port is less than the srsPowerThreshold, the related port shall be marked as DTX.

-  Radio needs to report SRS port power (dB) to DU.

•  If srsSinrThreshold > -40dB, Radio shall estimate the SRS simplified Sinr according to Massive MIMO Algorithm Specification §4.1.1.6.

-  Radio shall use SRS Power and Sinr jointly for DTX status decision. If either SRS power or SINR per port is less than the threshold pre-defined in srsPowerThreshold or srsSinrThreshold, the related port shall be marked as DTX.

-  Radio needs to report SRS port power (dB) and Sinr (dB) to DU.

Note, Simplified Sinr estimation is designed to identify significant interference to part of bandwidth and only for the purpose of DL subband BF fallback. It means absolute estimated Sinr values between different SRS configuration (for example: different SRS bandwidth or SRS comb) may be varies due to algorithm simplification.

**Output**

• Radio shall report SRS Power for each SRS port.

• If srsSinrThreshold > -40dB, Radio shall report SRS SINR for each SRS port.

• Radio shall report DTX status for each SRS port.

• Radio shall store SRS CE per SB in memory for SB BF users and store SRS Power per port in memory. Note, The 1st SRS CE and power result shall be always stored without check the SRS CE operation indicator value, for all later SRS CE and power results shall be stored according to the SRS CE operation indicator.

For the supported L1 HWs, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26944\_replaced([11107567](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kog6SfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

##### 2.4.5.3.2.0-6 Receiver algorithm - Radio Channel Estimation Correlation (ID: 11107630)

**2.4.5.3.2.0-6.0-1**  (ID: `11107633`)

Receiver algorithm - Radio
Radio shall calculate SRS CE correlations for the UE configured short-term BM, based on the same single PRB between two latest SRS periods, and report the results to DU. This metric is for monitoring the UE speed, UE with high speed would show lower correlation.

Input:

• NSRS is the number of PRBs of SRS subband, 5G\_UP\_ALG\_13942\_replaced([11107726](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koiIZvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

• PSRS is the start PRB of SRS subband, 5G\_UP\_ALG\_13942\_replaced([11107726](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koiIZvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

• Ssubband is the beamforming subband size, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_13821\_replaced([11094605](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmeS5vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

• Nsubband is the number of beamforming subbands, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_27598\_replaced([11107635](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kohhX_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

• Raw SRS channel estimation, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26688\_replaced([11107613](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kohhWPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

•  SRS CE operation indicator from DU, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_13879\_replaced([11156605](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ks2Ao_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Algorithm:

• Middle beamforming subband index: m =floor((PSRS+ Nsrs/2) / Ssubband ), where m =0..Nsubband -1.

• Radio shall store two latest channel estimation results Hmn-1, Hmn  of the mid PRB in middle beamforming subband, which located in same SRS subband of this UE, n is current SRS period index, n-1 is the previous SRS period index which has same mid PRB in same SRS subband with the current SRS period.

• Radio shall check the SRS CE operation indicator, if correlation calculation is requested, then Radio shall calculate the correlation for each SRS port via Hmn-1 and Hmn . Refer to Massive MIMO Algorithm Specification §5.1.2.3.2 for more detail. Else if correlation calculation is not requested,  If Hmn-1 is not available in the 1st SRS period, the correlation result shall be set to invalid value.

Output:

• Radio shall report correlation per SRS port to DU, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_21817\_replaced([11157124](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ks5rDvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for correlations averaging in DU.

For the supported L1 HWs, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26944\_replaced([11107567](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kog6SfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

##### 2.4.5.3.2.0-7 Receiver algorithm - Radio Number of beamforming subbands (ID: 11107635)

**2.4.5.3.2.0-7.0-2**  (ID: `12784958`)

Receiver algorithm - Radio
Below is input/output/algorithm for the number of beamforming subbands calculation.

Input:

• NBW is the number of PRBs of cell bandwidth, 5G\_UP\_ALG\_18\_replaced([11094261](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmaoifvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

• Ssubband is the beamforming subband size, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_13821\_replaced([11094605](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmeS5vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

• [CB009527-B] NSRS is the number of PRBs of SRS subband, 5G\_UP\_ALG\_13942\_replaced([11107726](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koiIZvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) [End CB009527-B]

Algorithm:

• Nsubband is the total number of beamforming subbands per cell bandwidth. It is given by Nsubband = Ceiling (NBW/Ssubband), the beamforming subband index range is from 0 to Nsubband -1. Note that the last beamforming subband size is NBW - (Nsubband -1)\*Ssubband PRBs which might be smaller than Ssubband .

• [CB009527-B] Msubband is the number of beamforming subbands per SRS subband. It is given by Msubband = NSRS/Ssubband . Note, the SRS subband NSRS is designed to be integer multiple of beamforming subbands Ssubband . [End CB009527-B]

Output:

• The the total number of beamforming subbands per cell bandwidth Nsubband.

• [CB009527-B] The the number of beamforming subbands per SRS subband Msubband. [End CB009527-B]

• The beamforming subband index, which always start index from 0 to Nsubband -1.

For the supported L1 HWs, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26944\_replaced([11107567](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kog6SfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

Radio shall **detect the interference for each beamforming subband based on SRS IQ power as below.**
Radio shall enable this function [Before PR826524] via R&D parameter *rdSubbandInterferDetFlag defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_24395\_replaced([11110519](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpFiBPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).*[End PR826524] [PR826524] if the R&D parameter *rdSubbandInterferDetType !=0 (Disabled), see [12527406: rdSubbandInterferDetType](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ElVeAhtVEfC3upM2pE5-4w?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9). [End PR826524]*Input:**SRS IQ data for each symbol and comb from Antenna, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26686\_replaced([11107579](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kog6TfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
*Slot is the system slot number, given by each SRS receive request from DU.M*subband is the number of beamforming subbands per SRS subband, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_27598\_replaced([11107635](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kohhX_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).*P*SRS is the start PRB of SRS subband, 5G\_UP\_ALG\_13942\_replaced([11107726](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koiIZvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).*S*subband is the beamforming subband size, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_13821\_replaced([11094605](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmeS5vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).**R&D parameter* rdIQSPikeTshForInterfer, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23233\_replaced([11110409](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpET4vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).*R&D parameter* rdIQSPikeTshForUninterfer, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23844\_replaced([11110420](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpET5vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).*R&D parameter* rdRatioTshOfInterferSb, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23234\_replaced([11110429](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpET6_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).*[PR813091] R&D parameter*rdSinrTshOfInterferSb*, see [12493591: rdSinrTshOfInterferSb](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_f6gAIBOFEfCph-OiSyk16Q?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9). [End PR813091]**[PR826524] R&D parameter* rdSubbandInterferDetType, see [12527406: rdSubbandInterferDetType](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ElVeAhtVEfC3upM2pE5-4w?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9). [End PR826524]*Algorithm:**Step* \*1: Radio shall select two pairs of normal TRX (both polarizations of each pair, four TRX in total)****• The first pair of TRX indices are {Slot mod NTrx/4, Slot mod NTrx/4+ NTrx/2}.
• The second pair of TRX indices are {(Slot +4} mod NTrx/4 + NTrx/4, (Slot +4} mod NTrx/4 + NTrx/4 + NTrx/2}.
• NTrx is total TRX number of both polarizations.**Step 2: Radio shall detect whether the SRS symbol is interfered or not for each UE per SRS port.**For each SRS subband per SRS port, Radio shall
• Estimate the SRS IQ [Before PR813091] linear [End PR813091] power* P*IQ,m [PR813091] in dBm [End PR813091] for each BF subband* m in the SRS subband *by averaging linearly power of the* N SRS subcarriers for all selected TRXs, where *N is the total number of SRS subcarriers of the BF subband* m, where *m=* (P*SRS*/S*subband*) .. *M*subband -1+*(P*SRS*/S*subband*).****\*******\*******\**
• Estimate the average SRS IQ [Before PR813091] linear [End PR813091] power [PR813091] in dBm [End PR813091] over all BF subbands [image-0][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical expression. The expression is written in a stylized font with a gradient effect, giving it a slightly blurred appearance. The expression consists of the letter "p" with a bar above it, indicating a conjugate or average. Below and to the right of the "p" is the subscript "IQ". The overall expression appears to be "$\bar{p}*{IQ}$".[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_3489.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]=[image-1][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical expression. The expression is:
$\sum*{m} P\_{IQ,m}/M\_{subband}$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_3489.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1].
• Estimate the [Before PR813091]*IQSpike\_m=*10*log10(P*IQ,m */ [image-2][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical expression. The expression is written in a stylized font and appears to be in a digital format. The expression consists of the letter "p" with a bar over it, followed by the subscript "10". The "p" is in a bold, sans-serif font, and the bar over it is a thin, horizontal line. The subscript "10" is smaller than the "p" and is positioned below and to the right of it. The colors in the image are muted and appear to be a gradient of brown, yellow, and blue, suggesting a digital rendering or a stylized photograph of the expression. The background is white, and the expression is centered within the frame.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_3489.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2])[End PR813091] [PR813091]*IQSpike\_m=P*IQ,m* - [image-3][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical expression. The expression is written in a stylized font and appears to be in a digital format. The expression consists of the letter "p" with a bar over it, followed by the subscript "10". The "p" is in a bold, sans-serif font, and the bar over it is a thin, horizontal line. The subscript "10" is smaller than the "p" and is positioned below and to the right of it. The colors in the image are muted and appear to be a gradient of brown, yellow, and blue, suggesting a digital rendering or a stylized photograph of the expression. The background is white, and the expression is centered within the frame.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_3489.003.png[/image\_path][/image-3][End PR813091] \*for each BF subband m.*****\***• Go through all BF subbands in the SRS subband, for any subband with* IQSpike\_m rdIQSPikeTshForInterfer[End PR813091] [PR813091]*(P*IQ,m*- AveIqSpikeOfNonInterfer)*> rdIQSPikeTshForInterfer[End PR813091], then corresponding BF subband m in this symbol shall be recognized as interfered.*****\**\***Step 3: Radio shall count the total number of interfered BF subbands for each UE per SRS port.**• Count the total number of interfered BF subbands *N*interf in the SRS subband for each UE per SRS port. [PR813091] If*N*interf = 0, Radio shall estimate the sinr of the SRS subband per SRS port, and derive the average sinr over the SRS ports of the UE in this SRS symbol, if the averaged sinr is less than* rdSinrTshOfInterferSb, then consider the whole SRS subband is interfered and change *N*interf = *M*subband  for the SRS ports of the UE in this SRS symbol, otherwise there's no interference detected and *N*interf  keep 0. [End PR813091] And then report the *N*interf to DU.******\*******• [PR826524] if the R&D parameter*rdSubbandInterferDetType ==2 (Fully Enabled), [End PR826524] During channel estimation (5G\_UP\_ALG\_26688\_replaced([11107613](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kohhWPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), Radio shall replace the CE of interfered BF subbands with the CE of the closest non-interfered subband, when there’s at least one non-interfered subband in the SRS subband.\***Step 4: [PR826524] if the R&D parameter *rdSubbandInterferDetType ==2 (Fully Enabled), [End PR826524] Radio shall determine whether the SRS subband per SRS port is high interfered.***Calculate ratio of interfered BF subbands in the SRS subband per SRS port, interfered ratio = *N*interf*/M\*subband.****If interfered ratio >=* rdRatioTshOfInterferSb, the corresponding SRS subband and port shall be reported to DU as high interfered.*Output:**-   The total number of interfered BF subbands in the SRS subband for each UE and SRS port* N*interf.**-   [PR826524] if the R&D parameter*rdSubbandInterferDetType ==2 (Fully Enabled), [End PR826524] The high interfered SRS subband status for each UE and SRS port.\*
 
For the supported L1 HWs, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26944\_replaced([11107567](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kog6SfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
