Receiver algorithm - Radio
12580921:
Receiver algorithm - Radio
**Purpose:**

Radio shall calculate the user correlation result based on Angular Power Spectrum (APS) algorithm.

**Input:**

From DU:

- A list of user pairs for which correlation shall be calculated, see [11143715](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr6zlfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ). For each current user i, there could be multiple correlation users j (j = 0, 1, …, maxCorrUsers\_i) for which the correlation with user i needs to be calculated. Note, Radio can support maximum 8 user correlation calculations per one SRS symbol ~~for frame structure 5GC001116 (see~~ ~~[11347007: TD-LTE aligned frames](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubJMwvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)~~~~) and Radio can support maximum 4 user correlation calculations per one SRS symbol for other frame structures~~.

- The selected SRS sub-band index for each correlation user j, see [11143715](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr6zlfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

- The srsPowerThreshold, see [11094702](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kme6AfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

From Radio:

- The SRS channel estimation per BF sub-band per port results of current user i and each correlation user j, see [11107613](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kohhWPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

- Number of TRX NTRX ∈ {64 [CB012971-E], 32 [End CB012971-E]}.  NTRX is equal to M*N*P, where M=number of TRX rows, N = number of TRX columns and P = 2 is number of TRX Polarizations.

- R&D parameter rdOverSampRatioAps, see [11110438](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpET7vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

**Algorithm:**

If the list of user pairs is not empty, e.g. the correlation to one or more other users j to the current users i has to be calculated, APS calculation is activated.

For the current user i and each correlation user j:

- Radio shall convert the SRS sub-band index to BF sub-band index (e.g. select the middle PRBs), and get the SRS channel estimation results of user i and user j with the selected BF sub-band index.

- **For each user: user i or user j:**

o Radio shall construct matrix [image-0][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical expression: $\Psi(l, p)\_{M \times N}$. The expression is written in black text on a white background. The symbol $\Psi$ is a Greek letter, commonly used in quantum mechanics and other fields of physics. The expression is followed by a subscript indicating a matrix of size M by N.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_3491.002.png[/image\_path][/image-0] for each valid SRS port *l and each polarization* p, based on the SRS channel estimation result of the one user. If the SRS power of a port is lower than srsPowerThreshold, the related SRS port is considered as not valid and shall be excluded from the calculations. Note, if the SRS power of all the ports is not valid, Radio shall mark the user i or user j as an invalid user and skip below APS calculation of this user.\*\*

o Radio shall perform zero padding (row & column wise) for [image-1][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical expression. The expression is written in black text on a white background. The expression is:
$\Psi(l, p)*{M \times N}$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_3491.003.png[/image\_path][/image-1], to obtain the [image-2][image\_desc]$\Psi^0(l,p)*{OM+ON}$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_3491.004.png[/image\_path][/image-2], where *OM = rdOverSampRatioAps**M and* ON = rdOverSampRatioAps**N.**\*\*

o Radio shall calculate the 2D FFT of [image-3][image\_desc]The image contains the mathematical expression $\Psi^0(l, p)*{OM \cdot ON}$. The expression is written in a dark color against a light background. The symbol $\Psi$ is a Greek letter, and it is superscripted with a zero. Following the symbol is a parenthesis containing the letters 'l' and 'p' separated by a comma. After the closing parenthesis, there are subscripts 'OM' and 'ON' separated by a dot.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_3491.005.png[/image\_path][/image-3], to obtain the 2D FFT matrix [image-4][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is:
$B(l, p)*{OM \times ON} = W\_{OM} \* \Psi^0(l, p)*{OM \times ON} \* W*{ON}^T$
The equation involves variables B, l, p, W, and $\Psi^0$. The subscripts OM and ON indicate dimensions or specific components. The asterisk (\*) likely represents a multiplication or convolution operation, and the superscript T on $W\_{ON}$ indicates a transpose.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_3491.006.png[/image\_path][/image-4],

where WOM is a matrix with size OM*OM, and the m-th row of the matrix is given by [image-5][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical expression enclosed in square brackets. The expression is a sequence of terms involving the exponential function with a complex argument.
The sequence starts with the number 1.
Following the 1, there are three terms of the form $e^{j \frac{2\pi m}{OM}}$.
The first term is $e^{j \frac{2\pi m}{OM}}$.
The second term is $e^{j \frac{2\pi m \* 2}{OM}}$.
The third term is $e^{j \frac{2\pi m \* (OM-1)}{OM}}$.
These terms are separated by spaces and ellipses (...), indicating a continuation of the pattern.
The overall expression appears to represent a discrete set of complex exponentials, possibly related to a Discrete Fourier Transform (DFT) or a similar signal processing concept. The variables used are 'j' (imaginary unit), 'm', 'OM', and numerical constants.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/img-05271647.png[/image\_path][/image-5],* m = 0, 1, … OM-1,\*

and WON is a matrix with size ON*ON, and the n-th row of the matrix is given by [image-6][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical expression enclosed in square brackets. The expression is a sequence of terms separated by spaces and ellipses. The terms are of the form $e^{j \frac{2\pi n k}{ON}}$, where $k$ takes values from 1 to $ON-1$. Specifically, the sequence starts with 1, followed by $e^{j \frac{2\pi n}{ON}}$, then $e^{j \frac{2\pi n \cdot 2}{ON}}$, and continues with ellipses, ending with $e^{j \frac{2\pi n \cdot (ON-1)}{ON}}$. The expression represents a discrete Fourier transform or a related mathematical concept.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/img-05271917.png[/image\_path][/image-6],* n = 0, 1, … ON-1.\*

o Radio shall compute the element-wise squared-magnitude of all elements, to obtain the APS per SRS port per polarization: [image-7][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in white text against a white background. The equation is:
$A(l, p)\_{OM \cdot ON} = B^*(l, p)*{OM \cdot ON} \circ B(l, p)*{OM \cdot ON}$
The equation involves variables $A$, $B$, $l$, and $p$. The subscript $OM \cdot ON$ appears on both sides of the equation. The symbol $B^*$ indicates the conjugate transpose of $B$, and the symbol $\circ$ likely represents a Hadamard product or element-wise multiplication.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_3491.009.png[/image\_path][/image-7].

o Radio shall average the APSs over all the valid SRS ports and all the TRX polarizations: [image-8][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical formula. The formula is:
$A\_{OM \cdot ON} = \frac{1}{P \cdot L} \sum\_{p=1}^{P} \sum\_{l=1}^{L} A(l, p)\_{OM \cdot ON}$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_3491.010.png[/image\_path][/image-8], where L is the number of valid SRS ports.

- **For each pair of users: user i and user j:**

o If either user i or user j in this pair was marked as invalid user, Radio shall report an invalid correlation value (e.g. >1) for this user i and user j pair without correlation calculations.

o If both user i and user j in this pair were valid users, Radio shall calculate the two users APS correlation result: [image-9][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical formula. The formula is:
$c\_{i,j} = \frac{\text{tr}(A\_i^T A\_j)}{|A\_i|\_F |A\_j|\_F}$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_3491.011.png[/image\_path][/image-9], where [image-10][image\_desc]The image contains a blurry, close-up view of a stylized letter "A" with a subscript "i". The "A" appears to be rendered in a metallic or glossy material, with highlights and shadows suggesting a three-dimensional form. The subscript "i" is smaller and positioned below and to the right of the "A". The background is white and out of focus.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_3491.012.png[/image\_path][/image-10] is the averaged APS of user i and [image-11][image\_desc]The image contains a stylized representation of the letter "A" with a subscript "i". The "A" is depicted in a gradient of colors, transitioning from a dark blue at the top to a lighter blue and then to a yellowish-brown at the bottom. The left leg of the "A" appears to be a dark, solid color, possibly black or a very dark blue, with a hint of brown at the bottom. The crossbar of the "A" is also dark. The right leg of the "A" is rendered with the same color gradient as the main body. The subscript "i" is smaller and positioned to the right of the "A", also featuring a similar color gradient, with the dot of the "i" being a lighter shade. The overall impression is one of a modern, somewhat abstract design.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_3491.013.png[/image\_path][/image-11] is the averaged APS of user j.

**Output:**

- The user correlation results [image-12][image\_desc]The image contains the text "cᵢ,ⱼ" in a blurred, stylized font. The letters are in a gradient of blue and orange, with a white outline. The text is centered within a black border.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_3491.014.png[/image\_path][/image-12] of the current user i and each correlation user j.

Refer to 5GMAX Massive MIMO Algorithm specification §7.2 (see [11101361](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcsvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for more details.

For the supported L1 HWs, refer to [11107567](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kog6SfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

Note, all the formulars above are theory description, the implementation shall select the most efficient way based on the HW and SW design.

---

##### 2.4.5.3.2.0-10 Receiver algorithm - Radio APS metric (ID: 13480974)

**2.4.5.3.2.0-10.0-1**  (ID: `13480975`)

Receiver algorithm - Radio
**Purpose:**

Radio shall calculate the Angular Power Spectrum (APS) metric for each user according to the indication from DU.

**Input:**

From DU:

- The APS metric calculation indication, see [13464702](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_CMaQETQeEfGxldjOZQLezQ).

- The selected SRS sub-band index for each user, see [11143715](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr6zlfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

- The srsPowerThreshold, see [11094702](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kme6AfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

From Radio:

- The SRS channel estimation per BF sub-band per port results of each user, see [11107613](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kohhWPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

- Number of TRX NTRX ∈ {64, 32}.  NTRX is equal to M*N*P, where M=number of TRX rows, N = number of TRX columns and P = 2 is number of TRX Polarizations.

**Algorithm:**

If the APS metric calculation indication is true, then for each user:

- Radio shall convert the SRS sub-band index to BF sub-band index (e.g. select the middle PRBs), and get the SRS channel estimation results of the user according to the selected BF sub-band index.

- Radio shall construct matrix [image-0][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical expression: $\Psi(l, p)\_{M \times N}$. The expression is written in black text on a white background. The symbol $\Psi$ is a Greek letter, commonly used in quantum mechanics and other fields of physics. The expression is followed by a subscript indicating a matrix of size M by N.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_3491.002.png[/image\_path][/image-0] for each valid SRS port *l and each polarization* p, based on the SRS channel estimation result of the user.\*\*

o When the SRS power of all the ports are received for the user, if the SRS power of a port is lower than srsPowerThreshold, the related SRS port is considered as not valid and shall be excluded from the calculations. Note, if the SRS power of all the ports is not valid, Radio shall skip the APS metric calculation of this user and report an invalid APS metric value for it.

o When the SRS power of all the ports are not received for the user, Radio shall skip the APS metric calculation of this user and report an invalid APS metric value for it.

- Radio shall perform zero padding (row & column wise) for [image-1][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical expression. The expression is written in black text on a white background. The expression is:
$\Psi(l, p)*{M \times N}$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_3491.003.png[/image\_path][/image-1], to obtain the [image-2][image\_desc]$\Psi^0(l,p)*{OM+ON}$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_3491.004.png[/image\_path][/image-2], where *OM = overSampRatioAps**M and* ON = overSampRatioAps**N. Note, here overSampRatioAps is fixed to 1, so actually OM=M and ON=N.**\*\*

- Radio shall calculate the 2D FFT of [image-3][image\_desc]The image contains the mathematical expression $\Psi^0(l, p)*{OM \cdot ON}$. The expression is written in a dark color against a light background. The symbol $\Psi$ is a Greek letter, and it is superscripted with a zero. Following the symbol is a parenthesis containing the letters 'l' and 'p' separated by a comma. After the closing parenthesis, there are subscripts 'OM' and 'ON' separated by a dot.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_3491.005.png[/image\_path][/image-3], to obtain the 2D FFT matrix [image-4][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is:
$B(l, p)*{OM \times ON} = W\_{OM} \* \Psi^0(l, p)*{OM \times ON} \* W*{ON}^T$
The equation involves variables B, l, p, W, and $\Psi^0$. The subscripts OM and ON indicate dimensions or specific components. The asterisk (\*) likely represents a multiplication or convolution operation, and the superscript T on $W\_{ON}$ indicates a transpose.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_3491.006.png[/image\_path][/image-4],

where WOM is a matrix with size OM*OM, and the m-th row of the matrix is given by [image-5][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical expression enclosed in square brackets. The expression is a sequence of terms involving the exponential function with a complex argument.
The sequence starts with the number 1.
Following the 1, there are three terms of the form $e^{j \frac{2\pi m}{OM}}$.
The first term is $e^{j \frac{2\pi m}{OM}}$.
The second term is $e^{j \frac{2\pi m \* 2}{OM}}$.
The third term is $e^{j \frac{2\pi m \* (OM-1)}{OM}}$.
These terms are separated by spaces and ellipses (...), indicating a continuation of the pattern.
The overall expression appears to represent a discrete set of complex exponentials, possibly related to a Discrete Fourier Transform (DFT) or a similar signal processing concept. The variables used are 'j' (imaginary unit), 'm', 'OM', and numerical constants.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/img-05271647.png[/image\_path][/image-5],* m = 0, 1, … OM-1,\*

and WON is a matrix with size ON*ON, and the n-th row of the matrix is given by [image-6][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical expression enclosed in square brackets. The expression is a sequence of terms separated by spaces and ellipses. The terms are of the form $e^{j \frac{2\pi n k}{ON}}$, where $k$ takes values from 1 to $ON-1$. Specifically, the sequence starts with 1, followed by $e^{j \frac{2\pi n}{ON}}$, then $e^{j \frac{2\pi n \cdot 2}{ON}}$, and continues with ellipses, ending with $e^{j \frac{2\pi n \cdot (ON-1)}{ON}}$. The expression represents a discrete Fourier transform or a related mathematical concept.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/img-05271917.png[/image\_path][/image-6],* n = 0, 1, … ON-1.\*

- Radio shall compute the element-wise squared-magnitude of all elements, to obtain the APS per SRS port per polarization: [image-7][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in white text against a white background. The equation is:
$A(l, p)\_{OM \cdot ON} = B^*(l, p)*{OM \cdot ON} \circ B(l, p)*{OM \cdot ON}$
The equation involves variables $A$, $B$, $l$, and $p$. The subscript $OM \cdot ON$ appears on both sides of the equation. The symbol $B^*$ indicates the conjugate transpose of $B$, and the symbol $\circ$ likely represents a Hadamard product or element-wise multiplication.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_3491.009.png[/image\_path][/image-7].

- Radio shall derive the APS metric of the user by averaging the APSs over all the valid SRS ports and all the TRX polarizations: [image-8][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical formula. The formula is:
$A\_{OM \cdot ON} = \frac{1}{P \cdot L} \sum\_{p=1}^{P} \sum\_{l=1}^{L} A(l, p)\_{OM \cdot ON}$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_3491.010.png[/image\_path][/image-8], where L is the number of valid SRS ports.

**Output:**

- The APS metric of each user.

Refer to 5GMAX Massive MIMO Algorithm specification §7.2 (see [11101361](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcsvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for more details.

For the supported L1 HWs, refer to [11107567](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kog6SfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

Note, all the formulars above are theory description, the implementation shall select the most efficient way based on the HW and SW design.

---

##### 2.4.5.3.2.0-11 Receiver algorithm - Radio Singular value calculation (ID: 12580757)

**2.4.5.3.2.0-11.0-1**  (ID: `12580758`)

Receiver algorithm - Radio
Purpose:

Radio shall calculate the singular values per UE for selected user(s).

Input:

* SRS channel estimation from Radio, see [11107613: Channel estimation](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kohhWPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)
* Singular value calculation indicator from DU, see [11156605: Indication for SRS decoding](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ks2Ao_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)

Algorithm:

* Radio shall calculate the singular values per UE for selected user(s) according to the Singular value calculation indicator from DU. Note, Radio can support maximum 2 users singular values calculation per SRS slot.
* Radio shall calculate the singular values using SVD method with the latest SRS channel estimation results on the middle BF subband of the SRS subband, according to 5GMAX Massive MIMO Algorithm specification §5.1.2.2.1.1 (see [11101361](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcsvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)).

Output:

* The singular values per UE for selected user(s).

For the supported L1 HWs, refer to [11107567](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kog6SfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

---

##### 2.4.5.3.3.0-3 Resource allocation Cyclic Shift and Combs configuration (ID: 11107743)

**2.4.5.3.3.0-3.0-5**  (ID: `13396399`)

Resource allocation
The DU (for Loki and Thor **[CB016062-BD] and GPU**[End CB016062-BD] L1 HWs) and the Radio (for Thor 7-2e L1 HW) shall support periodic SRS Comb2. For the CS combination per comb, refer to ([11351295](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubsmb_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).\*\*\*\*

The DU (for Loki and Thor **[CB016062-BD] and GPU**[End CB016062-BD] L1 HWs) and the Radio (for Thor 7-2e L1 HW)**shall support periodic SRS with Comb4 (KTC=4)** with below limitation,***\****\*

a) For the CS combination per comb, refer to ([11351295](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubsmb_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) (Note1)

b) SRS subband length *mSRS shall not be less than 8 PRBs, up to 4 CS can be supported per comb.* *Where* mSRS is given by {*C*srs*, B*srs} according to Table 6.4.1.4.3-1 of TS 38.211**.*****\****\*

c) up to 4 transmission combs per symbol per cell for KTC=4, but 2 combs in maximum would be configured from end to end.

Note1: There is a limitation for cyclic shift from higher layers configuration to avoid inter-UE interference, due to simulations indicate cyclic shift interval less than ¼ would degrade the performance.

---

### 2.4.5.4 Long Term CoMa filtering and best beam selection for UL and DL BM

#### 2.4.5.4.0-1 Long Term CoMa filtering and best beam selection for UL and DL BM Overview (ID: 13327064)

**2.4.5.4.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `13327070`)

Long Term CoMa filtering and best beam selection for UL and DL BM
NOTE: the common part area of this artifact applicable for both subfeatures CB015122-A and CB015122-C, depending on which one is implemented first.

Inputs:

* NRBTS/NRCELL.actInternalImprovements1.Bit1
* [CB015122-E] NRCELL.actEcpri72e [End CB015122-E]
* NRBTS/NRCELL.numberOfRxBeamforming
* NRBTS/NRCELL.actUlSrsBm
* [CB015122-B, CB015122-D]  NRBTS/NRCELL.actDlSrsBm [End CB015122-B, CB015122-D]

Algorithm:

If NRBTS/NRCELL.actInternalImprovements1.Bit1 is set to 1, all the artifacts in section [13326623](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_h_GKMNQZEfCnq6aYkXj8Pw?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) are applicable.

Note: DU (LT beam computer see [13397952](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_1qdpoQizEfGu_4itz1o3RA?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)) expects that the NRBTS/NRCELL.actUlSrsBm is set to true for the artifact in section [13327323](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_YC_d4tRPEfCnq6aYkXj8Pw?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) [CB015122-B, CB015122-D] and the NRBTS/NRCELL.actDlSrsBm is set to true for the artifact in section [13327324](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_mtpUcNRPEfCnq6aYkXj8Pw?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) [End CB015122-B, CB015122-D]

1) step 1: Long Term CoMa averaging and filtering

[CB015122-E]  If NRCELL.actEcpri72e is set to false [End CB015122-E] (that is eCPRI 7-2a),

DU (LT beam computer see [13397952](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_1qdpoQizEfGu_4itz1o3RA?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)) shall update a Long Term CoMa using the instantaneous CoMas averaged over different polarizations in artifact [13367271](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_R9b-0fbTEfC0Z6kKit-MzQ) and validated in artifact [13367270](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Lj97U_bTEfC0Z6kKit-MzQ) and averaged over the SRS ports according to the algorithm described under the artifacts in section [13327325](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ukvMgNRPEfCnq6aYkXj8Pw) for UL beams or [13327329](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_GUhbk9RQEfCnq6aYkXj8Pw) for DL beams.

[CB015122-E] If NRCELL.actEcpri72e is set to true (eCPRI 7-2e),

DU (LT beam computer see [13397952](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_1qdpoQizEfGu_4itz1o3RA?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)) shall update a Long Term CoMa using the instantaneous CoMas averaged over different polarizations in artifact [11107209](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kodP4_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) and validated in artifact [11107200](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kodP4fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) and over the SRS ports according to the algorithm described under the artifacts in section [13327325](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ukvMgNRPEfCnq6aYkXj8Pw?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) for UL beams or [13327329](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_GUhbk9RQEfCnq6aYkXj8Pw?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) for DL beams. [End CB015122-E]

Rationale: In the scope of this feature, for simplification purpose, instantaneous CoMas output (averaged over all the polarizations) and validation (SRS power computation, DTX / non DTX result and SRS validation  thresholds) have been aligned for the eCPRI 7-2a in L1-BB component compared to the legacy eCPRI 7-2e algorithm in the Radio, assuming that there won´t be negative KPI impact.

2) step 2: best beams determination

On receipt of an event from upper layer (L2(DU) see [13397952](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_1qdpoQizEfGu_4itz1o3RA?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)), DU (LT beam computer see [13397952](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_1qdpoQizEfGu_4itz1o3RA?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)) shall start to determine the best beams (independently for UL and DL):

* for UL beams according to artifacts in section:

-  [13327327](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_16kKkdRPEfCnq6aYkXj8Pw?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) if NRBTS/NRCELL.numberOfRxBeamforming = 4

-  [CB015122-C] [13327328](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_84nh0NRPEfCnq6aYkXj8Pw?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) if NRBTS/NRCELL.numberOfRxBeamforming = 8  [End CB015122-C] [CB015122-E] or 16 [End CB015122-E]

* [CB015122-B, CB015122-D] for DL beams according to artifacts in section [13327330](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_LzBroNRQEfCnq6aYkXj8Pw?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)  [End CB015122-B, CB015122-D]

About the figures describing how the Long Term Beam computer (which contains the Long Term CoMa averaging and filtering and the best beams determination functionalities) interacts with the other components, please refer to the artifact [13397952](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_1qdpoQizEfGu_4itz1o3RA?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9).

Notes:

* polarizations mean RU polarities.
* the state machines and events are defined in artifact [13328734](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_-30ziNTeEfCnq6aYkXj8Pw?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) for UL BM and in artifact [13328736](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_EyQxpdTfEfCnq6aYkXj8Pw?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) for DL BM.

Rationale:

With the move from L2(DU) to L1(BB) components (see [13397952](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_1qdpoQizEfGu_4itz1o3RA?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)) of the Long Term CoMa filtering and the best beam selection algorithm, it is expected to save CPU processing time at L2-PS level. It was agreed to align the outputs coming from Radio component  (see [13397952](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_1qdpoQizEfGu_4itz1o3RA?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)) in 7-2e and L1(BB) component (see [13397952](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_1qdpoQizEfGu_4itz1o3RA?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)) in 7-2a (that is instantaneous CoMa averaged over polarizations, SRS power and validation (DTX/non-DTX) result).

Output:

For a given UE, DU (LT beam computer see [13397952](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_1qdpoQizEfGu_4itz1o3RA "https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/web#action=com.ibm.rdm.web.pages.showartifactpage&artifacturi=https%3a%2f%2fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2frm%2fresources%2fmd_tauaapvfee-aqvopbp1qhq&oslc_config.context=https%3a%2f%2fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2fgc%2fconf")) shall estimate the UL [CB015122-B, CB015122-D] and DL [End CB015122-B, CB015122-D] beams and shall provide to the upper layer (L2(DU) see [13397952](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_1qdpoQizEfGu_4itz1o3RA "https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/web#action=com.ibm.rdm.web.pages.showartifactpage&artifacturi=https%3a%2f%2fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2frm%2fresources%2fmd_tauaapvfee-aqvopbp1qhq&oslc_config.context=https%3a%2f%2fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2fgc%2fconf")) an ordered list of UL best beams [CB015122-B, CB015122-D] and an ordered list of DL best beams [End CB015122-B, CB015122-D], with each list sorted in descending order of quality (starting from the best).

The following figures describe how the Long Term Beam Computer interacts with other components inside gNB-DU

Red box in the figures below indicates the new functional blocks moved to DU for the LT CoMa update and UL and DL beams determining,

[CB015122-E] If NRCELL.actEcpri72e is set to false [End CB015122-E] (eCPRI 7-2a):

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a block diagram illustrating a signal processing flow within a wireless communication system, likely a 5G New Radio (NR) system. The diagram depicts different functional blocks and their interconnections, organized by layers (L1, L2, L3) and components (RADIO, FH, DU).
Here's a breakdown of the elements:
**Left Side:**
\* **UE Transmits:** Indicates that the User Equipment (UE) transmits either TAS SRS (Transmit Antenna Switching Sounding Reference Signal) or Codebook SRS.
\* **RADIO:** Represents the radio frequency component.
\* **FH:** Likely stands for "Front-haul," indicating the interface between the RADIO and the baseband processing unit.
**L1 (BB) - Layer 1 (Baseband):** This section details the processing at the physical layer.
\* **NDM SRS MXNX2:** Input signal related to Non-Dynamic Measurement SRS.
\* **SRS pre-combination:** A block for combining SRS signals.
\* **SRS:** Output from the pre-combination block.
\* **SRS channel estimation:** A block for estimating the channel based on SRS.
\* **H:** Represents the estimated channel.
\* **Inst. H/V COMA:** Likely refers to instantaneous channel estimation in horizontal and vertical dimensions, possibly using COMA (Cooperative Multi-Antenna) techniques.
\* **Cov:** Represents covariance.
\* **beamforming:** A block responsible for beamforming.
\* **UE specific PUSCH Beams:** An input indicating UE-specific beams for Physical Uplink Shared Channel (PUSCH).
\* **SRS channel estimation:** Another SRS channel estimation block, possibly for a different path or purpose.
\* **RANK/PMI estimation:** A block for estimating Rank and Precoding Matrix Indicator, crucial for MIMO (Multiple-Input Multiple-Output) operations.
**LT Beam Computer:** This is a key component within the L1 (BB) processing.
\* **LT CoMa averaging and filtering:** A block for averaging and filtering, likely related to Long Term (LT) Cooperative Multi-Antenna (CoMa) processing.
\* **cell specific set of oDFT Beams:** An input indicating a cell-specific set of orthogonal DFT (Discrete Fourier Transform) beams.
\* **LT Cov:** Long Term Covariance.
\* **Beam determination:** A block that determines the optimal beams.
\* **Ordered list of best beam(s):** The output of the Beam determination block, providing a ranked list of the best beams.
**L2 (DU) (upper layer) - Layer 2 (Distributed Unit):**
\* **Beam selection:** A block that selects the best beam(s) from the ordered list.
**L3 (DU) (upper layer) - Layer 3 (Distributed Unit):**
\* This layer is shown as an input to the "Beam determination" block, suggesting it provides higher-layer information or control for beam determination.
**DU:** Denotes the Distributed Unit, which encompasses L1, L2, and L3 processing in this diagram.
**Arrows:** Green arrows indicate data flow, while blue arrows indicate control or input signals.
The diagram illustrates a process where SRS signals are received, processed for channel estimation, and then used by the LT Beam Computer to determine and select optimal beams for transmission, likely for uplink communication. The process involves both short-term (Inst. H/V COMA) and long-term (LT CoMa) processing, and considers cell-specific beam information.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/img-02141357.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

[CB015122-E] If NRCELL.actEcpri72e is set to true (eCPRI 7-2e),

[image-1][image\_desc]The image is a block diagram illustrating a process related to wireless communication, specifically focusing on beamforming and selection. The diagram is divided into several sections, representing different functional units and layers.
On the left side, under the heading "UE Transmits:", it indicates that the User Equipment (UE) transmits either "TAS SRS" or "Codebook SRS". Below this, there's a representation of a mobile phone with radiating waves, signifying wireless transmission.
The main processing flow is divided into two large boxes: "L1 (Radio) (lower layer)" and "LT Beam Computer".
Within the "L1 (Radio) (lower layer)" box, there are several sub-processes:
- "SRS reception per TRX": This block receives SRS (Sounding Reference Signal) per TRX (Transmit/Receive).
- "NDM SRS MXNX2": This input seems to be related to SRS.
- "SRS channel estimation": This process estimates the channel based on SRS. It receives input from "SRS reception per TRX" and outputs "H" (likely representing the channel estimate).
- "Computation of inst. H/V CoMa": This block computes instantaneous H/V CoMa (likely Channel Quality Indicator or similar metric) based on the channel estimate "H".
- "SRS beamforming": This block performs SRS beamforming. It receives "SRS" as input and outputs "UE specific PUSCH Beams".
- "SRS channel estimation" (second instance): This block also performs SRS channel estimation.
- "PMI,RI calculation": This block calculates Precoding Matrix Indicator (PMI) and Rank Indicator (RI) based on the channel estimate "H" from the second SRS channel estimation.
The "FH" (likely Front Haul) section is a vertical gray bar separating the "L1 (Radio)" and "LT Beam Computer" sections.
The "LT Beam Computer" section is further divided into:
- "LT CoMa averaging and filtering": This block receives "Cov" (likely Coverage or Covariance) as input and performs averaging and filtering.
- "Determination of best beams": This block receives the output from "LT CoMa averaging and filtering" (labeled "LT Cov") and determines the best beams. It also receives an input labeled "cell specific set of oDFT Beams".
- "Ordered list of best beam(s)": This is an output from "Determination of best beams".
- "Beam selection": This block receives the "Ordered list of best beam(s)" and performs beam selection.
Below the "LT Beam Computer" section, there are labels for different layers:
- "L1 (BB)" (Baseband)
- "L2 (DU) (upper layer)" (Data Unit, upper layer)
- "L3 (DU) (upper layer)"
The diagram also shows arrows indicating the flow of data and control signals between these blocks. Green arrows generally represent data flow, while blue arrows represent control or specific beam information.
Finally, at the bottom, there are labels "RADIO" and "DU" (likely Distributed Unit) indicating the physical location or functional grouping of these components.

#### 2.4.5.4.1 Periodic codebook SRS for UL BM

##### 2.4.5.4.1.1 SRS BM UE Context and State Machine for UL BM

###### 2.4.5.4.1.1.0-1 SRS BM UE Context and State Machine for UL BM Context and state Machine for UL BM (ID: 13336532)

**2.4.5.4.1.1.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `13328882`)

SRS BM UE Context and State Machine for UL BM
13328882:
SRS BM UE Context and State Machine for UL BM
13328882:
SRS BM UE Context and State Machine for UL BM
13328882:
SRS BM UE Context and State Machine for UL BM
13328882:
SRS BM UE Context and State Machine for UL BM
13328882:
SRS BM UE Context and State Machine for UL BM
13328882:
SRS BM UE Context and State Machine for UL BM
13328882:
SRS BM UE Context and State Machine for UL BM
13328882:
SRS BM UE Context and State Machine for UL BM
Rationale:

For any SRS BM UE context, upon reception of valid instantaneous CoMas, DU (LT beam computer see [13397952](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_1qdpoQizEfGu_4itz1o3RA?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)) shall update a Long Term CoMa using IIR filtering. But the determination of the UL best beams is triggered by an event from upper layer (L2(DU) see [13397952](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_1qdpoQizEfGu_4itz1o3RA?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)). Therefore DU (LT beam computer see [13397952](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_1qdpoQizEfGu_4itz1o3RA?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)) shall store the updated Long Term CoMa updated until this event is received. Consequently, DU (LT beam computer see [13397952](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_1qdpoQizEfGu_4itz1o3RA?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)) shall maintain a state machine to manage the timing and consistency between LT CoMa updates and beam selection triggering.

Algorithm:

DU (LT beam computer see [13397952](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_1qdpoQizEfGu_4itz1o3RA?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)) shall create a SRS BM UE context for UEs which are configured with codebook usage periodic SRS in the state SRS\_ULBM\_IDLE and shall wait for a first valid instantaneous CoMa.

Note: The instantaneous CoMas

* are provided and averaged over polarizations for each SRS port
* either by LT beam computer (see [13397952](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_1qdpoQizEfGu_4itz1o3RA)) [CB015122-E] when NRCELL.actEcpri72e is set to false [End CB015122-E] (see [13367271](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_R9b-0fbTEfC0Z6kKit-MzQ))
* [CB015122-E] or by Radio component (see [13397952](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_1qdpoQizEfGu_4itz1o3RA)) when NRCELL.actEcpri72e is set to true [End CB015122-E] (see [11107209](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kodP4_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
* and are validated
* either in LT beam computer (see [13397952](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_1qdpoQizEfGu_4itz1o3RA))  [CB015122-E] when NRCELL.actEcpri72e is set to false [End CB015122-E]  (see [13367270](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Lj97U_bTEfC0Z6kKit-MzQ)).
* [CB015122-E] or in Radio component  when NRCELL.actEcpri72e is set to true  (see [11107200](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kodP4fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) ). [End CB015122-E]

For any SRS BM UE context, the overall state machine is defined in the below diagram:

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating a state machine with two states: "SRS\_ULBM\_IDLE" and "SRS\_ULBM\_COMA". These states are represented by large circles.
There are five transitions between these states, indicated by numbered arrows:
1. An arrow labeled "1" points from "SRS\_ULBM\_IDLE" to "SRS\_ULBM\_COMA".
2. An arrow labeled "2" forms a self-loop on the "SRS\_ULBM\_COMA" state, indicating a transition from "SRS\_ULBM\_COMA" back to itself.
3. An arrow labeled "3" also forms a self-loop on the "SRS\_ULBM\_COMA" state, indicating another transition from "SRS\_ULBM\_COMA" back to itself.
4. An arrow labeled "4" curves from "SRS\_ULBM\_COMA" back to "SRS\_ULBM\_IDLE".
5. An arrow labeled "5" curves from "SRS\_ULBM\_COMA" back to "SRS\_ULBM\_IDLE".
The text within the circles is:
- SRS\_ULBM\_IDLE
- SRS\_ULBM\_COMA
The numbers labeling the transitions are:
- 1
- 2
- 3
- 4
- 5[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/img-03061023.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

Where the states are defined in the artifact [13413758](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_iKNxkRKBEfG3gf_qmG8Vww?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) and the events are defined in the artifact [13413759](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_p8GZghKBEfG3gf_qmG8Vww?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9).
