##### 2.4.5.4.2.3 Beam Selection for Dl

###### 2.4.5.4.2.3.0-1 Beam Selection for Dl Beamforming Gain tables for DL MU MIMO (ID: 13454898)

**2.4.5.4.2.3.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `13454899`)

Beam Selection for Dl
Input:

- NRCELL.actDlMuMimoEnh

* tapered and no tapered weight vectors in UL beamset (see [13338216](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_YXEAMdqAEfCmxLW1M7HMrQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) and [13337075](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ZK_mUdm2EfCmxLW1M7HMrQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9))

- RSRS,h and  RSRS,v Long Term CoMa in horizontal and vertical direction (see [13332439](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_J59cotXOEfCnq6aYkXj8Pw?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9))

* rrmTrxColumn and rrmTrxRow (see [13331607](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_UX1xktWeEfCnq6aYkXj8Pw)).

Algorithm:

If NRCELL.actDlMuMimoEnh is set to true,

DU (LT beam computer see [13397952](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_1qdpoQizEfGu_4itz1o3RA?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)) shall go through all horizontal DFT beams (over the index j) and shall store all the Kronecker product results of wh,jH . RnSRS,h . wh,jin  gain tables rrmDlBfGainTable (for without tapering and with tapering)

- If UL tapering is disabled, wh,j  is a column vector compiled of weights of a horizontal oversampled DFT beam without tapering.

- If UL tapering is enabled, wh,j  is a column vector compiled of weights of a horizontal oversampled DFT beam with tapering.

-  RSRS,h is the Long Term CoMa related to the horizontal direction (see [13332439](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_J59cotXOEfCnq6aYkXj8Pw?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9))

DU (LT beam computer see [13397952](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_1qdpoQizEfGu_4itz1o3RA?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)) shall go through all vertical DFT beams (over the index i) and and shall store all the Kronecker product results of  wv,iH . RSRS,v . wv,I in  gain tables rrmDlBfGainTable (for without tapering and with tapering)

- If UL tapering is disabled, wv,i is a column vector compiled of weights of a vertical oversampled DFT beam without tapering.

- If UL tapering is enabled, wv,i is a column vector compiled of weights of a vertical oversampled DFT beam with tapering.

-  RSRS,v is the Long Term CoMa related to the vertical direction (see [13332439](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_J59cotXOEfCnq6aYkXj8Pw?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9))

Note:

* DU (LT beam computer see [13397952](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_1qdpoQizEfGu_4itz1o3RA?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)) shall receive the set of horizontal and vertical oversampled DFT beams without and with tapering from upper layer during cell setup procedure (see [13338216](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_YXEAMdqAEfCmxLW1M7HMrQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) and [13337075](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ZK_mUdm2EfCmxLW1M7HMrQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)).

Output:

* beamforming Gain Tables for tapered and non tapered beams in horizontal and vertical direction.

---

###### 2.4.5.4.2.3.0-2 Beam Selection for Dl Beamforming weights of Dl beams (ID: 13338216)

**2.4.5.4.2.3.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `13338224`)

Beam Selection for Dl
Inputs:

- NRBTS/NRCELL/actDlSrsBm

- NRBTS/actNrLBmMimoRTXPowerSaving

* legacy 2D-DFT beamset (see [13337075](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ZK_mUdm2EfCmxLW1M7HMrQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9))
* power saving beamset  (see [11294877](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TqE3pPvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
* [CB015122-J] if NRCELL.actDlMuMimoEnh is set to true, tapered and not tapered UL beamset from upper layer (see [13337075](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ZK_mUdm2EfCmxLW1M7HMrQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)).[End CB015122-J]

Algorithm:

DU (LT beam computer see [13397952](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_1qdpoQizEfGu_4itz1o3RA)) shall use the sets of horizontal and vertical DFT beams in artifact [13337075](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ZK_mUdm2EfCmxLW1M7HMrQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) .

Note: If (NRBTS/NRCELL/actDlSrsBm is true) and (NRBTS/actNrLBmMimoRTXPowerSaving is true), DU (LT beam computer see [13397952](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_1qdpoQizEfGu_4itz1o3RA)) expects that upper layer (L3(DU) see [13397952](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_1qdpoQizEfGu_4itz1o3RA)) generates both legacy and power saving beamset (see [11294877](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TqE3pPvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and sends to DU (LT beam computer see [13397952](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_1qdpoQizEfGu_4itz1o3RA)).

If the cell is in massive MIMO power saving mode (see [11294877](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TqE3pPvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) , on receipt of the event(3) from the upper layer (L2(DU) see [13397952](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_1qdpoQizEfGu_4itz1o3RA) and [13337134](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_I5P1INnBEfCmxLW1M7HMrQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)), DU (LT beam computer see [13397952](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_1qdpoQizEfGu_4itz1o3RA)) shall use horizontal and vertical DFT beams which belong to power saving beamset (otherwise to legacy beamset) in the artifacts [13338221](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_0QEHY9qAEfCmxLW1M7HMrQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) and [13338222](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_5qzRwdqAEfCmxLW1M7HMrQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9).

[CB015122-J] if NRCELL.actDlMuMimoEnh is set to true, on receipt of the event(3) from the upper layer (L2(DU) see [13397952](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_1qdpoQizEfGu_4itz1o3RA) and [13337134](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_I5P1INnBEfCmxLW1M7HMrQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)), DU (LT beam computer see [13397952](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_1qdpoQizEfGu_4itz1o3RA)) shall use horizontal and vertical DFT beams which belong to tapered and non tapered beamset in the DL beam selection (see artifacts [13338221](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_0QEHY9qAEfCmxLW1M7HMrQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) and [13338222](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_5qzRwdqAEfCmxLW1M7HMrQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)).  [End CB015122-J]

Note:

* the 2D-DFT beams may comprise oversampled DFT weights vectors (see [11294877](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TqE3pPvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
* [CB015122-J] since DL MU MIMO shall be disabled during power saving mode, there is no tapering horizontal and vertical DFT beams for power saving beamset.  [End CB015122-J]

Output:

* the right beamset to use for the best beam determination.

---

###### 2.4.5.4.2.3.0-3 Beam Selection for Dl Joint searching method of best and second best DFT beam (ID: 13338217)

**2.4.5.4.2.3.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `13338218`)

Beam Selection for Dl
Determine the best DL beam and Beam selection methods for DL

---

**2.4.5.4.2.3.0-3.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `13338225`)

Beam Selection for Dl
The number of DL beams is provided by upper layer (see [11156252](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ks0LcPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)).

If the number of DL beams is equal to 1, then DU (LT beam computer see [13397952](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_1qdpoQizEfGu_4itz1o3RA)) shall use only 1 best beam (see [13338221](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_0QEHY9qAEfCmxLW1M7HMrQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)).

If the number of DL beams is greater than 1, then DU (LT beam computer see [13397952](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_1qdpoQizEfGu_4itz1o3RA)) shall provide to upper layer DU (L2(DU) see [13397952](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_1qdpoQizEfGu_4itz1o3RA)):

* 1 best beam (see [13338221](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_0QEHY9qAEfCmxLW1M7HMrQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9))
* 2 beams jointly determined as [13338222](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_5qzRwdqAEfCmxLW1M7HMrQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9).

Note: for RedCap UEs, only one beam is expected

---

**2.4.5.4.2.3.0-3.0-2**  (ID: `13338221`)

Beam Selection for Dl
Determine the best Dl beam

---

**2.4.5.4.2.3.0-3.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `13338227`)

Beam Selection for Dl
Inputs:

* beamset (see [13338216](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_YXEAMdqAEfCmxLW1M7HMrQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9))
* Long Term CoMa (see [13332439](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_J59cotXOEfCnq6aYkXj8Pw?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9))

Algorithm:

This requirement defines the algorithm for finding the best Dl beam based on Long Term CoMa filtered (see [13332439](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_J59cotXOEfCnq6aYkXj8Pw?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)).

When DU (LT beam computer see [13397952](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_1qdpoQizEfGu_4itz1o3RA)) receives an event from upper layers in the state SRS\_DLBM\_COMA in order to start the computation of the DL best beams (see [13337134](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_I5P1INnBEfCmxLW1M7HMrQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)), DU (LT beam computer see [13397952](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_1qdpoQizEfGu_4itz1o3RA)) shall find the best horizontal and vertical DFT beams (hBest and vBest) according to the same algorithm for finding the best 2D-DFT Dl beam in the artifact [13337124](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_wfJIodm-EfCmxLW1M7HMrQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)  . The best DL beam is the Kronecker product of DFT beams vBest and hBest.

Output:

- the best horizontal and vertical DL best beam

---

**2.4.5.4.2.3.0-3.0-3**  (ID: `13338222`)

Beam Selection for Dl
Joint determination of pair of best beams

---

**2.4.5.4.2.3.0-3.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `13338228`)

Beam Selection for Dl

13338228:
Beam Selection for Dl
Inputs:
- beamset (see [13338216](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_YXEAMdqAEfCmxLW1M7HMrQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9))
- Long Term CoMa (see [13332439](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_J59cotXOEfCnq6aYkXj8Pw?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9))
- NRCELL/dftBeamDiffThresholdH
- NRCELL/dftBeamDiffThresholdV
Algorithm:
The two beams are jointly selected by following steps:
Step 1: For all candidate horizontal and vertical DFT beams, the beam power is calculated as:
For horizontal beam j, Ph,j = abs(wHh,j \* RSRS,h \* wh,j)
For vertical beam j, Pv,j = abs(wHv,j \* RSRS,v \* wv,j)
Where
·  wh,j is the horizontal DFT beam weight vector (see [13338216](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_YXEAMdqAEfCmxLW1M7HMrQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)).
·  wv,j  is the vertical DFT beam weight vector (see [13338216](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_YXEAMdqAEfCmxLW1M7HMrQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)).
·  RSRS,h is the horizontal Long term CoMa (see [13332439](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_J59cotXOEfCnq6aYkXj8Pw?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)).
·  RSRS,v is the vertical Long Term CoMa (see [13332439](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_J59cotXOEfCnq6aYkXj8Pw?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)).
Note: the terms wHh,j \* RSRS,h \* wh,j and wHv,j \* RSRS,v \* wv,jare expected to be real positive value, but the abs() expression is a safe way in case of potential very small negative real value or complex term with a very small imaginary part due to numerical imprecision.
Step 2: DU (LT beam computer see [13397952](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_1qdpoQizEfGu_4itz1o3RA)) shall sort the horizontal beams according to their power in descending order and shall pick the first 10 of the sorted list of the horizontal beams. In the same way, DU (LT beam computer see [13397952](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_1qdpoQizEfGu_4itz1o3RA)) shall sort the vertical beams according to their power in descending order and shall pick the first 6 of the sorted list of the vertical beams. DU (LT beam computer see [13397952](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_1qdpoQizEfGu_4itz1o3RA)) shall generate a total of 60 2D-DFT beams by Kronecker product of horizontal beam and vertical beam. For each 2D-DFT beam, the beam power is the product of horizontal beam power and vertical beam power: Pj = Ph,j\* Pv,j
Step 3: DU (LT beam computer see [13397952](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_1qdpoQizEfGu_4itz1o3RA)) shall find the beam pair {beam a, beam b} from 60 2D-DFT beams:
For i from index of total 60 2D-DFT beams
For j = 0 to i
if (abs(Ph,i - Ph,j) < NRCELL/dftBeamDiffThresholdH) AND  (abs(Pv,i - Pv,j) < NRCELL/dftBeamDiffThresholdV) ,
Calculate the metric of beam pair: metric(i,j)= Pi \* Pj  \* (1 –corrMetric(i,j))2
where 
[image-0][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical formula. The formula is for `corrMetric(i, j)` and is defined as:
$$ \text{corrMetric}(i, j) = \frac{\text{abs}{(\mathbf{w}'*{h,i} \* R*{SRS,h} \* \mathbf{w}*{h,j}) \* (\mathbf{w}'*{v,i} \* R\_{SRS,v} \* \mathbf{w}\_{v,j})}^2}{P\_i \* P\_j} $$
Where:
- `corrMetric(i, j)` is the correlation metric between indices `i` and `j`.
- `abs{}` denotes the absolute value.
- `w'` represents a transposed vector.
- `w_h` and `w_v` represent horizontal and vertical weight vectors, respectively, with indices `i` and `j` indicating specific elements.
- `R_SRS` represents a matrix, likely related to SRS (Spatial Resolution Sampling or similar), with subscripts `h` and `v` for horizontal and vertical components.
- `*` denotes matrix or vector multiplication.
- `P_i` and `P_j` represent scalar values associated with indices `i` and `j`.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/img-12161425.png[/image\_path][/image-0]
end
End
End
where:
\* NRCELL/dftBeamDiffThresholdH defines the power threshold between two horizontal DFT beams which are candidate for this joint determination method.
\* NRCELL/dftBeamDiffThresholdV defines the power threshold between two vertical DFT beams which are candidate for this joint determination method.
Note: the terms inside abs{...} in the corrMetric(i,j) expression are expected to be real positive, but the abs{...} expression is a safe way in case of potential very small negative real value or complex term with a very small imaginary part due to numerical imprecision.
The beam pair (a,b) is the beam pair which achieves the maximum beam pair metric metric(i,j).
[CB015122-J] Note: The beam a has a higher value for the product (wHh,j \* RSRS,h \* wh,j ) \* ( wHv,j \* RSRS,v \* wv,j ) than the beam b. [End CB015122-J]
Step 4: Output beam pair (beam a, beam b).
Output:
- best beams pair

---

### 2.4.5.5 SRS for Positioning

#### 2.4.5.5.0-1 SRS for Positioning Overview (ID: 11107761)

**2.4.5.5.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11107764`)

SRS for Positioning
This chapter describes DU behavior for SRS reception for precise positioning usage. Periodic SRS is supported for precise positioning. (see 5G\_UP\_12488\_replaced([11351374](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubt0gvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

---

#### 2.4.5.5.1 Receiver algorithm

##### 2.4.5.5.1.0-1 Receiver algorithm DU SRS receiver Inputs/Outputs/Algorithm for TOE (ID: 11107769)

**2.4.5.5.1.0-1.0-3**  (ID: `12688623`)

Receiver algorithm
DU shall consider the inputs and the output of the whole L1 SRS for Time Offset Estimation (TOE) measurement and UL-SRS-RSRP measurement with SINR based reliability metrics.

Inputs:

* [Before CB013273-A] Number of TRX during cell setup phase (see 5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_2499\_replaced([11289661](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TqB0X_vcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), which is equal to M*N*P where:
* M = ruNumberOfRowsTRX (hardcoded to 1 in DU);
* N = ruNumberOfColumns (hardcoded to 2 in DU)
* P = number of Polarizations (hardcoded to 2 in the DU) [End CB013273-A]
* [CB013273-A] Number of TRX during cell setup phase (see 5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_2499\_replaced([11289661](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TqB0X_vcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) shall equal to number of streams of SubCell type.
* Refer to [11094176](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmaBffvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) for subcell type and number of antennas.  [End CB013273-A]
* Raw SRS received signal coming from the Radio
* SRS parameters for the SRS resource allocated for the UE. (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_27059\_replaced([11107841](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kojWlfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for supported SRS configurations)
* Distributed Antenna information from higher layer indicating whether it’s Distributed Antenna, and the concrete collocated antenna information.

Algorithm:

· refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_27296\_replaced([11107777](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koivdvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

Output:

· TOE measurement, SINR and UL-SRS-RSRP measurement.

---

The figure below indicates DU functional blocks to support SRS for positioning in TDD FR1, Non BF.
[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a block diagram illustrating a signal processing flow. The diagram is divided into three main sections: "RF", "L1(BB)", and "L2(BB)".
The "RF" section, depicted as a gray rectangle on the left, receives an input labeled "NDM SRS" indicated by a green arrow.
The "L1(BB)" section, enclosed in a light blue border, contains three blue rectangular blocks representing processing steps.
1. The first block, labeled "Raw Channel Estimation", receives the "NDM SRS" input from the "RF" section.
2. A green arrow leads from "Raw Channel Estimation" to a second block labeled "TO estimation".
3. A green arrow leads from "TO estimation" to the "L2(BB)" section.
4. A green arrow leads downwards from "Raw Channel Estimation" to a third block labeled "TO Compensation".
5. A green arrow leads from "TO Compensation" to a fourth block labeled "User Separation".
The "L2(BB)" section, enclosed in a light blue border on the right, is currently empty, suggesting it is the next stage of processing or an output area.
The overall flow shows data originating from the RF section, undergoing channel estimation and time offset (TO) estimation and compensation within the L1(BB) layer, and then proceeding to user separation before potentially moving to the L2(BB) layer.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_3515.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]
 
 
Main functions of DU to support SRS based TO estimation:
 DU expects to receive **[Before CB013273-A] 4**[CB013273-A]  number of TRX **[End CB013273-A] streams of raw SRS data from RU via Non Delay Managed (NDM) eAxCs. Refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_27062\_replaced([11107844](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kojWl_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for supported ports configuration.*****\*
 DU expects periodic SRS with one symbol and this SRS symbol is located in special slot of TDD FR1 frame structure.
 DU shall do raw channel estimation based on the received raw SRS data.
 DU shall do the TO estimation(TOE),  TOE* \*reliability determination, and send the TOE result from L1(BB) to L2(BB)**
 DU shall do the TO Compensation(TOC) based on the TOE result
 DU shall do the User Separation based on the TOC output
 
**Note:**
\* SRS symbol position is fixed to the last symbol of special slot. Refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_27062\_replaced([11107844](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kojWl_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
\* TOC and User Separation blocks are not used for TOE, but for future use of AoA and RSRP algorithms.
\* Term "NDM" is used due to historic reason that it has been widely used for a long time. It's actully "Scheduled Raw SRS" that is used for transmiting the periodic raw SRS data.
 
 
 
File: SRS for positioning architecture.pptx

##### 2.4.5.5.1.0-3 Receiver algorithm Raw Channel Estimation (ID: 11107791)

**2.4.5.5.1.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11107793`)

Receiver algorithm
For TDD FR1 with eCPRI fronthaul interface, DU shall perform raw channel estimation per polarization per TRX:

Input:

· Raw SRS received signals coming from Radio.

Algorithm:

· DU shall perform raw channel estimation according to 5GMax-5G New Radio Algorithm Specification Chapter 2.7.1

Output:

·   Raw channel estimates per polarization per TRX

---

##### 2.4.5.5.1.0-4 Receiver algorithm TO Estimation (ID: 11107795)

**2.4.5.5.1.0-4.0-4**  (ID: `13239893`)

Receiver algorithm
For TDD FR1 with eCPRI fronthaul interface, DU shall perform TO estimation(TOE) according to 5GMax-5G New Radio Algorithm Specification Chapter 2.7.2 per UE.

**Input:**

· Raw channel estimates per polarization per TRX.

 R&D parameter rdSrsFirstPeakDetectThreshold (Refer to  ([11110548](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpFiDfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), NRCELL.actSrsTdoaJumpMethod, NRCELL.srsTdoaJumpRate and NRCELL.srsJumpFirstPeakThreshold are used to detect the first peak.

 SFN initialization time between current TRP and serving cell.(Refer to ([11158027](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ks_Kl_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

 R&D parameter rdSearchWindowOption (Refer to ([11110563](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpGJEfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is used to control search window used in SRS TO estimation for positioning algorithm.

 Combined method in 5GMAX chapter 2.7.2.1 is used.

 R&D parameter rdToEAlgoSelection (Refer to ([11110570](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpGJE_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is used to select TOE method. Refer to Combined method in 5GMAX chapter 2.7.2.1 and Min method in 5GMAX chapter 2.7.2.2.

**Algorithm:**

· DU shall perform **IFFT on raw Channel Estimates with IFFT size 2048 for Comb2 or 1024 for Comb4.**

· DU shall perform **Integer Peak Determination.  When NRCELL.actSrsTdoaJumpMethod= false,R&D parameter rdSrsFirstPeakDetectThreshold (Refer to ([11110548](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpFiDfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is used to detect the first peak. Otherwise NRCELL.srsTdoaJumpRate and NRCELL.srsJumpFirstPeakThreshold are used to detect the first peak.**

· DU shall convert SFN initialization time difference between current cell and serving cell to TOE search window offset (-896 or +896 samples @ 122.88Msps).

· DU shall perform **Polyphase Filter Based Oversampling around the first peak detected in above step. This step is performed only when number of PRB is higher than 60.**

· DU shall perform **Oversample Peak Determination. This step is performed only when number of PRB is higher than 60.**

· DU shall perform **Parabolic Interpolation and get the final TOE.**

**Output:**

·TOE result per UE per TRP (collocated antennas).

·SINR per UE **per TRP (collocated antennas).**

·UL-SRS-RSRP per UE **per TRP (collocated antennas).**

**Rationale:**

·Simulations show that when PRB number is bigger than 60, polyphase filter-based oversampling leads to more accurate TO estimate.

·In shared TRP scenario, NRBTS.NRCELL.timingAdvanceOffset of serving cell and current cell for positioning UE may be different, thus DU shall convert SFN initialization time difference between current cell and serving cell to TOE search window offset.

·Multiple TOE shall be generated in case of Distributed Antenna mode. More specifically, two TOE for 2*2 RX (I.e., 2 TRPs each equipped with 2 antennas) and four TOE for 4*1 RX (I.e., 4 TRPs each equipped with single antenna).

[CB013273-A] Note: only Combined method in 5GMAX chapter 2.7.2.1 is supported when C8S is configured with co-located antennas [End CB013273-A]

---

##### 2.4.5.5.1.0-5 Receiver algorithm Positioning assistant measurements (SINR and UL-SRS-RSRP) (ID: 11107807)

**2.4.5.5.1.0-5.0-1**  (ID: `11107809`)

Receiver algorithm
For TDD FR1 with eCPRI fronthaul interface, DU shall report SINR and UL-SRS-RSRP measurements according to 5GMax-5G New Radio Algorithm Specification Chapter 2.7.6 and 2.7.7.

---

##### 2.4.5.5.1.0-6 Receiver algorithm TOE reliability determination (ID: 11107812)

**2.4.5.5.1.0-6.0-3**  (ID: `12731525`)

Receiver algorithm
For TDD FR1 with eCPRI fronthaul interface, DU shall perform TOE reliability determination for positioning SRS. If the estimated SINR is higher than a threshold, i.e. 10 ∗ log10(SINR) > Threshold, TO estimate would be determined as reliable. Otherwise, TO estimate is determined as unreliable.

The reliability thresholds which need to meet the requirement of 1m TOE accuracy can be found in the table below. They are generated based on Combined TOE method, comb2 and 4 co-located Rx antennas with granularity 8PRB. Since SRS sequence length of comb4 is a half of comb2, the granularity in the table should be 16PRB for comb4. Therefore, Thr can be retrieved by the index Roundup (NPRB /(4 ∗ KTC)) from the table, where NPRB is SRS PRB number corresponding to the NRCELL.srsForPositioning.cSRS and KTC is comb number defined by NRCELL.srsForPositioning.comb.

In case of 2 co-located Rx antennas and Combined TOE method, 2dB offset needs to be added on top of Threshold.

In case of 1Rx antenna or Min TOE method, 4dB offset needs to added on top of Threshold.

[CB013273-A]In case of 8 co-located Rx antennas and Combined TOE method, -0.8dB offset needs to added on top of Threshold. [End CB013273-A]

Refer to 5GMax-5G New Radio Algorithm Specification Chapter 2.7.8.

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| SRS PRB Number | SINR Threshold |
| 8 | -2 |
| 16 | -4 |
| 24 | -8 |
| 32 | -9.1 |
| 40 | -10 |
| 48 | -11 |
| 56 | -11.5 |
| 64 | -12.1 |
| 72 | -12.6 |
| 80 | -13.1 |
| 88 | -13.6 |
| 96 | -14 |
| 104 | -14.5 |
| 112 | -14.7 |
| 120 | -14.8 |
| 128 | -15.1 |
| 136 | -15.6 |
| 144 | -15.5 |
| 152 | -15.7 |
| 160 | -16.1 |
| 168 | -16.3 |
| 176 | -16.4 |
| 184 | -16.8 |
| 192 | -17.1 |
| 200 | -17.1 |
| 208 | -17.1 |
| 216 | -17.3 |
| 224 | -17.7 |
| 232 | -18 |
| 240 | -18 |
| 248 | -18 |
| 256 | -18 |
| 264 | -18.4 |
| 272 | -18.2 |

---

##### 2.4.5.5.1.0-7 Receiver algorithm TO Compensation (ID: 11107823)

**2.4.5.5.1.0-7.0-1**  (ID: `11107825`)

Receiver algorithm
For TDD FR1 with eCPRI fronthaul interface, DU shall perform TO compensation(TOC) after TOE according to 5GMax-5G New Radio Algorithm Specification Chapter 2.7.3 in section “TO compensation”.

Note:

This algorithm will be used in future AoA and RSRP function, but not by TOE function.

---

##### 2.4.5.5.1.0-8 Receiver algorithm User Separation (ID: 11107828)

**2.4.5.5.1.0-8.0-1**  (ID: `11107830`)

Receiver algorithm
For TDD FR1 with eCPRI fronthaul interface, DU shall perform User Separation after TOC according to 5GMax-5G New Radio Algorithm Specification Chapter 2.7.4 in section “User Separation”.

Note:

This algorithm will be used in future AoA and RSRP function, but not by TOE function.

---

##### 2.4.5.5.1.0-9 Receiver algorithm Supported SRS for positioning algorithm (ID: 11107833)

**2.4.5.5.1.0-9.0-2**  (ID: `11107839`)

Receiver algorithm
The following L1 HW shall support the below algorithms for SRS for positioning:

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Algorithm | Thor |
| Inputs/Outputs/Algorithm | 5G\_UP\_ALG\_27294\_replaced([11107769](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koivc_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) |
| Receiver algorithm overview | 5G\_UP\_ALG\_27296\_replaced([11107777](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koivdvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) |
| Raw Channel Estimation | 5G\_UP\_ALG\_27298\_replaced([11107791](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koivefvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) |
| [CB007880-A]Positioning assistant measurements (SINR and UL-SRS-RSRP) [End CB007880-A] | [CB007880-A] 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29508\_replaced([11107807](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koivivvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))[End CB007880-A] |
| [CB007880-A-UPD002] TOE reliability determination[End CB007880-A-UPD002] | [CB007880-A-UPD002] 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31709\_replaced([11107812](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kojWgfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))[End CB007880-A-UPD002] |
| TO Estimation | 5G\_UP\_ALG\_27333\_replaced([11107795](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koivfvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) |
| TO Compensation | 5G\_UP\_ALG\_27624\_replaced([11107823](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kojWjvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) |
| User Separation | 5G\_UP\_ALG\_27626\_replaced([11107828](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kojWkPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) |
| First Feature | CB010332-B |

[CB010680-H]

NOTE: Algorithms above shall be executed for each TRP in case of Distributed Antenna mode.

[End CB010680-H]

## 2.4.6 Measurements

### 2.4.6.0-1 Measurements Document Owner Sebastien Bonnet (Nokia) (ID: 11107882)

### 2.4.6.1 Measurements for Beam Formed Signal - System "Spatial Streams Wise" vs. L1 Internal "Antenna-Wise" naming convention

#### 2.4.6.1.0-1 Measurements for Beam Formed Signal - System "Spatial Streams Wise" vs. L1 Internal "Antenna-Wise" naming convention Overview (ID: 11107886)

**2.4.6.1.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11107889`)

Measurements for Beam Formed Signal - System "Spatial Streams Wise" vs. L1 Internal "Antenna-Wise" naming convention
With Digital Beamforming (5G\_UP\_912\_replaced([11353600](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucAve_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), Measurements definition for Beam Formed Signal in next Chapters refer to the System vs. L1 internal naming convention, illustrated in 5G\_UP\_13322\_replaced([11354466](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucIrQPvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_UP\_13325\_replaced([11354476](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucIrQvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_UP\_13330\_replaced([11354491](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucIrRPvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

The measurements which are defined as "antenna-wise" (as per old, legay non BF product, convention) stand for "spatial-stream-wise" as described in 5G\_UP\_13330\_replaced([11354491](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucIrRPvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)):

* Antenna-wise TOE on PUSCH (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_6375\_replaced([11107978](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kolLtPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))
* Antenna-wise TOE on SRS (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7897\_replaced([11108071](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_komZ1fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))
* Antenna-wise FOE on PUSCH (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_6798\_replaced([11108146](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_konA7_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))
* Antenna-wise FOE on PUCCH (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_6801\_replaced([11108207](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kooPBvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))
* Antenna-wise UL Received power for PUSCH (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7090\_replaced([11108736](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kou8tvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))
* Antenna-wise UL Received power for SRS (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7102\_replaced([11108812](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kowK0fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))
* Antenna-wise SINR for PUSCH (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7168\_replaced([11108996](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koyACvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))
* Antenna-wise SINR for SRS (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7182\_replaced([11109097](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kozOMPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))
* Antenna-wise RSSI on PUSCH (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7048\_replaced([11108553](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kosgd_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))
* Antenna-wise noise power on PUSCH (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_17562\_replaced([11108367](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koqENfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))
* Antenna-wise vs CPRI/eCPRI/internal Spatial Stream Mapping (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1739\_replaced([11109505](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko4tufvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

Same L1 internal naming convention (i.e. "antenna-wise" as "spatial-stream-wise") applies for Invalid Measurements as described in related Chapters:

* Invalid Measurement Cases (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7403\_replaced([11109713](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko7J9_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))
* Invalid Power-Related Measurements for Case 1 (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7421\_replaced([11109729](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko7J_fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))
* Invalid Measurement - SINR (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1746\_replaced([11109764](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko7xC_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))
* Invalid Measurement - TOE (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7654\_replaced([11109778](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko8YEfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))
* Invalid Measurement - FOE (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8416\_replaced([11109788](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko8YFfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

---

11107902:
Time Alignment
5GNB estimates the initial Timing Advance (TA) for a given 5GUE based on the detected RACH preamble. Then, it sends TA command in random access response (RAR). Once 5GUE is in connected mode, 5GNB tracks continuously the TA of UL signals and sends TA command through MAC Control Element if correction is required.

Receive Rx timing

The 5GNB should control the UE timing in such a manner that the UE is slightly late relative to the 5GNB Rx window where the signal is processed (i.e. where the FFT is performed). This is to insure a margin is kept in the case where the UE is slightly in advance versus the expected timing (in the case where the UE is slightly late compared to the expected timing, the Cyclic Prefix also serves as a margin). The interest of the margin is to make sure that the full UE signal is received such that there is no distortion introduced by the conversion to frequency domain.

The Timing Advance (TA) Command indicates the number of TA units that 5GUE shall advance or delay. One (1) TA unit corresponds to [image-0][image\_desc]16Ts/2μ[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_3549.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0], where [image-1][image\_desc]The image contains the text "Ts" in a stylized font. The letters are rendered with a gradient effect, transitioning through various colors like orange, yellow, blue, and purple. The background is white, and the text is enclosed within a black border.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_3549.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1] has been defined in 5G\_UP\_IF\_15\_replaced([11491325](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_tRtPzPv1Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and μ is given in 5G\_UP\_IF\_21\_replaced([11491448](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_tRt23vv1Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Only relative delay shall be considered, i.e. the FFT window shift shall not be considered in TA command and the target delay shall be 0Ts.

---

#### 2.4.6.2.1 TO estimation on PRACH

##### 2.4.6.2.1.0-1 TO estimation on PRACH Overview (ID: 11107907)

**2.4.6.2.1.0-1.0-3**  (ID: `12775491`)

TO estimation on PRACH
Purpose:

DU shall perform initial timing offset estimation for each detected preamble from PRACH reception, see [11107895: Introduction](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koj9mfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9).

Input:

* The search window settings, refer to [11105853: Preamble Search Window](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koQbm_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Frm%2Fcm%2Fstream%2F_UBmNVp5EEe2A-5U7tzedRA)
* The detected preamble, refer to [11105872: Preamble Peak Detection](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koRCovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Frm%2Fcm%2Fstream%2F_UBmNVp5EEe2A-5U7tzedRA)
* The PRACH SCS and PUSCH SCS, refer to [11491448](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_tRt23vv1Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)
* [CB008577-A] NRCELL.actPrachProbing [End CB008577-A]

Algorithm:

For each detected preamble:

* DU shall calculate the initial timing offset by counting the number of bins from the peak value (see [11105872: Preamble Peak Detection](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koRCovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Frm%2Fcm%2Fstream%2F_UBmNVp5EEe2A-5U7tzedRA)) to its corresponding search window start (see [11105853: Preamble Search Window](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koQbm_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Frm%2Fcm%2Fstream%2F_UBmNVp5EEe2A-5U7tzedRA)), refer to [11107915](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koj9nvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) for the related algorithm in 5GMax algorithm specification (see [11101310: The 5Gmax algorithm specification document](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Frm%2Fcm%2Fstream%2F_UBmNVp5EEe2A-5U7tzedRA)).
* DU shall provide the initial timing offset in "Ns × Ts" unit:
* Ns = 16/2μ, where μ=μPUSCH corresponding to the PUSCH SCS. Rationale, initial timing offset unit changes from PRACH SCS based to PUSCH SCS based, which can reduce the error introduced by rounding and unit convertion (keep the rationale only in this release requirement).
* + Ts is defined in [11491325: General](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_tRtPzPv1Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Frm%2Fcm%2Fstream%2F_KjDNNu3wEe-rL47x_rCCmw).

For non beamforming, DU shall provide one measurement across all the valid RX antennas for each detected preamble.

[Before CB008577-A]

For beamforming, DU shall provide one measurement for all the valid RX antennas associated with one beam for each detected preamble.

[End CB008577-A]

[CB008577-A]

For beamforming (see [11106028](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_lGJSdvvHEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), DU shall provide one measurement per beam for each detected preamble:

* If PRACH probing is disabled (NRCELL.actPrachProbing=false), DU only detect same preamble index once from one beam per RO, DU shall provide one measurement for the valid RX antennas associated with the one beam.
* If PRACH probing is enabled (NRCELL.actPrachProbing=true), DU can detect same preamble index up to 4 times from 4 beams per RO, as such DU shall provide up to 4 measurements, each measurement for 2 valid RX antennas associated with one beam (see [11106062](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koTe5_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[End CB008577-A]

Refer to [11105836](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koQblfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) for the PRACH valid RX antennas. Note, the RX antennas means the spatial streams input to the receiver.

Output:

* The initial timing offset, in "Ns × Ts" unit.

Note, DU shall also report the peak metric (see [11108880: Peak Metric for PRACH](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kowx6PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Frm%2Fcm%2Fstream%2F_UBmNVp5EEe2A-5U7tzedRA)) associated to initial timing offset, and details about the usage of the PRACH timing measurements is in [11152587: RA Response Timing Advance command](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kshQgvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9).

For the supported L1 HWs, refer to [11107915: Supported measurement for TOE for PRACH](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koj9nvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9).

| GPU | supported | supported | TBC | CB016066-C |

* Details for the algorithm to estimate the initial timing offset on PRACH are provided in 5GMAX algorithm specification (see [11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

#### 2.4.6.2.2 TO estimation on PUSCH

##### 2.4.6.2.2.0-1 TO estimation on PUSCH Overview (ID: 11107936)

**2.4.6.2.2.0-1.0-7**  (ID: `12747828`)

TO estimation on PUSCH
DU **or Radio shall estimate short term (ST) timing offset (TO) for each scheduled UE on PUSCH and shall report the TO estimate (TOE) that is used as input to update the TA measurement, see [11150011](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksRY6fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ). Only one measurement is reported across all the RX antennas.**

For MU receiver, the DU in C8 **or the Radio in C16'4**and C16'8 (cf. [11094165](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmaBcPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) shall combine the (ST) TO metric on own UE beams only.\*\*\*\*

[CB008465-AA] When UL CoMP is activated (NRCELL.actNrUlComp = 'basic') and when the UE is scheduled for joint reception or for SINR measurements on candidate cells (see [12763065](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_dTd9AIOSEfCOGMMH109ICw?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)), DU shall combine the short-term TO metric from all the receiving antennas of the serving cell only (see [12763061](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_gSJKEIOREfCOGMMH109ICw?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)). [End CB008465-AA]

For Thor 7-2e HW the Radio shall compute the Antenna-wise measurements as output from new Raw CEs (refer to [11102253](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knfmmfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

Additionally:

* DU or Radio reports the peak amplitude of each estimate to 5G-L2-PS.

Details about the usage of the PUSCH timing measurements in 5G-L2-PS are in [11150011](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksRY6fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

Details about PUSCH timing offset estimate algorithm are given in [11102411](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kniC0PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

The unit of TOE reported to 5G-L2-PS is equal to Tsμ where Tsμ = (1/2μ) \* Ts;

* Ts is defined in [11491325](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_tRtPzPv1Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ).
* μ  is defined in [11491448](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_tRt23vv1Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ).

DU or Radio shall report invalid measurement for TOE and its associated peak amplitude if the estimate is out of the allowed range, see [11109778](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko8YEfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

Note:

- For eCPRI 7-2e, Radio shall do the measurement and report result to 5G-L2-PS via DU, otherwise DU shall do the measurement and report to 5G\_L2\_PS.

TO estimation on PUSCH

| Loner | supported | supported | 2.3.4.1 and 2.8.6 |  |
| Loki | supported | supported | 2.3.4.1 and 2.8.6 |  |

\*Details for the algorithm to estimate the initial timing offset on PUSCH are provided in 5GMAX algorithm specification (see [11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for 23R1 version.

#### 2.4.6.2.3 Antenna-wise TO estimation on PUSCH

##### 2.4.6.2.3.0-1 Antenna-wise TO estimation on PUSCH Overview (ID: 11107978)

**2.4.6.2.3.0-1.0-8**  (ID: `13323488`)

Antenna-wise TO estimation on PUSCH
Antenna-wise (meaning "spatial-stream-wise" as per [11107886](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koj9lvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) or per RX Antenna stream, ST TOE is computed and reported to 5G-L2-PS for each scheduled UE on PUSCH. This estimate is used for TDD FR1 and FDD beamforming cells where TOE may depend on the beam.

The unit of antenna-wise TOE reported to 5G-L2-PS is equal to Tsμ where Tsμ = (1/2μ) \* Ts;

* Ts is defined in [11491325](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_tRtPzPv1Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ).
* μ  is defined in [11491448](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_tRt23vv1Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)

The peak amplitude of each estimate is also reported to 5G-L2-PS

Mapping between antenna-wise TOE and the spatial stream is given in [11109508](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko4tuvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

For MU receiver (cf. [11104114](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kn4oLPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)**and [11104177](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kn52QvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), mapping between antenna-wise TOE and the spatial stream is given in [11109559](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko5Uy_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) and [11109576](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko5Uz_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).**

[CB008465-AA] When UL CoMP is activated (NRCELL.actNrUlComp = 'basic'):

* When the UE is configured for joint reception (see [12763065](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_dTd9AIOSEfCOGMMH109ICw?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)), DU shall report antenna-wise ST TOE from all the receiving antennas of the serving and the helper cell (see [12763061](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_gSJKEIOREfCOGMMH109ICw?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)). The mapping of antenna-wise ST TOE to spatial streams of the serving and helper cell is given in [12775997](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_l3HqEYybEfCrqOdGJPoCRA?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) (\*).
* When the UE is configured for SINR measurements on candidate cells, DU shall report antenna-wise ST TOE from the receiving antennas of the serving cell,

\*: Antenna-wise ST TOE measurements from helper cell spatial streams are not used by L2-PS.

[End CB008465-AA]

DU **or Radio** shall report invalid measurement for antenna-wise TOE and its associated peak amplitude, see [11109778](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko8YEfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ):\*\*\*\*

* if the estimate is out of the allowed range
* **or** for each antenna when the Rx is failed (typically the case of RU lost or fiber broken or Rx failure, refer to [11377147](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ueUcnvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) and [11380144](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_uek7RPvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))\*\*\*\*
* **or for each antenna of other UE beams when UE is paired in 8Rx** or 16RX MU receiver (see. [11104193](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kn52R_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ), [11109576](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko5Uz_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))\*\*\*\*

For Thor 7-2e HW the Radio shall compute the Antenna-wise measurements as output from new Raw CEs (refer to [11102253](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knfmmfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

For L1 HW supported measurement refer to [11108000](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kolLvPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)

**NOTE:**

* This measurement is not used in 5G-L2-PS
* For eCPRI 7-2e, Radio shall do the measurement and report result to 5G-L2-PS via DU, otherwise DU shall do the measurement and report to 5G\_L2\_PS.

Spec. section** | **First Feature** |

#### 2.4.6.2.4 TO estimation on PUCCH

##### 2.4.6.2.4.0-1 TO estimation on PUCCH Overview (ID: 11108024)

**2.4.6.2.4.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11108026`)

TO estimation on PUCCH
5G-L1-UL estimates one short term (ST) timing offset (TO) on PUCCH and reports the TO estimate (TOE) to 5G-L2-PS to update the TA measurement. Only one measurement is reported across all the RX antennas.

Details about PUCCH timing offset estimate algorithm are given in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1338\_replaced([11105128](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koGqkvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Additionally, 5G-L1-UL reports the peak amplitude (or the correlation peak) of each estimate to 5G-L2-PS. Only one measurement is reported across al the RX antennas.

Details about the usage of the PUCCH timing measurements in 5G-L2-PS are in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3303\_replaced([11150011](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksRY6fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

The unit of TOE reported to 5G-L2-PS is equal to Tsμ where Tsμ = (1/2μ) \* Ts;

* Ts is defined in 5G\_UP\_IF\_15\_replaced([11491325](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_tRtPzPv1Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
* μ  is defined in 5G\_UP\_IF\_21\_replaced([11491448](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_tRt23vv1Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))

TOE on PUCCH is peformed only when:

- a PUCCH format 2 is scheduled over 3 RBs or more, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1349\_replaced([11105155](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koGqnfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

- a PUCCH format 3 is scheduled with 1 and 2 DMRS on all RBs, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1353\_replaced([11105165](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koHRofvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

5G-L1-UL does not compute the TOE when the UE is granted with one of the below configurations. It shall inform 5G-L2-PS that the reported measurement is invalid for TOE and its associated peak amplitude, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7654\_replaced([11109778](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko8YEfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)):

* PUCCH Format0 (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1341\_replaced([11105135](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koGqlfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and Format1 (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1345\_replaced([11105145](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koGqmfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))
* PUCCH Format2 with 1 or 2PRBs (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1349\_replaced([11105155](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koGqnfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

Also, 5G-L1-UL shall report invalid measurement for TOE and its associated peak amplitude if the estimate is out of the allowed range, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7654\_replaced([11109778](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko8YEfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

NOTE: For Format3, it is assumed that all the supported configurations always fulfill the minimum requirement to provide a valid ST TOE.

Supported PUCCH formats could be deduced from 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1139\_replaced([11104445](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kn85mfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

#### 2.4.6.2.5 TO estimation on SRS

##### 2.4.6.2.5.0-1 TO estimation on SRS Overview (ID: 11108046)

**2.4.6.2.5.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `11108051`)

TO estimation on SRS
DU(L1) **[CB007595-C]or Radio(L1)** [End CB007595-C] estimates one short term (ST) timing offset (TO) on SRS and reports the TO estimate (TOE) to 5G-L2-PS to update the TA measurement. Only one measurement is reported across all the RX antennas.\*\*\*\*

Additionally, DU(L1) **[CB007595-C]or Radio(L1)** [End CB007595-C] reports the peak amplitude (or the correlation peak) of each estimate to 5G-L2-PS. Only one measurement is reported across al the RX antennas.\*\*\*\*

The unit of TOE reported to 5G-L2-PS is equal to Tc.

Also, DU(L1) **[CB007595-C]or Radio(L1)** [End CB007595-C] shall report invalid measurement for TOE and its associated peak amplitude if the estimate is out of the allowed range, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7654\_replaced([11109778](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko8YEfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).\*\*\*\*

**NOTE: SRS timing measurements are currently used only for debug purposes.**

**[CB007595-C]**

**NOTE2:**

- "5G-L1-UL" is changed to "DU(L1)" since this version as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_20317\_replaced([11094116](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmZaYfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). (Note kept only in this version)

- For eCPRI 7-2e, Radio(L1) shall do the measurements and report results to 5G-L2-PS via DU(L1), otherwise DU(L1) shall do the measurements and report to 5G\_L2\_PS.

**[End CB007595-C]**

---

**2.4.6.2.5.0-2.0-5**  (ID: `13341647`)

TO estimation on SRS
Supported measurement for TOE on SRS

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **L1 HW** | **TOE on SRS** **([11108046](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kolyzPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))** | **Peak metric** **([11108046](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kolyzPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))** | **5GMAX algo. Spec. section**\* | **First Feature** |
| Loner | supported\*\* | supported\*\* | sections 2.6.4 and 2.7.6 |  |
| Loki | supported\*\* | supported\*\* | sections 2.6.4 and 2.7.6 |  |
| Thor | supported\*\* | supported\*\* | sections 2.6.4 and 2.7.6 | CB007487-D |
| **Thor (Radio 7-2e)** | supported\*\* | supported\*\* | sections 2.6.4 and 2.7.6 | CB007595-C |
| GPU | supported | supported | ~~TBC~~ section 2.6.4 | CB016066-C |

\*Details for the algorithm to estimate the initial timing offset on SRS are provided in 5GMAX algorithm specification ([11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

#### 2.4.6.2.6 Antenna-wise TO estimation on SRS

##### 2.4.6.2.6.0-1 Antenna-wise TO estimation on SRS Overview (ID: 11108071)

**2.4.6.2.6.0-1.0-4**  (ID: `11108084`)

Antenna-wise TO estimation on SRS
DU(L1) [CB007595-C] or Radio(L1) [End CB007595-C] estimates one antenna-wise [CNI-103796-K] (meaning "spatial-stream-wise" as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29129\_replaced([11107886](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koj9lvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) [End CNI-103796-K]  or per RX antenna stream, short term (ST) timing offset (TO) on SRS and reports the TO estimate (TOE) to 5G-L2-PS to update the TA measurement. Additionally, DU(L1) [CB007595-C]or Radio(L1) [End CB007595-C] reports the peak amplitude (or the correlation peak) of each estimate to 5G-L2-PS. This estimate is used for TDD FR1 beamforming cells where TOE may depend on the beam.

The unit of antenna-wise TOE reported to 5G-L2-PS is equal to Tsμ where Tsμ = (1/2μ) \* Ts;

* Ts is defined in 5G\_UP\_IF\_15\_replaced([11491325](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_tRtPzPv1Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
* μ  is defined in 5G\_UP\_IF\_21\_replaced([11491448](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_tRt23vv1Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))

Mapping between antenna-wise TOE and the spatial stream is given in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7236\_replaced([11109508](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko4tuvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

For MU receiver (cf. 5G\_UP\_ALG\_9417\_replaced([11104114](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kn4oLPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_UP\_ALG\_19605\_replaced([11104177](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kn52QvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), mapping between antenna-wise TOE and the spatial stream is given in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8995\_replaced([11109559](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko5Uy_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_19621\_replaced([11109576](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko5Uz_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

DU(L1) [CB007595-C]or Radio(L1) [End CB007595-C] shall report invalid measurement for antenna-wise TOE and its associated peak amplitude, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7654\_replaced([11109778](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko8YEfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)):

* if the estimate is out of the allowed range
* or for each antenna when the Rx is failed (typically the case of RU lost or fiber broken or Rx failure, refer to 5G\_UP\_6136\_replaced([11377147](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ueUcnvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_6137\_replaced([11380144](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_uek7RPvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

For L1 HW supported measurement refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7899\_replaced([11108086](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_komZ2vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

NOTE: This measurement is not used in 5G-L2-PS

[CB007595-C]

NOTE2:

- "5G-L1-UL" is changed to "DU(L1)" since this version as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_20317\_replaced([11094116](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmZaYfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). (Note kept only in this version)

- For eCPRI 7-2e, Radio(L1) shall do the measurements and report results to 5G-L2-PS via DU(L1), otherwise DU(L1) shall do the measurements and report to 5G\_L2\_PS.

[End CB007595-C]

---

### 2.4.6.3 Carrier Frequency Offset

#### 2.4.6.3.1 Frequency Offset estimate on PUSCH

##### 2.4.6.3.1.0-1 Frequency Offset estimate on PUSCH Overview (ID: 11108106)

**2.4.6.3.1.0-1.0-7**  (ID: `12774569`)

Frequency Offset estimate on PUSCH
For each scheduled UE, DU or Radio estimates a short term (ST) CFO metric (combined overall all the antennas). Then, it reports the estimated value to 5G-L2-PS. Processing of the metric in L2-PS is described in [11150515](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksT1LvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ). The most up-to-date metric should be then transferred to DU or Radio for CFO compensation on PUSCH.

For MU receiver, the DU in C8, or the Radio in C16'4 **and C16'8 (cf. [11094165](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmaBcPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) shall combine the (ST) CFO metric on own UE beams only.**

For Thor 7-2e HW the Radio shall compute the (ST) CFO metric based on new Raw Channel Estimates (refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_28100\_replaced([11102253](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knfmmfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

No CFO estimation is required from PRACH and no CFO compensation is expected for RACH PUSCH scheduled by RAR UL grant (where the coding rate is sufficiently low to ignore the distortion caused by CFO), see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3337\_replaced([11151189](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksXfi_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

[CB008465-AA] When UL CoMP is activated (NRCELL.actNrUlComp = 'basic') and when the UE is scheduled for joint reception or for SINR measurements on candidate cells (see [12763065](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_dTd9AIOSEfCOGMMH109ICw?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)), DU shall report the combined short-term ST CFO metric from all the receiving antennas of the serving cell only (see [12763061](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_gSJKEIOREfCOGMMH109ICw?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)). [End CB008465-AA]

For more details about FOE on PUSCH, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_886\_replaced([11102482](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knip6vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Note:

- For eCPRI 7-2e, Radio shall do the measurement and report result to 5G-L2-PS via DU, otherwise DU shall do the measurement and report to 5G\_L2\_PS.

---

**2.4.6.3.1.0-2.0-7**  (ID: `12774570`)

Frequency Offset estimate on PUSCH
Supported measurement for FOE for PUSCH:

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **L1 HW** | **FOE on PUSCH***([11108106](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_konA4fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))*\* | **5GMAX algo. Spec. section**\* | **First Feature** |
| Loner | supported\*\* | Section 2.3.3.3 |  |
| Loki | supported\*\* | Section 2.3.3.3 |  |
| Thor | supported\*\* [CB008465-AA] (Note 2) [End CB008465-AA] | Section 2.3.3.5 | CB007487-B CB007487-C CB007487-D |
| Thor (Radio 7-2e) | supported(Note)\*\* | Section 2.3.3.5 | CB007595-B |

(Note) Supported as output from new Raw CEs (refer to [11102253](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knfmmfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

#### 2.4.6.3.2 Antenna-wise Frequency Offset estimate on PUSCH

##### 2.4.6.3.2.0-1 Antenna-wise Frequency Offset estimate on PUSCH Overview (ID: 11108146)

**2.4.6.3.2.0-1.0-8**  (ID: `13323492`)

Antenna-wise Frequency Offset estimate on PUSCH
Additionally, for each scheduled UE on PUSCH, per antenna **(meaning "spatial-stream-wise" as per [11107886](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koj9lvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), or per beam, ST CFO metric is computed and reported to 5G-L2-PS. These estimates are used for TDD FR1 and FDD beamforming cells where FO could be beam dependent for a given UE.**

DU or Radio**reports the same ST FOE for each antenna, when FOE is PTRS based with DFT-s-OFDM (see [11102672](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knltNPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).**

Details about CFO LT averaging in 5G-L2-PS is given in [11150688](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksVDQ_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

Mapping between antenna-wise FOE and the spatial stream is given in [11109508](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko4tuvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

For MU receiver (cf. [11104114](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kn4oLPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ), [11104177](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kn52QvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), mapping between antenna-wise FOE power and the spatial stream is given in [11109559](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko5Uy_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) and [11109576](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko5Uz_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

[CB008465-AA] When UL CoMP is activated (NRCELL.actNrUlComp = 'basic'):

* When the UE is configured for joint reception (see [12763065](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_dTd9AIOSEfCOGMMH109ICw?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)), DU shall report antenna-wise ST CFO from all the receiving antennas of the serving and the helper cell (see [12763061](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_gSJKEIOREfCOGMMH109ICw?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)). The mapping of antenna-wise ST CFO to spatial streams of the serving and helper cell is given in [12775997](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_l3HqEYybEfCrqOdGJPoCRA?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9).
* When the UE is configured for SINR measurements on candidate cells, DU shall report antenna-wise ST CFO from the receiving antennas of the serving cell,

[End CB008465-AA]

DU or Radio shall report INVALID value, see [11109788](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko8YFfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ):

* for each antenna, when the Rx is failed (typically the case of RU lost or fiber broken or Rx failure, refer to [11377147](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ueUcnvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) and [11380144](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_uek7RPvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
* for each antenna of other UE beams when UE is paired in 8Rx or 16RX MU receiver (see. [11104193](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kn52R_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ), [11109576](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko5Uz_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

For Thor 7-2e HW the Radio shall compute the Antenna-wise measurements as output from new Raw CEs (refer to [11102253](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knfmmfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Note:

* For eCPRI 7-2e, Radio shall do the measurement and report result to 5G-L2-PS via DU, otherwise DU shall do the measurement and report to 5G\_L2\_PS.
* PTRS is not supported in eCPRI 7-2e.  Update above is to make it more future proofing.

For L1 HW supported measurement refer to [11108168](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_konn9_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

---

#### 2.4.6.3.3 Frequency Offset estimate on PUCCH

##### 2.4.6.3.3.0-1 Frequency Offset estimate on PUCCH Overview (ID: 11108191)

**2.4.6.3.3.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11108193`)

Frequency Offset estimate on PUCCH
For each scheduled UE on PUCCH, 5GNB 5G-L1-UL estimates a short term (ST) FOE metric (combined overall all the antennas) on PUCCH Format3. Then, it reports the estimated values to 5G-L2-PS. Processing of the metric in L2-PS is described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3322\_replaced([11150515](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksT1LvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). The most up-to-date LT FOE metric should be then transferred to 5G-L1-UL for CFO compensation on PUCCH.

For more details about FOE on PUCCH F3, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1380\_replaced([11105355](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koJt6_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Details about FOE LT averaging in 5G-L2-PS is given in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3324\_replaced([11150688](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksVDQ_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

##### 2.4.6.3.3.0-2 Frequency Offset estimate on PUCCH Supported measurement FOE for PUCCH (ID: 11108196)

**2.4.6.3.3.0-2.0-2**  (ID: `11108202`)

Frequency Offset estimate on PUCCH
Supported measurement for FOE on PUCCH Format3:

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| L1 HW | ST FOE all antennas (5G\_UP\_ALG\_8000\_replaced([11108191](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kooPAPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) | 5GMAX algo. Spec. section\* | First Feature |
| Loner | - | - |  |
| Loki | supported\*\* | Section 2.4.4.1 | CB008792-D |
| vDU |  | - |  |
| Thor | supported\*\* | Section 2.4.4.1 | CB008827-5G-A |

\*Details for the algorithm to estimate short term carrier frequency offset on PUSCH are provided in 5GMAX algorithm specification (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_695\_replaced([11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

---

#### 2.4.6.3.4 Antenna-wise Frequency Offset Estimate on PUCCH

##### 2.4.6.3.4.0-1 Antenna-wise Frequency Offset Estimate on PUCCH Overview (ID: 11108207)

**2.4.6.3.4.0-1.0-3**  (ID: `11108215`)

Antenna-wise Frequency Offset Estimate on PUCCH
For each scheduled UE on PUCCH, 5GNB 5G-L1-UL estimates a short term (ST) antenna-wise [CNI-103796-K] (meaning "spatial-stream-wise" as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29129\_replaced([11107886](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koj9lvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) [End CNI-103796-K] FOE metric on PUCCH Format3. Then, it reports the estimated values to 5G-L2-PS. Processing of the metric in L2-PS is described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3322\_replaced([11150515](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksT1LvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). The most up-to-date LT FOE metric should be then transferred to 5G-L1-UL for CFO compensation on PUCCH.

For beamforming cells, the antenna-wise FOE means FOE per beam, where FO could be beam dependent for a given UE

Details about FOE LT averaging in 5G-L2-PS is given in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3324\_replaced([11150688](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksVDQ_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Mapping between antenna-wise FOE and the spatial stream is given in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7236\_replaced([11109508](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko4tuvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

For each antenna, when the Rx is failed (typically the case of RU lost or fiber broken or Rx failure, refer to 5G\_UP\_6136\_replaced([11377147](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ueUcnvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_6137\_replaced([11380144](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_uek7RPvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), 5G-L1-UL shall report INVALID value, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8416\_replaced([11109788](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko8YFfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

For L1 HW supported measurement refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7603\_replaced([11108218](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kooPC_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

---

##### 2.4.6.3.4.0-2 Antenna-wise Frequency Offset Estimate on PUCCH Supported measurement for Antenna-wise FOE for PUCCH (ID: 11108218)

**2.4.6.3.4.0-2.0-4**  (ID: `11108229`)

Antenna-wise Frequency Offset Estimate on PUCCH
Supported measurement for Antenna-wise for FOE on PUCCH Format3:

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| L1 HW | ST FOE per antenna (5G\_UP\_ALG\_6801\_replaced([11108207](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kooPBvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) | 5GMAX algo. Spec. section\* | First Feature |
| Loner | - | - |  |
| Loki | supported\*\* | Section 2.4.4.1 | CB008792-D |
| vDU | - | - |  |
| Thor | supported\*\* | Section 2.4.4.1 | CB008827-5G-A |

\*Details for the algorithm to estimate short term carrier frequency offset on PUSCH are provided in 5GMAX algorithm specification (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_695\_replaced([11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

\*\* For antenna-wise invalid measurement(5G\_UP\_ALG\_6801\_replaced([11108207](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kooPBvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) in case of one or more Rx are failed, it is supported by Loki  [CB009029-5G-A] and Thor [End CB009029-5G-A] L1 HW.

---

### 2.4.6.4 Received Interference and Thermal Noise Power

#### 2.4.6.4.1 Overview

##### 2.4.6.4.1.0-1 Overview (ID: 11108237)

**2.4.6.4.1.0-1.0-5**  (ID: `13133164`)

Overview
Purpose:

The Estimate of UL **Interference and Thermal Noise Power.**

Actor: DU for PUCCH Channel and PRACH Signal, DU for non eCPRI 7-2e FH or Radio for eCPRI 7-2e FH for PUSCH Channel,

* shall perform the following algorithm for each:
* Configured subcell
* Channel of each configured subcell.

Inputs:

* Reference Signal after Raw Channel Estimate and Layer separation Processing steps ([11102011](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kndxY_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
* For eCPRI 7-2e, and for PUSCH only:
* Split Panel ([11354596](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucJSWfvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) Beamforming Configuration ([11155345](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksvS8vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) and [11155374](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksvS9PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) with
* Compensation factor defined in section §2.8 of 5Gmax algorithm specifications (see [11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and in section §5.3.1.1 of Massive MIMO Algorithm Specification (see [11101361](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcsvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[CB008577-C]

* For FDD BF and PUSCH only: split panel beamforming compensation factor defined in §2.8 of 5Gmax algorithm specifications (see [11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and in section §5.3.1.1 of Massive MIMO Algorithm Specification (see[11101361](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcsvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[End CB008577-C]

Algorithm:

For each configured subcell, DU or Radio shall:

* estimate one measurement for the **Interference and Thermal Noise Power (NOTE\*)**
* average it over all the PRBs
* average it over all the layers
* aggregate it over all the receiving spatial streams
* normalize it to one PRB

For 7-2e FH, Radio shall:

- compute the Interference and Thermal Noise Power based on the output of new Raw CEs algorithm (refer to [11102253](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knfmmfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

- apply the compensation factor, when Split panel configured.

[CB008577-C]

For FDD BF, DU shall apply the compensation factor for PUSCH when split panel beamforming configured (see [11354596](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucJSWfvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)).

[End CB008577-C]

Output:

- **Received Interference and Thermal Noise Power measurement per subcell for a given UL channel.**

NOTE\*: the thermal noise and the interference will be measured together, i.e. they won't be resolved independently.

Note:

* For eCPRI 7-2e, Radio shall do the measurement entity for PUSCH and report result to 5G-L2-PS via DU, otherwise DU shall do the measurement and report to 5G-L2-PS.

12747862:
Noise power on PUSCH
For each configured subcell, DU or Radio shall estimate the noise (interference + thermal noise) power for all the allocated PRBs resources on both used and unused DMRS REs**.**

DU or Radio shall average each measurement over all the allocated PRBs, over all the available layers (see [11103270](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kntpGPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and aggregate over all the receiving antennas or only on own UE antennas in case of pairing for C8 or C16'2 or C16'4 or C16'8 subcells (cf. [11094165](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmaBcPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[CB008465-AA] When UL CoMP is activated (NRCELL.actNrUlComp = 'basic') and when a UE is configured for joint reception or SINR measurements on candidate cells (see [12763065](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_dTd9AIOSEfCOGMMH109ICw?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)), DU shall aggregate its contribution to noise measurement over all the receiving antennas of the serving cell only (see [12763061](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_gSJKEIOREfCOGMMH109ICw?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)).

Rationale: Addition of contributions of helper cells to noise measurement would increase noise level, which is not expected for UL CoMP. [End CB008465-AA]

Noise component computed on **unused DMRS REs component is forced to zero** on UE paired antennas in case of MU pairing for C8 subcells **([11109576](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko5Uz_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) or C16'4 or C16'8 subcells.**\*\*\*\*

For 7-2e FH, the Radio shall :

- compute the Noise Power based on the output of new Raw CEs algorithm (refer to [11102253](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knfmmfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

- apply the compensation factor defined in section §2.8 of 5Gmax algorithm specifications (see [11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and in section §5.3.1.1 of Massive MIMO Algorithm Specification (see [11101361](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcsvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), when Split panel beamforming ([11354596](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucJSWfvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) configured ([11155345](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksvS8vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and ([11155374](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksvS9PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

For FDD BF, DU shall apply the split panel compensation factor defined in section §2.8 of 5Gmax algorithm specifications (see [11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and in section §5.3.1.1 of Massive MIMO Algorithm Specification (see [11101361](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcsvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), when split panel beamforming configured (see [11354596](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucJSWfvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)).

Then, one measurement normalized to one PRB shall be reported to 5G-L2-PS. The unit of this estimate is dBm.

For CPRI and eCPRI, additional compensation shall be performed, see [11109661](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko6i5_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

**NOTE: More details about the usage of this measurement in 5G-L2-PS are in [11119117](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqIq5PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) and [11119228](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqJR8PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ). This noise called [image-0][image\_desc]The image contains the text "K1 alloc" in a blurry, light-colored font against a white background. The text is slightly pixelated and has a subtle blue and yellow halo effect.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_3678.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0] in 5GMAX specification is averaged among all UEs (including paired UEs).**

**NOTE2:**

This noise is computed on both used DMRS resource elements (RE) and unused DMRS REs in UE allocated PRBs and may include thermal noise +intercelll interference+intracell interference (depending on UE pairing). Another noise power is computed on **used DMRS REs only cf. [11108307](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kopdJvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).**

NOTE 3:

- For eCPRI 7-2e, Radio shall do the measurement and report result to 5G-L2-PS via DU, otherwise DU shall do the measurement and report to 5G\_L2\_PS.

12747876:
