12747876:
Noise power on PUSCH per PRB
For each configured subcell for each slot, DU and Radio shall estimate the noise (interference + thermal noise) power per PRB for all the configured/allocated PRBs in the current slot for real and fake UE, with:

* Each of these estimates is averaged over all the available layers (see [11103270](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kntpGPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and averaged over all the receiving antennas (spatial streams). See [11108347](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kopdLfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).
* The unit of the estimates is W (Watt) for the Radio (for eCPRI 7-2e) and in dBm for the DU.

[CB008465-A] When UL CoMP is activated (NRCELL.actNrUlComp = 'basic') and when a UE is scheduled for joint reception SINR measurements on candidate cells (see [12763065](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_dTd9AIOSEfCOGMMH109ICw?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)), DU shall perform averaging of the noise on its allocated PRBs over all the receiving antennas of the serving cell only (see [12763061](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_gSJKEIOREfCOGMMH109ICw?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)).

Rationale: UL CoMP activation shall not have impact on noise and interference PM counters. **[End CB008465-A]**

For CPRI and eCPRI, DU and Radio (for eCPRI 7-2e) shall perform additional compensation shall be performed, see [11109661](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko6i5_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

For eCPRI 7-2e the Radio shall :

* compute the Noise Power per PRB based on the output of new Raw CEs algorithm (refer to [11102253](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knfmmfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
* apply the compensation factor defined in section §2.8 of 5Gmax algorithm specifications (see [11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and in section §5.3.1.1 of Massive MIMO Algorithm Specification (see [11101361](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcsvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), when Split panel beamforming ([11354596](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucJSWfvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) configured ([11155345](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksvS8vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) and [11155374](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksvS9PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

For FDD BF, DU shall apply the split panel compensation factor defined in section §2.8 of 5Gmax algorithm specifications (see [11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and in section §5.3.1.1 of Massive MIMO Algorithm Specification (see [11101361](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcsvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), when split panel beamforming configured (see [11354596](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucJSWfvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)).

The noise estimation algorithm on PUSCH is described in the 5Gmax algorithm specification (see [11108286](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koo2JPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

When only zero-valued samples are got from the radio module or (e)CPRI interface for all the receiving antennas (spatial streams) (e.g. broken antenna scenario or protection switching in TSN), or in case of noisy measurement, DU and Radio shall report INVALID value, see [11109729](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko7J_fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

NOTE: noise power per PRB for fake UE is not used from E2E or to update the counters, see [11180157](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27K80vvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ). Refer to [11108683](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kouVo_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) for more details about the fake UE.

**NOTE: The estimates of the noise power per PRB are used** for calculating the PM counters and in L2 power control function.\*\*\*\*

The noise power per PRB **on used DMRS REs only on the allocated PRBs resources gives level of thermal noise** + inter cell interference. It shall be used in L2 power control function for MU MIMO (cf. [11119117](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqIq5PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).\*\*\*\*

If MU-MIMO is enabled, DU and Radio PRB-wise noise power is averaged over all UEs sharing the same PRB.

Rationale

Depending on DMRS logical antenna ports, DMRS of UE shall use half REs as per figure below. Same CDM group 0/1 or 2/3 is prefered allocation in SU MIMO. Supported DMRS port allocation in UL MU MIMO is as [11104156](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kn5POvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

[image-0][image\_desc]The image displays a grid representing a resource allocation scheme, likely for wireless communication. The grid is oriented vertically, with the label "PRB" (Physical Resource Block) indicated by a double-headed arrow along the left side, suggesting the vertical axis represents PRBs. The grid itself is composed of many small squares, forming rows and columns.
A single column on the left side of the grid is highlighted with alternating gray and yellow squares. A legend at the top of the image explains the color coding:
- Gray squares represent "Antenna port 0/1".
- Yellow squares represent "Antenna port 2/3".
The text "DMRS Configuration Type 1" is positioned to the left of the grid, indicating the specific configuration being illustrated. The highlighted column shows a pattern of alternating gray and yellow squares, starting with gray at the top and ending with yellow at the bottom. This pattern suggests a specific distribution of reference signals across different antenna ports within the PRBs.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_3693.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

For SU MIMO UE or UEs paired within a single subcell (with same CDM group), noise measured on unused DMRS REs provides additional accuracy to measure thermal noise+ intercell interference.

For UEs pairing over 2 subcellls (with different CDM groups), noise measured **on unused DMRS REs provides intracell interference from the other CDM group** on top of thermal noise+ intercell interference.\*\*\*\*

Therefore noise [image-1][image\_desc]The image contains the text "K1 alloc". The text is in a dark color against a white background. The letters are slightly blurred, and there is a subtle rainbow effect around the edges of the characters.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_3693.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1] in **[11108250](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koo2FvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) computed on allocated PRBs on both used DMRS resource elements (RE)** and unused REs may include thermal noise +intercelll interference+intracell interference for UEs pairing over 2 subcellls.\*\*\*\*

##### 2.4.6.4.3.0-2 Noise power on PUSCH per PRB Supported measurement for noise power on PUSCH per PRB (ID: 11108347)

**2.4.6.4.3.0-2.0-6**  (ID: `12776680`)

Noise power on PUSCH per PRB
Supported measurement for noise power on PUSCH per PRB

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **L1 HW** | **Noise power on PUSCH per PRB ([11108309](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kopdJ_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))** | **5GMAX algo. Spec. section**\* | **First Feature** |
| Loner | - | - | - |
| Loki | supported | Section 2.8.3.2 | CB008021-A |
| Thor | supported **[CB008465-AA] (Note 2)** [End CB008465-AA]\*\*\*\* | Section 2.8.3.2 | CB009027-5G-A |
| Thor(Radio 7-2e) | supported (Note 1) | Section 2.8.3.2 | CB009514-F |
| GPU | supported (Note 1) | Section 2.8.3.2 | **CB016062-C** |

\*Details for the algorithm to estimate noise power on PUSCH are provided in 5GMAX algorithm specification (see [11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

For Loki, Thor [CB016062-C] and GPU [End CB016062-C] and **Thor (Radio 7-2e)** **L1 HW, additional update for Noise power on PUSCH per PRB as per [11108309](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kopdJ_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) (the noise power measurements are averaged over all the valid antennas (spatial streams)).**

Note 1: Supported as output from new Raw CEs (refer to [11102253](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knfmmfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) with compensation factor in Split Panel Beam Forming Mode.

[CB008465-AA] Note 2: Also supported with UL CoMP joint reception. [End CB008465-AA]

#### 2.4.6.4.4 Antenna-wise Noise power on PUSCH

##### 2.4.6.4.4.0-1 Antenna-wise Noise power on PUSCH Overview (ID: 11108367)

**2.4.6.4.4.0-1.0-9**  (ID: `12776693`)

Antenna-wise Noise power on PUSCH
For each configured subcell for each uplink slot, DU**or Radio shall estimate an antenna-wise (meaning "spatial-stream-wise" as per [11107886](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koj9lvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) noise (interference + thermal noise) power for each scheduled UE on PUSCH (real or fake UE).**

The UE antenna-wise measurement is averaged over all the available layers (see [11103270](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kntpGPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and normalized to one PRB (after the averaging over the PRBs allocated to this UE). The unit of the estimates is W (Watt). The detailed formula and algorithm are defined in 5GMAX algorithm specification (sections 2.7.3.1).

For CPRI and eCPRI, additional compensation shall be performed, see [11109661](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko6i5_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

When there is no PUSCH transmission, DU**or Radio**shall report INVALID value, see [11109729](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko7J_fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).\*\*\*\*

**NOTE: The estimates of the noise power per antenna (spatial stream) are initially intended for calculating the PM counters, see [11108683](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kouVo_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).**

Mapping between antenna-wise noise power and the spatial stream is given in [11109508](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko4tuvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

For MU receiver (cf. [11104114](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kn4oLPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ), [11104177](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kn52QvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)):

* mapping between antenna-wise noise power and the spatial stream is given in [11109559](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko5Uy_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) and [11109576](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko5Uz_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)**.**

**- for C8 or C16'4  or C16'8  subcells, the Noise component computed on unused DMRS REs is forced to zero for spatial streams of paired UE in case of MU pairing ([11109576](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko5Uz_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).**

[CB008465-AA] When UL CoMP is activated (NRCELL.actNrUlComp = 'basic'):

* When the UE is configured for joint reception (see [12763065](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_dTd9AIOSEfCOGMMH109ICw?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)), DU shall report antenna-wise noise power from all the receiving antennas of the serving and the helper cell (see [12763061](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_gSJKEIOREfCOGMMH109ICw?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) (\*). The mapping of antenna-wise noise power to spatial streams of the serving and helper cell is given in [12775997](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_l3HqEYybEfCrqOdGJPoCRA?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9).
* When the UE is configured for SINR measurements on candidate cells, DU shall report antenna-wise noise power from the receiving antennas of the serving cell.

\*: Antenna-wise noise power measurements from helper cell are actually not used for the computation of RTWP counters (see [11108683](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kouVo_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)).

[End CB008465-AA]

For eCPRI 7-2e the Radio shall:

* compute the Antenna-wise measurements as output from new Raw CEs (refer to [11102253](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knfmmfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
* apply the compensation factor defined in section §2.8 of 5Gmax algorithm specifications (see [11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and in section §5.3.1.1 of Massive MIMO Algorithm Specification (see [11101361](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcsvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), when Split panel beamforming ([11354596](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucJSWfvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) configured ([11155345](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksvS8vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) and [11155374](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksvS9PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) in UL.

For FDD BF, DU shall apply the split panel compensation factor defined in section §2.8 of 5Gmax algorithm specifications (see [11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and in section §5.3.1.1 of Massive MIMO Algorithm Specification (see [11101361](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcsvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), when split panel beamforming configured (see [11354596](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucJSWfvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)).

For Thor 7-2e HW and NRBTS\NRCELL\numberOfRxBeamforming=16RX and NRBTS\NRCELL\NRULMUMIMO\ulMaxMuMimoLayer **= 8, with TD/FD-PDP mixture algorithm introduction (refer to [11102322](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kngNrfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) the Radio shall compute the Antenna-wise measurements as output from Time Domain only for the user’s own beams and from the Frequency Domain for the other beams.**

**NOTE:**

- For eCPRI 7-2e, Radio (for eCPRI 7-2e) shall do the measurement and report result to 5G-L2-PS via DU, otherwise DU shall do the measurement and report to 5G\_L2\_PS.

For L1 HW supported measurement refer to [11108390](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koqEPfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

---

##### 2.4.6.4.4.0-2 Antenna-wise Noise power on PUSCH Supported measurement for antenna-wise noise power on PUSCH (ID: 11108390)

**2.4.6.4.4.0-2.0-7**  (ID: `12776700`)

| --- | --- | --- | --- |

| Loner | supported | Section 2.8.3.2 | CB009794-F |
| Loki | supported | Section 2.8.2 | CB009794-F |
| Thor | supported **[CB008465-AA]** (***)*\*[End CB008465-AA]** | Section 2.8.3.2 | CB009055-5G-X |

(\*\*) Supported as output new Raw CEs (refer to [11102253](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knfmmfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) with compensation factor in Split Panel Beam Forming Mode.

[CB008465-AA] (\*\*\*) Also supported with UL CoMP joint reception. [End CB008465-AA]

#### 2.4.6.4.5 Noise power on PUCCH

##### 2.4.6.4.5.0-1 Noise power on PUCCH Overview (ID: 11108413)

**2.4.6.4.5.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11108416`)

Noise power on PUCCH
For each configured subcell, 5GNB 5G-L1-UL shall estimate the noise (interference + thermal noise) power. This estimate is derived while considering only the allocated PRBs for a given UE. Then, 5G-L1-UL reports the estimated value (after normalizing to one PRB, averaging over all the layers and aggregating over all receiving antennas) to 5G-L2-PS. The unit of this estimate is dBm.

The noise estimation algorithm on PUCCH is described in the 5Gmax algorithm specification (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_6904\_replaced([11108419](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koqrR_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

For CPRI and eCPRI, additional compensation shall be performed, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7863\_replaced([11109661](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko6i5_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Detaills about the noise estimate for each PUCCH Format and its support per L1 HW are given in:

* Format0: 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1322\_replaced([11105226](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koH4ufvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
* Format1: 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1326\_replaced([11105236](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koH4vfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
* Format2: 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1330\_replaced([11105247](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koIfwfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
* Format3: 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1334\_replaced([11105258](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koIfx_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

Supported PUCCH formats could be deduced from 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1139\_replaced([11104445](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kn85mfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

NOTE:

* The noise estimate on non-allocated PRBs for the PUCCH symbol is not required. More details about the usage of this measurement in 5G-L2-PS are in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2647\_replaced([11119377](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqJ5BPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
* For Format1, the noise is estimated on unused cyclic shifts within one PRB. This estimate includes both thermal noise + intra-cell interference since no orthogonality between the received signals associated to different cyclic shifts, in particular for adjacent cyclic shifts.

---

##### 2.4.6.4.5.0-2 Noise power on PUCCH Supported measurement for noise power on PUCCH (ID: 11108419)

**2.4.6.4.5.0-2.0-5**  (ID: `13341888`)

Noise power on PUCCH
Supported measurement for noise power on PUCCH

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **L1 HW** | **Noise power on PUCCH ([11108413](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koqrRfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))** | **5GMAX algo. Spec. section**\* | **First Feature** |
| Loner | Supported\*\* | Section 2.8.3.3 |  |
| Loki | Supported\*\* | Section 2.8.3.3 |  |
| Thor | Supported\*\* | Section 2.8.3.3 | CB007487-B, CB007487-C, CB007487-D |
| GPU | Supported\*\* | TBC | CB016066-C |

\*Details for the algorithm to estimate noise power on PUCCH are provided in 5GMAX algorithm specification (see [11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

---

##### 2.4.6.4.5.0-3 Noise power on PUCCH Measurement of noise power for PUCCH in FDD supercell (ID: 11108433)

**2.4.6.4.5.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11108436`)

Noise power on PUCCH
In configuration of FDD supercell (MRBTS/NRBTS/NRCELL/actSupercell = True) on Loki and Thor DU shall perform noise power measurement on PUCCH F3 (5G\_UP\_ALG\_6903\_replaced([11108413](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koqrRfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) for all serving and non-serving subcells.

---

#### 2.4.6.4.6 Noise power on PUCCH per PRB

##### 2.4.6.4.6.0-1 Noise power on PUCCH per PRB Overview (ID: 11108442)

**2.4.6.4.6.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `11108447`)

Noise power on PUCCH per PRB
For each configured subcell for each slot, DU shall perform the following computations of the noise (interference + thermal noise) power estimation in the current slot:

Step 1: DU shall compute one noise estimate per PUCCH Format and per PRB, while considering all the UEs scheduled within the same PUCCH format and in the same PRB in the slot.

Step 2: DU shall compute one noise estimate per PRB averaged over all PUCCH formats scheduled in the same PRB in the slot.

DU shall average each of these estimates over all the receiving antennas (spatial streams). See 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8133\_replaced([11108450](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_korSU_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

The unit of the estimates is [Before CNI-126050-A] W (Watt) [CNI-126050-A] dBm [End CNI-126050-A].

For CPRI and eCPRI, additional compensation shall be performed, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7863\_replaced([11109661](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko6i5_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

DU(L1) reports the estimates to 5G-L2-PS.

The noise estimation algorithm on PUCCH is described in the 5Gmax algorithm specification (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_6904\_replaced([11108419](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koqrR_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

Details about the noise estimate for each PUCCH Format and its support per L1 HW are given in:

* Format0: 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1322\_replaced([11105226](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koH4ufvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
* Format1: 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1326\_replaced([11105236](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koH4vfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
* Format2: 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1330\_replaced([11105247](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koIfwfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
* Format3: 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1334\_replaced([11105258](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koIfx_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

Supported PUCCH formats could be deduced from 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1139\_replaced([11104445](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kn85mfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

When only zero-valued samples are got from the radio module or (e)CPRI interface for all the receiving antennas (spatial streams) (e.g. broken antenna scenario or protection switching in TSN), in case of noisy measurement, or for unallocated PRBs, DU shall report INVALID value, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7421\_replaced([11109729](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko7J_fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

NOTE:

* The noise estimate on non-configured PRBs for the PUCCH symbol is not required. These noise estimates per PRB are only used for computing the PM counters.
* For Format1, the noise is estimated on unused cyclic shifts within one PRB. This estimate includes both thermal noise + intra-cell interference since no orthogonality between the received signals associated to different cyclic shifts, in particular for adjacent cyclic shifts.

For the accuracy of NPE on PUCCH F1, refer to corresponding note in 2.4.2.5 of 5Gmax algorithm specifications.

---

##### 2.4.6.4.6.0-2 Noise power on PUCCH per PRB Supported measurement for noise power on PUCCH per PRB (ID: 11108450)

**2.4.6.4.6.0-2.0-4**  (ID: `13431633`)

Noise power on PUCCH per PRB
Supported measurement for noise power on PUCCH per PRB

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **L1 HW** | **Noise power** **on PUCCH per PRB** **([11108442](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_korSUPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))** | **5GMAX algo. Spec. section**\* | **First Feature** |
| Loner | - | - | - |
| Loki | Supported | Section 2.8.3.4 | CB008021-A |
| Thor | Supported | Section 2.8.3.4 | CB009027-5G-A |
| GPU | Supported | Section 2.8.3.4 | CB016062-C |

\*Details for the algorithm to estimate noise power on PUCCH are provided in 5GMAX algorithm specification (see ([11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

For Loki and Thor **L1 HW, additional update for Noise power on PU**CCH per PRB as per ([11108442](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_korSUPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) (the noise power measurements are averaged over all the valid antennas (spatial streams)).\*\*\*\*

For Thor L1 HW the noise power measurements are aggregated over all the valid antennas (spatial streams), as per ([11108442](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_korSUPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

12784592:
Noise power on PRACH
For each received PRACH, DU shall estimate the noise power on PRACH PRBs (common for all the detected preambles and aggregated overall the PRACH PRBs), and shall report the estimated value (after the aggregation or the averaging over all valid RX antennas (spatial streams)), see [11108475](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_korSW_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ), to 5G-L2-PS. The unit of this estimate is in [11108475](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_korSW_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

The noise estimation algorithm on PRACH is described in the 5Gmax algorithm specifications (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7036\_replaced([11108475](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_korSW_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

For CPRI and eCPRI, additional compensation shall be performed, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7863\_replaced([11109661](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko6i5_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

When no PRACH preamble detected DU shall report:

* INVALID value (see [11109801](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko8YG_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) when NIDD NRBTS/NRCell/actPrachNoPreambleNoisePwrEst = false

**- Valid Real Noise Power Estimated when no preamble detected and NIDD NRBTS/NRCell/actPrachNoPreambleNoisePwrEst =true. This applies also if one or more Rx are failed (i.e. RU lost or fiber broken or Rx failure, refer to [11377147](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ueUcnvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) and [11380144](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_uek7RPvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).**

**NOTE:** In UE PRACH signal presence (e.g. at SNR higher than -5dB under AWGN assumption, refer to Noise Power Estimation Measurement [[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a blurry, low-resolution icon representing a file. The icon depicts a white document with a folded corner, overlaid by a blue rectangular tab on the left side. The blue tab has a gradient effect, appearing lighter towards the right. Within the blue tab, there are white, stylized letters that appear to be "TV" or "TW". The document itself has a faint blue rectangular shape on its right side, also with a gradient. The overall impression is that of a file icon, possibly for a video or presentation file, given the "TV" or "TW" hint and the visual style.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_3737.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0] 5GMax-5G\_New\_Radio\_Measurements.docx](https://nokia.sharepoint.com/:w:/r/sites/5gmax/Shared%20Documents/GeneralDocuments/5G%20Algorithm%20Specifications/5GMax-5G_New_Radio_Measurements.docx?d=w3dd4cc5570664ed685e68366fa7749a8&csf=1&web=1&e=Xesb2M)) or when PRACH preamble is detected**noise estimate is unreliable and it's not used in 5G-L2-PS.**\*\*\*\*

Current noise estimate is used only for computing the performance counters if the NIDD parameter [Before CB012464-C-UPD002]  MRBTS/NRBTS/actNrUlRipMeasurement [CB012464-C-UPD002] NRBTS/NRPMRNL/NRPMCCP.actNrUlRipCounter [End CB012464-C-UPD002] is configured accordingly.

For this computation the same PRACH noise value (computed for all used PRACH PRBs and all detected PRACH occassions) is used for each PRACH PRB.

For the accuracy of NPE on PRACH, refer to corresponding note in 2.5.19 of 5Gmax algorithm specifications.

**Note2: "5G-L1-UL" is changed to "DU(L1)" since this version as per [11094116](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmZaYfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ). (Note kept only in this version)**

---

##### 2.4.6.4.7.0-2 Noise power on PRACH Supported measurement for noise power on PRACH (ID: 11108475)

**2.4.6.4.7.0-2.0-6**  (ID: `13341904`)

Noise power on PRACH
Supported measurement for noise power on PRACH

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **L1 HW** | **Noise power** **on PRACH** **([11108464](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_korSWPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))** | **5GMAX algo. Spec. section**\* | **First Feature** |
| Loner | Supported | Section  2.5.19 |  |
| Loki | Supported | Section  2.5.19 |  |
| vDU | - | - |  |
| Thor | Supported | Section  2.5.19 | CB007487-B CB007487-C CB007487-D |
| GPU | Supported | TBC | CB016066-C |

\*Details for the algorithm to estimate noise power on PRACH are provided in 5GMAX algorithm specification (see [11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

For Loner L1 HW, DU(L1) supports the report of Invalid PRACH Noise Power measurements (see [11108464](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_korSWPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

For Loki and Thor L1 HW, DU(L1) shall support the report of:

* Invalid PRACH Noise Power measurements (see [11108464](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_korSWPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) when NIDD NRBTS/NRCell/actPrachNoPreambleNoisePwrEst =  false
* Valid Real Noise Power Estimated when no preamble detected and NIDD NRBTS/NRCell/actPrachNoPreambleNoisePwrEst =true.

**NOTE: From System perspective, NIDD NRBTS/NRCell/actPrachNoPreambleNoisePwrEst is not applied to DU(L1) with Loner [CB016066-C] and GPU [End CB016066-C] L1 HW that shall ignore the Feature Activation Flag when set to true.**

The unit of the PRACH noise power is dBm, including all Invalid value scenarios (see [11108464](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_korSWPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

For Loner **L1 HWs the noise power measurements are aggregated over all the valid antennas (spatial streams), see [11108464](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_korSWPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).**

For Loki and Thor L1 HWs the noise power measurements are averaged over all the valid antennas (spatial streams), see [11108464](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_korSWPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

---

### 2.4.6.5 Per slot RSSI

#### 2.4.6.5.0-1 Per slot RSSI Overview (ID: 11108495)

**2.4.6.5.0-1.0-5**  (ID: `13134053`)

Per slot RSSI
For each scheduled UE on either PUSCH or PUCCH, DU or Radio shall estimate one measurement for the RSSI which can be used as a level indicator for the UL power control. Each RSSI measurement shall be averaged by over all the allocated REs for UL DMRSs (demodulation reference signals), averaged over all the layers and aggregated over all the receiving antennas. Then, one measurement normalized to one PRB shall be reported to L2.

The RSSI measurements are all UE related and shall be separately performed for:

- UL DMRS associated to PUSCH

- UL DMRS associated to PUCCH

The scaling factor applied at the radio module to reach a target noise bitwidth shall be compensated at DU or Radio for all the RSSI measurements (see [11108509](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kor5Z_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) & [11108603](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kotHiPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). Details about this scaling factor are in [11376973](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ueT1h_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

For eCPRI 7-2e, for PUSCH Channel the Radio shall :

* compute RSSI measurement based on the output of new Raw CEs algorithm (refer to [11102253](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knfmmfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
* apply the compensation factor defined in section §2.8 of 5Gmax algorithm specifications (see [11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and in section §5.3.1.1 of Massive MIMO Algorithm Specification (see [11101361](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcsvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), when Split panel beamforming ([11354596](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucJSWfvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) configured ([11155345](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksvS8vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) and [11155374](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksvS9PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[CB008577-C]

For FDD BF and PUSCH channel, DU shall apply the split panel compensation factor defined in section §2.8 of 5Gmax algorithm specifications (see [11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and in section §5.3.1.1 of Massive MIMO Algorithm Specification (see [11101361](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcsvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), when split panel beamforming configured (see [11354596](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucJSWfvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[End CB008577-C]

Note: Scaling Factor value is set as described in [11288559](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Tp7ttPvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

Note2:

- For eCPRI 7-2e, Radio(L1) shall do the measurement for PUSCH and report result to 5G-L2-PS via DU, otherwise DU shall do the measurement and report to 5G\_L2\_PS.

---

#### 2.4.6.5.1 Per slot RSSI on PUSCH

##### 2.4.6.5.1.0-1 Per slot RSSI on PUSCH Overview (ID: 11108512)

**2.4.6.5.1.0-1.0-6**  (ID: `12782826`)

Per slot RSSI on PUSCH
DU or Radio shall estimate one RSSI (Received Signal Strength Indicator) measurement for each scheduled UE on PUSCH per slot and report it to 5G-L2-PS. The RSSI measurement is averaged over all the layers, aggregated over received antennas and normalized to a single PRB. The unit is in dBm.

RSSI computation is based on de-boosted method where the power boost applied for DMRS is compensated (see 38.214 Table 6.2.2-1). Then DMRS based RSSI is aligned with the one for data. Also, similar results are obtained when NRBTS/NRCELL/actUlDataDmrsFdm is enabled or disabled (see [11349409](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubej-_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). More details in 5GMAX algorithm specification (see [11108527](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kor5bfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

For eCPRI 7-2e, the Radio shall:

* compute RSSI measurement based on the output of new Raw CEs algorithm (refer to [11102253](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knfmmfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

**- apply the compensation factor defined in section §2.8 of 5Gmax algorithm specifications (see [11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and in section §5.3.1.1 of Massive MIMO Algorithm Specification (see [11101361](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcsvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), when Split panel beamforming ([11354596](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucJSWfvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) configured ([11155345](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksvS8vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) and [11155374](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksvS9PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) in UL.**

For FDD BF, DU shall apply the split panel compensation factor defined in section §2.8 of 5Gmax algorithm specifications (see [11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and in section §5.3.1.1 of Massive MIMO Algorithm Specification (see [11101361](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcsvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), when split panel beamforming configured (see [11354596](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucJSWfvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[CB008465-AA] When UL CoMP is activated (NRCELL.actNrUlComp = 'basic'):

* When the UE is configured for joint reception (see [12763065](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_dTd9AIOSEfCOGMMH109ICw?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)), DU shall aggregate RSSI over all the receiving antennas of the serving and the helper cell (see [12763061](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_gSJKEIOREfCOGMMH109ICw?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)).
* When the UE is configured for SINR measurements on candidate cells, DU shall aggregate RSSI over the receiving antennas of the serving cell.

[End CB008465-AA]

When only zero-valued samples are got from the radio module or (e)CPRI interface for all the receiving antennas (e.g. broken antenna scenario), or in case of noisy measurement, DU or Radio shall report INVALID valuie, see [11109729](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko7J_fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

For CPRI and eCPRI, the scaling factor applied at the radio module to reach a target noise bitwidth shall be compensated at DU or Radio for all the RSSI measurements (see: [11108509](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kor5Z_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) & [11108603](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kotHiPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). Details about this scaling factor are in [11376973](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ueT1h_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) or [1138052](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_uenXgfvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) (for eCPRI 7-2e)**.**

**NOTE: In 5G-L2-PS, RSSI used for PM counters (see [11194079](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28wRPvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).**

Note2:

- For eCPRI 7-2e, Radio(L1) shall do the measurement and report result to 5G-L2-PS via DU, otherwise DU shall do the measurement and report to 5G\_L2\_PS.

---

**2.4.6.5.1.0-2.0-9**  (ID: `12782855`)

Per slot RSSI on PUSCH
Supported measurement for per slot RSSI on PUSCH

| Loki | supported with de-boost method | Section 2.8.2.1 | - |

#### 2.4.6.5.2 Antenna-wise RSSI on PUSCH

##### 2.4.6.5.2.0-1 Antenna-wise RSSI on PUSCH Overview (ID: 11108553)

**2.4.6.5.2.0-1.0-9**  (ID: `12776800`)

Antenna-wise RSSI on PUSCH
DU or Radio shall estimate RSSI on PUSCH per slot, per antenna (meaning "spatial-stream-wise" as per [11107886](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koj9lvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and normalized to 1 PRB for each UE scheduled on PUSCH. This estimate is reported to 5G-L2-PS. The unit of this estimate depends on the L1 HW**, see [11108577](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kosgf_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).**

RSSI computation is based on de-boosted method (see [11108527](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kor5bfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

For each antenna, when only zero-valued samples are got from the radio module or (e)CPRI interface, in case of noisy measurement, or if this antenna is not configured, DU **or Radio** shall report INVALID value for the respective RSSI measurement, see [11109729](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko7J_fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).\*\*\*\*

For CPRI and eCPRI, additional compensation shall be performed, see [11109661](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko6i5_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

**NOTE: This measurement is used in 5G-L2-PS** for PM counter updates as per [11215790](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxVfFPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ). **Also, you can refer to [11108683](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kouVo_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).**\*\*\*\*

Mapping between antenna-wise RSSI and the spatial stream is given in [11109508](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko4tuvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

For MU receiver (cf. [11104114](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kn4oLPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ), [11104177](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kn52QvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), mapping between antenna-wise RSSI and the spatial stream is given in [11109559](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko5Uy_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ), and [11109576](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko5Uz_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

[CB008465-AA] When UL CoMP is activated (NRCELL.actNrUlComp = 'basic'):

* When the UE is configured for joint reception (see [12763065](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_dTd9AIOSEfCOGMMH109ICw?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)), DU shall report antenna-wise RSSI from all the receiving antennas of the serving and the helper cell (see [12763061](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_gSJKEIOREfCOGMMH109ICw?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) (\*). The mapping of antenna-wise RSSI to spatial streams of the serving and helper cell is given in [12775997](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_l3HqEYybEfCrqOdGJPoCRA?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9).
* When the UE is configured for SINR measurements on candidate cells, DU shall report antenna-wise RSSI from the receiving antennas of the serving cell.

\*: Antenna-wise RSSI measurements from helper cell are actually not used for the computation of RTWP counters (see [11108683](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kouVo_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)).

[End CB008465-AA]

For eCPRI 7-2e, the Radio shall:

* compute the Antenna-wise measurements as output from new Raw CEs (refer to [11102253](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knfmmfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

**- apply the compensation factor defined in section §2.8 of 5Gmax algorithm specifications (see [11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and in section §5.3.1.1 of Massive MIMO Algorithm Specification (see [11101361](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcsvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), when Split panel beamforming ([11354596](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucJSWfvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) configured ([11155345](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksvS8vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) and [11155374](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksvS9PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).**

For FDD BF, DU shall apply the split panel compensation factor defined in section §2.8 of 5Gmax algorithm specifications (see [11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and in section §5.3.1.1 of Massive MIMO Algorithm Specification (see [11101361](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcsvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), when split panel beamforming configured (see [11354596](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucJSWfvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

**NOTE2:**

- For eCPRI 7-2e, Radio shall do the measurement and report result to 5G-L2-PS via DU, otherwise DU shall do the measurement and report to 5G\_L2\_PS.

For L1 HW supported measurement refer to [11108577](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kosgf_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

---

#### 2.4.6.5.3 Per slot RSSI on PUCCH

##### 2.4.6.5.3.0-1 Per slot RSSI on PUCCH Overview (ID: 11108606)

**2.4.6.5.3.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11108609`)

Per slot RSSI on PUCCH
5GNB 5G-L1-UL shall estimate one RSSI on PUCCH per slot for each UE, and report it to 5G-L2-PS for long term (LT) averaging. The unit of this measurement is dBm. The RSSI measurement is aggregated over received antennas and normalized to a single PRB. The unit is in dBm.

When only zero-valued samples are got from the radio module or (e)CPRI nterface for all the receiving antennas (e.g. broken antenna scenario), or in case of noisy measurement, 5G-L1-UL shall report INVALID valuie, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7421\_replaced([11109729](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko7J_fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

For CPRI and eCPRI, additional compensation shall be performed, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7863\_replaced([11109661](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko6i5_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

NOTE: In 5G-L2-PS, this measurement is used for PM counters (see 5G\_L2\_310\_replaced([11150654](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_24dnF_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

NOTE: CNI-88099-KA does not introduce functional change. It improves the specification

---

Per slot RSSI on PUCCH
Supported measurement for per slot RSSI on PUCCH

#### 2.4.6.5.4 Per symbol per subband RSSI for RIM

##### 2.4.6.5.4.0-1 Per symbol per subband RSSI for RIM Overview (ID: 11108628)

**2.4.6.5.4.0-1.0-3**  (ID: `12629988`)

Per symbol per subband RSSI for RIM
When NRBTS/NRCELL/actNrRim is not equal to 0 (see [11359995](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucrd3PvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), DU eCPRI 7-2a [CB010315-SR-A] and CPRI [End CB010315-SR-A] and the Radio (for eCPRI 7-2e, when NRBTS/actEcpri72e is set to "TRUE") shall perform per symbol per sub-band RSSI measurement for each slot used for RIM (Remote Interference Management) detection (see [11161259](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktTTp_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

For all the configured sub-cells, DU eCPRI 7-2a [CB010315-SR-A] and CPRI [End CB010315-SR-A] and the Radio (for eCPRI 7-2e) shall estimate one RSSI measurement for each sub-band and each symbol and report it for RI detection and mitigation decisions (see [11161619](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktVI0vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) and [11163368](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktYzNPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). The unit of the RSSI measurement is in dBm.

The RSSI measurement computation is described in 5GMAX algorithm specification section 2.7.2.4:

- For Delay Managed UL symbols (i.e. processed in L1 without delay, typically scheduled for PUSCH, PUCCH, aperiodic SRS): the default method should be used.

- For Non-Delay managed UL symbols (i.e. processed in DU and the Radio (for eCPRI 7-2e) with delay, typically periodic SRS, see [11106894](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kocBz_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), the RIM beam method should be used.

The DU eCPRI 7-2a [CB010315-SR-A] and CPRI [End CB010315-SR-A] and the Radio shall use the sub-band configuration (or the PRB range in frequency domain) and the symbol locations (in time domain) are provided from higher layers (see [11161420](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktUhwfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

The DU eCPRI 7-2a [CB010315-SR-A] and CPRI [End CB010315-SR-A] and the Radio (for eCPRI 7-2e) shall not perform RSSI computation per channel as per legacy RSSI measurements, but per sub-band  and symbols. On these symbols, any combination of multiplexed channel can be included:

- Blank symbols – nothing is scheduled on it

- NDM SRS symbols (eCPRI only)

- PUSCH symbols (potentially multiplexed with PRACH and/or long PUCCH)

On these symbols, DU eCPRI 7-2a [CB010315-SR-A] and CPRI [End CB010315-SR-A]and the Radio (for eCPRI 7-2e) shall compute RSSI on a sequence of n PRBs for every configured sub-band. This partition is independent on the scheduling or non-scheduling of PRBs or on channel multiplexing.

As specified in [11161420](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktUhwfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ), Two streams will be used in RI measurement request.

For Blank symbols in eCPRI, DU with eCPRI 7-2a and the Radio (for eCPRI 7-2e) shall use RI specific configuration for UL reception as [11161420](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktUhwfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

- For DU with eCPRI 7-2a , it shall create dedicated FCP sections(see [11378871](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_uedmhfvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ), [11379021](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ueeNmfvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) and [11379067](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_uee0oPvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for configuring the reception of I/Q data for unscheduled symbols and PRBs from L1Low.

- For eCPRI 7-2e, the Radio shall ignore the RI configuration from high layer for unscheduled symbols and PRBs.

DU eCPRI 7-2a [CB010315-SR-A] and CPRI [End CB010315-SR-A] and the Radio (for eCPRI 7-2e) shall report INVALID value for the below case, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7421\_replaced([11109729](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko7J_fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)):

- For DU eCPRI 7-2a [CB010315-SR-A] and CPRI [End CB010315-SR-A], When only zero-valued samples are got from the (e)CPRI interface for all the configured eAxC streams provided by high layer(e.g. broken antenna scenario) (see [11161259](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktTTp_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), or in case of noisy measurement, DU eCPRI 7-2a [CB010315-SR-A] and CPRI [End CB010315-SR-A] shall report INVALID value (Case1 in [11109713](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko7J9_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))(see [11109729](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko7J_fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

* For Radio (eCPRI 7-2e), When only zero-valued samples are got (e.g. broken antenna scenario) (see [11161259](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktTTp_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), or in case of noisy measurement, Radio shall report INVALID value (Case1 in [11109713](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko7J9_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))(see [11109729](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko7J_fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

[CB010315-SR-A] For CPRI, the scaling factor applied at the radio module to reach a target noise bit width shall be compensated at DU for all the RSSI measurements (see[11108509](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kor5Z_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) , [11108603](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kotHiPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) and [11108628](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kotukfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). Details about this scaling factor are in [11376973](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ueT1h_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ). [End CB010315-SR-A]

Note:    1) None-realtime for this RSSI measurement is acceptable

2) Dedicated message and report and dedicated processing chain shall be used to support RIM. This mechanism is to be implemented in EFS level.

---

### 2.4.6.6 UL Received Total Wideband Power (RTWP)

#### 2.4.6.6.1 RTWP measurement

##### 2.4.6.6.1.0-1 RTWP measurement Overview (ID: 11108654)

**2.4.6.6.1.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11108661`)

RTWP measurement
11108661:
RTWP measurement
11108661:
RTWP measurement
11108661:

11108661:
RTWP measurement
11108661:
RTWP measurement
For CPRI, 5G-L1-UL shall calculate the Received Total Wideband Power (RTWP) which is the power received in the whole configured UL bandwidth including all configured UL channels/signal as well as unallocated PRBs.

* RTWP is calculated as sum of power of all subcarriers in OFDM symbol calculated per antenna stream (AxC).

[image-0][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical formula. The formula is:
$RTWP\_{ant,l} = \sum\_{k=0}^{N\_{SC}^{RB} \* ulBandwidth - 1} |Y\_{k}^{ant,l}|^2 \left( \frac{rxScalingFactor}{[s\_{pre}(ant)]^2 N\_{FFT}} \right)$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_3801.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

where [image-1][image\_desc]The image contains the text "N^RB\_sc". The text is in a stylized font with a gradient effect, appearing to be in shades of blue, orange, and yellow. The "N" is large and in the foreground, with "RB" superscripted above and to the right of it. The "sc" is subscripted below and to the right of the "N". The text is set against a white background with a black border.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_3801.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1], rxScalingFactor, spre, NFFT are defined in 5GMAX algorithm specification (sections 2.2.1 and 2.2.3).

* The OFDM symbol for RTWP measurement is chosen in a following way:
* 5G-L1-UL calculates the symbol positions of the front-loaded DM-RS for each grant as specified in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_791\_replaced([11101674](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knaHDPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). In case of two symbol front-loaded DM-RS, both symbols are taken into calculations.
* For each symbol which contains DM-RS, 5G-L1-UL calculates the total bandwidth occupancy in PRBs.
* The symbol for RTWP measurement is the one which has the highest bandwidth occupancy in PRBs.
* If two symbols have the same bandwidth occupancy in PRBs, the first (with the lower index l) symbol is taken.
* If no PUSCH grant in the current slot, the symbol for RTWP measurements is the first available UL symbol in a slot.

RTWP is reported in dBm units for each antenna (AxC).

For each antenna, when only zero-valued samples are got from the radio module interface for the receiving antenna stream, in case of noisy measurement, or if this antenna is not configured, 5G-L1-UL shall report INVALID valuie for the respective RTWP measurement, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7421\_replaced([11109729](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko7J_fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))..

---

##### 2.4.6.6.1.0-2 RTWP measurement Supported measurement for RTWP (ID: 11108663)

**2.4.6.6.1.0-2.0-6**  (ID: `13431658`)

RTWP measurement
Supported measurement for RTWP

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **L1 HW** | **RTWP ([11108654](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kotumvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))** | **5GMAX algo. Spec.** **section**\* | **First Feature** |
| Loner | supported | **2.8.1** | - |
| Loki | supported | **2.8.1** | - |
| Thor | supported | 2.8.1 | CB007487-D |
| GPU | supported | 2.8.1 | CB016062-C |

\*Details for the algorithm to measure RTWP are provided in 5GMAX algorithm specification (see ([11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

#### 2.4.6.6.2 RTWP measurement for PUSCH

##### 2.4.6.6.2.0-1 RTWP measurement for PUSCH Overview (ID: 11108683)

**2.4.6.6.2.0-1.0-4**  (ID: `12780660`)

RTWP measurement for PUSCH
RTWP counters are based on RTWP measurement for dedicated PUSCH area during uplink slots. Special slots are excluded from measurements. It is applicable for both CPRI and eCPRI fronthaul.

For the uplink slot with no real UEs scheduling, the DU or Radio (Thor 7-2e) shall expect the detection (triggered by L2 (DU)) of fake UE that is scheduled as per [11215719](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxU4CvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

DU or Radio (Thor 7-2e) shall:

* report the following measurements (to L2 ~~(DU)~~) for each uplink slot with PUSCH transmission (either with real or fake UE):
* The antenna wise RSSI for PUSCH of the scheduled PRB for each UE as per [11108550](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kosgdvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ);
* The estimated antenna wise Noise (interference + thermal noise) for PUSCH of the scheduled PRB for each UE as per [11108364](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koqENPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ);

**- For eCPRI 7-2e, Radio shall apply the compensation factor defined in section §2.8 of 5Gmax algorithm specifications (see [11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and in section §5.3.1.1 of Massive MIMO Algorithm Specification (see [11101361](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcsvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), when Split panel beamforming ([11354596](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucJSWfvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) configured ([11155345](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksvS8vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) and [11155374](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksvS9PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).**

- For FDD BF, DU shall apply the split panel compensation factor defined in section §2.8 of 5Gmax algorithm specifications (see [11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and in section §5.3.1.1 of Massive MIMO Algorithm Specification (see [11101361](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcsvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), when split panel beamforming configured (see [11354596](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucJSWfvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Those measurements are used in gNB-DU for RTWP PM counter computation as specified in [11215790](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxVfFPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

[CB008465-AA] When UL CoMP is activated (NRCELL.actNrUlComp = 'basic') and when the UE is scheduled for joint reception or for SINR measurements on candidate cells (see [12763065](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_dTd9AIOSEfCOGMMH109ICw?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)), only antenna-wise RSSI and noise measurements from the serving are used for RTWP computation. DU or Radio reports antenna-wise measurements from the helper cell for troubleshooting. [End CB008465-AA]

Note: The legacy RTWP measurement for CPRI introduced by [11108651](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kotumfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) which covers the whole cell bandwidth are not used to update RTWP counters.

---

**2.4.6.6.2.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `12780789`)

RTWP measurement for PUSCH
DU shall support measurements for RTWP on PUSCH.

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **L1 HW** | RTWP on PUSCH **([11108683](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kouVo_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)**)\*\*\*\* | **5GMAX algo. Spec. section** | **First Feature** |
| Loner | supported | Section 2.8.1.2 | CB009794-F |
| Loki | supported | Section 2.8.1.2 | CB009794-F |
| Thor | supported [CB008465-AA] (\*) [End CB008465-AA] | Section 2.8.1.2 | CB009794-F |
| GPU | supported | Section 2.8.1.2 | CB016062-C |

[CB008465-AA] (\*) Also supported with UL CoMP joint reception. [End CB008465-AA]

### 2.4.6.7 UL Received Power

#### 2.4.6.7.1 UL Received power for PUSCH

##### 2.4.6.7.1.0-1 UL Received power for PUSCH Overview (ID: 11108698)

**2.4.6.7.1.0-1.0-6**  (ID: `12780807`)

UL Received power for PUSCH
For each scheduled UE on PUSCH, DU or Radio shall estimate one measurement (pre-equalizer) for the UL received power which can be used as a level indicator for the UL power control. Each UL received power measurement shall be averaged over all the allocated REs for UL DMRSs (demodulation reference signals), averaged over all the layers and aggregated over all the receiving antennas. Then, one measurement normalized to one PRB shall be reported to L2. The unit of each estimate is dBm.

For eCPRI 7-2e,  the Radio shall:

* compute the UL received power for PUSCH measurement from new Raw CEs (refer to [11102253](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knfmmfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

**- apply the compensation factor defined in section §2.8 of 5Gmax algorithm specifications (see [11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and in section §5.3.1.1 of Massive MIMO Algorithm Specification (see [11101361](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcsvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), when Split panel beamforming ([11354596](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucJSWfvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) configured ([11155345](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksvS8vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) and [11155374](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksvS9PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) in UL.**

- For FDD BF, DU shall apply the split panel compensation factor defined in section §2.8 of 5Gmax algorithm specifications (see [11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and in section §5.3.1.1 of Massive MIMO Algorithm Specification (see [11101361](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcsvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), when split panel beamforming configured (see [11354596](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucJSWfvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[CB008465-AA] When UL CoMP is activated (NRCELL.actNrUlComp = 'basic'):

* When the UE is configured for joint reception (see [12763065](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_dTd9AIOSEfCOGMMH109ICw?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)), DU shall aggregate UL received power over all the receiving antennas of the serving and the helper cell (see [12763061](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_gSJKEIOREfCOGMMH109ICw?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)).
* When the UE is configured for SINR measurements on candidate cells, DU shall aggregate UL received power over the receiving antennas of the serving cell.

Rationale: UL received power is used for closed loop power control (see [11119228](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqJR8PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)). Contribution from serving and helper cell shall be considered. [End CB008465-AA]

When only zero-valued samples are received from the radio module or (e)CPRI interface for all the receiving antennas (eg. broken antenna scenario), or in case of noisy measurement (see [11109729](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko7J_fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), DU or Radio shall report INVALID valuie, see [11109729](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko7J_fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

For CPRI and eCPRI, additional compensation shall be performed, see [11109661](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko6i5_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

**NOTE: This measurement is used in 5G-L2-PS to estimate the UL pathloss (see [11121531](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqYik_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and for PM counters (see [11194079](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28wRPvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and for SINR computation for closed Loop Power Control (see [11119228](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqJR8PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).**

**NOTE2:**

- For eCPRI 7-2e, Radio shall do the measurement and report result to 5G-L2-PS via DU, otherwise DU shall do the measurement and report to 5G\_L2\_PS.

---

##### 2.4.6.7.1.0-2 UL Received power for PUSCH Supported measurements for UL Received power for PUSCH (ID: 11108712)

**2.4.6.7.1.0-2.0-9**  (ID: `12780971`)

UL Received power for PUSCH
Supported measurement for UL Received power for PUSCH

| **L1 HW** | **UL Received power for PUSCH ([11108698](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kouVqPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))** | **5GMAX algo. Spec. section**\* | **First Feature** |
|  |  |  |  |
| Loner | supported | Section 2.8.4.1 | - |
| Loki | supported | Section 2.8.4.1 | - |
| Thor | supported [CB008465-AA] (\*\*\*) [End CB008465-AA] | Section 2.8.4.1 | CB007487-B |
| CB007487-C |  |  |  |
| CB007487-D |  |  |  |
| Thor (Radio 7-2e) | supported (\*\*) | Section 2.8.4.1 | CB007595-B |

(\*\*) Supported as output from new Raw CEs (refer to [11102253](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knfmmfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) with compensation factor in Split Panel Beam Forming Mode.

[CB008465-AA] (\*\*\*) Also supported with UL CoMP joint reception. [End CB008465-AA]

#### 2.4.6.7.2 Antenna-wise UL Received power for PUSCH

##### 2.4.6.7.2.0-1 Antenna-wise UL Received power for PUSCH Overview (ID: 11108736)

**2.4.6.7.2.0-1.0-8**  (ID: `13134212`)

Antenna-wise UL Received power for PUSCH
The per antenna power estimate is computed and reported to 5G-L2-PS with the same unit as in [11108694](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kouVp_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) for each UE sending data on PUSCH. This "spatial-stream-wise" (refer to [11107886](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koj9lvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) estimate is used for TDD FR1 and FDD beamforming cells where the estimate may depend on the beam for a given UE. The measurement is averaged over all the layers and normalized to a single PRB.

For each antenna, when only zero-valued samples are received from the radio module or (e)CPRI interface for all the receiving antennas (eg. broken antenna scenario), or in case of noisy measurement (see [11109729](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko7J_fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), DU**or Radio**shall report INVALID value, see [11109729](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko7J_fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).\*\*\*\*

Mapping between antenna-wise power and the spatial stream is given in [11109508](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko4tuvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

For MU receiver (cf. [11104114](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kn4oLPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ), [11104177](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kn52QvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), mapping between antenna-wise power and the spatial stream is given in [11109559](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko5Uy_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) and [11109576](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko5Uz_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

[CB008465-AA] When UL CoMP is activated (NRCELL.actNrUlComp = 'basic'):

* When the UE is scheduled for joint reception (see [12763065](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_dTd9AIOSEfCOGMMH109ICw?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)), DU shall report antenna-wise power estimate from all the receiving antennas of the serving and the helper cell (see [12763061](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_gSJKEIOREfCOGMMH109ICw?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)). The mapping of antenna-wise power to spatial streams of the serving and helper cell is given in [12775997](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_l3HqEYybEfCrqOdGJPoCRA?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9).
* When the UE is scheduled for SINR measurements on candidate cells, DU shall report antenna-wise power estimate from the receiving antennas of the serving cell.

[End CB008465-AA]

DU **or Radio** **does not compute the antenna-wise power on PUSCH for the non-configured antennas. It shall inform 5G-L2-PS that the respective measurement is invalid, see [11109729](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko7J_fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).**

For eCPRI 7-2e, the Radio shall:

* compute the Antenna-wise measurements as output from new Raw CEs (refer to [11102253](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knfmmfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

- apply the compensation factor defined in section §2.8 of 5Gmax algorithm specifications (see [11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and in section §5.3.1.1 of Massive MIMO Algorithm Specification (see [11101361](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcsvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), when Split panel beamforming ([11354596](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucJSWfvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) configured ([11155345](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksvS8vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) and [11155374](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksvS9PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) in UL.

- For FDD BF, DU shall apply the split panel compensation factor defined in section §2.8 of 5Gmax algorithm specifications (see [11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and in section §5.3.1.1 of Massive MIMO Algorithm Specification (see [11101361](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcsvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), when split panel beamforming configured (see [11354596](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucJSWfvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

For CPRI and eCPRI, additional compensation shall be performed, see [11109661](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko6i5_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

For L1 HW supported measurement refer to [11108759](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kou8vvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)

**NOTE: This measurement is not used in 5G-L2-PS**

Note2:

- For eCPRI 7-2e, Radio(L1) shall do the measurement and report result to 5G-L2-PS via DU(L1), otherwise DU(L1) shall do the measurement and report to 5G\_L2\_PS.

---

#### 2.4.6.7.3 UL Received Power for PUCCH

##### 2.4.6.7.3.0-1 UL Received Power for PUCCH Overview (ID: 11108785)

**2.4.6.7.3.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11108787`)

UL Received Power for PUCCH
For each scheduled UE on PUCCH, 5GNB 5G-L1-UL shall estimate one measurement for the UL received power which can be used as a level indicator for the UL power control. Each UL received power measurement shall be averaged over all the allocated REs, aggregated over all the receiving antennas. Then, one measurement normalized to one PRB shall be reported to L2. The unit of this estimate is dBm.

When only zero-valued samples or samples with values below assumed threshold are received from the radio module or (e)CPRI interface for all the receiving antennas (eg. broken antenna scenario), or in case of noisy measurement (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7421\_replaced([11109729](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko7J_fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), 5G-L1-UL shall report INVALID valuie, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7421\_replaced([11109729](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko7J_fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

For CPRI and eCPRI, additional compensation shall be performed, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7863\_replaced([11109661](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko6i5_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

For L1 HW supported:

* Time, Frequency and Technology configurations refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1139\_replaced([11104445](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kn85mfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)),
* Measurement refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7095\_replaced([11108789](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kovjyfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

NOTE: this measurement is used for SINR computation for closed Loop Power Control (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2647\_replaced([11119377](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqJ5BPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

---

##### 2.4.6.7.3.0-3 UL Received Power for PUCCH Measurement of UL received power for PUCCH in FDD supercell (ID: 11108805)

**2.4.6.7.3.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11108807`)

UL Received Power for PUCCH
In configuration of FDD supercell (MRBTS/NRBTS/NRCELL/cellTechnology = 'FDD' and MRBTS/NRBTS/NRCELL/actSupercell = True) on Loki and Thor DU shall perform received power measurement on PUCCH F3 (5G\_UP\_ALG\_7094\_replaced([11108785](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kovjx_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) for all serving and non-serving subcells.

#### 2.4.6.7.4 Antenna-wise UL Received Power for SRS

##### 2.4.6.7.4.0-1 Antenna-wise UL Received Power for SRS Overview (ID: 11108812)

**2.4.6.7.4.0-1.0-4**  (ID: `13134226`)

Antenna-wise UL Received Power for SRS
DU or Radio shall estimate per rx antenna UL received power on SRS for each UE and report it to 5G-L2-PS. The measurement is normalized to a single PRB. The unit of each estimate is dBm.

When only zero-valued samples are received from the radio module or (e)CPRI interface for each the receiving antenna stream, or in case of noisy measurement (see [11109729](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko7J_fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), DU or Radio shall report INVALID value, see [11109729](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko7J_fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

Mapping between antenna-wise (meaning "spatial-stream-wise" as per [11107886](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koj9lvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) **power estimate and the spatial stream is given in [11109508](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko4tuvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).**

For CPRI and eCPRI, additional compensation shall be performed, see [11109661](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko6i5_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

DU or Radio does not compute the antenna-wise power on SRS for the non-configured antennas. It shall inform 5G-L2-PS that the reported measurement associated to the non-configured antenna is invalid, see [11109729](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko7J_fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

For eCPRI 7-2e, the Radio shall apply the compensation factor defined in section §2.8 of 5Gmax algorithm specifications (see [11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and in section §5.3.1.1 of Massive MIMO Algorithm Specification (see [11101361](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcsvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), when Split panel beamforming ([11354596](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucJSWfvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) configured ([11155345](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksvS8vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) and [11155374](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksvS9PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) in UL.

[CB008577-C]

For FDD BF, DU shall apply the split panel compensation factor defined in section §2.8 of 5Gmax algorithm specifications (see [11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and in section §5.3.1.1 of Massive MIMO Algorithm Specification (see [11101361](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcsvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), when split panel beamforming configured (see [11354596](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucJSWfvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[End CB008577-C]

**NOTE: this measurement is not used in 5G-L2-PS**

**NOTE3: For eCPRI 7-2e, Radio shall do the measurements and report results to 5G-L2-PS via DU, otherwise DU shall do the measurements and report to 5G\_L2\_PS.**

For L1 HW supported measurement refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7103\_replaced([11108823](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kowK1fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

---

#### 2.4.6.7.7 Peak Metric for PRACH

##### 2.4.6.7.7.0-1 Peak Metric for PRACH Overview (ID: 11108883)

**2.4.6.7.7.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `12775776`)

Peak Metric for PRACH
Purpose:

DU shall perform peak metric estimation for each detected preamble from PRACH reception.

Input:

* The detected preamble, refer to [11105872: Preamble Peak Detection](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koRCovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Frm%2Fcm%2Fstream%2F_UBmNVp5EEe2A-5U7tzedRA)
* [CB008577-A] NRCELL.actPrachProbing [End CB008577-A]

Algorithm:

DU shall support peak metric computation for signal power estimation in PRACH signal bandwidth, for each detected preamble based on the peak value exceeding the detection threshold. Refer to [11105783](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koP0gvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) for the detection threshold and refer to [11105872](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koRCovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) for the peak value detection.

DU shall apply processing gain (compensation for the peak values), then aggregate the peak values over all valid RX antennas to derive the peak metric.

For CPRI and eCPRI, the DU shall perform power scaling and apply additional compensation (see [11109661](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko6i5_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) depending on L1 HW ([11108889](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kowx6_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

The peak metric estimation algorithm is defined in section "2.5.19 Signal and Noise Power Estimation" of the 5GMax algorithm specification (see [11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

For non beamforming, DU shall estimate one measurement across all the valid RX antennas for each detected preamble.

[Before CB008577-A]

For beamforming, DU shall estimate one measurement for all the valid RX antennas associated with one beam for each detected preamble.

[End CB008577-A]

[CB008577-A]

For beamforming (see [11106028](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_lGJSdvvHEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), DU shall estimate one measurement per beam for each detected preamble:

* If PRACH probing is disabled (NRCELL.actPrachProbing=false), DU only detect same preamble index once from one beam per RO, DU shall estimate one measurement for the valid RX antennas associated with the one beam.
* If PRACH probing is enabled (NRCELL.actPrachProbing=true), DU can detect same preamble index up to 4 times from 4 beams per RO, as such DU shall estimate up to 4 measurements, each measurement for 2 valid RX antennas associated with one beam (see [11106062](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koTe5_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[End CB008577-A]

Refer to [11105836](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koQblfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) for the PRACH valid RX antennas. Note, the RX antennas means the spatial streams input to the receiver.

Output:

DU shall estimate the peak metric measurement for each detected preamble in linear unit.

Note, L1(DU) shall report the measurements to L2(DU).

Refer to [11108889](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kowx6_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) for the L1 HWs support peak metric measurement.

---
