### 2.4.6.8 SINR

#### 2.4.6.8.1 SINR for PUSCH (post-equalized)

##### 2.4.6.8.1.0-1 SINR for PUSCH (post-equalized) Overview (ID: 11108902)

**2.4.6.8.1.0-1.0-5**  (ID: `12519745`)

SINR for PUSCH (post-equalized)
For each scheduled UE on PUSCH, DU:

* shall estimate one measurement for the SINR which can be used as an indicator for the UL link adaptation.
* compute each SINR measurement ~~is computed~~ at the equalizer output, per layer and averaged over all the allocated REs for one NR-slot (not normalized to one PRB).
* shall provide to 5G-L2-PS for long term averaging, one or more estimates ~~shall be then provided to 5G-L2-PS for long term averaging~~ depending on the number of layers.
* or (for eCPRI 7-2e) Radio shall take into account the signals received on all the active antennas ~~are taken into account~~.

[Before CB010981-F] For each scheduled UE and each layer, when MRBTS/NRBTS/NRCELL/actPuschPostEqSinrCompensation = TRUE,

DU shall perform post-Equalized SINR compensation stage algorithm (refer to [12519646: Post-Equalized SNR Compensation](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_xiD5MxkWEfC3upM2pE5-4w?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)Stage)

**[End CB010981-F]**

For eCPRI 7-2e:

* DU shall compute RE level SINR ~~is calculated in DU~~ based on β received from Radio
* Radio shall provide those inputs based on new Raw CEs (refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_28100\_replaced([11102253](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knfmmfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

Note: As a post-equalizer SINR is provided to 5G-L2-PS, its value depends on the equalizer type (e.g., MRC, IRC, etc.).

DU does not compute the SINR on PUSCH for the non-scheduled layer. It shall inform 5G-L2-PS that the reported measurement is invalid, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1746\_replaced([11109764](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko7xC_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

The post-equalizer SINR shall be used by 5G-L2-PS as described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3081\_replaced([11131259](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krCCw_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) where it is called 'sinr'. The distribution shall be impacted by NIDD NRBTS/NRCELL/actIrcRddDebiasing please refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29312\_replaced([11102962](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knpXnPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) .

Note2:

- For eCPRI 7-2e, βcorresponds to the the normalization factor **C in section 2.3.6 of 5GMAX algorithm specification, and the calculation of SINR can be found in the same chapter. (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_695\_replaced([11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).**

---

##### 2.4.6.8.1.0-2 SINR for PUSCH (post-equalized) Post-Equalized SINR Compensation Stage (ID: 12519646)

**2.4.6.8.1.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `12520827`)

SINR for PUSCH (post-equalized)
[Before CB010981-F]

Algorithm description for each granted UE on PUSCH and each layer

Inputs:

* NIDD parameter NRBTS/NRBTS/NRCELL/actPuschPostEqSinrCompensation
* Number of scheduled PRBs
* Number of input spatial streams: 2RX or 4RX
* Number of layers for the UE
* DMRS and DATA frequency-domain multiplexing configuration in DMRS OFDM symbols number of DMRS CDM group(s) without data (refer to [11101692](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knauFPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9))
* Post-equalized SINR Estimation at Equalizer output

Algorithm:

When (NRBTS/NRBTS/NRCELL/actPuschPostEqSinrCompensation = TRUE)

1. DU shall determine the right triplet (r,g,q) based on the number of scheduled PRBs, number of spatial streams, number of layers and DMRS/DATA FDM configuration.
2. DU shall perform Compensation Stage of post-equalized SINR at Equalizer Output using the selected (r,g,q) triplet, as described in 5GMax algorithm specification ([11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)).

Rational:

The post-equalized SINR estimation is found at Equalizer Output as being inaccurate:   
- Over-estimated for low and medium SINR working point,   
- Under-estimated for high SINR working point.  
This impacts System Scheduler decisions for SINR to MCS mapping in UL Inner Link Adaptation Step.  
To counteract Equalizer output accuracy loss, post-equalized SINR Compensation Stage Algorithm is introduced, refer to 5GMAX algorithm specification ([11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)).  
Simulations reference describing the precision loss and the Compensation Stage algorithm are available in [PostEqualizerSinrCompensationLoki.pptx](https://nokia.sharepoint.com/:p:/r/sites/5gmax/_layouts/15/Doc.aspx?sourcedoc=%7B8D62B6F8-1CF3-4B51-9243-82B66D381C44%7D&file=PostEqualizerSinrCompensationLoki.pptx&action=edit&mobileredirect=true)

Output:

* Compensated post-equalized SINR measurement.

Note: When SINR compensation is performed, compensated SINR is reported instead of input post-equalized SINR;

The set of (r,g,q) triplets is defined in 5GMax algorithm specification ([11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)).

For supported L1 HW refer to [11108915: Supported measurement for UL SINR for PUSCH](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koxY9fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)

[End CB010981-F]

---

##### 2.4.6.8.1.0-3 SINR for PUSCH (post-equalized) Supported measurement for UL SINR for PUSCH (ID: 11108915)

**2.4.6.8.1.0-3.0-8**  (ID: `13342410`)

SINR for PUSCH (post-equalized)
Supported measurement for UL SINR for PUSCH

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **L1 HW** | **UL SINR for PUSCH****([11108902](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koxY8PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))** | **5GMAX algo. Spec. section**\* | **First Feature** |
|  |  |  |  |
| Loner | supported\*\* | Section 2.8.5.1 | - |
| Loki | supported\*\* | Section 2.8.5.1 | - |
| Thor | supported\*\* | Section 2.8.5.1 | CB007487-B |
| CB007487-C |  |  |  |
| CB007487-D |  |  |  |
| Thor (Radio 7-2e) | supported\*\* | Section 2.8.5.1 | CB007595-B |
| GPU | supported | TBC | **CB016066-C** |

(\*) Details for the algorithm to estimate SINR for PUSCH are provided in 5GMAX algorithm specification (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_695\_replaced([11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

(\*\*) Averaging details: For Loner, Loki, Thor and Thor(Radio 7-2e) L1 HWs, the SINR is averaged among all data symbols.

Note: Difference between 1) sinr averaging described in annex D in [Receiver\_UL\_SINR\_input\_LA\_analysis](https://nokia.sharepoint.com/:p:/r/sites/5GRANASBBUArchitecture/Shared%20Documents/5GC000923%20Intra-DU%20UL%20COMP/5G%20link%20simulations/SINR%20checks%20for%20Link%20Adaptation/Receiver_UL_SINR_input_LA_analysis.pptx?d=wbc983c6c87604843a6ef9d6e400010ec&csf=1&web=1&e=YUxoMj) and 2) average among all data symbols is visible here:

<https://nokia.sharepoint.com/:p:/r/sites/5GSystemEngineering/User_Plane_Solution_Domain/L1/Shared%20Documents/Miscellaneous%20documents/Post_Eq_Sinr_measurements_No_Interference_LowSNR.pptx?d=w7687053ee5f34648a01e2e252f811101&csf=1&web=1&e=Bup9CR>

<https://nokia.sharepoint.com/:p:/r/sites/5GSystemEngineering/User_Plane_Solution_Domain/L1/Shared%20Documents/Miscellaneous%20documents/Post_Eq_Sinr_measurements_with_Interference.pptx?d=w18e72a953867434baf55616d8fc627ff&csf=1&web=1&e=Owq97K>

For eCPRI 7-2e,  β is calculated in "Thor (Radio 7-2e)" L1 HW, i.e. in Radio based on new Raw CEs (refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_28100\_replaced([11102253](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knfmmfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) , and post SINR is calculated in "Thor" L1 HW, i.e, DU.

SU SINR for PUSCH MU receiver (post-equalized)
For each scheduled paired UE (rank1 or rank2) on PUSCH and MU receiver only (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_19605\_replaced([11104177](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kn52QvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for 8Rx MU receiver or 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26101\_replaced([11104256](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kn6dXfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for 16Rx MU receiver), DU (when NRBTS/NRCELL/numberOfRxBeamforming = 8) or Radio (when NRBTS/NRCELL/numberOfRxBeamforming = 16) shall:

 estimate one additional measurement per layer when rdSuSinrFlag is TRUE (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_21886\_replaced([11148764](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksKrPfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and when the number of paired UEs is higher than 2 per PRB [CB008235-C-UPD002] for:

* "Normal UEs pairing" (see 5G\_UP\_13964\_replaced([11355535](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucR1OvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))),

- "Candidate UEs group" pairing (cf. 5G\_UP\_4977\_replaced([11355544](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucR1PPvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_UP\_ALG\_21191\_replaced([11146299](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksD9gPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_UP\_ALG\_9690\_replaced([11148139](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksI2BPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

* "Enhanced MU-MIMO" pairing (cf. 5G\_UP\_13281\_replaced([11355586](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucScQvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) when NRBTS\NRCELL\NRULMUMIMO\ulMaxMuMimoLayer higher than 4. The Radio shall estimate this measurement for all UEs in virtual UE if at least 1 PRB is shared by more than 2 UEs in the virtual UE (see example in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_32183\_replaced([11108971](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koyAA_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

[End CB008235-C-UPD002].

This measurement is called "SuPostCombSinr" in 5GMAX algorithm specification cf. 5G\_UP\_ALG\_22023\_replaced([11108980](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koyABfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

 not compute “SuPostCombSinr" and it shall report INVALID value, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1746\_replaced([11109764](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko7xC_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), when rdSuSinrFlag is FALSE OR when not all previous conditions are met.

DU (when NRBTS/NRCELL/numberOfRxBeamforming = 8) or Radio (when NRBTS/NRCELL/numberOfRxBeamforming = 16) shall compute “SuPostCombSinr” (when all the conditions are met, see above) at the equalizer output, using:

 The signals received on the Nrx active antennas of MU-IRC receiver

 Same Rdd matrix (Nrx x Nrx) as the one applied for post-equalized SINR estimation in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1701\_replaced([11108899](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kowx7_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

* Only channel estimates (Nrx x Nlayers)of the first DMRS and  layer(s) of interest for the paired UE  (see example below, link: [[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a blurry, low-resolution icon that appears to represent a file. The icon is predominantly white with a red rectangular shape overlaid on the left side. Within the red rectangle, there is a white stylized letter "P". To the right of the red rectangle, on the white background, there are some faint, blurry orange shapes that might be intended to represent text or another graphic element, but they are too indistinct to be deciphered. The overall impression is that of a file icon, possibly for a presentation or a document with a "P" in its name or type.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_3903.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0] Hestimates per Beam for SINR computation (Example of 3 paired UEs).pptx](https://nokia.sharepoint.com/:p:/r/sites/5Gdoc/AS_Tech_Spec/Drawings%20and%20diagrams%20for%205G/User%20Plane/5G%20UP%20Algorithms/2%20L1%20Algorithms%20and%20Configurations/UL%20-%20Measurements/Hestimates%20per%20Beam%20for%20SINR%20computation%20(Example%20of%203%20paired%20UEs).pptx?d=w1de10c13b95a49c683d237cef05ad8b2&csf=1&web=1&e=kSbjvp))

[image-1][image\_desc]The image depicts a diagram illustrating "Hestimates per Beam for SINR computation (Example of 3 paired UEs)". The diagram is divided into two main rows, each showing a sequence of oval shapes representing different H-estimates, connected by arrows to indicate a process or computation.
The top row shows a sequence of four oval shapes:
1. "H\_UE0 on Best beam UEO" (light green)
2. "H\_UE0 on 2nd Best beam UEO" (light green)
3. "H\_UE1 on Best beam UE1" (light yellow)
4. "H\_UE1 on Best beam UE2" (orange)
An arrow points from the fourth oval to the text "MU SINR UEO per layer when UE is paired". Following this text, there are three more oval shapes:
5. "H\_UE0 on Best beam UEO" (light green)
6. "H\_UE0 on 2nd Best beam UEO" (light green)
7. "H\_UE0 on Best beam UE1" (light yellow)
8. "H\_UE0 on Best beam UE2" (orange)
An arrow points from the last oval in this sequence to the text "SUpostCombSinr UEO per layer computed when UE is paired".
The bottom row shows a sequence of four oval shapes:
1. "H\_UE0 on Best beam UEO" (light green)
2. "H\_UE0 on 2nd Best beam UEO" (light green)
3. "H\_UE0 on 3rd beam UEO" (light blue)
4. "H\_UE0 on 4th beam UEO" (dark blue)
An arrow points from the last oval in this sequence to the text "SINR UEO in SU (i.e. not paired) per layer when UE is not paired".
The overall diagram visually explains how different H-estimates are used in SINR computations, distinguishing between scenarios where UEs are paired (for MU SINR and SUpostCombSinr) and unpaired (for SINR in SU). The colors of the ovals seem to differentiate between UEs (UE0, UE1, UE2) and potentially the beam quality or order.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_3903.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]

DU (when NRBTS/NRCELL/numberOfRxBeamforming = 8) or Radio (when NRBTS/NRCELL/numberOfRxBeamforming = 16)  shall average the estimated “SuPostCombSinr” over X equidistant PRBs across the PRB allocated for a virtual UE (when NRBTS/NRCELL/numberOfRxBeamforming = 8) or per UE  (when NRBTS/NRCELL/numberOfRxBeamforming = 16) as determined in 5GMAX for one NR-slot : [[image-2][image\_desc]The image is a blurry, pixelated icon that appears to represent a document or file. The icon is predominantly orange and white. On the left side, there is a large orange square with a white plus sign inside it. To the right of the orange square, there is a white shape that resembles a folded corner of a document, with some faint orange markings on it. The overall impression is that of a file with some sort of indicator or symbol on it.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_3903.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2] CB009363-SuPostCombSinr.pptx](https://nokia.sharepoint.com/:p:/r/sites/5gmax/Shared%20Documents/SimulationReports/NR/mMIMO/CB009363/CB009363-SuPostCombSinr.pptx?d=w97a0b0c08f124ae5bee64a0900d3aea5&csf=1&web=1&e=Ccvphn).

The Value of X is configurable via the R&D parameter:

- rdNbPrbSuSinr when NRBTS/NRCELL/numberOfRxBeamforming = 8, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23409\_replaced([11110497](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpE7A_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

- rdNbPrbSuSinr16Rx when NRBTS/NRCELL/numberOfRxBeamforming = 16 (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_30012\_replaced([11110503](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpE7BfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) ; Radio shall use only 4 subcarriers in the middle of selected PRBs and shall exclude PRB with central DC if selected.

For more details about the usage of the additional post-equalizer SINR, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_21886\_replaced([11148764](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksKrPfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) where it is called 'post SU-SINR'.

The existing SINR (described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1701\_replaced([11108899](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kowx7_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) when computed in MU receiver is called 'MU-SINR' in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_21939\_replaced([11148794](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksLSQ_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). The MU-SINR computation includes the complete Rdd matrix. DU (when NRBTS/NRCELL/numberOfRxBeamforming = 8) or Radio (when NRBTS/NRCELL/numberOfRxBeamforming = 16) shall always compute 'MU-SINR'.

---

##### 2.4.6.8.2.0-2 SU SINR for PUSCH MU receiver (post-equalized) Example for SuPostCombSinr in MU-MIMO pairing (ID: 11108971)

This is an example illustration supporting UP\_ALG\_21980 “SuPostCombSinr" measurement definition for:

* "Normal UEs pairing" (see 5G\_UP\_13964\_replaced([11355535](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucR1OvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))),

- "Candidate UEs group" pairing (cf. 5G\_UP\_4977\_replaced([11355544](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucR1PPvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_UP\_ALG\_21191\_replaced([11146299](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksD9gPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_UP\_ALG\_9690\_replaced([11148139](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksI2BPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

- "Enhanced MU-MIMO" pairing (cf. 5G\_UP\_13281\_replaced([11355586](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucScQvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) when NRBTS\NRCELL\NRULMUMIMO\ulMaxMuMimoLayer higher than 4.

[image-0][image\_desc]The image displays four diagrams illustrating different user equipment (UE) pairing scenarios in a cellular network, likely related to resource allocation within a Physical Resource Block (PRB). Each diagram represents a PRB and shows how different UEs are allocated resources across frequency and time (implied by the stacked blocks). The diagrams are arranged horizontally and are labeled with pairing types and maximum number of UEs per PRB.
**Diagram 1: "Normal" Pairing: not FDMed UEs, Max Nb of UEs in 1 PRB = 2**
This diagram shows two UEs, UE 1 (ROOT) and UE 2, allocated resources. UE 1 occupies a larger portion of the PRB, spanning across the entire frequency range shown. UE 2 occupies the remaining portion of the PRB. Below the diagram, it states "SuPostCombSinr" not reported (INVALID), and a red box indicates "Virtual UE".
**Diagram 2: "Normal" Pairing: not FDMed UEs, Max Nb of UEs in 1 PRB > 2**
This diagram shows three UEs: UE 1 (ROOT), UE 2, and UE 3. UE 1 (ROOT) occupies a significant portion. UE 2 occupies a portion next to UE 1. UE 3 occupies the remaining portion of the PRB. Below the diagram, it states "SuPostCombSinr" reported for all UEs in Virtual UE, and a red box indicates "Virtual UE".
**Diagram 3: "Candidate" Pairing: FDMed UEs, Max Nb of UEs in 1 PRB = 2**
This diagram shows three UEs: UE 1 (ROOT), UE 2, and UE 3. UE 1 (ROOT) occupies a large portion. UE 2 occupies a portion next to UE 1. UE 3 occupies a smaller portion below UE 2. Below the diagram, it states "SuPostCombSinr" not reported (INVALID), and a red box indicates "Virtual UE".
**Diagram 4: "Enhanced MU-MIMO" Pairing: FDMed UEs, Max Nb of UEs in 1 PRB > 2**
This diagram shows four UEs: UE 1 (ROOT), UE 2, UE 3, and UE 4. UE 1 (ROOT) occupies a large portion. UE 2 occupies a portion next to UE 1. UE 3 occupies a portion next to UE 2. UE 4 occupies a smaller portion below UE 2. Below the diagram, it states "SuPostCombSinr" reported for all UEs in Virtual UE, and a red box indicates "Virtual UE".
A vertical dashed blue arrow labeled "Frequency" is shown to the right of the first three diagrams, indicating the direction of frequency. A dashed black line labeled "#UEs per 1 PRB" runs horizontally across the top of the diagrams, suggesting a threshold or limit. The red boxes around the diagrams and the "Virtual UE" labels at the bottom indicate that these resource allocations are associated with a "Virtual UE" concept.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_3905.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

Pictures are under the link: [[image-1][image\_desc]The image is a blurry, low-resolution icon that appears to be for Microsoft Excel. It features the characteristic green color and a stylized "X" symbol. The icon is partially obscured by a white sheet of paper, also with a green overlay, suggesting a document or spreadsheet. The overall impression is that of a digital file icon.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_3905.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1] SuPostCombSinr\_Images.xlsx](https://nokia.sharepoint.com/:x:/r/sites/5Gdoc/AS_Tech_Spec/Drawings%20and%20diagrams%20for%205G/User%20Plane/5G%20UP%20Algorithms/2%20L1%20Algorithms%20and%20Configurations/UL%20-%20Measurements/SuPostCombSinr_Images.xlsx?d=w7d15d3f21a814a0796a46828ec5ea08e&csf=1&web=1&e=JKqqaZ)

##### 2.4.6.8.2.0-3 SU SINR for PUSCH MU receiver (post-equalized) Supported measurement for UL SU SINR for PUSCH MU receiver (ID: 11108980)

**2.4.6.8.2.0-3.0-3**  (ID: `11108989`)

SU SINR for PUSCH MU receiver (post-equalized)
Supported measurement for UL SU SINR for PUSCH MU receiver

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| L1 HW | UL SU SINR for PUSCH MU receiver (5G\_UP\_ALG\_21980\_replaced([11108940](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koxY_vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) | 5GMAX algo. Spec. section\* | First Feature |
| Loner | - | - | - |
| Loki | - | - | - |
| vDU | - | - | - |
| Thor | supported for C8 only | Section 2.3.6 | CB009363-OO |
| Thor (Radio 7-2e) | supported for C16'4 [CB008235-A] and C16'8 [End CB008235-A] | Section 2.3.6 | CB010708-F-UPD001 |

\*Details for the algorithm to estimate SU SINR for PUSCH MU receiver are provided in 5GMAX algorithm specification (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_695\_replaced([11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

#### 2.4.6.8.3 Antenna-wise SINR for PUSCH (pre-equalized)

##### 2.4.6.8.3.0-1 Antenna-wise SINR for PUSCH (pre-equalized) Overview (ID: 11108996)

**2.4.6.8.3.0-1.0-7**  (ID: `12748004`)

Antenna-wise SINR for PUSCH (pre-equalized)
~~Additionally,~~ For each scheduled UE on PUSCH, DU**or Radio**shall compute one SINR measurement per rx antenna stream and NR-slot for beam management usage.\*\*\*\*

The SINR estimation per rx antenna stream on PUSCH is calculated via per antenna stream UL receive signal power/UL receive noise power. This measurement is a pre-equalizer SINR and it gives different estimates compared to the SINR in [11108902](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koxY8PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ). Refer to 5Gmax algorithm specifications (see [11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for definition of UL receive noise power and UL receive signal power (see [11109016](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koynEfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) for reference).

[CB008465-AA] When UL CoMP is activated (NRCELL.actNrUlComp = 'basic'):

* When the UE is scheduled for joint reception (see [12763065](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_dTd9AIOSEfCOGMMH109ICw?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)), DU shall report antenna-wise pre-equalized SINR from all the receiving antennas of the serving and the helper cell (see [12763061](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_gSJKEIOREfCOGMMH109ICw?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)). The mapping of antenna-wise SINR to spatial streams of the serving and helper cell is given in [12775997](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_l3HqEYybEfCrqOdGJPoCRA?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9).
* When the UE is scheduled for SINR measurements on candidate cells, DU shall compute antenna-wise SINR from the receiving antennas of the serving cell and all candidate cells, and report antenna-wise SINR from serving cell only.

[End CB008465-AA]

Mapping between antenna-wise sinr estimate and the spatial stream is given in [11109508](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko4tuvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

For MU receiver (cf. [11104114](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kn4oLPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ), [11104177](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kn52QvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), mapping between antenna-wise sinr and the spatial stream is given in [11109559](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko5Uy_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)**and [11109576](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko5Uz_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).**

DU**or Radio**does not compute the antenna-wise SINR on PUSCH for the non-configured antennas. It shall inform 5G-L2-PS that the reported measurement associated to the non-configured antenna is invalid, see [11109764](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko7xC_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).\*\*\*\*

For Thor 7-2e HW the Radio shall compute the Antenna-wise measurements as output from new Raw CEs (refer to [11102253](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knfmmfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

For each antenna, when the Rx is failed (typically the case of RU lost or fiber broken or Rx failure, refer to [11377147](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ueUcnvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) and [11380144](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_uek7RPvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), DU**or Radio**shall report INVALID value, see [11109764](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko7xC_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).\*\*\*\*

For L1 HW supported measurement refer to [11109016](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koynEfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

The usage of this measurement in 5G-L2-PS is given in [11112474](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpWAvPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

Note:

- For eCPRI 7-2e, Radio shall do the measurement and report result to 5G-L2-PS via DU, otherwise DU shall do the measurement and report to 5G\_L2\_PS.

---

##### 2.4.6.8.3.0-2 Antenna-wise SINR for PUSCH (pre-equalized) Supported measurement for UL Antenna-wise SINR for PUSCH (ID: 11109016)

**2.4.6.8.3.0-2.0-9**  (ID: `12781181`)

Antenna-wise SINR for PUSCH (pre-equalized)
Supported measurement for UL Antenna-wise SINR for PUSCH (pre-equalizer)

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **L1 HW** | **UL Antenna-wise SINR for PUSCH****([11108996](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koyACvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))** | **5GMAX algo. Spec. section\* SINR** | **First Feature** |
| Loner | supported\*\* | Section 2.8.5.1 | 5GC002244-A |
| Loki | supported\*\* | Section 2.8.5.1 | 5GC001294-B |
| Thor | supported\*\*[CB008465-AA] (Note 2) [End CB008465-AA] | Section 2.8.5.1 | CB007487-B  CB007487-C  CB007487-D |
| Thor (Radio 7-2e) | supported\*\* (Note 1) | Section 2.8.5.1 | CB007595-B |
| GPU | supported | TBC | CB016066-C |

\*Details for the algorithm to estimate antenna-wise SINR for PUSCH are provided in 5GMAX algorithm specification (see [11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for 23R1 version.

\*\* For antenna-wise invalid measurement ([11108996](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koyACvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) in case of one or more Rx are failed, it is supported by Loner, Loki, Thor and Thor(Radio 7-2e) **L1 HW****.**

(Note 1) Supported as output from new Raw CEs (refer to [11102253](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knfmmfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

[CB008465-AA] (Note 2) Also supported with UL CoMP joint reception. [End CB008465-AA]

---

#### 2.4.6.8.4 Short-term pre-equalized SINR for UL CoMP

##### 2.4.6.8.4.0-1 Short-term pre-equalized SINR for UL CoMP Overview (ID: 12748020)

**2.4.6.8.4.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `12748040`)

Short-term pre-equalized SINR for UL CoMP
DU shall report short-term pre-equalized SINR for each UE scheduled for UL CoMP.

Input:

* UL CoMP activation flag (NRCELL.actNrUlComp)
* UL CoMP joint reception request or candidate cell SINR measurement request for the current UE (see [12742863](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_6OPBknyfEfCiUemk-K-C4g?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9))
* Serving cell ID
* Helper cell ID (when joint reception is requested)
* List of candidate cell IDs (when SINR measurements on candidate cells are requested)
* Antenna-wise SINR for the current UE on serving cell, helper cell and candidate cells (see [11108996](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koyACvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9))

Algorithm:

When NRCELL.actNrUlComp = "basic"

DU shall perform averaging in linear domain of antenna-wise SINR values (see [11108996](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koyACvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) from antennas of the serving cell.

When the UE is scheduled for joint reception

DU shall perform averaging in linear domain of antenna-wise SINR values (see [11108996](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koyACvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) from antennas of the helper cell.

Else, when the UE is scheduled for SINR measurement on candidate cells

For each candidate cell

DU shall perform averaging in linear domain of antenna-wise SINR values (see [11108996](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koyACvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) from antennas of the candidate cell.

Output:

* Averaged pre-equalized SINR

---

##### 2.4.6.8.4.0-2 Short-term pre-equalized SINR for UL CoMP Supported measurements for pre-equalized SINR for UL CoMP (ID: 12748023)

**2.4.6.8.4.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `12748062`)

Short-term pre-equalized SINR for UL CoMP
DU shall support short-term pre-equalized SINR measurement on following L1 HW.

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **L1 HW** | **Pre-equalized SINR** **for UL CoMP** **([12748020](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_9USZoH9qEfCiUemk-K-C4g?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)**)\*\*\*\* | **5GMAX algo. Spec. section** | **First Feature** |
| Loner | - |  |  |
| Loki | - |  |  |
| Thor | supported |  | CB008465-A |

---

#### 2.4.6.8.5 SINR for PUCCH

##### 2.4.6.8.5.0-1 SINR for PUCCH Overview (ID: 11109040)

**2.4.6.8.5.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11109043`)

SINR for PUCCH
For each scheduled UE on either PUCCH, 5GNB 5G-L1-UL shall estimate one measurement for the SINR. This estimate depends on PUCCH format as described in 5GMAX algorithm specification.

* For format0/1: No DMRS and no equalization is performed in L1. The SINR on PUCCH is computed as a ratio for RX power (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7094\_replaced([11108785](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kovjx_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and noise power (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_6903\_replaced([11108413](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koqrRfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))
* For other formats where DMRS is specified in 3GPP, the equalization is performed. For these formats, each SINR measurement is computed at the equalizer output, averaged over all the allocated REs for one NR-slot (not normalized to one PRB). The signals received on all the active antennas are taken into account.  Since only one port is used by PUCCH, one estimate shall be then provided to 5G-L2-PS for long term averaging

Note: As a post-equalizer SINR is provided to 5G-L2-PS (except for Format0/1, see above), its value depends on the equalizer type (e.g., MRC, IRC, etc.). For the usage of this measurement in 5G-L2-PS, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3623\_replaced([11160031](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktNNDvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4082\_replaced([11170623](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kt_3MPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4620\_replaced([11187546](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvcBofvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

For more details about the comparison between SINR post-equalizer and pre-equalizer (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2647\_replaced([11119377](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqJ5BPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), you can refer to 5GMAX algorithm specification, section 2.7.5.2 (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_695\_replaced([11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

Supported PUCCH formats could be deduced from 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1139\_replaced([11104445](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kn85mfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Since PUCCH is mapped to single antenna port (no layer mapping as per 38.211 section 6.3.2), 5G-L1-UL shall report invalid value for the SINR measurement associated to the layer index > 1, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1746\_replaced([11109764](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko7xC_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

##### 2.4.6.8.5.0-2 SINR for PUCCH Supported measurement for UL SINR for PUCCH (ID: 11109046)

**2.4.6.8.5.0-2.0-5**  (ID: `13342691`)

SINR for PUCCH
Supported measurement for UL SINR for PUCCH

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **L1 HW** | **UL SINR for PUCCH ([11109040](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koynGvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))** | **5GMAX algo. Spec. section**\* | **First Feature** |
| Loner | supported\*\* | Section 2.8.5.2 | - |
| Loki | supported\*\* | Section 2.8.5.2 | - |
| Thor | supported\*\* | Section 2.8.5.2 | CB007487-B CB007487-C CB007487-D |
| GPU | supported\*\* | TBC | CB016066-C |

\*Details for the algorithm to estimate SINR for PUCCH are provided in 5GMAX algorithm specification (see [11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

---

##### 2.4.6.8.5.0-3 SINR for PUCCH Measurement of UL SINR for PUCCH in FDD supercell (ID: 11109060)

**2.4.6.8.5.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11109063`)

SINR for PUCCH
In configuration of FDD supercell (MRBTS/NRBTS/NRCELL/cellTechnology = 'FDD' and MRBTS/NRBTS/NRCELL/actSupercell = True) on Loki and Thor DU shall perform PUCCH F3 SINR measurement for all serving and non-serving subcells as described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_28461\_replaced([11113896](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpfKqPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

#### 2.4.6.8.6 SINR for SRS

##### 2.4.6.8.6.0-1 SINR for SRS Overview (ID: 11109068)

**2.4.6.8.6.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `11109074`)

SINR for SRS
DU(L1) [CB007595-C]or Radio(L1) [End CB007595-C] shall compute the UL SINR based on SRS and report it to 5G-L2-PS to determine whether SRS measurements are reliable. Only one SINR measurement is reported and it's averaged over all RX antennas.

More details about its usage in 5G-L2-PS are given in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3081\_replaced([11131259](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krCCw_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[CB007595-C]

NOTE:

- "5G-L1-UL" is changed to "DU(L1)" since this version as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_20317\_replaced([11094116](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmZaYfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). (Note kept only in this version)

- For eCPRI 7-2e, Radio(L1) shall do the measurements and report results to 5G-L2-PS via DU(L1), otherwise DU(L1) shall do the measurements and report to 5G\_L2\_PS.

[End CB007595-C]

---

##### 2.4.6.8.6.0-2 SINR for SRS Supported measurement for UL SINR for SRS (ID: 11109077)

**2.4.6.8.6.0-2.0-6**  (ID: `13342697`)

SINR for SRS
Supported measurement for UL SINR for SRS

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **L1 HW** | **UL SINR for SRS ([11109068](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kozOI_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))** | **5GMAX algo. Spec. section**\* | **First Feature** |
| Loner | supported\*\* | Section 2.8.5.3 | - |
| Loki | supported\*\* | Section 2.8.5.3 | - |
| Thor | supported\*\* | Section 2.8.5.3 | CB007487-D |
| Thor (Radio 7-2e) | supported\*\* | Section 2.8.5.3 | **CB007595-C** |
| GPU | supported | TBC | CB016066-C |

\*Details for the algorithm to estimate SINR for SRS are provided in 5GMAX algorithm specification (see [11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

#### 2.4.6.8.7 Antenna-wise SINR for SRS

##### 2.4.6.8.7.0-1 Antenna-wise SINR for SRS Overview (ID: 11109097)

**2.4.6.8.7.0-1.0-3**  (ID: `11109105`)

Antenna-wise SINR for SRS
Additionally, DU(L1) [CB007595-C]or Radio(L1) [End CB007595-C] shall compute per rx antenna stream UL SINR based on SRS and report it to 5G-L2-PS. This [CNI-103796-K] "spatial-stream-wise" (refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29129\_replaced([11107886](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koj9lvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) [End CNI-103796-K] measurement is used for TDD FR1 beamforming cells, where the SINR is beam dependent for a give UE.

Mapping between antenna-wise sinr estimate and the spatial stream is given in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7236\_replaced([11109508](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko4tuvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

DU(L1) [CB007595-C]or Radio(L1) [End CB007595-C] does not compute the antenna-wise SINR on SRS for the non-configured antennas. It shall inform 5G-L2-PS that the reported measurement associated to the non-configured antenna is invalid, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1746\_replaced([11109764](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko7xC_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

For each antenna, when the Rx is failed(typically the case of RU lost or fiber broken or Rx failure, refer to 5G\_UP\_6136\_replaced([11377147](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ueUcnvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_6137\_replaced([11380144](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_uek7RPvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), DU(L1) [CB007595-C]or Radio(L1) [End CB007595-C] shall report INVALID value, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1746\_replaced([11109764](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko7xC_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

For L1 HW supported measurement refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7183\_replaced([11109107](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koz1MvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

NOTE: this measurement is not used in 5G-L2-PS.

[CB007595-C]

NOTE2:

- "5G-L1-UL" is changed to "DU(L1)" since this version as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_20317\_replaced([11094116](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmZaYfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). (Note kept only in this version)

- For eCPRI 7-2e, Radio(L1) shall do the measurements and report results to 5G-L2-PS via DU(L1), otherwise DU(L1) shall do the measurements and report to 5G\_L2\_PS.

[End CB007595-C]

---

#### 2.4.6.8.8 Port-wise SINR for SRS

##### 2.4.6.8.8.0-1 Port-wise SINR for SRS Ovewview (ID: 11109128)

**2.4.6.8.8.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `11109133`)

Port-wise SINR for SRS
DU(for 7-2a) [CB009527-A] and Radio(for 7-2e) [End CB009527-A] shall compute the simplified port-wise SINR based on SRS and report it for beam management (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_22633\_replaced([11157155](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ks6SEvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). This measurement is used for TDD FR1 short term beamforming cells, where the SINR is beam dependent for a give UE and SRS port.

Refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_14919\_replaced([11107414](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kofFF_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) [CB009527-A] and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26696\_replaced([11107622](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kohhW_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) [End CB009527-A] for the SINR calculation.

Note, this measurement shall report to L2(DU).

For the supported L1 HWs, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_24078\_replaced([11109136](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koz1QvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

**2.4.6.8.8.0-2.0-3**  (ID: `13396556`)

Port-wise SINR for SRS
DU(for 7-2a) and Radio(for 7-2e) shall support SRS port-wise SINR measurement for below L1 HWs:

| --- | --- | --- |
| **L1 HW** | **port-wise SINR for SRS ([11109128](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koz1OfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))** | **First Feature** |
| Loner | - | - |
| Loki | supported | CB008322-G |
| vDU | - | - |
| Thor | supported | CB008322-G |
| Thor (Radio 7-2e) | supported | CB009527-A |
| GPU | supported | CB016062-BD |

### 2.4.6.9 DTX

#### 2.4.6.9.1 DTX for data on PUSCH

##### 2.4.6.9.1.0-1 DTX for data on PUSCH Overview (ID: 11109150)

**2.4.6.9.1.0-1.0-5**  (ID: `12800275`)

DTX for data on PUSCH
For each UE, DU shall compute the PUSCH DTX metric when CRC is not OK according to the algorithm described in the 5Gmax algorithm specifications (see ([11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). When CRC is OK, DTX is set to FALSE. PUSCH DTX is used for multiple RRM algorithms.

For eCPRI 7-2e, Radio shall compute the PUSCH DTX metric and report DTX metric, DTX threshold and the temporary DTX status to DU according to the algorithm described in the 5Gmax algorithm specifications.

DU shall decide the final PUSCH DTX (used for multiple RRM algorithms) by taking the CRC result into consideration, if CRC is OK, final DTX is set to FALSE. If CRC is NOK, final DTX is set according to the metric comparison, as specified in 5Gmax algorithm specifications (see [11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

For eCPRI 7-2e and NRBTS\NRCELL\numberOfRxBeamforming=16RX and NRBTS\NRCELL\NRULMUMIMO\ulMaxMuMimoLayer **= 8, with TD/FD-PDP estimation mixture algorithm introduction (refer to [11102322](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kngNrfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) the Radio shall compute PUSCH DTX only for the beams belonging to the UE.**

When NRBTS\NRCELL\actOptPuschPrbAlloc = true, DU or Radio, for a given UE granted with PUSCH PRBs number (cf.  [11101624](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knZf_fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) and [11101647](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knaHBPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) equal to 1 or 2 PRBs,  
refer to "DTX for DATA on less than 3 PRBs PUSCH grant" [11109164](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko0cRvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ), **for DTX metric computation. If CRC is NOK: DU shall report final DTX according to the metric comparison.**

Note: For DTX metric, the DTX thresholds are defined for a given False Alarm **Target.  
- The False Alarm is defined as the Probability to detect Signal whereas no Signal presence on PUSCH Channel, as specified in 5Gmax algorithm specifications (see [11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).  
- The False Alarm Target is set to 0.5%,****or to 1% as function of the PUSCH Receiver order ([11094176](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmaBffvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), when the NIDD MRBTS/NRBTS/NRCELL/actOptPuschPrbAlloc = true****and the UE granted PRBs number as per [11109164](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko0cRvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).*****\****\*

[CB008465-AA] When UL CoMP is activated (NRCELL.actNrUlComp = 'basic'):

* When the UE is configured for joint reception (see [12763065](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_dTd9AIOSEfCOGMMH109ICw?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9))
* DU shall compute the DTX metric from the signal of the receiving antennas of the serving and the helper cell (see [12763061](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_gSJKEIOREfCOGMMH109ICw?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)).
* DU shall determine the DTX threshold considering joint reception receiver order:
  + If subcell type is 'A4', receiver order is 8Rx.
* When the UE is configured for SINR measurements on candidate cells, DU shall compute DTX metric and threshold from the signal of the serving cell.

[End CB008465-AA]

DTX thresholds presented in 5Gmax algorithm specifications (see [11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and they are specified in L1 EFS specification.

---

12800299:
DTX for data on PUSCH
12800299:
DTX for data on PUSCH
12800299:
DTX for data on PUSCH
When MRBTS/NRBTS/NRCELL/actOptPuschPrbAlloc = true, DU or Radio (for eCPRI 7-2e) shall perform PUSCH DTX algorithm on each PUSCH grant according to 5Gmax algorithm specifications [11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) as follows:

Input:

[CB008465-AA]

* UL CoMP activation flag (NRCELL.actNrUlComp)
* UL CoMP joint reception request for the current UE (see [12742863](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_6OPBknyfEfCiUemk-K-C4g?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9))

[End CB008465-AA]

* PUSCH Receiver order: number of spatial streams (e.g. 2Rx, or 4Rx, ...) configured per cell during cell setup phase ([11094176](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmaBffvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) [CB008465-A] or determined from joint reception request. [End CB008465-A]
* PUSCH PRB number ([11101624](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knZf_fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ), [11101647](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knaHBPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

Algorithm:

[CB008465-AA] When UL CoMP is activated (NRCELL.actNrUlComp = 'basic') and UE is scheduled for joint reception (see [12763065](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_dTd9AIOSEfCOGMMH109ICw?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9))

* If subcell type is 'A4', receiver order is 8RX

[End CB008465-AA]

When PUSCH Receiver order is equal to 2RX, DU shall compute PUSCH DTX metric based on legacy algorithm (as per [11109150](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko0cQfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)):

* For a given UE, DU shall use False Alarm
* Target = 0.5% when granted PRBs number is greater than 2 PRBs
* Target = 1% when granted PRBs number is equal to 1 or 2 PRBs,

When PUSCH Receiver order is higher than 2RX,

* For a given UE granted with PRBs number greater than 2 PRBs,
* DU or Radio shall compute PUSCH DTX metric based on legacy algorithm (as per [11109150](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko0cQfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9))
* For a given UE granted with PRBs number equal to 1 or 2 PRBs,
* DU or Radio**shall compute PUSCH DTX metric based on** new Noise Power Estimate, that is function of pre-Equalized and Noise Power Estimate based on covariance matrix of “disturbance” [image-0][image\_desc]The image contains the text "R̃dd" in a dark blue, slightly blurry font. The first letter is an uppercase "R" with a tilde above it. The following two letters are lowercase "d".[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_3972.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]([11102886](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knpXkPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ), [11102901](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knpXlPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))**. Both estimates are computed** on used DMRS (REs in Frequency Domain) only, as detailed in 5GMax algorithm specification ([11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).***\****\*

**Output:**

* Final DTX report for granted PUSCH.

**References:**

For general metric overview of DTX for DATA on PUSCH refer to [11109150](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko0cQfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

For supported L1 HW refer to [11109173](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko0cSPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)**.**

#### 2.4.6.9.2 DTX for HARQ-ACK on PUSCH

##### 2.4.6.9.2.0-1 DTX for HARQ-ACK on PUSCH Overview (ID: 11109207)

**2.4.6.9.2.0-1.0-5**  (ID: `11109221`)

DTX for HARQ-ACK on PUSCH
DU shall compute DTX metric for the HARQ-ACK bits received on PUSCH for each UE according to the algorithm described in the 5Gmax algorithm specifications (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_695\_replaced([11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7194\_replaced([11109224](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko1DW_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for the supported measurement per L1 HW).

* If the HARQ-ACK is transmitted with over 1 or 2 bits, LLR based Dtx detection method is used. The final DTX decision combines the outcome of this algorithm and DTX algorithm for data on PUSCH as described in 5GMAX algorithm specification The corresponding threshold and metric values are described in the 5Gmax algorithm specifications (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7194\_replaced([11109224](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko1DW_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).
* If the HARQ-ACK is transmitted with over [3..11] bits (Reed-Muller coding), Rel0d5SumAbsSoftBit Dtx detection method is used. The final DTX decision combines the outcome of this algorithm and DTX algorithm for data on PUSCH as described in 5GMAX algorithm specification. The corresponding threshold and metric values are described in ithe 5Gmax algorithm specifications (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7194\_replaced([11109224](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko1DW_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).
* If the HARQ-ACK is transmitted with equal or more than 12 bits (Polar coding) and less than 20bits, Path metric Dtx detection method is used. The final DTX decision combines the outcome of this algorithm and DTX algorithm for data on PUSCH as described in 5GMAX algorithm specification. The corresponding threshold and metric values, are described in the 5Gmax algorithm specifications (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7194\_replaced([11109224](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko1DW_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).
* If the HARQ-ACK is transmitted with equal or more than 20 bits (Polar coding), only CRC is used for Dtx detection as described in the 5Gmax algorithm specifications (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7194\_replaced([11109224](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko1DW_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

For the final DTX decision and when the number of HARQ-ACK bits is lower or equal than 11bits or UCI reception occurs during a SAg bundle or when the UE is configured with qam64LowSE MCS table, the DU shall apply a scaling (called as IfftThrFactor in 5GMAX algorithm specification) for the data DTX threshold as described in 5GMAX algorithm specification. This scaling depends on:

* the modulation scheme and the number of allocated PUSCH PRBs for the current UE.

Refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7185\_replaced([11109150](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko0cQfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for the DTX for data on PUSCH

[CB007595-B] For eCPRI 7-2e, the DU shall use the DTX for data on PUSCH estimated in the Radio (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7185\_replaced([11109150](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko0cQfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) as input for the final DTX decision when it applies [End CB007595-B]

The two terms used for HARQ-ACK DTX decision (i.e. the metric and the threshold) as well as the decision (i.e. DTX or nonDTX) are used for for DL HARQ retransmission (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4532\_replaced([11184193](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvQbg_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

Details about the supported number of HARQ-bits on PUSCH are given in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1082\_replaced([11103916](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kn2L6fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Note that the metric and the threshold used for DTX decision are not used in 5G-L2-PS and they are used only for debugging.

---

| Loner | supported with 'AdapV2’ or AdapV2.1' method\*\*\* | supported | supported | 2.3.12 | 2.3.12.1.2 & 2.3.12.1.3 | 2.3.12.2.2 & 2.3.12.2.3 | 2.3.12.3.1 | - |  |

NOTE: (Editorial NOTE to be removed from next release version). In the above table, the 5GMAX algorithm specification "sections" number are modified for alignment to 5GMAX algorithm paragraphs numbering refactoring.

\*\*\* For the determination of the scaling *IfftThrFactor (see [11109207](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko1DVfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), DU shall support:*

* 'AdapV2' **method:**

- when HARQ-ACK bits number is equal or less than 11bits with 64QAM, 256QAM tables and granted PRBs number is higher than 2.

* ‘AdapV2.1’ method:

-  for Loki and Thor **L1 HW and granted PRBs number smaller than 3,**

-  for Loner, Loki and Thor L1 HW and when HARQ-ACK bits number is equal or less than 19bits:

-  when UL slot aggregation is enabled and UCI reception occurs during a SAg bundle (cf.[11104379](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kn7rgfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)),

-  with 64QAMLow Mcs table (see [11103430](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knveNPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

#### 2.4.6.9.3 DTX for CSI Part1 on PUSCH

##### 2.4.6.9.3.0-1 DTX for CSI Part1 on PUSCH Overview (ID: 11109252)

**2.4.6.9.3.0-1.0-4**  (ID: `12575747`)

DTX for CSI Part1 on PUSCH
DU shall compute the PUSCH DTX metric for CSI part1 bits received on PUSCH according to the algorithm described in the 5Gmax algorithm specifications (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_695\_replaced([11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7199\_replaced([11109263](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko1qaPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for configuration).

* If the CSI part1 is transmitted with over 1 or 2 bits, LLR based Dtx detection method is used. The final DTX decision combines the outcome of this algorithm and DTX algorithm for data on PUSCH as described in 5GMAX algorithm specification The corresponding threshold and metric values are described in the 5Gmax algorithm specifications (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7199\_replaced([11109263](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko1qaPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).
* If the CSI part1 is transmitted with over [3..11] bits (Reed-Muller coding), Rel0d5SumAbsSoftBit Dtx detection method is used. The final DTX decision combines the outcome of this algorithm and DTX algorithm for data on PUSCH as described in 5GMAX algorithm specification. The corresponding threshold and metric values are described in ithe 5Gmax algorithm specifications (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7199\_replaced([11109263](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko1qaPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).
* If the CSI part1 is transmitted with equal or more than 12 bits (Polar coding) and less than 20bits, Path metric Dtx detection method is used. The final DTX decision combines the outcome of this algorithm and DTX algorithm for data on PUSCH as described in 5GMAX algorithm specification.. The corresponding threshold and metric values, are described in the 5Gmax algorithm specifications (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7199\_replaced([11109263](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko1qaPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).
* If the CSI part1 is transmitted with equal or more than 20bits, DTX decision is set according to CRC (11CRC bits in this case as per 3GPP).

For the final DTX decision and when the number of CSI part1 bits is lower or equal than 11bits , the DU shall apply a scaling (called as *IfftThrFactor in 5GMAX algorithm specification) for the data DTX threshold as described in 5GMAX algorithm specification. This scaling depends on:*

* the modulation scheme **and the number of allocated PUSCH PRBs for the current UE**

For the final DTX decision, when the UE is configured with qam64LowSE MCS table and when the number of CSI part1 bits is equal or more than 12 bits (Polar coding) and less than 20bits, the DU shall apply the scaling IfftThrFactor for the data DTX threshold as described in 5GMAX algorithm specification and it depends on:

the MCS index and the number of allocated PUSCH PRBs for the current UE.

[CB010981-B] When NRBTS/NRCELL/actPuschACSIPart2DtxEnhancement= true, DU shall compute a additional CSI part1 DTX decision as described in [12585288](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_lKcOZTV0EfCKaNlcHOQWmg). This DTX decision is used solely as input for CSI part2 DTX decision (see CSI part2 DTX detection in [11109286](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_IZZksPvIEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). [End CB010981-B]

Refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7185\_replaced([11109150](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko0cQfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for the DTX for data on PUSCH.

For eCPRI 7-2e, the DU shall use the DTX for data on PUSCH estimated in the Radio (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7185\_replaced([11109150](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko0cQfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) as input for the final DTX decision when it applies.

The two terms used for CSI Part1 DTX decision (i.e. the metric and the threshold) as well as the decision (i.e. DTX or nonDTX) should then be provided to 5G-L2-PS. **(add the reference for the usage).**

Details about the supported number of CSI part1 bits are given in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1056\_replaced([11103821](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kn09z_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Note that the metric and the threshold used for DTX decision are not used in 5G-L2-PS and they are used only for debugging.

~~Note: The false alarm target for this metric is set to 0.5% with false alarm is defined as the probability to detect UCI bits whereas nothing was sent on PUSCH.~~

---

##### 2.4.6.9.3.0-2 DTX for CSI Part1 on PUSCH Additional CSI Part1 DTX decision for CSI Part2 detection (ID: 12585288)

**2.4.6.9.3.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `12585289`)

DTX for CSI Part1 on PUSCH
Algorithm for additional CSI Part1 DTX decision

Input:

* NIDD parameter NRBTS/NRCELL/actPuschACSIPart2DtxEnhancement
* Number of CSI1 information bits
* CSI Part1 DTX metrics and thresholds
* Threshold reduction factor

Algorithm:

When NRBTS/NRCELL/actPuschACSIPart2DtxEnhancement = true

When CSI Part1 payload is less than 12 bits,

DU shall compute an additional biased DTX decision based on:

* Same DTX methods depending on the number of CSI Part1 information bits, as described in [11109252](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_H_i6QPvIEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)
* Thresholds lowered by the input threshold reduction factor.

Else

DU shall use CSI Part1 DTX decision as input to CSI Part2 DTX detection

Details are described in 5GMax Algorithm Specification (see [11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_xwpEkPvGEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

Rational:

DU shall combine CSI Part1 DTX decision with CSI Part2 DTX decision for twofold rationals:

* When CSI Part1 payload size is less than 12 bits, in order to reduce CSI Part2 misdetection probability  (refer to [12585692](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_gMce0DV-EfCKaNlcHOQWmg?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9))
* When CSI Part1 payload size is 12 bits or more and NRBTS/NRCELL/actPuschACSIPart2DtxEnhancement = true: CSI Part2 validity conditions in system are changed as per [11160579](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktPCNfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9).

Output:

Additional CSI Part1 DTX decision (DTX / nonDTX)

---

##### 2.4.6.9.3.0-3 DTX for CSI Part1 on PUSCH Supported measurement for DTX for CSI Part1 on PUSCH (ID: 11109263)

**2.4.6.9.3.0-3.0-7**  (ID: `13342753`)

DTX for CSI Part1 on PUSCH
Supported measurement for DTX for CSI Part1 on PUSCH

|  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **L1 HW** | **DTX for CSI Part1** **on PUSCH** **([11109252](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko1qZPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))** | **Metric for CSI Part1 on PUSCH** **([11109252](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko1qZPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))** | **Threshold for CSI Part1 on PUSCH** **([11109252](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko1qZPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))** | Biased CSI Part1 DTX decision ([12585288](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_lKcOZTV0EfCKaNlcHOQWmg)) | **5GMAX** | **algo.** | **Spec.** | **section**\* |  |  | **First Feature** |
| **General** | **[1..2] bits** | **[3..11] bits** | **[12..19] bits** | **≥ 20bits** |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| Loner | supported with 'AdapV2’ or 'AdapV2.1' method\*\*\* | supported | supported | - | 2.3.13 | 2.3.13.1.2 & 2.3.13.1.3 | 2.3.13.2.2 & 2.3.13.2.3 | 2.3.13.3.1 | 2.3.13.3.2 |  |  |
| Loki | supported with 'AdapV2’ or 'AdapaV2.1' method\*\*\* | supported | supported | Supported | 2.3.13 | 2.3.13.1.2 & 2.3.13.1.3 | 2.3.13.2.2 & 2.3.13.2.3 | 2.3.13.3.1 | 2.3.13.3.2 |  | CB007950-A |
| Thor | supported with 'AdapV2’ or 'AdapV2.1' method\*\*\* | supported | supported | supported | 2.3.13 | 2.3.13.1.2 & 2.3.13.1.3 | 2.3.13.1.2 & 2.3.12.1.3 | 2.3.13.3.1 | 2.3.13.3.2 |  | CB007487-B CB007487-C CB007487-D |
| GPU | supported | supported | supported | - | TBC | TBC | TBC | TBC | TBC |  | CB016066-C |

\*Details for the algorithm to estimate DTX for CSI Part1 on PUSCH are provided in 5GMAX algorithm specification (see [11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

\*\*\* For the determination of the scaling *IfftThrFactor (see [11109252](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko1qZPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), DU shall support:*

* 'AdapV2' **method:**

- when CSI Part1 bits number is equal or less than 11bits with 64QAM, 256QAM tables and granted PRBs number is higher than 2.

* ‘AdapV2.1’ method:

- for Loki and Thor L1 HW and PRBs granted number smaller than 3

- for Loner, Loki and Thor L1 HW and when CSI Part1 bits number is equal or less than 19bits:

-  with 64QAMLow Mcs table (see [11103430](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knveNPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

#### 2.4.6.9.4 DTX for CSI Part2 on PUSCH

##### 2.4.6.9.4.0-1 DTX for CSI Part2 on PUSCH Overview (ID: 11109286)

**2.4.6.9.4.0-1.0-4**  (ID: `12576104`)

DTX for CSI Part2 on PUSCH
DU shall compute the PUSCH DTX metric for CSI part2 bits received on PUSCH according to the algorithm described in the 5Gmax algorithm specifications (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_695\_replaced([11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7207\_replaced([11109297](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko2Rc_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

* If the CSI part2 is transmitted with over 1 or 2 bits, LLR based Dtx detection method is used. The corresponding threshold and metric values, are described in the 5Gmax algorithm specifications (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7207\_replaced([11109297](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko2Rc_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). When NIDD NRBTS/NRCELL/actPuschACSIPart2ThrAdj = true [CB010981-B] or NRBTS/NRCELL/actPuschACSIPart2DtxEnhancement = true [End CB010981-B], for any configured table with PUSCH MCS in QPSK modulation order, DU shall support the CSI Part2 DTX Threshold **Adjustment according to 5Gmax algorithm specifications (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_695\_replaced([11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))** . The final DTX decision combines the outcome of this algorithm and DTX algorithm for data on PUSCH as described in 5GMAX algorithm specification.\*\*\*\*
* If the CSI part2 is transmitted with over [3..11] bits (Reed-Muller coding), Rel0d5SumAbsSoftBit Dtx detection method is used. The corresponding threshold and metric values, are described in the 5Gmax algorithm specifications (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7207\_replaced([11109297](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko2Rc_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).  When NIDD NRBTS/NRCELL/actPuschACSIPart2ThrAdj = true [CB010981-B] or NRBTS/NRCELL/actPuschACSIPart2DtxEnhancement = true [End CB010981-B], for any configured table with PUSCH MCS in QPSK modulation order, DU shall support the CSI Part2 DTX Threshold **Adjustment according to 5Gmax algorithm specifications (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_695\_replaced([11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))**. The final DTX decision combines the outcome of this algorithm and DTX algorithm for data on PUSCH as described in 5GMAX algorithm specification.\*\*\*\*
* If the CSI part2 is transmitted with equal or more than 12 bits (Polar coding) and less than 20bits, Path metric Dtx detection method is used. The corresponding threshold and metric values, are described in the 5Gmax algorithm specifications (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7207\_replaced([11109297](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko2Rc_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). **The final DTX decision combines the outcome of this algorithm and DTX algorithm for data on PUSCH as described in 5GMAX algorithm specification**

For the final DTX decision and when the number of CSI part2 bits is lower or equal than 11bits, a scaling (called as IfftThrFactor in 5GMAX algorithm specification) is applied for the data DTX threshold as described in 5GMAX algorithm specification. This scaling depends on:

* the modulation scheme and the number of allocated PUSCH PRBs for the current UE

For the final DTX decision, when the UE is configured with qam64LowSE MCS table and when the number of CSI part2 bits is equal or more than 12 bits (Polar coding) and less than 20bits, the scaling IfftThrFactor is applied the data DTX threshold as described in 5GMAX algorithm specification and it depends on:

the MCS index and the number of allocated PUSCH PRBs for the current UE.

When CSI part2 is punctured by HARQ-ACK, specific DTX threshold values are used for the final DTX decision as specified in the 5GMAX algorithm specifications (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7207\_replaced([11109297](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko2Rc_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

[CB010981-B] When NIDD parameter NRBTS/NRCELL/actPuschACSIPart2DtxEnhancement = true, DU shall use CSI Part1 DTX decision as input to CSI Part2 DTX decision, as described in [12585692](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_gMce0DV-EfCKaNlcHOQWmg?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9). [End CB010981-B]

Refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7185\_replaced([11109150](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko0cQfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for the DTX for data on PUSCH.

For eCPRI 7-2e, the DU shall use the DTX for data on PUSCH estimated in the Radio (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7185\_replaced([11109150](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko0cQfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) as input for the final DTX decision when it applies

The two terms used for CSI Part2 DTX decision (i.e. the metric and the threshold) as well as the decision (i.e. DTX or nonDTX) should then be provided to 5G-L2-PS (refer to 5G\_L2\_5879\_replaced([11172350](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26VPW_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

Details about the supported number of CSI part2 bits are given in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1068\_replaced([11103859](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kn1k3PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Note that the metric and the threshold used for DTX decision are not used in 5G-L2-PS and they are used only for debugging.

---

##### 2.4.6.9.4.0-2 DTX for CSI Part2 on PUSCH Combining with CSI Part1 DTX decision (ID: 12585692)

**2.4.6.9.4.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `12585712`)

DTX for CSI Part2 on PUSCH
Algorithm for combining CSI Part2 with CSI Part1 DTX result

Input:

* NIDD NRBTS/NRCELL/actPuschACSIPart2DtxEnhancement
* NIDD NRBTS/NRCELL/actPuschACSIPart2ThrAdj
* Number of CSI Part2 information bits
* CSI Part2 DTX status
* Number of CSI Part1 information bits
* Biased CSI Part1 DTX status (see [12585288](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_lKcOZTV0EfCKaNlcHOQWmg))
* CSI Part1 DTX result

Algorithm:

When NRBTS/NRCELL/actPuschACSIPart2DtxEnhancement = true

When CSI Part1 payload size is less than 12 information bits

DU shall use additional biased CSI Part1 DTX decision described in [12585288](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_lKcOZTV0EfCKaNlcHOQWmg) as input to CSI Part2 DTX decision, as described in 5GMax algorithm specification (see [11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_xwpEkPvGEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Else when CSI Part1 payload size is 12 information bits or more (i.e. CSI1 is polar-encoded)

DU shall use CSI Part1 DTX decision (see [11109252](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_H_i6QPvIEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) as input to CSI Part2 DTX decision, as described 5GMax algorithm specification ([11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_xwpEkPvGEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Else

DU shall output CSI Part2 DTX decision based on NRBTS/NRCELL/actPuschACSIPart2ThrAdj as described in [11109286](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_IZZksPvIEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)​.​​​​​​

Rational:

DU shall combine CSI Part1 DTX decision with CSI Part2 DTX decision for twofold rationals:

* When CSI Part1 payload size is less than 12 bits, in order to reduce CSI Part2 misdetection probability  (refer to [12585692](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_gMce0DV-EfCKaNlcHOQWmg?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9))
* When CSI Part1 payload size is 12 bits or more and NRBTS/NRCELL/actPuschACSIPart2DtxEnhancement = true: CSI Part2 validity conditions in system are changed as per [11160579](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktPCNfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9).

Output:

CSI Part2 DTX decision

| Thor | supported with 'AdapV2’ or 'AdapV2.1' method \*\*\* | supported | supported \*\*\*\* | 2.3.13 | 2.3.13.1.2 & 2.3.13.1.3 | 2.3.13.2.2 & 2.3.13.2.3 | 2.3.13.3.1 | 2.3.13.3.2 | CB008325-5G-BA, CB008325-5G-CA |

NOTE: When CSI part2 is punctured by ACK-NACK, the DTX threshold values are specified in the section $2.3.13.1.2.1 and $2.3.13.1.2.2 of 5GMAX algorithm specifications (see [11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

\*Details for the algorithm to estimate DTX for CSI Part2 on PUSCH are provided in 5GMAX algorithm specification (see [11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

\*\*\* For the determination of the scaling *IfftThrFactor (see [11109286](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko1qb_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), DU shall support:*

* 'AdapV2' **method:**

- when CSI Part2 bits number is equal or less than 11bits with 64QAM, 256QAM tables and granted PRBs number higher than 2.

* ‘AdapV2.1’ method:

-  for Loki and Thor**L1 HW and PRBs granted number smaller than 3**

-  for Loner, Loki Thor L1 HW and when CSI Part2 bits number is equal or less than 19bits:

-  with 64QAMLow Mcs table (see [11103430](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knveNPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

***\* DU for Loki* \*and Thor** L1 HW shall perform:\*\*\*\*

* CSI Part2 DTX Threshold **Adjustment** (see [11109286](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko1qb_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) when NIDD NRBTS/NRCELL/actPuschACSIPart2ThrAdj = true as specified in §2.3.13.1**.2.2 and §2.3.13.2.**2.2 of 5GMAX algorithm specifications (see [11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))***\****\*
* or combine CSI Part2 DTX decision with CSI Part1 contribution when NIDD NRBTS/NRCELL/actPuschACSIPart2DtxEnhancement = true (see [12585692](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_gOxaEDV-EfCKaNlcHOQWmg) specified in § 2.3.13.1.3.1 and §2.3.13.2.3.1 of 5GMax algorithm specification (see [11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

**NOTE: From System perspective, DU for L1HW:  
- Loner [CB016061-B1] and GPU [End CB016061-B1] shall ignore NIDD NRBTS/NRCELL/actPuschACSIPart2ThrAdj or NRBTS/NRCELL/actPuschACSIPart2DtxEnhancement even when set to true.**

#### 2.4.6.9.5 PUCCH DTX

##### 2.4.6.9.5.0-1 PUCCH DTX Overview (ID: 11109316)

**2.4.6.9.5.0-1.0-3**  (ID: `13406069`)

PUCCH DTX
13406069:
PUCCH DTX
13406069:
PUCCH DTX
13406069:
PUCCH DTX
13406069:
PUCCH DTX
13406069:
PUCCH DTX
13406069:
PUCCH DTX

PUCCH DTX
13406069:

13406069:
PUCCH DTX
13406069:

13406069:

13406069:
PUCCH DTX

13406069:

13406069:

PUCCH DTX
13406069:
PUCCH DTX
13406069:
PUCCH DTX
13406069:
PUCCH DTX
13406069:
PUCCH DTX
DU should compute a metric that indicates whether a PUCCH signal was transmitted and received as expected. The false alarm target for this metric shall be lower than 1% to meet 3GPP minimum requirements in 38.104. False alram target for each PUCCH format is provided in 5GMAX algorithm specification.

**NOTE: the false alarm is defined as the probability to detect UCI reporting whereas nothing was sent on PUCCH as per 38.104 specification.**

For PUCCH Format 0 and Format 1:

* DTX or nonDTX decision depends on the comparison of one metric to the appropriate threshold as described in 5GMAX algorithm specification (see [11109326](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko2RffvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). The DTX thresholds are specified in 5GMAX and they depend on the presence of SR, AN, SR and AN, number of AN bits, etc.
* For Format0, since SR is reported in the same resource as for HARQ-AN (see 38.213 section 9.2.5.1), then only one DTX metric is computed for all UCI on PUCCH Format0 and it’s reported to 5G-L2-PS
* For Format1:
  + If only SR is expected and no HARQ-AN in a given slot: DTX is computed for PRBs resources configured for SR reporting (see 38.213 section 9.2.4) and one DTX metric is reported to 5G-L2-PS for the current UE
  + If only HARQ-AN is reported by the UE and no SR is expected in the same slot, DTX is computed for the PRBs resource configured for HARQ-AN (see 38.213 section 9.2.3) and one DTX metric is reported to 5G-L2-PS
  + If HARQ-AN is reported by the UE during one SR slot, DTX is computed for the 2 below PUCCH resources. Then it shall report two DTX decisions for the current UE, one for each resource. The final DTX decision for HARQ-AN feedback is done according to [11184193](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvQbg_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) and for SR decision is done according to [11187513](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvbamfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)
  + PRBs resource configured for SR which should be used by the UE to report HARQ-AN and positive SR in the same slot (see 38.213 section 9.2.5.1).
  + PRBs resource configured for HARQ-AN which should be used by the UE to report HARQ-AN in case of no positive SR (see 38.213 section 9.2.5.1).

For PUCCH Format 2:

* DTX metric depends on the number of UCI bits as described in 5GMAX algorithm specification (see [11109326](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko2RffvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). When the number of UCI bits exceed 19, CRC is taken into account for DTX detection. The DTX metric and the DTX thresholds are given in the same specification

For PUCCH Format 3:

* When the number of UCI bits is lower than 20 bits, [CB010737-B] three methods are proposed in 5GMax specification [End CB010737-B]:
* Method1: DTX metric is computed according to equivalent SNR in time domain (“**iFFTbased”) based algorithm as described in 5GMAX algorithm specification (see [11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), with DTX Threshold values (adapted to number of RX antennas) are given in Table 2‑32.**
* Method2: **DTX metric is computed according to “**DecodingBased” based algorithm as described in 5GMAX algorithm specification (see [11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), with DTX Threshold values (adapted to number of RX antennas) are given in Tables 2‑34 and 2-36 (respectively for Reed Muller code or Polar code and more than 1RX). Method2 is recommended as outperforming Method1, refer to NR 5GMAX simulations results: [[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a blurry, abstract representation of a file icon. It features a large, out-of-focus orange rectangle on the left, with a blurred white shape behind it. Within the orange rectangle, there's a very blurry, lighter-colored shape that might be a letter "p". To the right of the orange rectangle, there's a white area with some faint, blurry orange shapes that could be stylized text or graphics. The overall impression is of a digital file icon, possibly for a presentation or document, but the blurriness makes specific identification impossible.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_4023.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]performance evaluation of Long PUCCH with IRC or pre-whitening.pptx](https://nokia.sharepoint.com/:p:/r/sites/5gmax/_layouts/15/Doc.aspx?sourcedoc=%7BBDD569CE-AA6E-4C15-8D4B-561099477271%7D&file=performance%20evaluation%20of%20Long%20PUCCH%20with%20IRC%20or%20pre-whitening.pptx&action=edit&mobileredirect=true).\*\*\*\*
* [CB010737-B] Method3: Only when UCI is polar-encoded (from 12 to 19 information bits), DTX status is determined from decoder path metrics according to "PmMinBased" algorithm, as described in 5GMAX algorithm specification (see [11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). [End CB010737-B]
* Otherwise, when the number of UCI bits is higher than 19 bits , as described in 5GMAX algorithm specification (see [11109326](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko2RffvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), DTX decision is based on:
* CRC for correct 11bits CRC
* on iFFTbased DTX Method for not correct 11bits CRC,

For the supported method per L1 HW and 5GMAX section, refer to [11109326](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko2RffvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

The two terms used for PUCCH DTX decision (i.e. the metric and the threshold) as well as the decision (i.e. DTX or nonDTX) should then be provided to 5G-L2-PS. 5G-L2-PS could use the DTX indication to filter reliable measurements performed on PUCCH (e.g. TO), to handle DL retransmission, etc.

Note that the metric and the threshold used for DTX decision are not used in 5G-L2-PS and they are used only for debugging.

---
