| L1 HW | Format | DTX for PUCCH ([11109316](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko2RevvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) | DTX metric for PUCCH ([11109316](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko2RevvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) | DTX threshold for PUCCH ([11109316](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko2RevvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) | Polar decoder path metrics for PUCCH ([11109316](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko2RevvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) | 5GMAX algo. Spec. section *| First Feature |
| Loner | F0 | supported | supported | supported | - | section 2.4.1.6 | - |
| F1 | supported \*\* | supported \*\* | supported \*\* | - | section 2.4.2.8 | - |
| F2 | supported | supported | supported | - | section 2.4.3.10 | - |
| F3 | supported with iFFTbased Method | supported with iFFTbased Method | supported with iFFTbased Method | - | section 2.4.4.13 | - |
| Loki | F0 | supported | supported | supported | - | section 2.4.1.6 | - |
| F1 | supported \*\* | supported \*\* | supported \*\* | - | section 2.4.2.8 | CB007950-A |
| F2 | supported | supported | supported | - | section 2.4.3.10 | - |
| F3 | supported with iFFTbased, DecodingBased [CB010737-B] and PmMinBased [End CB010737-B] Method | supported with iFFTbased and DecodingBased Method | supported with iFFTbased and DecodingBased Method | [CB010737-B] Supported with PmMinBased  method [End CB010737-B] | section 2.4.4.13 | CB007950-A |
| Thor | F0 | - | - | - | | - | - |
| F1 | supported \*\* | supported \*\* | supported \*\* | - | section 2.4.2.8 | CB007487-B CB007487-C CB007487-D |
| F2 | - | - | - | | - | - |
| F3 | supported with iFFTbased, DecodingBased [CB010737-B] and PmMinBased [End CB010737-B] Method | supported with iFFTbased and DecodingBased Method | supported with iFFTbased and DecodingBased Method | [CB010737-B] Supported with PmMinBased method [End CB010737-B] | section 2.4.4.13 | CB007487-B CB007487-C CB007487-D |
| GPU | F0 | - | - | - | - | - | CB016066-C |
| F1 | supported (***) | supported (***) | supported (***) | - | ~~~~TBC~~~~ section 2.4.2.8 |
| F2 | - | - | - | - | - |
| F3 | supported(***) | supported (***) | supported (***) | - | ~~~~TBC~~~~ section 2.4.4.13 |
\* Details for the algorithm to estimate DTX on PUCCH are provided in 5GMAX algorithm specification (see [11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
(***) DU for GPU L1 HW shall support PUCCH F1 MLD/DTX after Interference Rejection (pre-whitening) and PUCCH F3 MMSE-IRC DTX Interference Rejection algorithm based. 
For PUCCH F3, DU shall support DTX algorithm, metric and thresholds output based on: 
- the most likely codeword deduced from Reed-Muller decoding (for 3 bits<= UCI information bits <= 11bit)
- post-combined frequency domain SNR based (for 12 bits<= UCI information bits <= 19bit)
as per 5Gmax algorithm specification (see [11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
\*\* PUCCH Format 1:
- MLD detection method is chosen based on the 5Gmax algorithm specification (see [11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) .**- DU for:**- Thor and GPU L1 HW when NRBTS/NRCELL/nbRxbeamforming = 8Rx and NRBTS/NRCELL/numberOfRxPucch= 8Rx
- Thor L1 HW when NRBTS/NRCELL/act8RXnonBeamforming = true**- Loki and Thor L1 HW for 2RX and 4RX when**NRBTS/NRCELL/actPucchF1InterfEnhancement = true****shall perform the MLD Signal Detection after Interference Rejection (pre-whitening) as based on 5Gmax algorithm specification (see [11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
Refer to Receiver scheme processing with pre-whitening [11104921](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koDnS_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) and without pre-whitening [11104914](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koDnSfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9).
From System perspective, NIDDs NRBTS/NRCELL/actPucchF1InterfEnhancement and NRBTS/NRCELL/actPucchF3EnhanceDTX, are not used (even when set to true) and are ignored in DU with Loner and GPU L1 HWs.
[Before CB010737-B]
For PUCCH Format 3 and when different DTX methods are supported (see above table), DU shall apply:
1) iFFTbased DTX Method for UCI information bits > 19 bits for not correct 11bits CRC
2)** DecodingBased DTX Method for 3 bits<= UCI information bits <= 19bits.**For L1 HW:
- Loki and Thor when NRBTS/NRCELL/actPucchF3EnhanceDTX = true;
3)** iFFTbased **DTX Method for 3 bits<= UCI information bits <= 19bits.**\*\*
For L1 HW:
- Loki and Thor when NRBTS/NRCELL/actPucchF3EnhanceDTX = false
From System perspective, NIDD NRBTS/NRCELL/actPucchF3EnhanceDTX is not used (even when set to true) and is ignored in DU with Loner and GPU L1 HW. 
NOTE: Based on NR 5GMAX L1 Algorithm ([11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), for PUCCH F3 Polar Code with UCI Payload for 12 bits<= UCI information bits <= 19bits when NIDD NRBTS/NRCELL/actPucchAdditionalDmrs = TRUE, DTX Decoding Based Method  (NRBTS/NRCELL/actPucchF3EnhanceDTX = TRUE) performs worse than iFFTbased DTX Method (NRBTS/NRCELL/actPucchF3EnhanceDTX = FALSE). Refer to  [[image-0][image\_desc]The image displays two overlapping icons, both representing Microsoft PowerPoint files. The icon in the foreground is a red rectangle with a white "P" in the center. The icon in the background is a white document with a red PowerPoint logo in the upper right corner.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_4030.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0] performance evaluation of NR PUCCH F3 DTX detection.pptx](https://nokia.sharepoint.com/:p:/r/sites/5gmax/_layouts/15/Doc.aspx?sourcedoc=%7B5A9590B8-8353-4663-A9AF-41BA1FF08ABF%7D&file=performance%20evaluation%20of%20NR%20PUCCH%20F3%20DTX%20detection.pptx&action=edit&mobileredirect=true) simulations. From System perspective, a PDL rule is introduced to avoid KPIs performance impact from features interactions: either NRBTS/NRCELL/actPucchAdditionalDmrs or NRBTS/NRCELL/actPucchF3EnhanceDTX needs to be disabled
[CB010737-B] For PUCCH Format 3, the selection of the DTX detection method is described in [13407455](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_SAoTUA2pEfGu_4itz1o3RA?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9).
[End CB010737-B]

---

##### 2.4.6.9.5.0-3 PUCCH DTX Selection of PUCCH Format3 DTX detection method (ID: 13407455)

**2.4.6.9.5.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `13407456`)

PUCCH DTX
Note (not to be kept in next version): This requirement results from refactoring of the note of PUCCH F3 DTX in [11109326](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko2RffvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9).

DU shall select the DTX detection method as per the following algorithm:

Input:

* UCI payload size
* UCI decoding output (CRC)
* NIDD parameter NRBTS/NRCELL/actPucchF3EnhanceDTX
* PUCCH additional DMRS configuration from upper layer, as described in [11600334](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Y3V7BfwOEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)

Algorithm:

When the number of UCI bits is greater or equal to 20

When CRC is passed

DU shall report the PUCCH signal as non-DTX'ed

Else

DU shall apply 'iFFTbased' DTX detection method

Else, when more than one DTX detection methods for PUCCH format 3 are supported by the L1 HW variant (see table in [11109326](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko2RffvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) and NRBTS/NRCELL/actPucchF3EnhanceDTX = TRUE

[Before CB010737-B]

DU shall apply 'DecodingBased' DTX detection method

[CB010737-B]

When the number of information bits is greater than 11 and and lower than 20 (UCI is polar-encoded) and PUCCH additional DMRS is configured (see [11600334](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Y3V7BfwOEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9))

DU shall apply 'PmMinBased' DTX detection method

Else

DU shall apply 'DecodingBased' DTX detection method

[End CB010737-B]

Else

DU shall apply 'iFFTbased' DTX detection method

When only 'iFFTbased' method is supported by the L1 HW variant (see table in [11109326](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko2RffvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)), DU shall ignore the value of parameter NRBTS/NRCELL/actPucchF3EnhanceDTX = TRUE

Output:

* PUCCH Format3 DTX status

[CB010737-B] When 'iFFTBased' or 'DecodingBased' method is applied: [End CB010737-B]

* PUCCH Format3 DTX metric (not used by upper layers)
* PUCCH Format3 DTX threshold (not used by upper layers)

[CB010737-B] When 'PmMinBased' method is applied:

* Path metric of the decoded codeword PMdecoded (not used by upper layers)
* Metric of the first path PMall(1) (not used by upper layers)

[End CB010737-B]

[Before CB010737-B] NOTE: Based on NR 5GMAX L1 Algorithm ([11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), for PUCCH F3 Polar Code with UCI Payload for 12 bits<= UCI information bits <= 19bits when NIDD NRBTS/NRCELL/actPucchAdditionalDmrs = TRUE, DTX Decoding Based Method  (NRBTS/NRCELL/actPucchF3EnhanceDTX = TRUE) performs worse than iFFTbased DTX Method (NRBTS/NRCELL/actPucchF3EnhanceDTX = FALSE). From System perspective, a PDL rule is introduced to avoid KPIs performance impact from features interactions: either NRBTS/NRCELL/actPucchAdditionalDmrs or NRBTS/NRCELL/actPucchF3EnhanceDTX needs to be disabled.

[End CB010737-B]

Refer to 5GMax simulations:

* 'DecodingBased' method: [performance evaluation of NR PUCCH F3 DTX detection.pptx](https://nokia.sharepoint.com/:p:/r/sites/5gmax/Shared%20Documents/SimulationReports/NR/CFAM/5GC000885%20Long%20PUCCH/performance%20evaluation%20of%20NR%20PUCCH%20F3%20DTX%20detection.pptx?d=w5a9590b883534663a9af41ba1ff08abf&csf=1&web=1&e=1T4btN)

[CB010737-B]

* 'PmMinBased' method: [performance evaluation of NR PUCCH F3 DTX detection with Polar code.pptx](https://nokia.sharepoint.com/:p:/r/sites/5gmax/Shared%20Documents/SimulationReports/NR/CFAM/5GC000885%20Long%20PUCCH/performance%20evaluation%20of%20NR%20PUCCH%20F3%20DTX%20detection%20with%20Polar%20code.pptx?d=w3a48f2e4fc6845d68addc9953ad8c8a1&csf=1&web=1&e=VlpDYr)

[End CB010737-B]

#### 2.4.6.9.6 SRS DTX

##### 2.4.6.9.6.0-1 SRS DTX Overview (ID: 11109359)

**2.4.6.9.6.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `11109364`)

SRS DTX
There should be a metric in DU(L1) [CB007595-C]or Radio(L1) [End CB007595-C] that indicates whether an SRS signal was transmitted and received as expected. The false alarm target for this metric is set to 1%, where the false alarm is defined as the probability to detect a signal whereas nothing was sent on SRS.

The DTX decision (i.e. DTX or nonDTX) should then be provided to 5G-L2-PS and 5G-L2-PS could use the DTX indication to filter reliable measurements performed on SRS (e.g. RI).

[CB007595-C]

NOTE:

- "5G-L1-UL" is changed to "DU(L1)" since this version as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_20317\_replaced([11094116](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmZaYfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). (Note kept only in this version)

- For eCPRI 7-2e, Radio(L1) shall do the measurements and report results to 5G-L2-PS via DU(L1), otherwise DU(L1) shall do the measurements and report to 5G\_L2\_PS.

[End CB007595-C]

---

**2.4.6.9.6.0-2.0-5**  (ID: `13342765`)

SRS DTX
Supported measurement for DTX on SRS

| Loner | supported \*\* | section ~~2.6.7~~ 2.6.6 | - |
| Loki | supported \*\* | section ~~2.6.7~~ 2.6.6 | - |
| Thor | supported \*\* | section ~~2.6.7~~ 2.6.6 | CB007487-B CB007487-C CB007487-D |
| Thor (Radio 7-2e) | supported \*\* | section ~~2.6.7~~ 2.6.6 | CB07595-C |
| GPU | supported **/**\* | ~~TBC~~ section 2.6.6 | CB016066-C |

\*Details for the algorithm to DTX on SRS are provided in 5GMAX algorithm specification (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_695\_replaced([11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

\*\* SRS DTX implementation is based on "antenna-wise" approach described in the 5Gmax algorithm specifications (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_695\_replaced([11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

\*\*\* SRS for DTX determination is calculated on allocated pilots (averaged over RX antennas and PRBs).

#### 2.4.6.9.7 SRS CoMa DTX

##### 2.4.6.9.7.0-1 SRS CoMa DTX Overview (ID: 11109383)

**2.4.6.9.7.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11109386`)

SRS CoMa DTX
There should be a metric in Radio(L1) that validate Gramian matrix Cov(H) and Cov(V) for each polarization per SRS port. Detail algorithm please refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23226\_replaced([11107200](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kodP4fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

The DTX decision (i.e. DTX or nonDTX) should then be provided to higher layer.

Supported measurement per L1 HW, please refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_24056\_replaced([11109388](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko24kPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

### 2.4.6.10 UL Rank

#### 2.4.6.10.0-1 UL Rank Overview - UL rank (ID: 11109409)

**2.4.6.10.0-1.0-7**  (ID: `12504349`)

UL Rank
For each scheduled UE on either PUSCH or SRS, DU or Radio shall estimate one measurement for the UL rank which shall be used as short term value to feed the long term rank computed by 5G-L2-PS cf. 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3223\_replaced([11139902](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krqU7_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

For SU receiver, DU or Radio shall use the signals received on all active antennas **(or "spatial streams" as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29129\_replaced([11107886](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koj9lvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) to derive those measurements.**

For MU receiver (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8756\_replaced([11103056](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knrMwPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for the supported L1 HWs),

o For C8 [CB008235-D-UPD002] , C16'4, and C16'8 [End CB008235-D-UPD002] when NRCELL.actPmiSinrUlMuMimo ([11355703](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucTDWfvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) is false, [Before CB008235-D-UPD002] C16'4, and C16'8 [End CB008235-D-UPD002], DU  or Radio shall use the signals received on own UE antennas **(meaning "spatial streams" as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29129\_replaced([11107886](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koj9lvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) to derive those measurements**

o For C8 [CB008235-D-UPD002] , C16'4, and C16'8 [End CB008235-D-UPD002] and NRCELL.actPmiSinrUlMuMimo ([11355703](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucTDWfvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))  is true, DU [CB008235-D-UPD002] or Radio [End CB008235-D-UPD002]shall use the signals received on all active antennas **(meaning "spatial streams" as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29129\_replaced([11107886](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koj9lvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) to derive those measurements**

In case of MU receiver, **noise used for Rank computation depends on** NRCELL.actPmiSinrUlMuMimo, please refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1033\_replaced([11103657](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knyhjPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).\*\*\*\*

The measurements shall be separately performed for:

- DMRS received on PUSCH scheduled with rank2

- 2 antennas ports signals received on SRS

**For Thor 7-2e HW the Radio shall compute UL Rank measurement for PUSCH Channel as output from new Raw CEs (refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_28100\_replaced([11102253](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knfmmfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))**

Supported measurement per L1 HW, please refer to :

* 5G\_UP\_ALG\_6419\_replaced([11103744](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kn0WsvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for PUSCH
* 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1525\_replaced([11106411](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koXwXvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for SRS

---

#### 2.4.6.10.0-2 UL Rank Invalid measurement for UL rank (ID: 11109429)

**2.4.6.10.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11109432`)

UL Rank
The computation of the rank (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7419\_replaced([11109409](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko3fl_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is not done when PUSCH is received with spatial multiplexing rank 1 or single antenna. 5G-L1-UL shall inform 5G-L2-PS with INVALID value for the corresponding parameter, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_6465\_replaced([11109746](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko7xA_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Invalid measurement for UL rank per L1 HW is given in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7476\_replaced([11109434](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko4GoPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

---

#### 2.4.6.10.0-3 UL Rank Supported Invalid measurement for UL rank (ID: 11109434)

**2.4.6.10.0-3.0-5**  (ID: `13342766`)

UL Rank
Invalid measurement for UL rank [11109429](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko3fnvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) per L1 HW is given in the below table.

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **L1 HW** | **Invalid measurement UL rank ([11109429](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko3fnvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))** | **First Feature** |
| Loner | supported | - |
| Loki | supported | - |
| Thor | supported | CB007487-B CB007487-C CB007487-D |
| Thor (Radio 7-2e) | supported | CB07595-C |
| GPU | supported | CB016066-C |

---

#### 2.4.6.10.1 Number of valid SRS port

##### 2.4.6.10.1.0-1 Number of valid SRS port Overview (ID: 11109451)

**2.4.6.10.1.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11109454`)

Number of valid SRS port
5GNB 5G-L1-UL shall estimate the valid number of SRS port for each UE and report to 5G-L2-PS when short term beamforming is active, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_14363\_replaced([11107520](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kogTOvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

### 2.4.6.11 UL TPMI and ulPmiSINR

#### 2.4.6.11.0-1 UL TPMI and ulPmiSINR Overview - UL TPMI and ulPmiSINR (ID: 11109458)

**2.4.6.11.0-1.0-8**  (ID: `12504351`)

UL TPMI and ulPmiSINR
For each scheduled UE on either PUSCH or SRS, DU or Radio shall compute and report the following measurements:

* ulRank1PMI, ulPmiRank1Sinr
* ulRank2PMI, ulPmiRank2Sinr for each layer

Relation between those measurements

* ulPmiRank1Sinr is used to find the UL best TPMI to be used for rank 1 (ulRank1PMI)
* ulPmiRank2Sinr for each layer is used to find the UL best TPMI to be used for rank2  (ulRank2PMI)

More details about these metrics selection are given in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1037\_replaced([11103694](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knzInfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Use in L2-PS

ulRank1PMI and ulRank2PMI shall be used by 5G-L2-PS depending on the scheduled rank (from long term rank computation, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3223\_replaced([11139902](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krqU7_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

ulPmiRank1Sinr and ulPmiRank2Sinr shall be used by 5G-L2-PS :

* in link adaptation as described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3081\_replaced([11131259](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krCCw_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
* in IRC gain function as described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_30843\_replaced([11140125](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krsKFPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
* in Spectral efficiency check function as described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_30873\_replaced([11139935](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krq78_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

Validity of the measurements

For SU receiver, DU or Radio shall use the signals received on all active antennas (or "spatial streams" as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29129\_replaced([11107886](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koj9lvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) to derive those measurements

For MU receiver (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8756\_replaced([11103056](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knrMwPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for the supported L1 HWs),

o For C8 [CB008235-D-UPD002] C16'4, and C16'8 [End CB008235-D-UPD002] when NRCELL.actPmiSinrUlMuMimo ([11355703](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucTDWfvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) is false [Before CB008235-D-UPD002] C16'4 and C16'8 [End CB008235-D-UPD002], DU or Radio shall use the signals received on own UE antennas  (meaning "spatial streams" as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29129\_replaced([11107886](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koj9lvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) to derive those measurements

o For C8 [CB008235-D-UPD002] C16'4, or C16'8 [End CB008235-D-UPD002] and NRCELL.actPmiSinrUlMuMimo ([11355703](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucTDWfvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))  is true, DU [CB008235-D-UPD002] or Radio [End CB008235-D-UPD002]shall use the signals received on all active antennas (meaning "spatial streams" as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29129\_replaced([11107886](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koj9lvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) to derive those measurements

In case of MU receiver, **noise used for TPMI computation depends on** NRCELL.actPmiSinrUlMuMimo **([11355703](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucTDWfvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), please refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1037\_replaced([11103694](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knzInfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).**

The measurements shall be separately performed for:

- DMRS received on PUSCH scheduled with rank2

- 2 antennas ports signals received on SRS

The codebook used for the computation of the metrics is either 5G\_UP\_ALG\_992\_replaced([11103324](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knuQH_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) or 5G\_UP\_ALG\_994\_replaced([11103365](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knu3IvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Supported measurements per L1 HW

Please refer to :

* 5G\_UP\_ALG\_6419\_replaced([11103744](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kn0WsvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for PUSCH
* 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1525\_replaced([11106411](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koXwXvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for SRS

Note: For eCPRI 7-2e, Radio shall do the measurement as output from new Raw CEs (refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_28100\_replaced([11102253](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knfmmfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and report result to 5G-L2-PS via DU, otherwise DU shall do the measurement and report to 5G\_L2\_PS.

---

#### 2.4.6.11.0-2 UL TPMI and ulPmiSINR Invalid measurement for UL TPMI and ulPmiSINR (ID: 11109482)

**2.4.6.11.0-2.0-2**  (ID: `11109488`)

UL TPMI and ulPmiSINR
The computation of the TPMI and its associated SINR (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7420\_replaced([11109458](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko4GrPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), for both rank1 and rank2, is not done when PUSCH is received with spatial multiplexing rank 1 or single antenna. DU(L1) [CB007595-B]or Radio(L1) [End CB007595-B] shall inform 5G-L2-PS that the measurements are invalid, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_6465\_replaced([11109746](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko7xA_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for TPMI and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1746\_replaced([11109764](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko7xC_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for its associated SINR.

Invalid measurement for UL TPMI and ulPmiSINR per L1 HW is given in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7480\_replaced([11109490](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko4ttPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[CB007595-B]

Note:

- "5G-L1-UL" is changed to "DU(L1)" since this version as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_20317\_replaced([11094116](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmZaYfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). (Note kept only in this version)

- For eCPRI 7-2e, Radio(L1) shall do the measurement and report result to 5G-L2-PS via DU(L1), otherwise DU(L1) shall do the measurement and report to 5G\_L2\_PS.

[End CB007595-B]

---

| Loner | supported | - |
| Loki | supported | - |

### 2.4.6.12 Mapping between antenna-wise measurement and spatial streams

#### 2.4.6.12.0-1 Mapping between antenna-wise measurement and spatial streams Overview for SU receiver (ID: 11109508)

**2.4.6.12.0-1.0-7**  (ID: `13455702`)

Mapping between antenna-wise measurement and spatial streams
For L1 HWs, CPRI and eCPRI modes, the mapping between ST antenna-wise **(meaning "spatial-stream-wise" as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29129\_replaced([11107886](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koj9lvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))** measurement with index *i and the spatial stream is given in the below table.*\*\*\*\*

These measurements are:

* Antenna-wise TOE on PUSCH (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_6375\_replaced([11107978](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kolLtPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))
* Antenna-wise TOE on SRS (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7897\_replaced([11108071](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_komZ1fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) **cf. note2**
* Antenna-wise FOE on PUSCH (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_6798\_replaced([11108146](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_konA7_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))
* Antenna-wise FOE on PUCCH (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_6801\_replaced([11108207](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kooPBvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) cf. note3
* Antenna-wise UL Received power for PUSCH (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1688\_replaced([11108734](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kou8tfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))
* Antenna-wise UL Received power for SRS (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7102\_replaced([11108812](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kowK0fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) cf. note2
* Antenna-wise SINR for PUSCH (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1704\_replaced([11108993](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koyACfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))
* Antenna-wise SINR for SRS (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7182\_replaced([11109097](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kozOMPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) **cf. note2**
* Antenna-wise RSSI on PUSCH (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7048\_replaced([11108553](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kosgd_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))
* Antenna-wise noise power on PUSCH (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_17562\_replaced([11108367](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koqENfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

For the supported measurement per L1 HW, refer to the respective requirement stated above.

**Mapping between antenna-wise measurement and spatial stream for (e)CPRI**

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Subcell Type (see 5G\_UP\_1721\_replaced([11372234](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ud8CE_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) | Measurement for antenna i | CPRI/eCPRI spatial stream | First Feature |
| A2 or C2 | antenna 0 | (ce)AxC0 |  |
| antenna 1 | (ce)AxC1 |  |  |
| A4 or C4 | antenna 0 | (ce)AxC0 |  |
| antenna 1 | (ce)AxC1 |  |  |
| antenna 2 | (ce)AxC2 |  |  |
| antenna 3 | (ce)AxC3 |  |  |
| A8 or C8 | antenna 0 | ceAxC0 | CB009363-A |
| antenna 1 | ceAxC1 | CB009363-A |  |
| antenna j (j=2...6) | ceAxCj  (j=2...6) | CB009363-A |  |
| antenna 7 | ceAxC7 | CB009363-A |  |

**NOTE : although antenna-wise measurements are introduced for FR1 TDD Rx beamforming cells with eCPRI RU, L1 behavior is the same among:**

- CPRI or eCPRI FR1 TDD 2RX beamforming

- CPRI or eCPRI FR1 TDD 2RX non beamforming

- CPRI or eCPRI FR1 TDD 4Rx beamforming

- CPRI or eCPRI FR1 TDD 4Rx non beamforming

- CPRI or eCPRI **FR1 FDD 2Rx non beamforming**

- CPRI or eCPRI FR1 FDD 4Rx non beamforming

-    eCPRI FR1 FDD 8Rx beamforming

- **[CB014128-E]CPRI or** [End CB014128-E] eCPRI FR1 FDD 8Rx non beamforming\*\*\*\*

- FR2 TDD 2Rx

---

#### 2.4.6.12.0-2 Mapping between antenna-wise measurement and spatial streams Overview for SU receiver (eCPRI 7-2e) (ID: 11109526)

**2.4.6.12.0-2.0-4**  (ID: `12688147`)

Mapping between antenna-wise measurement and spatial streams
For eCPRI 7-2e, the Radio shall apply the mapping between ST antenna-wise (meaning "spatial-stream-wise" as per [11107886](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koj9lvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) measurement with index *i and the internal spatial stream (in the Radio)  as given in the below table.*

These measurements are:

* Antenna-wise TOE on PUSCH (see [11107975](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kolLs_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
* Antenna-wise TOE on SRS (see [11108071](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_komZ1fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) cf. note2
* Antenna-wise FOE on PUSCH (see [11108146](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_konA7_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
* Antenna-wise FOE on PUCCH (see [11108207](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kooPBvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) cf. note3
* Antenna-wise UL Received power for PUSCH (see [11108734](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kou8tfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
* Antenna-wise UL Received power for SRS (see [11108812](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kowK0fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) cf. note2
* Antenna-wise SINR for PUSCH (see [11108993](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koyACfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
* Antenna-wise SINR for SRS (see [11109097](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kozOMPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) cf. note2
* Antenna-wise RSSI on PUSCH (see [11108553](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kosgd_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
* Antenna-wise noise power on PUSCH (see [11108367](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koqENfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

For the supported measurement per L1 HW, refer to the respective requirement stated above.

**Mapping between antenna-wise measurement and internal spatial stream in the radio for eCPRI 7-2e**

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Subcell Type (see  [11094165](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmaBcPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) | Measurement for antenna i | internal spatial ID | First Feature |
| **[Before CB008235-X] C4’2 (Radio)**[End CB008235-X]\*\*\*\* | **[Before CB008235-X] antenna 0**[End CB008235-X]\*\*\*\* | **[Before CB008235-X]  0**[End CB008235-X]\*\*\*\* | CB007595-B |
| **[Before CB008235-X] antenna 1**[End CB008235-X]\*\*\*\* | **[Before CB008235-X]  1**[End CB008235-X]\*\*\*\* | CB007595-B |  |
| **[Before CB008235-X]antenna 2**[End CB008235-X]\*\*\*\* | **[Before CB008235-X]  2**[End CB008235-X]\*\*\*\* | CB007595-B |  |
| **[Before CB008235-X]antenna 3**[End CB008235-X]\*\*\*\* | **[Before CB008235-X]  3**[End CB008235-X]\*\*\*\* | CB007595-B |  |
| C16’2 | antenna 0 | 0 | **CB010708-A** |
|  | antenna 1 | 1 | **CB010708-A** |
|  | antenna j (j=2...**14)** | j (j=2...**14)** | **CB010708-A** |
|  | antenna**15** | 15 | **CB010708-A** |

**[Before CB008235-X]**NOTE3:\*\*\*\*

- For eCPRI 7-2e, C4’2 (Radio) is supported in Radio, antenna-wise measurements of PUSCH and SRS **are implemented at Radio(L1). Internal spatial stream ID's used in Radio(L1).****[End CB008235-X]**

For eCPRI 7-2e, C16'2 shall support 8**or 16 streams  thanks to Pusch ([11102901](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knpXlPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and SRS ([11106163](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koUtB_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) reception indication.  
For each UE, the Radio shall concatenate the measurements on one stream (cf. SRAN\_SiSo\_FH.1023\_replaced([10948986](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_3W2POfuyEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), SRAN\_SiSo\_FH.1371\_replaced([10948990](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_3W2PPfuyEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). UE beam(s) order is as per UE grant** .\*\*\*\*

For eCPRI mode, the mapping for MU receiver between ST antena-wise (meaning "spatial-stream-wise" as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29129\_replaced([11107886](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koj9lvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) measurement with index *i and the spatial stream is given in the below table.*

These measurements are:

* Antenna-wise TOE on PUSCH (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1635\_replaced([11107975](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kolLs_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))
* Antenna-wise FOE on PUSCH (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_6798\_replaced([11108146](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_konA7_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))
* Antenna-wise UL Received power for PUSCH (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1688\_replaced([11108734](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kou8tfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

**Mapping between antenna-wise measurement and spatial stream for (e)CPRI**

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Subcell Type (see 5G\_UP\_1721\_replaced([11372234](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ud8CE_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) | Measure for antenna i and User j  (j=0,1 per UE pair) | eCPRI spatial stream |
| C4 | antenna 0 for UE j | ceAxC0 carries [Before CNI-137616-B] best beam for UE0 in UE pair or **[End CNI-137616-B] second best beam of UE1 in UE pair** |
| antenna 1 for UE j | ceAxC1 carries [Before CNI-137616-B] best beam for UE0 in UE pair or **[End CNI-137616-B] second best beam of UE1 in UE pair** |  |
| antenna 2 for UE j | ceAxC2 carries best beam for UE1 in UE pair |  |
| antenna 3 for UE j | ceAxC3 carries best beam for UE1 in UE pair |  |

The table is illustrated as follows :

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating a wireless communication system. On the left, there are two input blocks labeled "UE0' best beam" and "UE1' best beam". Each of these blocks has two arrows pointing to a central block labeled "4RX IRC MU receiver". The arrows from "UE0' best beam" are blue, and the arrows from "UE1' best beam" are green. The central receiver block has four purple arrows pointing outwards, enclosed in a purple oval. To the right of the oval, there is a text box with the following content: "L 1 Measurements: number of outputs for each UE is equal to the total number of antennas (=4) of the Subcell Type > number of streams per UE (=2) from FCP message".[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_4106.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

Please refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_11081\_replaced([11104129](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kn5PMPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for "MU vs SU receiver : number of outputs vs number of inputs" summary.

Please refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8996\_replaced([11109635](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko573PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for L1 HW support.

[Before CNI-137616-B] (\*) Let's assume a case where UE1 has the largest PRB allocation. For PRB regions where only UE1 is scheduled, either best beam of UE0 or second best beam of UE1 might be considered (cf. 5G\_UP\_ALG\_11355\_replaced([11104143](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kn5PNfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). 5G-L2-PS shall decide this based on rdUlMuReceiverBeamUsedOverlappingPRB, as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_11359\_replaced([11149352](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksNHevvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). [End CNI-137616-B]

For eCPRI mode, the mapping for MU receiver between ST antenna-wise  [CNI-103796-K] (meaning "spatial-stream-wise" as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29129\_replaced([11107886](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koj9lvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) [End CNI-103796-K] measurement with index i and the spatial stream is given in the below table.

These measurements are:

* Antenna-wise TOE on PUSCH (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1635\_replaced([11107975](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kolLs_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))
* Antenna-wise FOE on PUSCH (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_6798\_replaced([11108146](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_konA7_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))
* Antenna-wise UL Received power for PUSCH (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1688\_replaced([11108734](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kou8tfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))
* Antenna-wise SINR for PUSCH (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1704\_replaced([11108993](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koyACfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))
* Antenna-wise RSSI on PUSCH (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7048\_replaced([11108553](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kosgd_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))
* Antenna-wise noise power on PUSCH (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_17562\_replaced([11108367](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koqENfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

Mapping between antenna-wise measurement and spatial stream for (e)CPRI (example for 4UEs rank1)

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Subcell Type (see 5G\_UP\_1721\_replaced([11372234](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ud8CE_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) | Measure for antenna i and User j  (j=0,1,2,3 per UE pair) | eCPRI spatial stream | First feature |
| C8 | antenna 0 for UEj | ceAxC0 carries best beam for UE0 in UE pair | CB009363-F |
| antenna 1 for UEj | ceAxC1 carries best beam for UE0 in UE pair |  |  |
| antenna i for UEj | ceAxC 2i carries best beam for UE i in UE pair |  |  |
| antenna i+1 for UEj | ceAxC (2i+1) carries best beam for UE i in UE pair |  |  |
| antenna 6 for UEj | ceAxC6 carries best beam for UE3 in UE pair |  |  |
| antenna 7 for UEj | ceAxC7 carries best beam for UE3 in UE pair |  |  |

The table is illustrated as follows :

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating a multi-user receiver system. On the left, there are four boxes labeled "UE0' best beam", "UE1' best beam", "UE2' best beam", and "UE3' best beam". Each of these boxes is connected by a set of four horizontal arrows pointing to the right, indicating data streams or signals. These arrows converge and enter a larger blue rectangular box in the center, labeled "8RX IRC MU receiver". To the right of the receiver box, there is a cluster of eight purple arrows pointing to the right, enclosed within an oval shape. Finally, to the right of the oval, there is a yellow text box containing the following text: "L 1 Measurements: number of outputs for each UE is equal to the total number of antennas (=8) of the subcell type > number of streams per UE (=2 or 4) from FCP message." The diagram visually represents the input of best beam signals from multiple UEs into an 8-antenna receiver, followed by an output that is related to L1 measurements based on the number of antennas and streams per UE.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_4110.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

For Antenna-wise TOE and  Antenna-wise FOE on PUSCH, the number of valid measurements is equal to the number of streams (2 or 4 or  6) and corresponds to the ceAxC of the UE beam(s). In the 4 rank1 example, UE1 has best beam on ceAxC (2, 3) and valid measurements on ceAxC (2, 3); remaining measurements are invalid.

For remaining measurements,  the number of valid measurements is equal to 8.

In order to find streams from own UE beam(s), please refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_19619\_replaced([11104193](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kn52R_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Please refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8996\_replaced([11109635](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko573PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for L1 HW support.

Please refer to [Link to figures](https://nokia.sharepoint.com/:w:/r/sites/ASCOP/AS%20Documents/FS1TAs-CFAMs/CB/CB009000%20-%20CB009499/CFAM/CB009363%208Rx%20IRC%20receiver%20ABIP%20FR1%20TDD/CP2/cb009363_figures.docx?d=wfe6d7bb83a8548d49a63752c2b515742&csf=1&web=1&e=Mlb2YA) if needed.

Note : NoisePower (5G\_UP\_ALG\_1652\_replaced([11108250](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koo2FvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is still computed using all antennas, but unused DMRS REs component is forced to zero on UE paired antennas.

For eCPRI mode, the mapping for MU receiver between ST antena-wise measurement with index i and the spatial stream is given in the below table.

These measurements are in Radio:

* Antenna-wise TOE on PUSCH (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1635\_replaced([11107975](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kolLs_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))
* Antenna-wise FOE on PUSCH (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_6798\_replaced([11108146](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_konA7_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))
* Antenna-wise UL Received power for PUSCH (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1688\_replaced([11108734](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kou8tfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))
* Antenna-wise SINR for PUSCH (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1704\_replaced([11108993](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koyACfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))
* Antenna-wise RSSI on PUSCH (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7048\_replaced([11108553](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kosgd_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))
* Antenna-wise noise power on PUSCH (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_17562\_replaced([11108367](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koqENfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

Mapping between antenna-wise measurement and spatial stream for (e)CPRI for 2UEs rank2

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Subcell Type (see 5G\_UP\_1721\_replaced([11372234](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ud8CE_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) 5G\_UP\_ALG\_6\_replaced([11094165](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmaBcPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) | Measure for antenna i and User j  (j=0,1 per UE pair) | Order of the measurements for UEj across all antennas as follows | First feature |
| C16'4 | Measure on antenna 0 for UEj | antenna 0 carries best beams for UEj in UE pair | CB010708-C |
| Measure on antenna 1 for UEj | antenna 1 carries best beams for UEj in UE pair |  |  |
| … | … |  |  |
| Measure on antenna 6 for UEj | antenna 6 carries best beams for UEj |  |  |
| Measure on antenna 7 for UEj | antenna 7 carries best beams for UEj |  |  |
| Measure on antenna 8 for UEj | antenna 8 carries best beams for paired UE |  |  |
| Measure on antenna 9 for UEj | antenna 9 carries best beams for paired UE |  |  |
| … | … |  |  |
| Measure on antenna 14 for UEj | antenna 14 carries best beams for paired UE |  |  |
| Measure on antenna 15 for UEj | antenna 15 carries best beams for paired UE |  |  |

The table is illustrated as follows[image-0][image\_desc]The image depicts a block diagram illustrating a wireless communication system with two User Equipments (UEs), UE0 and UE1, and a 16RX IRC MU receiver.
On the left side of the diagram, there are two sets of rectangular boxes, each representing beams from a UE. The top set, associated with UE0, lists "UE0' best beam", "UE0' 2nd beam", "UE0' 3rd beam", and "UE0' 4th beam". These are enclosed by a red oval. The bottom set, associated with UE1, lists "UE1' best beam", "UE1' 2nd beam", "UE1' 3rd beam", and "UE1' 4th beam". These are enclosed by a yellow oval.
To the right of these UE beam boxes, there are sets of green and blue arrows originating from curly braces, indicating signals. The green arrows correspond to UE0's beams, and the blue arrows correspond to UE1's beams.
In the center, a large blue rectangle is labeled "16RX IRC MU receiver". Arrows from both UE0 and UE1 are directed towards this receiver.
Above the receiver and the arrows, a yellow text box states: "Each UE shall have 8 first measurements from his own 8 antennas." Below this, "UE0" and "UE1" are labeled, with red and yellow ovals respectively highlighting groups of arrows pointing towards the receiver. The red oval encloses 8 green arrows for UE0, and the yellow oval encloses 8 blue arrows for UE1.
Below the receiver, another yellow text box states: "Each UE shall have 8 last measurements from paired UE antennas." Below this, there are two more ovals, a red one enclosing 8 blue arrows (presumably from UE1 to UE0) and a yellow one enclosing 8 green arrows (presumably from UE0 to UE1).
The diagram illustrates a system where UEs transmit signals, and a receiver processes these signals, potentially for beamforming or interference cancellation. The ovals highlight specific groups of signals or measurements being considered.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_4112.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]:

Each UE shall have the measurements on own antennas first followed by measurements on paired UE antennas.

For Antenna-wise TOE and  Antenna-wise FOE on PUSCH, the number of valid measurements is equal to the number of streams (8) and corresponds to the number of beams of the UE grant. It means for TOE and FOE (0-7) , valid measurements on antennas (8-15); remaining measurements are invalid.

For remaining measurements,  the number of valid measurements is equal to 16.

For each UE, the Radio shall concatenate the measurements on one stream (cf. SRAN\_SiSo\_FH.1023\_replaced([10948986](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_3W2POfuyEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), SRAN\_SiSo\_FH.1371\_replaced([10948990](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_3W2PPfuyEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). The order of measurements within UE beams is as per PUSCH grant (5G\_UP\_ALG\_26123\_replaced([11104269](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kn7EYvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_UP\_12233\_replaced([11380715](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ueolp_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

Please refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8996\_replaced([11109635](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko573PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for L1 HW support.

[CB010708-C-UPD001]

Please refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26413\_replaced([11109603](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko571fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[End CB010708-C-UPD001]

---

For eCPRI 7-2e mode and for each paired UE j, the Radio shall consider the mapping for MU receiver between ST antenna-wise measurement with index i  (i=0..15) and the internal spatial stream idx (x=0…15) in the Radio as given in the below table.
 
NOTE: Internal spatial stream refers to the input streams for L1 in the Radio
 
These measurements are in Radio:
\* Antenna-wise TOE on PUSCH (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1635\_replaced([11107975](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kolLs_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))
\* Antenna-wise FOE on PUSCH (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_6798\_replaced([11108146](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_konA7_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))
\* Antenna-wise UL Received power for PUSCH (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1688\_replaced([11108734](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kou8tfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))
\* Antenna-wise SINR for PUSCH (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1704\_replaced([11108993](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koyACfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))
\* Antenna-wise RSSI on PUSCH (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7048\_replaced([11108553](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kosgd_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))
\* Antenna-wise noise power on PUSCH (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_17562\_replaced([11108367](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koqENfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))
 
Table: Example of Mapping between antenna-wise measurement and internal spatial stream (in the Radio) for eCPRI 7-2e for 2UEs rank2 [CB010708-G-UPD001] or 2 UEs rank1 [End CB010708-G-UPD001]

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Subcell Type (see 5G\_UP\_1721\_replaced([11372234](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ud8CE_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) 5G\_UP\_ALG\_6\_replaced([11094165](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmaBcPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) | Measure for antenna i (i=0..15) and User j (j=0,1 per UE pair) | Internal spatial stream idx, x=0…15 | First feature |
| C16'4 [CB010708-G-UPD001] or C16'2 [End CB010708-G-UPD001] | Measure on antenna 0 for UEj | Stream id0 carries best beams for UE0 | CB010708-C |
| Measure on antenna 1 for UEj | Stream id1 carries best beams for UE0 |  |  |
| … | … |  |  |
| Measure on antenna 6 for UEj | Stream id6 carries best beams for UE0 |  |  |
| Measure on antenna 7 for UEj | Stream id7 carries best beams for UE0 |  |  |
| Measure on antenna 8 for UEj | Stream id8 carries best beams for UE1 |  |  |
| Measure on antenna 9 for UEj | Stream id9 carries best beams for UE1 |  |  |
| … | … |  |  |
| Measure on antenna 14 for UEj | Stream id14 carries best beams for UE1 |  |  |
| Measure on antenna 15 for UEj | Stream id15 carries best beams for UE1 |  |  |
|  |  |  |  |
| The table is illustrated as follows: |  |  |  |
| [image-0][image\_desc]The image depicts a block diagram illustrating a multi-user MIMO (Multiple-Input Multiple-Output) receiver system. The system involves two User Equipments (UEs), labeled UE0 and UE1, each with multiple beams. |  |  |  |
| On the left side of the diagram, there are two sections, one for UE0 and one for UE1. Each section lists four beams: "best beam", "2nd beam", "3rd beam", and "4th beam". The beams for UE0 are highlighted with a red circle, and the beams for UE1 are highlighted with a yellow circle. To the right of each beam listing, there are corresponding arrows representing spatial streams. UE0 has four green arrows, and UE1 has four blue arrows. |  |  |  |
| In the center of the diagram is a large blue rectangle labeled "16RX IRC MU receiver". This indicates a receiver with 16 receive antennas, employing Interference Rejection Combining (IRC) for Multi-User (MU) operation. |  |  |  |
| On the right side of the diagram, there are two sections showing antenna-wise measurements. The top section, labeled "Measurements on UEO antennas (S0=0, N0=4)", displays four green arrows within a red oval, representing measurements on UE0's antennas. The bottom section, labeled "Measurements on UE1 antennas (S1=8, N1=4)", displays four blue arrows within a yellow oval, representing measurements on UE1's antennas. Below these sections, there are labels "Antenna-wise measurements i (i=0...15) for UEO" and "Antenna-wise measurements i (i=0...15) for UE1", indicating that measurements are taken across 16 antennas for each UE. |  |  |  |
| The overall diagram illustrates how spatial streams from different UEs are processed by a multi-antenna receiver, with a focus on beam selection and antenna-wise measurements.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_4116.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0] |  |  |  |
|  |  |  |  |
| For antenna-wise TOE and  antenna-wise FOE on PUSCH measurements, the Radio shall consider the following for each paired UE j: |  |  |  |
| - antenna-wise measurement i=Sj…Sj+2xNj are set according to the valid estimates |  |  |  |
| - Remaining antenna-wise measurement are set to invalid |  |  |  |
| Where: |  |  |  |
| - Nj corresponds to the number of beams of the UE grant (e.g. for 2 rank2 paired UEs with N0=N1=4, 8 valid measurements for each paired UE). |  |  |  |
| - Sj is the index of the 1st internal spatial stream carrying the best beam for UE j (in the above example, S0=0 for UE0 and S1=8 for UE1) |  |  |  |
|  |  |  |  |
| For remaining antenna-wise measurements,  the number of valid measurements is equal to 16. |  |  |  |
|  |  |  |  |
| For each UE, the Radio shall concatenate the measurements on one stream (cf. SRAN\_SiSo\_FH.1023\_replaced([10948986](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_3W2POfuyEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), SRAN\_SiSo\_FH.1371\_replaced([10948990](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_3W2PPfuyEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). The order of measurements within UE beams is as per PUSCH grant (5G\_UP\_ALG\_26123\_replaced([11104269](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kn7EYvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_UP\_12233\_replaced([11380715](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ueolp_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). |  |  |  |
|  |  |  |  |
| [CB010708-G-UPD001] For C16'4, [End CB010708-G-UPD001] The requirement is valid for up to 4 paired UEs  per PRB (cf. 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26109\_replaced([11104290](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kn7EavvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26112\_replaced([11104308](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kn7rcPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). |  |  |  |
| [CB010708-G-UPD001] For C16'2, [End CB010708-G-UPD001] The requirement is valid for up to 2 paired UEs rank1 per PRB (cf. 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26109\_replaced([11104290](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kn7EavvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26112\_replaced([11104308](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kn7rcPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). |  |  |  |
| For Thor 7-2e HW the Antenna-wise measurements are output from new Raw CEs (refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_28100\_replaced([11102253](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knfmmfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) |  |  |  |
|  |  |  |  |
| Note : NoisePower (5G\_UP\_ALG\_1652\_replaced([11108250](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koo2FvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is still computed using all antennas, but unused DMRS REs component is forced to zero on UE paired antennas. |  |  |  |
|  |  |  |  |
| Please refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8996\_replaced([11109635](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko573PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for L1 HW support. |  |  |  |
| Source of figure can be found here: |  |  |  |

11109632:
Mapping between antenna-wise measurement and spatial streams
For eCPRI 7-2e mode and for each paired UE j, the Radio shall consider the mapping for MU receiver between ST antenna-wise (meaning "spatial-stream-wise" as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29129\_replaced([11107886](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koj9lvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) measurement with index i  (i=0..15) and the internal spatial stream idx (x=0…15) in the Radio as given in the below table.
 
NOTE: Internal spatial stream refers to the input streams for L1 in the Radio
 
These measurements are in Radio:
\* Antenna-wise TOE on PUSCH (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1635\_replaced([11107975](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kolLs_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))
\* Antenna-wise FOE on PUSCH (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_6798\_replaced([11108146](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_konA7_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))
\* Antenna-wise UL Received power for PUSCH (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1688\_replaced([11108734](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kou8tfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))
\* Antenna-wise SINR for PUSCH (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1704\_replaced([11108993](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koyACfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))
\* Antenna-wise RSSI on PUSCH (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7048\_replaced([11108553](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kosgd_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))
\* Antenna-wise noise power on PUSCH (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_17562\_replaced([11108367](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koqENfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))
 
Table: Example of Mapping between antenna-wise measurement and internal spatial stream (in the Radio) for eCPRI 7-2e for 4UEs rank2

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Subcell Type (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_6\_replaced([11094165](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmaBcPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) | Measure for antenna i (i=0..15) and User j (j=0..3 per UE pair) | Internal spatial stream idx, x=0…15 | First feature |
| C16'8 | Measure on antenna 0 for UEj | Stream id0 carries best beams for UE0 | CB008235-A |
| .. | … |  |  |
| Measure on antenna 3 for UEj | Stream id3 carries best beams for UE0 |  |  |
| Measure on antenna 4 for UEj | Stream id4 carries best beams for UE1 |  |  |
| .. | .. |  |  |
| Measure on antenna 7 for UEj | Stream id7 carries best beams for UE1 |  |  |
| Measure on antenna 8 for UEj | Stream id8 carries best beams for UE2 |  |  |
| … | … |  |  |
| Measure on antenna 11 for UEj | Stream id11 carries best beams for UE2 |  |  |
| Measure on antenna 12 for UEj | Stream id12 carries best beams for UE3 |  |  |
| … | … |  |  |
| Measure on antenna 15 for UEj | Stream id15 carries best beams for UE3 |  |  |
|  |  |  |  |
|  |  |  |  |
| The table is illustrated as follows:[image-0][image\_desc]The image depicts a block diagram illustrating a wireless communication system, likely a multi-user MIMO (Multiple-Input Multiple-Output) system. The diagram is divided into three main sections: "Internal spatial stream Idx", a central block labeled "16RX IRC MU receiver", and two sections on the right showing "Measurements on UEO antennas" and "Measurements on UE3 antennas". |  |  |  |
| The left section, "Internal spatial stream Idx", shows four pairs of User Equipment (UE) entries: UE0, UE1, UE2, and UE3. Each UE has two entries: "best beam" and "2nd beam". These entries are visually grouped by colored ellipses: red for UE0, yellow for UE1, blue for UE2, and green for UE3. Each UE entry is associated with a set of colored arrows pointing to the right, indicating spatial streams. UE0 has red arrows, UE1 has yellow arrows, UE2 has blue arrows, and UE3 has green arrows. The number of arrows per UE pair is two, suggesting two spatial streams per UE. |  |  |  |
| The central block represents a "16RX IRC MU receiver". This indicates a receiver with 16 receive antennas, employing Interference Rejection Combining (IRC) for multi-user (MU) operation. |  |  |  |
| The right side of the diagram illustrates measurements taken on antennas for specific UEs. |  |  |  |
| The top right section, "Measurements on UEO antennas (S0=0, N0=2)", shows colored circles (red, yellow, blue, green) aligned vertically. Each circle contains multiple green arrows pointing to the right. The red circle corresponds to UE0, and it has four green arrows. The yellow circle for UE1 also has four green arrows, the blue circle for UE2 has four green arrows, and the green circle for UE3 has four green arrows. Below these circles, there are labels "Antenna-wise measurements i (i=0...15) for UEO". This suggests that for UE0, measurements are taken across 16 antennas (indexed from 0 to 15). The notation (S0=0, N0=2) likely refers to specific parameters related to spatial streams and noise for UE0. |  |  |  |
| The bottom right section, "Measurements on UE3 antennas (S3=12, N3=2)", mirrors the structure of the UE0 measurement section but is labeled for UE3. It also shows colored circles with green arrows, and the green circle corresponding to UE3 has four green arrows. The label below reads "Antenna-wise measurements i (i=0...15) for UE3". The notation (S3=12, N3=2) likely refers to parameters for UE3. |  |  |  |
| The diagram visually represents how spatial streams from different UEs are processed by a multi-antenna receiver and how antenna-wise measurements are collected for these UEs. The color coding helps to distinguish between different UEs and their associated spatial streams and measurements.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_4119.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0] |  |  |  |
|  |  |  |  |
|  |  |  |  |
|  |  |  |  |
| For antenna-wise TOE and  antenna-wise FOE on PUSCH measurements, the Radio shall consider the following for each paired UE j: |  |  |  |
| - antenna-wise measurement i=Sj…Sj+2xNj are set according to the valid estimates |  |  |  |
| - Remaining antenna-wise measurement are set to invalid |  |  |  |
| Where: |  |  |  |
| - Nj corresponds to the number of beams of the UE grant (e.g. for 4 rank2 paired UEs with N0=N1=N2=N3=2, 4 valid measurements for each paired UE). |  |  |  |
| - Sj is the index of the 1st internal spatial stream carrying the best beam for UE j (in the above example, S0=0 for UE0 and S3=12 for UE3) |  |  |  |
|  |  |  |  |
| For remaining antenna-wise measurements,  the number of valid measurements is equal to 16. |  |  |  |
|  |  |  |  |
| For each UE, the Radio shall concatenate the measurements on one stream (cf. SRAN\_SiSo\_FH.1023\_replaced([10948986](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_3W2POfuyEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), SRAN\_SiSo\_FH.1371\_replaced([10948990](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_3W2PPfuyEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). The order of measurements within UE beams is as per PUSCH grant (5G\_UP\_ALG\_26123\_replaced([11104269](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kn7EYvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_UP\_12233\_replaced([11380715](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ueolp_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). |  |  |  |
|  |  |  |  |
| For Thor 7-2e HW the Antenna-wise measurements are output from new Raw CEs (refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_28100\_replaced([11102253](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knfmmfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) |  |  |  |
| The requirement is valid for up to 8 paired UEs  per PRB (cf. 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26109\_replaced([11104290](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kn7EavvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26112\_replaced([11104308](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kn7rcPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) [CB008235-C] without or with UE Groups Pairing algorithm (cf. 5G\_UP\_13281\_replaced([11355586](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucScQvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))  [End CB008235-C] |  |  |  |
|  |  |  |  |
| Note : NoisePower (5G\_UP\_ALG\_1652\_replaced([11108250](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koo2FvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is still computed using all antennas, but unused DMRS REs component is forced to zero on UE paired antennas. |  |  |  |
|  |  |  |  |
| Please refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8996\_replaced([11109635](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko573PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for L1 HW support. |  |  |  |
| Source of figure can be found here: |  |  |  |

Mapping between antenna-wise measurement and spatial streams

#### 2.4.6.12.0-10 Mapping between antenna-wise measurement and spatial streams Mapping between antenna-wise measuremnts and spatial streams of serving and helper cell for UL CoMP (ID: 12775997)

**2.4.6.12.0-10.0-1**  (ID: `12775998`)

Mapping between antenna-wise measurement and spatial streams
For CPRI and eCPRI mode, in non-beamforming deployment, when UL CoMP is activated and UE is ocnfigured for joint reception (see [12763061](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_gSJKEIOREfCOGMMH109ICw?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)), DU shall support the mapping between ST antenna-wise (meaning "spatial-stream-wise" as per [11107886](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koj9lvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))**measurement with index *i and the spatial stream of serving and helper cell as given in the below table.***

These measurements are:

* Antenna-wise TOE on PUSCH (see [11107978](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kolLtPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
* Antenna-wise FOE on PUSCH (see [11108146](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_konA7_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
* Antenna-wise UL Received power for PUSCH (see [11108734](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kou8tfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
* Antenna-wise pre-equalized SINR for PUSCH (see [11108993](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koyACfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
* Antenna-wise RSSI on PUSCH (see [11108553](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kosgd_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
* Antenna-wise noise power on PUSCH (see [11108367](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koqENfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

**Mapping between antenna-wise measurement and spatial stream for CPRI**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Subcell Type (see [11372234](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ud8CE_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) | Measurement for antenna i | Cell | (e)CPRI (e)AxC spatial stream | First Feature |
| A4 | Antenna 0 | Serving | 0 | CB008465-AA |
| Antenna 1 | 1 |  |  |  |
| Antenna 2 | 2 |  |  |  |
| Antenna 3 | 3 |  |  |  |
| Antenna 4 | Helper | 0 |  |  |
| Antenna 5 | 1 |  |  |  |
| Antenna 6 | 2 |  |  |  |
| Antenna 7 | 3 |  |  |  |

DU shall support the mapping between antenna-wise measurements and spatial streams of serving and helper cells for UL CoMP joint reception for the following L1 HW.

| Loki | - | - |

### 2.4.6.13 ScalingFactor compensation

#### 2.4.6.13.0-1 ScalingFactor compensation Overview (ID: 11109661)

**2.4.6.13.0-1.0-5**  (ID: `13135956`)

ScalingFactor compensation
For CPRI and eCPRI, the scaling factor **applied at the radio module to reach a target noise bitwidth shall be compensated at DU(L1) for all the UL power measurements given in the following.**

For eCPRI 7-2e, the compensation on the measuresurements of PUSCH and SRS shall be done in Radio(L1).

Details about this scaling factor are in [11376973](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ueT1h_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) and [11379940](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_uejtIPvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) for CPRI and eCPRI respectively. For eCPRI 7-2e, details about scaling factor are in [11380527](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_uenXgfvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

This compensation is valid for the below measurements:

* RSSI: [11108512](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kor5aPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ), [11108553](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kosgd_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ), [11108606](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kotHifvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)
* RX power: [11108698](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kouVqPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ), [11108736](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kou8tvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ), [11108785](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kovjx_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ), [11108812](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kowK0fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)
* Noise power: [11108254](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koo2F_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ), [11108309](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kopdJ_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ), [11108367](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koqENfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ), [11108413](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koqrRfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ), [11108442](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_korSUPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ), [11108464](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_korSWPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)
* PeakMetric: [11108883](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kowx6fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)

Note: For CPRI rxScalingFactor value is set as described in [11288559](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Tp7ttPvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ), [11288565](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Tp7ttfvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

For eCPRI 7-2e, when Split Panel  ([11354596](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucJSWfvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) Beam Forming Mode configured ([11155345](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksvS8vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) and [11155374](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksvS9PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) in UL,  the Radio shall apply the compensation factor defined in section §2.8 of 5Gmax algorithm specifications (see [11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and in section §5.3.1.1 of Massive MIMO Algorithm Specification (see [11101361](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcsvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[CB008577-C]

For FDD BF, DU shall apply the split panel compensation factor defined in section §2.8 of 5Gmax algorithm specifications (see [11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and in section §5.3.1.1 of Massive MIMO Algorithm Specification (see [11101361](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcsvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), when split panel beamforming configured (see [11354596](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucJSWfvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[End CB008577-C]

---

### 2.4.6.14 RIM-RS measurements

#### 2.4.6.14.0-1 RIM-RS measurements (ID: 11109678)

**2.4.6.14.0-1.0-4**  (ID: `12643712`)

RIM-RS measurements
When NRBTS/NRCELL/actNrRim is set to 3  or 4 , for each detected RIM-RS sequence (see [11110094](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpACevvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), DU with eCPRI 7-2a [CB010315-SR-B] and CPRI [Before CB010315-SR-C](only with actNrRim equals to 3) [End CB010315-SR-C][End CB010315-SR-B] and the Radio (for eCPRI 7-2e, when NRCELL/actEcpri72e is set to "TRUE"

) shall perform RIM-RS signal power measurements according to 5GMAX algorithm specification in section  2.10.2, Then shall report the estimated measurements to high layer.

Only one measurement is reported for each detected RIM-RS sequence across all the RX antennas and all the allocated PRBs for this sequence.

Additionally:

the DU with eCPRI 7-2a [CB010315-SR-B] and CPRI [Before CB010315-SR-C](only with actNrRim equals to 3) [End CB010315-SR-C][End CB010315-SR-B]  and the Radio (for eCPRI 7-2e, when NRCELL/actEcpri72e is set to "TRUE") shall report the peak value of each detected RIM-RS sequence to high layer.

. The peak Value is determined according to 5GMAX algorithm specification in section 2.10.2.

Details about the usage of the reported RIM-RS measurements in gNB-DU are in [11163032](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktYMKvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) and [11163050](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktYMLfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

Details about RIM-RS detection algorithm are given in [11110094](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpACevvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

The unit of RIM-RS signal power measurements reported to gNB-DU is equal to dBm. The peak Value is a power ratio (see 5GMAX algorithm specification in section  2.10.2) and it’s reported without any unit.

Note:RIM-RS in RIM Framework-1(i.e. NRBTS/NRCELL/actNrRim = 4) shall include both RS type 1 and type 2.

---

#### 2.4.6.14.0-2 RIM-RS measurements RIM-RS detection error case (ID: 11109689)

**2.4.6.14.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11109693`)

RIM-RS measurements
The 5G-L1-UL shall report INVALID value for the below case which assumes no RIM-RS detection in the current slot, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7421\_replaced([11109729](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko7J_fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_13521\_replaced([11109810](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko8YH_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)):

* When only zero-valued samples are got from the radio module or (e)CPRI interface for all the receiving antennas (e.g. broken antenna scenario)

RIM-RS measurements

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |

Spec. section\* | First Feature |

### 2.4.6.15 Invalid measurements

#### 2.4.6.15.0-1 Invalid measurements Introduction - Invalid Measurements Cases (ID: 11109713)

**2.4.6.15.0-1.0-5**  (ID: `11109726`)

Invalid measurements
In this chapter, the values of measurements which are foreseen as Invalid are provided. Some of these measurements are reported via counters and/or used in RRM functionalities.

A measurement reported by DU(L1) or Radio(L1) is assumed as INVALID when:

* Case1: zero-valued samples got from the radio module or (e)CPRI interface for all the receiving antennas (meaning "spatial-stream-wise" as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29129\_replaced([11107886](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koj9lvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), typically the case of broken antenna, or noisy measurements
* Case2: the measurement can't be computed for the current configuration (example UL rank or TPMI on PUSCH for single layer configuration, [CB007491-F] example FOE on PUCCH for DMRS configuration [End CB007491-F]) or for PRACH Channel when NRBTS/NRCell/actPrachNoPreambleNoisePowerEst =  false
* Case3: one or more Rx (meaning "spatial-stream-wise" as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29129\_replaced([11107886](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koj9lvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) are failed (typically the case of RU lost or fiber broken or Rx failure, refer to 5G\_UP\_6136\_replaced([11377147](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ueUcnvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_6137\_replaced([11380144](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_uek7RPvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

Note: Additional cases can be identified during the implementation for Invalid measurements (eg. estimate does not match with l1-l2 interface range, etc). In this case, their values shall be set to Invalid as described in the following.

Note2:

- "5G-L1-UL" is changed to "DU(L1)" since this version as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_20317\_replaced([11094116](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmZaYfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). (Note kept only in this version)

- Case3 is not supported in eCPRI 7-2e.

---

#### 2.4.6.15.0-2 Invalid measurements Invalid power-related measurements for Case1 (5G_UP_ALG_7403_replaced( 11109713 )) (ID: 11109729)

**2.4.6.15.0-2.0-6**  (ID: `11109744`)

Invalid measurements
In the case of Invalid measurement for RSSI, RTWP, UL RX power, RIM-RS signal power **[CNI-126050-A] PRACH Noise Power and Noise Power per PRB** [End CNI-126050-A], DU **or Radio** shall report to L2(DU) the value -140dBm.***\****\*

For "Antenna-wise" (meaning "spatial-stream-wise") L1 internal naming convention refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29129\_replaced([11107886](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koj9lvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

**[Before CNI-126050-A]**

In the case of Invalid measurements for PRACH Noise Power and Noise Power per PRB DU(L1) **shall report to 5G-L2-PS the value 10^(-17) W (Watt).**

**[End CNI-126050-A]**

For these measurements, refer to:

* RSSI on PUSCH: 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7039\_replaced([11108512](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kor5aPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
* RSSI on PUCCH: 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7052\_replaced([11108606](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kotHifvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
* RTWP: 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7061\_replaced([11108654](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kotumvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) (Not supported in eCPRI 7-2e)
* UL received power on PUSCH: 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7068\_replaced([11108698](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kouVqPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
* Antenna-wise UL Received power for PUSCH: 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7090\_replaced([11108736](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kou8tvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
* Antenna-wise RSSI on PUSCH: 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7048\_replaced([11108553](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kosgd_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
* UL Received Power for PUCCH: 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7094\_replaced([11108785](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kovjx_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
* Antenna-wise UL Received Power for SRS: 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7102\_replaced([11108812](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kowK0fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
* Noise power on PUSCH per PRB: 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8119\_replaced([11108309](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kopdJ_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))**[Before CB009514-F](Not supported in eCPRI 7-2e)**[End CB009514-F]\*\*\*\*
* Antenna-wise Noise power on PUSCH: 5G\_UP\_ALG\_17561\_replaced([11108364](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koqENPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
* Noise power on PUCCH per PRB: 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8130\_replaced([11108442](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_korSUPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
* RSSI for RIM: 5G\_UP\_ALG\_9425\_replaced([11108628](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kotukfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
* RIM-RS signal power: 5G\_UP\_ALG\_13135\_replaced([11109689](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko6i-_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) **[Before CB012564-B] (Not supported in eCPRI 7-2e)**[End CB012564-B]\*\*\*\*
* SRS power per antenna for each polarization:5G\_UP\_ALG\_23226\_replaced([11107200](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kodP4fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

**Note:**

- Whether “DU” or “Radio” is used depends on where the measurement is implemented. Refer to the related requirements for details.

* Post SU SINR on PUSCH : 5G\_UP\_ALG\_21980\_replaced([11108940](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koxY_vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
* SINR associated to TPMI on PUSCH: 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7420\_replaced([11109458](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko4GrPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
* SINR on PUCCH: 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7173\_replaced([11109040](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koynGvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
* Antenna-wise SINR for SRS: 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7182\_replaced([11109097](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kozOMPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

Note:

* In case of eCRRI 7-2e, Radio shall do the measurements of antenna-wise SINR on PUSCH and SRS, otherwise DU shall do the measurements, [CB010708-F-UPD001] except Post SU SINR on PUSCH (in: 5G\_UP\_ALG\_21980\_replaced([11108940](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koxY_vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) which is estimated in the Radio [End CB010708-F-UPD001]
* Case3 is not supported in eCPRI 7-2e.

#### 2.4.6.15.0-7 Invalid measurements Invalid measurements of  - TOE (ID: 11109778)

**2.4.6.15.0-7.0-3**  (ID: `11109785`)

Invalid measurements
For Case2 (5G\_UP\_ALG\_7403\_replaced([11109713](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko7J9_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) of invalid measurements of TOE,  and Case3 (5G\_UP\_ALG\_7403\_replaced([11109713](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko7J9_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) antenna-wise [CNI-103796-K] (meaning "spatial-stream-wise" as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29129\_replaced([11107886](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koj9lvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) [End CNI-103796-K] invalid measurements of TOE, DU(L1) [CB007595-B]or Radio(L1) [End CB007595-B] shall report to 5G-L2-PS the values:

* −32768 for TOE measurement
* -1 for the peak amplitute associated to TOE

For TOE on PUCCH and its associated peak amplitude measurements, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_6378\_replaced([11108024](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kolyxPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

For TOE on PUSCH and its associated peak amplitude measurements, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1625\_replaced([11107936](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kokkpfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

For TOE on SRS and its associated peak amplitude measurements, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7887\_replaced([11108046](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kolyzPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

For antenna-wise TOE on PUSCH and its associated peak amplitude measurements, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_6375\_replaced([11107978](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kolLtPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

For antenna-wise TOE on SRS and its associated peak amplitude measurements, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7897\_replaced([11108071](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_komZ1fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[CB007595-B]

Note:

- "5G-L1-UL" is changed to "DU(L1)" since this version as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_20317\_replaced([11094116](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmZaYfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). (Note kept only in this version)

- Radio(L1) shall do the TOE measuremenet on PUSCH [CB007595-C] and SRS[End CB007595-C] in case of eCPRI 7-2e, otherwise DU(L1) shall do the measurements.

- Case3 is not supported in eCPRI 7-2e.

[End CB007595-B]

#### 2.4.6.15.0-10 Invalid measurements Invalid Measurements of PRACH noise power (ID: 11109801)

Invalid measurements

[End CNI-126050-A]

* Noise power on PRACH: 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7035\_replaced([11108464](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_korSWPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

#### 2.4.6.15.0-11 Invalid measurements Invalid Measurements of RIM-RS Peak Value (ID: 11109810)

Invalid measurements

### 2.4.6.16 Positioning measurements

#### 2.4.6.16.1 UL TOE based on SRS for Positioning

##### 2.4.6.16.1.0-1 UL TOE based on SRS for Positioning Overview (ID: 11109820)

**2.4.6.16.1.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `11109825`)

UL TOE based on SRS for Positioning
For TDD FR1, eCPRI 7-2a and Non-BF cell, DU shall perform TOE upon reception of SRS configuration via MEASUREMENT REQUEST from LMF (Location Management Function).

[CB007880-A] In addition to TOE value, DU shall also measurement SINR per UE per TRP (collocated antennas) and  UL-SRS-RSRP per UE per TRP (collocated antennas) to assist TOE measurement. Refer to 5GMAX chapter 2.7.6 and 2.7.7 for UL-SRS-RSRP and SINR measurements, respectively.[End CB007880-A].

Periodic SRS on special slot uplink symbol sent from the UE are taken into account to derive the TOE measurement. Refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_27058\_replaced([11107767](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koivcvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for the TOE estimation algorithm.

UL TOE based on SRS for Positioning

| Thor | supported | section 2.7 | CB010332-B |
