#### 2.4.6.16.2 Timing Advance Estimation on PRACH for Positioning

##### 2.4.6.16.2.0-1 Timing Advance Estimation on PRACH for Positioning Overview (ID: 11109835)

**2.4.6.16.2.0-1.0-3**  (ID: `12775812`)

Timing Advance Estimation on PRACH for Positioning
**Purpose:**

DU shall perform timing advance estimation (timing offset estimation) on each detected preamble from PRACH reception, for UL NR E-CID positioning Timing Advance (TADV) measurement report (see [11520212](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_tU7i5fv1Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

**Input:**

- The time offset corresponding to the peak value of the strongest path, see [11107905](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koj9m_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

- MRBTS/NRBTS/NRCELL/actUlNrEcIdTAdvEnh.

- The inputs of [11109843](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko8_MPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

- [CB008577-E] NRCELL.actPrachProbing [End CB008577-E]

**Algorithm:**

DU shall provide the TADV based on PRACH reception which equals to (TgNB-RX – TgNB-TX) + NTAoffset. Note, this TADV is different with 38.215 section 5.2.7 (exclude NTAoffset) but same with 38.133 section 13.5.1 (include NTAoffset) as a final result, so gNB-DU shall avoid to add NTAoffset again during TADV report mapping (see [11324983](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Tsq4ovvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). Refer to MRBTS/NRBTS/NRCELL/timingAdvanceOffset for the NTAoffset.

When MRBTS/NRBTS/NRCELL/actUlNrEcIdTAdvEnh is false,**DU shall re-use the PRACH initial timing advance as the Timing Advance (TADV) estimation and report to higher layer:**

- The initial timing advance resolution is "Ns × Ts" (see [11107905](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koj9m_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). **DU shall convert the timing advance value to Tc unit for TADV measurement report.**

- DU shall provide the TADV based on PRACH initial timing advance for the strongest path. Note, this is different with 38.215 section 5.2.7 which based on the first detected path in time. The strongest path usually matches with the first detected path, but not always.

When MRBTS/NRBTS/NRCELL/actUlNrEcIdTAdvEnh is true, DU shall perform the timing advance estimation resolution and accuracy improvement algorithms.

- **First path detection, see [11109843](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko8_MPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).**

- **Quadratic interpolation, see [11109848](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko8_MvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).**

- DU shall report the improved TADV in Tc unit.

DU shall support **the online modification of the parameter** MRBTS/NRBTS/NRCELL/actUlNrEcIdTAdvEnh.\*\*\*\*

For non beamforming, DU shall provide one measurement across all the valid RX antennas for each detected preamble.

[Before CB008577-E]

For beamforming, DU shall provide one measurement for all the valid RX antennas associated with one beam for each detected preamble.

[End CB008577-E]

[CB008577-E]

For beamforming (see [11106028](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_lGJSdvvHEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), DU shall provide one measurement per beam for each detected preamble:

* If PRACH probing is disabled (NRCELL.actPrachProbing=false), DU only detect same preamble index once from one beam, DU shall provide one measurement for the valid RX antennas associated with the one beam.
* If PRACH probing is enabled (NRCELL.actPrachProbing=true), DU can detect same preamble index up to 4 times from 4 beams, as such DU shall provide up to 4 measurements, each measurement for 2 valid RX antennas associated with one beam (see [11106062](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koTe5_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[End CB008577-E]

Refer to [11105836](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koQblfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) for the PRACH valid RX antennas. Note, the RX antennas means the spatial streams input to the receiver.

**Output:**

- The TADV measurement report for UL NR E-CID positioning purpose, in Tc unit (see [11491325](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_tRtPzPv1Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ) for Tc definition).

Note, L1(DU) shall report the measurements to L2(DU).

Refer to [11109853](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko9mMPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) for the L1 HWs support timing advance estimation measurement report and the improvement algorithms. Refer to [11366259](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_udadovvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)**for the timing advance resolution information.**

---

##### 2.4.6.16.2.0-2 Timing Advance Estimation on PRACH for Positioning First path detection (ID: 11109843)

**2.4.6.16.2.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11109845`)

Timing Advance Estimation on PRACH for Positioning
Purpose:

For normal PRACH receiver, DU shall detect the peak value (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_28581\_replaced([11105872](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koRCovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and derive the corresponding timing offset (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1614\_replaced([11107905](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koj9m_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) based on the strongest path, and the strongest path usually matches with the first path but not always. For positioning accuracy purpose, DU shall detect the first path for timing advance estimation (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31689\_replaced([11109835](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko8_LfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), which is align with 38.215 section 5.2.7 (see 5G\_UP\_IF\_7546\_replaced([11520212](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_tU7i5fv1Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

Input:

- the peak value of the strongest path, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_28581\_replaced([11105872](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koRCovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

- the time offset corresponding to the peak value of the strongest path, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1614\_replaced([11107905](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koj9m_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

- the PRACH DTX threshold, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1466\_replaced([11105812](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koP0jvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1469\_replaced([11105826](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koQbkfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_15426\_replaced([11105877](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koRCpPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

- the NIFFT power metric samples, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_15409\_replaced([11105738](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koPNcPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Algorithm:

DU shall consider the peak value and timing offset of the strongest path for preamble detection is same with the first path by default.

For a strong enough peak value, DU shall further search the earlier weaker path based on the strongest path, the NIFFT power metric samples and the PRACH DTX threshold. If DU detects an earlier weaker path successfully, it will consider the earlier weaker path as the first path of the detected preamble. Then DU shall derive a new power metric value and related new timing offset of the first path for timing advance estimation.

Refer to section "2.5.15.2 First Path Time Offset Estimation" of the 5GMax algorithm specification (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_695\_replaced([11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) for more details about the first path detection algorithm.

Output:

- the power metric value of the first path.

- the timing offset corresponding to the power metric value of the first path.

For the supported L1 HWs, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31692\_replaced([11109853](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko9mMPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

##### 2.4.6.16.2.0-3 Timing Advance Estimation on PRACH for Positioning Quadratic interpolation (ID: 11109848)

**2.4.6.16.2.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11109850`)

Timing Advance Estimation on PRACH for Positioning
Purpose:

DU shall perform quadratic interpolation to improve the timing advance estimation resolution and accuracy, due to fixed IFFT size and timing offset estimation resolution in L1 HWs.

Input:

- the power metric value and the time offset of the first path, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_32166\_replaced([11109843](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko8_MPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

- the NIFFT power metric samples, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_15409\_replaced([11105738](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koPNcPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Algorithm:

DU shall perform quadratic interpolation based on the power metric value of the first path and its left and right neighbouring power metric samples for each detected preamble. DU shall search the maximum value after quadratic interpolation and derive the new timing offset corresponding to this maximum value.

DU shall use the new timing offset value after quadratic interpolation as the improved timing advance (TADV) estimation.

For the corner cases when the power metric of the first path is at the start or end position of the detection window, DU shall not perform quadratic interpolation and shall report the time offset without interpolation as the TADV estimation.

Refer to section "2.5.15.1 Time Offset Interpolation" of the 5GMax algorithm specification (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_695\_replaced([11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) for more details about the quadratic interpolation algorithm.

Output:

- the improved TADV estimation for positioning.

For the supported L1 HWs, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31692\_replaced([11109853](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko9mMPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

##### 2.4.6.16.2.0-4 Timing Advance Estimation on PRACH for Positioning Configurations support timing advance measurement for positioning (ID: 11109853)

**2.4.6.16.2.0-4.0-3**  (ID: `11109861`)

Timing Advance Estimation on PRACH for Positioning
DU shall support the timing advance estimation measurement report [CB013507-A] and related improvement algorithms [End CB013507-A] (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31689\_replaced([11109835](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko8_LfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) in below L1 HWs:

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| L1 HW | TADV measurement report | Quadratic interpolation | First path detection |
| Loner | Supported | - | - |
| Loki | Supported | Supported | Supported |
| Thor | Supported | Supported | Supported |
| First Feature | CB009842-A | CB013507-A | CB013507-A |

Note:

* For Loner L1 HW, gNB does not support TADV measurement report for TDD FR2 from E2E POV.

## 2.4.7 Performance

**2.4.7.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `11109871`)

Performance
[Before CNI-103796-M] L1 performance for PUSCH, measured by PUSCH metrics, for the Nokia implementation for vRAN shall be not worse than for Flex RAN implementation [End CNI-103796-M]

## 2.4.8 Broken Antenna Handling

### 2.4.8.0-2 Broken Antenna Handling Introduction (ID: 11109879)

**2.4.8.0-2.0-2**  (ID: `11109884`)

Broken Antenna Handling
[Before CNI-103796-M] In broken Rx antenna scenarios for all uplink channels, 5G-L1-UL shall be able to receive zero-valued IQ samples or non-zero-valued thermal noises IQ samples from broken antennas.  For PUCCH/SRS/PUSCH, L1 doesn’t handle broken antennas. [End CNI-103796-M]

---

### 2.4.8.0-3 Broken Antenna Handling Broken antenna handling for TDD (ID: 11109887)

**2.4.8.0-3.0-2**  (ID: `11109892`)

Broken Antenna Handling
[Before CNI-103796-M] For PRACH in TDD, broken antenna detection is supported and handled for vDU L1 HW.

In 2 RX configuration, the number of valid RX antennas is 2 by default. In case 1 Rx antenna is broken, the number of valid RX antenna is 1.

In 4 RX configuration for TDD FR1, the number of valid RX antennas is 4 by default. In case 1 or 2 or 3 Rx antennas are broken, the number of valid RX antennas is 3 or 2 or 1 correspondingly. For debug purpose, L1 should generate some internal logs to show which and how many Rx antennas are broken.

Refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_6\_replaced([11094165](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmaBcPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for mapping of ulSubcellType to number of configured Rx antennas.

The number of valid RX antennas will impact the antenna correlation processing and DTX threshold calculation.

PRACH broken antenna detection algorithm is described in section 2.5.7 “Broken Antenna Detection and Antenna-Specific Scaling” in 5GMax-5G New Radio Algorithm Specification (5G\_UP\_ALG\_695\_replaced([11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).[End CNI-103796-M]

---

### 2.4.8.0-4 Broken Antenna Handling Broken antenna handling for FDD (ID: 11109894)

**2.4.8.0-4.0-2**  (ID: `11109899`)

Broken Antenna Handling
[Before CNI-103796-M] For PRACH in FDD, broken antenna handling is not supported vDU L1 HW.

If 2 Rx antennas is configured, 5G-L1-UL shall always use 2 Rx for DTX threshold calculation.

If 4 Rx antennas is configured, 5G-L1-UL shall always use 4Rx for DTX threshold calculation.

Refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_6\_replaced([11094165](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmaBcPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for mapping of ulSubcellType to number of configured Rx antennas.

Refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1466\_replaced([11105812](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koP0jvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for PRACH DTX Threshold. [End CNI-103796-M]

---

### 2.4.8.0-5 Broken Antenna Handling Active RX input handling for PRACH Receiver in GPU (ID: 13446610)

**2.4.8.0-5.0-1**  (ID: `13446611`)

Broken Antenna Handling
For PRACH Receiver Detection Algorithm (refer to [11105783](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koP0gvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), DU for GPU L1 HW shall support Active RX inputs (refer to 5GMAX Specification [11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) algorithm.

Input: N RX = Number of configured spatial streams inputs to PRACH receiver as in [11094165](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmaBcPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) supported setup.

Algorithm:   
DU for GPU L1 HW shall perform the "active" RX spatial stream determination procedure (refer to 5GMax algorithm specification [11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for configured setup inputs to PRACH receiver.  
For each of N RX configured inputs to PRACH receiver, DU shall:

* evaluate the strength of the signal input
* compute the power and determine if RX spatial stream not active (e.g. faulty or signal input below Channel Sensitivity).

Output: M active RX spatial streams input to PRACH Receiver Detection Algorithm.

Rationale:  
- Failure in RX input to PRACH Receiver (i.e. Broken RX) significantly changes the detection threshold. To determine whether RX is faulty, it is necessary to calculate the power at that intensity.

---

## 2.4.9 UL FDM Configurations

### 2.4.9.0-1 UL FDM Configurations Document Owner Feng-Frank Li (NSB) (ID: 11109904)

### 2.4.9.1 FDM Configurations

#### 2.4.9.1.0-1 FDM Configurations UL FDM combination for TDD FR1 (ID: 11109909)

**2.4.9.1.0-1.0-5**  (ID: `13431690`)

FDM Configurations
For TDD FR1 DU for Loner L1 HW, Loki L1 HW, Thor **[CB016061-D] and GPU** [End CB016061-D] L1 HW and Thor (Radio 7-2e) L1 HW shall support reception of FDMed channels on the same subcells for:\*\*\*\*

FDM PUSCH + Long PUCCH

FDM Long PUCCH + Long PUCCH

FDM PUSCH + PRACH + Long PUCCH

FDM SRS + Long PUCCH

---

#### 2.4.9.1.0-2 FDM Configurations UL FDM combination in PRACH slot for TDD FR1 (ID: 11109919)

**2.4.9.1.0-2.0-2**  (ID: `11109924`)

FDM Configurations
PUSCH and short PUCCH within the PRACH slots is TDM manner as in legacy. The multiplexed PRACH and PUxCH is coming from different UE in the PRACH slot.

The FDMed channels from 5GC001348-A onward can come from the same or different subcells.

In PRACH slot, UE is also scheduled with PUSCH transmission. The maximum number of PUSCH grants per PRACH slot is specified in 5G\_UP\_318\_replaced([11362802](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_uc-YzvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)),5G\_UP\_IF\_1637\_replaced([11513792](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_tT62Svv1Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and depends on the system release. The number of the PUSCH grant in this feature does not take the PRACH UE into account which is applicable for all sub features.

UE is also scheduled with PUCCH format 0 transmission in PRACH slot. The maximum number of PUCCH format 0 can be multiplexed in PRACH slot is specified in 5G\_UP\_3230\_replaced([11397508](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_uf2tp_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and depends on the system release.

UE is also scheduled with PUCCH format 2 transmission in PRACH slot. The maximum number of PUCCH format 2 can be multiplexed in PRACH slot is specified in 5G\_UP\_3230\_replaced([11397508](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_uf2tp_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and depends on the system release.

Note:

1. For the CPRI fronthaul based RU, the subcell used for PUSCH and PRACH reception depend on the receiving beam of each channel. If they are from the same beam (sector beam/coarse beam/refined beam) then the same subcell can be used otherwise separate subcells shall be used.
2. For the eCPRI fronthaul based RU, in case FD-beamforming is not supported (can not mapping different beam to the same eAxC) then the subcell used for PUSCH and PRACH reception depend on the receiving beam of each channel. If they are from the same beam (sector beam/coarse beam/refined beam) then the same subcell can be used otherwise separate subcells shall be used.
3. The FDM capacity here assume that there is subcell can be used for FDM PUSCH receiving in PRACH slot.
4. For the eCPRI fronthaul based RU, in case FD-beamforming is supported with MADE based RU, the PRB allocation in frequency domain shall follow region division limitation of the RU.
5. In 5GC001348 for TDD FR1 and TDD FR2, this feature does not increase the number of CSI reports per PUCCH symbol. It only introduces FDM of PUCCH format 2 for HARQ feedback.
6. In 5GC001348 the PUCCH format 2 FDM with PUCCH format 2 for periodic CSI report usage is applicable for FR1 FDD only.

[CB007595-B]

1. For eCPRI 7-2e, only long PUCCH is supported(format 1 and format 3). Only FD-beamforming is supported and there is no restriction on the subcell usage for PUSCH and PRACH as stated in bullet 2 above.

[End CB007595-B]

---

#### 2.4.9.1.0-3 FDM Configurations Supported UL FDM combination in PRACH slot for TDD FR1 (ID: 11109927)

**2.4.9.1.0-3.0-4**  (ID: `13431692`)

FDM Configurations
The supported UL FDM combination in PRACH slot for TDD FR1(refer to ([11109919](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko-NTPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))  for different L1 variants is as below table:

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  | ([11109919](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko-NTPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) | First feature |
| Loner L1 HW | supported |  |
| Loki L1 HW | supported |  |
| Thor L1 HW | supported | CB008325-5G-AA |
| Thor (Radio 7-2e) L1 HW | supported | CB007595-B |
| GPU | supported | **CB016061-D** |

---

#### 2.4.9.1.0-4 FDM Configurations UL FDM for FDD FR1 (ID: 11109936)

**2.4.9.1.0-4.0-1**  (ID: `11109939`)

FDM Configurations
FDD FR1 L1: FDM of channels are done on the same subcells for

FDM PUSCH + Long PUCCH

FDM Long PUCCH + Long PUCCH

Note: for FDD more than one PUSCH reources can be FDMed.

FDD FR1 L1: FDM of channels are done on the same subcells for

FDM PUSCH + PRACH + Long PUCCH

---

#### 2.4.9.1.0-5 FDM Configurations UL FDM combination in PRACH slot for FDD FR1 Loner HW (ID: 11109942)

**2.4.9.1.0-5.0-1**  (ID: `11109943`)

FDM Configurations
PUSCH and short PUCCH within the PRACH slots is TDM manner as in legacy. The multiplexed PRACH and PUxCH is coming from different UE in the PRACH slot.

As in FDD FR1, the cells are configured without beamforming, all the uplink channels are received from the same sector beam and the same subcell.

In PRACH slot, UE is also scheduled with PUSCH transmission. The maximum number of PUSCH grants per PRACH slot is specified in 5G\_UP\_318\_replaced([11362802](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_uc-YzvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)),5G\_UP\_IF\_1637\_replaced([11513792](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_tT62Svv1Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and depends on the system release. The number of the PUSCH grant in this feature does not take the PRACH UE into account which is applicable for all sub features.

UE is also scheduled with PUCCH format 0 transmission in PRACH slot. The maximum number of PUCCH format 0 can be multiplexed in PRACH slot is specified in 5G\_UP\_3230\_replaced([11397508](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_uf2tp_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and depends on the system release.

UE is also scheduled with PUCCH format 2 transmission in PRACH slot. The maximum number of PUCCH format 2 can be multiplexed in PRACH slot is specified in 5G\_UP\_3230\_replaced([11397508](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_uf2tp_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and depends on the system release.

Note:

1. In FDD FR1, there is no beamforming or eCPRI fronthaul support.

---

#### 2.4.9.1.0-6 FDM Configurations UL FDM combination in PRACH slot for FDD FR1 Loki HW (ID: 11109946)

**2.4.9.1.0-6.0-2**  (ID: `11109952`)

FDM Configurations
The multiplexed PRACH and PUxCH is coming from different UE in the PRACH slot.

As in FDD FR1, the cells are configured without beamforming, all the uplink channels are received from the same sector beam and the same subcell.

In PRACH slot, UE is also scheduled with PUSCH transmission. The maximum number of PUSCH grants per PRACH slot is specified in 5G\_UP\_318\_replaced([11362802](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_uc-YzvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)),5G\_UP\_IF\_1637\_replaced([11513792](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_tT62Svv1Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and depends on the system release. The number of the PUSCH grant in this feature does not take the PRACH UE into account which is applicable for all sub features.

Note:

1. In FDD FR1, there is no beamforming [Before CB007814-D] or eCPRI fronthaul [End CB007814-D] support.

---

## 2.4.10 RIM-RS Reception

### 2.4.10.0-1 RIM-RS Reception Document Owner Sebastien Bonnet (Nokia) (ID: 11109956)

### 2.4.10.1 Introduction

#### 2.4.10.1.0-1 Introduction Overview (ID: 11109962)

**2.4.10.1.0-1.0-4**  (ID: `12643735`)

Introduction
When NRBTS/NRCELL/actNrRim is set to 3   or 4  , if RI is detected (details see [11360252](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_uctTC_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), the DU with eCPRI 7-2a [CB010315-SR-B] and CPRI [Before CB010315-SR-C](only with actNrRim equals to 3) [End CB010315-SR-C][End CB010315-SR-B] and the Radio (for eCPRI 7-2e, when NRCELL/actEcpri72e is set to "TRUE")  shall perform the following actions via requests per slot configuration provided by high layers (see [11161857](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktWW9_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)):

* generate the RIM-RS candidate root sequences
* perform data fetching(DU with eCPRI 7-2a [CB010315-SR-B] and CPRI [Before CB010315-SR-C](only with actNrRim equals to 3) [End CB010315-SR-C][End CB010315-SR-B] only)
* do RIM-RS signal detecting
* perform RIM-RS signal power and peak value measurement
* report RIM-RS detection and measurement result

Note:1) RIM-RS in RIM Framework-1(i.e. NRBTS/NRCELL/actNrRim = 4) shall include both RS type 1 and type 2.

L1 High Level Processing Showing
Here is L1 High Level processing showing for eCPRI.
 
Figure 1 RIM-RS L1 High Level Receiver Processing Reference Model for eCPRI
 
[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a flowchart illustrating a process related to radio units (RU) and their functions, specifically focusing on RIM-RS (Radio Interface Measurement Reference Signal) and its associated processing.
The flowchart is divided into several sections, indicated by dashed boxes and connecting arrows.
**Top Section:**
\* **RU Function:** A blue dashed box containing the text "RU Function".
\* Below this, a red dashed box contains "DU(eCPRI 7-2a)/ Radio(CPRI 7-2e) Function".
\* **RIM Framework-1:** A rectangular box with the text "RIM Framework-1 Subcarrier offset New introduced".
\* **RIM-RS Candidate Root Sequences:** A rectangular box with the text "RIM-RS Candidate Root Sequences".
**Left Section (within a larger blue dashed box):**
\* **Normal CP remove:** A rectangular box. An arrow points from "RX Signal from RU as normal PUSCH channel" to this box.
\* **Frequency-domain RX signal (FFT grid):** A rectangular box. An arrow points from "Normal CP remove" to this box.
\* **Normal phase compensation:** A rectangular box. An arrow points from "Frequency-domain RX signal (FFT grid)" to this box.
**Right Section (within a larger red dashed box):**
\* **RIM-RS resource de-mapping:** A rectangular box. An arrow points from "Normal phase compensation" and another arrow points from "RIM Framework-1 Subcarrier offset New introduced" to this box. An arrow also points from "RIM-RS Candidate Root Sequences" to this box.
\* **Frequency correlation:** A rectangular box. An arrow points from "RIM-RS resource de-mapping" to this box.
\* **1024 IFFT:** A rectangular box. An arrow points from "Frequency correlation" to this box.
\* **PAPR vs threshold:** A rectangular box. An arrow points from "1024 IFFT" to this box.
\* **Signal power measurement:** A rectangular box. An arrow points from the "PAPR vs threshold" box to this box.
\* **Conditional Logic:** A rectangular box containing two lines of text:
\* "If PAPR < threshold (IsRimRs: false)"
\* "If PAPR > threshold (IsRimRs: true)"
An arrow points from "PAPR vs threshold" to this conditional logic box. An arrow also points from this conditional logic box back to the "Signal power measurement" box, creating a feedback loop.
The overall flow suggests that a received signal undergoes CP removal and frequency-domain processing. This processed signal is then used for RIM-RS resource de-mapping, which is also influenced by RIM Framework-1 and candidate root sequences. The de-mapped RIM-RS data is then subjected to frequency correlation, a 1024 IFFT, and a PAPR (Peak-to-Average Power Ratio) versus threshold comparison. Based on this comparison, a signal power measurement is performed, and a flag (IsRimRs) is set to true or false.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_4255.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]
 
Note: 1) the above figure is based on 100MHz, SCS=30kHz;
         2) this design aims to make RU transparent.
         3) It's just for general understanding, detail implementation please follow 5GMax algorithm section 2.10.
         4) This High level chart is valid for both RIM Framework-0 and RIM Framework-1. Please note subcarrier offset function block is new introdecd by RIM Framework-1.
         5) RIM-RS in RIM Framework-1(i.e. NRBTS/NRCELL/actNrRim = 4) shall include both RS type 1 and type 2.
         6) Subcarrier frequency offset is new introduced for the mapping of RIM-RS to the physical resources to support mixed bandwidths scenario in RIM Framework-1(see 5G\_UP\_11416\_replaced([11360126](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucsr8vvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).
        7) Although there are different formulars between 5GMax algorithms and gNB-DU algorithms in 5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_12403\_replaced([11279177](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpUCrvvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for the determination of the physical resources that shall be used for RIM-RS transmission, the same results are achieved.
       8) The figure is stored in [image-1][image\_desc]The image contains blue text on a black background. The text appears to be a URL or a file path. The text reads: "RAN ARS - 5GX Spec. Repository - DLL-RI M- Tree\_View(sharepoint.com)". The text is underlined with a blue line.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_4255.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]
       9) [Before CB012564-C][CB012564-B]  the Radio (for eCPRI 7-2e, [Before CB009514-L]when NRBTS/actEcpri72e is set to "Ecpri72eULDL"[CB009514-L] when NRCELL/actEcpri72e is set to "TRUE"[End CB009514-L]
) shall support RIM Framework-0 [End CB012564-B][End CB012564-C]

Here is L1 High Level processing showing for CPRI
 
Figure 2 RIM-RS L1 High Level Receiver Processing Reference Model for CPRI
 
[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a flowchart illustrating a signal processing procedure. The flowchart is enclosed within a dashed red rectangle, indicating a specific process or system.
Here's a breakdown of the elements and their flow:
1. **Input:** The process begins with an "RX Signal from RU as normal PUSCH channel" box on the left.
2. **Initial Processing:**
\* An arrow leads from the input box to a "RIM-RS CP remove" box.
\* Another arrow leads from the "RX Signal from RU as normal PUSCH channel" box to a "Signal power measurement" box.
3. **Intermediate Processing:**
\* An arrow goes from "RIM-RS CP remove" to a "Frequency-domain RX signal (FFT grid)" box.
\* An arrow goes from "Frequency-domain RX signal (FFT grid)" to a "RIM-RS resource de-mapping" box.
\* An arrow goes from "RIM-RS resource de-mapping" to a "Frequency correlation" box.
\* An arrow goes from "Frequency correlation" to a "1024 IFFT" box.
\* An arrow goes from "1024 IFFT" back to the "Frequency correlation" box, forming a loop.
4. **Conditional Logic:**
\* An arrow from the "Signal power measurement" box leads to a box containing two conditional statements: "If PAPR < threshold (IsRimRs: false)" and "If PAPR > threshold (IsRimRs: true)".
\* An arrow from the "PAPR vs threshold" box points to the conditional logic box.
5. **External Inputs/References:**
\* A "DU(CPRI) Function" box is positioned above and to the left of the main flowchart, with a dashed red outline.
\* A "RIM Framework- 1 Subcarrier offset New introduced" box is positioned above the flowchart, with an arrow pointing to the "RIM-RS resource de-mapping" box.
\* A "RIM-RS Candidate Root Sequences" box is positioned above the flowchart, with an arrow pointing to the "Frequency correlation" box.
The overall flow suggests a process that receives a signal, removes CP, transforms it to the frequency domain, de-maps resources, performs frequency correlation, and then uses an IFFT and PAPR thresholding for further processing or decision-making. The "DU(CPRI) Function" and "RIM Framework" elements likely represent contextual information or control parameters for the main signal processing chain.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/img-05281119.png[/image\_path][/image-0]
 
Note: 1) the above figure is based on 100MHz, SCS=30kHz;
         2) this design aims to make RU transparent.
         3) It's just for general understanding, detail implementation please follow 5GMax algorithm section 2.10.
          4）This figure is implemented both for RIM framework-0 [CB010315-SR-C] and RIM framewoek-1 which new introduces subcarrier offset level in RIM-RS resource mapping [End CB010315-SR-C].
          5) The figure source is in the URL:[RAN A&S- 5G-CB & 2/3/4G Spec. Repository of Architecture and Specification - Technical reference - All Documents](https://nokia.sharepoint.com/sites/ASCOP/AS%20Documents/Forms/AllItems.aspx?id=%2Fsites%2FASCOP%2FAS%20Documents%2FFS1TAs%2DCFAMs%2FCB%2FCB010000%20%2D%20CB010499%2FCFAM%2FCB010315%20NR%20RIM%20feature%20support%20for%20FR1%2DTDD%20CPRI%2FTechnical%20reference&viewid=73435348%2D8379%2D4262%2Da3aa%2Dffe0b7322e0c)

---

### 2.4.10.2 RIM-RS Candidate Root Sequence Generation

#### 2.4.10.2.0-1 RIM-RS Candidate Root Sequence Generation RIM-RS candidate root sequence generation (ID: 11109999)

**2.4.10.2.0-1.0-4**  (ID: `12643749`)

RIM-RS Candidate Root Sequence Generation
12643749:
RIM-RS Candidate Root Sequence Generation
12643749:
RIM-RS Candidate Root Sequence Generation
12643749:
RIM-RS Candidate Root Sequence Generation
12643749:

RIM-RS Candidate Root Sequence Generation
For each configuration of

[image-0][image\_desc]The image contains three mathematical expressions separated by commas. The first expression is "n\_SCID". The second expression is "N\_RB^RIM". The third expression is "c\_init(i)".[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/img-06121302.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

and sub-carrier offset(only when NRBTS/NRCELL/actNrRim = 4) for each start PRB index (see [11360126](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucsr8vvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))  (see [11161857](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktWW9_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), the DU with eCPRI 7-2a[CB010315-SR-B] and CPRI [Before CB010315-SR-C](only with actNrRim equals to 3) [End CB010315-SR-C][End CB010315-SR-B] and the Radio (for eCPRI 7-2e, when NRCELL/actEcpri72e is set to "TRUE") shall generate one RIM-RS candidate root sequence (see [11109959](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko-0WvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), see 3GPP 38.211 V16.7 Clause 7.4.1.6.2).

The number of RIM-RS candidate root sequences shall be up to 8  each for RS1 and RS2 .

Note: RIM-RS in RIM Framework-1(i.e. NRBTS/NRCELL/actNrRim = 4) shall include both RS type 1 and type 2.

---

### 2.4.10.3 RIM-RS Receiver Data Fetching

#### 2.4.10.3.0-1 RIM-RS Receiver Data Fetching RIM-RS receiver data fetching for eCPRI (ID: 11110030)

**2.4.10.3.0-1.0-5**  (ID: `13462981`)

RIM-RS Receiver Data Fetching
13462981:
RIM-RS Receiver Data Fetching
13462981:
RIM-RS Receiver Data Fetching
13462981:
RIM-RS Receiver Data Fetching
13462981:
RIM-RS Receiver Data Fetching
If actNrRim is configured to 3-“framework-0” OR 4-“framework-1”**, according to the RIM-RS per slot detection configuration provided by higher layers (see [11161857](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktWW9_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), DU with eCPRI 7-2a shall perform the following RIM-RS data fetching:**

For eCPRI 7-2a,

* Two dedicated eAxC streams shall be used for RIM-RS data reception:

- eAxC index 2, 3 in C4 subcell case

- eAxC index 0, 1 in C2 subcell case

**- eAxC index 6, 7 in C8 subcell** case\*\*\*\*

* For the overlapped PRBs with PUSCH/ap-SRS
* For C4 subcell case, RIM-RS reception will reuse the PUSCH/ap-SRS I/Q data samples associated to eAxC index 2 and eAxC index 3 streams. For BF mode, eAxC 2 and eAxC 3 streams shall be configured with sector beam (no dedicated RIM-RS beam is assumed) (see [11162584](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktXlF_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

- For C2 subcell case, RIM-RS reception will reuse the PUSCH/ap-SRS **I/Q data samples associated to eAxC index 0 and eAxC index 1 streams for non-beamforming scenario.**

* For C8 subcell case, (BF mode only supported) eAxC 6 and eAxC 7 streams shall be configured with sector beam (no dedicated RIM-RS beam is assumed) (see [11162584](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktXlF_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
* For the overlapped PRBs (blank regions) with PUCCH/PRACH (see [11360520](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucvINvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

- DU with eCPRI 7-2a shall ignore these PRBs I/Q data samples, that is no need to do definite data fetching for RIM-RS purpose. For 3GPP compliancy purpose (see 38.211 Chapter 7.4.1.6.4.3 V16.7 specification for [image-0][image\_desc]The image contains text that appears to be "N RIM R.H.". The text is blurry and has a gradient effect, with colors ranging from blue to orange. The letters are large and bold, with the "N" being the largest and most prominent. The "RIM" is positioned to the right of the "N", and "R.H." is located below and slightly to the right of the "N". The background is white and out of focus.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_4267.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]), DU with eCPRI 7-2a shall fill these PRBs with zero values to perform RIM-RS signal detection.

* The UL symbols of special slot where pSRS is present will be handled as scheduled region for RIM-RS detection. DU is expecting to receive IQ data for the whole bandwidth per [11378290](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ueZ8LfvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) and shall do precombination for the received IQ data according to configuration. In case of BF mode and RI and RIM-RS are configured in the same slot, RIM-RS sector beam shall be used to do precombine both for RI and RIM-RS. (See [11161420](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktUhwfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
* For unscheduled PRBs in eCPRI, RIM-RS specific UL reception request shall be used. DU with eCPRI 7-2a needs to create dedicated FCP messages (see [11378871](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_uedmhfvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ), [11378985](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ueeNk_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ), [11379021](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ueeNmfvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) and [11379067](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_uee0oPvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for configuring the reception of I/Q data for unscheduled symbols and PRBs from L1Low. RIM-RS sector beam shall be used in case of BF mode (see [11162584](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktXlF_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ), see [11162620](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktXlI_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

RI measurement request will be configured with same indexes of streams as RS detection (see [11161420](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktUhwfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Note:

1) Only C2 with non-BF shall be supported for Loki L1 HW, C2 with BF shall be excluded due to impact PUSCH performance much for RIM-RS reception mechanism.

2) **In RIM Framework-1, RIM-RS shall include both RS1 and RS2.**

3) **In RIM Framework-1, one more PRB may be needed for receiving data, that is the number of PRB shall be (48+1) or (96+1) (see [11360126](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucsr8vvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).**

4) When RIM-RS detection is scheduled on special slots with NDM-SRS symbols:

* In BF mode [CB015735-A]:
* When TDD frame structure is TD-LTE-aligned (see [11347007](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubJMwvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)), DU shall support the processing of periodic SRS for SU-MIMO or beam management and RIM-RS detection on the same symbols. In that case, in eCPRI-7-2a only, it is assumed that RIM-RS detection is not requested on symbols where periodic SRS is scheduled (see details in [11162042](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktW-APvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)).
* For other frame structures, [End CB015735-A] the scheduling of periodic SRS for SU-MIMO or beam management is not supported (see [11162042](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktW-APvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)).
* In non-BF mode, requesting periodic SRS for ~~global~~ positioning is allowed. [CB015735-B] In RI state 2, [End CB015735-B] L1 shall discard periodic SRS processing and fill output measurements (see [11107769](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koivc_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) with invalid values (see [11162042](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktW-APvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)).

---

#### 2.4.10.3.0-2 RIM-RS Receiver Data Fetching RIM-RS receiver data fetching for CPRI (ID: 12684138)

**2.4.10.3.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `12684147`)

RIM-RS Receiver Data Fetching
DU with CPRI shall get all the IQ data configured as [11161857](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktWW9_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)

---

Here are some examples for data fetching showing to help understanding.

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a screenshot of a spreadsheet with colored cells. The title of the spreadsheet is "PUSCH without RIM-RS".
The spreadsheet has the following columns: "PRB Index", "eAxC0", "eAxC1", "eAxC2", "eAxC3", and "sectionID".
The rows represent "PRB Index" values, starting with "K1(0)" and incrementing.
Here's a breakdown of the content:
\* **Row 1 (Header):** "PUSCH without RIM-RS" is centered across the top. Below this, "C4" is highlighted in red.
\* **Row 2 (Column Headers):** "PRB Index", "eAxC0", "eAxC1", "eAxC2", "eAxC3", "sectionID".
\* **Rows 3-6 (UE1 Allocation):**
\* "K1(0)" and "K1(0)+1" are listed under "PRB Index".
\* "UE1" and "Beam0" are in the "eAxC0" column, colored green.
\* "UE1" and "Beam1" are in the "eAxC2" column, colored yellow.
\* "A" is in the "sectionID" column.
\* "K1(0)+30" and "K1(0)+31" are listed under "PRB Index", with no data in the subsequent columns.
\* **Rows 7-10 (UE2 Allocation):**
\* "K1(0)+40" and "K1(0)+41" are listed under "PRB Index".
\* "UE2" and "Beam2" are in the "eAxC0" column, colored blue.
\* "UE2" and "Beam3" are in the "eAxC1" column, colored red.
\* "B" is in the "sectionID" column.
\* "K1(0)+47" is listed under "PRB Index", with no data in the subsequent columns.
\* **Bottom Row:** "PUSCH" is repeated under "eAxC0", "eAxC1", "eAxC2", and "eAxC3".
The table can be represented as follows:

| PRB Index | eAxC0 | eAxC1 | eAxC2 | eAxC3 | sectionID |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| K1(0) | UE1 |  | UE1 |  | A |
| K1(0)+1 | Beam0 |  | Beam1 |  |  |
| ... |  |  |  |  |  |
| K1(0)+30 |  |  |  |  |  |
| K1(0)+31 |  |  |  |  |  |
| ... |  |  |  |  |  |
| K1(0)+40 |  |  |  |  |  |
| K1(0)+41 | UE2 | UE2 |  |  | B |
| ... | Beam2 | Beam3 |  |  |  |
| K1(0)+47 |  |  |  |  |  |
|  | PUSCH | PUSCH | PUSCH | PUSCH |  |

Figure1: RI is not detected, RIM-RS detection is not triggered

[image-1][image\_desc]The image contains a spreadsheet-like table with colored cells, illustrating a concept related to PUSCH (Physical Uplink Shared Channel) with RIM-RS (Reference Interference Measurement - Reference Signal).
The table has the following columns: "PRB Index", "eAxC0", "eAxC1", "eAxC2", "eAxC3", and "sectionID".
The rows are indexed by "PRB Index" values such as "K1(0)", "K1(0)+1", "K1(0)+30", "K1(0)+31", "K1(0)+40", "K1(0)+41", and "K1(0)+47".
The cells are colored and contain text indicating different assignments or states.
Here's a breakdown of the table content:

| PRB Index | eAxC0 | eAxC1 | eAxC2 | eAxC3 | sectionID | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| K1(0) | UE1 |  |  |  | A |  |
| K1(0)+1 | Beam0 | Sector | Sector |  |  |  |
|  |  | Beam | Beam |  |  |  |
| K1(0)+30 |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| K1(0)+31 |  |  | Sector |  | C | unscheduled PRBs |
|  |  |  | Beam |  |  |  |
| K1(0)+40 |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| K1(0)+41 | UE2 |  | Sector |  | B | PUSCH with eAxC 0,1,2 and 3 |
|  | Beam2 |  | Beam |  |  |  |
| K1(0)+47 |  |  |  |  |  |  |
|  | PUSCH | PUSCH | PUSCH | PUSCH |  | RIM-RS reuse |
|  |  | RIM-RS | RIM-RS |  |  | PUSCH with eAxC 2 and 3 |
| Additionally, there is a red cell at the top labeled "C4" and the title "PUSCH with RIM-RS". There are also red arrows pointing to specific cells with explanatory text. One arrow points to the "C" sectionID cell with the text "unscheduled PRBs". Another set of arrows points to the bottom row of "PUSCH" labels, indicating "RIM-RS reuse" and "PUSCH with eAxC 2 and 3". There is also text above the "B" sectionID cell stating "PUSCH with eAxC 0,1,2 and 3".[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_4269.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1] |  |  |  |  |  |  |

Figure2: RI is detected, RIM-RS detection is triggered

Note: 1) Take [image-2][image\_desc]The image contains blurry, stylized text that appears to be "N RIM R.B.". The letters are in a gradient of blue and orange, with a black outline. The text is angled and appears to be on a white background.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_4269.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2]==48 as example.

| PRB Index | eAxC0 | eAxC1 | sectionID |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| K1(0) | UE1 |  | A |
| K1(0)+1 |  |  |  |
| ... |  |  |  |
| K1(0)+30 |  |  |  |
| K1(0)+31 |  |  |  |
| ... |  |  |  |
| K1(0)+40 |  |  |  |

The cells corresponding to "UE1" and "UE2" are colored green and blue respectively.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_4271.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0] |  |  |  |

Figure1: RI is not detected, RIM-RS detection is not triggered

[image-1][image\_desc]The image is a screenshot of a spreadsheet that illustrates the concept of PUSCH (Physical Uplink Shared Channel) with RIM-RS (Radio Interface Measurement - Reference Signal). The spreadsheet is organized into columns and rows, with different colored cells indicating specific assignments.
Here's a breakdown of the content:
**Header:**
\* "PUSCH with RIM-RS" is the main title.
\* Below this, "C2" is highlighted in red.
\* The column headers are "PRB Index", "eAxC0", "eAxC1", and "sectionID".
**Rows and Cells:**
\* **PRB Index Column:** This column lists various "K1" values, indicating Physical Resource Blocks (PRBs). Examples include "K1(0)", "K1(0)+1", "K1(0)+30", "K1(0)+31", "K1(0)+40", "K1(0)+41", and "K1(0)+47". Ellipses (...) indicate a continuation of the sequence.
\* **eAxC0 Column:**
\* The cells corresponding to "K1(0)" through "K1(0)+30" are colored green and labeled "UE1".
\* The cells corresponding to "K1(0)+31" through "K1(0)+40" are colored yellow.
\* **eAxC1 Column:**
\* The cells corresponding to "K1(0)" through "K1(0)+30" are colored green.
\* The cells corresponding to "K1(0)+31" through "K1(0)+40" are colored yellow.
\* The cells corresponding to "K1(0)+41" through "K1(0)+47" are colored blue and labeled "UE2".
\* **sectionID Column:**
\* The cell corresponding to the green section is labeled "A".
\* The cell corresponding to the yellow section is labeled "C".
\* The cell corresponding to the blue section is labeled "B".
**Annotations and Labels:**
\* An arrow points to the yellow section with the text "unscheduled PRBs".
\* Below the blue section, there are labels "PUSCH" and "RIM-RS" repeated.
\* An arrow points to the "PUSCH" label in the blue section with the text "PUSCH with eAxC 0,1".
\* Another arrow points to the "RIM-RS" label in the blue section with the text "RIM-RS reuse PUSCH with eAxC 0 and 1".
**Table Representation:**

| PRB Index | eAxC0 | eAxC1 | sectionID |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| K1(0) | UE1 |  | A |
| K1(0)+1 |  |  |  |
| ... |  |  |  |
| K1(0)+30 |  |  |  |
| K1(0)+31 |  |  | C |
| ... |  |  |  |
| K1(0)+40 |  |  |  |
| K1(0)+41 |  | UE2 | B |
| ... |  |  |  |
| K1(0)+47 |  |  |  |
| **Note:** The colored cells in the original image represent contiguous blocks of PRBs. The table above is a simplified representation and does not fully capture the visual grouping indicated by the colors. The annotations provide additional context to the visual representation.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_4271.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1] |  |  |  |

Figure2: RI is detected, RIM-RS detection is triggered

Note: 1) Take [image-2][image\_desc]The image contains blurry, stylized text that appears to be "N RIM R.B.". The letters are in a gradient of blue and orange, with a black outline. The text is angled and appears to be on a white background.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_4271.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2]==48 as example.

Please see example below with and without RIM-RS:

[image-0][image\_desc]The image contains a spreadsheet-like table with a title "PUSCH SU MIMO without RIM-RS". The table has two main sections: a column on the left labeled "PRB Index" and several columns to the right representing different "eAxC" values (eAxC0 to eAxC7).
The "PRB Index" column lists values from K1(0) to K1(0) +11.
The columns for eAxC0 to eAxC7 contain information about UE1 and different beams. Specifically:
- For eAxC0, it shows UE1, Beam0, and "ie. best beam".
- For eAxC1, it shows UE1, Beam1, and "ie. 2nd best beam".
- For eAxC2, it shows UE1, Beam3, and "i.e. 3rd best beam".
- For eAxC3, it shows UE1, Beam4, and "i.e. 4th best beam".
- The columns eAxC4, eAxC5, eAxC6, and eAxC7 are colored blue and yellow respectively, and appear to be empty in the visible rows, except for the header row.
The bottom row of the table contains the label "PUSCH" repeated under each eAxC column.
The table is color-coded, with eAxC0 and eAxC1 being green, eAxC2 and eAxC3 being yellow, and eAxC4 and eAxC5 being blue. eAxC6 and eAxC7 are yellow.
Here is the table in markdown format:

| PRB Index | eAxC0 | eAxC1 | eAxC2 | eAxC3 | eAxC4 | eAxC5 | eAxC6 | eAxC7 |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| K1(0) | UE1 | UE1 | UE1 | UE1 |  |  |  |  |
| K1(0) +1 | Beam0 | Beam1 | Beam3 | Beam4 |  |  |  |  |
| K1(0) +2 | ie. best beam | ie. 2nd best beam | i.e. 3rd best beam | i.e. 4th best beam |  |  |  |  |
| K1(0) +3 |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| K1(0) +4 |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| K1(0) +5 |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| K1(0) +6 |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| K1(0) +7 |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| K1(0) +8 |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| K1(0) +9 |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| K1(0) +10 |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| K1(0) +11 |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
|  | PUSCH | PUSCH | PUSCH | PUSCH | PUSCH | PUSCH | PUSCH | PUSCH |

The table has columns labeled "PRB Index", "eAxC0", "eAxC1", "eAxC2", "eAxC3", "eAxC4", "eAxC5", "eAxC6", and "eAxC7". The "PRB Index" column lists values from "K1(0)" to "K1(0) +11". The other columns contain information about UE1, beams, and PUSCH. Some cells are colored green, yellow, blue, and light green, indicating different configurations or assignments. The last row of the table contains the text "RIM-RS reuse PUSCH with eAxC 6,7".
The table can be represented as follows:

| PRB Index | eAxC0 | eAxC1 | eAxC2 | eAxC3 | eAxC4 | eAxC5 | eAxC6 | eAxC7 |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| K1(0) | UE1 | UE1 | UE1 | UE1 | UE1 | UE1 | UE1 | UE1 |
| K1(0) +1 | Beam0 | Beam1 | Beam3 |  |  |  | Sector Beam |  |
| K1(0) +2 | ie. best beam | ie. 2nd best beam | i.e. 3rd best beam |  |  |  |  |  |
| K1(0) +3 |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| K1(0) +4 |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| K1(0) +5 |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| K1(0) +6 |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| K1(0) +7 |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| K1(0) +8 |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| K1(0) +9 |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| K1(0) +10 |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| K1(0) +11 |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
|  | PUSCH | PUSCH | PUSCH | PUSCH | PUSCH | PUSCH |  |  |

Performance of UE1 is expected to be only slightly degraded in this example, only 4th beam information is modified.

The "PRB Index" column lists values from "K1(0)" to "K1(0) +11".
The subsequent columns represent different "eAxC" (likely referring to antenna carriers or similar technical terms) and show assignments for User Equipment (UE) and beam configurations.
Here's a breakdown of the table content:

| PRB Index | eAxC0 | eAxC1 | eAxC2 | eAxC3 | eAxC4 | eAxC5 | eAxC6 | eAxC7 |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| K1(0) | UE1 | UE2 | UE3 |  | UE4 |  |  |  |
| K1(0) +1 | Beam0 | Beam0 | Beam0 |  | Beam0 |  | Beam0 |  |
| K1(0) +2 | ie. best beam UE1 | ie. best beam UE2 | i.e. best beam UE3 |  | i.e. best beam UE4 |  |  |  |

| K1(0) +7 |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |

| K1(0) +11 |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |

| \* "eAxC6" and "eAxC7" have an orange/yellow background.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_4275.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0] |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |

[image-1][image\_desc]The image is a screenshot of a spreadsheet displaying a table related to PUSCH MU MIMO with RIM-RS. The table has a header row and multiple data rows. The first column is labeled "PRB Index" and lists indices from "K1(0)" to "K1(0) +11". The subsequent columns are labeled "eAxC0" through "eAxC7".
The cells within the table contain information about User Equipment (UE) and beam configurations for different eAxC (e.g., eAxC0, eAxC1, etc.) and PRB indices. Some cells are colored green, yellow, or blue, visually grouping certain eAxC columns.
At the bottom of the table, there are labels "PUSCH" repeated under several eAxC columns, and a final label "RIM-RS reuse PUSCH with eAxC 6,7" spanning the last two eAxC columns.
Here is the table represented in markdown:

| PRB Index | eAxC0 | eAxC1 | eAxC2 | eAxC3 | eAxC4 | eAxC5 | eAxC6 | eAxC7 |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| K1(0) | UE1 | UE2 | UE3 |  | UE4 |  |  |  |
| K1(0) +1 | Beam0 | Beam0 | Beam0 |  | Beam0 |  | Sector Beam |  |
| K1(0) +2 | ie. best beam UE1 | ie. best beam UE2 | i.e. best beam UE3 |  |  |  |  |  |
| K1(0) +3 |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| K1(0) +4 |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| K1(0) +5 |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| K1(0) +6 |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| K1(0) +7 |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| K1(0) +8 |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |

|  |  |  |  |  |  |  | RIM-RS reuse PUSCH with eAxC 6,7 |  |

Performance of UE4 is expected to be worse as the only beam of UE4 is modified. Performance of the whole pairing can also be degraded as correaltion criteria might not be verified anymore.

#### 2.4.10.3.0-7 RIM-RS Receiver Data Fetching RIM-RS receiver data buffer (ID: 11110087)

**2.4.10.3.0-7.0-3**  (ID: `12644541`)

RIM-RS Receiver Data Fetching
The DU with eCPRI 7-2a [CB010315-SR-B] and CPRI [End CB010315-SR-B] and the Radio (for eCPRI 7-2e, when NRCELL/actEcpri72e is set to "TRUE") shall be able to buffer all the RIM-RS data received in special/UL slot (see [11100241](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knMElPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for RIM-RS signal detection in DL slot one by one(see [11110094](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpACevvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

### 2.4.10.4 RIM-RS Signal Detection

#### 2.4.10.4.0-1 RIM-RS Signal Detection RIM-RS signal detection (ID: 11110096)

**2.4.10.4.0-1.0-6**  (ID: `12646337`)

RIM-RS Signal Detection
The DU with eCPRI 7-2a [CB010315-SR-B] and CPRI [Before CB010315-SR-C](only with actNrRim equals to 3) [End CB010315-SR-C][End CB010315-SR-B]] and the Radio (for eCPRI 7-2e,  when NRCELL/actEcpri72e is set to "TRUE") shall perform RIM-RS detection according to 5GMAX algorithm section 2.10.2 (see [11109959](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko-0WvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). For the detected RIM-RS, the DU with eCPRI 7-2a [CB010315-SR-B] and CPRI [Before CB010315-SR-C](only with actNrRim equals to 3) [End CB010315-SR-C][End CB010315-SR-B] and the Radio (for eCPRI 7-2e, when NRBTS/actEcpri72e is set to "Ecpri72eULDL") shall perform the respective RIM-RS signal measurement (see [11109676](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko6i9fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

For each slot with RIM-RS detection, the DU with eCPRI 7-2a [CB010315-SR-B] and CPRI [Before CB010315-SR-C](only with actNrRim equals to 3) [End CB010315-SR-C][End CB010315-SR-B] and the Radio (for eCPRI 7-2e,  when NRCELL/actEcpri72e is set to "TRUE")  shall use the following parameters which are specified in [11161857](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktWW9_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) when NRBTS/NRCELL/actNrRim = 3  or 4 :

- index of spatial streams (shall be  the last two AxC/eAxC spatial streams. For eCPRI 7-2e the spatial streams refer to the internal streams in the Radio as per SRAN\_SiSo.FH.2978. When NRBTS/NRCELL.nbRxBeamforming=16RX and eCPRI 7-2e, the last two streams always correspond to the indices 14 and 15)

* number of symbols and their symbol indexes,
* For each symbol with RIM-RS detection, the following configurations are needed:

>PRB range with start PRB index and PRB number (up to 4 PRB ranges)

> sub-carrier offset for each start PRB index, only when NRBTS/NRCELL/actNrRim = 4

> nSCID (up to 8 nSCID, with one nSCID value refers to one RIM-RS candidate root sequence, see [11109999](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko_bZvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

[CB014153-SR-C] – configurable RIM-RS detection threshold(NRBTS/NRCELL/NRRIM\_PROFILE.rimRsDetectionThreshold, see [12706353](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kg3OM2IcEfC3KtQ77je1xA?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9 "https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/bi_kg3om2icefc3ktq77je1xa?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9"))[End CB014153-SR-C]

Note: RIM-RS in RIM Framework-1(i.e. NRBTS/NRCELL/actNrRim = 4) includes both RS type 1 and type 2.

Note: when RIM Framework-1 is enable(i.e. NRBTS/NRCELL/actNrRim = 4) and  subcarrier offset is not equal to 0, the number of PRBs(49/97) is absolute value to indicate data receiving, but the RE mapping shall be (48*12)/(96*12), the actual occupied PRBs index will be (48+1)/(96+1)(see [11360126](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucsr8vvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

12646353:
RIM-RS Signal Detection
RIM-RS signal detection is not restricted in real-time. It can be done in ULPHY "idle time (DL slot)".

Here is one example to help understanding:

[image-0][image\_desc]The image displays a timeline illustrating the relationship between data reception, RIM-RS detection, and RIM-RS detection reports, with a ratio of 3/7 for UL/DL data slots.
The top row, labeled "ulDIDataSlotRatio 3/7", shows a sequence of data slots represented by colored blocks. Blue blocks indicate DL (Downlink) data, while yellow and red blocks represent UL (Uplink) data. The sequence is: D, D, D, D, S, U, D, D, S, U, U, D, D, D, S, U, D, D, S, U, U.
The second row, "FCP/Data reception", shows received data segments. The first segment is "S U", followed by "S U U", and then another "S U U".
The third row, "RIM-RS detection", depicts detected RIM-RS signals as pairs of yellow and red blocks. There are four such pairs shown, spaced out along the timeline.
The fourth row, "RIM-RS detection report", shows shorter vertical bars, also in yellow and red, representing reports related to RIM-RS detection. These reports are more frequent than the detected signals.
A red arrow originates from the first "S U" segment in the "FCP/Data reception" row and points to the first yellow block in the "RIM-RS detection report" row. Another red arrow originates from the "S U" segment in the "FCP/Data reception" row and points to the first yellow block in the "RIM-RS detection report" row. Below these arrows, text reads: "SFN and slot shall be the RIM-RS signal reception timing". This indicates that the timing of the SFN (System Frame Number) and slot for RIM-RS signal reception is synchronized with the FCP/Data reception.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/img-06061825.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

Note:the timing specified in the figure are used to both the DU with eCPRI 7-2a [CB010315-SR-B] and CPRI [End CB010315-SR-B] and the Radio (eCPRI 7-2e).

---

Here are some examples for RIM-RS signal detection request to help understanding.

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating a grid-based resource allocation system, likely for wireless communication. The grid is composed of cells, and different colored blocks represent allocated resources.
On the left side of the diagram, there is a vertical list labeled "nSCID (Up to 8)" with letters A through H. This likely represents different SCIDs (Scrambling Identification) or some form of identifier.
In the upper right section, there is a label "Frequency Block (Up to 4)". Below this label, a horizontal row of four colored blocks is shown: red, yellow, green, and blue. These blocks occupy four consecutive cells in the grid.
In the lower part of the diagram, a single yellow block labeled "A" is shown. An arrow points from this yellow block to the left, and a text box next to it reads "one deteion resource unit". This suggests that the yellow block labeled "A" represents a single resource unit that has been detected or identified.
The grid itself appears to be a visual representation of time-frequency resources, where rows might represent different SCIDs or other allocation dimensions, and columns might represent time or frequency subcarriers. The colored blocks indicate how these resources are utilized. The presence of the "nSCID" label and the letters A-H suggests that the rows are associated with these SCIDs. The colored blocks in the upper right likely represent a set of frequency blocks allocated to a particular SCID or group of SCIDs. The single yellow block labeled "A" in the lower section, with the accompanying text, highlights a specific detected resource unit.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_4290.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

Figure1: Description for detection resource unit

[image-1][image\_desc]The image displays a grid with colored cells containing letters. The grid is organized into columns labeled "symbol index 5 (Up to 32)", "symbol index 6", "symbol index 9", and "symbol index 10". Each of these columns contains a series of rows, with letters from A to H. The cells are colored red, yellow, green, and blue.
Here's a representation of the table in markdown format:

| Symbol Index | Row A | Row B | Row C | Row D | Row E | Row F | Row G | Row H |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **5 (Up to 32)** | A (Red), A (Red), A (Yellow), A (Green), A (Blue) | B (Red), B (Red), B (Yellow), B (Green), B (Blue) | C (Red), C (Red), C (Yellow), C (Green), C (Blue) | D (Red), D (Red), D (Yellow), D (Green), D (Blue) | E (Red), E (Red), E (Yellow), E (Green), E (Blue) | F (Red), F (Red), F (Yellow), F (Green), F (Blue) | G (Red), G (Red), G (Yellow), G (Green), G (Blue) | H (Red), H (Red), H (Yellow), H (Green), H (Blue) |
| **6** | A (Red), A (Yellow) | B (Red), B (Yellow) | C (Red), C (Yellow) | D (Red), D (Yellow) | E (Red), E (Yellow) | F (Red), F (Yellow) | G (Red), G (Yellow) | H (Red), H (Yellow) |
| **9** | A (Green), A (Blue) | B (Green), B (Blue) | C (Green), C (Blue) | D (Green), D (Blue) | E (Green), E (Blue) | F (Green), F (Blue) | G (Green), G (Blue) | H (Green), H (Blue) |
| **10** | A (Red) | B (Blue) | C (Yellow) | D (Red) | E (Blue) | F (Red), F (Yellow) | G (Green) | H (Yellow) |
| The image also contains some isolated colored cells with letters outside the main grid structure, specifically: |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| - A red 'B' in the column for "symbol index 6" and row B. |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| - A red 'D' in the column for "symbol index 9" and row D. |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| - A green 'B' in the column for "symbol index 10" and row B. |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| - A red 'F' and a yellow 'F' in the column for "symbol index 10" and row F. |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| - A blue 'E' in the column for "symbol index 10" and row E. |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| - A blue 'F' in the column for "symbol index 10" and row F. |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| - A green 'G' in the column for "symbol index 10" and row G.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_4290.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1] |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |

Figure2: Possible detection request example in one slot

Note: 1) RIM-RS candidate root sequence generation may be up to 8 in one slot;

2) RIM-RS frequency block may be up to 4 in one slot;

3) 5G-L2-PS will guarantee the detection resource units in one slot not exceed 5G-L1-UL capacity;(see 5G\_UP\_5449\_replaced([11399499](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ugBFt_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

4) take 4 symbols as example. symbol index may be inconsecutive.

---

### 2.4.10.5 RIM-RS Detection and Measurement Report

#### 2.4.10.5.0-1 RIM-RS Detection and Measurement Report RIM-RS detection and measurement report (ID: 11110163)

**2.4.10.5.0-1.0-4**  (ID: `12646483`)

RIM-RS Detection and Measurement Report
For each slot with RIM-RS detection, DU with eCPRI 7-2a [CB010315-SR-B] and CPRI [End CB010315-SR-B] and the Radio (for eCPRI 7-2e, when NRCELL/actEcpri72e is set to "TRUE")  shall perform RIM-RS detection as described in [11110096](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpACe_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) (up to 32 detections per symbol) and shall report the following contents for RS1 or RS2

o Timing Information:

• SFN and slot for RIM-RS signal received in the air interface (see [11110132](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpApgfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ), see [11162649](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktYMIvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ), see [11162888](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktYMJfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

o For each symbol with RIM-RS signal detected

• For each detected RIM-RS sequence, DU with eCPRI 7-2a [CB010315-SR-B] and CPRI [End CB010315-SR-B] and the Radio(for eCPRI 7-2e, when NRCELL/actEcpri72e is set to "TRUE") shall report the respective RIM RS triplet which includes:

Ø Start PRB index and nSCID (see [11162649](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktYMIvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ), see [11162888](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktYMJfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

Ø Signal Power and Peak Value (see [11109678](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko6i9_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ), see [11163050](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktYMLfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

o The symbol indices which are configured for detection but can’t be completed in the current slot (see [11161857](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktWW9_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Note: RIM-RS in RIM Framework-1(i.e. NRBTS/NRCELL/actNrRim = 4) shall include both RS type 1 and type 2.

---

### 2.4.10.6 Supported RIM-RS Reception

#### 2.4.10.6.0-1 Supported RIM-RS Reception Supported RIM-RS reception (ID: 11110182)

**2.4.10.6.0-1.0-5**  (ID: `12646505`)

Supported RIM-RS Reception
For TDD FR1,when NRBTS/NRCELL/actNrRim = 3  or 4  , Loki and Thor  DU HW with eCPRI 7-2a [CB010315-SR-B] and CPRI [Before CB010315-SR-C](only with actNrRim equals to 3) [End CB010315-SR-C][End CB010315-SR-B] and the Radio (for eCPRI 7-2e, when NRCELL/actEcpri72e is set to "TRUE") shall support the below algorithms for RIM-RS reception:

* RIM-RS reception introduction, see [11109959](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko-0WvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)
* RIM-RS candidate root sequence generation, see[11109996](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko_bZfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)
* RIM-RS receiver data fetching, see [11110030](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko_ba_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)(Only for DU HW with eCPRI 7-2a [CB010315-SR-B] and CPRI [Before CB010315-SR-C](only with actNrRim equals to 3) [End CB010315-SR-C][End CB010315-SR-B])
* RIM-RS signal detection, see [11110094](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpACevvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)
* RIM-RS detection and measurement report, see [11110163](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpAph_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)

For TDD FR1, Loki and Thor  DU HW with eCPRI 7-2a [CB010315-SR-B] and CPRI [Before CB010315-SR-C](only with actNrRim equals to 3) [End CB010315-SR-C][End CB010315-SR-B] and the Radio (for eCPRI 7-2e, when NRCELL/actEcpri72e is set to "TRUE"), additional update for RIM-RS candidate root sequence generation as per [11109999](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko_bZvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

For TDD FR1 Loki  and Thor  DU HW with eCPRI 7-2a [CB010315-SR-B] and CPRI [Before CB010315-SR-C](only with actNrRim equals to 3) [End CB010315-SR-C][End CB010315-SR-B], DU shall perform RIM-RS receiver data fetching for the overlapped region with PUCCH/PRACH with special handling, see [11110030](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko_ba_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

For TDD FR1 Loki  and Thor  DU HW with eCPRI 7-2a, RIM-RS receiver data fetching in NDM UL symbols (overlapping with pSRS), update introduced for the beam weight and PatternID, see [11110030](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko_ba_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

Note: 1) RIM-RS in RIM Framework-1(i.e. NRBTS/NRCELL/actNrRim = 4) shall include both RS type 1 and type 2.

---

11110210:
OFDM Symbol phase compensation
Unlike LTE, Dc (Direct carrier) subcarrier for 5G/NR s used to send the signal as per the formula in 38.211 section 5.4.

Since Dc subcarriers for the transmitter and the receiver are not necessarily perfectly synchronized, phase compensation is introduced in 3GPP to cope with this potential issue. The phase compensation is included in the up conversion formula in 38.211 section 5.4 for all channels, except for PRACH. It corresponds to the following term which is symbol basis and it depends on the numerology, and on the configured carrier frequency (arfcn).

[image-0][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical expression. The expression is:
$e^{-j2\pi f\_0(t\_{start,l}^{\mu} + N\_{CP,l}^{\mu}T\_c)}$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_4306.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

where the different variables are defined in 38.211 section 5.3.1.

For CPRI, DU(L1) shall apply conjugated phase compensation for each symbol for all the UL channels and signals, except for PRACH.

The conjugated operation is applied to the phase compensation value computed from the algorithm in 5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_1997\_replaced([11274097](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpFZJPvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

For eCPRI**[CB007595-B] /eCPRI 7-2e**[End CB007595-B], OFDM symbol phase compensation shall not be performed in DU(L1)**[CB007595-B] /Radio(L1)**[End CB007595-B]. It is done on RU side. Please refer to 5G\_RAN\_RF\_1038\_replaced([11288379](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Tgrn6PvYEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).***\****\*

Note: More details about the implementation status could be found in 5GMAX algo spec. section 4.1.3 and in 5G\_L1\_19625\_replaced([11397316](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e8xZS_vvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) (TBC).

The OFDM symbol phase compensation is in L1HW supported as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_17688\_replaced([11110233](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpBQn_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

**[CB007595-B]**

**Note:**

- "5G-L1-UL" is changed to "DU(L1)" since this version as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_20317\_replaced([11094116](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmZaYfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). (Note kept only in this version)

**[End CB007595-B]**

---

### 2.4.11.0-3 OFDM Symbol phase compensation RIM-RS phase compensation in RIM-RS Receiver (ID: 11110213)

**2.4.11.0-3.0-4**  (ID: `11110220`)

OFDM Symbol phase compensation
In UL for eCPRI FH, the phase compensation is performed in RU as for all UL signals/channels  (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_17441\_replaced([11110202](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpBQk_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) including RIM-RS signal.

While for RIM-RS transmitter the 5G-L1-DL shall perform additional operation for RIM-RS phase compensation purposes (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_12974\_replaced([11101247](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knV1kvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))),

for RIM-RS receiver processing (depicted in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_13035\_replaced([11109974](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko_bYPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) the 5G-L1-UL shall not perform similar additional step.

[CB010315] [DRAFT]

For CPRI FH:

In UL for CPRI FH the phase compensation is performed in 5G-L1-UL as for all UL signals/channels  (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_17441\_replaced([11110202](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpBQk_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) including RIM-RS signal,

for RIM-RS receiver processing refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_13034\_replaced([11109989](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko_bY_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). [End CB010315]

Rational:

* RIM-RS detector is based on PAPR after RIM-RS signal peak correlation position per symbol,
* no impact to PAPR - same performance is as expected - with/without phase compensation

Only minor impact to peak position which is not the key point to detect RIM-RS signal.

Refer to more details in 5GMAX algo spec. section 2.10.2.

The RIM-RS phase compensation in RIM-RS Receiver is in L1HW supported as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_17688\_replaced([11110233](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpBQn_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

### 2.4.11.0-4 OFDM Symbol phase compensation 7.5kHz UL frequency shift (ID: 11110223)

**2.4.11.0-4.0-3**  (ID: `11110231`)

OFDM Symbol phase compensation
For FDD CPRI DSS, gNB shall support 7.5kHz UL frequency shift (see 5G\_UP\_1432\_replaced([11359220](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucl-Q_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_CP\_IF\_RRC\_2794\_replaced([11340377](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_wV7T6PvjEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

For this purpose, DU shall perfrom the 7.5kHz UL channel raster shift  (see 38.104 §5.4.2.1) before the FFT operation for any UL signal to ensure that NR UL grid is aligned with LTE UL grid in an orthogonal manner based on OFDM waveform.

For FDD eCPRI DSS mode (see 5G\_UP\_1432\_replaced([11359220](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucl-Q_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_CP\_IF\_RRC\_2794\_replaced([11340377](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_wV7T6PvjEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and non-DSS mode but with the BPF flag for UL shift is set to true (refer to frequencyShiftPerformedOnRu in 5G\_CP\_IF\_RRC\_2794\_replaced([11340377](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_wV7T6PvjEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), the 7.5 kHz UL frequency shift is performed by RU instead of DU according to SRAN\_RU\_SFS.1066 (/RA System General Modules/SRAN System Modules/SRAN\_requirements\_to\_Radio\_Units).

[CB008925-A] DU shall also apply 7.5kHz UL frequency shift for any UL signals when NRCELL.nbIoTMode = ‘guardbandNR’ and CPRI FH to ensure that NR UL grid is aligned with LTE NBIoT grid. [End CB008925-A]

For CPRI FH, the 7.5kHz UL channel raster shift is in L1HW supported as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_17688\_replaced([11110233](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpBQn_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

### 2.4.11.0-5 OFDM Symbol phase compensation Supported UL OFDM symbol phase compensation in L1 HW (ID: 11110233)

**2.4.11.0-5.0-9**  (ID: `13478625`)

OFDM Symbol phase compensation
The UL OFDM symbol phase compensation is supported in L1HW as follows:

|  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **L1 HW** | **Loner** | **Loki** | **Thor** | **Thor (Radio 7-2e)** | **GPU** | **First Feature** |
| OFDM symbol phase compensation ([11110202](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpBQk_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) | Supported | Supported | Supported | Supported | NOTE 1 | - |
| 7.5kHz UL channel raster shift \*([11110223](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpBQm_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) | Supported for CPRI | Supported for CPRI | Supported for CPRI | Supported for CPRI | - | - |
| RIM-RS OFDM symbol phase compensation ([11110213](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpBQlvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) | - | Supported, refer to ([11110213](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpBQlvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) | Supported, refer to ([11110213](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpBQlvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) | Supported, refer to ([11110213](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpBQlvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) | - | CB006991-C |

* DU shall support “NRCELL.nbIoTMode = ‘guardbandNR’ for Loki and Thor L1 HWs

NOTE 1: From E2E System perspective and not for CPRI FH case, the operation is performed by:  
- Radio(L1) with eCPRI FH   
- [CB016406-A] or by FHGW CPRI/eCPRI FH conversion [End CB016406-A]
